# AnalytIQ Master Development Checklist

> Checkbox-first, LLM-native roadmap for development execution.

---

## Global Definition of Done

- [ ] Code implemented
- [ ] Unit tests added or updated
- [ ] Integration tests added where applicable
- [ ] E2E tests added for critical user flows
- [ ] Error handling implemented
- [ ] Retries/backoff implemented where external services are called
- [ ] Structured logging added
- [ ] Security and privacy implications reviewed
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] Pull request reviewed
- [ ] Merged to main branch

---

# Release 1 - MVP

Phase 1 foundation: connector + governance, semantic layer, session planner, gold table data modeler, XGBoost training with walk-forward backtest, and basic artifact generation (KPI + primary time series). Workspace artifact list and manual sharing.

## Milestone A - Connectors & Governance Foundation

### Milestone Success Criteria
- [ ] Snowflake connector registers and stores credentials encrypted
- [ ] Automated ingestion profiling runs and generates governance_manifest
- [ ] Governance manifest API available and viewable in integration UI

---

## Sprint 1 - Integration & Ingestion APIs

**Sprint Goal:** Expose connector registration, secure credential storage, and sampling/profile pipeline for new integrations.

### Sprint Completion Checklist
- [ ] All P0 stories complete
- [ ] All blocking defects resolved
- [ ] Tests passing
- [ ] Demo-ready increment produced
- [ ] Milestone accepted

### Epic: Connectors

#### Requirement / Feature: Snowflake connector implementation

**Feature ID:** `F-001`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, data`

**Rationale:** MVP requires a working Snowflake connector as canonical warehouse for Phase 1.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-001-01: As a Workspace Admin, I want to register a Snowflake integration and store credentials securely, so that AnalytIQ can connect to the canonical warehouse without exposing secrets and with enforceable RBAC and audit trails.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] A backend API POST /api/v1/integrations accepts Snowflake connection metadata (account, region, role, database, schema) and returns an integration_id.
- [ ] - [ ] Credentials (username/password or key pair) are never persisted in plain text in the application DB; they are stored encrypted in the configured secrets manager (Vault or AWS Secrets Manager).
- [ ] - [ ] The integration registration UI (workspace-admin role) collects required fields and shows validation errors for missing/invalid values.
- [ ] - [ ] On registration, the system performs an ephemeral credential validation attempt via a connection-proxy (no credentials logged) and surfaces pass/fail to the user with actionable error messages.
- [ ] - [ ] Integration records in Postgres include metadata only (integration_id, workspace_id, connection_profile, manifest_version=null) and do not contain secrets.
- [ ] - [ ] All registration actions are logged to the audit log with actor_id, integration_id, timestamp, and outcome (success|failure).
- [ ] - [ ] Only workspace Admins can create or update integration records; RBAC enforced server-side.
- [ ] - [ ] Unit, integration, and security tests cover validation, happy path, and failure cases; CI gates prevent merge if tests fail.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-001-01: Backend: Implement integrations API endpoints for Snowflake registration and metadata storage
    - [ ] - [ ] Define OpenAPI schema for POST /api/v1/integrations and GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement input validation (JSON Schema / TypeScript types) for connection_profile fields
    - [ ] - [ ] Persist integration metadata to Postgres (integration table) without any secrets field
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement RBAC checks (workspace admin only) on create/update/delete endpoints
    - [ ] - [ ] Add structured logging and trace_id propagation for each API call
- [ ] TASK-F-001-02: Backend: Integrate with secrets manager for secure credential storage
    - [ ] - [ ] Add an abstraction layer for secrets manager (interface for Vault and AWS Secrets Manager)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement Vault client and AWS Secrets Manager client implementations, with configuration via env/secret config
    - [ ] - [ ] On integration register, write credentials to secrets manager and store returned secret_id in Postgres
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure secrets are encrypted at rest and access is limited to connector worker role
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests that mock secrets manager responses and assert secret_id persistence
- [ ] TASK-F-001-03: Backend: Implement connection validation via a connection-proxy service
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement a short-lived connection-proxy that retrieves credentials from secrets manager and attempts a read-only Snowflake connection
    - [ ] - [ ] Execute a lightweight introspection query (SELECT current_version() or SELECT 1) to validate connectivity and permissions
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure credentials are never logged; redact or mask any errors containing secrets
    - [ ] - [ ] Return standardized error codes/messages to the integrations API for UI consumption
    - [ ] - [ ] Add integration and unit tests using a Snowflake test account (or emulator) to validate behavior
- [ ] TASK-F-001-04: Frontend: Add 'Add Snowflake' UI flow in Integration Management
    - [ ] - [ ] Design and implement a form to collect connection_profile fields (account, region, role, database, schema, credential type)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement client-side validation and helpful inline error messages
    - [ ] - [ ] Call POST /api/v1/integrations and display progress spinner + validation result
    - [ ] - [ ] Show actionable remediation tips on test-connection failure (network, permissions, wrong role)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement toast/inline success and failure states and route to integration detail on success
- [ ] TASK-F-001-05: Security & DevOps: Secrets access controls, CI validation, and deployment config
    - [ ] - [ ] Add IAM policies and Vault ACLs for connector worker role that limit secret read to integration-service
    - [ ] - [ ] Add secrets manager config and Terraform changes to deployment manifests
    - [ ] - [ ] Add CI checks to ensure no plain-text credentials in code or checked-in config
    - [ ] - [ ] Document runbooks for rotating Snowflake credentials and secret revocation
- [ ] TASK-F-001-06: Testing: Unit, integration, and E2E tests for registration flow
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement unit tests for API input validation and RBAC
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement integration tests that mock secrets manager and connection-proxy to simulate success/failure
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement an E2E test that runs the full UI flow with a test Snowflake account in staging
    - [ ] - [ ] Add tests to assert audit log entries created correctly

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for input validation, RBAC checks, and controller error paths
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests that mock Vault/AWS Secrets Manager and verify secret_id persistence
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests for connection-proxy including simulated Snowflake auth failures, network timeouts, and permission errors
- [ ] - [ ] E2E test in staging using a throwaway Snowflake account to verify full UI flow
- [ ] - [ ] Security scan verifying no secret leakage in logs and no credentials in DB snapshots
- [ ] - [ ] Load test: simulate 50 concurrent registration attempts to validate connection-proxy rate-limiting and queuing behavior

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] POST /api/v1/integrations successfully registers a Snowflake integration and returns integration_id
- [ ] - [ ] Credentials are stored only in the configured secrets manager; application DB contains only secret_id reference
- [ ] - [ ] Connection validation run completes with clear pass/fail in UI; no secrets appear in logs
- [ ] - [ ] RBAC enforced: only workspace Admins can register/update integrations
- [ ] - [ ] Automated tests (unit/integration/e2e) pass in CI pipeline
- [ ] - [ ] Documentation updated: admin guide for Snowflake registration and secret rotation

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] None (horizontal: depends on secrets manager being available in target environment)

###### Agent Notes
- Connector registration is deterministic; do not use LLMs for validation logic. Implement connection validation as a synchronous short-lived operation with clear deterministic error codes. For any automated agent that may reference integration metadata, only provide schema/statistics and never raw credentials. Use trace_id propagation for all calls to diagnose failures.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-001-02: As a Data Engineer, I want the system to profile Snowflake tables and produce a versioned governance_manifest (schema, column profiles, PII flags, health_score), so that downstream agents and users can rely on a canonical, auditable source of truth about the Snowflake integration.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] A governance worker can run a profiling job for a registered Snowflake integration that produces governance_manifest JSON conforming to the PRD schema (manifest_version semver, tables map, columns stats, pii_flags, lineage_edges, dq_gate_status).
- [ ] - [ ] Profiling samples up to 10,000 rows per table (or full table if < 10k) and computes null_pct, distinct_count, p25/p50/p75, mean, stddev, top_5_values for each column.
- [ ] - [ ] PII detection runs using regex + token-classifier; columns flagged with pattern_type (email, ssn, phone, credit_card, full_name) and included in manifest.pii_flags.
- [ ] - [ ] The governance_manifest is persisted and versioned in Postgres (and optionally S3 for archival) and accessible via GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest.
- [ ] - [ ] The profiling job enforces the Row count minimum rule (>=100) and populates dq_gate_status accordingly (PASS|WARN|BLOCK) per PRD rules.
- [ ] - [ ] Profiling respects data minimization: raw sampled rows are never sent to LLMs; only statistics and sanitized top_values are provided to agents.
- [ ] - [ ] Profiling job includes robust retry/timeout (configurable) and produces deterministic error details for operators.
- [ ] - [ ] Tests cover high null rates, PK uniqueness violations, PII detection, and schema drift detection.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-001-07: Backend/Data: Implement profiling worker to introspect Snowflake and compute column statistics
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement Snowflake introspection queries to list schemas/tables/columns respecting role-based access
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement sample extractor to fetch up to 10,000 rows per table (use LIMIT, ORDER BY deterministic key if available)
    - [ ] - [ ] Compute per-column stats: null_pct, distinct_count, min/max, p25/p50/p75, mean, stddev, top_5_values
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement primary-key uniqueness check and row_count calculation
    - [ ] - [ ] Package results into governance_manifest JSON and validate against JSON Schema
- [ ] TASK-F-001-08: Backend/Data: Implement PII detection pipeline (regex + lightweight token classifier)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement deterministic regex checks (email, ssn, phone, cc, IP) applied to sample values
    - [ ] - [ ] Integrate a token-classifier model (small, sandboxed) for fuzzy PII detection with a tunable confidence threshold
    - [ ] - [ ] Combine regex + classifier results into unified pii_flags entries with pattern_type and confidence
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure classifier runs locally/internally; never call external LLM for raw row content
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests with synthetic sample data covering each PII type
- [ ] TASK-F-001-09: Backend: Persist and version governance_manifest; expose retrieve API
    - [ ] - [ ] Create migrations: governance_manifests table (manifest_version, integration_id, generated_at, json_blob, generated_by)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement manifest semver increment logic (minor on profile changes, major on schema changes)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest to fetch latest and historical manifests (paginated)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add indexing for integration_id + generated_at
    - [ ] - [ ] Add audit log entry on manifest write
- [ ] TASK-F-001-10: Backend: DQ gate evaluation and enforcement
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement deterministic DQ gate checks per PRD (PK uniqueness, null rate thresholds, row count minimum, freshness SLA)
    - [ ] - [ ] Compute health_score using defined penalties and include in manifest.tables[].health_score
    - [ ] - [ ] Set dq_gate_status: PASS|WARN|BLOCK and populate human_review_required flag
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit structured DQ gate events to pipeline audit log and SSE stream (dq_gate events)
- [ ] TASK-F-001-11: Frontend/UX: Integration detail view - show profiling progress and manifest
    - [ ] - [ ] Add a profiling progress UI with stepper: Catalog → Profile → Define → Health_score → PII_scan
    - [ ] - [ ] Show latest governance_manifest summary (tables count, worst health_score, PII count) and link to full manifest JSON
    - [ ] - [ ] Provide 'Re-run profiling' button with confirmation and estimated duration
    - [ ] - [ ] Display DQ gate statuses and human-review items with links to Review Queue where applicable
- [ ] TASK-F-001-12: Testing & QA: profiling worker tests and performance validation
    - [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for stats calculations including edge cases (all-null column, low-cardinality, high-cardinality)
    - [ ] - [ ] Integration tests against a Snowflake QA instance with synthetic tables to validate sampling limits and PK uniqueness detection
    - [ ] - [ ] Performance tests to ensure profiling of a 1M-row table samples within PRD latency targets (governance < 30s P50 where possible)
    - [ ] - [ ] Security tests to ensure no raw rows are exposed via APIs or logs

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for stats functions (percentiles, mean, stddev) with synthetic column vectors
- [ ] - [ ] Component tests for PII detection combining regex and classifier with labeled datasets
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests against staging Snowflake with tables covering edge-cases (all nulls, >10k rows, PK duplicates)
- [ ] - [ ] End-to-end run: register integration -> run profiling -> retrieve manifest -> assert dq_gate_status and health_score
- [ ] - [ ] Security tests verifying that sampled raw rows are never returned to agents or logs and that top_5_values are sanitized
- [ ] - [ ] Performance tests profiling 10 representative large tables to validate latency targets and resource usage

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Governance worker produces manifest JSON that validates against the PRD JSON Schema
- [ ] - [ ] Manifest persisted and retrievable via GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest with historical versions
- [ ] - [ ] PII detection flags columns with pattern_type and confidence; columns with PIIs are marked as requiring admin review for ML use
- [ ] - [ ] DQ gates computed and reflected in manifest.dq_gate_status with appropriate PASS|WARN|BLOCK
- [ ] - [ ] Profiling job produces deterministic logs/traces and emits SSE dq_gate events for pipeline visibility
- [ ] - [ ] Automated tests included in CI for correctness, performance, and security

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] US-F-001-01: Snowflake integration registration and secrets storage must be implemented and deployed
- [ ] - [ ] Snowflake test account / staging credentials available for integration tests

###### Agent Notes
- The profiling worker is deterministic and must produce structured JSON only. Any AI components (token classifier) should be small, internally hosted, and only operate on sampled values — never on full PII datasets. Provide clear error codes for schema access denied, insufficient privileges, and sample read timeouts. When producing the manifest, include provenance: trace_id, integration_id, worker_version, and sample_query signatures to enable reproducible audits.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-001-03: As an Analyst, I want a sampling and preview pipeline for new Snowflake integrations that returns sanitized sample rows and schema previews (without exposing raw data to LLM agents), so that I can quickly validate that the expected tables and columns are present and start session planning.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] A sampling API GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/tables/{table}/sample returns up to 1,000 sanitized rows and schema metadata (column types, semantic_type suggestions) for UI preview.
- [ ] - [ ] Sample rows are stored temporarily encrypted (S3 or DB) and are TTL'd (e.g., 24h) and access-controlled; raw samples are not provided to any LLM agent.
- [ ] - [ ] The sample extractor enforces row limits (max 10k for profiling, 1k for preview) and deterministic ordering when possible to ensure reproducible previews.
- [ ] - [ ] The preview UI displays sample rows, column stats (null_pct, distinct_count), and suggested semantic_type; the UI marks any PII-matched columns and hides/masks values by default (mask pattern configurable per column).
- [ ] - [ ] The preview pipeline logs access events (who fetched sample, when, for which integration/table) into the audit log and enforces workspace-level access controls.
- [ ] - [ ] Tests cover masking behavior, TTL expiration, access control, and API error cases (table not found, permission denied).

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-001-13: Backend/Data: Implement sample collector and storage for table preview
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement job to extract deterministic sample rows (ORDER BY primary key or deterministic hash) with configurable limit (default 1000)
    - [ ] - [ ] Sanitize sample rows: mask PII columns per policy (replace characters with '*', preserve length) and remove binary/large fields
    - [ ] - [ ] Store sample securely in encrypted S3 or Postgres bytea with metadata (created_at, expires_at, integration_id, table_name)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement TTL cleanup worker to delete expired samples
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests for masking and deterministic sampling
- [ ] TASK-F-001-14: Backend: Implement sample preview API and access control
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/tables/{table}/sample returning schema metadata + sanitized sample rows
    - [ ] - [ ] Enforce workspace membership and per-resource RBAC on preview endpoints
    - [ ] - [ ] Return helpful error codes: 404 table not found, 403 insufficient privileges, 429 sampling rate limited
    - [ ] - [ ] Instrument API with tracing and logs that do not contain raw values (mask logs)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add integration tests for API responses and RBAC behavior
- [ ] TASK-F-001-15: Frontend: Table preview UI for newly registered Snowflake integrations
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement 'Preview table' action in Integration Detail that calls sample API and shows schema + sample rows
    - [ ] - [ ] Display column-level stats (null_pct, distinct_count) and suggested semantic_type with an 'Edit suggestion' affordance
    - [ ] - [ ] Mask PII columns by default and provide a toggle to reveal values for workspace Admins (reveal action audited)
    - [ ] - [ ] Show sample TTL and a button to 'Refresh sample' to re-run extraction
    - [ ] - [ ] Handle API error states with clear remediation steps
- [ ] TASK-F-001-16: Security & Privacy: Ensure samples are not leaked to LLM agents and apply data minimization
    - [ ] - [ ] Enforce policy: no service call to any LLM or external model includes raw sampled rows; only sanitized stats may be provided
    - [ ] - [ ] Add middleware to redact any sampled-row payloads before they reach logging or monitoring sinks
    - [ ] - [ ] Document sampling retention policy and provide admin toggle for sample retention TTL
    - [ ] - [ ] Add security tests verifying that LLM-agent inputs contain only schema/stats and not sample_row data
- [ ] TASK-F-001-17: Testing & QA: E2E and security tests for sampling and preview
    - [ ] - [ ] E2E test: register integration -> profile -> preview a table -> validate masked values and schema metadata
    - [ ] - [ ] Security test: attempt to call preview API with non-member credentials and assert 403
    - [ ] - [ ] Regression tests for TTL cleanup to ensure expired samples removed
    - [ ] - [ ] Accessibility tests for the preview UI (keyboard nav, aria attributes)

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for masking logic and deterministic sampling
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests for sample storage and TTL cleanup worker
- [ ] - [ ] E2E UI test to validate preview flow and masked/unmasked behavior for Admins
- [ ] - [ ] Security test to assert no raw sampled rows included in LLM inputs or outbound logs
- [ ] - [ ] Load test: request preview for 200 different tables concurrently to validate rate limiting and connection pooling

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] GET sample API returns up to 1,000 sanitized rows and schema metadata; response times meet artifact preview UX targets (<500ms median for cached samples)
- [ ] - [ ] Samples stored encrypted with TTL and are cleaned up automatically; no sample persists beyond TTL unless explicitly retained by Admin
- [ ] - [ ] Masking rules applied consistently and toggle-to-reveal audited
- [ ] - [ ] No LLM agent or external service receives raw sample rows; data minimization rules enforced in code and tests
- [ ] - [ ] Automated tests and E2E coverage added and passing in CI

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] US-F-001-01: Integration must be registered and credentials available via secrets manager
- [ ] - [ ] US-F-001-02: Profiling worker must be able to sample rows for metadata and suggested semantic types

###### Agent Notes
- Preview pipeline must never feed raw sample rows into any LLM. When agents require context for session planning, provide only schema metadata and aggregated statistics (null_pct, distinct_count, top_values sanitized). Implement deterministic sampling ordering to ensure reproducible previews; include sample_query_signature in audit logs to allow forensic reproduction.

#### Requirement / Feature: Warehouse interface abstraction (dialect-aware SQL layer)

**Feature ID:** `F-002`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, data`

**Rationale:** Abstracted warehouse interface enables future connectors and consistent SQL generation.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-001

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-002-01: As a backend/platform engineer, I want a single, language-agnostic WarehouseInterface contract and a pluggable dialect-aware SQL generator, so that all connectors and pipeline stages can generate safe, parameterized, dialect-correct SQL consistently.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Define a WarehouseInterface (code-level contract) with core methods: compile_select(query_spec), compile_create_table(table_spec), compile_insert_select(insert_spec), quote_identifier(), parameterize(value), and explain_sql(sql).
- [ ] - [ ] Provide a pluggable dialect registration API where each dialect implements the contract and can be loaded at runtime by name.
- [ ] - [ ] Generated SQL must be parameterized (no inline literal concatenation) and escape/quote identifiers according to the active dialect.
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for the core interface and a mock dialect achieve >= 90% code coverage for the module.
- [ ] - [ ] Provide API documentation (OpenAPI snippets and README) showing usage patterns for compile_* methods and error classes.
- [ ] - [ ] Error handling: consumer-facing exceptions include actionable messages and structured error codes; all SQL generation errors are logged and surfaced to the pipeline as dq_gate type: error.
- [ ] - [ ] Verify thread-safety / concurrency by running concurrent compile calls (10x parallel) with no shared-state corruption.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-002-01: Design WarehouseInterface contract and API surface
    - [ ] - [ ] Draft TypeScript / Python interface definitions for WarehouseInterface with method signatures and typed input/output schemas
    - [ ] - [ ] Define a minimal JSON schema for query_spec, table_spec, and insert_spec used by compile_* methods
    - [ ] - [ ] Document expected error classes and structured error codes (e.g. SQL_SYNTAX_ERROR, UNSUPPORTED_FEATURE, PARAMETERIZATION_ERROR)
    - [ ] - [ ] Review design with data engineering and security leads; incorporate feedback
    - [ ] - [ ] Create README and OpenAPI example snippets demonstrating interface usage
- [ ] TASK-F-002-02: Implement core SQL generation engine and registry
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement base SQL generation module that implements parameter handling, identifier quoting hooks, and template composition helpers
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement runtime dialect registry that can register/unregister dialect modules by key
    - [ ] - [ ] Add compile-time validation of input specs against the JSON schemas with descriptive error messages
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement structured logging for compile requests (trace_id, user_id, session_id, spec_hash)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests for base engine behaviors (parameter ordering, quoting, schema validation)
- [ ] TASK-F-002-03: Create a `mock` dialect for development and testing
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement a simple MockDialect that inherits the WarehouseInterface contract and returns deterministic SQL strings for testing
    - [ ] - [ ] Add mock dialect to test registry and CI test matrix
    - [ ] - [ ] Write unit tests that exercise compile_select/create/insert for edge cases (reserved words, unicode identifiers, NULL values)
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate concurrency with stress tests invoking mock dialect in parallel
    - [ ] - [ ] Publish a short example app that compiles a session_spec into SQL using the mock dialect
- [ ] TASK-F-002-04: Security & validation work for SQL generation
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement parameterization-enforcement linter that fails compilation if any literal interpolation is detected
    - [ ] - [ ] Add whitelist rules to reject DDL/DDL-like constructs in compile_select unless explicitly requested by caller (e.g. compile_create_table allowed only for trusted services)
    - [ ] - [ ] Integrate static SQL injection heuristics and unit tests proving injection attempts are parameterized or rejected
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure generated SQL never includes plaintext credentials and add tests that would catch accidental leaks
    - [ ] - [ ] Add SCA / dependency checks for any third-party templating libraries used
- [ ] TASK-F-002-05: Frontend integration: surface SQL generation errors and warnings in pipeline UI
    - [ ] - [ ] Add a deterministic error payload format from backend that includes error_code, message, remediation_hint, and spec_reference
    - [ ] - [ ] Add frontend components to display SQL generation warnings/errors in the session run stream (streaming events: dq_gate / error)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add retry / contact support CTA and include trace_id for ops debugging
    - [ ] - [ ] Add automated UI tests (Cypress or Playwright) that assert error message rendering for sample error payloads
    - [ ] - [ ] Log UI interactions with the error card for monitoring (user clicked retry, viewed details)
- [ ] TASK-F-002-06: CI, linting, and developer docs
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit test job for warehouse interface to CI pipeline with badge
    - [ ] - [ ] Add lint rules and pre-commit hooks (formatting, types, no-console) for this package
    - [ ] - [ ] Publish internal package docs & code examples into the engineering handbook
    - [ ] - [ ] Create a migration/usage guide for downstream teams to adopt the new interface
    - [ ] - [ ] Schedule a 1-hour knowledge-share session with platform and data-engineering teams

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for interface methods including negative/error cases
- [ ] - [ ] Schema validation tests for query_spec/table_spec/insert_spec JSON schemas
- [ ] - [ ] Concurrency/stress test: 10x parallel compile calls with mock dialect
- [ ] - [ ] Security tests verifying parameterization and SQL-injection heuristics
- [ ] - [ ] Contract tests ensuring dialect registry loads/unloads modules correctly
- [ ] - [ ] CI integration test that runs against the mock dialect and verifies trace_id logging

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] WarehouseInterface contract implemented and merged to main branch
- [ ] - [ ] Mock dialect implemented and covered by unit tests >= 90% for this module
- [ ] - [ ] OpenAPI snippets + README published in repo docs
- [ ] - [ ] CI green for unit tests and linting, PR reviewed by at least one data engineer and one security engineer
- [ ] - [ ] Frontend displays SQL generation errors with trace_id and remediation_hint
- [ ] - [ ] Tracing (trace_id) propagated and visible in logs for compile requests

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-001
- [ ] Governance manifest availability (Stage 0) for spec examples

###### Agent Notes
- Implement a minimal, strongly-typed core API first (TypeScript or Python depending on platform language parity). Prioritize parameterized SQL generation to eliminate injection risk. Keep the base engine stateless and delegate dialect-specific behaviors to small modules implementing only the necessary overrides (quoting rules, type mapping, LIMIT/OFFSET pagination syntax, CTAS vs CREATE TABLE semantics). Expose a compile_explain(mode='debug') to help downstream agents produce human-readable SQL for audit logs. Include structured error codes so pipeline orchestrator can map errors to dq_gate events.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-002-02: As a data engineer, I want a production Snowflake dialect implementation and a library of SQL templates for gold table DDL and INSERT-SELECT patterns, so that the data modeler and feature-engineering agents can write gold tables and feature tables reliably to Snowflake.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Implement a SnowflakeDialect module that conforms to WarehouseInterface and supports Snowflake-specific features: quoted identifiers using double-quotes, identifier case rules, VARIANT JSON handling, CTAS vs CREATE TABLE AS SELECT handling, transient/temporary table options, clustering/partition hints (if provided).
- [ ] - [ ] Provide a tested template function to generate gold table CREATE TABLE DDL and INSERT INTO ... SELECT statements for common patterns (join paths, derived columns, partitioning by date column).
- [ ] - [ ] Generated SQL must be syntactically valid for Snowflake (validated via SQL linter or a non-production read-only validation job against a Snowflake test account or adapter mock).
- [ ] - [ ] Include type mapping for common semantic types (dimension|measure|date|id|flag|geo) to Snowflake SQL types with unit tests for each mapping.
- [ ] - [ ] Error handling: dialect returns structured UnsupportedFeature error for features not available in Snowflake (e.g. certain connector-specific hints), and these are surfaced to pipeline with remediation.
- [ ] - [ ] Provide examples and unit tests demonstrating safe parameterization and correct quoting for edge cases (identifiers with spaces, non-ASCII).

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-002-07: Implement SnowflakeDialect core behaviors
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement identifier quoting and case normalization functions (double-quotes, uppercase/lowercase rules as configured)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement literal/parameter formatting strategy that produces Snowflake-safe parameter placeholders
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement type mapping table from semantic_type -> Snowflake SQL type with tests
    - [ ] - [ ] Add Snowflake-specific DDL options (TRANSIENT, clustering, time-travel comments) toggled via table_spec flags
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests for quoting and parameterization edge cases (spaces, unicode, reserved words)
- [ ] TASK-F-002-08: Create gold table DDL and INSERT-SELECT template library for Snowflake
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement template to generate CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS gold_schema.gold_table (columns...) with metadata comments for lineage and feature_manifest_version
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement INSERT INTO ... SELECT template that composes joins based on validated semantic join paths and includes a SELECT list derived from feature manifest
    - [ ] - [ ] Add optional CTAS mode for large writes and a safe fallback to CREATE + INSERT when CTAS is not desired
    - [ ] - [ ] Add support for writing VARIANT grain_keys and JSON-encoding helper
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests that compare generated SQL against expected canonical SQL strings for sample specs
- [ ] TASK-F-002-09: Validate Snowflake SQL via linter and lightweight integration check
    - [ ] - [ ] Integrate a SQL linter/formatter that understands Snowflake dialect and add validation step in CI
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement a non-destructive Snowflake validation runner that runs EXPLAIN on generated queries against a sandbox schema (read-only validation account or SQL parser adapter)
    - [ ] - [ ] Create a smoke integration test (non-destructive) that runs EXPLAIN for CREATE and INSERT queries and asserts no parse errors
    - [ ] - [ ] Add retry/backoff semantics for validation runner and log parse failures with trace_id and failing SQL snippet
    - [ ] - [ ] Add a CI job that runs the linter and EXPLAIN checks for Snowflake dialect templates
- [ ] TASK-F-002-10: DevOps: feature-flag and staged rollout for Snowflake dialect
    - [ ] - [ ] Add feature flag gating in configuration so Snowflake dialect can be enabled per workspace or per environment
    - [ ] - [ ] Deploy dialect module behind a service version tag and create a rollout plan (canary -> 10% -> 100%)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add monitoring dashboards for compile failures and latency (per workspace) for Snowflake dialect
    - [ ] - [ ] Add automated rollback scripts for disabling dialect on error spikes
    - [ ] - [ ] Document deployment runbook for on-call engineers
- [ ] TASK-F-002-11: Security & compliance review for Snowflake dialect
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure the dialect never serializes or logs database credentials; add unit tests to assert no credentials in output logs
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate that any metadata written into DDL comments does not include PII or secrets
    - [ ] - [ ] Run threat model review for SQL generation paths and add mitigations for query plan exposure
    - [ ] - [ ] Add role-based access checks so only authorized services can trigger CREATE TABLE operations
    - [ ] - [ ] Produce a short security report documenting findings and remediation steps
- [ ] TASK-F-002-12: Documentation and examples for data engineers
    - [ ] - [ ] Create example notebook/snippets showing how to generate a gold table DDL and execute it via the warehouse proxy
    - [ ] - [ ] Add best-practices doc for choosing CTAS vs INSERT modes and partitioning guidance
    - [ ] - [ ] Publish type mapping table and migration notes for teams moving from raw SQL to the dialect templates
    - [ ] - [ ] Record a short screencast (5-10 minutes) demonstrating generation and validation workflow
    - [ ] - [ ] Collect FAQ from early adopters and add to docs

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for type mapping, quoting, and template generation
- [ ] - [ ] Linter/formatter CI job validating generated SQL style
- [ ] - [ ] Non-destructive EXPLAIN integration test against Snowflake sandbox or SQL parser adapter
- [ ] - [ ] End-to-end integration smoke test: compile -> explain -> (optionally) dry-run write to sandboxed schema with admin oversight
- [ ] - [ ] Security tests ensuring no secrets logged and role-based guardrails on DDL operations
- [ ] - [ ] Performance test measuring compile latency and template rendering time under load (50 compile/sec simulated)

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] SnowflakeDialect passes all unit tests and linter checks in CI
- [ ] - [ ] Integration EXPLAIN checks return no parse errors for canonical specs
- [ ] - [ ] Templates for gold table DDL and INSERT-SELECT available and documented
- [ ] - [ ] Feature-flag rollout plan created and first canary rollout executed successfully
- [ ] - [ ] Security review completed and any required mitigations implemented

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-001
- [ ] WarehouseInterface core module (US-F-002-01)

###### Agent Notes
- Snowflake is the MVP warehouse for Phase 1. Keep Snowflake-specific logic encapsulated and minimal: quoting rules, type mapping, and CTAS vs CREATE/INSERT differences. Avoid embedding any runtime credentials here; code should produce SQL and parameter lists only. Prefer EXPLAIN validation step over destructive writes in CI. If Snowflake-specific optimizer hints are needed later, expose them via optional table_spec flags behind guarded feature flags.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-002-03: As an orchestrator/AI agent developer, I want the pipeline (data modeler, feature engineering, gold writer) to use the WarehouseInterface for all SQL compilation and execution requests, so that DQ gates, auditing, tracing, and retries are consistent across connectors and stages.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Data modeler and feature engineering agents call WarehouseInterface.compile_* methods instead of inlined SQL builders.
- [ ] - [ ] All compiled SQL is recorded in pipeline audit logs with trace_id, spec_hash, governance_manifest_version, and semantic_layer_version.
- [ ] - [ ] Add an execution wrapper that executes compiled SQL against the connector proxy, enforces a per-query timeout, and returns structured results or well-defined errors.
- [ ] - [ ] Implement retry/backoff policy for transient warehouse errors and classify errors into retryable vs fatal.
- [ ] - [ ] DQ gate checks that rely on counts/statistics (e.g., row count tolerance) must assert expected rows using parameterized COUNT(*) queries produced by compile_select in a deterministic way.
- [ ] - [ ] End-to-end tests that run a sample session_spec (small dataset) and validate that the gold table is created and written and DQ gates produce PASS/WARN/FAIL as expected.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-002-13: Integrate WarehouseInterface into data modeler subagent
    - [ ] - [ ] Replace inlined SQL generation in data modeler with calls to WarehouseInterface.compile_create_table and compile_insert_select
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure modeler attaches governance_manifest_version and semantic_layer_version to generated table metadata comments
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests asserting modeler calls compile_* with correct spec and handles errors from the compile step
    - [ ] - [ ] Update modeler to validate compiled SQL via compile_explain('fast') before executing
    - [ ] - [ ] Add logging of compiled SQL, spec_hash, and trace_id at INFO level
- [ ] TASK-F-002-14: Integrate WarehouseInterface into feature engineering subagent
    - [ ] - [ ] Modify feature engineer to request SELECT lists and transformation SQL fragments from the interface rather than building strings inline
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure leakage-risk flags are reflected in generated SQL (e.g., hold features by excluding them until confirmed) and compile time checks enforce that
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests to confirm that features with leakage_risk > 0.7 are excluded from compiled SQL
    - [ ] - [ ] Add audit entries for every feature addition/removal with reasoning in the manifest
    - [ ] - [ ] Add integration test that verifies feature manifest version is embedded into the produced gold table metadata
- [ ] TASK-F-002-15: Implement execution wrapper, retries, and query timeout enforcement
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement a warehouse executor service that accepts compiled SQL + parameters and executes them via the connector proxy
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement retry/backoff policy configurable per-error-code with exponential backoff and jitter (max 3 retries)
    - [ ] - [ ] Enforce per-query timeout (default 5 minutes) and return structured TIMEOUT error to orchestrator
    - [ ] - [ ] Add observability metrics: query_latency_ms, query_rows_written, query_error_count, retries_count
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit and integration tests simulating transient failures and ensuring correct retry behavior
- [ ] TASK-F-002-16: Wire audit logging, tracing, and DQ gate integration
    - [ ] - [ ] Add structured audit event emission after compilation and after execution (fields: trace_id, session_id, model_card_id, governance_version, semantic_version, sql_hash)
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure pipeline SSE stream emits dq_gate events with compilation/execution outcomes (PASS/WARN/FAIL and details)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement DQ helper functions that use compile_select to produce COUNT / distribution queries and call the executor in read-only mode
    - [ ] - [ ] Add tests that assert DQ gate behavior when row counts mismatch >2% and when prediction distribution deviates > 3σ
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure audit events are append-only and persisted to the pipeline audit store
- [ ] TASK-F-002-17: End-to-end integration tests and test data
    - [ ] - [ ] Create a small deterministic test dataset and governance manifest fixture for Stage 0 to Stage 5 flows
    - [ ] - [ ] Write end-to-end tests that run a full session_spec through data modeler -> feature engineer -> compile -> execute -> gold write (sandboxed)
    - [ ] - [ ] Assert gold table schema, row counts, feature_manifest_version, and that DQ gate results are correct
    - [ ] - [ ] Add nightly integration test job that runs end-to-end happy path and records performance baselines
    - [ ] - [ ] Add a documented local-run recipe for engineers to run the same end-to-end test against a local DuckDB/sandbox adapter
- [ ] TASK-F-002-18: Monitoring, alerts, and runbook for execution failures
    - [ ] - [ ] Create dashboards for compile failures, execution errors, and DQ gate failure rates with relevant drill-downs
    - [ ] - [ ] Configure alerting for sudden spikes in compile/execution errors (SLO breach) to on-call channel
    - [ ] - [ ] Create runbook documenting steps to investigate trace_id, reproduce failing SQL, and remediate common errors
    - [ ] - [ ] Add a one-click action in ops console to disable auto-execution for a workspace if systemic failure detected
    - [ ] - [ ] Run a postmortem simulation with the on-call team to validate runbook quality

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests verifying orchestrator handles compile/execution successes and failures
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests for DQ gate queries generated by compile_select (COUNT, distribution checks)
- [ ] - [ ] E2E test: small session_spec executes end-to-end and gold tables are written and validated by DQ gates
- [ ] - [ ] Fault-injection tests: simulate transient DB errors and assert retry/backoff behavior
- [ ] - [ ] Performance benchmarks for query execution latency and gold table write durations

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Data modeler and feature engineering agents invoke WarehouseInterface for all SQL generation paths
- [ ] - [ ] Execution wrapper enforces timeouts and retries and returns structured errors
- [ ] - [ ] Audit events for compiled and executed SQL are emitted with required metadata
- [ ] - [ ] End-to-end integration tests pass in CI nightly job
- [ ] - [ ] Monitoring and alerting configured with documented runbook

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-001
- [ ] WarehouseInterface core module (US-F-002-01)
- [ ] SnowflakeDialect (US-F-002-02)

###### Agent Notes
- Prioritize non-destructive validation (EXPLAIN) during early rollout to prevent accidental writes. Ensure compile and execute steps include trace_id so that agents can map errors back to user-facing SSE messages (dq_gate/error). Keep executor stateless and idempotent where possible (idempotency by spec_hash and model_id). Use the same parameterization format across dialects so orchestrator does not need dialect-specific execution logic. Expose a 'dry_run' flag used by CI and by the pipeline pre-write validation step.

### Epic: Security

#### Requirement / Feature: Secrets manager integration for credentials storage

**Feature ID:** `F-003`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, security`

**Rationale:** Credentials must be stored encrypted and not exposed to agents or logs.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-003-01: As a workspace admin, I want connector credentials stored in a dedicated secrets manager via the backend API, so that credentials are encrypted at rest, never logged or returned in API responses, and only accessible to authorized runtime services.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Credentials are stored in a secrets manager (AWS Secrets Manager or HashiCorp Vault) and backend records store only a secret_id reference, never plaintext credentials
- [ ] API to create/update an integration returns secret_id and integration metadata but never returns secret plaintext
- [ ] Secrets are encrypted at rest using KMS or Vault-managed master key with AES-256 equivalent
- [ ] Secrets are retrievable only via backend secret-retrieval proxy API restricted by service IAM role and scoped to the integration_id
- [ ] No secret values appear in application logs, agent payloads, governance manifests, or audit records (only secret_id recorded)
- [ ] UI credential entry form posts directly to backend over TLS, with client-side validation and no client-side persistent storage of credentials
- [ ] Automated unit and integration tests verify that secret plaintext cannot be read from API responses, logs, or agent inputs

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-003-01: Design and implement a secrets-store abstraction and configuration
    - [ ] Draft secrets-store interface specifying create, read, update, delete, rotate, and list operations and error codes
    - [ ] Define secret payload schema for connector credentials (fields, required/optional, metadata tags, ttl/rotation flags)
    - [ ] Implement configuration to toggle provider (aws_secrets_manager | vault) and provider-specific parameters
    - [ ] Document secrets-store initialization sequence and failure modes for startup healthchecks
    - [ ] Add feature-flag gating for rollout to MVP workspaces
- [ ] TASK-F-003-02: Implement backend API endpoints to persist and reference secrets
    - [ ] Add POST /api/v1/integrations to accept integration metadata + credentials and store credentials using secrets-store.create
    - [ ] Ensure API returns integration_id and secret_id only; never include credentials in response body
    - [ ] Add PATCH /api/v1/integrations/{id}/credentials to rotate/update credentials using secrets-store.update
    - [ ] Add GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest to return governance manifest and integration metadata without secret data
    - [ ] Add input validation and clear error codes for missing/invalid credential fields
- [ ] TASK-F-003-03: Implement a secret-retrieval proxy used by query-execution and connector runtime
    - [ ] Build internal-only endpoint GET /internal/secrets/{secret_id} that returns credentials to authorized services only
    - [ ] Enforce mutual TLS / IAM role check on internal endpoints; validate calling service identity via short-lived signing token
    - [ ] Cache secrets in-memory for short TTL (e.g. 60s) per process with explicit cache eviction on rotate
    - [ ] Instrument retrieval call path with structured audit log entry recording secret_id, caller_service, timestamp, and masked outcome
    - [ ] Implement error handling and retry backoff for secret retrieval failures
- [ ] TASK-F-003-04: Integrate secrets usage into connector registration and runtime execution paths
    - [ ] Modify connector registration flow to call secrets-store.create and persist returned secret_id on integration record
    - [ ] Change runtime connector code to call internal secret-retrieval proxy instead of reading credentials from DB
    - [ ] Ensure connector runtime never logs full credential values; log only masked indicators (e.g. last 4 chars if safe)
    - [ ] Add a unit that verifies connector runtime fails safely when secret retrieval is unauthorized or missing
    - [ ] Add metrics for secret retrieval latency, errors, and cache hit ratio to observability
- [ ] TASK-F-003-05: Frontend: credential entry UI and client-side validation
    - [ ] Add secure credential form that POSTs to backend /api/v1/integrations with fields marked required/optional
    - [ ] Validate credential fields client-side (non-empty, host format, port numeric, username present) before submit
    - [ ] Disable browser autofill and prevent saving credentials to localStorage/sessionStorage
    - [ ] Show success message that integration is saved and secret_id returned; never display credential plaintext
    - [ ] Add clear error UI for API validation errors and for network/timeouts
- [ ] TASK-F-003-06: Logging, monitoring, and audit: ensure secrets are not logged and access is auditable
    - [ ] Add centralized audit record for each create/read/update/delete/rotate that stores secret_id, integration_id, actor_id, action, and timestamp
    - [ ] Implement log sanitizer middleware to redact credential fields in any incoming request payloads and stack traces
    - [ ] Create observability dashboards for secret access errors and access frequency
    - [ ] Add an alert rule for unexpected high-rate secret retrievals or failed retrieval spikes
    - [ ] Update incident runbook with secret-access breach steps

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for secrets-store abstraction (mock provider) covering create/read/update/delete and error handling
- [ ] Integration tests with a real or local mock secrets provider verifying secret write and retrieval via proxy
- [ ] API contract tests ensuring POST/GET responses never contain credential plaintext
- [ ] End-to-end test: register connector via UI, run a sample query through connector runtime, assert runtime succeeded and secret plaintext not present in logs or governance manifest
- [ ] Security test: verify that an unauthorized caller to /internal/secrets/{secret_id} receives 403 and request is audited
- [ ] Log scanning test: automated scan of recent logs to assert no credential patterns captured
- [ ] Regression test: agent or LLM inputs do not contain secret fields when running governance agent

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Secrets-store abstraction implemented and configured for at least one provider (AWS Secrets Manager or Vault)
- [ ] Backend integration endpoints persist credentials to secrets manager and store only secret_id in DB
- [ ] Internal secret-retrieval proxy enforces caller identity and returns credentials only to authorized services
- [ ] Frontend credential form posts securely and never stores credentials locally
- [ ] All code paths that touch secrets are covered by unit tests and integration tests
- [ ] Logs and telemetry contain no plaintext secrets and contain audit references to secret_id only
- [ ] Documentation updated: developer integration guide, runbook, and admin UI help text

###### Agent Notes
- Agents must never receive secret plaintext. When agent tasks need connectivity metadata, provide only non-sensitive connection metadata (host, port, driver, masked_user) and the integration_id/secret_id reference. Secret retrieval for live queries must be performed server-side via the internal secret-retrieval proxy; agents must never call secrets-store APIs directly or be passed credentials in prompts or tool inputs.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-003-02: As a DevOps/security engineer, I want the secrets manager and access control provisioned by infrastructure-as-code and runtime policies, so that the system enforces least privilege, key management, secret rotation, and auditability in production.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Terraform (or equivalent) modules provision secrets manager resources, KMS key, and IAM roles for service access in target environment
- [ ] IAM roles and policies grant only required permissions to the backend secret-retrieval service and deny direct access from unapproved principals
- [ ] Automatic rotation schedule is configured and can be triggered via API; rotation does not break running connector jobs because of short-term caching and graceful refresh
- [ ] Audit logs for secret access are forwarded to centralized logging (e.g. CloudWatch/Honeycomb) with strict retention and access control
- [ ] CI/CD pipelines do not store secrets in environment variables or build logs; pipeline uses secret references during deploy
- [ ] Runbooks and emergency rotation procedures are documented and verified by a tabletop exercise

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-003-07: Provision secrets manager and KMS resources via IaC
    - [ ] Add Terraform module to create AWS Secrets Manager secret or Vault mount path plus KMS key resource with rotation enabled
    - [ ] Parameterize module with workspace tagging, encryption key policy, and admins list
    - [ ] Add outputs for secret ARN/mount path and KMS key id for use by backend service deployment
    - [ ] Write idempotent apply/plan CI job to validate resource changes in non-prod environment
    - [ ] Document required environment-level variables and permissions for apply
- [ ] TASK-F-003-08: Create and bind IAM roles and policies for secret access
    - [ ] Design least-privilege policy granting secrets:GetSecretValue and kms:Decrypt to runtime role scoped to secret ARNs used by service
    - [ ] Create runtime IAM role for backend services (API servers, connectors) and attach policy
    - [ ] Create deployer/admin role with permissions to create/rotate secrets and to manage rotation schedules
    - [ ] Add trust policies limiting role assumption to specific service principals and CI/CD pipeline identities
    - [ ] Add policy tests using policy simulator to validate deny paths
- [ ] TASK-F-003-09: Configure secret rotation and rotation-safe retrieval
    - [ ] Enable automatic rotation using provider native rotation or create scheduled Lambda/Vault job that rotates secrets
    - [ ] Implement rotation handler that validates new credentials by attempting a test connection before promoting
    - [ ] Ensure runtime secret cache invalidation mechanism triggers on rotation events (watch SNS/Events hook or Vault TTL)
    - [ ] Add a scheduled job that verifies secret integrity and connectivity weekly
    - [ ] Document and test rollback of failed rotations
- [ ] TASK-F-003-10: Ensure CI/CD and deployments handle secrets safely
    - [ ] Remove hardcoded secrets from pipeline definitions and replace with secret references fetched at deploy time (via short-lived tokens)
    - [ ] Ensure build logs redact environment variable values and mask secrets in CI job output
    - [ ] Add policy to deny creation of PRs containing detected credential regex patterns via pre-commit hook
    - [ ] Update deploy scripts to use service role assumption patterns to fetch runtime credentials securely
    - [ ] Add monitoring to detect pipeline-run attempts to access secret-management APIs from unexpected identities
- [ ] TASK-F-003-11: Monitoring, alerting, and runbook
    - [ ] Create dashboards for secret-access events, secret-rotation failures, and unusual access patterns
    - [ ] Add alert rules for repeated unauthorized access attempts, rotation failures, and high retrieval error rate
    - [ ] Write incident runbook for suspected secret compromise and forced rotation steps
    - [ ] Conduct a tabletop exercise rotating one non-production credential and verify system recovers
    - [ ] Publish runbook and notify SRE/security on-call rotations

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Run Terraform plan/apply in sandbox environment and validate resources created as expected
- [ ] Use IAM policy simulator to validate deny/allow scenarios for the runtime and deployer roles
- [ ] Execute end-to-end rotation test: rotate secret, validate rotation handler verifies connection, verify running connectors reload credentials without failing
- [ ] Simulate unauthorized access attempt and assert alert triggers and audit record created
- [ ] Penetration test focused on secrets endpoints to verify IAM enforcement and absence of SSRF/open endpoints
- [ ] CI job runs that assert no secrets present in logs and that pipeline fetches secrets securely

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] IaC module deploys secrets manager resources and KMS keys in non-prod and prod with reviewed IAM policies
- [ ] Runtime IAM roles and policies enforce least privilege and are documented
- [ ] Secret rotation is configured and tested with a safe promotion/rollback flow
- [ ] CI/CD pipelines do not expose secrets and use secret references via the approved mechanism
- [ ] Monitoring and alerts for secrets exist and trigger on anomalous access
- [ ] Runbook validated by tabletop exercise and stored in runbook repository

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] TASK-F-003-01 (secrets-store abstraction) must be implemented to consume provisioned resources
- [ ] Deployment pipeline and environment access configured for target cloud account

###### Agent Notes
- Agents may need to know whether an integration has valid connectivity but must not be given secret plaintext. Provide agents the integration_id, host/port metadata, and a connectivity check result boolean. Do not include secret rotation tokens, KMS keys, or any part of the secret in agent inputs or logs. Any agent-run connectivity test must call a server-side API which performs the secret retrieval under IAM checks and returns only pass/fail plus non-sensitive error reasons.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-003-03: As a QA lead, I want comprehensive automated and manual tests that validate secrets are never exposed to agents or logs and that error paths handle invalid credentials safely, so that platform security and reliability are assured for MVP.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Automated unit and integration tests assert no API response, log, or governance manifest contains credential plaintext
- [ ] End-to-end tests simulate connector registration and execution, verifying connectors succeed or fail with safe error messages (no plaintext)
- [ ] Agent-level tests confirm LLM prompts and tool inputs never contain secret values and only include metadata/secret_id references
- [ ] Logging sanitization tests validate request/response bodies are redacted for credential patterns
- [ ] A minimal manual penetration test checklist is executed and documented with remediation items resolved

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-003-12: Create unit tests for backend secrets APIs and redaction middleware
    - [ ] Write unit tests verifying secrets-store.create returns secret_id and that backend persists secret_id only
    - [ ] Test secret-retrieval proxy returns credentials only to authorized callers and returns 403 otherwise
    - [ ] Add tests for log-sanitizer middleware showing credential fields are redacted from captured logs
    - [ ] Add tests for API error paths to assert responses do not include credentials or stack traces
    - [ ] Integrate unit tests into CI with required 100% coverage for secrets-related modules
- [ ] TASK-F-003-13: Write integration tests using a local mock secrets provider
    - [ ] Create a mock secrets provider container that implements create/read/update/delete and rotation endpoints
    - [ ] Write integration test that registers an integration and verifies secret created in mock provider and DB stores secret_id
    - [ ] Run end-to-end connector job in test harness calling internal secret-retrieval proxy and assert job uses retrieved creds and succeeds
    - [ ] Assert logs from integration runtime do not contain credential plaintext via log collection in test harness
    - [ ] Add tests for secret rotation flow and assert connector job recovers after rotation
- [ ] TASK-F-003-14: End-to-end system tests including agent boundary checks
    - [ ] Execute full pipeline run in staging that registers a connector, runs governance agent, builds manifest, and assert governance manifest has no credential fields
    - [ ] Run session flow that would otherwise require connector creds and validate agents receive only non-sensitive metadata
    - [ ] Replay recorded agent prompts and assert secret patterns do not appear in agent inputs or outputs
    - [ ] Assert governance agent profiling uses sampled data/stats only and never receives raw secret values
    - [ ] Document and store test artifacts and traces for audit
- [ ] TASK-F-003-15: Security/pen-test checklist and remediation
    - [ ] Create a minimal penetration test checklist targeting secret creation, retrieval, rotation, and admin workflows
    - [ ] Run checklist in staging (or with third-party vendor if available) and log findings
    - [ ] Prioritize and fix any critical/major findings, retest to confirm remediation
    - [ ] Document residual low/medium risks and accepted mitigations in security register
    - [ ] Get SRE/security owner sign-off that critical findings are resolved
- [ ] TASK-F-003-16: Add smoke and regression tests for logging and observability
    - [ ] Create smoke tests to assert audit entries emitted on create/read/update/delete of secrets
    - [ ] Add regression tests that scan recent logs for credential regex patterns and fail if found
    - [ ] Add tests that validate metrics for secret retrieval latency and error rate are emitted
    - [ ] Create an automated periodic job that runs the log-scan test in staging and notifies the security channel on failure
    - [ ] Document expected observability signals and thresholds in test descriptions

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Run unit test suite on PRs touching secrets code; fail PR if any test fails
- [ ] Run integration test suite in ephemeral staging environment with mock secrets provider
- [ ] Execute E2E test that simulates connector registration and a sample query, assert no secrets in logs or manifests
- [ ] Run agent-input scan tests to ensure agent prompts do not include secret fields
- [ ] Run scheduled log-scan job daily in staging for early detection of leaks
- [ ] Perform a manual penetration checklist against staging and document fixes

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] All unit, integration, and E2E tests pass in CI for secrets-related code
- [ ] Agent boundary tests confirm agents never receive or log secret plaintext
- [ ] Pen-test checklist executed and all critical/major findings remediated
- [ ] Log redaction validated via automated scans and integrated into CI
- [ ] Test artifacts, run results, and security sign-off are stored in test-runner and security repositories

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] TASK-F-003-02 (backend API endpoints) and TASK-F-003-03 (secret-retrieval proxy) must be implemented and deployed to test environment

###### Agent Notes
- When simulating end-to-end flows for tests, ensure test agents are passed only metadata (integration_id, host, port, masked_username) and not secret fields. Tests that assert agent behavior should use deterministic, mocked agent inputs to validate absence of secrets in prompt content. For any test that must validate secret retrieval, perform retrieval via the internal proxy only and assert access control succeeds or fails per test scenario.

### Epic: Integrations

#### Requirement / Feature: Integration registration and connector provisioning API

**Feature ID:** `F-004`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, api`

**Rationale:** Programmatic integration registration enables onboarding and triggers governance runs.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-001
- [ ] F-003

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-004-01: - [ ] As a workspace admin, I want to register a new data integration via a secure REST API, so that integrations can be created programmatically and immediately scheduled for governance profiling.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] POST /api/v1/integrations accepts a validated JSON payload and returns 201 with integration_id when authorized and valid.
- [ ] - [ ] API validates required fields (workspace_id, connector_type, connection_config, owner_id) and returns 4xx with structured errors for invalid input.
- [ ] - [ ] Request is authorized only for workspace admins (ABAC enforced) and returns 403 for insufficient permissions.
- [ ] - [ ] Integration record is persisted in Postgres with versioned manifest pointer and created_at/created_by audit fields.
- [ ] - [ ] On successful create the API enqueues an asynchronous governance profiling job and returns a job_id + trace_id in the response.
- [ ] - [ ] No plaintext credentials are logged anywhere and only a secrets reference (not raw creds) is persisted in the integration record.
- [ ] - [ ] Endpoint responds within P50 latency targets defined in the PRD under normal load.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-004-01: - [ ] Design API contract and JSON schema for integration registration
    - [ ] - [ ] Define request JSON schema: workspace_id, connector_type, connection_config (typed per connector), metadata, owner_id, schedule (optional)
    - [ ] - [ ] Define response schema: integration_id, created_at, status: queued, job_id, trace_id, links (manifest, status)
    - [ ] - [ ] Update OpenAPI spec and TypeScript types for server & client
    - [ ] - [ ] Create API error model for consistent 4xx/5xx responses
- [ ] TASK-F-004-02: - [ ] Implement POST /api/v1/integrations backend handler (backend)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add route + controller that performs input validation and authorization (ABAC) checks
    - [ ] - [ ] Persist integration metadata to Postgres with status=CREATED and secrets_ref placeholder
    - [ ] - [ ] Generate and persist trace_id for distributed tracing
    - [ ] - [ ] Enqueue governance profiling job to job-queue (BullMQ/Redis) with integration_id and trace_id
- [ ] TASK-F-004-03: - [ ] Implement DB migrations and integration model (data)
    - [ ] - [ ] Create integrations table schema: id(uuid), workspace_id, connector_type, metadata(jsonb), secrets_ref, manifest_version, status, created_by, created_at, updated_at
    - [ ] - [ ] Add indices on workspace_id and status for list & query performance
    - [ ] - [ ] Add audit log append-only table or ensure pipeline audit event created after create
- [ ] TASK-F-004-04: - [ ] Implement client-side admin form and API integration (frontend)
    - [ ] - [ ] Create admin Integration Create form UX with fields mapped to schema and connector-specific dynamic form sections
    - [ ] - [ ] Wire the form to POST /api/v1/integrations and show queued job status and error messages
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate inputs client-side to prevent common mistakes (missing host, invalid port, invalid credential format)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add success toast with link to integration details page and job trace link
- [ ] TASK-F-004-05: - [ ] Add logging, tracing, and metrics (devops/observability)
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit structured logs for request start/end with trace_id and user_id but never include secrets
    - [ ] - [ ] Create OpenTelemetry spans for API handler and queue enqueue operations
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit Prometheus metrics: integrations_created_total, integrations_create_errors_total, enqueue_latency_seconds
- [ ] TASK-F-004-06: - [ ] Implement tests and API docs (testing)
    - [ ] - [ ] Write unit tests for validation and authorization logic
    - [ ] - [ ] Write integration tests that hit the API, assert DB record, and assert job queued (mock queue)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add contract tests for OpenAPI generation and TypeScript client
    - [ ] - [ ] Add e2e smoke test that simulates admin creating an integration and verifies job status via job_id

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit test: validate schema rejection of malformed connector payloads
- [ ] - [ ] Unit test: reject unauthorized user (403) for non-admin user
- [ ] - [ ] Integration test: successful POST persists DB row and enqueues job (mock Redis)
- [ ] - [ ] Integration test: response contains job_id, trace_id and correct 201 status
- [ ] - [ ] E2E test: admin creates integration from frontend; verify server response and UI shows queued state
- [ ] - [ ] Load test: 100 concurrent integration create requests, measure p95 latency and queue saturation behavior
- [ ] - [ ] Security test: automated scan ensures no creds appear in logs for create request

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] API implemented, reviewed, and merged to main branch
- [ ] - [ ] DB migration applied in CI with rollback path documented
- [ ] - [ ] Unit, integration, and e2e tests passing in CI with >= 90% coverage for the new code paths
- [ ] - [ ] OpenAPI spec and frontend wiring updated; story demo-able end-to-end in a staging environment
- [ ] - [ ] Metrics, logs, and tracing visible in observability dashboards
- [ ] - [ ] Security review passed: no plaintext secrets in logs, secrets stored via secrets_ref

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] F-001
- [ ] - [ ] F-003
- [ ] - [ ] Secrets manager (Vault/AWS SM) integration available
- [ ] - [ ] Job queue infra (Redis + BullMQ) provisioned

###### Agent Notes
- - [ ] Controller should generate an idempotency key for retries; governance job enqueue must be idempotent and safe to run multiple times for same integration_id. Use JSON Schema validation server-side and return detailed field-level errors. Include trace_id in response and propagate it to downstream jobs. Enforce ABAC using workspace role checks. Record an audit event in pipeline audit log after row persisted.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-004-02: - [ ] As the platform operator, I want the system to provision connector resources and run an automated governance profiling job after integration registration, so that the governance manifest is generated, stored versioned, and available via API for downstream stages.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] A provisioning worker picks up queued integration jobs and attempts to provision the connector without embedding secrets in logs.
- [ ] - [ ] Governance profiling job runs asynchronously and produces a governance_manifest JSON stored in object storage (S3) and a manifest_version persisted on the integration record.
- [ ] - [ ] GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest returns the latest governance_manifest only to authorized workspace members and returns 404 if no manifest yet.
- [ ] - [ ] Governance job emits SSE / job-status events with types compliant with PRD streaming protocol (dq_gate, agent_start, agent_complete, error).
- [ ] - [ ] Job implements retry/backoff (exponential up to 3 retries) for transient connector errors and marks job as FAILED after retry exhaustion.
- [ ] - [ ] Any schema change detected increments manifest semver (major on schema change, minor on profile-only change) and stored version reflects correct semver rules.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-004-07: - [ ] Implement provisioning worker and connector proxy layer (backend/data)
    - [ ] - [ ] Build connector proxy that obtains credentials from secrets manager using secrets_ref and opens ephemeral connections to data warehouses
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement connector adapter interface with methods: test_connection(), sample_table_rows(), introspect_schema(), profile_table()
    - [ ] - [ ] Add connector-specific adapters for Snowflake (MVP) and a test adapter (DuckDB) for CI
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure connector proxy uses least-privilege roles and logs only connector metadata (no secrets)
- [ ] TASK-F-004-08: - [ ] Implement governance profiling job runner (AI agent orchestration)
    - [ ] - [ ] Create job orchestration flow that runs: CATALOG → PROFILE → DEFINE → HEALTH_SCORE → PII_SCAN (per PRD order)
    - [ ] - [ ] Integrate with governance agent (local orchestrator or LLM agent layer) with strict schema inputs (no raw rows to LLM)
    - [ ] - [ ] Persist intermediate profiling artifacts and final governance_manifest JSON to S3 with manifest_version and generated_at
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit structured dq_gate events to job-status SSE and pipeline audit log while running
- [ ] TASK-F-004-09: - [ ] Implement GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest endpoint and access control (backend)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add route to fetch manifest meta and signed S3 URL (or proxy the manifest) for workspace members
    - [ ] - [ ] Enforce ABAC: workspace members only; return 403 for outsiders
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement pagination/versions listing: allow ?version= or default latest
    - [ ] - [ ] Add caching layer (Redis) with a short TTL for manifest fetches
- [ ] TASK-F-004-10: - [ ] Implement job resiliency: retries, timeout, and failure handling (devops/ops)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add exponential backoff retry policy for transient connector failures (3 retries, jitter)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement job timeouts and mark job FAILED after configurable timeout; send notification to owner
    - [ ] - [ ] On persistent data-access permission failures, open human_required SSE event with remediation payload
    - [ ] - [ ] Create dashboards & alerts for job failure rate and queue depth
- [ ] TASK-F-004-11: - [ ] Add schema-diffing and manifest semver logic (data)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement fingerprinting of schema on first run and on each run compare; determine major vs minor version bump rules
    - [ ] - [ ] Persist previous fingerprint and include diff summary in audit log and manifest metadata
    - [ ] - [ ] Auto-create human_review_required flag when major version bump detected
- [ ] TASK-F-004-12: - [ ] Implement tests and observability for profiling jobs (testing/observability)
    - [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for connector adapter interface and mocks
    - [ ] - [ ] Integration tests that run the profiling job against a sample DuckDB dataset and assert governance_manifest schema and semver behavior
    - [ ] - [ ] End-to-end test for SSE job-status stream and event ordering (agent_start → dq_gate → agent_complete)
    - [ ] - [ ] Load test for profiling pipeline with 10 simultaneous integrations to validate queue and worker scaling

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit test: connector adapter test_connection success/failure and credential error handling
- [ ] - [ ] Integration test: full profiling run against DuckDB verifying governance_manifest schema properties (tables, columns, pii_flags, health_score)
- [ ] - [ ] E2E test: POST integration → worker consumes job → manifest created → GET manifest returns JSON and correct semver
- [ ] - [ ] SSE test: validate ordering and presence of events: agent_start, dq_gate (per-table), agent_complete, artifact_ready or error
- [ ] - [ ] Failure mode test: verify exponential backoff and final FAILED state after retries
- [ ] - [ ] Security test: verify no secrets appear in logs and that manifest excludes raw sample rows (samples only metadata/stats)
- [ ] - [ ] Performance test: simulate 10 concurrent profiling runs and measure job completion within PRD targets

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Provisioning worker and governance job runner deployed to staging and successfully executed against test warehouses
- [ ] - [ ] governance_manifest JSON files stored in S3 and accessible via API with correct semver logic
- [ ] - [ ] SSE job-status stream implements PRD event types and trace_id propagation end-to-end
- [ ] - [ ] Retries, timeouts and human_required pause behavior implemented and covered by tests
- [ ] - [ ] Observability dashboards and alerts in place for failures and queue health
- [ ] - [ ] Security review completed: connectors fetch secrets from secrets manager via proxy and do not log secrets

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] US-F-004-01
- [ ] - [ ] Secrets manager available (Vault/AWS SM)
- [ ] - [ ] Job queue infra provisioned
- [ ] - [ ] AI agent orchestration runtime available (LLM proxy or local agent runtime)

###### Agent Notes
- - [ ] Governance agent must receive only metadata and aggregated statistics (no raw rows). Use the PRD Stage 0 prompt & output schema (governance_manifest). Enforce deterministic JSON outputs and validate them against the schema; any schema validation error should create a dq_gate error event and halt job. Propagate trace_id and job_id through all agent calls. Use idempotent writes to S3 and manifest versioning rules: major bump on schema change, minor bump on profile-only change.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-004-03: - [ ] As a security engineer, I want connector credentials stored, retrieved, rotated, and audited via a secrets manager and accessed only by a connector proxy, so that credentials are never leaked, are auditable, and comply with enterprise security requirements.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Integration registration request that contains credentials stores them only in the designated secrets manager and only stores a secrets_ref in Postgres.
- [ ] - [ ] Secrets retrieval for provisioning happens through a short-lived temporary credential exchange to the connector proxy; application servers do not retain plaintext credentials.
- [ ] - [ ] Audit records are written whenever secrets are created, accessed, rotated, or deleted showing actor_id, timestamp, integration_id, and action (exclude secret contents).
- [ ] - [ ] Secrets can be rotated via API/CLI and rotation updates are picked up by next provisioning run without code changes.
- [ ] - [ ] Security automated scans and unit tests verify that credentials do not appear in logs, traces, or governance_manifest.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-004-13: - [ ] Integrate with chosen secrets manager (security/devops)
    - [ ] - [ ] Provision Vault (or configure AWS Secrets Manager) with encryption keys and access policies scoped to service accounts
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement server-side code to write secrets into the manager and store the secrets_ref in integrations table
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement IAM roles/policies for worker service accounts to read specific secrets
    - [ ] - [ ] Create CI scripts to populate test secrets in test environment and tear them down
- [ ] TASK-F-004-14: - [ ] Implement secrets access flow and connector proxy token exchange (backend/security)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement short-lived credentials retrieval endpoint for the provisioning worker with strict RBAC and mutual-TLS if available
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure secrets are provided only to the connector proxy process and not to general application code paths
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement automatic deletion of plaintext secrets from memory after usage and memory-safety review
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests and fuzz tests to ensure secrets are not accidentally serialized
- [ ] TASK-F-004-15: - [ ] Implement secret rotation API and process (backend/ops)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add API to trigger rotation: POST /api/v1/integrations/{id}/rotate-secrets that records rotation event and updates secrets_ref
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement rolling rotation strategy for connectors that require downtime (if any), and document per-connector rotation steps
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure next provisioning job picks up rotated secrets automatically
- [ ] TASK-F-004-16: - [ ] Add auditing, monitoring, and security tests (security/testing)
    - [ ] - [ ] Write audit events for secret create/read/rotate/delete with actor_id and integration_id to append-only audit log
    - [ ] - [ ] Add automated tests confirming secrets are not in logs or traces (scan logs in CI for secret patterns)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add SAST/DAST checklist and run a pen-test focused on secrets handling for the new endpoints
- [ ] TASK-F-004-17: - [ ] Documentation and runbook for ops and admins (docs/devops)
    - [ ] - [ ] Document how to register connectors with and without credentials (secrets_ref usage), and how rotation works
    - [ ] - [ ] Provide runbook for recovery if secret rotate fails or if connector secret is compromised
    - [ ] - [ ] Add troubleshooting steps for permissions errors seen during provisioning

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit test: writing and reading secrets_ref maps to secrets manager without storing plaintext in DB
- [ ] - [ ] Integration test: provisioning worker retrieves secret via secure exchange and can authenticate to test DuckDB/Snowflake instance
- [ ] - [ ] Security test: automated log scan for secret regex patterns after running full create+provision flow
