- [ ] - [ ] Rotation test: trigger rotate-secrets and verify next provisioning job uses new secret and old secret is revoked
- [ ] - [ ] Access control test: ensure non-authorized identities cannot call secrets retrieval endpoint (403)

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Secrets stored in the approved secrets manager and integration records reference secrets_ref only
- [ ] - [ ] Connector proxy obtains secrets via short-lived retrieval only; application logs contain no secret material
- [ ] - [ ] Rotation API functional and covered by tests; rotation events audited
- [ ] - [ ] Security review completed and pen-test issues addressed or documented as accepted risk with mitigation
- [ ] - [ ] Runbook and docs published in internal docs site

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] US-F-004-01
- [ ] - [ ] Vault or AWS Secrets Manager provisioned
- [ ] - [ ] Connector proxy infra provisioned

###### Agent Notes
- - [ ] Secrets must never be passed to any LLM agent. Governance agent inputs must only include schema metadata and aggregates. Ensure secrets_ref naming uses deterministic UUIDs and that secrets manager audit logs are forwarded to central SIEM. Implement telemetry to count secret access events for anomaly detection. Follow PRD rule: 'Credentials are never logged, never included in governance manifests, and never transmitted to LLM agents.'

### Epic: Ingestion

#### Requirement / Feature: Ingestion profiling job framework (sample + column profiling)

**Feature ID:** `F-005`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, data`

**Rationale:** Profiling is required to compute column stats, null rates and support governance manifest generation.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-004

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-005-01: As a data engineer, I want an ingestion profiling job that samples source tables and computes per-column statistics (null rates, distinct counts, histograms, min/max/percentiles, top values) so that a versioned governance_manifest can be generated and downstream stages can rely on accurate column-level metadata.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] The profiler samples up to 10,000 rows per table by default and falls back to full-table if row_count < 10,000.
- [ ] - [ ] For each column the profiler computes: null_pct, distinct_count, min, max, p25, p50, p75, mean, stddev, top_5_values.
- [ ] - [ ] The profiler flags semantic_type (dimension|measure|date|id|text|flag|geo|unknown) using deterministic heuristics + value statistics and returns definition_confidence (high|medium|low).
- [ ] - [ ] The profiler runs a PII scan (regex + token classifier) and marks pii_flags in the manifest for any positive detection.
- [ ] - [ ] The profiler persists a governance_manifest JSON that conforms to the PRD manifest schema and increments semver according to schema/profile changes.
- [ ] - [ ] The profiler emits structured logs and an SSE pipeline event with dq_gate_status PASS|WARN|BLOCK on completion.
- [ ] - [ ] Unit and integration tests cover sampling, stats calculation, PII detection, and manifest write; all tests pass in CI.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-005-01: Implement sampling module (connector-agnostic) with size limits and deterministic reservoir sampling fallback
    - [ ] - [ ] Define sampling interface and config (max_sample_rows default 10000, per_table timeout_ms)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement connector adapters to perform lightweight SELECT ... LIMIT queries for Snowflake and DuckDB (use dialect-aware SQL generator)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add deterministic reservoir sampling algorithm for sources that cannot easily LIMIT large streaming results
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement error handling for timeout, permission, or network errors and map to retryable/non-retryable error codes
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests mocking connector responses including edge cases: empty table, single-row table, huge tables
- [ ] TASK-F-005-02: Implement column profiler computations (null_pct, distinct_count, percentiles, moments, top-k values)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement numeric summary calculations (min, max, mean, stddev, p25/p50/p75) with streaming algorithms to support large samples
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement distinct_count estimation using HyperLogLog where full distinct scan is expensive; wire fallback to exact distinct_count for small samples
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement top_5_values extraction with frequency counts
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement robust null_pct calculation and validation against sample_count
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests to validate calculated stats against known fixtures and randomized property tests
- [ ] TASK-F-005-03: Integrate semantic_type heuristics and natural_language_definition generation
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement deterministic heuristics (name patterns + value patterns) to select semantic_type candidates
    - [ ] - [ ] Generate a short natural_language_definition per column using templates and computed stats (do not call LLM for MVP)
    - [ ] - [ ] Assign definition_confidence rules (high if name+stats align, medium if partial, low otherwise)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests for heuristic coverage across typical column name patterns and value distributions
    - [ ] - [ ] Log rationale for each semantic_type decision in the manifest for auditability
- [ ] TASK-F-005-04: Implement PII detection pipeline (regex set + token classifier integration)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement deterministic regex detectors for email, SSN, phone, credit card, IP, and simple names
    - [ ] - [ ] Integrate a lightweight token-classifier model (local infer or hosted) to detect false positives/negatives and combine results with regex
    - [ ] - [ ] Record pii_flags with pattern_type and confidence in manifest; do not mask values
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit and integration tests with synthetic PII-laden samples and negative controls
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure no raw PII values are logged; only sanitized detection metadata is logged
- [ ] TASK-F-005-05: Persist governance_manifest and implement semver/versioning logic
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement JSON manifest writer that stores manifest in workspace store (Postgres metadata + S3 JSON blob) with manifest_version semver
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement version bump rules: major on schema change (added/removed column), minor on profile change (stats change beyond threshold)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement atomic write and manifest lineage link (integration_id, generated_at, generated_by)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add DB indices for integration_id + manifest_version and add retention policy
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement manifest read endpoint for other services to consume (GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest)
- [ ] TASK-F-005-06: Add logging, observability, and trace propagation for profiling runs
    - [ ] - [ ] Add structured logs for profiler start/step/finish with trace_id and integration_id
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit OpenTelemetry spans for sampling -> profiling -> manifest persist steps
    - [ ] - [ ] Add telemetry metrics: profiling_duration_seconds, tables_profiled_count, manifest_write_success
    - [ ] - [ ] Add alert rule for profiling failures rate > 5% in 1 hour
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit/integration tests verifying trace_id propagation in headers
- [ ] TASK-F-005-07: Testing: unit, integration and performance tests for profiler core
    - [ ] - [ ] Write unit tests for each stats calculator and PII detector
    - [ ] - [ ] Write integration tests that run end-to-end against an in-memory DuckDB instance with realistic fixtures
    - [ ] - [ ] Add performance test that profiles a table with 1M rows using sampling and records P50/P95 durations compared to time targets
    - [ ] - [ ] Add CI job to run profiler tests and fail the build on regressions

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for sampling, statistics, PII detector, and semantic heuristics with > 90% coverage
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests using DuckDB fixture covering end-to-end manifest generation
- [ ] - [ ] Property-based tests for numeric summary functions and reservoir sampling determinism
- [ ] - [ ] Performance test: profile a 1M-row synthetic table and assert P50 < 30s, P95 < 90s for sample-based profile
- [ ] - [ ] Security test: ensure no raw credentials or raw PII values are present in logs or manifest
- [ ] - [ ] Regression test: manifest JSON validates against provided JSON Schema and retains required fields

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Profiler samples and computes all required stats and PII flags for target connectors (Snowflake, DuckDB) per PRD
- [ ] - [ ] governance_manifest JSON persisted and retrievable via API and validated against JSON Schema
- [ ] - [ ] Semver bump rules implemented and manifest version recorded
- [ ] - [ ] Observability (logs, metrics, traces) instrumented and alerts configured
- [ ] - [ ] All unit/integration tests pass in CI and performance targets met for profiling operation P50 < 30s

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-004
- [ ] Secrets Manager (credentials retrieval)
- [ ] Connector adapters (Snowflake, DuckDB) implementation

###### Agent Notes
-  - [ ] Output must be governance_manifest JSON exactly matching PRD schema; include fields: manifest_version, workspace_id, integration_id, generated_at, tables, lineage_edges, dq_gate_status, human_review_required.
 - [ ] Use sample_limit=10000 default; expose config override via job params.
 - [ ] Produce deterministic natural_language_definition strings (no LLM calls for MVP).
 - [ ] Use HyperLogLog for distinct_count estimation when sample_count > 100k; otherwise compute exact from sample.
 - [ ] PII detection must combine regex hits (low-cost) with token-classifier probabilities (higher recall); record pattern_type and confidence between 0.0-1.0.
 - [ ] On any PK uniqueness violation set table-level dq_gate_status BLOCK and include details in manifest.tables[table].dq_reasons.
 - [ ] Return clear error codes for upstream consumers: 1001=connector_error, 1002=auth_error, 1003=profiling_timeout, 1004=validation_error.
 - [ ] Emit SSE event type 'dq_gate' with payload { integration_id, manifest_version, dq_gate_status } on completion.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-005-02: As a platform/operator, I want the profiling job framework to run as a scheduled, retryable job with idempotent semantics and pipeline event streaming so that profiling can be run automatically, retried safely, and UI/agents can observe progress in real time.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Profiling jobs are enqueued to Redis/BullMQ (or equivalent) with job metadata and provenance (integration_id, requested_by, run_type: initial|scheduled|manual).
- [ ] - [ ] Jobs support automatic retries with exponential backoff and idempotency key to prevent duplicate manifest writes.
- [ ] - [ ] Job worker emits SSE events for types: agent_start, dq_gate, agent_complete, error with trace_id and job_id.
- [ ] - [ ] Scheduled nightly runs can be configured per-integration and can be paused/resumed via API.
- [ ] - [ ] Backfill mode supports profiling multiple historical snapshots and writes manifest with explicit backfill metadata.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-005-08: Implement job orchestration layer for profiling jobs (enqueue, schedule, retry)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add job producer API endpoint (POST /api/v1/integrations/{id}/profile/run) that enqueues a profiling job with parameters
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement worker process using BullMQ with concurrency controls and job timeouts
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement idempotency using job_id + manifest_version hint to avoid duplicate writes
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement retry policy: 3 retries, exponential backoff, classify errors as retryable or fatal
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit/integration tests for enqueue/run/retry behavior including simulated transient failures
- [ ] TASK-F-005-09: Emit pipeline streaming events (SSE) for profiling lifecycle
    - [ ] - [ ] Extend existing SSE server to accept profiler job events and include standard event types per PRD (agent_start, dq_gate, agent_complete, error)
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit progress events at key steps: sampling_started, profiling_completed, pii_scan_completed, manifest_persisted
    - [ ] - [ ] Include trace_id, job_id, integration_id, and minimal DQ summary in each event payload
    - [ ] - [ ] Add tests that validate SSE payload schema and ordering under normal and error conditions
    - [ ] - [ ] Add client-side (integration) test that consumes SSE events and asserts correct state transitions
- [ ] TASK-F-005-10: Implement scheduling and backfill control plane
    - [ ] - [ ] Add scheduling config storage for each integration (cron expression + timezone + enabled flag)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement scheduler service that enqueues profiling jobs per schedule and respects pause/resume
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement backfill mode in producer API that accepts date snapshots and enqueues one job per snapshot
    - [ ] - [ ] Add endpoints to list past profiling runs with statuses and artifacts (GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/runs)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add integration tests that run scheduler against a mocked Redis to verify enqueues
- [ ] TASK-F-005-11: Add observability, alerting, and metrics for job queue and workers
    - [ ] - [ ] Export metrics: queue_length, jobs_failed_count, job_retry_count, job_processing_latency_seconds
    - [ ] - [ ] Add dashboard (Grafana) panels for profiling job health and set alert: job_failure_rate > 5% in 1h
    - [ ] - [ ] Add log correlation: job_id and trace_id included in worker logs and manifested in OpenTelemetry traces
    - [ ] - [ ] Add runbook documentation for common profiler failures
- [ ] TASK-F-005-12: Security: ensure worker retrieves credentials securely and never logs secrets
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement Secrets Manager integration (Vault/AWS SM) retrieval by worker using short-lived access tokens
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure credentials are provided to connector adapters in-memory and never persisted to disk or logs
    - [ ] - [ ] Add auditing to record which principal started scheduled/manual profiling runs
    - [ ] - [ ] Add security tests: simulate compromised logs to verify no secrets written
- [ ] TASK-F-005-13: Testing: end-to-end job flow and failure mode testing
    - [ ] - [ ] Create an end-to-end test that enqueues a profiling job and validates manifest persisted and SSE events delivered
    - [ ] - [ ] Create chaos tests that simulate transient DB connectivity failures to validate retry behavior
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate idempotency by running duplicate job requests with same idempotency key and asserting a single manifest write

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Integration test for job enqueue -> worker -> manifest persist pipeline with mocked connectors
- [ ] - [ ] Retry/failure test simulating transient connector errors and asserting retry counts and final status
- [ ] - [ ] SSE consumer test that asserts event types and payload schemas
- [ ] - [ ] Security test verifying secrets are never logged and access to Secrets Manager is audited
- [ ] - [ ] Load test enqueuing 100 concurrent profiling jobs and measuring queue/backlog metrics

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Job orchestration enqueues and processes profiling jobs with retries and idempotency
- [ ] - [ ] SSE events emitted in correct sequence and consumed by a test client
- [ ] - [ ] Scheduler runs respect cron/timezone and pause/resume controls
- [ ] - [ ] Secrets retrieval implemented; no credentials or raw PII appear in logs
- [ ] - [ ] Monitoring dashboards and alerts configured and runbook published

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-004
- [ ] Redis/BullMQ (job queue infra)
- [ ] Secrets Manager
- [ ] SSE server already implemented as per PRD

###### Agent Notes
-  - [ ] Worker must accept job payload: { job_id, integration_id, requested_by, run_type, sample_limit, idempotency_key, run_snapshot (optional) }.
 - [ ] Jobs must write manifest only after full validation; use two-phase commit: write manifest to S3 then update metadata in Postgres within a transaction to ensure idempotency.
 - [ ] Emit SSE events with minimal sensitive data; NEVER include column samples or raw values in SSE payloads.
 - [ ] Classify errors: transient_db (retry), auth_error (fatal), schema_mismatch (fatal, surface to UI). Map to documented error codes.
 - [ ] Scheduler should support manual override to force immediate run for troubleshooting.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-005-03: As a QA lead and downstream agent author, I want profiling outputs validated, schema-enforced, and versioned so that the semantic layer and subsequent agents can consume manifests deterministically and low-confidence items can be surfaced for human review.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Every persisted governance_manifest validates against the JSON Schema; invalid manifests are rejected and job marked ERROR with reason.
- [ ] - [ ] Manifest versioning follows semver rules and a manifest change diff is recorded for each minor/major bump.
- [ ] - [ ] Items with definition_confidence=low set human_review_required: true for that table/column and overall manifest human_review_required flag is set accordingly.
- [ ] - [ ] Consumers (semantic layer builder) can fetch specific manifest_version and receive deterministic content.
- [ ] - [ ] Error handling returns clear remediation messages for failures (e.g., PK uniqueness violation blocking semantic layer).

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-005-14: Implement JSON Schema validation and manifest acceptance pipeline
    - [ ] - [ ] Author canonical JSON Schema for governance_manifest based on PRD table schema (strict required fields and types)
    - [ ] - [ ] Integrate schema validator into the manifest writer; reject invalid manifests with descriptive errors
    - [ ] - [ ] Add automated diff/compare step that computes whether change is schema-only or profile-only to determine semver bump type
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests that assert acceptance/rejection for malformed manifests and correct semver bump decisions
- [ ] TASK-F-005-15: Implement human_review flagging and review queue integration
    - [ ] - [ ] When any column/table has definition_confidence=low, set manifest.human_review_required=true and record review reasons
    - [ ] - [ ] Persist review entries in ReviewQueue table with pointer to manifest_version and affected columns
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement minimal API endpoint to list review queue items (GET /api/v1/review-queue) for workspace admins
    - [ ] - [ ] Add tests that create low-confidence items and assert they appear in the review queue
- [ ] TASK-F-005-16: Implement manifest diff storage and lineage metadata
    - [ ] - [ ] Compute and store manifest_diffs (changed_tables, changed_columns, change_type) on each persist
    - [ ] - [ ] Expose API to retrieve manifest history and diff for an integration (GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifests)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add indices and retention policy for manifest history
    - [ ] - [ ] Add tests that assert diffs are correctly classified and retrievable
- [ ] TASK-F-005-17: Frontend: expose manifest status and review indicators in minimal admin UI (MVP)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement a simple UI endpoint that lists latest manifest per integration with health_score and human_review badge
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement a manifest details view that displays table health scores and a link to raw JSON (read-only)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add frontend validation to ensure only authorized workspace admins can access review queue endpoints
    - [ ] - [ ] Add e2e tests that validate UI shows review items for low-confidence manifests
- [ ] TASK-F-005-18: Testing: validation, contract and consumer integration tests
    - [ ] - [ ] Contract test between profiler manifest output and semantic layer input that verifies required fields present
    - [ ] - [ ] Negative tests: attempt to ingest manifest with missing required fields and assert rejection and clear error message
    - [ ] - [ ] E2E test: run profiler, generate manifest with low-confidence items, ensure ReviewQueue receives items and API surfaces them

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Schema validation unit tests including all required/optional permutations
- [ ] - [ ] Contract tests with semantic layer builder to ensure deterministic manifest consumption
- [ ] - [ ] Security tests verifying only admins can access review endpoints and manifest raw JSON
- [ ] - [ ] E2E QA test to simulate low-confidence manifest and ensure UI presents review item and blocks ML stages until review completed
- [ ] - [ ] Regression tests to ensure semver bump logic remains stable

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Manifest JSON Schema enforced and validator integrated into pipeline
- [ ] - [ ] Semver bumping implemented with stored diffs for audit
- [ ] - [ ] Human review queue receives low-confidence items and is queryable via API
- [ ] - [ ] Minimal admin UI surfaces manifest status and review badge
- [ ] - [ ] Consumer contract tests pass for semantic layer integration

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-004
- [ ] Semantic layer service (consumer contract)
- [ ] Workspace admin RBAC (for review queue access)

###### Agent Notes
-  - [ ] Use strict JSON Schema; fields marked required in PRD must be present or manifest write fails.
 - [ ] Semver bump rule: detect schema delta (added/removed column or type change) => major++; else if profile stats changed beyond thresholds (null_pct delta > 1% or top_values change) => minor++; otherwise patch++.
 - [ ] When human_review_required=true, set dq_gate_status=BLOCK for any downstream ML consumption attempts and return human_required SSE event with payload describing columns needing review.
 - [ ] Ensure manifest API supports versioned retrieval: /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest?version=1.2.0.
 - [ ] Provide clear remediation messages: e.g., 'PK uniqueness violation on table X: 3 duplicate rows detected; resolve PK or deduplicate source.'

## Sprint 2 - Governance Agent & UI

**Sprint Goal:** Implement governance agent pipeline, manifest schema, DQ checks, and integration detail UI for review.

### Sprint Completion Checklist
- [ ] All P0 stories complete
- [ ] All blocking defects resolved
- [ ] Tests passing
- [ ] Demo-ready increment produced
- [ ] Milestone accepted

### Epic: Governance

#### Requirement / Feature: Governance agent pipeline scaffold (catalog → profile → define → manifest)

**Feature ID:** `F-006`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, agent`

**Rationale:** Stage 0 governance manifest is the canonical input for downstream stages and must exist in MVP.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-005

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-006-01: As a workspace admin / data engineer, I want an automated governance agent that catalogs schemas, profiles tables, generates human-readable definitions, computes table health scores and PII flags, and emits a versioned governance_manifest JSON, so that downstream pipeline stages receive a canonical, auditable, and immutable manifest for safe ML and analytics use.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] The governance agent produces governance_manifest JSON matching the PRD schema (manifest_version semver, workspace_id, integration_id, generated_at, tables, lineage_edges, dq_gate_status, human_review_required).
- [ ] - [ ] Catalog step enumerates all accessible schemas/tables and emits per-column semantic_type, nullable, and estimated_cardinality for every column observed.
- [ ] - [ ] Profile step samples up to 10,000 rows per table and computes null_pct, distinct_count, min/max/p25/p50/p75/mean/stddev and top_5_values for each column.
- [ ] - [ ] Define step outputs natural_language_definition and definition_confidence (high|medium|low) per table and column, with low-confidence flagged for human review; no hallucinated column names or inferred data beyond observed samples.
- [ ] - [ ] Health scoring algorithm implemented and applied per table with penalties per PRD rules and produced health_score 0–100.
- [ ] - [ ] PII scan applies regex + token classifier and flags columns with pattern_type without exposing or sending raw row values to LLMs.
- [ ] - [ ] Manifest versioning follows semver rules: minor increment on profile changes, major on schema changes; manifest write is atomic and immutable (new version for updates).
- [ ] - [ ] All manifest generation errors emit structured logs and pipeline-level telemetry with trace_id and produce a dq_gate STATUS of WARN|BLOCK|PASS as appropriate.
- [ ] - [ ] Performance targets: per-table manifest generation P50 < 30s, P95 < 90s for catalog+profile+define steps on typical tables as per PRD.
- [ ] - [ ] Governance agent never sends raw data rows to LLMs; only schema, aggregated statistics, and value samples (no PII values) are used for prompts.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-006-01: Design and freeze governance_manifest JSON schema and validation rules
    - [ ] - [ ] Collect required fields from PRD and produce canonical JSON Schema draft for governance_manifest
    - [ ] - [ ] Define semver increment rules (major/minor/patch) and edge cases (concurrent writes)
    - [ ] - [ ] Define dq_gate_status enum mapping (PASS|WARN|BLOCK) and human_review_required boolean logic
    - [ ] - [ ] Create unit tests for JSON Schema validation and sample manifests
    - [ ] - [ ] Peer review schema with data and product leads and finalize
- [ ] TASK-F-006-02: Implement cataloging module (schema & table introspection)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement connector-agnostic introspection APIs using existing warehouse interface (F-005 dependency) to list schemas/tables and column metadata
    - [ ] - [ ] Add dialect-aware estimations for estimated_cardinality using EXPLAIN or COUNT DISTINCT heuristics when supported; fallback to reservoir sampling for large tables
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement permission error handling and surface missing privileges as warnings in manifest and logs
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests and integration tests using mocked connectors and a local DuckDB dev fixture
- [ ] TASK-F-006-03: Implement profiling module (sampling + statistics)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement safe sampling logic: sample up to 10,000 rows per table with deterministic seed and timeout handling
    - [ ] - [ ] Compute per-column stats (null_pct, distinct_count, min,max,p25,p50,p75,mean,stddev) with numeric, date, and text handling
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement top_5_values extraction with frequency counts and capped memory usage
    - [ ] - [ ] Add error and timeout instrumentation; if sampling times out, mark table as WARN and include partial stats
    - [ ] - [ ] Write unit and integration tests with synthetic datasets covering edge cases (all-null column, high-cardinality, extreme skew)
- [ ] TASK-F-006-04: Implement define step (natural language definitions via agent) and confidence scoring
    - [ ] - [ ] Create system prompt per PRD that instructs agent to only use provided aggregated stats and sample value summaries; forbid raw data or hallucination
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement agent wrapper that supplies typed JSON input (catalog + profile) and enforces JSON Schema output using tool-use API
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate and sanitize agent outputs; run deterministic rules to downgrade confidence if samples are ambiguous
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement retries with backoff for transient LLM errors and fallback to deterministic definition templates when agent repeatedly fails
    - [ ] - [ ] Build automated tests that assert no raw data leaks in prompt or logs
- [ ] TASK-F-006-05: Implement PII scanning (regex + token classifier) and flagging
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement deterministic regex detectors for email, SSN, phone, credit_card, IP, name patterns as per PRD
    - [ ] - [ ] Integrate fine-tuned token classifier model for ambiguous cases, run on sampled values only; ensure classifier runs in safe environment with no logging of raw values
    - [ ] - [ ] Aggregate and emit pii_flags array in manifest with column and pattern_type; mark columns as blocked for ML until admin approval
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests and privacy review; document false-positive mitigation steps for admins
- [ ] TASK-F-006-06: Implement manifest writer, versioning store, and immutability guarantees
    - [ ] - [ ] Choose storage backing (Postgres + object store) and implement manifest write API with transactional guarantees
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement semver increment logic and conflict resolution for concurrent manifest writes
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement manifest retrieval API to return latest and historical versions
    - [ ] - [ ] Add audit log entry for every manifest write with actor=system, trace_id, input_hash, output_hash
- [ ] TASK-F-006-07: Logging, observability, and metrics for governance agent
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit structured logs for each pipeline step with trace_id, timings, error codes, and dq_gate outcomes
    - [ ] - [ ] Add OpenTelemetry spans for catalog/profile/define/write phases and export to Jaeger/Honeycomb
    - [ ] - [ ] Create dashboard widget with per-workspace manifest generation latency and failure rates
    - [ ] - [ ] Add alert rule for manifest generation failures > 3x in 1 hour
- [ ] TASK-F-006-08: Testing and QA for governance agent
    - [ ] - [ ] Write unit tests for catalog/profile/define/PII modules with edge cases
    - [ ] - [ ] Create integration test that runs end-to-end against local DuckDB and a mocked LLM agent
    - [ ] - [ ] Perform load test on manifest writer with 100 small tables and 10 large tables and validate latency targets
    - [ ] - [ ] Perform security review to ensure no raw data or credentials are logged or sent to LLM

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for JSON Schema validation, semver logic, and PII regexes
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests: end-to-end manifest generation using a DuckDB dev dataset and a mocked LLM response
- [ ] - [ ] Contract tests: verify manifest output conforms to PRD schema across multiple workspace/integration permutations
- [ ] - [ ] Security tests: static analysis, secrets leak detection, and confirm no PII raw values in logs or prompts
- [ ] - [ ] Performance/load tests: per-table generation latency P50/P95 against representative datasets
- [ ] - [ ] Failure mode tests: simulate connector permission errors, sampling timeouts, LLM errors and assert correct dq_gate_status and remediation messaging

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] All acceptance criteria above are met and automated unit/integration tests pass in CI
- [ ] - [ ] Governance agent code merged, containers built, and deployed to staging environment
- [ ] - [ ] Manifest JSON Schema validated in CI and sample manifests stored in artifact repo
- [ ] - [ ] OpenTelemetry traces and logs visible in staging dashboards and alerts configured
- [ ] - [ ] Security review completed: no raw data in prompts or logs; secrets usage via secrets manager confirmed
- [ ] - [ ] Performance gates verified against PRD latency targets in staging

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] F-005 (warehouse connector foundation and connector interface)

###### Agent Notes
- Use claude-sonnet-4-6 (per PRD) for define step. Strictly enforce: prompt only contains column-level aggregated stats and top_values (masked for potential PII). Validate agent JSON output against schema; treat any validation failure as a manifest generation error and retry up to 2 times. Always log prompt/response metadata (model, tokens, latency) but never raw sampled rows or sensitive values. Implement deterministic fallback definitions if agent is unavailable.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-006-02: As a workspace analyst or admin, I want to retrieve the governance_manifest via API and review low-confidence definitions in a UI review queue so that I can accept, edit, or reject definitions before downstream ML stages can use them.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] API endpoint GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest returns the latest manifest and supports query params ?version={semver} and pagination for tables.
- [ ] - [ ] API enforces workspace-level ACLs: workspace members can read, only admins can approve/reject PII-blocked columns for ML use.
- [ ] - [ ] Frontend review UI shows per-table health_score, PII flags, low-confidence definitions marked with Review badge, and allows Accept/Edit/Reject actions.
- [ ] - [ ] Edit action updates the manifest by creating a new semver-minor version and appends an audit log entry with user_id and changeset; Accept marks definition_confidence=high and is recorded in audit log.
- [ ] - [ ] Reject action hides the definition from ML explores (sets human_review_required true) and records reason in audit trail.
- [ ] - [ ] UI and API sanitize and validate admin edits; invalid edits are rejected with clear error messages.
- [ ] - [ ] Manifest retrieval and review operations are covered by automated tests and access is logged for audit.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-006-09: Implement API endpoints for manifest retrieval and review actions
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest with optional ?version param and secure access checks
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement POST /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest/review to accept/edit/reject definitions with payload validation
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement PATCH semantics to create new manifest versions atomically on Accept/Edit actions
    - [ ] - [ ] Add rate-limiting and RBAC checks (admins only for approve/reject; members read-only)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit and integration tests for API endpoints including permission scenarios
- [ ] TASK-F-006-10: Design and implement frontend Review Queue UI
    - [ ] - [ ] Create Review Queue screen showing list of tables with health_score and review-badges
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement table-level drill-in showing column profiles, definition text, confidence, and sample top_values (masked for PII)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement Accept / Edit / Reject UI flows with client-side validation and preview of resulting manifest diff
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement optimistic UI and handle server-side validation errors gracefully with inline messages
    - [ ] - [ ] Add accessibility attributes and keyboard flows; include unit and e2e tests (Cypress or Playwright)
- [ ] TASK-F-006-11: Implement manifest edit validation, sanitization, and storage
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate admin edits against governance_manifest JSON Schema before persisting
    - [ ] - [ ] Sanitize free-text definitions to prevent XSS in UI (store safe HTML or plain text only)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement atomic manifest update that computes new semver and writes to manifest store with audit metadata
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement rollback endpoint for last-manifest version and unit tests for rollback behavior
- [ ] TASK-F-006-12: Implement audit logging and change diff visualization
    - [ ] - [ ] Append audit log entries for all review actions with actor, timestamp, before/after diffs, and trace_id
    - [ ] - [ ] Expose audit entries in the UI with ability to view diff inline
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure audit log is append-only and accessible via API for compliance
- [ ] TASK-F-006-13: Testing and QA for manifest review UI and API
    - [ ] - [ ] Write API contract tests and e2e tests that simulate Accept/Edit/Reject flows including permission violations
    - [ ] - [ ] Perform usability testing with 2–3 data engineers to validate review UX and error messages
    - [ ] - [ ] Add security tests to confirm no raw PII values are shown in UI and XSS is prevented
    - [ ] - [ ] Load test review endpoints for concurrent reviewers to ensure no semver conflicts
- [ ] TASK-F-006-14: DevOps: Deploy review service and enable feature flag
    - [ ] - [ ] Add CI pipeline job to build and deploy frontend review UI to staging behind a feature flag
    - [ ] - [ ] Configure backend manifest review API deployment and DB migrations for audit log
    - [ ] - [ ] Add smoke tests in deployment pipeline that validate manifest read/write roundtrip
    - [ ] - [ ] Document rollback steps for manifest storage migrations

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] API unit tests for GET/PATCH/POST endpoints and permission scenarios
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests for manifest version creation and retrieval including semver increments
- [ ] - [ ] E2E UI tests for review flows (Accept/Edit/Reject) and error handling using Playwright/Cypress
- [ ] - [ ] Security tests: XSS, CSRF, auth bypass, and ensure no raw PII in UI or logs
- [ ] - [ ] Concurrency tests: simulate 5 concurrent editors to validate semver conflict handling and UI optimism

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] API endpoints implemented, documented, and protected by RBAC; integration tests passing in CI
- [ ] - [ ] Frontend Review Queue merged, accessible behind feature flag, with e2e tests and accessibility checks
- [ ] - [ ] Manifest edits produce new immutable manifest versions and audit log entries
- [ ] - [ ] Security review completed: no PII displayed, XSS mitigations in place, sensitive data never logged
- [ ] - [ ] Manual acceptance: product demo to data engineering reviewer and sign-off recorded

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] US-F-006-01 (governance manifest generation)
- [ ] - [ ] F-005 (connector foundation for sample previews)

###### Agent Notes
- When surfacing top_values in UI, mask values if PII flag present. The UI must never show raw sensitive values; show obfuscated sample (e.g., '****@example.com' or 'PHONE-REDACTED'). All review edits must pass backend JSON Schema validation. Use the same trace_id across frontend action and backend write for observability.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-006-03: As the pipeline orchestrator and platform operator, I want deterministic data quality gates enforced against the governance_manifest (PK uniqueness, null rate for key columns, row_count minimum, freshness SLA, PII blocking) and integrated with the agent orchestration so that pipeline advancement is blocked or paused with human_required SSE events when critical failures occur.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Implement deterministic DQ rule engine that evaluates manifest and computed statistics and returns per-table gate results (PASS|WARN|BLOCK) in the manifest.dq_gate_status and emits a detailed dq_gate event to SSE stream.
- [ ] - [ ] Critical rules (PK uniqueness violation, null rate > threshold on id/date columns, PII detection) block downstream Stage 1 semantic layer creation and set human_review_required=true.
- [ ] - [ ] Non-critical rules (freshness SLA exceedance, row_count warnings) produce WARN and do not silently block but are logged and surfaced in the UI.
- [ ] - [ ] Orchestrator consumes dq_gate results and emits SSE events of type dq_gate and human_required with remediation payloads (recommended fix, offending table/column, trace_id).
- [ ] - [ ] Max automated repair attempts and clear escalation path implemented: when DQ gate is BLOCK, pipeline halts and returns actionable remediation; when WARN, pipeline proceeds with note in manifest.
- [ ] - [ ] All DQ evaluations are logged and auditable; a copy is stored with the manifest version for reproducibility.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-006-15: Design and implement deterministic DQ rule engine
    - [ ] - [ ] Translate PRD DQ rules into executable rule definitions with parameters and severity mapping
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement engine that accepts governance_manifest and returns per-table and per-column gate results and remediation suggestions
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement configurable thresholds (workspace-level overrides) and default PRD values
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests covering PASS/WARN/BLOCK outcomes for each rule
- [ ] TASK-F-006-16: Integrate DQ gate evaluation into manifest generator and orchestrator
    - [ ] - [ ] After manifest creation, run DQ engine and update manifest.dq_gate_status and human_review_required flags before persisting
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure orchestration layer reads updated manifest and halts/continues per gate outcomes
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit SSE events with type:dq_gate containing structured details and remediation suggestions
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement retry logic for transient DQ evaluation errors and traceable failure reasons
- [ ] TASK-F-006-17: Implement remediation suggestions and human_required messaging
    - [ ] - [ ] For each BLOCK rule, implement a recommended remediation step (e.g., 'add unique PK', 'reduce nulls', 'approve PII for ML use')
    - [ ] - [ ] Construct human_required SSE payloads that include exact offending join/column and suggested SQL or UI steps
    - [ ] - [ ] Wire human_required events to Review Queue UI so users can act on the exact item from the UI
    - [ ] - [ ] Add tests that simulate BLOCK outcomes and assert UI receives actionable SSE payload
- [ ] TASK-F-006-18: Security and governance: ensure PII rules block ML use until admin overrides
    - [ ] - [ ] When PII detected, mark column as blocked_for_ml and ensure downstream feature engineering and model training pipeline checks this flag and refuses usage
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement admin override flow that records explicit approval and creates a new manifest version with approval audit record
    - [ ] - [ ] Add policy enforcement tests to ensure PII columns are never included in SHAP tables or exported artifacts
- [ ] TASK-F-006-19: Observability, SSE events, and alerting for DQ gates
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement structured SSE events: type=dq_gate with details, type=human_required when pipeline halts
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure SSE stream includes trace_id and link to manifest version and remediation suggestions
    - [ ] - [ ] Add alert rules for repeated BLOCK events and high failure rates, surface via email/Slack to workspace admins
    - [ ] - [ ] Add CI tests to verify correct SSE payload shape and required fields per PRD streaming protocol
- [ ] TASK-F-006-20: Testing & QA for DQ enforcement
    - [ ] - [ ] Create integration tests that run manifest -> DQ engine -> orchestrator and assert correct pipeline halt/continue behavior
    - [ ] - [ ] Simulate edge cases: borderline null_pct values, intermittent freshness lag, intermittent PK violation then repaired
    - [ ] - [ ] Security tests to confirm PII-blocking protections and admin override audit trail
    - [ ] - [ ] Run fault-injection tests to validate orchestrator handles DQ engine timeouts cleanly

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for each DQ rule mapping PASS/WARN/BLOCK against synthetic manifests
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests for end-to-end: manifest -> DQ engine -> orchestrator decisions and SSE events
- [ ] - [ ] E2E tests that exercise admin override and verify resulting manifest version and downstream pipeline behavior
- [ ] - [ ] Load tests for DQ engine when evaluating manifests with 100+ tables concurrently
- [ ] - [ ] Security tests ensuring PII columns are blocked from ML and cannot be used until explicit admin approval

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] DQ rule engine implemented, integrated with manifest generation, and prevents downstream progression on BLOCK rules
- [ ] - [ ] SSE dq_gate and human_required events emitted with required payloads and visible in staging streams
- [ ] - [ ] Admin override flow implemented with audit trail and manifest versioning
- [ ] - [ ] Automated tests for DQ engine and orchestration integration pass in CI
- [ ] - [ ] Alerts and dashboards for DQ gate failures configured in staging

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] US-F-006-01 (manifest generation)
- [ ] - [ ] F-005 (connector foundation for PK checks and sampling)

###### Agent Notes
- DQ engine must be deterministic and independent of LLM outputs. For any rule that uses statistical thresholds (e.g., null_pct, freshness), compute values from the manifest's aggregated stats; do not re-sample source tables in the DQ engine unless explicitly configured. SSE event payloads must include remediation suggestions using deterministic rule-to-remediation mapping; do not include raw sample values in SSE payloads. Maintain one trace_id per pipeline run propagated to all events.

#### Requirement / Feature: Governance manifest schema (versioned semver JSON)

**Feature ID:** `F-007`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, data`

**Rationale:** Manifest schema defines the contract between Stage 0 and all downstream agents.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-006

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-007-01: As a governance agent engineer, I want the system to generate a versioned governance_manifest JSON (semver) for each integration run, so that downstream agents and services receive a deterministic, validated contract describing tables, column profiles, health scores, PII flags, lineage, and DQ gate status.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The governance agent produces a governance_manifest JSON matching the required manifest schema for every completed integration run
- [ ] The manifest JSON is semver-versioned and stored immutably (append-only) with created_at and manifest_version fields
- [ ] The generation process enforces the Stage 0 steps in order: CATALOG → PROFILE → DEFINE → HEALTH_SCORE → PII_SCAN → OUTPUT, and rejects incomplete outputs
- [ ] The manifest generation fails with a structured error when any deterministic validator (e.g., sampling, profile computation) fails and logs remediation guidance
- [ ] The manifest is persisted to durable storage and an audit log entry is written with trace_id, integration_id, manifest_version, and dq_gate_status
- [ ] A GET API returns the latest manifest and supports fetching by specific version ID

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-007-01: Implement governance agent generation pipeline (backend / AI orchestration)
    - [ ] Implement orchestration step runner that executes CATALOG, PROFILE, DEFINE, HEALTH_SCORE, and PII_SCAN in strict sequence
    - [ ] Integrate existing connector interface (dependency F-006) to introspect schemas and sample rows (respect sample limits)
    - [ ] Compute per-column statistics (null_pct, distinct_count, min/max/p25/p50/p75/mean/stddev) using deterministic SQL queries or sampling strategy
    - [ ] Generate natural_language_definition and definition_confidence for each table and column using validated prompt template
    - [ ] Ensure governance agent outputs only structured JSON and validate intermediate outputs against JSON Schema before proceeding to next step
    - [ ] Add retry-with-backoff for transient warehouse query errors and surface deterministic error messages for non-retryable failures
- [ ] TASK-F-007-02: Design and implement governance_manifest JSON Schema and validators
    - [ ] Write a comprehensive JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12) that enforces required fields, types, enums, and nested shapes based on the PRD schema
    - [ ] Implement runtime validator using a proven JSON Schema library and reject manifests failing schema validation with clear error payloads
    - [ ] Add automated tests that validate produced manifests against the JSON Schema using multiple synthetic integration samples
    - [ ] Include explicit checks that manifest_version is valid semver and generated_at is ISO8601
- [ ] TASK-F-007-03: Persist manifests immutably and add audit logging (data / devops / security)
    - [ ] Create DB migration to add manifest_versions table (manifest_id, integration_id, manifest_version, storage_uri, generated_at, dq_gate_status, created_by, trace_id)
    - [ ] Store the manifest JSON in object storage (S3-compatible) and save URI in the manifest_versions table; enable server-side encryption
    - [ ] Write an append-only audit log entry (Postgres or append-only event store) for each manifest generation with trace_id and stage outcomes
    - [ ] Ensure access control: manifest retrieval API enforces workspace membership and never returns credentials or raw sampled rows
- [ ] TASK-F-007-04: Expose API endpoints to fetch manifest and list versions (backend / api)
    - [ ] Implement GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest to return latest manifest metadata and a signed URL or proxied JSON payload
    - [ ] Implement GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest?version={semver} to return a specific manifest version
    - [ ] Add pagination/listing endpoint: GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifests to return manifest history with filtering by dq_gate_status and date range
    - [ ] Add OpenAPI docs for new endpoints and include example responses
- [ ] TASK-F-007-05: Implement observability, telemetry, and structured error handling for manifest generation
    - [ ] Emit structured metrics: generation_duration_seconds, manifest_size_bytes, dq_gate_pass_count, dq_gate_warn_count, dq_gate_block_count
    - [ ] Log structured traces with trace_id propagated from orchestration runtime to warehouse queries and object storage writes
    - [ ] Capture error classification (transient / deterministic / validation) and surface remediation text in logs and API error responses
    - [ ] Add alerting rule for manifest generation failure rate > 5% over 30m to on-call channel
- [ ] TASK-F-007-06: Add unit and integration tests for governance agent logic (testing)
    - [ ] Write unit tests for each orchestration step (catalog, profile, define, health_score, pii_scan) using mocked connector responses
    - [ ] Create integration tests that run the full pipeline against a test Snowflake/DuckDB instance seeded with representative tables (dependency F-006)
    - [ ] Add JSON Schema conformance tests that verify generated manifests across edge cases: empty tables, high null_pct, PII present, many-to-many lineage
    - [ ] Add CI step to run manifest tests and fail on schema drift or changed expected outputs

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for each orchestration step with edge case mocks
- [ ] Integration test: full pipeline run against a seeded test warehouse; assert manifest JSON conformance and semver generation
- [ ] Schema validation test: feed malformed intermediate outputs and assert pipeline blocks with clear errors
- [ ] DQ gate tests: assert dq_gate_status equals PASS|WARN|BLOCK under predefined table conditions (e.g., PK uniqueness violation => BLOCK)
- [ ] Security test: scan manifests for accidental sensitive data, assert no credentials or PII sample rows embedded
- [ ] Performance test: measure manifest generation P50/P95 against PRD targets and add a regression guard in CI

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Governance agent produces manifest JSON matching approved JSON Schema for all tested integrations
- [ ] Manifests are stored immutably with semver and accessible via API endpoints
- [ ] All unit and integration tests pass in CI and manifest schema validation tests included
- [ ] Observability metrics and alerts are in place and trace propagation works end-to-end
- [ ] Security review confirms no credentials or raw row data are stored in manifests and manifests are encrypted at rest
- [ ] Code reviewed, merged, and deployed to staging environment with demo integration run

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-006
- [ ] Connector interfaces for target warehouses (Snowflake/DuckDB test instance)
- [ ] Object storage and DB migration framework

###### Agent Notes
- The governance agent must strictly follow the Stage 0 prompt sequence: CATALOG > PROFILE > DEFINE > HEALTH_SCORE > PII_SCAN > OUTPUT. Always emit only structured JSON (no free text). Do not include or log database credentials or sampled raw rows. Use deterministic queries and fixed sample sizes (10k rows or table size). Apply JSON Schema validation between steps and fail fast on structural mismatch. Semver rules: increment minor on profile-only changes (statistics change), increment major when schema changes (table/column add/remove or column type change). Write manifests as immutable artifacts: never update an existing manifest_version row; create a new version and write audit log. Include dq_gate_status and human_review_required boolean in output. Ensure telemetry (generation time, size, dq outcomes) and propagate trace_id to all emitted logs.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-007-02: As a data platform operator, I want deterministic semver rules and immutable manifest versioning with ability to list, retrieve, and roll back manifests, so that governance changes are auditable and downstream agents always consume a clear versioned contract.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every manifest write is append-only and assigned a unique manifest_version following semver rules
- [ ] System automatically decides minor vs major increment based on deterministic diff between new and previous manifest (schema vs profile changes) and records rationale in audit log
- [ ] APIs exist to list manifest versions, fetch specific versions, and mark an active manifest for an integration
- [ ] A rollback operation creates a new manifest version that re-publishes a prior manifest content (preserving immutability) and records rollback reason and actor
- [ ] Access to rollback and version management is restricted to workspace admins via ABAC and audited

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-007-07: Implement semver decision engine and deterministic manifest diffing (backend / data)
    - [ ] Implement deterministic diff algorithm that compares prior and new manifest JSONs and detects schema changes (tables added/removed, columns added/removed, type changes) vs profile changes (stats, null_pct, row_count)
    - [ ] Implement semver bump rules: major for schema changes, minor for profile changes, patch for non-functional metadata changes; record change_reason in audit log
    - [ ] Add unit tests covering diff scenarios that expect correct semver increments
    - [ ] Document edge cases (e.g., column renamed equivalence heuristic) and fallback to conservative major bump when ambiguous
- [ ] TASK-F-007-08: Implement manifest version listing, retrieval, and 'active' pointer (backend / api)
    - [ ] Add API GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifests (list) with filtering by dq_gate_status, version range, date
    - [ ] Add API GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifests/{version} (retrieve exact version)
    - [ ] Implement PUT /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifests/{version}/activate to mark a version as active (writes a new audit event and sets active pointer)
    - [ ] Ensure all endpoints enforce workspace-level ABAC and return explicit 403 on unauthorized access
- [ ] TASK-F-007-09: Implement rollback flow (admin only) that preserves immutability
    - [ ] Implement POST /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifests/{version}/rollback that creates a new manifest_version record copying content from target version and appends a rollback_reason and actor metadata
    - [ ] Write audit log entries for rollback including original_version, new_version, actor_id, and provided justification
    - [ ] Add safety checks: disallow rollback if target version dq_gate_status == BLOCK unless an override flag with documented reason is provided
    - [ ] Add unit and integration tests for rollback happy path and forbidden cases
- [ ] TASK-F-007-10: Enforce immutability and retention / lifecycle policy (devops / data)
    - [ ] Add DB constraints and application-level checks to prevent updating existing manifest_version rows; enforce INSERT-only semantics
    - [ ] Implement retention policy config (default keep 90 versions) with a background job that archives older manifests to cold storage and logs archival events
    - [ ] Ensure archived manifests remain retrievable via a proxied read and mark archived flag in metadata
    - [ ] Add migration and rollback tests for retention job
- [ ] TASK-F-007-11: Add RBAC and audit controls around version management (security / backend)
    - [ ] Integrate ABAC checks so only workspace admins can activate or rollback manifests
    - [ ] Record actor_id and IP address for all version management actions in audit log
    - [ ] Add a read-only `manifest_access` role for downstream services that need to fetch manifests without admin rights
    - [ ] Add automated tests asserting unauthorized users receive 403 for protected endpoints

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for diff engine covering schema add/remove/rename and profile delta cases
- [ ] Integration tests for list/retrieve/rollback flows including audit log assertions
- [ ] Security tests for ABAC enforcement: attempt protected actions with non-admin account and assert 403
- [ ] Resilience test: simulate concurrent manifest writes and assert semver monotonicity and no lost-writes
- [ ] Manual QA: operator performs rollback in staging and verifies new manifest is created and active pointer updated

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Semver diff engine correctly classifies schema vs profile changes in unit/integration tests
- [ ] Manifest list/retrieve/activate/rollback endpoints implemented and covered by tests
- [ ] All version writes are append-only and protected by DB constraints and ABAC rules
- [ ] Retention and archival job runs in staging and archives older manifests to cold storage without data loss
- [ ] Admin workflows audited and RBAC validated; security review passed

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] TASK-F-007-01 (manifest generation pipeline)
- [ ] Persistent storage for manifests (object store + DB)
- [ ] Workspace RBAC service

###### Agent Notes
- Implement deterministic JSON diffing that flags schema-level changes separately from profile-only deltas. When ambiguous (e.g., column rename), prefer conservative major bump and record the ambiguous diff detail. Always create a new manifest_version record for rollbacks (never mutate prior rows). Propagate trace_id to audit logs for correlation. Ensure APIs are idempotent where appropriate and fail with clear error codes and remediation guidance.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-007-03: As an analyst or governance reviewer, I want a simple UI to inspect manifest versions, see table health scores, low-confidence definitions, PII flags, and trigger human review actions, so that I can accept/edit/reject low-confidence items and ensure downstream ML stages are blocked until required reviews are completed.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A manifest viewer UI shows the latest manifest summary for an integration including workspace_id, integration_id, manifest_version, generated_at, dq_gate_status, and human_review_required
- [ ] UI lists tables with health_score, row_count, freshness_lag_hrs, and definition_confidence and highlights low-confidence items with a Review badge
- [ ] Reviewer actions exist: Accept / Edit / Reject for a table or column definition; Accept marks definition_confidence=high for the UI and writes a review record; Edit launches an inline editor to change natural_language_definition and stores the edit as a review event (does not mutate original manifest)
- [ ] Human review actions are recorded as separate review records linked to manifest_version and do not mutate the immutable manifest artifact
- [ ] A manual re-run trigger for the governance agent is available to re-generate manifest after corrections; re-run respects semver rules

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-007-12: Design manifest viewer UI and review UX (frontend / design)
    - [ ] Create wireframe for manifest summary page showing version selector, table list with health badges, and quick filters (low-confidence, pii_flags, dq_gate_status)
    - [ ] Define UI components: manifest header, table row card, column detail drawer, review action modal
    - [ ] Design inline edit UX for natural_language_definition with 'Save review' that records a review event but does not change existing manifest JSON
    - [ ] Obtain product/design sign-off on accessibility and keyboard navigation for the review modal
- [ ] TASK-F-007-13: Implement frontend manifest viewer and version selector (frontend)
    - [ ] Implement React/vanilla JS page that calls GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifests to list versions and loads selected manifest via API
    - [ ] Render table list with health_score, definition_confidence badge, row_count, freshness lag, and pii_flags icons
    - [ ] Add version selector dropdown and 'Download JSON' button that streams manifest file to the browser
    - [ ] Add client-side validation to ensure the manifest file rendered matches the server JSON (checksum compare) and surface mismatch warnings
- [ ] TASK-F-007-14: Implement review actions and review record storage (backend + frontend)
    - [ ] Add DB table review_records(manifest_id, table_name, column_name nullable, action enum{accept,edit,reject}, actor_id, comment, edited_definition JSON nullable, created_at)
    - [ ] Implement POST /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifests/{version}/reviews to create a review record with strict validation and ABAC checks
    - [ ] Wire frontend review modal to POST review records and show optimistic UI feedback; on success show persisted review in review timeline
    - [ ] Add endpoint GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifests/{version}/reviews to show review history in the UI
- [ ] TASK-F-007-15: Add manual re-run trigger and reviewer workflow integration (backend / orchestration)
    - [ ] Expose POST /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest/recompute (admin+owner) to enqueue a governance manifest generation job referencing the same integration_id
    - [ ] Ensure recompute job uses current connector credentials and creates a new manifest_version on completion with semver bump rules applied
    - [ ] In UI, add 'Recompute manifest' button with confirmation and show job status stream via SSE (type: agent_start/agent_complete/dq_gate/event types)
    - [ ] Add guard: disallow recompute if another generation job for same integration is in progress
- [ ] TASK-F-007-16: Test UI end-to-end and add QA checks (testing / QA)
    - [ ] Create Cypress/Selenium E2E tests that load manifest viewer, select versions, perform accept/edit/reject actions and assert review_records created
    - [ ] Add accessibility tests (axe) to verify ARIA attributes and keyboard navigation for review modal and version selector
    - [ ] Write manual QA script for product reviewers to validate review flow in staging and confirm that edits are stored as review records without mutating manifest
    - [ ] Add regression checks for large manifests (100+ tables) to assert UI performance and pagination

###### Test Plan
- [ ] End-to-end test: reviewer accepts a low-confidence definition and a review_record is created and visible in manifest history
- [ ] Edit flow test: reviewer edits a definition, confirm edited_definition stored in review_records and original manifest JSON unchanged
- [ ] Recompute test: reviewer triggers recompute and validates new manifest semver bump behavior in UI and API
- [ ] Performance test: manifest viewer renders manifests with 200 tables within acceptable load time (< 2s list render)
- [ ] Security test: attempt review actions as non-admin and assert 403; verify audit log contains actor and IP

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Manifest viewer UI deployed to staging with version selector and download functionality
- [ ] Review actions (Accept/Edit/Reject) persist review records and are visible in UI history without mutating manifests
- [ ] Manual recompute trigger enqueues generation job and produces new manifest version per semver rules
- [ ] E2E UI tests and accessibility tests pass in CI
- [ ] Security review confirms only authorized users can perform reviews and recompute; audit logs record all reviewer actions

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] TASK-F-007-01 (manifest generation)
- [ ] TASK-F-007-04 (manifest APIs)
- [ ] RBAC service for ABAC checks

###### Agent Notes
- UI actions MUST create review records that reference manifest_version and not mutate manifest JSON. Replayable audit trail is required. Recompute calls should enqueue background jobs and stream progress via existing SSE event protocol (agent_start, dq_gate, agent_complete). When showing PII flags, do not expose detected sample values; only show pattern_type and column name. Ensure the UI only requests manifests for integrations within the caller's workspace and that endpoints enforce ABAC server-side.

#### Requirement / Feature: PII detection service (regex + token classifier, manifest flags)

**Feature ID:** `F-008`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, data`

**Rationale:** PII flags are required to block ML use and satisfy security requirements.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-005
- [ ] F-006

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-008-01: As a governance engineer, I want an automated PII detection service that applies deterministic regex scans and a token-classifier model at integration time so that detected PII columns are surfaced in the governance manifest (with pattern type, confidence, and evidence) and blocked from downstream ML use.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] The governance agent runs a PII_SCAN step on every integration run and outputs pii_flags in the governance_manifest schema for each table/column.
- [ ] - [ ] The detection combines a curated set of regex patterns (email, SSN, phone, credit card, IP, full name heuristics) and a token-classifier that returns type and confidence for each sampled column.
- [ ] - [ ] For any column with a PII hit and classifier_confidence >= 0.6 the manifest marks pii_flags with pattern_type and confidence and sets dq_gate_status to BLOCK for ML use (but not for descriptive-only workflows unless admin allows).
- [ ] - [ ] The service stores up to N example values (anonymized / hashed) that triggered patterns for audit (configurable, default 5) and never sends full raw rows to LLMs or external logs.
- [ ] - [ ] The governance_manifest version increments (minor) when profile changes and includes PII flags as specified in the PRD manifest schema.
- [ ] - [ ] Unit and integration tests cover regex-only hits, classifier-only hits, combined hits, and false-positive mitigation cases.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-008-01: Design and implement regex-based PII scanner module
    - [ ] - [ ] Define canonical regex patterns for: email, SSN (US), phone (multiformat), credit card (Luhn-lite), IPv4/IPv6, name heuristics and document rationale
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement performant streaming regex scanner usable on sampled column data (10k row cap) with configurable timeout and sampling strategy
    - [ ] - [ ] Add configuration to enable/disable specific patterns per workspace and to set sample_size and max_example_values
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests for each pattern with positive and negative examples and edge cases
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement structured output format: { column, pattern_type, regex_match_count, example_matches }
    - [ ] - [ ] Add logging for scanner start/stop, rows_scanned, time_ms and errors with trace_id
- [ ] TASK-F-008-02: Integrate token-classifier model for PII verification
    - [ ] - [ ] Select token-classifier approach: use hosted fine-tuned model or on-prem model endpoint; document model spec and performance SLA
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement adapter code to call classifier with column sample text batches and collect per-token labels and aggregated column confidence
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement retries/backoff, timeout (configurable, default 5s), and circuit-breaker to avoid blocking manifest generation
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit and integration tests for classifier adapter with mocked endpoint returning deterministic responses
    - [ ] - [ ] Instrument metrics: classifier_latency_ms, classifier_success_rate, classifier_confidence_distribution
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure classifier input is limited to aggregated statistical summaries and hashed examples — never raw PII in logs
- [ ] TASK-F-008-03: Merge regex + classifier results and update governance manifest writer
    - [ ] - [ ] Define decision logic: regex hits escalate to PII flag immediately; classifier can add/confirm flags; combine confidences using defined formula and thresholds
    - [ ] - [ ] Extend governance_manifest schema to include pii_flags array entries matching PRD: { column, pattern_type, confidence, evidence_hashes[] }
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement manifest writer changes and version bumping rules (minor on profile change, major on schema change)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add audit log event emission for each flagged column (PII_FLAG_CREATED/UPDATED) including actor: governance_agent and trace_id
    - [ ] - [ ] Add integration tests that run a full manifest generation end-to-end with sample tables and assert pii_flags present
- [ ] TASK-F-008-04: Backend: add DQ gate behavior and enforcement hooks for PII flags
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement DQ gate rule that maps any pii_flag with confidence >= 0.6 to governance_manifest.dq_gate_status BLOCK for ML downstream
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement API flag (allow_ml_use: bool) in manifest that remains false until explicit admin approval API is called
    - [ ] - [ ] Add deterministic failure behavior: pipeline stops before Stage 3 training if any required column is flagged PII and allow_ml_use=false
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests simulating pipeline runs blocked by PII flags and ensure appropriate error messages and remediation suggestions returned
    - [ ] - [ ] Add structured error payload for streaming SSE events type=dq_gate with detailed hit list for UI consumption
- [ ] TASK-F-008-05: Frontend: display PII flags in integration manifest UI and Review Queue
    - [ ] - [ ] Add PII badge and expandable column list to the Integration -> Manifest view showing pattern_type, confidence, and anonymized evidence_count
    - [ ] - [ ] Add action buttons: Request Review, Approve for ML (admin only), Add Annotation/Note
    - [ ] - [ ] Wire the UI to the GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest endpoint and show DQ gate status prominently
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit/integration UI tests for rendering and permission gating
    - [ ] - [ ] Add logging for UI actions (approve/reject) with user id and trace_id
- [ ] TASK-F-008-06: Security & data privacy: secrets, masking, and audit controls
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure classifier credentials/secrets stored in Vault/AWS Secrets Manager with least privilege and rotation policy
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement evidence handling: store only hashed/anonymized examples (SHA-256 + truncated) and never full plaintext in DB or logs
    - [ ] - [ ] Conduct brief threat model for PII detection flow and document residual risks and mitigations
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests verifying evidence storage does not contain raw matches
    - [ ] - [ ] Add security review checklist item and remediate issues before merge
- [ ] TASK-F-008-07: Testing & QA: synthetic datasets and regression tests
    - [ ] - [ ] Prepare synthetic test datasets covering varied PII cases, non-PII ambiguous columns, and international phone formats
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement automated integration tests that run manifest generation on synthetic datasets in CI
    - [ ] - [ ] Add regression tests to assert false-positive and false-negative rates remain within acceptable bounds on baseline dataset
    - [ ] - [ ] Add performance tests verifying governance manifest generation stays within latency SLAs for per-table targets
    - [ ] - [ ] Document test datasets and expected outcomes in test fixtures

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for each regex pattern with positive/negative cases
- [ ] - [ ] Mocked classifier adapter tests for different classifier responses and timeouts
- [ ] - [ ] End-to-end integration tests running governance agent on synthetic tables asserting manifest.pii_flags and dq_gate_status
- [ ] - [ ] Performance tests measuring governance manifest generation P50/P95 against PRD targets
- [ ] - [ ] Security tests verifying no raw PII in logs and evidence storage is hashed
- [ ] - [ ] False-positive/false-negative regression test suite run nightly and gated
- [ ] - [ ] Manual exploratory QA with real connector to Snowflake (dev workspace) using curated sample datasets

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Regex scanner and classifier adapter implemented and merged behind feature flag
- [ ] - [ ] Governance manifest schema updated and versioned according to PRD
- [ ] - [ ] DQ gate blocks ML use on flagged columns and produces SSE dq_gate events
- [ ] - [ ] UI surfaces PII flags and admin approval actions with RBAC enforced
- [ ] - [ ] Unit, integration, regression, and performance tests added and passing in CI
- [ ] - [ ] Security review completed and secrets stored properly, evidence hashed
- [ ] - [ ] Observability metrics (latency, success rates) emitted and dashboarded

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-005
- [ ] F-006

###### Agent Notes
- Implement regex-first pipeline stage to ensure deterministic, explainable hits; use token-classifier as a secondary verification and confidence score. Keep classifier inputs limited to sampled strings and hashed examples. Default thresholds: classifier_confidence >= 0.6 to flag; examples_saved_per_column default 5; sample_size default 10,000 rows or full table if smaller. Emit structured SSE events (type=dq_gate) when PII flags cause blocking so front-end review flows can surface necessary actions. Feature flag the classifier integration to allow rollout and rollback.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-008-02: As a data scientist or ML pipeline operator, I want PII manifest flags to automatically block feature engineering and model training until an admin explicitly approves ML use, so that models do not inadvertently train on PII and the system enforces privacy controls.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] When governance_manifest contains any pii_flags for columns that would be used as features, the Stage 3 feature engineering agent halts and returns a human_required SSE event containing the flagged columns and recommended remediation.
- [ ] - [ ] The pipeline must reject training runs, and the UI shows a clear block with remediation steps: remove column, approve for ML (admin), or redact/mask column upstream.
- [ ] - [ ] Admin approval endpoint exists: POST /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest/approve_pii with audit trail and expires_at optional; approval toggles allow_ml_use for specific columns only after storing approver_id and timestamp.
- [ ] - [ ] Audit log records every override decision, including approver_id, justification text, and trace_id; overrides are immutable and visible in manifest history.
- [ ] - [ ] Automated tests verify pipeline blocking behavior and approval flow, and ML stages cannot proceed without explicit allow_ml_use.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-008-08: Enforce PII flags in pipeline orchestration & Stage 3 checks
    - [ ] - [ ] Add pipeline pre-check that reads governance_manifest.pii_flags and cross-references planned feature candidates before starting feature engineering
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement human_required SSE event payload schema listing flagged columns, confidence, recommended action, and trace_id
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement deterministic halt behavior: pipeline stops with DQ_GATE_BLOCK and persists run_state for resumption after approval
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests for pre-check logic with multiple scenarios (no PII, PII in unused columns, PII in required features)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add logging for decision path and reason codes
- [ ] TASK-F-008-09: Implement admin approval API and manifest update workflow
    - [ ] - [ ] Create POST /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest/approve_pii endpoint with RBAC to allow only workspace admins
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate request payload: columns[], approver_id, justification, optional expires_at; return updated manifest version
    - [ ] - [ ] Store immutable audit record including approver_id, timestamp, justification_string, previous_manifest_version, trace_id
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure approval toggles allow_ml_use per-column and that approval does not remove original pii_flags (it annotates them as approved_for_ml:true)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add integration tests for approval flow and ensure downstream pipeline resumes correctly after approval
- [ ] TASK-F-008-10: Frontend: review queue and approval UI for flagged PII
    - [ ] - [ ] Add Review Queue card for manifest entries with pii_flags marked low/medium/high confidence and show approve button for admins
    - [ ] - [ ] Include a justification input that is required on approve and stored in audit log
    - [ ] - [ ] On approval, update UI state to reflect approved_for_ml:true and show who approved and when
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement notifications: email/in-app to original pipeline owner when approval occurs so they can resume the run
    - [ ] - [ ] Add UI tests for permission gating and optimistic UI update
- [ ] TASK-F-008-11: Backend: resume orchestration and audit trail after approval
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement pipeline resume endpoint and scheduler hook that checks for pending runs associated with manifest version and resumes Stage 3 after approval
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure resumed run uses the manifest version that includes the approval annotation and records resumed_by and resumed_at in run metadata
    - [ ] - [ ] Add tests simulating approval and resume including concurrent approval and manifest regeneration race conditions
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit audit log events for resume and link to original run trace_id
- [ ] TASK-F-008-12: Security & compliance: role checks, immutability, and export
    - [ ] - [ ] Enforce RBAC on approve endpoint (workspace admin only) and validate via ABAC attributes
    - [ ] - [ ] Make approval audit entries append-only and exportable via /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest/audit
    - [ ] - [ ] Add automated test verifying non-admin cannot approve and that approval entries cannot be altered via other endpoints
    - [ ] - [ ] Document compliance reasoning for the approval workflow for internal audit
- [ ] TASK-F-008-13: Testing: end-to-end ML pipeline blocked/unblocked scenarios
    - [ ] - [ ] Create CI integration test that triggers a session_spec that would use a flagged column and assert pipeline stops with human_required event
    - [ ] - [ ] Create test that posts an approval and asserts pipeline resumes and Stage 3 completes (or fails for other reasons)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add negative tests where approval is expired or revoked and pipeline refuses to resume
    - [ ] - [ ] Add load test ensuring approval/resume logic scales for concurrent manifest approvals

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests for pipeline halt on PII flag and SSE human_required payload correctness
- [ ] - [ ] API contract tests for approve endpoint including RBAC and payload validation
- [ ] - [ ] UI acceptance tests for review queue approve/justify flow and notification delivery
- [ ] - [ ] Concurrency tests for resume behavior when multiple runs target same manifest
- [ ] - [ ] Security tests ensuring only admin can approve and approvals are append-only and exportable

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Pipeline pre-check correctly halts Stage 3 when feature candidates include flagged PII columns
- [ ] - [ ] Admin approval API implemented with RBAC, immutability, and audit log
- [ ] - [ ] UI review queue shows flags and allows approvals with justification
- [ ] - [ ] Pipeline resume flow tested and working in CI with race-condition tests
- [ ] - [ ] Documentation for admin workflows and governance behavior published
- [ ] - [ ] Tests for blocking and approval flows present and passing

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-005
- [ ] F-006
- [ ] US-F-008-01

###### Agent Notes
- Pipeline orchestration must treat PII flags as deterministic ground truth unless an explicit admin override exists. Approval should not remove the PII flag; instead, annotate the flag as approved_for_ml with approver metadata and optional expiry. Use SSE type=human_required to surface required actions to UI and store run_state to enable safe resume. Implement idempotent resume logic to avoid double-execution and include trace_id propagation for auditability.

##### User Story
