- [ ] US-F-008-03: As a QA and security lead, I want monitoring, logging, test coverage, and rollout controls for the PII detection service (including performance and false-positive monitoring) so that the service is observable, reversible, and meets latency and reliability SLAs for production use.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Observability metrics emitted for regex scanner and classifier: rows_scanned, regex_matches_count, classifier_latency_ms, classifier_success_rate, flags_created_count, false_positive_alert_count.
- [ ] - [ ] Alerting rules exist: classifier_error_rate > 5% in 5m triggers pager, average classifier_latency_ms > 2000ms triggers performance alert, false_positive_rate drift over baseline triggers review.
- [ ] - [ ] CI includes unit tests, integration tests, synthetic dataset regression tests, and nightly dataset runs to measure false-positive/false-negative drift.
- [ ] - [ ] Feature flag exists to enable/disable classifier integration per workspace for staged rollout; when disabled system falls back to regex-only detection.
- [ ] - [ ] Canary deployment plan with sample workspace and rollback playbook documented and verified.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-008-14: Add observability and metrics for PII detection components
    - [ ] - [ ] Instrument regex scanner with metrics: scanner_rows_scanned, scanner_time_ms, scanner_match_count
    - [ ] - [ ] Instrument classifier adapter with metrics: classifier_latency_ms, classifier_success_rate, classifier_confidence_histogram
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit structured logs for each detection decision including trace_id, manifest_id, integration_id, pattern_types, and anonymized evidence_hashes
    - [ ] - [ ] Publish Prometheus metrics and create Grafana dashboard tiles for quick health view
    - [ ] - [ ] Add OpenTelemetry trace propagation from orchestration through detection components
- [ ] TASK-F-008-15: Add alerting and SLA checks for detection service
    - [ ] - [ ] Define alert thresholds: classifier_error_rate > 5% (5m), classifier_latency_p95 > 5s, false_positive_rate_delta > 10% vs baseline
    - [ ] - [ ] Configure alerts to notify SRE/pager via Slack/email and create runbook links
    - [ ] - [ ] Add synthetic heartbeat job that runs detection on canned dataset every 6 hours and evaluates expected flags; alert on divergence
    - [ ] - [ ] Add SLA dashboard with weekly uptime and average detection latency
- [ ] TASK-F-008-16: CI/CD: feature flagging, canary, and rollback
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement feature flag gating for token-classifier per workspace and global toggle for safe rollback
    - [ ] - [ ] Add canary deployment pipeline stage that rolls classifier to 5% of workspaces first and monitors metrics for 24–72 hours
    - [ ] - [ ] Automate promotion steps conditional on canary metrics passing (no regression in false-positive rate and latency)
    - [ ] - [ ] Document rollback playbook and automate immediate disable via feature flag if alerts triggered
- [ ] TASK-F-008-17: Testing: false positive / false negative tracking and nightly regression
    - [ ] - [ ] Create labeled ground truth datasets for baseline FP/FN rates across several connector types (Snowflake, BigQuery dev dumps)
    - [ ] - [ ] Schedule nightly CI job to run detection on ground truth and log regression metrics into time-series DB
    - [ ] - [ ] Fail PRs if local test suite shows new deterministic regressions (e.g., pattern match removed unexpectedly)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add mutation tests to ensure patterns are robust to common edge cases
- [ ] TASK-F-008-18: Security & Compliance: evidence retention, export, and access control
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement RBAC for access to PII evidence and audit logs; only workspace admins + compliance role can view hashed evidence and audit entries
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement export endpoint for audit trail that redacts hashed evidence by default and requires elevated permission to include evidence_hashes
    - [ ] - [ ] Add retention policy for detection logs and hashed evidence (default 90 days) and configuration to extend for compliance
    - [ ] - [ ] Add tests verifying access controls and retention behavior
- [ ] TASK-F-008-19: Documentation & runbooks
    - [ ] - [ ] Document detection design, thresholds, false-positive mitigation strategy, and admin approval workflow in internal Confluence
    - [ ] - [ ] Publish runbook for responding to detection alerts and for administering approvals
    - [ ] - [ ] Provide developer guide for adding new regex patterns and retraining/updating classifier
    - [ ] - [ ] Add README in repo describing feature flag usage and canary rollout plan

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Validate Prometheus metrics appear for scanner and classifier in test environment
- [ ] - [ ] Trigger synthetic job divergence and verify alert fires and runbook steps are actionable
- [ ] - [ ] Run canary on a single workspace and validate no regression in FP/FN metrics for 72 hours before full rollout
- [ ] - [ ] Execute CI regression suite nightly and verify no drift relative to baseline dataset
- [ ] - [ ] Perform access control tests to confirm only authorized roles can view evidence and exports

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Metrics and dashboards deployed and accessible to SRE and product security
- [ ] - [ ] Alerting configured with runbooks and verified by running simulated failures
- [ ] - [ ] Feature flag implemented and end-to-end canary rollout tested
- [ ] - [ ] Nightly regression tests for FP/FN implemented and passing on baseline
- [ ] - [ ] Documentation, runbooks, and rollback playbook published and reviewed
- [ ] - [ ] All tests (unit/integration/regression) passing in CI and gated

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-005
- [ ] F-006
- [ ] US-F-008-01
- [ ] US-F-008-02

###### Agent Notes
- Roll out the token-classifier behind a workspace-level feature flag to enable safe staged rollouts. Use canary monitoring for 72-hour windows with automated metric comparison to baseline. Persist only hashed/anonymized evidence for audit; avoid storing raw PII. Expose clear metrics that map to production SLAs: detection_latency_p95 < PRD target, classifier_success_rate > 99%, and false_positive_rate within baseline ±10%. Provide runbook that includes immediate feature-flag disable and manifest rollback steps.

#### Requirement / Feature: Table health scoring engine (nulls, freshness, PK checks)

**Feature ID:** `F-009`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, data`

**Rationale:** Health score appears in manifest and guides semantic layer exposure.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-005

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-009-01: As a data engineer or workspace admin, I want an automated table health scoring engine that computes per-table and per-column health metrics (null rates, freshness lag vs SLA, primary-key uniqueness, row-count trend and PII flags), so that governance manifests contain a deterministic 0-100 health_score used to gate semantic layer exposure and downstream ML pipelines.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Compute null_pct per column and include as numeric field in governance_manifest.tables.[table].columns.[column].null_pct
- [ ] Compute freshness_lag_hrs per table and include as governance_manifest.tables.[table].freshness_lag_hrs
- [ ] Detect primary key candidates and compute pk_uniqueness_violations; if any violations exist, surface violation_count and sample rows in the manifest
- [ ] Compute row_count trend (week-over-week pct change) and include trend_pct and a boolean declining flag if decline > 5%
- [ ] Calculate table health_score 0–100 using documented formula: null penalties (−1pt per 1% avg null rate across key columns), freshness lag penalty (−2pt per hour beyond SLA), PK uniqueness (−20pt if violations), row count trend (−10pt if declining >5%); clamp result to 0–100
- [ ] Include pii_flags per column and ensure columns flagged as PII are present in governance_manifest.tables.[table].pii_flags
- [ ] All computed fields appear in governance_manifest JSON and pass JSON Schema validation for Stage 0 manifest
- [ ] Health score algorithm is deterministic, unit-tested, and returns identical scores given identical input profiles

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-009-01: Design health score algorithm and manifest fields
    - [ ] Draft precise formula mapping inputs to health_score with examples and edge cases
    - [ ] Define new manifest fields and types: health_score, freshness_lag_hrs, pk_violation_count, row_count_trend_pct, declining_flag, pii_flags
    - [ ] Update Stage 0 governance_manifest JSON Schema with new fields and add semantic documentation
    - [ ] Review design with data engineering, semantic layer owner, and product to ensure gating semantics align with pipeline DQ gates
- [ ] TASK-F-009-02: Implement null rate and column profiling in profiling job
    - [ ] Add SQL sampling logic to compute null_pct, distinct_count, top_5_values for each column (sample up to 10,000 rows)
    - [ ] Add aggregation code to produce per-column stats and p25/p50/p75/mean/stddev
    - [ ] Add robust handling for very large tables (use approximate counting where supported by warehouse dialect)
    - [ ] Add unit tests to validate null_pct computation using controlled sample data
- [ ] TASK-F-009-03: Implement freshness SLA evaluation and freshness_lag_hrs calculation
    - [ ] Read table-level last_updated timestamp using connector metadata or user-configured freshness column
    - [ ] Compute freshness_lag_hrs = current_time_utc − last_updated_ts in hours, with timezone normalization
    - [ ] Apply default SLA (48hrs) if table SLA not set and include sla_hours in manifest
    - [ ] Add error handling and fallback when last_updated is unavailable (set freshness_lag_hrs = null and add dq_gate WARN)
- [ ] TASK-F-009-04: Implement primary-key candidate detection and uniqueness check
    - [ ] Generate PK candidate sets: single-column id-like names and composite keys formed from low-null, high-cardinality columns up to 3 columns
    - [ ] Execute uniqueness queries (COUNT(*) vs COUNT(DISTINCT key)) with sampling/approximate path for large tables
    - [ ] Record pk_violation_count and attach up to N sample violating rows (redacted) in manifest
    - [ ] Add logic to mark PK uniqueness as BLOCK in DQ gate when violations > 0
- [ ] TASK-F-009-05: Implement row count trend and declining detection
    - [ ] Query weekly row counts for last 8 weeks or available history
    - [ ] Compute week-over-week % change and overall trend_pct
    - [ ] Set declining_flag true if week-over-week average decline > 5%
    - [ ] Add tests using synthetic time-series data to validate calculation
- [ ] TASK-F-009-06: Compute final health_score and integrate into manifest generation
    - [ ] Aggregate penalties: compute avg null penalty across key columns, freshness penalty beyond SLA, PK penalty, row-trend penalty per spec
    - [ ] Clamp score to 0–100 and annotate breakdown fields (score_breakdown object) for transparency
    - [ ] Update governance manifest writer to include health_score and score_breakdown per table
    - [ ] Add unit tests asserting score result for canonical examples (perfect table, high-null table, stale table, PK-violating table)
- [ ] TASK-F-009-07: Add validation, error handling, logging, and telemetry
    - [ ] Add structured logs for profiling steps with trace_id and table identifier
    - [ ] Ensure errors in sampling or uniqueness checks produce WARN or FAIL in dq_gate_status but do not crash the entire manifest run
    - [ ] Emit Prometheus metrics: governance_manifest_runtime_seconds, health_score_distribution_histogram, pk_check_failures_total
    - [ ] Add retry/backoff for transient warehouse errors and clear error messages for owners

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for null_pct computation with controlled datasets (including all-null, no-null, mixed)
- [ ] Unit tests for freshness_lag_hrs using mocked timestamps and different timezones
- [ ] Unit tests for PK detection using synthetic unique and duplicate datasets
- [ ] Integration test: run profiling on a small test database and assert manifest JSON includes health fields and passes JSON Schema validation
- [ ] Property tests: deterministic health_score given identical input snapshot
- [ ] Error scenarios: simulate warehouse query failure and assert dq_gate_status=FAIL or WARN is set and error is logged but manifest generation continues for other tables
- [ ] Performance test: run profiling on representative table sizes to verify governance manifest generation P50 < 30s (target) and P95 < 90s

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Algorithm design document reviewed and accepted by data engineering and product
- [ ] Profiling job computes null_pct, column stats, freshness_lag_hrs, pk_violation_count, row_count_trend and writes them into governance_manifest following updated JSON Schema
- [ ] health_score and score_breakdown fields present and deterministic for all tables processed in unit/integration runs
- [ ] All new code covered by unit tests >= 90% for critical modules and integrated into CI pipeline
- [ ] Structured logs and telemetry emitted; no plaintext credentials or sample PII logged
- [ ] Backward compatibility: manifest consumers still parse existing fields; new fields optional for older clients

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-005
- [ ] Stage 0 governance manifest base schema (existing)
- [ ] Warehouse connector reliability from Release 1 (Snowflake)

###### Agent Notes
- Governance agent must compute and emit deterministic numeric fields only; no free-form text. Health score formula must follow the documented penalty rules exactly. Agent outputs must be valid governance_manifest JSON (semver increment on profile changes). When sampling is used for large tables, include an explicit sampling_strategy field in the manifest to allow downstream auditors to understand approximation. Never send raw row data to LLMs; only statistics and small redacted violation samples (max 5 rows, PII removed) may be attached. Log trace_id for each table run to enable traceability across agent calls.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-009-02: As a product user and semantic-layer consumer, I want the table health_score and related DQ fields surfaced via the governance_manifest API and the workspace UI (table health dashboard and health badges), so that semantic layer builders and analysts can review health, block or allow tables for ML use, and see health change history in the manifest history.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Governance manifest GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest returns health_score, score_breakdown, freshness_lag_hrs, pk_violation_count, row_count_trend_pct, declining_flag, and pii_flags for every table
- [ ] Frontend table health dashboard shows health_score with color-coded badge (green >= 80, amber 50–79, red < 50) and link to detailed manifest JSON view
- [ ] Tables with PK uniqueness violations are marked with a blocking icon and a tooltip that explains the PK violation and points to remediation steps
- [ ] Semantic layer ingestion process reads governance_manifest and rejects tables where dq_gate_status == BLOCK or pk_violation_count > 0 unless admin override is set; rejection recorded in audit log
- [ ] Manifest versioning applied: any profile change increments minor semver and schema change increments major semver; UI shows manifest.version and generated_at
- [ ] API returns appropriate HTTP error codes and JSON error details for invalid integration_id, permission denied, or manifest not found

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-009-08: Extend governance_manifest API and backend endpoints to include new health fields
    - [ ] Update backend manifest serializer to include health_score, score_breakdown, freshness_lag_hrs and other new fields
    - [ ] Add API integration tests for GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest asserting new fields present and types correct
    - [ ] Implement permission checks for manifest retrieval (workspace membership) and return 403 for unauthorized
    - [ ] Add pagination/version query params and implement manifest history retrieval endpoint
- [ ] TASK-F-009-09: Implement frontend Table Health Dashboard and badges
    - [ ] Design UI components: health badge, score breakdown modal, pk violation card, freshness indicator
    - [ ] Implement dashboard page to list tables with sortable health_score, filter by health range and PII flags
    - [ ] Add details view that fetches and displays raw governance_manifest JSON with syntax highlighting and score_breakdown
    - [ ] Add accessibility attributes (aria-label) and responsive layout; add unit/E2E tests for UI components
- [ ] TASK-F-009-10: Integrate health_score into semantic layer ingestion and blocking rules
    - [ ] Update semantic layer ingestion pipeline to read dq_gate_status and health_score and apply blocking logic per policy
    - [ ] Add admin override flow: allow explicit override stored in governance metadata with audit entry
    - [ ] Add logs and audit events whenever a table is blocked or allowed due to health_score change
    - [ ] Add integration tests: ingest manifest with blocked table and assert semantic layer creation is prevented
- [ ] TASK-F-009-11: Implement manifest versioning and UI display
    - [ ] Ensure manifest writer increments semver per rules: schema changes => major, profile changes => minor
    - [ ] Store manifest versions in Postgres with generated_at and generated_by fields
    - [ ] Expose manifest history in UI with ability to compare two versions (diff health_score and columns)
    - [ ] Add E2E test for manifest version lifecycle: write initial manifest, update profile, ensure minor bump, simulate schema change, ensure major bump
- [ ] TASK-F-009-12: Add API error handling, logging, and client-friendly messages
    - [ ] Return structured error payloads {code, message, details} for common errors (not found, unauthorized, invalid manifest)
    - [ ] Log errors with trace_id and minimal sensitive context; scrub any credentials and PII from logs
    - [ ] Rate-limit manifest retrieval endpoints and respond with 429 with Retry-After header for high traffic
    - [ ] Add unit tests and contract tests for error cases
- [ ] TASK-F-009-13: Add frontend indicators for manifest health trends and alerts
    - [ ] Show small sparkline of historical health_score per table in list view
    - [ ] Add contextual banner when health_score drops > 10 points since artifact creation
    - [ ] Implement click-to-open remediation suggestions based on score_breakdown (e.g., 'Investigate high null rate on column X')
    - [ ] Add UI tests to validate alerts and banner behavior

###### Test Plan
- [ ] API contract tests: validate manifest schema includes health fields for multiple manifest versions
- [ ] E2E test: simulate a table with PK violation and assert UI shows blocking icon and semantic ingestion prevents explore creation
- [ ] UI component tests: health badge color logic, accessibility checks (axe), responsive layout checks
- [ ] Integration test: admin override flow modifies ingestion decision and creates an audit log entry
- [ ] Security test: ensure manifest endpoint does not expose database credentials or raw PII samples beyond allowed redacted violation samples
- [ ] Load test: simulate concurrent manifest GET requests to ensure rate limits and 429 behavior

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Backend API returns manifest with new health fields and passes integration tests
- [ ] Frontend table health dashboard implemented, accessible to workspace members, and passes accessibility checks
- [ ] Semantic layer ingestion blocks tables per policy and records audit entries; admin override is supported and audited
- [ ] Manifest versioning implemented and visible in UI with diff capability
- [ ] API returns correct HTTP error codes and structured error JSON; rate limiting and permission checks in place
- [ ] End-to-end tests covering manifest generation -> API -> UI -> semantic ingestion are green in CI

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] US-F-009-01 completion (health computation)
- [ ] F-005
- [ ] Semantic layer ingestion service (Stage 1) interfaces

###### Agent Notes
- When the governance agent writes the manifest, ensure manifest.version increments and that health fields are included exactly as typed. The frontend expects numeric health_score and score_breakdown object with field-level penalties. Downstream consumers (semantic layer) will rely on dq_gate_status and pk_violation_count to block ingestion; include explicit flags for blocking decisions. Manifest history must be queryable by version id. Avoid sending raw rows except redacted violation samples (max 5 rows) and ensure PII is not present in these samples.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-009-03: As an SRE/QA and security lead, I want the health scoring engine to be production-rigorous with observability, alerts, security-safe handling of PII, and automated tests, so that the system is reliable, auditable, and safe for enterprise use.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Instrumentation exposes Prometheus metrics for manifest generation duration, health_score distribution, pk_check_failures_total, and manifest_generation_errors_total
- [ ] Distributed traces include trace_id propagated from the orchestration layer through profiling, scoring, and manifest write
- [ ] Alerting rules created: manifest_generation_failure (pager on critical), health_score_drop_gt_10 (email/Slack), pk_violation_detected (email to dataset owner)
- [ ] CI runs unit, integration, and E2E tests for the health scoring engine on every PR; test coverage thresholds enforced for critical modules
- [ ] All logs scrub sensitive values: database credentials never logged, PII flags logged only as structured flags (no raw PII)
- [ ] Secrets and connector credentials continue to live in secrets manager and are never included in manifest or logs

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-009-14: Add observability: metrics, logging, traces
    - [ ] Instrument profiling and scoring code to emit Prometheus metrics and labels (workspace_id, integration_id, table_name)
    - [ ] Add OpenTelemetry traces with spans for catalog -> profile -> score -> manifest_write and propagate trace_id through agent calls
    - [ ] Standardize structured logging format (JSON) and include trace_id, table, manifest_version
    - [ ] Create Grafana dashboards for health_score trends and manifest generation latency
- [ ] TASK-F-009-15: Implement alerting and notification rules
    - [ ] Define alert for manifest_generation_errors_total > 0 with severity critical and route to on-call
    - [ ] Define alert for health_score drop of >10 points since previous manifest and route to workspace owner via Slack/email
    - [ ] Create alert for pk_violation_count > 0 with dataset owner notification and include link to manifest detail
    - [ ] Document alert runbooks with remediation steps and runbook links
- [ ] TASK-F-009-16: Security and PII safe handling
    - [ ] Ensure redacted violation sample rows remove or hash values for columns flagged as PII before attaching to manifest
    - [ ] Add automated checks in CI to scan logs for credentials or raw PII patterns and fail build if detected
    - [ ] Update secrets usage docs and confirm connectors use secrets manager; add runtime assertion that credentials are not serialized into manifest or logs
    - [ ] Perform a security review with InfoSec and capture signoff
- [ ] TASK-F-009-17: Performance and load testing
    - [ ] Create synthetic datasets of various sizes and run profiling to measure manifest generation P50/P95/P99
    - [ ] Run stress tests for concurrent manifest generations across many integrations to validate queue/backoff and rate limiting
    - [ ] Document scaling guidelines and thresholds for when to move to async job mode
    - [ ] Add automated nightly performance regression test comparing current run times with baseline
- [ ] TASK-F-009-18: QA automation and CI integration
    - [ ] Add unit tests, integration tests, and contract tests to CI with mandatory pass for merges to main
    - [ ] Add test fixtures and mocks for warehouse connectors to enable repeatable tests in CI
    - [ ] Create E2E smoke test that runs a full governance manifest generation on a test integration and asserts presence of health_score and no critical dq_gate failures
    - [ ] Add test matrix to run schema-change scenarios and ensure semver logic behaves correctly
- [ ] TASK-F-009-19: Audit logging and compliance
    - [ ] Write audit entries for manifest generation events: actor/service, manifest_version, tables_processed, dq_gate_outcomes
    - [ ] Persist audit logs to append-only store accessible to workspace admins and for export
    - [ ] Add API for audit retrieval for a manifest run and add retention policy documentation
    - [ ] Add tests verifying audit log entries are created for manifest runs and overrides

###### Test Plan
- [ ] CI unit tests for all new modules run on PR and coverage threshold enforced
- [ ] Integration tests for observability: assert metrics emitted, traces recorded, and logs contain trace_id
- [ ] Alerting test: trigger synthetic health_score drop and verify Slack/email notification received and runbook link present
- [ ] Security tests: run log-scanning tool in CI to detect PII or credentials and assert zero findings
- [ ] Performance tests: baseline manifest generation times recorded and compared with new runs; regressions > 10% flagged
- [ ] Audit tests: simulate admin override and assert audit entries created and retrievable via audit API

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Prometheus metrics and Grafana dashboards deployed and showing live data for test workspace
- [ ] Tracing implemented end-to-end with trace_id linkage and visible in Jaeger/Honeycomb
- [ ] Alerts configured in alerting system with test notifications to Slack/email and runbooks linked
- [ ] CI pipeline enforces test suite and coverage thresholds; failing tests block merges
- [ ] Security review passed and logs do not contain credentials or raw PII; manifest redact rules applied
- [ ] Audit logs capture manifest generation and blocking decisions and are queryable

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] US-F-009-01 for instrumentation points
- [ ] US-F-009-02 for UI/consumer behavior
- [ ] Platform observability stack (Prometheus, Grafana, OpenTelemetry)
- [ ] Secrets manager integration

###### Agent Notes
- Telemetry and observability are critical for diagnosing manifest generation failures. Ensure governance agent includes trace_id input and echoes trace_id in all emitted manifest metadata. Do not include raw sample values for PII columns; include only pii_flags and redacted_sample_rows hashed or removed. Alerts should include manifest.version and a deep link to the manifest details in the UI. Design tests to run deterministically using mocked time and database fixtures.

#### Requirement / Feature: Integration detail UI: governance manifest viewer (frontend screen)

**Feature ID:** `F-010`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `frontend`

**Rationale:** Product needs a screen for users to review manifest results and low-confidence definitions in MVP mockups.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-007
- [ ] F-009
- [ ] F-008

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-010-01: As a workspace admin or analyst, I want an Integration Detail screen that displays the governance manifest for a selected integration, so that I can inspect table health, column profiles, PII flags, lineage, and manifest metadata to assess data readiness before starting analysis.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Display manifest metadata: manifest_version, generated_at, integration_id, workspace_id, dq_gate_status, human_review_required.
- [ ] Render a paginated/sortable list of tables contained in the manifest with table name, health_score (0-100), row_count, freshness_lag_hrs, and definition_confidence badge (high|medium|low).
- [ ] For each table card, provide an expand/collapse to show column-level summary: column name, semantic_type, null_pct, distinct_count, top_values (top_5_values), and definition_confidence.
- [ ] Show PII flags per table and per column with pattern_type and a prominent critical badge where present.
- [ ] Render lineage view section listing lineage_edges for the selected table (from->to), with link to open referenced table card.
- [ ] Provide a 'Download manifest JSON' button that fetches the canonical manifest and downloads the raw JSON file.
- [ ] Show last-fetched timestamp and manifest_version; UI must refresh manifest on user action and reflect server manifest_version changes.
- [ ] All network calls must show loading states, handle errors with user-facing message + retry, and log errors to client telemetry.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-010-01: Design and implement Integration Detail UI layout and components
    - [ ] Create wireframe and component breakdown for Integration Detail screen (header, table list, table card, column table, lineage panel, manifest metadata footer).
    - [ ] Design accessible badges and status chips for health_score, definition_confidence, dq_gate_status, and PII flags (include ARIA roles).
    - [ ] Implement React/TS components: IntegrationHeader, TableCard, ColumnProfileTable, LineagePanel, ManifestFooter following existing design tokens.
    - [ ] Implement skeleton loaders and empty states for all panels (table list empty, column list empty, lineage empty).
    - [ ] Write component unit tests for rendering states (loading, success, error) with Jest + React Testing Library.
    - [ ] Create Storybook stories for all components to validate visual QA and accessibility.
- [ ] TASK-F-010-02: Integrate frontend with governance manifest API
    - [ ] Implement REST client call to GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest with workspace auth token and trace_id header.
    - [ ] Map manifest schema fields to UI models and implement defensive parsing with fallback defaults for missing fields.
    - [ ] Implement caching strategy: short client-side cache (5 minutes) with manual refresh button that invalidates cache.
    - [ ] Implement retry/backoff for transient network errors and surface error messages with actionable remediation.
    - [ ] Add telemetry event on manifest fetch success/failure including manifest_version and latency.
- [ ] TASK-F-010-03: Backend contract validation & data shaping (coordination task with backend)
    - [ ] Confirm API contract: validate existence and shape of /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest against PRD schema; list required fields and types.
    - [ ] Implement backend-facing schema validator in frontend (JSON Schema) to assert server response matches expected manifest shape and throw descriptive errors when mismatch.
    - [ ] Create error mapping table for known server error codes (401, 403, 404, 429, 500) to user messages and UI flows.
    - [ ] Coordinate with backend team to ensure manifest_version and generated_at are present, and to support an 'If-None-Match' / ETag header for efficient refreshes.
- [ ] TASK-F-010-04: Security & access control enforcement in UI
    - [ ] Check user roles via current auth context and hide or show admin-only controls (e.g., download JSON) per RBAC rules.
    - [ ] Ensure all fetch calls include auth headers and honor workspace scoping; block requests if workspace mismatch detected and show explanatory message.
    - [ ] Implement client-side guard to redact PII pattern samples in UI if the user does not have 'PII_view' permission, showing 'Redacted' plus tooltip.
    - [ ] Write security tests verifying unauthorized users cannot access download or edit actions.
- [ ] TASK-F-010-05: Logging, telemetry, and observability
    - [ ] Instrument manifest view actions with telemetry events: view_opened, table_expanded, manifest_downloaded, lineage_clicked, refresh_clicked.
    - [ ] Capture performance metrics: manifest_fetch_latency, manifest_parse_time, table_render_time, and send to observability backend.
    - [ ] Log client-side schema validation failures with trace_id and server payload (redact sensitive data) to Sentry/Honeycomb.
    - [ ] Add feature-flag gating for the new UI screen to support staged rollout and A/B testing.
- [ ] TASK-F-010-06: Testing & QA for Integration Detail screen
    - [ ] Write integration tests verifying end-to-end data flow with mocked API responses (success, partial, error).
    - [ ] Write accessibility tests: axe checks for ARIA labels on interactive elements and color contrast checks for badges.
    - [ ] Create E2E Cypress tests covering typical user flows: open integration, expand table, view column details, download manifest, refresh manifest, and error recovery.
    - [ ] Run cross-browser smoke tests (Chrome, Firefox, Safari) and mobile responsive checks < 768px.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for each React component covering rendering with valid, partial, and missing manifest fields.
- [ ] Mocked API contract tests ensuring frontend JSON Schema validation errors are handled and surfaced to users.
- [ ] E2E Cypress scenario: open Integration Detail, expand first table, verify displayed health_score, click 'Download manifest', validate file contents match mocked manifest.
- [ ] Accessibility test: run axe on Integration Detail screen and assert no critical violations.
- [ ] Security test: simulate user without PII_view permission and assert PII samples are redacted.
- [ ] Load test: simulate manifest with 200 tables to validate UI pagination and render performance.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Integration Detail screen implemented and merged behind feature flag.
- [ ] All listed acceptance criteria pass in manual QA and automated test suites.
- [ ] Unit tests: >= 90% coverage for new components; integration/E2E tests green in CI.
- [ ] Telemetry events and client-side logs are emitted and visible in observability dashboard.
- [ ] Security review completed: RBAC and PII redaction behaviors validated.
- [ ] Performance: manifest fetch path meets P95 latency target for local mock (<= 500ms for API + parse in CI harness).
- [ ] Documentation: README for the screen, API contract assumptions, and Storybook stories published.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-007
- [ ] F-009
- [ ] F-008
- [ ] /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest (backend must be available per PRD)

###### Agent Notes
- Use the governance_manifest schema defined in the PRD as the single source of truth. Prefer ETag/If-None-Match or manifest_version to avoid unnecessary payloads and to render stale-while-revalidate UX. Respect privacy: never send raw PII values to telemetry. Coordinate with backend to ensure the GET manifest endpoint supports pagination or reasonable payload sizes for large integrations. Implement defensive parsing: fields may be missing when manifest generation is in-progress; display 'profiling in progress' state for such tables.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-010-02: As a data steward, I want to review and resolve low-confidence definitions surfaced by the governance/semantic pipeline, so that I can promote correct definitions to high confidence or request edits and unblock downstream ML usage.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] UI surfaces a 'Review Queue' section listing only tables/columns with definition_confidence == 'low' or human_review_required == true.
- [ ] Each low-confidence item exposes actions: Accept (promote), Edit (open editor modal with current definition text), Reject (mark as rejected with reason).
- [ ] Accept must invoke a backend API to mark the specific definition as accepted and update the semantic layer confidence; UI must optimistically update and reconcile with server response.
- [ ] Edit must validate input client-side (non-empty definition, max length 1000 chars) and POST changes to backend review endpoint; show validation errors inline.
- [ ] Reject must require a reason (min 10 chars) and record audit log entry visible in manifest history.
- [ ] All review actions must be auditable: append actor_id, timestamp, action, and comment to the audit trail accessible from the manifest metadata panel.
- [ ] Actions must handle server-side validation errors and show clear remediation messages; no silent failures.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-010-07: Implement Review Queue UI and modal flows
    - [ ] Add Review Queue section to Integration Detail screen with filter 'low-confidence only' default.
    - [ ] Implement ReviewItem component showing metric/column name, sample definition, confidence, and action buttons (Accept/Edit/Reject).
    - [ ] Implement EditDefinition modal with WYSIWYG/plain text editor, inline validation, cancel and save actions, and character count.
    - [ ] Implement Reject modal that requires a rejection reason with client-side min length validation and shows consequences to user.
    - [ ] Add optimistic UI updates and rollback behavior on server rejection; show toast notifications for success/error.
- [ ] TASK-F-010-08: Backend integration for review actions (coordinate with API owners)
    - [ ] Define and verify backend endpoints for review actions: POST /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/definitions/{def_id}/accept, /edit, /reject or similar agreed contract.
    - [ ] Implement call wrappers with idempotency keys for Accept and Reject to prevent double actions from retries.
    - [ ] Surface server-side validation errors (e.g., invalid definition text, missing permission) and map to client error messages.
    - [ ] Ensure backend emits audit log events for each review action that are queryable via existing artifact/manifest audit endpoints.
- [ ] TASK-F-010-09: Permissions, audit & compliance
    - [ ] Enforce that only users with Reviewer or Admin roles can perform Accept/Edit/Reject actions; show disabled state with tooltip otherwise.
    - [ ] Add client-side confirmation dialogues for destructive actions (Reject) with explicit 'I understand' checkbox for critical rejections.
    - [ ] Ensure every action includes actor_id and trace_id in the request headers so backend can associate audit events.
    - [ ] Expose a lightweight audit history view per definition in the manifest metadata panel showing last 5 events.
- [ ] TASK-F-010-10: Validation, conflict handling, and optimistic concurrency
    - [ ] Implement optimistic concurrency: when saving edited definition, include definition_version or manifest_version to detect conflicts and surface 'conflict detected' UI with a diff.
    - [ ] When conflict occurs, show side-by-side diff and provide option to overwrite or cancel; do not auto-merge.
    - [ ] Validate edited definition client-side for required fields and safe characters; reject inputs that exceed length limits or contain disallowed markup.
    - [ ] Log conflict and resolution actions to telemetry for later analysis.
- [ ] TASK-F-010-11: Testing & QA for review flows
    - [ ] Write unit tests for Review Queue components and modal validation logic.
    - [ ] Write integration tests with mocked backend responses for accept/edit/reject success, validation errors, permission denied, and conflict scenarios.
    - [ ] E2E tests: reviewer opens queue, edits a low-confidence definition, saves, sees audit entry, and verifies downstream blocking/unblocking behavior via mocked DQ gate state changes.
    - [ ] Security tests to ensure non-reviewers cannot perform review actions via UI or by forging requests (403 expected).

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for ReviewItem and modal validation rules.
- [ ] Integration tests covering API success and failure responses; validate optimistic update rollback on failure.
- [ ] E2E flow: reviewer accepts a definition and verifies the definition_confidence updates to 'high' in the manifest list and the item disappears from low-confidence queue.
- [ ] Conflict test: simulate concurrent edit by another user and verify diff UI is displayed and options behave correctly.
- [ ] Permission test: verify reviewer actions return 403 when user lacks permission and UI disables buttons accordingly.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Review Queue UI integrated and functional behind feature flag.
- [ ] Review actions call agreed backend endpoints and produce audit events visible in manifest history.
- [ ] Optimistic UI updates and conflict handling implemented with clear user flows.
- [ ] All acceptance criteria verified by QA and passing automated tests (unit/integration/E2E).
- [ ] RBAC enforcement verified and security tests passed.
- [ ] Documentation for reviewers on how to use the queue and the consequences of Accept/Edit/Reject.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-007
- [ ] F-009
- [ ] F-008
- [ ] Backend review API endpoints for semantic definitions (coordinate with semantic layer owners)

###### Agent Notes
- The frontend must not attempt to infer or alter definitions locally beyond client-side validation; all authoritative state changes must be executed via backend review endpoints to preserve immutability and audit trail requirements. Use manifest_version/definition_version to implement optimistic locking. Keep edit payload minimal: { definition: string, comment?: string, actor_id, definition_version }.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-010-03: As an SRE/DevOps and product owner, I want the Integration Detail UI to surface manifest version history, error states, and support robust telemetry and observability, so that we can diagnose manifest generation problems and track user actions for audit and troubleshooting.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Show manifest version history section listing previous manifest_version values, generated_at timestamps, and a summary of changes (tables added/removed, DQ status changes) for the last 10 versions.
- [ ] When manifest fetch returns DQ_GATE status BLOCK or server returns 5xx, show prominently a danger state with recommended remediation actions and link to integration health dashboard.
- [ ] All user actions on the Integration Detail screen (refresh, download, review actions) must emit structured telemetry events with trace_id, user_id, workspace_id, and manifest_version.
- [ ] UI must display friendly, actionable error messages for network errors, permission errors, and server-side schema mismatches; errors must include a 'Report issue' button that bundles anonymized telemetry with user's selected screenshot option.
- [ ] Implement client-side rate-limit handling: when 429 is returned, show cooldown UI with Retry-After and exponential backoff for background refresh attempts.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-010-12: Implement manifest version history UI and diff summary
    - [ ] Add Manifest History accordion showing last 10 manifest versions with generated_at, manifest_version, and dq_gate_status.
    - [ ] Implement lightweight diff summary computation in frontend: count of tables added/removed, tables with changed health_score > 5, columns with PII flag changes (use server-provided change_summary if available).
    - [ ] Provide 'Compare' UI: select two versions and show side-by-side summary for quick review (no full schema diff).
    - [ ] Add 'Re-fetch this version' CTA that triggers a server-side retrieval if the backend supports historical manifests; otherwise show note 'Historical retrieval not available'.
- [ ] TASK-F-010-13: Error handling, reporting, and backoff strategy
    - [ ] Standardize error UI components for network, auth, permission, and server validation errors with consistent UX patterns.
    - [ ] Implement 429 handling: parse Retry-After header, display countdown UI, and schedule automatic retries using exponential backoff (capped) for background refreshes.
    - [ ] Add 'Report issue' flow that collects trace_id, manifest_version, URL, and anonymized API payload (redact PII) and sends to support intake endpoint.
    - [ ] Add explicit display for DQ_GATE BLOCK/WARN states with recommended remediation links (e.g., 'Open Governance Console').
- [ ] TASK-F-010-14: Telemetry & observability integration
    - [ ] Emit structured telemetry for: manifest_view_opened, manifest_refresh, manifest_downloaded, review_action_performed, manifest_fetch_error with metadata fields.
    - [ ] Ensure telemetry includes workspace_id, integration_id, manifest_version, user_role, and trace_id for correlation with backend logs.
    - [ ] Add client-side metric for manifest payload size and parse time to detect large manifests causing UI slowness.
    - [ ] Create a pre-built dashboard (Grafana/Honeycomb) widget tracking manifest fetch error rate, average latency, 429 rate, and review action volumes for initial SRE monitoring.
- [ ] TASK-F-010-15: Testing: resilience, observability and audit
    - [ ] Write automated tests simulating 429 with Retry-After header to assert UI cooldown behavior and retry schedule.
    - [ ] Write tests to simulate 500 and 502 responses and verify 'Report issue' flow captures required telemetry and shows remediation.
    - [ ] Verify that all emitted telemetry events are received and parsable in the staging observability stack.
    - [ ] Perform manual fault injection in staging (drop manifest service, slow responses) to validate UI graceful degradation and user guidance.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for manifest history component and diff summary logic.
- [ ] Integration tests simulating server returning DQ_GATE BLOCK to validate danger UI and remediation links.
- [ ] Resilience tests for 429/Retry-After behavior and exponential backoff timer correctness.
- [ ] Observability verification tests to ensure telemetry payloads contain required fields and are ingested.
- [ ] Manual SRE runbook walkthrough to confirm how to triage manifest fetch failures and correlate trace_id with backend logs.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Manifest history and error UX implemented and verified in staging.
- [ ] Telemetry events are emitted and visible in observability dashboards with correct metadata.
- [ ] Backoff and retry logic for 429/5xx implemented and tested.
- [ ] Support 'Report issue' flow validated end-to-end and ticket creation verified.
- [ ] Performance and resilience tests pass; SRE runbook updated with manifest troubleshooting steps.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-007
- [ ] F-009
- [ ] F-008
- [ ] Observability stack (Honeycomb/Grafana/Sentry) integration
- [ ] Backend support for manifest versioning/history (if not available, UI shows historical retrieval unavailable)

###### Agent Notes
- Prefer server-provided change_summary for manifest history if available to avoid expensive client diffs. Always redact PII from telemetry and error reports. Use trace_id header propagation end-to-end for correlating frontend telemetry with backend agent logs. If backend does not expose historical manifests, implement a lightweight UI note and request API enhancement as a follow-up (tie to F-009).

## Milestone B - Semantic Layer & Session Planning

### Milestone Success Criteria
- [ ] Semantic layer builder produces Cube.dev-compatible schema from manifest
- [ ] Session planner maps natural language intent to a session_spec with >0.85 confidence
- [ ] Frontend provides new-analysis chat screen and explore/metric picker as in MVP mockups

## Sprint 3 - Semantic Layer Construction

**Sprint Goal:** Transform governance manifest into a versioned semantic schema and provide edit/review APIs.

### Sprint Completion Checklist
- [ ] All P0 stories complete
- [ ] All blocking defects resolved
- [ ] Tests passing
- [ ] Demo-ready increment produced
- [ ] Milestone accepted

### Epic: Semantic Layer

#### Requirement / Feature: Semantic layer builder (manifest → cube_schema generator)

**Feature ID:** `F-011`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, agent`

**Rationale:** Stage 1 must convert governance output into a canonical semantic vocabulary for the UI and agents.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-007
- [ ] F-009

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-011-01: As a data platform engineer, I want an automated agent that converts a governance_manifest into a Cube.dev-compatible cube_schema JSON, so that downstream agents and the UI have a canonical, versioned semantic vocabulary to use.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - Given a valid governance_manifest JSON, the agent returns a cube_schema JSON that validates against the cube_schema JSON Schema (Cube.dev v0.35+) with no schema errors.
- [ ] - All measures in the manifest are converted to metric definitions with name, sql_expression, aggregation, description, allowed_filter_dimensions, and format fields populated or explicitly null when not inferable.
- [ ] - All dimensions/date/flag/geo columns are converted with name, sql_expression, data_type, hierarchy entries where applicable, and is_primary_date flagged where detected.
- [ ] - Join paths are enumerated from lineage_edges and annotated with join_type inferred from FK null_pct and include a note when left join is used due to null_pct > 2%.
- [ ] - Duplicate logical metrics across tables are deduplicated into a single metric definition; deduplication logic either emits a UNION or a prioritized source with a documented note.
- [ ] - Every metric and dimension receives a confidence tag (high|medium|low); definitions with 'low' are marked for human review and not allowed in ML feature candidates until accepted.
- [ ] - The agent persists the generated cube_schema into the workspace semantic Git repo under /semantic/{workspace_id}/schema/{semver_file}.json and increments semver according to the change rules (patch/minor/major).
- [ ] - All failures (validation, LLM non-JSON output, ambiguous joins, many-to-many) produce structured error objects logged and returned with remediation hints; no silent failures.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-011-01: - Implement the semantic generator agent service (backend AI orchestrator) that accepts governance_manifest and returns cube_schema JSON.
    - [ ] - Write the agent wrapper service skeleton with input validation and trace_id propagation.
    - [ ] - Implement system prompt per PRD (semantic layer architect) with strict output-only JSON instruction.
    - [ ] - Integrate with LLM client (claude-sonnet-4-6) with configurable timeouts, retries (exponential backoff), and rate-limit handling.
    - [ ] - Add a safe-parsing pipeline that rejects non-JSON, runs JSON Schema validation, and records parsing errors.
    - [ ] - Add structured logging for request/response metadata excluding PII and raw data samples (only stats).
- [ ] TASK-F-011-02: - Implement deterministic post-processing to enforce cube_schema shape and apply business rules (deduplication, join inference, confidence assignment).
    - [ ] - Implement metric deduplication algorithm: detect same logical metric by name/normalized definition/top_values and produce UNION or priority note.
    - [ ] - Implement join inference using lineage_edges + FK null_pct rules to set join_type and detect many-to-many edges.
    - [ ] - Implement automatic date hierarchy inference for date columns based on naming and distribution.
    - [ ] - Implement confidence assignment heuristics combining governance definition_confidence, profile stats, and rule-based checks.
    - [ ] - Implement semver delta calculator to determine patch/minor/major bump per PRD rules.
- [ ] TASK-F-011-03: - Implement JSON Schema validation and canonicalizer to enforce Cube.dev compatibility and deterministic ordering for git diffs.
    - [ ] - Source or author a Cube.dev v0.35+ JSON Schema and embed in service for validation.
    - [ ] - Implement canonical JSON ordering (stable key ordering) and whitespace-minified output for deterministic commits.
    - [ ] - Implement a post-validation fixer that surfaces non-fixable issues to the review queue with actionable messages.
    - [ ] - Add unit tests covering valid/invalid cube_schema samples and the canonicalizer behavior.
    - [ ] - Add validation metrics to monitoring (counts of pass/fail, validation latency).
- [ ] TASK-F-011-04: - Persist generated schema to workspace-scoped Git repository and atomically create a versioned file.
    - [ ] - Implement Git workspace interface (create branch/commit/tag) with workspace-scoped credentials stored in vault.
    - [ ] - Create commit message and semver file naming convention /semantic/{workspace_id}/schema/{explore_name}@{semver}.json.
    - [ ] - Implement rollback and idempotent write behavior to avoid duplicate commits on retries.
    - [ ] - Emit an internal audit event recording governance_manifest_version → semantic_layer_version mapping.
    - [ ] - Add observability: commit latency, success/failure counters, and last_commit metadata endpoints.
- [ ] TASK-F-011-05: - Add unit/integration tests and CI checks for the agent code path.
    - [ ] - Write unit tests for prompt->LLM wrapper to simulate LLM valid JSON, malformed JSON, and non-JSON responses.
    - [ ] - Write integration tests that run end-to-end with example governance_manifest fixtures and assert cube_schema validity and git commit.
    - [ ] - Add schema fuzz tests with edge-case governance manifests (missing lineage, PII flags present, extremely high null_pct).
    - [ ] - Add CI step to run JSON Schema validation and canonicalizer on PRs.
    - [ ] - Add test coverage threshold enforcement for new modules.
- [ ] TASK-F-011-06: - Implement observability, error handling, and security safeguards for the agent.
    - [ ] - Implement distributed tracing propagation and include trace_id in logs and agent responses.
    - [ ] - Implement structured error responses (error_code, severity, remediation_hint) and map to HTTP status codes.
    - [ ] - Ensure no raw table rows or credentials are sent to the LLM; only schema-level metadata and statistical summaries are passed.
    - [ ] - Add rate-limiting and circuit breaker around LLM calls to protect workspace budget.
    - [ ] - Add secrets access via Vault/AWS Secrets Manager; ensure no secrets logged.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - Unit tests for prompt composition, LLM wrapper, and JSON parsing.
- [ ] - Integration tests: feed sample governance_manifests and assert cube_schema fields (metrics/dimensions/joins/explores) and file persistence.
- [ ] - Schema validation tests using Cube.dev JSON Schema for a variety of manifests (happy path + edge cases).
- [ ] - Failure-mode tests: malformed LLM output, LLM timeouts, git failures — verify structured errors and retry/circuit-breaker behavior.
- [ ] - Load test: batch-generate 50 manifests concurrently to verify rate-limiting and queueing operates within SLA.
- [ ] - Security test: static analysis to ensure no secrets in logs and LLM inputs contain only metadata.
- [ ] - Acceptance test: manual QA via workspace dev UI to review generated schemas and confirm low-confidence items land in review queue.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - The semantic generator agent is deployed to dev and accepts governance_manifest fixtures via POST /internal/semantic/generate with authentication.
- [ ] - Generated cube_schema passes Cube.dev JSON Schema and the service writes deterministic, versioned files to the workspace Git repo.
- [ ] - All unit and integration tests pass in CI, and code coverage for new modules meets the threshold.
- [ ] - Error handling has documented error codes and retry semantics; invalid LLM outputs are captured and surfaced to the review queue.
- [ ] - Observability dashboards show validation pass rate, LLM latency, and git commit success rate; logs include trace_id for every run.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - F-007
- [ ] - F-009

###### Agent Notes
- Implement agent with strict system prompt per PRD and enforce output-only JSON. Use JSON Schema validation as a hard gate. Favor deterministic post-processing over relying on LLM to perfectly normalize names. If LLM returns ambiguous or missing fields, produce structured diagnostics with suggested human edits. Rate-limit calls and implement retries/backoff. Persist only canonicalized output to Git. Log prompt/response hashes (not full content) for audit and debugging. Always tag outputs with semantic_layer_version and governance_manifest_version.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-011-02: As a workspace admin and analyst, I want APIs and a simple review workflow to fetch, preview, edit, and accept/reject low-confidence semantic definitions produced by the generator, so that human review gates ensure ML only consumes high-confidence items.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - A GET API /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema returns the latest semantic schema list and versions with confidence tags for each item.
- [ ] - A POST API /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema/{version}/review accepts actions: accept, edit (with payload), reject; returns updated semantic schema version and audit entry.
- [ ] - Low-confidence definitions are discoverable via a Review Queue API /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/reviews and include the generated definition, raw rationale, and suggested edits.
- [ ] - Edits produce a new semver bump per PRD rules and trigger a re-validation run; accept sets confidence to high and makes metric available to ML feature selection.
- [ ] - Access control enforced: only workspace admins (or members with edit permission) can accept/reject; all actions recorded in audit log with actor + timestamp.
- [ ] - Frontend minimal review UI can list review items and call the review APIs; UI displays diffs and suggested remediation.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-011-07: - Design and implement REST API endpoints for semantic schema retrieval, review actions, and review queue.
    - [ ] - Define API contracts (OpenAPI) for GET /schema, GET /schema/{version}, GET /reviews, POST /schema/{version}/review.
    - [ ] - Implement backend handlers with input validation, auth checks (ABAC), and structured responses.
    - [ ] - Ensure APIs surface confidence, governance_manifest_version, diff links to git commit, and actionable remediation_hint fields.
    - [ ] - Implement pagination for review queue and filtering by confidence/severity.
    - [ ] - Add unit tests for endpoint input validation and permission checks.
- [ ] TASK-F-011-08: - Implement review actions that map to semantic Git operations and version bump logic.
    - [ ] - Implement accept action: update semantics file confidence flags, bump semver (patch/minor/major per rules), commit to Git.
    - [ ] - Implement edit action: accept edited payload, run deterministic canonicalizer and JSON Schema validator, create new semver commit or return validation errors.
    - [ ] - Implement reject action: mark definition as rejected, annotate with reason, and prevent ML inclusion until un-rejected.
    - [ ] - Emit audit event for each review action with actor, timestamp, and previous/next semantic_layer_version.
    - [ ] - Add rollback capability for accidental accepts (create revert commit).
- [ ] TASK-F-011-09: - Implement a lightweight frontend review queue view (UX + frontend API integration) to display low-confidence items and allow Accept/Edit/Reject.
    - [ ] - Add a Review Queue page component listing items with name, type, confidence, short definition, and risk badges.
    - [ ] - Implement diff viewer UI between generated and edited definitions (text + structured fields).
    - [ ] - Wire Accept/Edit/Reject buttons to backend APIs with optimistic UI updates and error handling.
    - [ ] - Add inline validation feedback for edits using the same Cube.dev JSON Schema client-side.
    - [ ] - Add unit/integration tests for the UI component and access control behavior.
- [ ] TASK-F-011-10: - Implement notifications and SSE events to surface review items to admins and to notify when review actions complete.
    - [ ] - Implement SSE topic /sse/workspaces/{workspace_id}/semantic to stream new_review and review_completed events.
    - [ ] - Hook Git commit events for new semantic versions to emit review_completed events once generated.
    - [ ] - Implement email/Slack notification job configuration for workspace admins (opt-in) on high-severity review items.
    - [ ] - Add retry/backoff for notification delivery and log notification failures.
    - [ ] - Add tests verifying event emission and consumer reception semantics.
- [ ] TASK-F-011-11: - Add security, access control, and audit logging for all review actions.
    - [ ] - Enforce ABAC: verify acting principal has role allowed to perform reviews; return 403 otherwise.
    - [ ] - Record every review API call in the workspace audit log with actor, action, payload_hash, and trace_id.
    - [ ] - Ensure review edits are sanitized and large text fields are size-limited to protect storage and performance.
    - [ ] - Add tests for permission enforcement and audit log entries.
    - [ ] - Rotate and store API keys and Git credentials via Vault; prevent exposure in logs.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - API contract tests against OpenAPI spec for all semantic endpoints.
- [ ] - Role-based access control tests verifying 403 for unauthorized roles and success for permitted roles.
- [ ] - End-to-end scenario tests: generator produces low-confidence items → appears in review queue → admin edits → new semver created → ML inclusion allowed.
- [ ] - UI integration tests (Cypress or Playwright) for review workflows including diff viewer and optimistic updates.
- [ ] - Security tests for injection and large-payload handling; ensure edits are sanitized and size-limited.
- [ ] - Observability tests to verify SSE streams and notification deliveries.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - Review APIs are implemented, secured, documented in OpenAPI, and pass end-to-end integration tests.
- [ ] - Frontend review queue lists low-confidence items and can Accept/Edit/Reject, producing semver-updated schema commits.
- [ ] - All review actions are audited and surfaced via SSE/notifications; permissions enforced and tested.
- [ ] - Human-reviewed items when Accepted are available to ML pipelines and marked with confidence: high.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - TASK-F-011-01
- [ ] - TASK-F-011-04

###### Agent Notes
- The review workflow must be deterministic and auditable. Do not allow LLM to autonomously flip low-confidence items to high confidence. All review edits should run through the same validator and canonicalizer as the generator output. Use semver bump rules from PRD to decide file naming and commit metadata. Emit clear remediation_hint in review items for humans (e.g., 'missing join path; confirm primary key'). Ensure the API never exposes database credentials or sample rows — only column-level stats and definition text.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-011-03: As a QA lead and data engineer, I want deterministic validators that detect join integrity issues (many-to-many, missing PK uniqueness, FK null_pct thresholds) and block or flag explores per PRD rules so that semantic layer definitions used by ML are safe and auditable.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - The validator examines governance_manifest.lineage_edges, column null_pct, and PK uniqueness information to detect many-to-many relationships and PK violations.
- [ ] - When a many-to-many or PK uniqueness violation is detected the semantic generator marks the affected explore as BLOCKED and includes a remediation_hint referencing offending tables/columns and suggested fixes.
- [ ] - FK null_pct > 2% leads to inferred left join with an explanatory note in cube_schema join_path metadata.
- [ ] - Validator outputs a deterministic DQ gate result object { gate_name, status: PASS|WARN|BLOCK, details } that's persisted to audit logs and returned in the generator response.
- [ ] - Validator tests include synthetic manifests that exercise each gate and assert correct PASS/WARN/BLOCK outcomes.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-011-12: - Implement the join & integrity validator module used both pre- and post-generator to enforce PRD gating rules.
    - [ ] - Parse lineage_edges and map FK relationships to column profile stats (null_pct, distinct_count).
    - [ ] - Implement many-to-many detection algorithm (both sides cardinality and repeated join targets) and mark blocked explores.
    - [ ] - Implement PK uniqueness check using governance_manifest PK uniqueness flags and table profile metrics; surface violations.
    - [ ] - Implement decision matrix for join_type inference (inner vs left) based on null_pct thresholds and produce explanatory notes.
    - [ ] - Unit test each rule with prepared governance_manifest fixtures.
- [ ] TASK-F-011-13: - Integrate validator into the generator pipeline and into the review API so gates run synchronously and results are persisted.
    - [ ] - Add validator invocation immediately after generator output and before Git commit; block commit if any BLOCK results present.
    - [ ] - Persist DQ gate results to audit store and include gate_id and status in the generator response payload.
    - [ ] - Emit human_readable remediation_hint for every BLOCK/WARN item with exact file/column references and suggested remediation steps.
    - [ ] - Ensure validator operates offline (deterministic) without additional LLM calls.
    - [ ] - Add integration tests verifying blocked commits and review queue entries for blocked explores.
- [ ] TASK-F-011-14: - Add frontend indicators and error messaging for blocked explores and join issues in the review UI and schema listing.
    - [ ] - Add BLOCK/WARN badges and remediation_hint text to schema listing and review queue items.
    - [ ] - For blocked explores, surface a 'Define bridge table' suggested action with a link to integration docs.
    - [ ] - Provide a compact view of offending lineage edges and sample stats for each issue (no raw rows).
    - [ ] - Add UI tests to assert badge visibility and that blocked items cannot be accepted until resolved.
    - [ ] - Add accessibility labels and ensure messages are readable by screen readers.
- [ ] TASK-F-011-15: - Add deterministic testing harness and CI checks for validator module.
    - [ ] - Create a suite of governance_manifest fixtures for pass/warn/block scenarios.
    - [ ] - Add CI job to run validator tests and fail PRs when regressions occur.
    - [ ] - Add mutation tests to ensure validator does not silently change behavior when code evolves.
    - [ ] - Measure validator latency and add perf regression alerts if thresholds exceeded.
    - [ ] - Include validator coverage in test reports.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - Unit tests for each validator rule with controlled governance_manifest inputs.
- [ ] - Integration tests where generator output triggers validator BLOCK and prevents commit; assert review queue item created.
- [ ] - End-to-end tests simulating human resolution: developer adds bridge table and reruns generator → validator PASS → commit succeeds.
- [ ] - Performance tests to ensure validator latency meets pipeline SLA (< 5s median for typical manifests).
- [ ] - Security/privilege tests ensuring validator cannot access raw data or credentials and only consumes manifest fields.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - Validator module implemented, covered by unit/integration tests, and integrated into the generator pipeline.
- [ ] - BLOCK/WARN results prevent automatic schema commit and create review queue entries with remediation hints.
- [ ] - CI runs include validator tests; PRs with validator regressions fail.
- [ ] - Frontend surfaces blocked explores and remediation steps; acceptance tests cover the flow.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - TASK-F-011-02
- [ ] - TASK-F-011-04

###### Agent Notes
- Keep the validator fully deterministic — do not call LLM. Use governance_manifest metrics only. Provide explicit remediation_hint strings that map to UI actions (e.g., 'Define bridge table between table_a.col_x and table_b.col_y to resolve many-to-many'). Ensure validator is idempotent and stable for auditability — any change to validator logic should increment validator_version stored with semantic_layer_version.

#### Requirement / Feature: Semantic layer storage & Git integration (workspace-scoped schema repo)

**Feature ID:** `F-012`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, infra`

**Rationale:** Versioned storage enables schema diffs, rollbacks, and auditability per spec.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-011

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-012-01: As a platform engineer, I want a workspace-scoped Git-backed schema repository that stores Cube.dev-compatible semantic schema files under /semantic/{workspace_id}/schema/, so that each workspace has a versioned, auditable semantic layer that downstream agents and APIs can read and update.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create a Git-backed storage location scoped per workspace under /semantic/{workspace_id}/schema/ in the configured Git host (S3-backed git repo or Git server).
- [ ] Expose backend API endpoints: GET /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema (list), GET /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema/{file} (retrieve), POST /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema (create/update), DELETE /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema/{file}.
- [ ] All created/updated schema files are valid Cube.dev v0.35+ YAML/JSON and pass server-side schema validation before commit.
- [ ] Commits are made with structured commit messages including governance_manifest_version, actor_id, and semver bump suggestion; commit metadata stored in Postgres artifact table for audit.
- [ ] Read APIs return schema file content plus commit metadata (commit_id, author, message, timestamp, semver).
- [ ] Repository access is isolated by workspace ACLs; operations reject when caller lacks workspace permission with 403.
- [ ] All operations are logged (structured events) and traced with trace_id; sensitive data (DB credentials) never recorded in logs.
- [ ] Operations are idempotent for identical payload+metadata; concurrent update conflicts are detected and surfaced as 409 with diff payload.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-012-01: Design Git-backed workspace schema storage service and API contract
    - [ ] Draft storage service architecture diagram showing Git adapter, workspace isolation, and Postgres commit metadata table
    - [ ] Define API request/response JSON schemas and error codes for schema file operations (list/get/create/update/delete)
    - [ ] Specify semver bump rules mapping (patch/minor/major) and metadata fields required in commit messages
    - [ ] Define validation rules for Cube.dev-compatible schema (YAML schema or JSON Schema) and failure response format
    - [ ] Create ticket for infra changes required (S3 buckets / Git server / credentials) and list required secrets
- [ ] TASK-F-012-02: Implement Git adapter service (backend) with workspace scoping
    - [ ] Create service module implementing clone/pull/commit/push with retries and exponential backoff
    - [ ] Implement per-workspace repository initialization (namespace path) and permission checks
    - [ ] Implement atomic commit flow: write file -> git add -> git commit -> git push, with rollback on failure
    - [ ] Add optimistic concurrency control: read current HEAD commit_id and require client-provided base_commit to prevent lost updates
    - [ ] Add instrumentation: structured logs, OpenTelemetry span creation per operation, and metrics for operation latency/errors
- [ ] TASK-F-012-03: Add server-side schema validation and YAML/JSON serialization
    - [ ] Integrate Cube.dev schema JSON Schema validator and add unit tests for valid/invalid examples
    - [ ] Implement canonical serialization: normalize ordering and whitespace to produce stable diffs
    - [ ] Validate semantic layer content for required fields (explore name, metrics, dimensions, joins) and confidence tags
    - [ ] Return detailed validation errors including file path, line/column (when possible), and machine-parsable error codes
    - [ ] Log validation failures as DQ events to audit log with governance_manifest_version linkage
- [ ] TASK-F-012-04: Implement backend API endpoints and controller logic
    - [ ] Implement GET list and GET file endpoints that read from local clone/cache with 5-minute cache TTL
    - [ ] Implement POST create/update that validates payload, computes semver suggestion, commits to Git, and writes commit metadata to Postgres
    - [ ] Implement DELETE endpoint with soft-delete option (mark deleted in metadata and commit a deletion), require Owner role
    - [ ] Return structured responses including commit metadata and suggested semver increment when applicable
    - [ ] Add unit and integration tests for each endpoint, including permission and error cases
- [ ] TASK-F-012-05: Implement access control, secrets, and repo provisioning in infra (DevOps)
    - [ ] Provision per-environment Git host or S3-backed git repository and store credentials in secrets manager (Vault/AWS SM)
    - [ ] Implement workspace-level deploy keys/secrets with rotation policy and document the process
    - [ ] Enforce network egress rules so Git pushes use a NAT or VPC endpoint per environment
    - [ ] Add CI job to provision new workspace repo during workspace creation flow
    - [ ] Create runbook for recovery: repo re-init, reapply commits from metadata, and reconcile missing commits
- [ ] TASK-F-012-06: Add observability, logging, and auditing for all schema repo operations
    - [ ] Emit structured audit events to audit log table for create/update/delete with actor, workspace_id, governance_manifest_version, commit_id
    - [ ] Add metrics: ops/sec, commit failures, validation failures, push latency; expose to Grafana dashboard
    - [ ] Add OpenTelemetry traces propagated from orchestrator trace_id to Git adapter spans
    - [ ] Implement alerting: >5 validation failures per hour or >3 push failures per 10 minutes trigger PagerDuty
    - [ ] Create dashboard documenting recent commit history per workspace and top validation error types

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for validation and API controllers covering success and error paths
- [ ] Integration tests against ephemeral Git host (in CI) exercising clone/commit/push/pull
- [ ] Contract tests ensuring API responses match OpenAPI schema
- [ ] Permission tests verifying Owner/Editor/Viewer roles and rejection behavior
- [ ] Load test: simulate 50 concurrent commits to same workspace to validate conflict handling and rate limits
- [ ] Chaos test: simulate Git host flaps and verify retries and error propagation
- [ ] Manual QA: create/update/delete flows via Postman and UI smoke test against staging

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] All API endpoints implemented, documented (OpenAPI), and passing unit + integration tests
- [ ] Git adapter tested end-to-end in staging: commit, push, fetch, and conflict scenarios covered
- [ ] Server-side validation enforces Cube.dev schema compatibility and rejects invalid commits with clear errors
- [ ] Postgres commit metadata table populated correctly and queryable for audit and UI
- [ ] RBAC enforced and verified with automated permission tests; unauthorized actions return 403
- [ ] Operational runbook and IaC changes merged; secrets stored in secrets manager
- [ ] Observability: logs, metrics, traces, and alerts in place with dashboards
- [ ] Code reviewed and merged; feature flagged off by default and enabled for a pilot workspace

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-011
- [ ] Postgres metadata table and audit log schema (infra)
- [ ] Secrets manager setup for Git host credentials

###### Agent Notes
- The semantic layer agent (Stage 1) will call the POST endpoint with cube_schema JSON. The server must validate Cube schema deterministically before committing. Provide clear machine-readable validation error codes so agent can surface low-confidence items to Review Queue. Ensure the API accepts a governance_manifest_version in the payload to link commits to upstream F-011 output. Implement idempotency by requiring base_commit and computing commit hash; return 409 with diff when head changed.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-012-02: As a data engineer, I want semantic schema file versioning with semver rules, diffs, and rollback APIs so that I can audit changes, perform rollbacks, and trigger dependent artifact re-validation when major changes occur.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each commit to the workspace schema repo produces a stored semver version derived from rules: patch for definition text changes, minor for new metric/dimension added, major for metric rename/aggregation/join type change.
- [ ] Provide APIs: GET /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/versions (list semver history), POST /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/versions/{version}/rollback to rollback to a prior version, and GET /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/diff?from={v1}&to={v2} returning machine-parsable diff.
- [ ] Rollback operation creates a new commit (not destructive) and records rollback reason, actor_id, and governed_manifest_version; rollback does not delete history.
- [ ] Major version bumps automatically enqueue re-validation jobs for dependent artifacts and mark artifacts as requiring human review; job enqueue events include artifact ids.
- [ ] Diff API supports YAML-aware diffs (semantic diffs), not raw text diffs, and highlights metric/dimension/join changes with structured change types.
- [ ] All versioning operations adhere to workspace ACLs and are logged to audit table with traceability.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-012-07: Implement semver computation engine and change-type classifier
    - [ ] Define deterministic rules mapping file diffs to semver bump categories (patch/minor/major) and document edge cases
    - [ ] Implement diff parser that loads old/new Cube schema into AST and classifies changes (metric added/removed/renamed, aggregation changed, join type changed)
    - [ ] Write unit tests for each change classification rule with representative schema pairs
    - [ ] Expose a library function computeSemverBump(oldSchema, newSchema) returning {bump: 'patch'|'minor'|'major', reasons: []}
    - [ ] Add telemetry to emit classification metrics for later analysis
- [ ] TASK-F-012-08: Implement versions API and machine-parsable semantic diff endpoint
    - [ ] Implement GET versions endpoint reading commit history and mapping commit metadata to semver tags
    - [ ] Implement GET diff endpoint that returns structured JSON diff: [{type: 'metric_added', name, before, after, path, confidence_change}]
    - [ ] Optimize diff generation: on large repos, compute diff from commit patch (use cached AST snapshots if available)
    - [ ] Add unit and integration tests for diff content correctness and performance
    - [ ] Document API schema and include example responses
- [ ] TASK-F-012-09: Implement rollback API behavior and safety checks
    - [ ] Implement POST rollback that creates a new commit restoring files to chosen version snapshot and records rollback metadata
    - [ ] Add pre-rollback checks: prevent rollback if there are unmerged changes by other actors unless force=true and owner role
    - [ ] Enqueue re-validation and artifact impact analysis jobs after rollback; return job ids in response
    - [ ] Add tests covering forced and non-forced rollback, owner/non-owner paths, and rollback audit entries
    - [ ] Add confirmation email / notification to workspace admins when rollback occurs (configurable)
- [ ] TASK-F-012-10: Implement artifact re-validation trigger and dependency graph integration
    - [ ] Integrate with artifact metadata store to find artifacts depending on changed metrics/dimensions using precomputed dependency indexes
    - [ ] Implement enqueue job that triggers deterministic artifact re-validation pipeline (non-blocking) and records re-validation request in audit log
    - [ ] Add throttling logic to avoid enqueuing floods when multiple commits occur in quick succession (debounce window 2m)
