    - [ ] Implement test harness that simulates a major change and validates that dependent artifacts are enqueued for re-validation
    - [ ] Expose endpoint to query re-validation status per artifact
- [ ] TASK-F-012-11: Add UI hooks for version history and rollback (backend support + minimal frontend)
    - [ ] Add backend endpoints required by UI for list/diff/rollback (already planned) and add CORS + auth policies
    - [ ] Create minimal frontend prototype (workspace admin console) that lists versions and shows semantic diff with human-friendly highlights
    - [ ] Implement rollback flow UI with confirmation modal and reason input, showing affected artifacts count
    - [ ] Add frontend unit tests and accessibility checks for the rollback modal
    - [ ] Instrument UI actions to emit audit events and add to workspace activity feed

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for semver classification using a corpus of curated schema diffs
- [ ] Integration tests: commit sequence producing patch/minor/major and verifying resulting semver tags
- [ ] End-to-end test: major change triggers re-validation job and marks artifacts as needing review
- [ ] Permission tests for rollback API verifying only Owner can force rollback
- [ ] Load tests: simulate 100 version listings and 10 diff requests per second
- [ ] Manual QA: admin walks through version history, inspects diffs, and performs rollback on staging

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Semver bump computation is deterministic and covered by unit tests
- [ ] Diff API returns YAML-aware structured diffs and is validated by integration tests
- [ ] Rollback creates a new commit and enqueues re-validation jobs; audit entries recorded
- [ ] Dependent artifacts are correctly discovered and re-validation jobs enqueued for major changes
- [ ] Frontend prototype for version history/diff/rollback integrated with backend and smoke-tested in staging
- [ ] Documentation updated: versioning rules, rollback runbook, and API examples
- [ ] Security review completed for rollback endpoint to prevent privilege escalation

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] US-F-012-01
- [ ] Artifact metadata and dependency index service
- [ ] Job queue (Redis + BullMQ) for re-validation jobs

###### Agent Notes
- When computing semver bump, the semantic layer agent should receive the computed bump and change reasons in the POST response so it can annotate the governance_manifest linkage. Re-validation jobs must accept a payload: {workspace_id, changed_paths, change_types, trigger_commit_id} and produce deterministic dq_gate results. Provide idempotent job handling and idempotency keys to avoid duplicate re-validation triggers when CI pipelines or automated agents retry commits.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-012-03: As a workspace admin/reviewer, I want low-confidence or conflicting semantic definitions to surface in a human review workflow with edit/accept/reject actions, so that no ML pipeline consumes low-confidence definitions without explicit acceptance and conflicts are resolved before promotion.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Schema files carry confidence flags per definition (high|medium|low) and commits that include low-confidence items cause those items to be flagged in the Review Queue API.
- [ ] When two definitions for the same metric name conflict, the commit is accepted but both definitions are flagged as conflicting and an unresolved conflict entry is created in Review Queue with diffs and actor ids.
- [ ] Expose Review Queue APIs: GET /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/review-queue (list), POST /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/review-queue/{item_id}/action with actions accept|edit|reject and reason payload.
- [ ] Accept action marks definition as high-confidence and updates schema (create new semver-patched commit) and logs audit entry; edit opens a temporary draft file allowing reviewer edits and validation before commit.
- [ ] Reject action removes the definition from active schema by creating a patch commit that deletes or disables the item and records reviewer comment; rejected items cannot be used in ML until reintroduced and accepted.
- [ ] No ML pipeline (Stage 3+) may use low-confidence or conflicting definitions unless explicit acceptance event exists in audit log; enforcement is validated by automated tests.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-012-12: Design Review Queue data model and APIs
    - [ ] Design DB schema for review_queue table with fields: item_id, workspace_id, commit_id, file_path, change_type, conflict_with (nullable), created_by, created_at, details (json), status
    - [ ] Design API contract for listing, filtering by status/confidence/type, and performing actions (accept/edit/reject)
    - [ ] Define UI payload for editor integration: draft content, validation errors, suggested fixes
    - [ ] Define retention policy for review items and audit linking to commit history
    - [ ] Document RBAC rules: who can resolve (workspace admin vs assigned reviewers)
- [ ] TASK-F-012-13: Implement review queue backend and isolation rules
    - [ ] Implement creation of review items during commit validation when low-confidence or conflicts detected
    - [ ] Implement GET list and POST action endpoints with permission checks and input validation
    - [ ] Implement edit flow: create draft branch or staged file, validate edits, commit as new patch with reviewer as author
    - [ ] Implement accept flow: mark item accepted, update confidence flags in schema, commit a patch and annotate commit message
    - [ ] Implement reject flow: create patch disabling metric/dimension (e.g. add disabled: true) and record reason in audit
- [ ] TASK-F-012-14: Integrate review queue with ML pipeline enforcement (deterministic checks)
    - [ ] Add pre-flight check in session start and Stage 3 feature selection that queries review queue for used definitions and blocks pipeline if unresolved low-confidence items are referenced
    - [ ] Implement deterministic error response with remediation payload specifying offending definitions and review links
    - [ ] Add automated tests that attempt to start a pipeline referencing low-confidence/conflict items and expect human_required events
    - [ ] Document pipeline behavior for users and include UI links to review queue items in the human_required SSE event payload
    - [ ] Add logging to track how often pipelines blocked due to review items
- [ ] TASK-F-012-15: Implement minimal frontend Review Queue UI (backend + frontend tasks)
    - [ ] Create Review Queue list page showing item summary, diff view, affected artifacts count, and action buttons
    - [ ] Implement diff viewer highlighting semantic changes (metric rename, aggregation change, join type change)
    - [ ] Implement edit modal with YAML editor, validation on-save, and preview of semver impact
    - [ ] Implement accessibility and keyboard navigation for the review list and modals
    - [ ] Add client-side tests and storybook entries for review components
- [ ] TASK-F-012-16: Add tests, logging, and monitoring for review workflow operations
    - [ ] Unit tests for review item creation logic and state transitions (open -> accepted/edited/rejected)
    - [ ] Integration tests for full edit->commit->pipeline unblock flow
    - [ ] Add audit log entries for all reviewer actions with reason and resulting commit_id
    - [ ] Add metrics for review queue size, time-to-resolve, and blocked pipelines count
    - [ ] Set alert when median time-to-resolve > 48 hours for production workspaces

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for review queue state machine and API authorization
- [ ] Integration tests: commit with low-confidence item creates review item; reviewer accepts and pipeline can then consume definition
- [ ] End-to-end test using SSE: start session that references low-confidence def, verify human_required event with review link
- [ ] UI manual QA: reviewer edits YAML, sees validation errors, accepts, and verifies commit history
- [ ] Security test: ensure users without reviewer role cannot perform accept/reject actions
- [ ] Performance test: stress review queue with 500 items and measure listing latency

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Review Queue persists items for low-confidence and conflict cases and exposes APIs for actions
- [ ] UI allows reviewers to accept/edit/reject items; edits validated before commit
- [ ] ML pipelines blocked when referencing unresolved items and SSE human_required messages include review links
- [ ] All reviewer actions create atomic commits and audit log entries linking reviewer, reason, and commit
- [ ] Automated test coverage for enforcement and resolution flows
- [ ] Operational monitoring in place (queue size, resolution time) and runbooks for long-blocked items

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] US-F-012-01
- [ ] Semantic layer validation library
- [ ] Session orchestration pipeline hooks for human_required events

###### Agent Notes
- The semantic layer agent should attach definition confidence and origin (governance_manifest_version) to each metric/dimension when producing schema. When conflicts are detected, agent should still produce both variants in a conflict marker so Review Queue can present side-by-side diffs. SSE events for human_required must include the review_item_id and a pre-signed reviewer URL (short-lived) to reduce friction. Ensure the agent does not attempt automated acceptance — human action required for low confidence.

#### Requirement / Feature: Explore editor API (create/edit/validate cube schema)

**Feature ID:** `F-013`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, api`

**Rationale:** APIs allow UI to present and edit explores and metrics as required by MVP.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-011
- [ ] F-012

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-013-01: As a data engineer, I want a stable REST API to create a new explore (cube schema file) in the workspace semantic repo, so that UI and automation can add explores programmatically and version them.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] API accepts a cube-schema JSON payload (cube.dev-compatible) and returns 201 + semantic_schema_version on success
- [ ] API validates payload against server-side JSON Schema and returns descriptive 4xx errors for invalid fields
- [ ] On success the schema file is persisted to the workspace semantic git repo under /semantic/{workspace_id}/schema/{explore_name}.yml and committed with semver tagging
- [ ] New explore is assigned confidence: low|medium|high; low-confidence items are flagged in metadata and cannot be used for ML until human review
- [ ] Action is logged in pipeline audit log with actor, trace_id, input_hash, and commit sha
- [ ] Endpoint enforces RBAC: only users with 'semantic_editor' or workspace Admin role can create explores

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-013-01: Design API contract and JSON Schema validation for Create Explore
    - [ ] Draft OpenAPI spec for POST /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/explores
    - [ ] Define request/response shapes including semantic_schema_version, commit_sha, confidence flags
    - [ ] Write JSON Schema for cube.dev-compatible fields required by MVP (metrics, dimensions, join_paths, explores metadata)
    - [ ] Add examples and error payload schema for 400/401/403/409/500
    - [ ] Peer-review API contract with backend and product
- [ ] TASK-F-013-02: Implement backend endpoint to accept, validate, persist, and version new explore
    - [ ] Implement controller to parse request, authenticate user, check RBAC
    - [ ] Apply JSON Schema validation and return structured error messages with error codes
    - [ ] On pass, write YAML/JSON file to workspace semantic repo path and create git commit
    - [ ] Bump semver: determine patch/minor/major rules and store new semantic_schema_version
    - [ ] Return 201 with { semantic_schema_version, commit_sha, file_path }
- [ ] TASK-F-013-03: Integrate audit logging and tracing for Create Explore
    - [ ] Emit structured audit event to pipeline audit log: { actor, action:create_explore, workspace_id, explore_name, input_hash }
    - [ ] Attach trace_id to logs and propagate to downstream jobs
    - [ ] Log validation errors with sanitized payload diffs (no PII, no credentials)
    - [ ] Add Prometheus metrics: semantic_api_create_requests_total, semantic_api_create_errors_total, create_latency_seconds
- [ ] TASK-F-013-04: Write frontend client and UI contract for create explore flow (API consumer)
    - [ ] Define minimal frontend JSON payload builder that maps UI fields to cube schema fields
    - [ ] Implement API client method with error handling and retry/backoff for 409 conflicts
    - [ ] Show user-friendly validation messages mapped from backend error codes
    - [ ] Add unit tests for client mapping and error case rendering
- [ ] TASK-F-013-05: Security: implement authorization, input sanitization, and secrets handling
    - [ ] Validate JWT and ensure RBAC check uses ABAC policy for resource workspace_id
    - [ ] Sanitize inputs to prevent path traversal and YAML/JSON injection
    - [ ] Prevent commit messages or schema fields from containing secrets or credentials (validation rule)
    - [ ] Add automated static analyzer rule for API inputs and run in CI
- [ ] TASK-F-013-06: Testing: unit, integration and API contract tests
    - [ ] Add unit tests for validation logic with positive and negative cases
    - [ ] Add integration tests that spin up a test repo, call endpoint and verify git commit and semver bump
    - [ ] Add contract tests to assert OpenAPI responses and error schemas
    - [ ] Create fuzz/negative tests for malicious payloads and ensure 4xx responses

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests: validate request schema, RBAC gate, semver bump logic
- [ ] Integration: call POST with valid schema -> assert 201, file exists, commit sha in repo, audit event emitted
- [ ] Negative: send invalid schema -> assert 400 with field-level errors
- [ ] Conflict: create explore with same name concurrently -> assert 409 conflict and safe retry guidance
- [ ] Security: call without auth -> assert 401; call with insufficient role -> assert 403
- [ ] Performance: 95th percentile latency < 500ms for in-memory validation; end-to-end persistence < 3s (git commit included) in CI
- [ ] Regression: run OpenAPI contract tests to ensure no breaking response changes

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] POST API implemented and merged to main
- [ ] OpenAPI docs updated and published
- [ ] Automated JSON Schema validation in place and tested
- [ ] New explore files persisted to semantic repo with semver commits
- [ ] RBAC enforced and security checks in place
- [ ] Unit and integration tests passing in CI with >=90% coverage on new code
- [ ] Audit logging and Prometheus metrics emitting
- [ ] Feature demoed and accepted by product owner

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-011
- [ ] F-012

###### Agent Notes
- API must validate cube.dev-compatible schema subset. Use deterministic server-side JSON Schema. Persist files to workspace-scoped git repo; enforce semver rules: patch for doc/description edits, minor for added metric/dimension, major for breaking changes (rename, aggregation change, join type change). Flag low-confidence fields with human_review_required=true. Do not accept any credentials/connection strings in payload. Emit audit event with input_hash and commit_sha. Propagate trace_id in headers for observability.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-013-02: As an analyst using the explore editor UI, I want to edit an existing explore via an API (patch/replace) and preview validation results, so that I can iterate on metric definitions safely and see whether changes will break downstream artifacts.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] API supports PATCH /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/explores/{explore_name} and PUT for full replace
- [ ] API returns validation report including: schema_validation_pass, join_integrity_warnings, many_to_many_flag, affected_artifacts_count, semantic_version_change(recommended)
- [ ] If change is major-breaking per semver rules, response includes a list of artifacts and models that require re-validation
- [ ] Concurrent edits produce deterministic conflict responses (409) with server-provided diff and suggested merge steps
- [ ] Edit action is audited and logged, with pre-change snapshot stored as previous semver version

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-013-07: Design API contract for edit (PATCH/PUT) and validation preview endpoint
    - [ ] Add OpenAPI entries for PATCH and PUT and a validation preview query param (?preview=true)
    - [ ] Define validation_report JSON schema returned by preview
    - [ ] Specify HTTP 409 diff response format for concurrent edits
    - [ ] Document semver impact logic and fields returned to client
- [ ] TASK-F-013-08: Implement backend edit handlers with dry-run validation and impact analysis
    - [ ] Implement PATCH/PUT handlers with RBAC and input validation
    - [ ] Implement dry-run validator that checks: JSON Schema, join path integrity, many-to-many detection, metric name collisions, aggregation changes
    - [ ] Compute affected_artifacts by scanning artifact metadata for references to changed metric/dimension names
    - [ ] Return validation_report with actionable warnings and recommended semver bump
    - [ ] If preview=false, persist commit and bump semver accordingly
- [ ] TASK-F-013-09: Implement conflict detection and three-way merge guidance
    - [ ] Lock explore file per edit transaction to detect race conditions
    - [ ] On conflict, compute line-based YAML diff and return structured diff with suggestions (accept remote, accept local, manual merge)
    - [ ] Offer server-side auto-merge for non-overlapping edits and return merged commit_sha
    - [ ] Add retry guidance headers and idempotency token support
- [ ] TASK-F-013-10: Frontend: expose edit UI behaviors and preview validation report
    - [ ] Implement edit form that calls preview endpoint and renders validation_report checklist
    - [ ] Show affected artifacts list with links and severity badges (warn/critical)
    - [ ] Show semver change recommendation and require user confirmation for major changes
    - [ ] Handle 409 conflict flows with a UI diff viewer and merge buttons
- [ ] TASK-F-013-11: Testing: validation, impact analysis, and conflict scenarios
    - [ ] Unit tests for validation rules and affected_artifacts computation
    - [ ] Integration test for preview=true which validates but does not persist
    - [ ] Simulate concurrent edit race: assert 409 and diff payload returned
    - [ ] E2E test: edit that triggers semver major -> UI requires confirmation, artifacts flagged
- [ ] TASK-F-013-12: DevOps & backup: semantic repo snapshot and rollback hooks
    - [ ] Implement automatic backup of repo before major-change commits
    - [ ] Expose rollback API for last n commits for workspace admins
    - [ ] Add CI job to run semantic schema validation on every commit to the repo

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests: validation rules for aggregation change detection and join integrity
- [ ] Integration: preview endpoint returns warnings but does not commit; then run same call with preview=false and assert commit created
- [ ] Conflict test: two parallel edit requests -> one succeeds, other receives 409 with structured diff
- [ ] Impact test: rename metric referenced by artifact -> preview shows affected_artifacts_count > 0 and lists artifact ids
- [ ] Security test: ensure only allowed roles can perform edits and semver-major commits require elevated permission
- [ ] Performance: validation preview response time P95 < 1s for typical explore size (<= 200 fields)

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] PATCH/PUT and preview endpoints implemented and merged
- [ ] Preview validation_report covers JSON Schema, joins, many-to-many, collisions, and affected artifacts
- [ ] Frontend renders preview with clear warnings and merge UI for conflicts
- [ ] Concurrent edit conflicts resolved via 409 + diff response
- [ ] Semver bump logic applied correctly and previous versions retained
- [ ] Automated tests and CI checks added; semantic repo commit hooks enabled
- [ ] Documentation updated with editing and rollback procedures

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] US-F-013-01
- [ ] F-011
- [ ] F-012

###### Agent Notes
- Dry-run validation must be deterministic and not call LLMs. Use cached semantic layer and artifact metadata to compute affected artifacts. Many-to-many detection rules: if a join path would produce fan-out (based on FK estimated_cardinality and null_pct thresholds from governance manifest), flag as blocking. For semver decision, follow spec: description changes -> patch; add metric/dim -> minor; rename/aggregation/join_type change -> major. Keep previous versions accessible and record who approved major changes; major commits should require an 'approved_by' header or elevated role. Emit audit events for preview and commit attempts.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-013-03: As a QA lead, I want a validation API that verifies a full workspace semantic schema and runs deterministic checks (schema conformance, join integrity, metric collisions, many-to-many detection), so that CI and human reviewers can surface issues before artifacts use the schema.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Endpoint exists: POST /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/validate that accepts either the workspace schema or a single explore and returns a structured validation_report
- [ ] Validation report includes granular rule results with severity (PASS|WARN|BLOCK), file/line references where possible, and suggested remediation steps
- [ ] Validator is deterministic, idempotent, and runs in CI with exit codes reflecting PASS vs BLOCK
- [ ] Validation returns a machine-readable SARIF-like output to support UI diffing and IDE integration
- [ ] All BLOCK findings must prevent CI merge and raise an actionable human_review_required flag in the response

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-013-13: Design and implement the validation rules engine
    - [ ] Codify rules from PRD: JSON Schema conformance, join integrity, many-to-many detection, metric name collisions, FK null_pct thresholds, PII presence in measures
    - [ ] Implement rule severity mapping (PASS|WARN|BLOCK) and remediation texts
    - [ ] Implement deterministic rule execution with stable ordering and idempotent outputs
    - [ ] Expose plugin hook points for future LLM-aided suggestions (but keep core deterministic)
- [ ] TASK-F-013-14: Implement validation API and CI integration
    - [ ] Create POST /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/validate that accepts repo snapshot or file path
    - [ ] Make endpoint return validation_report JSON and SARIF-compatible output option
    - [ ] Integrate validator into repo git commit hook and CI pipeline to block merges on BLOCK findings
    - [ ] Provide CLI tool for local validation referencing workspace config
- [ ] TASK-F-013-15: Implement detailed error reporting and line/file mapping
    - [ ] When validating YAML files, compute file/line ranges for flagged properties using YAML AST parsing
    - [ ] Include sample offending YAML snippet in remediation payloads (sanitized)
    - [ ] Add links to governance manifest entries and artifact references for impacted items
    - [ ] Ensure error payloads are safe to display in UI (no secrets)
- [ ] TASK-F-013-16: Testing: validation engine correctness and CI blocking behavior
    - [ ] Unit tests for each rule with PASS/WARN/BLOCK cases
    - [ ] Integration test: run validator against known-bad workspace snapshot and assert CI exit code non-zero
    - [ ] SARIF output tests: assert fields present and parsable by SARIF viewers
    - [ ] Fuzz test: feed malformed YAML/JSON and ensure validator returns structured BLOCK rather than crash
- [ ] TASK-F-013-17: Observability and telemetry for validator runs
    - [ ] Emit metrics: validation_runs_total, validation_block_findings_total, validation_duration_seconds
    - [ ] Log validation runs with trace_id and store reports in audit log for 90 days
    - [ ] Add a small dashboard for validation health and top failing rules

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests: each rule exercised with PASS/WARN/BLOCK expectations
- [ ] Integration: validate full workspace schema with intentionally introduced many-to-many join -> expect BLOCK and remediation instructions
- [ ] CI simulation: run validator in CI mode and assert non-zero exit for BLOCK and zero for PASS/WARN-only
- [ ] SARIF validation: ensure SARIF output loads into SARIF viewer and errors map to file/line
- [ ] Load test: run validator on large workspace (<= 100 explores) and assert P95 runtime under 5s for in-memory checks
- [ ] Security: ensure validator never outputs sensitive values from schema (run redaction tests)

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Validation engine implemented and merged
- [ ] API endpoint and CLI available for workspace-level and per-file validation
- [ ] CI pipeline blocks merges on BLOCK findings by consuming validator exit code
- [ ] SARIF-compatible output produced for IDE/CI integration
- [ ] Comprehensive unit/integration tests for rules, with >=95% coverage on new code
- [ ] Validation runs logged and metrics exposed
- [ ] Documentation for validation rules and remediation steps published

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] US-F-013-01
- [ ] US-F-013-02
- [ ] F-011
- [ ] F-012

###### Agent Notes
- Validator must be deterministic and not call any LLM. Rules are derived from PRD tables (many-to-many, FK null_pct > threshold => left join, PK uniqueness from governance manifest). Provide SARIF output to enable IDE integrations and PR annotations. Integrate with git hooks and CI to block merges on BLOCK findings. Keep future hook points for optional LLM suggestions but ensure suggestions are separate and marked as such.

#### Requirement / Feature: Human review queue backend (low-confidence definitions)

**Feature ID:** `F-014`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend`

**Rationale:** Low-confidence semantic items must be reviewable before ML use per PRD.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-013
- [ ] F-007

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-014-01: As a workspace admin, I want all low-confidence semantic definitions surfaced in a persistent review queue with APIs to list, accept, edit, and reject items, so that humans can validate or correct them before they are used by ML pipelines.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - AC1: A persistent review_queue DB table exists with references to governance_manifest entries and semantic definition ids, status (pending/accepted/edited/rejected), created_at, updated_at, reviewer_id, workspace_id, and commit_id (nullable) recorded.
- [ ] - AC2: Backend REST APIs exist: GET /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/review-queue (list with filters), GET /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/review-queue/{id}, POST /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/review-queue/{id}/accept, POST /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/review-queue/{id}/reject, POST /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/review-queue/{id}/edit and respond with standardized JSON (200/400/403/404).
- [ ] - AC3: Accept action creates a new semantic schema commit in the workspace Git repo (semantic/{workspace_id}/schema/...), bumps semantic_layer_version semver (patch for text changes), and returns commit_id in API response.
- [ ] - AC4: Edit action validates the edited definition via the existing cube_schema JSON schema; validation errors return 400 with field-level error messages and are logged.
- [ ] - AC5: Reject action marks the definition rejected and prevents ML pipeline uses until re-submitted; API returns a human-readable reason and records audit log entry.
- [ ] - AC6: All API calls enforce ABAC: only workspace members with review permission (role: Admin or Editor as configured) can mutate; GET is allowed to workspace members with Viewer role. Unauthorized requests return 403.
- [ ] - AC7: All operations write structured audit logs with: actor_id, action, target_definition_id, workspace_id, before_payload (truncated), after_payload (truncated), timestamp, trace_id.
- [ ] - AC8: Unit and integration tests cover CRUD paths, validation failures, permission checks, and git commit success/failure error handling with >= 90% coverage on new code.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-014-01: Design DB schema & migration for review queue and audit records
    - [ ] - Create SQL migration to add table semantic_review_queue (id uuid PK, workspace_id, definition_ref, manifest_version, definition_type, proposed_text, current_text, status enum, created_by, created_at, updated_at, review_reason, commit_id nullable, metadata jsonb).
    - [ ] - Create SQL migration to add table semantic_review_audit (id uuid PK, review_id FK, actor_id, action, payload jsonb, timestamp, trace_id).
    - [ ] - Add indexes on workspace_id, status, and created_at; add FK constraints to governance_manifest table if present.
    - [ ] - Add DB tests (migration sanity run, rollback test) to CI migration pipeline.
    - [ ] - Add repository changelog entry and DB deploy plan for rollout with backfill strategy for existing low-confidence items.
- [ ] TASK-F-014-02: Implement review-queue REST API endpoints and handlers
    - [ ] - Implement controllers for list, get, accept, edit, reject with input validation and JSON Schema for request bodies.
    - [ ] - Implement service layer functions that perform DB transactions, state transitions, and return standardized responses (including commit_id on accept).
    - [ ] - Add concurrency protection: optimistic locking via row_version/timestamp to prevent double-accept edits (return 409 on conflict).
    - [ ] - Implement structured logging at debug/info/error levels (include trace_id) and consistent error codes for clients.
    - [ ] - Add unit tests for handler logic (happy path + error cases) and integration tests hitting a test DB instance.
- [ ] TASK-F-014-03: Integrate with workspace Git-backed semantic schema (commit on accept/edit)
    - [ ] - Implement a Git composer service that writes updated Cube.dev YAML/JSON into workspace semantic repo path semantic/{workspace_id}/schema/{explore}.yml and performs commit+push under a service account key.
    - [ ] - On accept: generate commit message, bump semver (patch for text, minor if new metric/dimension), sign commit metadata with actor_id and trace_id, store commit_id in review record.
    - [ ] - On edit: write edited definition to a temp branch and run cube_schema validator before committing; on validation failure, surface errors to API caller and do not commit.
    - [ ] - Handle Git failures with retries (3 attempts with exponential backoff) and atomic DB transaction rollback if commit fails.
    - [ ] - Add integration tests that use a local Git repo fixture to assert file writes, semver bumps, and commit_id persistence.
- [ ] TASK-F-014-04: Implement server-side validation & conflict detection
    - [ ] - Reuse JSON Schema validator for cube_schema format to validate any edited/accepted definitions.
    - [ ] - Implement deduplication/conflict detection: if two definitions claim the same metric name but with different sql_expression, flag conflict and set status=conflict and prevent accept until resolved.
    - [ ] - Return a structured diff payload via API for conflict resolution containing both versions and highlighted fields.
    - [ ] - Add unit tests covering validation rules and conflict detection logic.
- [ ] TASK-F-014-05: Expose API hooks for frontend/UI and minimal backend notifications
    - [ ] - Add paginated list response with filtering parameters (status, explore, created_by, since) and stable sort by created_at desc.
    - [ ] - Add websocket/SSE notification event emission (human_review_required, review_updated) or publish to Redis channel for UI real-time updates.
    - [ ] - Document API contract in OpenAPI and update swagger with examples and error codes.
    - [ ] - Add integration tests that subscribe to SSE channel and verify events emitted on accept/edit/reject actions.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - Unit tests: service layer, validators, conflict detector; assert expected DB state transitions and error codes.
- [ ] - Integration tests: API routes against test DB and local Git repo; verify commit_id returned and file written to repo.
- [ ] - Permission tests: call endpoints with Viewer/Editor/Admin tokens and assert 403 for unauthorized actions.
- [ ] - Concurrency tests: attempt double-accept with two parallel requests and assert one succeeds and other returns 409.
- [ ] - Failure injection: simulate Git push failure and verify DB rollback and error returned to client.
- [ ] - Performance: list endpoint returns first 100 items under 200ms on test data of 10k review items (measure and tune indexes).

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - All DB migrations applied without data loss in test environment and migration tests pass.
- [ ] - REST API endpoints implemented, documented (OpenAPI), and passing unit and integration tests.
- [ ] - Git commit integration writes schema files and returns commit_id on accept; commit failures handled gracefully.
- [ ] - Validation and conflict detection run on edit/accept; invalid edits return 400 with field-level messages.
- [ ] - RBAC enforced for each endpoint and covered by tests.
- [ ] - Structured audit logs emitted and persisted for every state change.
- [ ] - CI pipeline runs and new tests meet coverage bar; feature gated behind a config flag.
- [ ] - Ops runbook updated for deploying migration and rollback steps.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-013
- [ ] F-007

###### Agent Notes
- When writing/accepting definitions ensure the semantic_layer agent validation pipeline is invoked synchronously to prevent stale acceptances. Agents must not receive raw PII data — only definition text and metadata. Commits should include metadata for traceability (actor, review_id, manifest_version). Keep Git writes idempotent: if same commit_id exists, return success without duplicate write. Use workspace-scoped service account with least privilege for Git operations.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-014-02: As a pipeline orchestrator, I want the ML pipeline to block use of any low-confidence semantic definitions and raise a human_required SSE event with actionable details, so that models never train on unreviewed definitions.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - AC1: Any pipeline stage that resolves semantic definitions for ML (feature engineering, model training) validates definition_confidence != 'low' before using; if low, the pipeline emits SSE event type human_required with payload describing definition id(s), workspace_id, session_id, and remediation steps.
- [ ] - AC2: The pipeline rejects start of stage 3 (feature engineering) and stage 4 (model training) when low-confidence items are present; response/state is persisted to pipeline_run table with status HUMAN_REQUIRED and reason.
- [ ] - AC3: The review-queue APIs and pipeline integrate so that when an item is Accepted via API (and commit_id present), any pipeline_run in HUMAN_REQUIRED state is automatically re-queued and an SSE event agent_complete/pipeline_resume is emitted.
- [ ] - AC4: Unit and integration tests cover gating logic, event emission, and automatic re-queueing on accept, including test cases with multiple simultaneous low-confidence items.
- [ ] - AC5: Error handling exists: if review-queue service unreachable, pipeline returns a deterministic error with remediation and retries up to 3 times before failing with an operable message.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-014-06: Add gating checks to pipeline services (feature engineer, training orchestrator)
    - [ ] - Add API call or DB lookup in the pipeline pre-flight to fetch definitions referenced by the session_spec and check definition_confidence.
    - [ ] - Implement mapping from session_spec to definition_refs and log which definitions are low.
    - [ ] - If low found, write pipeline_run.status = HUMAN_REQUIRED with JSON payload listing definitions and stop advancement; include trace_id.
    - [ ] - Add unit tests for mapping logic and gating decision branches.
    - [ ] - Add feature flag to allow bypass for internal testing (logged and auditable).
- [ ] TASK-F-014-07: Emit human_required SSE events and integrate with job queue
    - [ ] - Implement SSE event payload format per system spec: type: human_required, payload: { pipeline_run_id, session_id, workspace_id, definitions:[{id,name,confidence}], remediation: string }.
    - [ ] - Publish event to session SSE stream and also push to Redis pub/sub so UI and notification workers can consume.
    - [ ] - Update job queue (BullMQ) to set job to paused state and persist pick-up criteria for resume (dependent on commit_id).
    - [ ] - Add integration tests verifying SSE delivery and job queue pause/resume flow.
- [ ] TASK-F-014-08: Implement automatic resume hook triggered by review acceptance
    - [ ] - Add listener in review-queue service to publish review.accepted events to Redis/channel with commit_id and affected definition ids.
    - [ ] - Add pipeline consumer that listens for review.accepted; when commit_id matches expectation and pipeline_run.status=HUMAN_REQUIRED, validate now-high-confidence and re-queue the job (or resume) and emit pipeline_resume SSE event.
    - [ ] - Ensure resume performs final semantic schema validation before proceeding.
    - [ ] - Add integration tests simulating accept + resume and verifying pipeline completes to next stage.
- [ ] TASK-F-014-09: Add robust error handling and observability for gating path
    - [ ] - Implement retries with exponential backoff for transient errors when contacting review-queue service (3 retries).
    - [ ] - Record Prometheus metrics: review_queue_block_count, review_resume_count, review_block_latency, review_api_error_count.
    - [ ] - Add detailed logs (including trace_id) for gate failure reasons and remediation suggestions.
    - [ ] - Add Sentry error breadcrumbs for any unhandled exceptions.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - Unit tests: gating logic, session_spec -> definition mapping, error branches.
- [ ] - Integration tests: simulate session starting that references a low-confidence metric; assert pipeline_run HUMAN_REQUIRED and SSE event payload correctness.
- [ ] - End-to-end test: create a review_queue item, start pipeline, accept via API, assert pipeline resumes and reaches feature engineering stage.
- [ ] - Load test: simulate 100 concurrent pipeline runs referencing low-confidence items and assert queueing/resuming behavior remains correct and metrics reflect blocking/resuming counts.
- [ ] - Security test: ensure that SSE events do not leak sensitive payloads; fields are sanitized.
- [ ] - Chaos test: simulate review-queue service downtime and assert pipeline retries and returns correct error to user after retries exhausted.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - Pipeline pre-flight checks reject ML stages when low-confidence definitions exist and persist HUMAN_REQUIRED state.
- [ ] - SSE human_required events emitted with actionable payload and visible in session SSE stream.
- [ ] - Review acceptance triggers automatic pipeline resume if all blocking definitions are accepted; resume path verified in integration tests.
- [ ] - Retries and error handling implemented and tested; metrics and logs available on staging.
- [ ] - Feature flag for bypass exists and is off by default in production.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-013
- [ ] F-007

###### Agent Notes
- Agents that consume semantic schema must rely on the authoritative semantic_layer_version and should re-fetch after commit_id changes. When the pipeline emits human_required, include precise definition identifiers (not raw SQL) and a short human-readable explanation (one sentence). Never send raw data samples to agents during this flow—only definition metadata. Ensure the resume hook performs final deterministic JSON Schema validation before allowing ML use.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-014-03: As an SRE/QA lead, I want audit logging, RBAC enforcement, notifications, and monitoring around the human review queue so that reviewers are alerted, actions are traceable, and the system is observable and secure.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - AC1: Every state-changing action on review_queue (create, accept, edit, reject) produces an audit record persisted to semantic_review_audit with actor_id, action, payload hash, timestamp, and trace_id; records are immutable.
- [ ] - AC2: Notifications are sent when new review items are created or when pipeline_run enters HUMAN_REQUIRED: email to workspace admins and Slack message to configured channel with link to review UI; notification failures retry with backoff and fallback to email.
- [ ] - AC3: RBAC enforced server-side: only users with appropriate ABAC attributes may mutate queue items. Attempts to bypass must log security incident metrics and return 403.
- [ ] - AC4: Monitoring dashboards display key metrics (pending_review_count, avg_time_to_review, blocked_pipeline_count) and alert when pending_review_count > 50 for > 1 hour.
- [ ] - AC5: Penetration test for review endpoints completed and critical severity vulnerabilities addressed before merge to main.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-014-10: Implement structured audit logging and immutable audit store
    - [ ] - Extend semantic_review_audit to store actor_id, action, review_id, shrinked_payload_hash, full_payload_archived (S3 with encryption), timestamp, trace_id.
    - [ ] - Implement service-layer calls to write audit entries within the same DB transaction as state transitions (or after commit with reliable queue if necessary).
    - [ ] - Add access controls to audit records: only workspace admins and compliance role can query audit history; write-only for services.
    - [ ] - Add backups and retention policy docs for audit store (retain 2 years, archived to cold storage).
    - [ ] - Add tests verifying audit entries created for each transition and that payload archival works.
- [ ] TASK-F-014-11: Implement notifications (Slack + email) and retry/fallback logic
    - [ ] - Implement notification service integration: Slack webhook (workspace-scoped), SMTP/email template service, and feature-flagged toggle per workspace.
    - [ ] - On new review item creation or pipeline HUMAN_REQUIRED, send notification to workspace admins with direct deep link to review item; include minimal payload (definition name, reason, link).
    - [ ] - Add retry/backoff and fallback-to-email strategy if Slack fails or rate-limited.
    - [ ] - Add opt-out configuration UI backend flag for notification preferences (stored in workspace settings).
    - [ ] - Add tests validating notification send flows and retry logic using mock providers.
- [ ] TASK-F-014-12: Add RBAC & security checks and run security testing
    - [ ] - Ensure service-layer enforces ABAC for all endpoints using existing auth middleware and validate using unit tests for all role permutations.
    - [ ] - Sanitize all inputs to avoid injection (no raw SQL in API responses), escape definitions when committing to Git, and enforce size limits on edited text.
    - [ ] - Run automated static analysis (Snyk, bandit) and address findings; add dependency vetting step to CI.
    - [ ] - Coordinate a focused penetration test on review endpoints (OWASP top 10) and remediate findings with tracking tickets.
    - [ ] - Add tests asserting endpoints return 401/403 for unauthorized access and that audit logs capture attempted unauthorized access.
- [ ] TASK-F-014-13: Build monitoring dashboards and alerts
    - [ ] - Add Prometheus metrics: semantic_review_pending_count, semantic_review_accept_rate, semantic_review_latency_seconds, semantic_review_api_errors_total, review_blocked_pipeline_count.
    - [ ] - Create Grafana dashboard with KPIs: pending queue size, avg time to review, number of pipelines blocked by reviews, top blocking definitions, error rates.
    - [ ] - Create alert rules: pending_review_count > 50 for 1h -> page on-call; review_api_error_rate > threshold -> create incident.
    - [ ] - Add runbook for on-call to triage high pending queue and notification delivery failures.
    - [ ] - Add synthetic test to CI that asserts list endpoint healthcheck < 300ms from staging every 5 min.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - Security tests: static analysis, dependency scanning, penetration test focused on endpoints and Git integration.
- [ ] - Integration tests: notification delivery (mocked), audit entries persistence, RBAC scenario matrix.
- [ ] - Load test: simulate notification bursts and pending queue growth to ensure alerts trigger and metrics are accurate.
- [ ] - E2E acceptance: create review items, receive notification, perform accept, verify audit and pipeline resume flow.
- [ ] - Compliance check: ensure PII controls are respected (definitions may reference PII column names but no data uploaded).

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - Audit logs persisted and immutable with archived full payloads available in S3 and access controls configured.
- [ ] - Notifications deliver to Slack and email; retries and fallbacks tested and documented.
- [ ] - RBAC enforced and verified by security tests; pen-test critical issues resolved.
- [ ] - Monitoring dashboards display required metrics and alerts with documented runbooks.
- [ ] - All security, privacy, and observability requirements reviewed and signed off by SRE and Security lead.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-013
- [ ] F-007

###### Agent Notes
- Notification messages must avoid exposing raw definition SQL or any PII. Audit payload archival should be encrypted server-side and referenced via a secure S3 URI in the audit row. Agents that read the audit should only see metadata; only compliance role can retrieve full payload. Monitoring instrumentation points should include trace_id to correlate pipeline runs, SSE events, and Git commits for troubleshooting. Ensure instrumentation tags include workspace_id and environment.

#### Requirement / Feature: Semantic layer editor UI (frontend screen: explore / metric editor)

**Feature ID:** `F-015`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `frontend`

**Rationale:** MVP mockups include a semantic editor for administrators to accept or edit definitions.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-013
- [ ] F-014

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-015-01: As a workspace admin, I want a metric editor UI for an Explore that surfaces semantic metric/dimension definitions and confidence badges so that I can accept, edit, or reject LLM-generated definitions and make them available (or block them) for downstream ML pipelines.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Display an Explore header with name, description, semantic_layer_version, and last_updated.
- [ ] List metrics and dimensions with natural-language definition, confidence (high|medium|low) badge, and sparkline preview where available.
- [ ] Low-confidence items show a persistent 'Review' action; clicking opens an editor modal with raw definition, rendered SQL expression preview, and ability to Accept / Edit / Reject.
- [ ] Accept action marks item as high confidence, triggers a PATCH to semantic layer API and increments semantic_layer_version (minor/patch as per semver rules) and logs an audit event.
- [ ] Edit action validates input client-side and server-side, triggers re-validation (async) and prevents ML usage until re-validation passes and admin Accepts.
- [ ] Reject action marks definition archived/removed from active schema and surfaces a conflict resolution flow if other definitions reference the same metric name.
- [ ] All UI actions handle API errors with user-facing messages, retry buttons, and write structured errors to browser console and client telemetry.
- [ ] All interactive elements have aria-label attributes and keyboard accessibility; color contrast meets WCAG AA.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-015-01: Implement Explore list and metric/dimension cards UI
    - [ ] Create Explore header component showing name, description, version, last updated timestamp
    - [ ] Create MetricCard component showing name, natural-language definition, confidence badge, and sparkline thumbnail
    - [ ] Create DimensionCard component showing name, type, hierarchies, and filterable toggle
    - [ ] Implement client-side state management for selected Explore and live preview data via window.__analytiq_state
    - [ ] Add skeleton loading states and panel-level error card with retry button
- [ ] TASK-F-015-02: Implement Metric Editor modal (Accept / Edit / Reject) UI and client validation
    - [ ] Design editor modal with raw definition textarea, SQL-expression preview, change reason field, and Accept/Edit/Reject buttons
    - [ ] Implement client-side validation rules: non-empty definition, no forbidden tokens, max length 2000 chars
    - [ ] Add inline validation UI and disable Accept until definition passes basic lint rules
    - [ ] Add keyboard shortcuts (Enter to save, Esc to cancel) and focus trap for accessibility
    - [ ] Add client-side optimistic UI update with rollback on server error
- [ ] TASK-F-015-03: Integrate frontend with semantic layer APIs for fetch / patch / review actions
    - [ ] Implement GET /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema to fetch Explore and metric lists and map to UI models
    - [ ] Implement PATCH /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema/metrics/{metric_name} to submit Accept or Edit with payload { action, definition, reason }
    - [ ] Implement POST /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema/metrics/{metric_name}/reject to archive definition and optionally provide replacement
    - [ ] Implement polling/websocket for async re-validation status and display spinner/state in editor modal
    - [ ] Implement structured error handling for 4xx/5xx and show retry option in UI
- [ ] TASK-F-015-04: Add conflict resolution UI and diff viewer for conflicting metric definitions
    - [ ] Detect name conflicts from API and display ConflictBadge on affected metric cards
    - [ ] Implement two-pane diff viewer (original vs alternate definition) with highlighted token diffs and inline comments
    - [ ] Add 'Choose preferred definition' action that triggers server-side merge and logs resolution event
    - [ ] Disable Accept in editor until conflict resolved; show required resolution modal when attempting to Accept a conflicted name
    - [ ] Write unit tests for diff rendering and resolution actions
- [ ] TASK-F-015-05: Accessibility, localization, telemetry, and QA integration
    - [ ] Add aria-labels, roles, and keyboard navigation tests for all interactive components
    - [ ] Hook up telemetry events for view_explore, open_editor, accept_definition, edit_definition, reject_definition, conflict_resolved
    - [ ] Internationalize visible strings and supply English messages fixture
    - [ ] Create UI storybook stories for MetricCard, MetricEditor, and ConflictDiff
    - [ ] Write frontend unit tests and integration tests for happy and error flows

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit test MetricCard rendering for high|medium|low confidence states and missing data
- [ ] Unit test MetricEditor client-side validation rules and keyboard shortcuts
- [ ] Integration test: fetch Explore -> open editor -> Accept -> confirm PATCH called and UI updates
- [ ] E2E test: simulate backend re-validation failing then passing and assert modal displays states correctly
- [ ] Accessibility test: axe-core run verifying no critical violations on Explore screen
- [ ] Visual regression test for metric cards and modal using snapshot testing
- [ ] Error handling test: force 500 on PATCH and assert rollback and user-visible retry

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Explore and metric/dimension cards implemented and visually match MVP mockups
- [ ] Metric Editor modal implemented with client-side validation and accessibility attributes
- [ ] Frontend integrated with semantic layer read/write APIs and handles async re-validation states
- [ ] Conflict diff viewer implemented and name conflict resolution flow functional
- [ ] Frontend unit tests coverage >= 80% for new components, Storybook stories created, and E2E tests added
- [ ] Feature deployed to staging behind feature flag and smoke-tested

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-013
- [ ] F-014
- [ ] /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema (backend read)
- [ ] /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema/metrics/{metric_name} (backend write)

###### Agent Notes
- Frontend should be thin and deterministic: fetch full Explore JSON and render cards. Do not attempt to locally compute semver increments; rely on backend response with new version. Use single JS state object window.__analytiq_state to maintain selected Explore and editor state. Keep artifact size small; component CSS must use CSS custom properties. Telemetry events must include trace_id when available.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-015-02: As a data engineer, I want the semantic editor UI to surface LLM confidence, validation results, and re-validation feedback so that I can triage low-confidence metrics and trigger re-validation workflows without leaving the UI.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each metric/dimension row shows a confidence badge (high|medium|low) and a validation status icon (pending|pass|fail).
- [ ] Clicking a 'Run re-validation' action triggers an async validation job via backend and displays job status in UI until complete.
- [ ] Validation failures surface deterministic failure reasons (e.g. many-to-many join detected, PK uniqueness violation, SQL parse error) with links to relevant lineage/manifest entries.
- [ ] Re-validation success moves the item to pass state; UI displays a one-line change summary returned from backend.
- [ ] Low-confidence items cannot be toggled to 'available_for_ml' until they are Accepted after a passing validation.
- [ ] All validation operations are auditable; user actions generate an audit record visible in UI via a small activity log.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-015-06: Display confidence + validation status and small activity log
    - [ ] Extend MetricCard to render confidence badge and validation status icon with tooltip
    - [ ] Add activity_log component showing last 5 audit events for selected metric with timestamp and actor
    - [ ] Fetch audit events via GET /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema/metrics/{metric_name}/audit
    - [ ] Add 'Run re-validation' button and disable if a validation job is in progress
    - [ ] Add unit tests for activity log and status rendering
- [ ] TASK-F-015-07: Trigger re-validation and show async job status in the UI
    - [ ] Implement POST /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema/metrics/{metric_name}/validate to start validation job
    - [ ] Implement polling or SSE subscription for validation job status with exponential backoff
    - [ ] Display detailed validation result pane with deterministic failure reasons and remediation suggestions
    - [ ] If validation fails, show 'Request manual review' CTA that opens Review Queue (US-F-015-03)
    - [ ] Log re-validation start/complete events to telemetry with job_id and duration
- [ ] TASK-F-015-08: Surface deterministic validation errors with links to lineage / governance manifest
    - [ ] Map backend validation error codes to human-readable messages and remediation tips
    - [ ] Link offending validation items to governance manifest entries fetched from /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest
    - [ ] If failure is 'many-to-many', show required bridge table note and action to open create-PDT flow link
    - [ ] Add copy-to-clipboard for validation error details for ticketing
    - [ ] Add tests that simulate each deterministic validation error mapping
- [ ] TASK-F-015-09: Enforce ML availability gating in UI
    - [ ] Disable 'mark available for ML' toggle for low/medium confidence until Accept + validation pass
    - [ ] Show tooltip explaining gating rules and link to governance policy docs
    - [ ] Add client-side guard preventing accidental enabling and confirm modal requiring reason
    - [ ] Add integration test verifying gating behavior end-to-end
    - [ ] Record gating changes in audit log and telemetry

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Integration test that starts a re-validation job and asserts UI shows progress and final status
- [ ] Simulate backend validation failure codes and assert correct remediation messages and links
- [ ] E2E test verifying ML gating: attempt to mark low-confidence item available_for_ml and assert block with explanatory modal
- [ ] Audit log test: accept/edit/reject actions produce audit entries and activity log displays them
- [ ] Performance test: ensure polling for validation status scales when 50 concurrent validations are active (frontend load testing)

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Confidence badge and validation status visible on all metric/dimension cards
- [ ] Re-validation request triggers backend job and UI shows live status until completion
- [ ] Validation failures map to deterministic messages and provide lineage links
- [ ] ML availability gating enforced in the UI and audited
- [ ] Integration and E2E tests covering re-validation flows pass and are included in CI

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-013
- [ ] F-014
- [ ] /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest (governance manifest read)
- [ ] /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema/metrics/{metric_name}/validate (validation job)

###### Agent Notes
- Validation job status must be treated as authoritative from backend; UI should only display but not re-evaluate validation rules. Map backend error codes to localized message keys. Use SSE where possible for job updates to minimize polling. Include trace_id in all re-validation requests for observability.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-015-03: As a product QA lead and delivery engineer, I want the semantic editor UI's frontend updates to be backed by robust backend API contracts, versioned semantic schema persistence, and audit logging so that edits are durable, versioned, and reproducible across sessions and artifacts.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PATCH/POST operations from the editor persist changes to workspace-scoped Git storage under /semantic/{workspace_id}/schema/ and return new semantic_layer_version following semver rules.
- [ ] Backend validates edits with deterministic JSON Schema and returns detailed validation errors; frontend surfaces these errors inline.
- [ ] Every Accept/Edit/Reject action writes a structured audit log entry containing actor_id, action, reason, old_definition_hash, new_definition_hash, timestamp, and trace_id.
- [ ] Server enforces RBAC: only users with Admin or Editor role (as per ABAC rules) can Accept or Edit; Reject may require Owner role and will return 403 otherwise.
- [ ] All write operations are idempotent and return appropriate HTTP status codes; conflict resolution returns 409 with conflict payload.
- [ ] Backend exposes endpoints used by frontend and publishes OpenAPI contract; CI validates contract via contract tests.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-015-10: Implement backend endpoints for semantic edits, validation, and audit logging
    - [ ] Implement PATCH /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema/metrics/{metric_name} to accept edits with payload {action, definition, reason, client_version}
    - [ ] Implement POST /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema/metrics/{metric_name}/reject to archive or mark as rejected
    - [ ] Implement POST /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema/metrics/{metric_name}/validate to queue validation job
    - [ ] Write audit log entries to append-only audit store (Postgres table or event stream) for every write action
    - [ ] Return structured responses with new semantic_layer_version and change_hash
- [ ] TASK-F-015-11: Persist semantic schema changes to Git-backed storage and implement semver rules
    - [ ] Implement service that writes updated Cube-dev YAML/JSON into workspace Git repo under /semantic/{workspace_id}/schema/{explore}.yml
    - [ ] Implement semver bump logic on server: patch for definition text change, minor for added metric/dimension, major for breaking changes per PRD rules
    - [ ] Create branch+commit flow with commit message and author metadata and push to repo; handle push failures with retry
    - [ ] Expose resulting commit id, semantic_layer_version, and file diff in API response
    - [ ] Write unit tests for semver decision matrix
- [ ] TASK-F-015-12: Enforce RBAC, validation, idempotency, and conflict handling
    - [ ] Integrate ABAC checks to deny Accept/Edit/Reject to unauthorized principals; return 403 with reason
    - [ ] Validate incoming definition JSON with backend JSON Schema and return a structured error list for 400 responses
    - [ ] Implement optimistic concurrency control: require client_version or base_commit_id; return 409 with conflict payload on mismatch
    - [ ] Make write endpoints idempotent via idempotency-key header support and safe retries
    - [ ] Add server-side logging for audit, errors, and validation failures with trace_id propagation
- [ ] TASK-F-015-13: Create API contract tests, integration tests, and CI checks
    - [ ] Publish OpenAPI spec for new endpoints and include example payloads
    - [ ] Write Pact/contract tests that mock frontend expectations and run as part of CI
    - [ ] Write end-to-end integration tests: Web UI -> API -> Git storage -> validation job queue -> audit entry
    - [ ] Add DB migration for audit table and write rollback scripts
    - [ ] Add CI job to run API contract tests and schema validation
- [ ] TASK-F-015-14: DevOps, security hardening, observability and release automation
    - [ ] Deploy backend endpoints to staging behind feature flag and run smoke tests
    - [ ] Add RBAC test users and automated tests for permission enforcement
    - [ ] Instrument distributed tracing: ensure trace_id is in all logs and audit entries
    - [ ] Add monitoring alerts for validation job failures > 5% and API error rate > 2%
    - [ ] Document API usage and rollout plan for frontend feature flag gating

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for PATCH/POST handlers validating success, 400 validation error, 403 RBAC denial, 409 conflict cases
- [ ] Integration tests verifying Git commit created with expected file contents and semver bump
- [ ] Contract tests verifying OpenAPI matches frontend expectations for request/response shapes
- [ ] E2E test: UI triggers Accept -> backend persists -> semantic_layer_version increments -> audit entry visible
- [ ] Security test: verify unauthorized user cannot call endpoints (403) and audit logs show attempted access
- [ ] Load test: simulate 200 concurrent edit requests and assert API error rate < 2% and no data corruption

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Backend endpoints implemented and covered by unit and integration tests
- [ ] Semantic schema changes are persisted to Git with semver version returned to frontend
- [ ] RBAC and idempotency enforced; conflict handling returns 409 with resolution data
- [ ] Audit log entries are written for all write operations and visible in activity log API
- [ ] OpenAPI contract published and contract tests pass in CI
- [ ] Staging deployment completed and smoke-tested; feature gated behind flag for rollout

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-013
- [ ] F-014
- [ ] workspace Git service (semantic storage)
- [ ] validation job queue (Stage 1 validator)
- [ ] ABAC authorization service

###### Agent Notes
- Backend must be deterministic and authoritative for semver and validation results; do not replicate semver logic in frontend. Use a Git-backed writer service to persist Cube.dev YAML. All write flows must include trace_id and client_version for OCC. Expose short, machine-readable error codes with human-readable messages to simplify frontend mapping. Ensure audit store is append-only and tamper-evident.

## Sprint 4 - Session Planning & Data Selection UX

**Sprint Goal:** Implement session planner agent and the conversation-based data selection UI per MVP.

### Sprint Completion Checklist
- [ ] All P0 stories complete
- [ ] All blocking defects resolved
- [ ] Tests passing
- [ ] Demo-ready increment produced
- [ ] Milestone accepted

### Epic: Session Planner

#### Requirement / Feature: Session planner agent (intent classification → session_spec)

**Feature ID:** `F-016`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, agent`

**Rationale:** Stage 2 is core UX: mapping plain-English requests to session_spec JSON.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-011
- [ ] F-014

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-016-01: As an analyst, I want to describe my analytic question in plain English and receive a validated session_spec JSON, so that the pipeline can deterministically execute my requested analysis without exposing table or column names.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] The agent accepts a plain-English user utterance and returns a session_spec JSON conforming to the SessionSpec schema in the PRD.
- [ ] - [ ] The agent classifies intent into one of {descriptive, diagnostic, predictive, prescriptive} with an intent_confidence value between 0 and 1.
- [ ] - [ ] If intent_confidence < 0.85 the agent asks exactly one multiple-choice clarifying question (3–5 options) and does not emit a session_spec.
- [ ] - [ ] The produced session_spec includes target_metric, feature_candidates (only semantic-layer names), date_range, grain, output_type, explores_used, semantic_layer_version, and governance_manifest_version.
- [ ] - [ ] The agent never exposes raw table or column names in UI-facing text or session_spec; only semantic layer vocabulary is used.
- [ ] - [ ] All session_spec outputs pass JSON Schema validation server-side; any validation failure is logged and returned as a structured error to the client.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-016-01: Design and implement the AI agent system prompt and instruction set for the session planner that enforces the response protocol and JSON output format.
    - [ ] - [ ] Draft system prompt aligned with PRD §4.3.1 and Table 12 including certainty thresholds and single-clarifying-question rule.
    - [ ] - [ ] Define expected JSON Schema for session_spec (TypeScript/JSON Schema) matching PRD Table 28.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add explicit LLM output format enforcement instructions: 'output only JSON, no free text, validate with schema before returning'.
    - [ ] - [ ] Review the prompt with product, data, and security to ensure no PII or raw data references are allowed.
- [ ] TASK-F-016-02: Integrate LLM calls into backend agent runtime with strict schema validation and retry/error handling.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement adapter to call configured LLM (claude-sonnet-4-6) with deterministic params (temperature, max_tokens, top_p) and request/response logging hooks.
    - [ ] - [ ] Run JSON Schema validation on LLM output; on failure run controlled repair request (1 retry) with the LLM to correct format; fail with structured error after retry.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement explicit detection of hallucinations (unexpected keys or values) and reject outputs that reference raw tables/columns.
    - [ ] - [ ] Instrument metrics: LLM latency, tokens in/out, schema validation failures, confidence distribution.
- [ ] TASK-F-016-03: Backend API: implement /api/v1/sessions POST flow that routes user message to session planner agent and returns streaming SSE events of planning stage.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement request handler that sanitizes user input and attaches semantic layer context (semantic_layer_version) and governance manifest metadata.
    - [ ] - [ ] Publish SSE events per PRD streaming protocol: type: planning, agent_start, agent_complete, human_required, error.
    - [ ] - [ ] On agent_complete, persist draft session_spec to DB with status 'pending_confirmation' and return session_spec to frontend in SSE.
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure API enforces authorization (workspace membership) and records trace_id for distributed tracing.
- [ ] TASK-F-016-04: Frontend: conversation UX to capture user utterance, display agent clarifying question(s), show selectable metric/dimension lists, and display session_spec preview for user confirmation.
    - [ ] - [ ] Build conversational input component that sends messages to /api/v1/sessions and listens to SSE events.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement UI card to render multi-choice clarifying question (3–5 options) and send selected answer back to session endpoint.
    - [ ] - [ ] Display metric and dimension option lists using semantic layer names, with sparkline placeholders (frontend uses API to fetch preview values), allowing user to pick 1–3 explores and target metric.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement plain-English session_spec summary modal and a 'Confirm' button that triggers session_spec confirmation endpoint and advances pipeline.
- [ ] TASK-F-016-05: Logging, audit, and persistence: store session_spec with provenance, confidence, and semantic_layer + governance manifest versions.
    - [ ] - [ ] Define DB schema for persisted session_spec with fields per PRD Table 28 plus created_at, created_by, trace_id.
    - [ ] - [ ] On persistence, record semantic_layer_version and governance_manifest_version used for planning retrieval.
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit audit log event with actor, session_id, raw_user_text (sanitized), intent, intent_confidence, and session_spec hash.
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure LLM prompt/response logging follows security policy (no raw rows) and retention policy (90 days).
- [ ] TASK-F-016-06: Security and privacy: enforce LLM data minimization and prevent raw data exposure from backend to LLM.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement code that constructs LLM context payloads containing only semantic_layer vocabulary, summary statistics, and definitions — never raw row data or credentials.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add automated unit tests that assert no raw table/column names or sample rows appear in LLM request payloads.
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure secrets (DB creds, service tokens) are not passed to the LLM and are pulled in server-side as needed by other agents.
    - [ ] - [ ] Conduct a security review and sign-off before enabling LLM calls in non-dev environments.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for prompt builder and LLM adapter including schema-validation failure path and retry behavior.
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests hitting /api/v1/sessions with mocked LLM responses: (a) high-confidence output -> session_spec emitted; (b) low-confidence output -> clarifying question emitted; (c) malformed JSON -> repair retry -> structured error after retry.
- [ ] - [ ] End-to-end UI test: user types 'Predict net revenue next 14 days by location', agent presents explores and metrics, user confirms, session_spec persisted and pipeline advances.
- [ ] - [ ] Security tests verifying no raw rows or table/column names are sent to LLM by inspecting outgoing LLM payloads.
- [ ] - [ ] Performance test: planning stage first-token latency P50 < 2s, P95 < 10s (per PRD Table 30).

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Session planner agent prompt implemented and approved by product and data owners.
- [ ] - [ ] Backend LLM integration returns session_spec JSON that passes schema validation in >95% of test prompts after a single repair retry.
- [ ] - [ ] Frontend conversation flow supports clarifying question, option selection, and explicit user confirmation.
- [ ] - [ ] SessionSpec persisted with semantic_layer_version and governance_manifest_version and recorded in audit log.
- [ ] - [ ] Automated unit and integration tests pass in CI; telemetry and tracing enabled for planning stage.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] F-011
- [ ] - [ ] F-014
- [ ] - [ ] /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema endpoint (semantic layer read access)

###### Agent Notes
- Use claude-sonnet-4-6 as the pinned model. Set temperature=0.0 for deterministic output; include explicit system instructions to 'output only JSON' and to validate against provided JSON Schema. Provide the semantic_layer snippet limited to explores, metric and dimension display names, short definitions, and semantic_layer_version. If intent_confidence < 0.85 return a single multiple-choice clarifying question with options derived from top-3 ambiguous dimensions/metrics. Always log intent_confidence and schema validation result with trace_id.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-016-02: As a backend engineer, I want the session planner to enforce deterministic validation, error handling, and observability so that invalid or low-confidence session_specs are caught early and are auditable.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] All session_spec outputs pass a strict JSON Schema validator before persisting or returning to clients.
- [ ] - [ ] Any schema validation failure is retried once with a targeted LLM repair call; persistent failures return a structured error with remediation guidance.
- [ ] - [ ] All decisions (intent classification, clarifying question, chosen explores, selected metrics) are recorded in the audit log with trace_id and timestamps.
- [ ] - [ ] SSE streaming events conform to the types in PRD §7.1.1 (planning, agent_start, agent_complete, human_required, error) and are emitted in the correct sequence.
- [ ] - [ ] Observability dashboards show LLM call rate, schema validation failure rate, intent_confidence histogram, and mean planning latency.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-016-07: Implement server-side JSON Schema validator and repair retry orchestration for agent outputs.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add a JSON Schema definition for SessionSpec in the backend service (shared TypeScript/JSON Schema library).
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement a validation wrapper that returns detailed validation errors (missing fields, type mismatches, enum violations).
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement a single repair retry flow: send validator errors + original LLM response back to LLM with an instruction to fix only the JSON shape; limit to 1 retry.
    - [ ] - [ ] On second failure, return HTTP 422 with structured error JSON containing validation error list and user-friendly remediation steps.
- [ ] TASK-F-016-08: Implement SSE event emission and state transitions for the planning stage.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement event producer emitting types: planning -> agent_start -> (human_required | agent_complete | error) with payload per PRD.
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure SSE connection recovers gracefully on transient disconnects and resumes with last known state.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add tests that assert correct event ordering given simulated LLM latencies and errors.
    - [ ] - [ ] Update frontend to handle and render these event types (coordination with TASK-F-016-04).
- [ ] TASK-F-016-09: Build audit logging and trace propagation for planning decisions.
    - [ ] - [ ] Define audit event schema for session planning events with fields: session_id, actor_id, event_type, payload_hash, intent, intent_confidence, semantic_layer_version, governance_manifest_version.
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit audit events to append-only audit log storage and to observability pipeline (OpenTelemetry traces + events).
    - [ ] - [ ] Add policy to redact sensitive user input in logs except sanitized summary stored in audit for reproducibility.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement retention and export controls per security policy.
- [ ] TASK-F-016-10: Implement monitoring dashboards and alerts for key planning metrics.
    - [ ] - [ ] Instrument metrics: planning_latency_ms, llm_schema_validation_failures, llm_calls_per_minute, avg_intent_confidence, clarifying_questions_rate.
    - [ ] - [ ] Create Grafana dashboards for these metrics and add alert rules for schema_validation_failure_rate > 1% and planning_latency_p95 > 10s.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add runbook describing steps for investigating repeated schema validation failures (include sample trace_id lookup).
    - [ ] - [ ] Add CI job to fail PRs if unit tests regress observability instrumentation.
- [ ] TASK-F-016-11: Add security checks and automated tests preventing data leakage to LLMs.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests that scan outgoing LLM payloads for patterns matching table/column names, sample values, or PII and fail build if detected.
    - [ ] - [ ] Integrate static analyzer that flags code paths that could include raw sample rows in payload construction.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add CI step to assert LLM logging complies with retention and redaction policies.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for validator covering positive and negative cases, including schema edge cases (missing prediction_horizon when predictive intent).
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests that simulate LLM malformed JSON and verify repair retry and then structured error after second failure.
- [ ] - [ ] End-to-end tests validating SSE event sequence under normal and error flows.
- [ ] - [ ] Load test to simulate 100 concurrent planning requests and validate planning_latency_p95 target and LLM adapter backoff behavior.
- [ ] - [ ] Security regression tests checking that outbound LLM payloads contain only allowed fields (semantic names, short statistics).

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Backend validator enforces full SessionSpec schema; repair retry implemented.
- [ ] - [ ] SSE events emitted in the correct order and received by frontend in integration tests.
- [ ] - [ ] Audit log contains full planning decision trace for 100% of confirmed session_specs.
- [ ] - [ ] Monitoring dashboards available and alerts configured; runbook published.
- [ ] - [ ] Automated security tests preventing raw data in LLM payloads passing in CI.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] F-011
- [ ] - [ ] F-014
- [ ] - [ ] Observability platform (OpenTelemetry + Grafana)

###### Agent Notes
- When instructing the LLM to repair an invalid JSON, include only the validator errors and the exact JSON Schema path references; instruct LLM to 'return only corrected session_spec JSON and nothing else'. Keep temperature=0, max_retries=1 for repair. Emit full trace_id on every LLM call so the observability pipeline can stitch prompt/response logs.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-016-03: As a workspace admin, I want every session_spec to be tied to the exact semantic_layer_version and governance_manifest_version so that sessions are reproducible and auditable.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Every persisted session_spec includes semantic_layer_version and governance_manifest_version fields populated from the authoritative services.
- [ ] - [ ] The session_spec cannot be confirmed if the provided semantic_layer_version or governance_manifest_version is missing or stale relative to the current workspace state; the agent must surface a warning and allow user to continue with explicit confirmation or refresh the semantic layer.
- [ ] - [ ] The session_spec stored for an artifact is immutable; edits create a forked session_spec version with linkage to original.
- [ ] - [ ] APIs that return a session_spec (GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/spec) include the version fields and a reproducibility note in the response.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-016-12: Fetch and attach semantic_layer_version and governance_manifest_version to the agent context and final session_spec.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement backend calls to /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema to fetch current semantic_layer_version and minimal vocabulary snippet.
    - [ ] - [ ] Query governance manifest endpoint /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest and include manifest_version in the planning context.
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure version fields are added to the session_spec payload and persisted atomically with session record.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests that simulate missing or mismatched versions and verify error/warning behavior.
- [ ] TASK-F-016-13: Implement immutability and forking semantics for persisted session_specs.
