    - [ ] Ensure generator omits auto-dropped columns from INSERT-SELECT and records dropped_features in split_config
    - [ ] Write unit tests for leakage gating logic with mocked feature manifests
- [ ] TASK-F-021-05: Implement dry-run execution and static SQL linting against warehouse dialect
    - [ ] Implement a dry-run execution mode that runs the generated INSERT-SELECT and DDL in a transaction or validation-only session (using warehouse EXPLAIN or query parser) and returns parse/compile errors
    - [ ] Integrate SQL linting rules (no SELECT *, explicit aliases, no non-deterministic functions) and surface lint warnings
    - [ ] Add retry-safe rollback behavior for dry-run sessions and ensure no data mutations in dry-run
    - [ ] Log dry-run SQL text, compilation result, and error traces to observability with masked credentials
    - [ ] Add integration tests that run dry-run against a local DuckDB dev instance and a Snowflake staging account

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit test: feed minimal valid session_spec + semantic_layer and assert JSON output schema validates and ddl/insert_sql are non-empty
- [ ] Integration test: dry-run generated SQL against DuckDB dev and Snowflake staging to assert compile success
- [ ] Negative test: provide session_spec with missing join path and assert generator returns BLOCK with remediation describing required bridge table
- [ ] Edge test: simulate high-cardinality join that would produce fan-out and assert fan-out is detected and suggested fixes are present
- [ ] Leakage test: include a lag-zero target feature and assert leakage_risk flagged and feature held for user confirmation or auto-dropped per threshold

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] SQL generation service implemented and code-reviewed
- [ ] Output JSON Schema validated end-to-end with sample session_spec inputs
- [ ] Dry-run compile tests pass for Snowflake dialect and DuckDB dev
- [ ] Unit and integration tests covering positive and negative generation paths added and passing in CI
- [ ] Logging (trace_id) and error mapping to dq_gate statuses implemented
- [ ] Documentation updated: API contract, output schema, and example DDL + INSERT-SELECT

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-016
- [ ] F-011
- [ ] F-007

###### Agent Notes
- Agent must output only the defined structured JSON schema. Do not include raw row data or samples. Use governance_manifest and cube_schema as the single source of truth for column types and join paths. Deterministically select join paths (e.g., shortest path then lowest null_pct). Compute output_hash as SHA256 of canonicalized {ddl, insert_sql, session_id, semantic_layer_version}. All messages must include trace_id for observability.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-021-02: As a QA lead and product owner, I want deterministic validation and clear failure modes for grain uniqueness, join integrity, leakage, and row-count tolerance during DDL generation, so that the pipeline blocks unsafe gold table writes and surfaces actionable remediation to the user.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Grain uniqueness probe runs automatically and returns PASS|WARN|BLOCK with exact offending join and example keys when duplicates exist
- [ ] Fan-out/join integrity checks detect many-to-many or multiplicative joins and return a recommended remediation (add dedupe key, switch join type, or define bridge) with code-snippet suggestions
- [ ] Leakage checks flag features with risk and classify actions: HOLD (0.3–0.7) with user confirmation required, DROP (>0.7) with automatic exclusion and notification
- [ ] Row count estimation compares expected vs produced rows and triggers BLOCK when discrepancy > 2% or returns WARN when within 2–5%
- [ ] All validation failures map to deterministic dq_gate responses and are logged with reproducible diagnostics

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-021-06: Implement grain uniqueness diagnostics and duplicate row sample extraction
    - [ ] Compose deterministic SQL probes that compute COUNT(*) and COUNT(DISTINCT grain_key_hash) over the join preview date range
    - [ ] If duplicates exist, generate a limited sample query (row limit 20) to capture representative offending key combinations (no PII)
    - [ ] Format diagnostics with offending_join_path, example_key_values (anonymized if PII flagged), and suggested SQL fix (dedupe row_number) text
    - [ ] Add automated unit tests for uniqueness detection including synthetic schemas that produce duplicates
    - [ ] Log the probe results into audit table gold.modeler_diagnostics_{run_id}
- [ ] TASK-F-021-07: Implement join graph analysis and fan-out risk scoring
    - [ ] Build a deterministic join graph walker that enumerates all valid join paths from root grain to each explore source
    - [ ] Compute estimated multiplicative cardinality using distinct counts and compute fan_out_score per join edge
    - [ ] Flag edges where multiplicative estimate > 1.05 or where FK null_pct indicates optional join and recommend left join with note
    - [ ] Create remediation text with explicit SQL snippet to dedupe or to introduce bridge table with example schema
    - [ ] Unit test graph scenarios including many-to-many, optional FK, and chained 1:N joins
- [ ] TASK-F-021-08: Implement row count expectation and tolerance verification
    - [ ] Derive expected row_count_by_grain by extrapolating upstream table row counts taking date_range filters into account
    - [ ] Compare produced row count post-insert (or dry-run estimation) with expected and compute percent_delta
    - [ ] Surface BLOCK when percent_delta > 2% and include both expected and actual counts in diagnostics
    - [ ] Implement a WARN path for 2–5% with recommendation to review filters and joins
    - [ ] Create automated tests simulating discrepancy scenarios
- [ ] TASK-F-021-09: Create deterministic dq_gate result packaging and audit logging
    - [ ] Define dq_gate payload structure with fields: status, failures[], warnings[], run_id, trace_id, remediation_actions[]
    - [ ] Write results to pipeline audit log and to gold.modeler_diagnostics_{run_id} in the warehouse
    - [ ] Emit streaming SSE event type: dq_gate with the dq_gate payload for the orchestrator to consume
    - [ ] Implement retries for diagnostic probes with exponential backoff and failure escalation to human_required state
    - [ ] Add end-to-end tests that assert SSE dq_gate events emitted for each failure type

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for join graph walker covering simple and complex join topologies
- [ ] Integration tests that simulate duplicate rows and assert BLOCK with sample key payload
- [ ] End-to-end pipeline test where generator emits dq_gate BLOCK and orchestrator halts with human_required message
- [ ] Security test verifying that sample key snippets exclude or mask columns flagged as PII
- [ ] Performance test measuring validation probe runtime against performance targets and ensuring probes finish within feature engineering time budget

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] All validation checks implemented and producing PASS|WARN|BLOCK deterministically
- [ ] Diagnostics include precise offending join and sample keys (anonymized when PII flagged)
- [ ] dq_gate payloads emitted and stored in audit log for every run
- [ ] Automated tests covering positive and negative validation scenarios pass in CI
- [ ] User-facing remediation messages reviewed by product and UX and stored in localization files

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-016
- [ ] F-011
- [ ] F-007

###### Agent Notes
- Validation logic must be deterministic and deterministic ordering of join edges must be used. When producing sample offending keys, redact PII flagged by governance_manifest: replace with '<REDACTED_PI I>' token. Include remediation SQL snippets that are executable (e.g., row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY keys ORDER BY updated_at DESC) > 1 THEN drop). Package all outputs as JSON for the orchestrator; do not emit free-form text except inside remediation strings.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-021-03: As an SRE/security engineer and product operator, I want the Data Modeler to support dry-run execution, permission checks, secure logging, and an optional execute path to actually write the gold table, so that writes are auditable, safe, and follow workspace security policies.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] System supports two modes: dry_run and execute; dry_run performs parsing/compile/plan checks only and never mutates data, execute performs the write within a controlled transaction and records outcome
- [ ] Before execute, the system verifies warehouse write permissions for the service role and fails with explicit 'insufficient_permissions' dq_gate BLOCK if credentials lack required privileges
- [ ] All write attempts (successful or blocked) are logged with trace_id, workspace_id, session_id, modeler_version, and output_hash to audit logs and stored in a modeler_runs table
- [ ] Execution path writes the gold table with lineage metadata table and updates feature_manifest_version/manifest pointers atomically
- [ ] Secrets and credentials are never logged; only credential existence and permission checks are logged

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-021-10: Add execute/dry_run orchestration, transactional write wrapper, and permission checks
    - [ ] Extend generation API to accept mode: dry_run|execute and require explicit operator confirmation for execute
    - [ ] Implement permission probe that runs a zero-row write test (create temporary table and drop) to verify CREATE TABLE/INSERT privileges and map failures to 'insufficient_permissions' error
    - [ ] Implement transactional write wrapper that writes to a versioned table name and swaps alias (or uses CREATE OR REPLACE) atomically where supported by dialect
    - [ ] Record run metadata to modeler_runs audit table with run_id, trace_id, workspace_id, session_id, mode, status, and output_hash
    - [ ] Add integration tests for permission-denied, dry_run, and successful execute against staging warehouse
- [ ] TASK-F-021-11: Implement secure logging, observability, and distributed tracing integration
    - [ ] Ensure every run receives a trace_id and propagate it to all SQL probes, dry-runs, and writes
    - [ ] Log structured events to centralized logging (OpenTelemetry / log ingestion) with fields: trace_id, run_id, workspace_id, agent_name, dq_gate_status
    - [ ] Mask any sensitive fields before logging; never log database credentials or raw data rows
    - [ ] Emit metrics for success_rate, avg_generation_time, validation_failures by type to monitoring dashboard
    - [ ] Add alert rule for repeated 'insufficient_permissions' failures per workspace
- [ ] TASK-F-021-12: Add security checks for PII and governance manifest integration to block unsafe columns
    - [ ] Before generating final DDL, cross-check columns against governance_manifest pii_flags and auto-exclude or require admin_approval when PII present
    - [ ] When PII is present and admin_approval absent, return dq_gate BLOCK and include list of pii_columns in remediation
    - [ ] Implement unit tests that simulate pii_flags and confirm generator blocks or requests approval
    - [ ] Update documentation for admins on how to approve PII for ML use and how approvals are recorded
- [ ] TASK-F-021-13: DevOps: CI/CD, secrets handling, and staged rollout
    - [ ] Add CI pipeline steps to run unit and integration tests for SQL generation against DuckDB dev container
    - [ ] Add feature flag and configuration for staged rollout (enable for pilot workspaces only)
    - [ ] Ensure warehouse credentials are retrieved from Secrets Manager at runtime and not stored in code or logs
    - [ ] Deploy to staging environment and run smoke tests before enabling in production

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Permission test: run permission probe with a role lacking CREATE privilege and assert dq_gate BLOCK with 'insufficient_permissions' code
- [ ] Dry-run safety test: run in dry_run mode and verify no tables created and compile succeeds/fails deterministically
- [ ] Execute success test: run execute mode in staging and assert table created with expected row counts, metadata comments present, and modeler_runs entry recorded
- [ ] PII block test: set governance_manifest with PII flag on a column and assert BLOCK and remediation message
- [ ] Audit log test: confirm run entries appear in audit storage and contain required metadata without secrets

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Dry-run and execute modes implemented and tested
- [ ] Permission checks correctly detect insufficient privileges and block execute path
- [ ] All runs logged to modeler_runs with traceable metadata and no sensitive data in logs
- [ ] PII checks integrated and block or require admin approval per governance policy
- [ ] CI/CD pipeline runs tests on PRs and gating passes before merge

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-016
- [ ] F-011
- [ ] F-007

###### Agent Notes
- Execution must be idempotent: use deterministic output_hash and write to versioned table names. For execute mode, prefer creating gold_{...}_{version} and updating a metadata alias rather than in-place mutation to preserve lineage. Permission probe must be non-invasive (temporary table test) and cleaned up. All audit records must reference trace_id for cross-service correlation.

#### Requirement / Feature: Temporal split configuration service (train/val/test + checks)

**Feature ID:** `F-022`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, data`

**Rationale:** Walk-forward backtesting requires time-based splits; default rules applied in MVP.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-021

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-022-01: As a backend/data engineer, I want a deterministic temporal split configuration service that returns a validated train/validation/test split_config JSON for a gold table request, so that downstream agents (feature engineer, training orchestrator) can rely on canonical, auditable, and reproducible time-based splits (MVP defaults: train 70% oldest, val 15% middle, test 15% newest) and we can enforce minimum-data and walk-forward requirements.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - The service exposes a well-documented API endpoint POST /api/v1/splits/generate that accepts a gold_table_spec + desired split strategy and returns split_config JSON.
- [ ] - The default split when strategy=temporal uses oldest 70% → middle 15% → newest 15% by row_count; offers deterministic boundaries (by timestamp) and includes explicit ISO8601 date ranges and row counts for each split.
- [ ] - The service validates and returns DQ checks: minimum_training_rows >= 500, test_rows >= 100, at least 5 windows possible for 5-window walk-forward or returns actionable failure with remediation hints.
- [ ] - The service computes walk-forward window boundaries (5 expanding windows) and includes them in split_config.walk_forward_windows array.
- [ ] - The service returns structured error codes and messages for all validation failures (e.g., ERR_MIN_TRAIN_ROWS, ERR_INSUFFICIENT_WINDOWS, ERR_DATE_PARSING) and logs them with trace_id.
- [ ] - Generated split_config is immutable when persisted and includes metadata: generated_at, generator_version, governance_manifest_version, semantic_layer_version, request_id.
- [ ] - Unit and integration tests cover nominal path, boundary cases (exact 500 rows, small datasets), and error paths; CI passes.
- [ ] - All inputs are schema-validated server-side; invalid requests return 4xx with descriptive error body; unauthorized requests return 401/403.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-022-01: Design split_config JSON schema and validation rules
    - [ ] - Define split_config JSON fields: split_boundaries (timestamp/row_index), split_proportions, row_counts, walk_forward_windows[], metadata (generated_at, generator_version, request_id, governance_manifest_version, semantic_layer_version), dqc_results[]
    - [ ] - Write OpenAPI spec additions for POST /api/v1/splits/generate and GET /api/v1/splits/{id}
    - [ ] - Define error code taxonomy (ERR_MIN_TRAIN_ROWS, ERR_MIN_TEST_ROWS, ERR_INSUFFICIENT_WINDOWS, ERR_INVALID_DATE_FIELD, ERR_TOO_FEW_ROWS_TO_SPLIT) and corresponding HTTP status mapping
    - [ ] - Create JSON Schema for input gold_table_spec validation and output split_config validation
    - [ ] - Document default temporal rules (70/15/15) and configurable override options in the API spec
- [ ] TASK-F-022-02: Implement split computation core library (backend service module)
    - [ ] - Implement deterministic partitioning function that computes date-boundaries given ordered timestamp column and row_counts
    - [ ] - Implement proportional split calculation that maps percentages to exact breakpoints using stable sort and ties-handling rules (e.g., inclusive/exclusive boundaries)
    - [ ] - Implement walk-forward window generator (5 expanding windows) that returns train/val ranges for each window and validates no overlap with final test split
    - [ ] - Implement row-count and date-based validation checks (min training rows >= 500, min test rows >= 100, at least 5 windows or return ERR_INSUFFICIENT_WINDOWS)
    - [ ] - Add unit tests for computation functions including edge cases for identical timestamps and sparse dates
- [ ] TASK-F-022-03: Create API endpoints and controller wiring
    - [ ] - Implement POST /api/v1/splits/generate: validate auth, validate input schema, call split core, persist split_config if requested
    - [ ] - Implement GET /api/v1/splits/{id}: return persisted split_config, support optional ?include_checks=true
    - [ ] - Implement idempotency handling for repeated generate calls with same request_id: return same persisted config or a clear diff if inputs changed
    - [ ] - Add request/response logging (trace_id, workspace_id, session_id, duration) with structured fields and appropriate log levels
    - [ ] - Integrate input validation errors into standardized API error response format used across the platform
- [ ] TASK-F-022-04: Persistence: DB schema and migrations to store split_config and audit metadata
    - [ ] - Design table gold_split_configs: (id uuid, workspace_id, session_id, gold_table_spec jsonb, split_config jsonb, generator_version, created_by, created_at, request_id unique, status enum)
    - [ ] - Write SQL migration for the table and index on request_id, workspace_id, created_at
    - [ ] - Implement repository functions (insert, get_by_id, get_by_request_id) with retries and transactional safety
    - [ ] - Add retention policy note: default TTL 90 days for transient splits unless session persisted; include soft-delete flag
    - [ ] - Add DB unit tests/migration tests
- [ ] TASK-F-022-05: Validation, error handling, and DQ check implementations
    - [ ] - Implement minimum training/test row checks with clear remediation messages and suggested user actions (expand date_range, lower min_rows) in dqc_results
    - [ ] - Implement final-test-split reservation check to ensure test is most recent 15% and has no non-null actuals if forecast horizon is future (DQ rule ties to Stage 5 requirement)
    - [ ] - Implement leakage-related validations that mark features crossing into test window (hold feature or flag in dqc_results) — return hold flags for feature_engineer to request confirmation
    - [ ] - Add robust date parsing and timezone normalization; return ERR_INVALID_DATE_FIELD for malformed date fields
    - [ ] - Add retry/backoff logic for transient DB/warehouse metadata fetch errors and surface useful messages
- [ ] TASK-F-022-06: Integrate with Data Modeler subagent (F-021) interfaces and contract tests
    - [ ] - Coordinate with F-021 to confirm gold_table_spec shape expected by generator and agree on API payloads
    - [ ] - Implement contract tests (mock governance_manifest + semantic_layer_version) that ensure split generator outputs consumable walk_forward_windows and split metadata
    - [ ] - Add example split_config fixtures used by Data Modeler unit tests and the feature engineering agent
    - [ ] - Add end-to-end integration test that runs Data Modeler -> splits service -> returns split and verifies expected JSON schema
    - [ ] - Document the contract in the inter-agent API docs and update agent prompt notes
- [ ] TASK-F-022-07: Observability, metrics, security and deploy
    - [ ] - Add Prometheus metrics: splits_generated_total, splits_generation_errors_total, avg_generation_latency_seconds, splits_persisted_total
    - [ ] - Add structured tracing (OpenTelemetry) propagation: use trace_id from orchestrator; include in logs and persisted metadata
    - [ ] - Implement RBAC checks: ensure only workspace members with session access can generate/persist splits
    - [ ] - Add Helm/K8s deployment manifests or update existing service chart, include feature flag (feature.split_service.enabled) for safe rollouts
    - [ ] - Add secrets/config entries for generator_version and feature-flag toggles; validate in staging before prod rollout

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - Unit tests for split computation (edge cases: exactly 500 rows, identical timestamps, sparse timestamps)
- [ ] - Integration tests: POST generate -> DB persist -> GET by id returns same config, with mock governance_manifest inputs
- [ ] - Contract tests with Data Modeler subagent (F-021) to ensure split_config consumes expected fields
- [ ] - API contract tests for error responses (invalid gold_table_spec, unauthorized, malformed dates)
- [ ] - Load test: simulate 100 concurrent split generation requests, validate latency SLO < P95 5s for small tables (profile-only), ensure no DB deadlocks
- [ ] - Security tests: RBAC enforcement tests, attempt cross-workspace access and verify rejection
- [ ] - End-to-end pipeline smoke test: run session pipeline through Stage 3 with generated split_config included and assert pipeline proceeds when checks pass and halts on failures

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - POST /api/v1/splits/generate implemented and documented in OpenAPI with examples
- [ ] - split_config JSON schema validated by unit tests and contract tests with F-021
- [ ] - DQ checks implemented and return structured dqc_results with error codes for failures
- [ ] - Persistence with audit metadata is implemented and migrations applied in CI pipeline
- [ ] - CI pipeline runs unit + integration tests and coverage > 80% for new modules
- [ ] - Logging, metrics, and tracing integrated, and basic dashboard created for key metrics
- [ ] - Security: endpoint enforces authentication + workspace-scoped authorization
- [ ] - Deployed to staging behind feature flag and smoke-tested with orchestration trace_id propagation

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-021
- [ ] - Governance manifest and semantic_layer_version available from Stage 0/1 services for contract inputs

###### Agent Notes
- Implement this as a stateless microservice library + thin controller. The core split logic must not rely on LLMs — deterministic outcomes are required. Expose a version field (generator_version) in output so downstream agents can detect behavioral changes. Keep all input validation server-side; never accept client-provided split dates without canonicalizing to workspace timezone. Persist request_id to support idempotency. In logs and persisted metadata include trace_id/session_id/workspace_id to enable end-to-end traceability. Coordinate schema evolution with F-021 via contract tests; changes to split_config must bump generator_version and trigger regression tests in the training orchestrator.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-022-02: As an orchestrator/pipeline engineer, I want the pipeline to validate and enforce temporal split checks before feature engineering and training so that failing datasets (insufficient rows, insufficient windows, test size too small) block progression with actionable remediation rather than causing wasted compute or incorrect backtests.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - The orchestrator receives split_config (generated by service) and runs deterministic DQ rules; pipeline halts with human_required streaming event when a critical DQ gate fails.
- [ ] - For warnings (e.g., marginal training rows 500-550), the orchestrator surfaces a WARN streaming event and continues only if an explicit human override flag is set.
- [ ] - The orchestrator records DQ gate outcomes in the pipeline audit log with normalized fields: gate_id, status (PASS/WARN/FAIL), reason, suggested_action, timestamp, actor (system/user).
- [ ] - If split_config indicates insufficient windows for 5-window walk-forward, the orchestrator either retries with adjusted window count (configurable fallback) or halts and requests user action; behavior is configurable via pipeline config.
- [ ] - Integration tests validate that pipeline blocks correctly for ERR_MIN_TRAIN_ROWS and proceeds for valid splits.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-022-08: Add pipeline gating logic to orchestrator for split DQ checks
    - [ ] - Implement gate evaluator module that ingests split_config and produces gate_results with statuses and suggested_action fields
    - [ ] - Map split service error codes to gate_ids used in pipeline audit log (e.g., GATE_SPLIT_MIN_TRAIN_ROWS -> ERR_MIN_TRAIN_ROWS)
    - [ ] - Implement streaming SSE event emission types: dq_gate (with details) and human_required when fail requires user intervention
    - [ ] - Implement retry policy for transient errors versus hard-block errors
    - [ ] - Add unit tests for gate evaluator mapping and event emission
- [ ] TASK-F-022-09: Implement human-in-the-loop override flow for held features and split warnings
    - [ ] - Add endpoint POST /api/v1/sessions/{id}/splits/approve to accept user-approved overrides with reason and actor metadata
    - [ ] - Ensure approvals are recorded in audit log and persisted with split_config.approvals[] including actor_id and timestamp
    - [ ] - Update orchestrator to accept override token and resume pipeline execution when approval present
    - [ ] - Implement UI-safe guard: approval TTL (e.g., 24 hours) after which pipeline must revalidate splits
    - [ ] - Add tests ensuring that approved overrides allow pipeline continuation and are auditable
- [ ] TASK-F-022-10: Audit logging and observability integration for split gate outcomes
    - [ ] - Design audit log event schema additions for split gate results and overrides (fields: trace_id, session_id, split_config_id, gate_id, status, reason, suggested_action, actor_id)
    - [ ] - Implement append-only audit log write on gate outcome and on override calls
    - [ ] - Add dashboard panels to staging Grafana for split gate failure counts and avg time-to-override
    - [ ] - Add alerts for repeated failures across workspaces (rate > threshold) indicating systematic data quality problems
    - [ ] - Add tests verifying audit entries are written for PASS/WARN/FAIL cases
- [ ] TASK-F-022-11: Configurable fallback policy for insufficient windows
    - [ ] - Implement configuration option in pipeline (split_fallback_policy) that allows fallback to 3 expanding windows if 5 cannot be created and behavior=auto_fallback
    - [ ] - Ensure fallback is recorded as a gate_result with reason and chosen policy
    - [ ] - Add unit tests for fallback policy selection and effect on generated walk_forward_windows
    - [ ] - Add documentation explaining tradeoffs of reducing windows (less robust validation)
    - [ ] - Add integration test ensuring training orchestrator computes metrics using the fallback windows when policy applied

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - Unit tests for orchestrator gate evaluator (all gate code paths PASS/WARN/FAIL/fallback)
- [ ] - Integration test scenario: small dataset -> POST generate returns ERR_MIN_TRAIN_ROWS -> orchestrator halts and emits human_required SSE with remediation text
- [ ] - Integration test scenario: borderline dataset -> generate returns WARN -> orchestrator continues only after approval endpoint is called
- [ ] - End-to-end smoke: start session pipeline in staging with valid split_config and assert pipeline progresses to feature engineering stage
- [ ] - Security test: ensure approval endpoint requires session owner or workspace admin role; test unauthorized approval is rejected
- [ ] - Load test: orchestrator handles concurrent sessions generating/validating splits without blocking threads (stress test SSE event emission)

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - Orchestrator gate evaluator consumes split_config and emits deterministic dq_gate events for PASS/WARN/FAIL
- [ ] - Human-in-the-loop approval endpoint implemented and audited; pipeline resumes on valid approval
- [ ] - Audit log writes for split gates present and queryable; dashboards created for key metrics
- [ ] - Fallback policy implemented and tested; default behavior is to halt unless auto_fallback enabled
- [ ] - Integration tests added to CI to validate blocking behavior on split failures

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-021
- [ ] - Split generation service (US-F-022-01) must be deployed to staging for integration tests

###### Agent Notes
- Gate rules are deterministic and must not call LLMs. Use the same error codes provided by the split service so mapping is straightforward. Emit dq_gate SSE events with trace_id and split_config_id for full traceability. Approvals must be idempotent and signed by actor_id; orchestrator should attach approval metadata to pipeline trace. Default pipeline behavior: block on critical failures; warn on borderline and require explicit approval. Keep fallback policy configurable per workspace for early adopter flexibility.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-022-03: As a product analyst / power user, I want a lightweight frontend preview and override UI that shows split date ranges, row counts, walk-forward windows, and DQ checks so that I can review, accept, or request changes to the temporal splits before training proceeds.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - The session UI includes a 'Split Preview' panel after Data Modeler stage that displays the split_config with clear date ranges, row counts, and a summarized dqc_results list.
- [ ] - The UI shows walk-forward windows (5 windows) as a compact visual timeline and allows users to expand each window to see train/validation date ranges.
- [ ] - When DQ checks include HOLD flags or critical errors, the UI shows actionable suggestions (expand date range, lower horizon, approve hold feature) and a prominent 'Request change' or 'Approve and continue' button.
- [ ] - Approve action calls POST /api/v1/sessions/{id}/splits/approve with required metadata and shows success/failure states; only authorized users can approve.
- [ ] - All UI calls handle errors gracefully: show inline error card with retry button, and log client-side errors to the observability pipeline.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-022-12: Design Split Preview UI mockup and accessibility guidelines
    - [ ] - Create low-fidelity mockups showing split date ranges, row counts, DQ summary, and walk-forward visual timeline
    - [ ] - Define accessible labels, aria attributes, and keyboard interactions for the timeline and expand/collapse windows
    - [ ] - Produce copy text guidelines for DQ messages and suggested remediation language
    - [ ] - Create a small interaction spec describing approve and request-change flows
- [ ] TASK-F-022-13: Implement frontend Split Preview panel (React/vanilla per product stack)
    - [ ] - Implement API client to call GET /api/v1/splits/{id} and render split_config with metadata
    - [ ] - Render walk-forward windows as compact timeline with expand-to-detail functionality and row counts per window
    - [ ] - Render DQ results list with color-coded statuses and suggested actions
    - [ ] - Add 'Approve and continue' and 'Request change' buttons wired to POST /api/v1/sessions/{id}/splits/approve and POST /api/v1/sessions/{id}/splits/request_change respectively
    - [ ] - Add loading skeletons and error cards with retry controls
- [ ] TASK-F-022-14: Frontend authorization, validation and client-side error handling
    - [ ] - Enforce UI-level role checks and disable approve button for unauthorized users; show tooltip explaining permission requirements
    - [ ] - Validate returned split_config against client-side JSON schema and surface mismatch errors for early detection
    - [ ] - Capture and send client-side errors to observability (Sentry) with context (workspace_id, session_id, split_config_id)
    - [ ] - Add unit tests for UI components (render with success, warn, fail states) and accessibility snapshot tests
    - [ ] - Add e2e tests simulating user approving split and verifying orchestrator resumes
- [ ] TASK-F-022-15: Localization, documentation, and UX polish
    - [ ] - Add copy to explain default 70/15/15 behavior and what walk-forward windows mean; include link to help docs
    - [ ] - Ensure timeline uses workspace timezone and renders ISO8601 tooltips on hover
    - [ ] - Add small confirmation modal for approve action requiring user to type 'APPROVE' for irreversible approvals (configurable)
    - [ ] - Add help icon with quick tooltip examples of remediation steps (expand date range, change horizon)
    - [ ] - Update product docs and changelog referencing new Split Preview panel

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - Component unit tests for SplitPreview rendering PASS/WARN/FAIL cases
- [ ] - Accessibility tests: aria attributes, keyboard navigation through timeline, color contrast checks
- [ ] - E2E scenario: user views split preview for a session, approves split, orchestrator resumes; assert artifact proceeds to feature engineering
- [ ] - Security test: unauthorized user attempts approve action and receives 403; audit log entry not recorded for unauthorized attempt
- [ ] - Usability test: run a short moderated session with product analyst to verify messages are actionable and clear

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - Split Preview UI implemented and accessible; displays split_config, walk-forward windows, and DQ summary
- [ ] - Approve and request-change actions wired to backend endpoints and require proper authorization
- [ ] - Frontend unit and e2e tests added to CI; accessibility checks included
- [ ] - Client-side error handling exists and errors are reported to observability
- [ ] - UX copy and docs updated; QA sign-off completed on staging

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] US-F-022-01
- [ ] - POST/GET split endpoints must exist and return the documented schema

###### Agent Notes
- Keep the UI minimal for MVP: read-only preview + approve/req-change buttons. Approvals must call the orchestrator-approved endpoint and include user_id, reason, and trace_id. Do not implement free-form split editing in MVP — only preview and approve flows. Ensure timezone normalization on display matches backend normalization. All UI interactions must be idempotent: repeated clicks produce exactly one approval record.

#### Requirement / Feature: Grain uniqueness & join-path validator (detect fan-out)

**Feature ID:** `F-023`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, data`

**Rationale:** Fan-out detection is a critical gate; prevents silently inflated metrics.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-021
- [ ] F-011

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-023-01: As a data modeler subagent and backend engineer, I want an automated validator that verifies grain uniqueness and detects join-path fan-out before gold table creation, so that pipelines never silently produce inflated metrics or duplicate rows.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Run the validator automatically during the Data Modeler subagent step (Stage 3) before emitting CREATE TABLE DDL or INSERT SELECT statements
- [ ] Return a deterministic result enum: PASS | WARN | FAIL with structured payload describing offending join edges and duplicate row samples
- [ ] Block pipeline progression on FAIL and surface a human-readable remediation message and suggested fix actions
- [ ] Mark WARN when join introduces multiplicative row inflation below configurable threshold and require user acknowledgment to proceed
- [ ] Log every validator run with trace_id, input join-path metadata, sample counts, and output result to the pipeline audit log (append-only)
- [ ] Expose validator output via /api/v1/sessions/{id}/spec or /api/v1/gold/{workspace_id}/{table}/validate endpoint for programmatic consumption
- [ ] Execute within performance target: validation run for joins across up to 5 tables with 100k sampled rows completes within 60s (MVP soft target)
- [ ] Do not send raw data rows to LLM agents; only send aggregated stats and top-n offending samples (anonymized) when needed

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-023-01: Design validator algorithm and data contract
    - [ ] Define input schema for validator: grain_keys[], join_paths[], column_cardinalities, fk_null_pct, expected_row_count
    - [ ] Define output schema JSON: { result: PASS|WARN|FAIL, offending_joins: [{ left_table, right_table, join_type, multiplier_estimate, sample_rows_hash }], diagnostics, suggested_fixes }
    - [ ] Specify sampling strategy: stratified random sample per table with deterministic seed using trace_id
    - [ ] Specify multiplicative inflation metric definition and thresholds (e.g., multiplier > 1.01 => WARN, >1.05 => FAIL by default configurable)
    - [ ] Document error codes and remediation codes for programmatic clients (e.g., ERR_GRAIN_DUPLICATE, ERR_MANY_TO_MANY)
- [ ] TASK-F-023-02: Implement core validator engine (backend/data)
    - [ ] Implement SQL generator that computes distinct counts for grain keys on each table and for joined paths using warehouse dialect abstraction
    - [ ] Implement join-path fan-out estimator that computes expected multiplier = joined_row_count / base_grain_row_count and captures offending FK columns
    - [ ] Implement duplicate-row sampler that returns top-10 offending grain key combinations (hashed/anonymized) and counts
    - [ ] Implement deterministic sampling with limits (max 100k rows per table) and timeouts with graceful fallback
    - [ ] Implement aggregator to produce the output schema and include suggested fixes based on detected issue type
- [ ] TASK-F-023-03: Integrate validator into Data Modeler subagent flow and DQ gate
    - [ ] Invoke validator at the end of join path planning stage and before generating final CREATE TABLE/INSERT statements
    - [ ] Wire validator result into Stage 3 DQ gate logic to PASS/WARN/FAIL the pipeline according to thresholds
    - [ ] Ensure DATA MODELER subagent uses validator diagnostics to alter generated DDL (e.g., add dedupe via ROW_NUMBER() partition pattern when user accepts fix)
    - [ ] Add structured error/hold message to the session streaming SSE channel type 'dq_gate' with payload linking to remediation actions
    - [ ] Ensure failure transitions set session state to human_required with clear remediation text and suggested fixes
- [ ] TASK-F-023-04: Add API endpoints and backend services for validator results and re-run
    - [ ] Add GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/grain_validator to return last validator run result and diagnostics
    - [ ] Add POST /api/v1/sessions/{id}/grain_validator/recheck to trigger a re-check (idempotent) with optional override parameters
    - [ ] Add POST /api/v1/sessions/{id}/apply_join_fix to accept an automated fix (dedupe key, aggregation, switch join type) and persist change to session_spec
    - [ ] Add RBAC checks so only session owner or workspace editor can apply fixes
    - [ ] Add rate limiting and request size validation to protect warehouse from accidental heavy queries
- [ ] TASK-F-023-05: Implement logging, tracing, and audit integration
    - [ ] Emit structured logs for every validator run including trace_id, session_id, workspace_id, start_ts, end_ts, sampled_row_counts, result, and diagnostic codes
    - [ ] Emit an audit event to pipeline audit log with immutable payload and link to validator result JSON
    - [ ] Add OpenTelemetry spans around validator SQL queries and aggregate computation
    - [ ] Mask PII in any sample outputs and ensure hashed sample_rows_hash is recorded instead of raw key values
    - [ ] Add dashboard metrics (Prometheus): validator_runs_total, validator_failures_total, validator_duration_seconds histogram
- [ ] TASK-F-023-06: Backend security and error handling
    - [ ] Validate all external inputs against JSON schema and return structured 4xx errors for invalid requests
    - [ ] Implement graceful timeout handling: if validator sampling queries exceed timeout, return WARN with partial diagnostics and allow user to re-run with larger sample settings
    - [ ] Ensure credentials for warehouse connections are never logged and use secrets manager references
    - [ ] Implement circuit breaker for repeated heavy validator runs per workspace to protect warehouse costs
    - [ ] Add unit tests for error branches, malformed joins, and timeouts

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Write unit tests for SQL generation, multiplier computation, and output schema validation
- [ ] Write integration tests against DuckDB test harness and Snowflake simulator using synthetic datasets that include: perfect one-to-one joins, small fan-out (WARN), large fan-out (FAIL), many-to-many relationships, and PK violations
- [ ] Write end-to-end tests that run a full Stage 3 pipeline with validator: expect pipeline to halt on FAIL and proceed on PASS
- [ ] Create performance test job that measures validator runtime with 1k/10k/100k sample sizes and records durations
- [ ] Run security tests to ensure PII is masked and secrets not logged
- [ ] Add fuzz tests for malformed join-path inputs and large join graph shapes

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Merge request with unit and integration tests passed and code reviewed
- [ ] Validator engine deployed to staging and callable via API endpoints
- [ ] Validator invoked in Data Modeler subagent and blocks pipeline appropriately for FAIL
- [ ] Structured logs and audit events produced for validator runs
- [ ] Performance target validated on benchmark datasets (<= 60s for 5-table, 100k sample)
- [ ] Documentation added: design doc, API spec, config knobs (thresholds, sample limits)

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-021
- [ ] F-011

###### Agent Notes
- When implementing the validator, prefer deterministic SQL-based strategies over ML heuristics. Use the warehouse dialect abstraction layer (already present from F-011) to emit efficient COUNT(DISTINCT ...) and approximate_count_distinct where available. Keep raw row samples out of LLM inputs; only send aggregated diagnostics and hashed/anonymized sample identifiers. Make validation thresholds configurable via workspace settings and ensure RBAC checks guard any automatic remediation endpoints.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-023-02: As an analyst or workspace user, I want actionable remediation suggestions and one-click fixes for detected fan-out (e.g., add deduplication key, change join type, add bridge table), so that I can quickly resolve grain uniqueness issues and continue the analysis without writing SQL.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Present validator diagnostics in the session UI as a human-readable card listing offending join edges, multiplier estimate, and example counts
- [ ] Show at least three automated suggested fixes with clear pros/cons: add dedupe key (ROW_NUMBER() based), change join type (inner/left), aggregate child table before join, propose bridge table for many-to-many
- [ ] Allow user to accept a suggested fix which triggers backend endpoint to modify session_spec and re-run Stage 3 automatically
- [ ] Record the chosen remediation in the session audit trail and include it in the resulting artifact lineage
- [ ] Ensure UI actions require confirmation for destructive fixes and show preview of expected row counts after fix before applying
- [ ] Disable one-click fixes when the suggested fix would require human creation of a bridge table; instead show a guided workflow to create a bridge table

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-023-07: Design remediation UX and API contract
    - [ ] Design UI card wireframe for validator diagnostics showing offending joins, multiplier, suggested fixes, and Apply / Preview / Dismiss buttons
    - [ ] Define the API contract for apply-fix: input { session_id, fix_type, parameters } and output { new_session_spec_diff, preview_estimates }
    - [ ] Define preview API that returns expected row_count and example aggregated diagnostics post-fix without persisting changes
    - [ ] Document permissions required to apply fixes and audit entries to be created
    - [ ] Specify UX text for pros/cons and confirmation dialogs for each fix type
- [ ] TASK-F-023-08: Implement frontend UI components and flows
    - [ ] Implement validator result card component in session run view with accessible markup and keyboard navigation
    - [ ] Implement preview modal that calls preview endpoint and renders expected row counts and a diff of DDL changes
    - [ ] Implement Apply flow that calls apply-fix endpoint and shows progress + SSE messages for pipeline re-run
    - [ ] Implement error and warning states with clear remediation instructions and retry options
    - [ ] Add unit and integration tests for the new components and flows
- [ ] TASK-F-023-09: Implement backend apply-fix logic and session_spec mutation
    - [ ] Implement server-side handlers to transform session_spec according to fix_type: add dedupe logic, wrap child table in aggregated CTE, change join_type, or flag bridge_table_needed
    - [ ] When applying dedupe fix, generate deterministic ROW_NUMBER() PARTITION BY grain_keys ORDER BY <stable_key> pattern and add to DDL generation path
    - [ ] When applying aggregate fix, generate pre-aggregated CTE with grouped metrics at grain and use that in join path
    - [ ] Persist session_spec diff and new session run record; ensure re-run scheduling is transactional
    - [ ] Add validation to ensure applied fix results in PASS or WARN rather than an immediate FAIL; otherwise abort and surface reason
- [ ] TASK-F-023-10: Audit, lineage, and artifact integration
    - [ ] Write audit event when user previews or applies a fix containing: user_id, session_id, fix_type, parameters, timestamp
    - [ ] Include remediation choice in artifact lineage metadata (gold.feature_manifest and artifact.record.lineage)
    - [ ] If fix required adding a bridge table, create a task artifact with instructions and link to workspace data engineering queue
    - [ ] Update artifact DQ footer to show that a manual remediation was applied and the validator state at time of write
    - [ ] Add tests verifying lineage carries remediation info into persisted gold table metadata
- [ ] TASK-F-023-11: Frontend/backend security and validation for apply-fix
    - [ ] Validate all apply-fix parameters server-side against JSON schema and current session_spec state
    - [ ] Enforce RBAC: only session owner or workspace editors can apply fixes; log attempts by unauthorized principals
    - [ ] Rate-limit apply-fix calls to prevent accidentally repeated heavy re-runs
    - [ ] Ensure input sanitization to prevent injection into SQL generator (use parameterized templates)
    - [ ] Add integration tests for authorization and invalid-parameter handling

###### Test Plan
- [ ] End-to-end UI test: validator FAIL -> user views fixes -> preview shows expected counts -> apply fix -> pipeline re-runs and results in PASS
- [ ] Unit test: apply-fix handlers produce correct session_spec diffs for each fix_type
- [ ] Integration test: applying aggregate fix produces gold table with expected distinct row count
- [ ] Security test: unauthorized user cannot apply fixes and receives 403
- [ ] Usability test: show sample analyst flows to product and data engineers and capture feedback

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] UI shows validator diagnostics and suggested fixes in staging
- [ ] Preview endpoint returns correct estimated row counts for representative datasets
- [ ] Apply-fix endpoint mutates session_spec and triggers a re-run which respects the new join path
- [ ] Audit events and lineage metadata include remediation details
- [ ] Frontend and backend unit/integration tests passed and code reviewed
- [ ] UX text reviewed by product and data team for clarity

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] TASK-F-023-01
- [ ] TASK-F-023-02
- [ ] F-021
- [ ] F-011

###### Agent Notes
- Front-end should not render raw offending key values; use hashes or aggregated counts. Apply-fix server handlers must operate on the session_spec object and rely on canonical SQL generator (from F-011) to avoid duplicating SQL logic. Always return a preview estimate before persisting changes. For MVP, prioritize three fix types: dedupe via ROW_NUMBER(), aggregate child table, and change join type. Bridge table creation should be a guided manual workflow (create ticket) not automated in MVP.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-023-03: As QA lead and SRE, I want a comprehensive test harness, monitoring, and CI checks for the grain uniqueness validator, so that validator regressions are caught early and production runs are observable and within performance budgets.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Provide unit tests covering algorithm branches, threshold thresholds, error handling, and SQL generation
- [ ] Provide integration tests that run against an in-memory DuckDB instance and a Snowflake staging connector using synthetic datasets that include many-to-many relationships and PK violations
- [ ] Add E2E pipeline tests that validate pipeline halts on FAIL and proceeds on PASS with applied fixes
- [ ] Add CI gate that fails pull requests when validator-related tests regress or coverage drops below threshold
- [ ] Add monitoring: Prometheus metrics exported (validator_runs_total, validator_failures_total, validator_duration_seconds) and Grafana dashboard with SLOs
- [ ] Add alerting: PagerDuty or Slack alert when validator failure rate exceeds configured threshold or avg duration exceeds SLA
- [ ] Add regression dataset bundle and test data generator for repeatable tests

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-023-12: Create unit tests and test fixtures
    - [ ] Write unit tests for multiplier computation with synthetic numeric inputs covering PASS/WARN/FAIL thresholds
    - [ ] Write unit tests for SQL generation templates across supported dialects using small schema mocks
    - [ ] Create fixtures for malformed join inputs, empty joins, and missing FK metadata
    - [ ] Add tests for timeout and partial-sample WARN behavior
    - [ ] Add tests that verify PII masking on sample outputs
- [ ] TASK-F-023-13: Implement integration tests with DuckDB harness and Snowflake staging
    - [ ] Provision DuckDB in CI runner and load synthetic datasets representing 1:1, 1:many, many:many join shapes
    - [ ] Create Snowflake-staging test configuration with small test warehouse and run validator against it (mocks allowed if access restricted)
    - [ ] Automate runs that assert expected validator result for each synthetic scenario
    - [ ] Record sample queries and results as artifacts for debugging
    - [ ] Ensure cleanup scripts remove test artifacts after runs
- [ ] TASK-F-023-14: Add E2E pipeline tests and CI integration
    - [ ] Build an E2E test that simulates a full Stage 3 run including validator and either halting or proceeding depending on input
    - [ ] Integrate tests into CI pipeline; mark validator tests as required checks
    - [ ] Fail PR merge if tests fail or if new code increases average validator runtime beyond regression threshold
    - [ ] Add labeling for slow tests and configure nightly runs for longer performance benchmarks
    - [ ] Document how to run E2E tests locally for developer iteration
- [ ] TASK-F-023-15: Implement observability, metrics, and alerting
    - [ ] Expose Prometheus metrics: validator_runs_total (labels: workspace_id, result), validator_duration_seconds histogram, validator_sample_rows_total
    - [ ] Create Grafana dashboard template showing run rate, failure rate, p95 duration, and top offending workspaces
    - [ ] Create alert rules: alert when failure_rate > 2% over 15min or p95 duration > 120s over 15min
    - [ ] Wire alerts to Slack channel and PagerDuty escalation policy
    - [ ] Add runbook describing steps to investigate validator alerts and remediate common causes
- [ ] TASK-F-023-16: Add CI pre-commit hooks and static analysis
    - [ ] Add JSON schema validation step in CI for validator output contract
    - [ ] Add linter rules for new code and ensure code formatting in pre-commit config
    - [ ] Add contract tests that validate API swagger/OpenAPI specs for new endpoints
    - [ ] Integrate security scanner to check for logging of secrets and PII
    - [ ] Add PR template checklist requiring test coverage and performance notes for validator changes

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Run unit and integration test suite on each PR; require 100% pass for merge
- [ ] Run nightly performance benchmark with representative datasets and record durations
- [ ] Run synthetic failure injection tests that simulate slow warehouse responses and ensure timeouts behave as WARN
- [ ] Perform chaos test: simulate repeated heavy validator runs and verify circuit breaker triggers
- [ ] Run security scans to ensure no PII or credentials present in logs or artifacts

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] All unit, integration, and E2E tests pass in CI and are required for PR merges
- [ ] Prometheus metrics and Grafana dashboards deployed to staging with alerts configured
- [ ] Regression datasets and test generator committed to repository and documented
- [ ] CI gates enforce validator test success and performance budgets
- [ ] On-call runbook published and on-call notified of new alert rules

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] TASK-F-023-02
- [ ] TASK-F-023-05
- [ ] F-021
- [ ] F-011

###### Agent Notes
- Provide a stable synthetic dataset generator to reproduce join shapes deterministically; include small CSV fixtures for 1:1, 1:many, many:many. CI must be configured to run DuckDB-based integration tests without external warehouse credentials. Alerts should favor Slack for immediate visibility and PagerDuty for high-severity production incidents. Keep test runtimes reasonable by limiting sample sizes in CI; reserve large-scale benchmarks for nightly runs.

#### Requirement / Feature: Target leakage scan service (leakage_risk scoring)

**Feature ID:** `F-024`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, data`

**Rationale:** Leakage detection is a blocking quality gate required by PRD.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-021
- [ ] F-005

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-024-01: As a data scientist / modeler, I want an automated leakage_risk scoring service that computes a deterministic leakage score (0–1) for each candidate feature column, so that the pipeline can surface high-risk features and block or require confirmation before training.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Implement a leakage_risk scoring algorithm that returns a numeric score 0.0–1.0 for each feature column based only on server-side statistics and metadata (no raw row data outside warehouse).
- [ ] Return a JSON payload for each feature: { feature_name, derivation, leakage_risk, evidence: [{ rule, metric, value }], sample_stats_reference }.
- [ ] Flag features for three buckets: safe (<=0.3), hold_for_user_confirmation (>0.3 and <=0.7), auto_drop (>0.7).
- [ ] Ensure leakage_risk calculations run within feature-engineering timeframe targets (P50 < 60s for typical gold_table_spec < 100k rows statistical sampling).
- [ ] Log deterministic reasoning and evidence for every computed leakage_risk item to audit trail with trace_id.
- [ ] Ensure no PII columns are used for scoring and leak evidence does not include raw PII values; block scoring with a clear error if a feature depends on PII without admin approval.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-024-01: Design leakage_risk scoring specification and deterministic rule set
    - [ ] Review PRD leakage definitions and existing DQ gate thresholds to extract required rules and thresholds
    - [ ] Document deterministic scoring rules (e.g., temporal proximity tests, lag-zero detection, forward-looking window correlation, timestamp null alignment, aggregation-of-target checks) with formulae
    - [ ] Define evidence items schema: { rule_id, description, metric_name, metric_value, threshold, pass_fail }
    - [ ] Define output JSON schema for leakage_risk service and add JSON Schema for validation
    - [ ] Record performance SLOs and sampling strategy for different warehouse sizes
- [ ] TASK-F-024-02: Implement backend leakage_risk microservice and API
    - [ ] Create new service module leakage_scan_service in backend codebase following existing service patterns
    - [ ] Implement API endpoint POST /api/v1/leakage_scan that accepts gold_table_spec and feature_candidates and returns leakage manifest JSON
    - [ ] Add dialect-aware SQL builders to sample necessary aggregated statistics from warehouse via existing warehouse interface (Snowflake, BigQuery, Postgres, DuckDB)
    - [ ] Implement sampling safeguards: limit sampled rows, use precomputed column stats from governance manifest when available
    - [ ] Implement request validation and JSON Schema enforcement for input and output
    - [ ] Add unit tests for API input validation, schema compliance, and error paths
- [ ] TASK-F-024-03: Implement deterministic leakage detection checks (core rules)
    - [ ] Implement lag-zero detection: flag any feature derived from the target or same timestamp where derivation uses target without lag
    - [ ] Implement forward-looking window correlation: compute correlation between candidate feature shifted backward/forward relative to target to detect future leakage
    - [ ] Implement aggregation leakage detection: detect features aggregated over a window including the prediction point (e.g., rolling including current row) and compute contribution score
    - [ ] Implement join-based leakage detection: check if join keys allow rows with future timestamps to attach (using source PK/FK freshness and max(timestamp) per key)
    - [ ] Implement temporal-exposure scoring: compute min_lead_time and min_lag and translate to score using defined decay function
    - [ ] Create deterministic composition of rule-level evidence into final leakage_risk using weighted sum and normalization
- [ ] TASK-F-024-04: Integrate leakage scan with feature manifest generation pipeline (Data Modeler subagent)
    - [ ] Add a pipeline step call to leakage_scan_service after enriched_feature_manifest is proposed and before final manifest lock
    - [ ] Ensure leakage_scan_service receives only schema/meta/sampled-statistics and not raw rows in memory outside warehouse proxy
    - [ ] On response, annotate enriched_feature_manifest entries with leakage_risk and evidence array
    - [ ] Add logic to mark features with leakage_risk > 0.7 as dropped automatically and add an audit event
    - [ ] Add logic to mark features with leakage_risk > 0.3 as 'held' and emit a pipeline SSE event human_required with required payload
    - [ ] Add integration tests for the pipeline stage with mocked leakage_scan responses
- [ ] TASK-F-024-05: Add logging, observability, and audit trail for leakage decisions
    - [ ] Emit structured logs per-scan including trace_id, workspace_id, session_id, gold_table_spec_id, elapsed_time_ms, and counts of features per bucket
    - [ ] Write leakage_scan results to audit log table gold.leakage_scans_{session_id} with full evidence JSON and versioned schema
    - [ ] Instrument OpenTelemetry spans for the leakage_scan_service and add metrics: requests_total, requests_failed, avg_latency_ms, features_scanned_total
    - [ ] Add alerting rule for scan failures or latency > threshold to DevOps dashboard
    - [ ] Add retention policy: leakage evidence stored 90 days by default
- [ ] TASK-F-024-06: Implement security checks and PII-safe behavior
    - [ ] Add pre-scan check using governance_manifest to detect any feature that references columns with PII flags and block unless admin_approved_for_ml=true
    - [ ] Ensure all sample queries redact or hash any column that may contain PII before any in-memory processing
    - [ ] Add unit tests that verify that PII-flagged columns trigger block and that logs do not contain raw PII
    - [ ] Document threat model and approval workflow for admin enabling PII features for ML
    - [ ] Add API-level permission check: only session owner or workspace admin can run leakage scans that involve low-confidence semantic definitions

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Write unit tests for every deterministic rule with synthetic examples (lag-zero, forward correlation, aggregation inclusion)
- [ ] Write integration tests that spin up a test warehouse (DuckDB) with seeded tables and run full leakage_scan via POST /api/v1/leakage_scan
- [ ] Write end-to-end pipeline test that runs data modeler subagent and verifies features are annotated/dropped/held as expected
- [ ] Run performance tests for typical small/medium table sizes to ensure latency SLOs; run load tests for concurrent scans
- [ ] Run security tests to confirm no raw PII is logged and that PII-flagged features are blocked
- [ ] Run deterministic regression tests to ensure the same seed dataset yields identical leakage_risk and evidence

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Write and review leakage scoring spec and JSON Schema checked into repo docs
- [ ] Merge backend leakage_scan_service with unit tests and integration tests into main branch
- [ ] Validate that leakage_scan runs in pipeline stage on representative dataset and produces expected buckets
- [ ] Verify logs, traces, and audit table entries appear with correct fields and evidence
- [ ] Confirm PII-safety tests pass and appropriate blocking occurs
- [ ] Deploy service to staging environment and run end-to-end acceptance tests

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-021
- [ ] F-005

###### Agent Notes
- Leakage detection must be deterministic, explainable, and auditable. Avoid any ML/LLM heuristics for core scoring — use statistical rules with clear evidence fields. Use governance_manifest stats where possible to avoid heavy sampling. Provide a JSON Schema for evidence and ensure all outputs are validated. For future improvements consider optional ML classifier trained on labeled leakage examples, but not in MVP. Ensure the service supports multiple warehouse dialects and uses the existing warehouse proxy to prevent raw credentials or rows from being sent to LLMs.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-024-02: As an orchestrator / pipeline runner, I want the pipeline to automatically act on leakage_risk results (auto-drop >0.7, hold 0.3–0.7 for human confirmation, proceed with safe features) so that model training never proceeds with high-leakage features and borderline features require explicit human consent.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Ensure pipeline halts training when any remaining selected features have leakage_risk >0.7 and emits a clear error with remediation steps.
- [ ] Ensure pipeline emits a human_required SSE event when any feature has leakage_risk >0.3 and <=0.7 with payload describing held features and evidence and a one-click confirm/decline action path.
- [ ] Ensure when user confirms held features via the UI, confirmation provenance is recorded in the audit log with actor_id and timestamp and the pipeline resumes.
- [ ] Ensure when held features are declined, they are removed from feature manifest and training proceeds automatically.
- [ ] Ensure pipeline enforces the PRD rule: lag-zero values of target metric flagged and flagged features are never silently allowed without explicit confirmation.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-024-07: Add pipeline control logic to enforce leakage-based gating
    - [ ] Modify the data modeler subagent pipeline orchestration to call leakage_scan_service after enriched_feature_manifest step
    - [ ] Implement gating logic: if any feature has leakage_risk >0.7 -> block and produce pipeline error with remediation text
    - [ ] Implement gating logic: if any feature has 0.3 < leakage_risk <=0.7 -> mark features held and emit SSE human_required event with payload
    - [ ] Implement resumption handlers: listen for user confirmation events and update manifest accordingly
    - [ ] Write end-to-end tests that simulate all three paths: proceed, hold+confirm, block+fail
- [ ] TASK-F-024-08: Extend SSE streaming protocol and session state for human confirmation
    - [ ] Add new SSE event type payload: type: leakage_hold with fields { held_features, evidence_summary, actions: [confirm, decline], timeout }
    - [ ] Implement server-side handler to accept POST /api/v1/sessions/{id}/leakage_decision with user decision and optional notes
    - [ ] Add server-side validation to ensure decision actor has permission (session owner or workspace admin)
    - [ ] Ensure SSE human_required events include trace_id and session_spec snapshot for audit
    - [ ] Add tests for SSE emission and for the decision endpoint including permission checks
- [ ] TASK-F-024-09: Add audit recording and manifest versioning for decisions
    - [ ] When a user confirms or declines held features, write an audit event with { session_id, user_id, decision, held_features, timestamp, evidence_snapshot }
    - [ ] Increment feature_manifest_version when held features are removed or confirmed
    - [ ] Expose the decision and previous manifest version in the artifact metadata and model card lineage
    - [ ] Add tests verifying audit event persistence and correct manifest versioning
- [ ] TASK-F-024-10: Add error handling, rollback, and retry semantics for gating failures
    - [ ] Implement idempotent behavior for leakage_decision endpoint to handle duplicate user clicks
    - [ ] Implement automatic rollback of partially written manifests if pipeline is blocked due to post-scan errors
    - [ ] Implement retry/backoff for transient warehouse sampling errors with clear user-facing message if persistent
    - [ ] Instrument metrics for gating failures and human hold rates for monitoring
    - [ ] Add integration tests covering transient failure recovery and idempotency
- [ ] TASK-F-024-11: Add devops and release steps for new pipeline gate
    - [ ] Add migration scripts for new audit tables and SSE event schema to infra repo
    - [ ] Update deployment manifests and CI pipelines to include leakage_scan_service and new endpoint
    - [ ] Add smoke tests in deployment pipeline that run a minimal session through the gating path
    - [ ] Coordinate release with semantic layer and session spec teams (F-021, F-005 dependencies)
    - [ ] Document operational runbook for failures and rollback

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Create pipeline integration test scenarios for: (a) all safe features, (b) held features then confirm, (c) held features then decline, (d) auto-drop features >0.7
- [ ] Simulate warehouse transient failure to verify retry/backoff and user-facing messaging
- [ ] Test SSE event visibility across web clients and ensure session owner receives the human_required event
- [ ] Test permission matrix for leakage_decision endpoint (owner vs viewer vs admin)
- [ ] Load test concurrent sessions emitting leakage_hold events to measure SSE throughput

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Pipeline halts, holds, and proceeds correctly across block/hold/proceed cases on staging environment
- [ ] SSE leakage_hold events are emitted and human decisions resume pipeline reliably
- [ ] Audit log contains entries for every decision with correct provenance
- [ ] Idempotency and retry behaviors verified in tests
- [ ] Deployment steps and runbook published and team notified

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-021
- [ ] F-005

###### Agent Notes
- The gating logic must be deterministic and auditable. Use SSE human_required events defined in PRD streaming protocol. Do not rely on LLMs to decide gating; the LLM may be used later to generate human-friendly explanations but core gating must be rule-based. Keep the decision payload minimal but include evidence snapshots to allow reproducibility. Ensure tight coupling with manifest versioning so that confirmations produce a new manifest version and are visible in artifact lineage.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-024-03: As an analyst using the UI, I want a simple, clear confirmation experience showing held features, their leakage_risk and evidence so that I can make an informed decision to include or exclude borderline features before model training proceeds.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Display a modal or in-line confirmation panel listing held features with leakage_risk, short natural-language reason, and evidence links (expandable) when pipeline emits leakage_hold SSE event.
- [ ] Provide two primary actions: Confirm (include features) and Exclude (remove features), plus optional 'Request human review' that notifies workspace admins.
- [ ] Ensure UI action calls POST /api/v1/sessions/{id}/leakage_decision and displays progress and final pipeline status (resumed or blocked).
- [ ] Ensure confirmation events are permission-checked: only session owners or workspace admins can confirm; other roles see read-only explanation with 'Request review' option.
- [ ] Log user action in audit trail and show confirmation provenance in the session activity panel and artifact DQ footer.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-024-12: Design UI confirmation experience and copy
    - [ ] Design modal wireframe showing held_features list with columns: feature_name, leakage_risk (color-coded), short_reason, evidence_expand
    - [ ] Write concise user-facing copy for reasons and remediation suggestions for each evidence type (e.g., 'Contains target values at same timestamp — considered lag-zero')
    - [ ] Create accessible UI spec: keyboard focus, aria labels, role announcements, and responsive layout
    - [ ] Align copy with legal/security guidance to avoid exposing PII in UI
    - [ ] Get design sign-off from product and UX
- [ ] TASK-F-024-13: Implement frontend SSE listener and confirmation UI
    - [ ] Implement client-side SSE handler to subscribe to session streaming and listen for type: leakage_hold events
    - [ ] Implement confirmation modal component using existing UI library and follow accessibility rules
    - [ ] Implement evidence expand UI which fetches evidence details (if necessary) from /api/v1/leakage_scan/{scan_id}/evidence
    - [ ] Implement action handlers that call POST /api/v1/sessions/{id}/leakage_decision and show in-progress spinner and success/error notifications
    - [ ] Add unit and integration tests for the component and SSE handling
- [ ] TASK-F-024-14: Implement frontend permission, audit display, and activity feed integration
    - [ ] Add permission checks to hide confirm button for non-owner/non-admin roles and show 'Request review' button instead
    - [ ] Implement 'Request review' action that opens a lightweight modal to notify admins via the existing notification channel
    - [ ] Display the decision provenance in the session activity timeline and in artifact Data Quality footer after pipeline resumes
    - [ ] Add tests verifying UI visibility rules across roles
- [ ] TASK-F-024-15: Add end-to-end QA, accessibility, and usability testing
    - [ ] Write E2E tests (Cypress or Playwright) that run a session to the gating point, surface modal, confirm features, and verify pipeline resumes
    - [ ] Run accessibility tests (axe) against modal and evidence expand flows
    - [ ] Run user acceptance testing with a small group of analysts to validate copy clarity
    - [ ] Document known limitations in UI help text (e.g., evidence is summary-level and links to audit for full trace)
- [ ] TASK-F-024-16: Add analytics and monitoring for human decision flows
    - [ ] Emit frontend telemetry events for leakage_hold_shown, leakage_confirm_clicked, leakage_exclude_clicked, leakage_request_review_clicked with session_id and feature counts
    - [ ] Add dashboard showing number of holds per day, avg time-to-decision, percentage confirmed vs excluded
    - [ ] Set alert on high hold rates (>10% of sessions) to product/engineering
    - [ ] Add retention policy for UI telemetry

###### Test Plan
- [ ] E2E user flow tests: session runs -> leakage_hold SSE arrives -> modal displays -> confirm -> pipeline resumes and artifact produced
- [ ] E2E user flow tests: session runs -> leakage_hold SSE arrives -> exclude -> feature removed -> train completes without excluded features
- [ ] Role-based access tests: viewer cannot confirm, admin can confirm; request-review triggers notification
- [ ] Accessibility tests: keyboard navigation, ARIA, color contrast for risk color-coding
- [ ] Usability tests: time-to-decision measurement and qualitative feedback capture

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Confirmation UI implemented and accessible in staging
- [ ] SSE events trigger modal and actions call the decision endpoint
- [ ] Role-based visibility and request-review flows validated
- [ ] E2E tests, accessibility tests, and UX sign-off completed
- [ ] Telemetry and dashboards show human decision metrics and are linked in runbook

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-021
- [ ] F-005

###### Agent Notes
- Keep the UI thin and focused: provide clear, non-technical explanations and link to the evidence stored in the audit table. Avoid showing raw sampled values to prevent accidental PII exposure — present aggregated evidence items only. Use existing SSE client infra and session activity feed. For later iterations consider adding a 'why' natural-language summary generated by an explanation agent, but ensure the generation uses only the deterministic evidence (not raw rows) and is logged.

### Epic: Gold Layer

#### Requirement / Feature: Gold table writer (atomic write to analytics_{workspace} schema)

**Feature ID:** `F-025`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, data`

**Rationale:** Persisting the gold table in the warehouse is required before training and artifact generation.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-021
- [ ] F-022

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-025-01: As a platform data engineer, I want the system to perform an atomic write of the generated gold table into analytics_{workspace} schema, so that downstream training and artifact generation always read a consistent, fully validated gold table and we can rollback on failure without producing partial or inconsistent artifacts.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] The gold table is written into analytics_{workspace} using a single atomic operation (no partial visibility of incomplete rows) for supported warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery, DuckDB).
- [ ] - [ ] The writer implements write-to-temp-then-atomic-swap semantics (temp table -> validate -> rename/move -> metadata update) and falls back to transactional semantics when the warehouse supports transactions.
- [ ] - [ ] Row count after write is within 2% of expected row count derived from the session_spec and validated pre-write; if outside 2% the write is blocked and a detailed delta is surfaced.
- [ ] - [ ] Prediction distribution check (mean/std/p5/p95) vs training target distribution is executed post-write and blocks the write if mean deviates > 3σ.
- [ ] - [ ] CI integrity: lower_ci_95 < predicted_value < upper_ci_95 must hold for > 99% of rows; violations block the write and surface a model implementation error.
- [ ] - [ ] Forecast horizon integrity: no forecast-split row has non-null actual_value; violation blocks the write.
- [ ] - [ ] Writes are idempotent for the same session_id + model_id pair and subsequent retries do not create duplicate tables or orphaned temp tables.
- [ ] - [ ] All successful writes produce an audit record with trace_id, session_id, model_id, gold_table_name, row_count, validation results, and governance_manifest_version.
- [ ] - [ ] On partial failure (warehouse error, network), temp objects are cleaned up and the system leaves no visible partial gold table.
- [ ] - [ ] Behavior is covered by unit, integration and end-to-end tests in CI and documented in the runbook.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-025-01: Implement backend gold table writer service (API + orchestrator) that performs temp-write -> validate -> atomic-swap workflow
    - [ ] - [ ] Design API contract for writer service: input payload (session_spec, model_id, feature_manifest_version, expected_row_count, ddl_or_select_sql, workspace_id, trace_id).
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement REST endpoint /internal/gold/write that accepts writer payload and enqueues job with trace_id.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement orchestrator that executes: create temp table, stream insert, run validations, swap/rename to final table, write metadata, cleanup temp tables.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement temp table naming convention and TTL policy to avoid collisions (e.g. analytics_{workspace}.tmp_gold_{session_id}_{uuid}).
    - [ ] - [ ] Add retries with exponential backoff and idempotency token handling (session_id+model_id) to avoid duplicate final swaps.
- [ ] TASK-F-025-02: Implement deterministic post-write data quality gate checks (row count tolerance, distribution checks, CI integrity, forecast horizon integrity)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement row count comparator that computes expected_row_count from session_spec and validates written row_count within configurable tolerance (default 2%).
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement distribution comparator: compute mean/std/p5/p95 for predicted_value and compare with stored training distribution; block on >3σ deviation.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement CI integrity check: compute percent rows where lower_ci_95 < predicted_value < upper_ci_95 and require ≥ 99%; block otherwise.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement forecast horizon integrity check: ensure all rows with split='forecast' have actual_value IS NULL; block otherwise.
    - [ ] - [ ] Return a structured dq_gate_result JSON with PASS|WARN|BLOCK and detailed diagnostics for all checks.
- [ ] TASK-F-025-03: Implement warehouse SQL dialect adapter and temp->swap implementations for Snowflake, BigQuery, DuckDB (dev), and Postgres (future-proof)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement Snowflake temp table creation + metadata-driven rename (CREATE TABLE <final> AS SELECT ... OR CREATE TABLE <temp>; then ALTER TABLE RENAME or CREATE TABLE AS and DROP).
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement BigQuery temp dataset/table write + atomic table patch via table copy or dataset-level swap pattern.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement DuckDB/local dev path using transactional CREATE TABLE and RENAME semantics for local testing.
    - [ ] - [ ] Abstract adapter behind a WarehouseClient interface to allow adding Redshift/Databricks later.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests that validate adapter SQL generation and no-ops for unsupported operations.
- [ ] TASK-F-025-04: Implement audit logging, observability and metadata recording (artifact lineage tables + audit_log events)
    - [ ] - [ ] Create persistence schema for gold_write_audit (trace_id, session_id, model_id, workspace_id, gold_table_name, temp_table_name, start_ts, end_ts, status, row_count, dq_results JSON, error_message).
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit OpenTelemetry trace spans for each major step: create-temp, insert, validations, swap, metadata-write.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add structured logs (INFO/ERROR) with trace_id propagated and redact secrets; ensure logs include warehouse SQL job ids.
    - [ ] - [ ] Hook audit record write to the successful completion path and to failure/cleanup paths.
- [ ] TASK-F-025-05: Add cleanup, retry, and failure-handling mechanisms (safety net for temp objects and partial failures)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement cleanup job that deletes orphaned temp tables older than TTL and links to audit records for manual investigation.
