    - [ ] - [ ] On failed validation or swap, ensure writer performs rollback logic: drop final partial table (if any), drop temp table, and mark audit status=BLOCK with diagnostics.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add alerting hook (emit event to monitoring system) when cleanup fails or when blocked writes exceed threshold.
    - [ ] - [ ] Document runbook steps for manual recovery when automatic cleanup cannot run (stored procedure, admin console steps).

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests: simulate success, DQ failures, CI violations, forecast integrity violation, and verify correct audit status and cleanup.
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests: run against Snowflake test account to validate temp table creation, atomic swap, and that no partial table is visible on failure.
- [ ] - [ ] Idempotency test: run same writer payload twice and assert only one final table exists and audit records show dedup behavior.
- [ ] - [ ] Performance test: measure write times for 100K rows; confirm within P50/P95 targets in PRD (feature engineering / gold write targets).
- [ ] - [ ] Chaos tests: induce network/warehouse errors during swap and assert cleanup and consistent audit state.
- [ ] - [ ] Security tests: verify no credentials appear in logs and RBAC prevents unauthorized write attempts.
- [ ] - [ ] E2E acceptance: pipeline run from session_spec through model training to gold write to artifact render verifies the artifact reads data produced by the writer.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Code merged to main and feature-flagged behind 'gold-writer' feature flag.
- [ ] - [ ] All unit tests for writer logic and DQ checks pass locally and in CI (coverage threshold defined).
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests pass against a Snowflake CI fixture and DuckDB local test harness verifying atomic swap and DQ gate behavior.
- [ ] - [ ] End-to-end test demonstrates session_spec -> gold table written -> artifact generation reads same table successfully.
- [ ] - [ ] Documentation updated: API contract, runbook, and schema for audit records.
- [ ] - [ ] Observability: traces and audit events show pipeline steps and can be searched by trace_id.
- [ ] - [ ] Security review passed for secrets handling and no credentials leaked to logs.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] F-021 (Data modeler subagent producing CREATE TABLE DDL and SELECT insert SQL)
- [ ] - [ ] F-022 (Feature engineering subagent providing feature_manifest and expected row counts)
- [ ] - [ ] Warehouse connector infra (Snowflake connector configured and approved)
- [ ] - [ ] Secrets manager and warehouse credential rotation (for runtime write permissions)

###### Agent Notes
- The writer agent consumes a session_spec, model_id and either: (a) generated CREATE TABLE DDL + INSERT SELECT SQL from F-021, or (b) a SELECT query plus a desired final table name. For Snowflake prefer pattern: write into analytics_{workspace}.tmp_gold_{session_id}_{uuid}, run validations with SELECT COUNT(*), descriptive statistics. If all pass, execute ALTER TABLE <temp> RENAME TO <final> or COPY INTO depending on account privileges; if rename is not allowed, use CREATE TABLE <final> AS SELECT * FROM <temp> within the same transaction when supported. For BigQuery use dataset-level temp tables and table copy to final name. Always implement idempotency using a unique idempotency_token = session_id + model_id + writer_version. Propagate trace_id through logs and all downstream audit records. Do not send raw rows to LLM agents; only metadata and validation results. Ensure DQ gate decisions return structured JSON with codes and human-friendly messages for UI display. On blocked writes, persist diagnostics (sample failing rows up to 1000) to a secured diagnostics bucket for human review; redact PII. Implement a configurable timeout and a cleanup TTL for temp tables. Prefer safe defaults (block on any critical DQ gate) and expose a workspace-admin override only in exceptional circumstances recorded in audit log.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-025-02: As a data modeler/AI subagent, I want the system to validate and apply the CREATE TABLE DDL and INSERT-SELECT plan (ddl_or_select_sql) and produce the final gold table following the workspace naming convention analytics_{workspace}.gold_{session_topic}_{grain}_{version}, so that lineage, versioning and governance requirements are met and downstream consumers can reference a stable, versioned table.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] The writer accepts CREATE TABLE DDL or INSERT SELECT SQL produced by the data modeler and validates the DDL against the warehouse schema (column types, not-null constraints) before executing data writes.
- [ ] - [ ] The final table naming uses the convention analytics_{workspace}.gold_{session_topic}_{grain}_{version} and increments version semantically (patch on minor changes, major on schema change) recorded in gold_table_metadata.
- [ ] - [ ] Grain uniqueness validation runs before final swap: writer asserts uniqueness of grain keys and aborts if duplicates are present, returning offending join diagnostics.
- [ ] - [ ] The writer records the feature_manifest_version and governance_manifest_version in the gold table metadata and in the audit log for full lineage.
- [ ] - [ ] The writer supports a dry-run validation mode that executes all validations without performing the swap, returning the same diagnostic payloads.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-025-06: Validate incoming DDL/INSERT SQL and perform dry-run validations (schema compatibility, type checks, grain uniqueness)
    - [ ] - [ ] Parse DDL or infer schema from SELECT using the warehouse EXPLAIN or dry-run path to ensure target column types and nullability match feature_manifest expectations.
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate that all required lineage metadata (feature_manifest_version, governance_manifest_version) is present in the writer payload; reject if missing.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement grain uniqueness check SQL that GROUP BY grain_keys HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 and return sample offending rows capped at 1000.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement dry-run mode that executes validations and returns dq_gate_result but does not perform final table swap.
- [ ] TASK-F-025-07: Implement versioning metadata and gold_table_metadata management
    - [ ] - [ ] Define gold_table_metadata schema (gold_table_name, version, semantic_version, created_by_session, created_at, feature_manifest_version, governance_manifest_version, model_id, md5_schema_hash).
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement logic to compute next version: if schema hash unchanged then increment patch, schema change -> bump major or minor according to policy and record note.
    - [ ] - [ ] Write metadata to both warehouse metadata table (analytics_{workspace}.gold_table_metadata) and central Postgres artifact record store.
    - [ ] - [ ] Surface version conflict errors when two concurrent writes attempt to publish the same semantic version; enforce serialization by acquiring a lightweight advisory lock per workspace+table name.
- [ ] TASK-F-025-08: Implement grain/join integrity validation and remediation hints
    - [ ] - [ ] Run join fan-out detection SQL when source joins are provided: detect multiplicative row expansion and report offending join path with suggested remedies (dedup key, change join type).
    - [ ] - [ ] If duplicates detected, attach remediation suggestion object to dq_gate_result specifying columns to dedupe or join change.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add automated sample query generation that demonstrates a recommended dedupe (e.g., SELECT DISTINCT ON grain_keys ... or ROW_NUMBER() partition pattern).
    - [ ] - [ ] Log the generated remediation hint to audit record and include in UI error payload.
- [ ] TASK-F-025-09: Add schema-compatibility and column type coercion handlers
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement safe coercion rules (e.g., integer->float, smaller varchar->larger varchar) and reject unsafe coercions (string->date) unless explicitly requested via a manifest flag.
    - [ ] - [ ] When coercions applied, record transformation steps in feature_manifest and gold_table_metadata and include SQL comments in final DDL.
    - [ ] - [ ] Provide a preview diff of schema changes in the dry-run response to allow human review before final publish.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests for multiple coercion scenarios and schema-diff outputs.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for DDL parsing, schema hash calculation, semantic-version bumping and dry-run responses.
- [ ] - [ ] Integration test: run writer in dry-run mode against Snowflake fixture and assert schema diffs and suggested remediation when join returns duplicates.
- [ ] - [ ] Concurrency test: simulate two concurrent publishes and assert advisory lock prevents double-publish and one attempt receives a conflict error.
- [ ] - [ ] Coercion tests: validate permitted coercions succeed and unsafe coercions fail with clear messages.
- [ ] - [ ] E2E test: from feature_manifest + ddl_or_select_sql to final gold table verify metadata persisted and name follows naming convention.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Dry-run validations produce the same diagnostic payload as a full write (except for the final swap) and are exercised in CI.
- [ ] - [ ] Version metadata is persisted in both warehouse metadata table and central Postgres store with correct semantic versioning increments.
- [ ] - [ ] Grain uniqueness and join integrity checks detect known fan-out cases in fixtures and produce remediation hints.
- [ ] - [ ] Schema-coercion logic is documented and unit-tested; unsafe coercions are rejected with clear error messages.
- [ ] - [ ] Concurrency locking prevents two writers from producing conflicting versions for the same final table.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] US-F-025-01 (writer orchestration implementation)
- [ ] - [ ] F-021 (Data modeler DDL/SELECT producer)
- [ ] - [ ] Central metadata Postgres database available for writing gold_table_metadata

###### Agent Notes
- The writer must treat the incoming DDL/SELECT as authoritative only after validations pass. Use warehouse EXPLAIN / dry-run facilities where available to infer output schema without materializing rows (Snowflake information_schema or BigQuery dry-run). Compute a stable schema hash (canonicalized column order and types) to detect schema changes. For versioning use semver: patch for non-schema changes (e.g., additional computed columns with same types), minor for additive compatible changes, major for incompatible schema changes. Acquire a workspace+table advisory lock before finalizing version increment to avoid races. When join fan-out is detected, include a deterministic remediation SQL snippet that can be copy-pasted into the UI for human review. Do not auto-apply remediation without explicit user confirmation. Store sample offending rows in a secure diagnostics store and reference via secure signed URL in the DQ payload (redact PII).

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-025-03: As a platform API consumer (artifact engine, dashboard), I want the gold table writer to expose the newly published gold table via the gold layer API with appropriate access controls, caching and observability, so that artifacts and scheduled refresh jobs can query the table reliably and securely.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] After successful write and metadata commit, the gold layer API /api/v1/gold/{workspace_id}/{table} returns paginated, RBAC-enforced access to the new table within 30s of write completion.
- [ ] - [ ] Embed token enforcement and allowed_origins are applied for public/embed use cases; requests without proper scope are rejected with 403.
- [ ] - [ ] API returns metadata headers indicating model_id, feature_manifest_version, governance_manifest_version, gold_table_version and last_refreshed timestamp.
- [ ] - [ ] Query caching is applied at service level with TTL=5 minutes and cache invalidation occurs immediately after write of the gold table.
- [ ] - [ ] Query latency for typical artifact queries meets PRD targets (P50 < 500ms) or returns streaming pagination when longer.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-025-10: Wire gold layer API to published gold table metadata and enforce RBAC and embed token checks
    - [ ] - [ ] Update /api/v1/gold/{workspace_id}/{table} to read gold_table_metadata and deny access if metadata indicates blocked DQ status.
    - [ ] - [ ] Enforce workspace membership and role-based access; implement embed token verification (HS256) and allowed_origins check for iframe requests.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add response headers: X-Analytiq-Model-Id, X-Analytiq-Feature-Manifest, X-Analytiq-Governance-Manifest, X-Analytiq-Gold-Version.
    - [ ] - [ ] Return structured error payloads for DQ-blocked tables with link to diagnostics and remediation steps.
- [ ] TASK-F-025-11: Implement cache invalidation and query caching for gold table reads
    - [ ] - [ ] Add server-side Redis cache layer keyed by workspace_id + table_name + query_signature with TTL 5 minutes.
    - [ ] - [ ] On successful gold write, invalidate cache keys for affected table and notify CDN/CDN-like cache if applicable.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement cache warm-up hook that runs a representative artifact query after publish to reduce first-load latency.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add metrics for cache hit/miss rate and expose to dashboard.
- [ ] TASK-F-025-12: Add observability and alerting for gold API reads and post-write availability
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit availability metric when gold table publish completes and when API serves first query for that table; create SLO dashboards.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add alert if gold table cannot be queried within 60s after successful publish.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add logging of slow queries (>1s) for gold tables and trace_id propagation for end-to-end troubleshooting.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add synthetic test that queries a freshly-published gold table in staging at regular intervals to validate end-to-end path.
- [ ] TASK-F-025-13: Add security tests and rate-limiting for gold API access
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit & integration tests to validate embed token rejection when allowed_origins mismatch or token expired.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement per-embed-token rate limit (default public tokens: 100 req/hr) and enforce with 429 responses when exceeded.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add monitoring rule to track unusual access patterns for potential exfiltration and auto-mute public tokens when threshold breached.
    - [ ] - [ ] Document token generation flow and embed token scopes for product docs.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests: RBAC enforcement, embed token validation, metadata header presence.
- [ ] - [ ] Integration test: publish a gold table and assert API can query it within 30s and returns metadata headers.
- [ ] - [ ] Cache tests: write table -> warm-up -> subsequent reads hit cache; invalidation on new publish clears cache.
- [ ] - [ ] Load test: simulate artifact-level read patterns to validate rate limits and P50 latency targets.
- [ ] - [ ] Security test: simulate expired token / wrong allowed_origins and assert 403 / 429 when rate-limited.
- [ ] - [ ] End-to-end test: artifact generation reads the published table and renders KPI + time-series panel successfully.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] API serves the newly-written gold table and includes all required metadata headers.
- [ ] - [ ] RBAC and embed-token enforcement verified by automated security tests.
- [ ] - [ ] Cache invalidation occurs immediately after write in integration tests and synthetic warm-up reduces first-read latency.
- [ ] - [ ] Observability dashboards show publish availability and slow-query logs; alerts configured.
- [ ] - [ ] Documentation updated for API behavior, token scopes, and rate limits.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] US-F-025-01 (writer orchestration and audit events)
- [ ] - [ ] US-F-025-02 (gold_table_metadata persisted)
- [ ] - [ ] Auth & embed token service implemented (existing system components)

###### Agent Notes
- Gold API must treat gold_table_metadata authoritative for access control and DQ state. After writer audit commit, writer should emit a cache-invalidate message to Redis pub/sub and optionally CDN invalidation. Embed tokens are HS256 JWTs scoped to artifact_id/workspace_id with allowed_origins claim; validate origin server-side on each gold API call. For performance, implement optional streaming pagination for large queries (cursor-based). Propagate trace_id from writer through metadata into API logs to allow correlating publish -> first-read. Implement conservative rate limits for public tokens and provide workspace admin hooks to increase limits for trusted consumers. Ensure SSO and ABAC checks are applied prior to returning any rows; if row-level security is configured, pass through principals to the warehouse proxy layer so source-level RLS can apply.

## Sprint 6 - Feature Manifest & Versioning (scaffold)

**Sprint Goal:** Provide a minimal feature manifest and versioning so the gold table carries provenance. Full FE deferred.

### Sprint Completion Checklist
- [ ] All P0 stories complete
- [ ] All blocking defects resolved
- [ ] Tests passing
- [ ] Demo-ready increment produced
- [ ] Milestone accepted

### Epic: Feature Manifest

#### Requirement / Feature: Feature manifest scaffold (empty/enriched placeholder stored with gold table)

**Feature ID:** `F-026`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, data`

**Rationale:** Even with minimal FE, gold table must record feature manifest version for lineage.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-025

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-026-01: As a Data Engineer, I want the gold-layer writer to persist a feature-manifest scaffold (an immutable versioned placeholder) alongside every generated gold table, so that every gold table carries explicit feature-manifest provenance even when full feature enrichment is deferred.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Persist a row in analytics_{workspace}.feature_manifests whenever a gold table is created
- [ ] Record manifest_version (semver), workspace_id, session_id, generated_at, gold_table_name and feature_list (empty array if none)
- [ ] Record manifest_version in the gold table metadata (gold table has metadata column or external reference field manifest_version)
- [ ] Ensure existing feature_manifest rows are immutable; updates create a new manifest row with bumped semver
- [ ] Expose a secured API GET /api/v1/feature_manifests/{manifest_id} returning manifest JSON with 200 on success and 404 for unknown id
- [ ] Ensure server-side JSON Schema validation rejects invalid manifests and logs validation failures
- [ ] Log a structured audit event for each manifest write with trace_id, actor, session_id, and manifest_version

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-026-01: Create data warehouse migration and DDL for analytics feature_manifests table
    - [ ] Draft table schema DDL: feature_manifests(manifest_id UUID PK, manifest_version VARCHAR, workspace_id VARCHAR, session_id VARCHAR, gold_table_name VARCHAR, manifest_json VARIANT/JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMP, created_by VARCHAR)
    - [ ] Add unique constraint on (workspace_id, manifest_version) to enforce versioning
    - [ ] Add immutable flag/comment and a trigger that prevents UPDATEs on manifest rows
    - [ ] Write Snowflake/BigQuery/Postgres-compatible migration scripts; mark production dialects
    - [ ] Run migration locally in dev DuckDB/Docker Postgres and validate schema
- [ ] TASK-F-026-02: Implement gold-layer writer integration to create manifest scaffold on gold table write
    - [ ] Add manifest creation call in Stage 5 writer pipeline immediately after gold table write but within same transaction where possible
    - [ ] Generate manifest_id (UUID) and initial manifest_version '0.1.0' for the first manifest per session
    - [ ] Persist manifest JSON containing required fields and empty feature list placeholder if feature engineering is skipped
    - [ ] Write manifest_version into gold table metadata column or into workspace artifacts lineage table (configurable per warehouse)
    - [ ] Add idempotency key using session_id + gold_table_name to prevent duplicate manifests on retries
- [ ] TASK-F-026-03: Add backend service API endpoints and controllers for feature_manifest retrieval and listing
    - [ ] Design API: GET /api/v1/feature_manifests/{manifest_id} and GET /api/v1/workspaces/{workspace_id}/feature_manifests?session_id=
    - [ ] Implement controller layer, service layer, and data access object (DAO) for manifest reads
    - [ ] Enforce authorization: workspace membership required and RBAC check for read access
    - [ ] Add pagination for listing endpoint and limit response size for manifest_json
    - [ ] Add OpenAPI docs and example responses for API endpoints
- [ ] TASK-F-026-04: Implement validation, error handling, and logging for manifest creation
    - [ ] Define JSON Schema for minimal manifest and embed in backend validation module
    - [ ] Validate manifest_json against schema before insert and reject with structured error on mismatch
    - [ ] Add retry/backoff logic for transient DB errors and surface persistent failures to pipeline as DQ gate failure
    - [ ] Emit structured logs (trace_id, session_id, manifest_id, manifest_version, outcome) to observability pipeline
    - [ ] Add metric emit for manifest_write_success and manifest_write_failure for Prometheus/Grafana
- [ ] TASK-F-026-05: Write tests: unit, integration, and pipeline-level tests to cover manifest scaffold behavior
    - [ ] Write unit tests for DAO and schema validation module covering valid and invalid manifests
    - [ ] Write integration tests that run a Stage 5 gold write and assert manifest row created and gold table metadata updated
    - [ ] Write idempotency test that retries a successful gold write and asserts no duplicate manifests
    - [ ] Write immutability test that attempts to UPDATE manifest row and expects DB-level rejection
    - [ ] Add contract test for API endpoint including authorization scenarios
- [ ] TASK-F-026-06: Add documentation, runbooks and developer examples for using the manifest scaffold
    - [ ] Document feature_manifest table schema and example manifest JSON in internal docs
    - [ ] Add example curl commands for GET endpoints and example responses
    - [ ] Add runbook for manifest_write_failure including remediation steps
    - [ ] Add a short 'how to test locally' section in README for devs

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Execute unit tests for DAO and validator with coverage > 80%
- [ ] Run integration test that executes full Stage 5 gold write in test environment and asserts manifest row and gold_table metadata
- [ ] Simulate transient DB failure and assert retry/backoff and eventual failure logged and pipeline DQ gate triggered
- [ ] Run security test to confirm only authorized workspace members can access GET /api/v1/feature_manifests/{id}
- [ ] Run migration verification on a disposable DB instance to confirm immutability trigger prevents UPDATEs
- [ ] Run load test to create manifests at expected pipeline throughput and verify manifest_write_success metric continuity

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Runbooked migration applied to dev and staged to production migration pipeline
- [ ] Gold-layer write produces a manifest row and gold table metadata manifest_version for every successful gold table write
- [ ] API endpoints implemented, documented, and secured with RBAC
- [ ] JSON Schema validation enforced with failing manifests blocked and logged
- [ ] Automated tests (unit + integration + idempotency + immutability) pass in CI
- [ ] Observability metrics and logs for manifest writes are emitted and dashboards/alerts created
- [ ] Code reviewed, PR merged, and deployable artifacts created

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-025
- [ ] Stage 5 gold-layer writer service (existing pipeline component)

###### Agent Notes
- When implementing the writer integration, prefer a single transactional write where the manifest reference is inserted after the gold table write completes; if the target warehouse does not support multi-statement transactions across metadata and table creation, use idempotency keys and a compensating write step. Use JSON Schema for validation; do not send raw row-level data to any LLM. Emit structured logs with trace_id; propagate trace_id from orchestrator into manifest write calls.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-026-02: As a QA Lead / Compliance Officer, I want feature manifests to be versioned, immutable, schema-validated, and retrievable via API so that lineage is auditable and manifests cannot be silently modified after gold table creation.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Ensure each manifest row is immutable; attempt to update a manifest row fails with DB/HTTP error
- [ ] Ensure manifest versions follow semver; new versions are created for changes rather than UPDATEs
- [ ] Ensure API returns 403 for unauthorized read attempts and 200 for workspace members
- [ ] Ensure manifest JSON conforms to agreed JSON Schema and violations are rejected with clear error messages
- [ ] Ensure every manifest write produces an audit log entry with trace_id, user_id, action=create, and manifest_id

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-026-07: Design and implement JSON Schema for feature manifest scaffold
    - [ ] Define required manifest fields: manifest_version, workspace_id, session_id, generated_at, gold_table_name, feature_list (array), provenance_notes (optional)
    - [ ] Define types and constraints for each field and semver regex for manifest_version
    - [ ] Version the schema and store it in the schema registry (or repo) with changelog
    - [ ] Write unit tests for the schema validator covering allowed and rejected payloads
- [ ] TASK-F-026-08: Enforce immutability at DB and application layer
    - [ ] Add DB trigger or constraint to prevent UPDATE on feature_manifests rows and return informative error
    - [ ] Implement application-layer check to reject any update attempts with 409 Conflict and guidance
    - [ ] Implement 'new_version' API path or logic that creates a new manifest row with incremented semver
    - [ ] Add tests to assert update attempts fail and new_version flows create new rows
- [ ] TASK-F-026-09: Implement audit logging and access controls around manifest reads
    - [ ] Add audit table entries for manifest_create and manifest_read including actor_id, ip, trace_id
    - [ ] Wire manifest read endpoints to append audit entries asynchronously
    - [ ] Add RBAC checks using workspace ACLs and write unit tests for unauthorized access
    - [ ] Add security review checklist and perform threat modeling for manifest endpoint
- [ ] TASK-F-026-10: Execute compliance and regression test suite for manifest lifecycle
    - [ ] Run automated tests that attempt illegal updates and assert DB/application rejects them
    - [ ] Run API contract tests validating semver format and field presence
    - [ ] Run manual exploratory test to verify audit logs surface proper info for a sample session
    - [ ] Document known failure modes and expected remediation messages for users

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Run unit tests for JSON Schema validator for positive and negative cases
- [ ] Run DB immutability test hitting trigger/constraint directly and via API
- [ ] Run RBAC tests with users in different roles to confirm access behavior
- [ ] Run audit log ingestion test ensuring create/read events populate audit table
- [ ] Conduct pen-test-style endpoint fuzzing to validate error handling and ensure no PII leakage in error messages

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Manifest JSON Schema published and versioned in repo
- [ ] Immutability enforced at DB-level and application rejects updates with 409
- [ ] Audit logging captures create/read events with required metadata
- [ ] RBAC enforced for manifest endpoints and tested
- [ ] QA regression tests pass and are included in CI
- [ ] Security review completed and any high/critical findings are addressed

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-025
- [ ] Auth/ACL service for RBAC

###### Agent Notes
- Treat manifest JSON as metadata only; never include sample rows. Use semver regex ^\d+\.\d+\.\d+$ for manifest_version validation. For immutability, prefer database-level enforcement (trigger/constraint) plus defensive application checks. Ensure audit logs are append-only and include trace_id; do not log DB credentials or raw data.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-026-03: As an AI-coding-agent planner and backend engineer, I want a minimal agent contract and service stub that can produce an 'enriched placeholder' manifest (empty feature list plus derived metadata) so that pipeline Stage 3/5 can call a deterministic agent or fallback to create a safe manifest when full feature engineering is unavailable.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Provide a deterministic agent contract that accepts gold_table_spec and returns manifest JSON conforming to manifest JSON Schema
- [ ] Ensure agent returns placeholder feature entries with derivation: 'placeholder' and leakage_risk: 1.0 for placeholders
- [ ] Ensure agent calls are logged with model, prompt_hash, latency, and no raw row data sent to LLM
- [ ] Ensure pipeline uses agent output if available; otherwise uses deterministic server-side placeholder generator
- [ ] Ensure automated tests validate agent contract outputs and fallback deterministic generator

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-026-11: Design agent contract and JSON input/output schemas for placeholder manifest generation
    - [ ] Write agent input schema: { workspace_id, session_id, gold_table_name, gold_table_schema_summary, requested_features (optional) }
    - [ ] Write agent output schema matching feature_manifest JSON Schema and include field feature_list: [{ name, derivation_method, leakage_risk, notes }]
    - [ ] Define deterministic placeholder rules: derivation_method='placeholder', leakage_risk=1.0, importance_estimate=null
    - [ ] Store schemas in repo and register in agent schema registry
- [ ] TASK-F-026-12: Implement agent stub service and deterministic fallback generator
    - [ ] Implement a stateless agent stub endpoint that accepts gold_table_spec and returns validated placeholder manifest synchronously
    - [ ] Implement deterministic fallback generator (no LLM) that mirrors stub behavior when LLM call is disabled or fails
    - [ ] Add feature to pipeline to call agent stub with timeout (e.g., 5s) and fallback on timeout/error
    - [ ] Add unit tests covering agent success, timeout, and fallback paths
- [ ] TASK-F-026-13: Add observability and safe-logging for agent calls
    - [ ] Log agent call metadata: agent_name, model (if LLM), prompt_hash, latency_ms, result_manifest_id
    - [ ] Ensure no PII or raw rows are logged; only schema summaries are allowed
    - [ ] Emit metrics agent_call_success and agent_call_failure and dashboard them
    - [ ] Create alert rule for agent_call_failure rate > 5% over 10 minutes
- [ ] TASK-F-026-14: Write integration and contract tests for agent outputs and pipeline integration
    - [ ] Write contract tests verifying agent output passes manifest JSON Schema
    - [ ] Write integration tests that run Stage 3/5 pipeline using agent stub and asserting manifest persisted
    - [ ] Write error-injection tests where agent returns malformed JSON and assert pipeline falls back to deterministic generator
    - [ ] Add tests to CI to run agent contract checks on PRs that change schema

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Run contract tests to validate agent inputs and outputs against registered JSON Schema
- [ ] Run pipeline integration test that simulates both agent success and agent error scenarios and asserts manifest persisted
- [ ] Simulate LLM timeout and verify fallback deterministic generator runs and manifest is created
- [ ] Run logs inspection test to ensure no raw row data is in logs; run CI linting for secret exposure

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Agent contract and schemas committed and versioned
- [ ] Agent stub implemented and integrated into pipeline with timeout/fallback
- [ ] Agent and fallback outputs validated against manifest JSON Schema
- [ ] Observability metrics and alerts in place for agent failures
- [ ] Tests for agent success/failure/fallback pass in CI

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-025
- [ ] Agent orchestration & schema registry

###### Agent Notes
- Agent must be deterministic for placeholder generation; prefer a simple deterministic algorithm rather than LLM at MVP. If LLM is used later, require prompt templating, prompt_hash logging, and JSON Schema validation of outputs. Timebox calls and implement clear fallbacks. Do not include raw rows in any agent input; only supply schema summaries and column-level stats. Use idempotency via session_id+gold_table_name to avoid duplicate manifests.

#### Requirement / Feature: Feature manifest versioning & metadata (semver + storage)

**Feature ID:** `F-027`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, data`

**Rationale:** Manifest versioning is required for reproducibility and artifact provenance.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-026

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-027-01: As a data engineer, I want governance manifests to be versioned with semver semantics and stored immutably, so that every downstream artifact can reference an authoritative manifest version for provenance and reproducibility.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A governance_manifest JSON is written with a manifest_version following semantic versioning (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH).
- [ ] Version bump rules implemented: MAJOR when schema changes, MINOR on profile/metadata changes, PATCH on non-functional metadata (e.g. generated_at re-run) per documented rules.
- [ ] Every manifest write is atomic and immutable: existing manifest versions are read-only after commit.
- [ ] A persistent version history is queryable via API and lists manifest_version, generated_at, author, change_reason, and trace_id.
- [ ] Manifest schema validation (JSON Schema) runs before persist; invalid manifests are rejected with explicit error codes and logged.
- [ ] GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest returns the requested manifest version and supports ?version= and ?latest parameters.
- [ ] Writes and reads include distributed trace_id and are logged in the pipeline audit log.
- [ ] Failures (DB constraint, storage error, validation error) return deterministic error payloads and produce alerting events.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-027-01: Design governance_manifest storage schema and APIs
    - [ ] Draft Postgres table schema for manifest metadata: id (uuid), integration_id, manifest_version, semantic_version_parts, storage_uri, created_by, created_at, change_reason, trace_id, dq_gate_status, human_review_required, is_latest boolean
    - [ ] Define object storage layout (S3-compatible) for JSON blobs: /manifests/{workspace_id}/{integration_id}/{manifest_version}.json and lifecycle tags
    - [ ] Define API contract changes and query parameters for GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest (support version and latest)
    - [ ] Produce JSON Schema for governance_manifest to validate required fields (tables, columns, lineage_edges, dq_gate_status, etc.)
    - [ ] Review design with data engineering, security, and product owners and iterate
- [ ] TASK-F-027-02: Implement manifest semver bumping logic and validation service (backend)
    - [ ] Implement semver library integration (well-tested npm/python lib) with explicit bump rules mapping (schema_change => major, profile_change => minor, metadata_rewrite => patch)
    - [ ] Implement manifest comparator that diffs previous manifest JSON to determine change type (schema vs profile vs metadata) deterministically
    - [ ] Wire comparator into an atomic transaction that computes next manifest_version and writes metadata row + blob storage
    - [ ] Add optimistic concurrency checks to prevent race conditions when two writes occur simultaneously
    - [ ] Add unit tests for comparator edge cases (column rename, column type change, column addition, only stats changed)
- [ ] TASK-F-027-03: Persist manifests immutably (DB metadata + object storage blob)
    - [ ] Implement backend endpoint to persist manifest: validate JSON Schema, compute bump type, compute new semver, upload blob to object storage with content-addressed checksum
    - [ ] Insert metadata row into Postgres in same transaction as storage registration; set previous is_latest=false and new is_latest=true atomically
    - [ ] Store storage_uri and checksum in metadata row; enable tags/metadata for retention and classification
    - [ ] Ensure manifest blob ACLs are private by default and lifecycle rules are set (retention period configurable)
    - [ ] Add integration tests that simulate partial failures (storage success + DB failure, DB success + storage failure) and validate rollback semantics
- [ ] TASK-F-027-04: Expose manifest retrieval & history APIs (backend + frontend minimal)
    - [ ] Implement GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifest?version={semver}|latest that reads metadata row and streams JSON blob from storage with caching headers
    - [ ] Implement GET /api/v1/integrations/{id}/manifests (list) returning paginated history with created_by, created_at, manifest_version, change_reason, dq_gate_status
    - [ ] Add backend RBAC checks: workspace member may read, only workspace admin may write or rollback
    - [ ] Implement a minimal frontend endpoint (internal admin console) to view manifest history and download manifest JSON
    - [ ] Add integration tests for API with mocked storage and DB
- [ ] TASK-F-027-05: Implement observability, logging, and error handling for manifest operations
    - [ ] Log manifest write and read events in pipeline audit log with trace_id, user_id, integration_id, manifest_version, and outcome
    - [ ] Emit structured metrics to monitoring system for write_success, write_failure, validation_errors, read_latency
    - [ ] Add alerts for repeated validation failures or storage errors (SLO breach), with runbook links
    - [ ] Implement retry/backoff for transient storage/DB failures and surface non-retriable errors to the caller
    - [ ] Document error codes and remediation messages for API consumers
- [ ] TASK-F-027-06: QA & testing automation for governance manifests
    - [ ] Author unit tests for semver bump logic and manifest comparator (cover all change-types)
    - [ ] Author integration tests for end-to-end persist + read with real DB and localstack S3 in CI
    - [ ] Add contract tests for GET API ensuring response matches JSON Schema and includes required headers
    - [ ] Create manual QA checklist for product reviewers: validate version bump behaviour by editing integration data sets
    - [ ] Add performance test for manifest generation latency to assert P50 < 30s, P95 < 90s in test env

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests: comparator semantics, semver bumping, JSON Schema validation (automated CI)
- [ ] Integration tests: end-to-end persist + retrieve with Postgres + S3-local (CI pipeline)
- [ ] Error path tests: simulate storage failure, DB failure, validation failure, and confirm atomic rollback
- [ ] API contract tests: GET responses conform to JSON Schema and include required audit fields
- [ ] Performance test: manifest persist P50/P95 targets measured in staging
- [ ] Manual QA: product reviewer creates a new integration, triggers manifest re-run, and verifies expected semver increment and immutability
- [ ] Security scan: ensure manifest blobs are not publicly accessible and metadata access enforces RBAC

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Manifest storage schema and object storage layout are implemented and committed to repo
- [ ] Semver bump logic implemented with comparator and unit tests covering expected change types
- [ ] API endpoints for persist and retrieve implemented, RBAC enforced, and documented
- [ ] Integration tests pass in CI against ephemeral DB + local S3; error/rollback scenarios covered
- [ ] Observability (logs + metrics) and alerts configured and validated in staging
- [ ] Product owner acceptance: manual verification of manifest creation, version history, and retrieval
- [ ] Documentation updated: API spec, manifest JSON Schema, operational runbooks for failures

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-026
- [ ] Postgres metadata DB and object storage (S3) infra available
- [ ] RBAC/auth service (OAuth) available

###### Agent Notes
- Implement comparator as deterministic, diffs-only algorithm (no ML). Use JSON canonicalization (sorted keys) before diff to avoid noisy semantic version bumps. Use a tested semver library. Persist manifest JSON in S3 with checksum and content-type application/json. Tie every write to pipeline trace_id and record in audit log. Expose lightweight admin UI for viewing manifest history (deferred full FE UX). Ensure schema-change detection identifies column rename (treated as major) vs column add (minor) vs only stats change (minor).

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-027-02: As a feature engineer / modeler, I want feature manifests to be versioned (semver) and to include governance_manifest_version and feature metadata (derivation, leakage_risk, importance_estimate), so that the gold table and model card record exact feature provenance and allow reproducible retraining.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Feature manifest JSON produced by the feature engineering subagent conforms to an agreed JSON Schema and includes feature_manifest_version (semver), feature list with derivation, leakage_risk (0–1), importance_estimate (MI score), and governance_manifest_version that was used as input.
- [ ] Feature manifest persisted to the same versioned storage and metadata table as governance manifests; a storage URI and checksum recorded.
- [ ] The gold layer table metadata includes feature_manifest_version and feature_manifest_storage_uri; model_card includes feature_manifest_version used for training.
- [ ] Feature manifest generation is validated: collinearity, null rate, and minimum training rows checks run and failures marked in manifest with clear messages.
- [ ] APIs exist to GET feature manifest by workspace + version and GET latest; feature manifest is included in artifact lineage when artifact is saved.
- [ ] Feature manifest writes are atomic, immutably versioned, and audited with trace_id.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-027-07: Design the feature_manifest JSON Schema and storage metadata
    - [ ] Draft JSON Schema fields: feature_manifest_version, generated_at, generated_by_agent, governance_manifest_version, features: [{ name, derivation, leakage_risk, imputation, encoding, importance_estimate, dropped: bool, drop_reason }], statistics_summary, validation_checks, trace_id
    - [ ] Define mapping from feature_manifest_version semantics to bump rules (e.g., new feature added => minor, derivation change => major, metadata-only regen => patch)
    - [ ] Specify Postgres metadata table fields or extend existing manifest metadata table to support feature manifests
    - [ ] Review schema with feature engineering and data science teams
- [ ] TASK-F-027-08: Implement feature manifest generator in feature engineering subagent (AI integration)
    - [ ] Instrument feature engineering agent to compile enriched_feature_manifest per DOCX Table 14 output format
    - [ ] Validate feature manifest against JSON Schema before persist and surface any validation errors as part of the DQ gate output
    - [ ] Attach governance_manifest_version and session_spec id to the manifest metadata
    - [ ] Emit detailed feature-level logs for lineage and reproducibility (derivation SQL/expressions stored or pointer to artifact)
    - [ ] Add unit and integration tests for manifest generation logic (including leakage_risk edge cases)
- [ ] TASK-F-027-09: Persist feature manifests atomically and update gold table metadata (backend/data)
    - [ ] Reuse manifest persistence service from governance manifests to store feature manifest blob and metadata row with semver bump logic
    - [ ] Update gold table writer (Stage 5) to include feature_manifest_version and feature_manifest_uri in table metadata (Postgres gold metadata and gold table comments/metadata in warehouse if supported)
    - [ ] Ensure gold layer API /api/v1/gold/{workspace}/{table} returns the manifest_version in response headers or metadata payload
    - [ ] Add integration test that creates a gold table and asserts stored metadata references the persisted feature manifest
- [ ] TASK-F-027-10: Expose feature manifest retrieval APIs and link to model_card & artifact metadata
    - [ ] Implement GET /api/v1/features/{workspace_id}/manifests?version=|latest returning manifest JSON with proper caching headers
    - [ ] Update model_card generation step to include feature_manifest_version and feature_manifest_uri in the model_card JSON
    - [ ] Include feature_manifest_version in artifact lineage metadata when artifact is saved (artifact record schema)
    - [ ] Add integration tests verifying model_card contains feature_manifest_version and that artifact lineage references the manifest
- [ ] TASK-F-027-11: Testing, QA, and documentation for feature manifest
    - [ ] Add unit tests for generator: correct leakage_risk scoring, derivation recording, drop logging
    - [ ] Integration tests verifying end-to-end: session_spec -> feature engineer -> feature_manifest -> gold_table metadata -> model_card linkage
    - [ ] Manual QA: run a sample session that triggers feature engineering and confirm manifest fields in UI and API
    - [ ] Document feature manifest schema and examples in internal developer docs

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests: validate fields, bump logic, and leakage_risk thresholds
- [ ] Integration tests: simulate session runs producing feature_manifest and assert linkage in gold table and model_card
- [ ] DQ gate tests: create inputs that trigger collinearity and post-imputation null checks to ensure manifest flags validation_failures
- [ ] Manual QA: run an end-to-end session in staging and inspect artifact lineage for feature_manifest_version
- [ ] Regression tests: ensure older sessions still return historical feature manifests correctly

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Feature manifest JSON Schema approved and checked into repo
- [ ] Feature engineering agent emits validated feature_manifest JSON on every run and persists it immutably
- [ ] Gold table writer attaches feature_manifest_version to table metadata; model_card includes feature_manifest_version
- [ ] APIs for retrieving feature manifests implemented and secured with RBAC
- [ ] Automated tests (unit + integration) cover generation, persist, retrieval, and linkage
- [ ] Product sign-off: feature manifest appears in artifact lineage UI and in gold table metadata

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] US-F-027-01 (manifest storage service)
- [ ] Stage 3 feature engineering agent implementation (F-026 output)
- [ ] Model training pipeline (to record feature_manifest_version in model_card)

###### Agent Notes
- Feature engineering agent must emit the full derivation trace (either inline SQL expressions or a pointer to a stored transformation record) to ensure reproducibility. Use the same storage/persist flow as governance manifests to avoid duplicate infra. The feature manifest JSON should be canonicalized before semver diffing. Leakage_risk calculation is deterministic and must be logged per feature. Ensure features dropped due to collinearity are recorded with rationale.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-027-03: As a security / SRE engineer, I want manifest storage and retrieval to be secure, access-controlled, auditable, and recoverable, so that manifests are protected, traceable, and maintainable in production.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Manifest blobs and metadata are stored with encryption at rest and access controls applied (object store private + DB row-level ACLs).
- [ ] API endpoints enforce RBAC: only workspace members can read manifests; only admins/integration owners can write/rollback; audit log records actor + timestamp + trace_id for every operation.
- [ ] Retention policy is configurable; manifests older than retention are archived to cold storage but metadata remains discoverable.
- [ ] Backup & restore procedures exist: daily metadata DB backup and periodic object storage backup; restore tested in staging with documented runbook.
- [ ] Audit and access logs are integrated with centralized logging/observability (OpenTelemetry logs + metrics) and searchable for incident response.
- [ ] Performance SLOs for manifest reads are met in staging (P50 < 500ms for cached read, P95 < 2s for non-cached read).

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-027-12: Enforce RBAC and authorization on manifest endpoints
    - [ ] Integrate with existing auth service to require valid OAuth token for manifest APIs; verify workspace membership
    - [ ] Implement role checks: write/rollback restricted to workspace admin or integration owner, read allowed to workspace members
    - [ ] Add fine-grained ACL enforcement for historical manifests if privacy flag present (e.g., PII-related manifests require admin access)
    - [ ] Add unit and integration tests for RBAC rules and unauthorized access attempts
- [ ] TASK-F-027-13: Ensure data-at-rest and in-transit encryption and storage hardening
    - [ ] Confirm object storage uses server-side encryption (SSE-S3/SSE-KMS) and restrict bucket policies to workspace principals
    - [ ] Ensure Postgres metadata DB has disk encryption enabled and secrets rotated per infra policy
    - [ ] Enforce TLS for all API endpoints and storage access; fail open on TLS errors not permitted
    - [ ] Document and verify encryption configuration in staging
- [ ] TASK-F-027-14: Implement audit logging and observability integration
    - [ ] Emit structured audit events for manifest create/read/list/rollback with fields: actor_id, workspace_id, integration_id, manifest_version, trace_id, operation, outcome
    - [ ] Ship audit events to centralized log store (e.g., ELK/CloudWatch) and ensure retention policy aligned with compliance
    - [ ] Add dashboard for manifest operations (writes per hour, validation failures, RBAC denials)
    - [ ] Add automated alert on suspicious activity (e.g., repeated failed writes or unauthorized read attempts)
- [ ] TASK-F-027-15: Implement backup, archive, and restore procedures for manifests
    - [ ] Create scheduled job to snapshot metadata DB daily and copy manifest blobs to a cold storage location with versioned prefixes
    - [ ] Implement an archival lifecycle rule that moves blobs older than configurable retention to Glacier/cold tier and marks metadata as archived
    - [ ] Document restore runbook and automate a test restore in staging quarterly
    - [ ] Add integration test to validate archive retrieval for an archived manifest
- [ ] TASK-F-027-16: Security & compliance testing and hardening
    - [ ] Run threat model for manifest storage and API access; document attack surface and mitigations
    - [ ] Perform access control review and automated SAST scan on API code paths
    - [ ] Coordinate a targeted penetration test on manifest endpoints in staging
    - [ ] Implement fixes for any findings and re-run verification

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Access control tests: assert that reader-only tokens cannot write; admin tokens can write and rollback
- [ ] Encryption verification: confirm object blobs and DB data are encrypted and keys rotated
- [ ] Audit log tests: create, read, list, rollback manifests and verify audit events appear with correct fields
- [ ] Backup & restore test: run scheduled backup job in staging and perform full restore of manifest blob + metadata
- [ ] Penetration test: run focused auth/ACL tests and validate mitigations
- [ ] Performance tests: measure GET manifest latency under cold/hot cache in staging

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] RBAC enforced on all manifest APIs with tests for allowed/denied operations
- [ ] Manifests stored with encryption-at-rest and transport encryption; access policies validated
- [ ] Audit logging of all manifest operations enabled and integrated into centralized logging with retention
- [ ] Backup and archive pipeline operational and restore tested in staging with documented runbook
- [ ] SLOs for read performance measured and met in staging and documented
- [ ] Security review completed and remediation applied for any findings

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] Cloud object storage with SSE and lifecycle policies
- [ ] Centralized logging and monitoring stack
- [ ] Auth service with workspace membership/roles

###### Agent Notes
- Tie manifest operations to existing distributed tracing (trace_id) and ensure trace_id propagates through agent invocations so we can map agent outputs to manifest writes. Use IAM roles for service-to-service access to S3; avoid long-lived credentials. For rollback operations implement a guarded flow: create a new MAJOR (or patch depending on policy) manifest that references the previous content rather than mutating history. Consider soft-delete + archived flag for regulatory needs rather than hard delete.

- [ ] Model card is generated and persisted

### Epic: Training

#### Requirement / Feature: XGBoost training executor (local sync runner for <100K rows)

**Feature ID:** `F-028`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, ml`

**Rationale:** XGBoost is primary algorithm for regression tasks in MVP per PRD.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-025
- [ ] F-026

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-028-01: As a backend/ML engineer, I want a local synchronous XGBoost training executor for datasets under 100K rows that runs inside the orchestrator, so that small-model training completes inline with the UI/agent loop and produces a promoted model card and trial leaderboard.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] The runner accepts a gold table (CSV/DB query result) and session_spec and returns a model_card JSON and leaderboard entries on success.
- [ ] - [ ] The runner enforces minimum and maximum dataset checks: rejects training if training rows < 500 with a clear error code and message.
- [ ] - [ ] The runner implements walk-forward backtesting with 5 expanding windows and computes per-window MAPE, RMSE, and directional accuracy and aggregates mean and worst window metrics.
- [ ] - [ ] The runner runs within synchronous time budget for <100K rows: typical training (P50) < 3 minutes and P95 < 8 minutes; if training approaches 15 minutes, it returns a structured warning and switches to async mode with job id.
- [ ] - [ ] The runner performs a configurable randomized hyperparameter search (default 30 trials) with early stopping and logs each trial to the leaderboard including hyperparams, validation_mape, walk_forward_mapes, and duration.
- [ ] - [ ] The runner serializes the winning model to model registry (local dev file store or configured registry) and produces model_card fields matching PRD model_card schema.
- [ ] - [ ] The runner surfaces deterministic reproducible results given a seed parameter and logs seed in model_card.
- [ ] - [ ] The runner includes robust error handling: validates inputs, catches XGBoost exceptions, returns structured error objects, and writes pipeline audit log entries (trace_id, errors, timings).

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-028-01: Design training executor API + schemas
    - [ ] - [ ] Define input contract: fields required from session_spec, gold_table pointer (warehouse query or in-memory CSV), prediction_horizon, target_type, feature_manifest_version, random_seed, max_sync_time.
    - [ ] - [ ] Define output contract: model_card JSON (per PRD), leaderboard_row schema, training trace/audit record schema, error schema.
    - [ ] - [ ] Draft OpenAPI snippet for endpoint (internal) and example JSON payloads for success/error cases.
    - [ ] - [ ] Coordinate schema with orchestration layer team to ensure trace_id propagation and streaming status events.
- [ ] TASK-F-028-02: Implement walk-forward splitter & validation checks
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement deterministic 5-window expanding walk-forward splitter that returns training/validation windows and final held-out test split (most recent 15%).
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement checks: minimum training rows >= 500, minimum test rows >= 100, and no overlap between windows; return structured DQ gate failure if checks fail.
    - [ ] - [ ] Unit test splitter on synthetic time-series datasets with known boundaries and edge cases (ties, missing dates, irregular frequency).
    - [ ] - [ ] Instrument logging and metrics for split sizes and durations.
- [ ] TASK-F-028-03: Implement XGBoost training loop with randomized hyperparameter search
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement XGBoost training wrapper supporting regression/classification/multiclass with early stopping using validation folds from walk-forward windows.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement randomized hyperparameter search driver (default 30 trials) with ability to double trials per repair step; include configurable search space per target_type.
    - [ ] - [ ] Integrate deterministic seed into parameter sampling and training for reproducibility.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add early stop and time budget enforcement that aborts long trials and surfaces partial results for audit.
- [ ] TASK-F-028-04: Implement trial logging (leaderboard) and model_card creation
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement per-trial record writer that persists trial metadata: trial_id, algorithm, hyperparameters (JSON), validation_mape, test_mape (if available), training_duration_seconds, walk_forward_mapes (array of 5), promoted boolean flag.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement model_card builder that assembles final fields from winning trial: model_id, algorithm, hyperparameters, feature_manifest_version, feature_count, top_features, backtest_results, validation_mape, test_mape, promotion_status, training_duration_seconds, trained_at, model_registry_uri.
    - [ ] - [ ] Serialize model artifact to configured model registry backend (local file-based for dev; MLflow/registry adapter interface) and populate model_registry_uri.
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit audit log event linking session_id, trace_id, model_id and leaderboard rows.
- [ ] TASK-F-028-05: Implement synchronous runner orchestration & timeout fallback
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement a synchronous executor that runs training and returns final model_card when within time budget; integrate with orchestrator call pattern (agent_start/agent_complete events).
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement timeout detection: if total runtime nears max_sync_time (configurable, default 15min), gracefully abort and convert job to async with persistent job record and return job_id to caller.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement status polling endpoints for async fallback jobs and email/in-app notification hooks for completion.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit/integration test that simulates timeouts and verifies proper transition to async flow.
- [ ] TASK-F-028-06: Add validation, error handling, and observability
    - [ ] - [ ] Add input validation (schema checks) and return structured DQ_GATE or error events for invalid inputs or failed preconditions.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add try/catch around XGBoost calls and surface user-friendly remediation messages (e.g., 'insufficient rows', 'feature leakage detected', 'memory error — reduce features').
    - [ ] - [ ] Integrate OpenTelemetry traces and metrics (training_time_seconds, trials_count, memory_peak_mb) and ensure propagation of trace_id.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add logs at INFO/debug levels and structured error logs with stack traces to centralized logging system.
- [ ] TASK-F-028-07: Write tests and CI pipeline steps
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement unit tests for splitter, metric calculations, hyperparameter search sampling, and model_card builder.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement integration tests that run a full training end-to-end on synthetic datasets <100K rows and verify model_card and leaderboard persistence.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement performance tests to measure typical P50/P95 training time under controlled CI environment and fail build if regressions exceed targets.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add CI job that runs the end-to-end training tests in a reproducible deterministic environment (fixed seed).

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for walker splitter, hyperparam sampler, and training wrapper with edge case parameterization.
- [ ] - [ ] Integration test: run full training with synthetic time-series (e.g., 80k rows), assert model_card fields present and leaderboard length >= 1.
- [ ] - [ ] Negative test: run with <500 training rows and assert DQ gate error with correct error code and message.
- [ ] - [ ] Timeout test: artificially cap training time to ensure fallback to async path and verify job record creation and completion notification.
- [ ] - [ ] Reproducibility test: run identical payload twice with same seed and assert identical hyperparam sampling order and deterministic model_card top-level metrics within floating tolerance.
- [ ] - [ ] Observability test: verify traces and metrics appear in telemetry backend and logs include trace_id.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Training executor API implemented and code reviewed.
- [ ] - [ ] All unit and integration tests pass in CI and training performance targets validated against synthetic datasets.
- [ ] - [ ] Model_card and leaderboard rows persisted to configured registry/storage and retrievable via internal APIs.
- [ ] - [ ] Error handling, logging, and OpenTelemetry tracing integrated and verified.
- [ ] - [ ] Documentation updated: executor API contract, config flags (max_sync_time, default_trials), and developer runbook for switching to async.
- [ ] - [ ] Feature toggles (dev vs prod registry) are present and tested.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] F-025
- [ ] - [ ] F-026
- [ ] - [ ] Internal model registry interface (dev stub can be used)

###### Agent Notes
- The executor must be implemented as a pluggable service invoked by the training orchestrator agent. Use XGBoost native Python API (xgboost.XGBRegressor / XGBClassifier) with nthreads controlled by config. Keep memory footprint low by using DMatrix and streaming from disk when necessary. Persist models to mlflow-compatible path and return model_registry_uri. Use deterministic random_seed passed from orchestrator for reproducibility and record it in model_card. Ensure all outputs conform to PRD model_card and leaderboard schema and that every trial is appended atomically to the leaderboard store so artifact leaderboard panel can stream partial progress.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-028-02: As a QA lead / data scientist, I want the walk-forward backtesting, metric calculations, promotion criteria enforcement, and a limited automated repair loop implemented, so that models are validated deterministically and repaired when common failures occur before surfacing to users.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Walk-forward backtest computes MAPE, RMSE, and directional accuracy for each of 5 expanding windows and returns array of 5 window stats.
- [ ] - [ ] Validation metric reported is the arithmetic mean across the 5 windows and worst-window check computes worst window vs mean and enforces worst <= 1.5× mean.
- [ ] - [ ] Promotion criteria implemented for regression per PRD: validation_mape < 15%, worst_window_mape < 25%, test_mape <= validation_mape × 1.2, training_time_seconds < 900 (15 minutes), and test_size >= 100 rows. All must pass simultaneously to promote.
- [ ] - [ ] Repair loop implements targeted repairs (expand hyperparam trials, increase regularization/reduce max_depth, recursive feature elimination to top-30 features, add calendar features for failing windows) with up to 3 cycles; each repair is recorded in model_card.repair_cycles and audit log.
- [ ] - [ ] If repairs exhaust or an unrecoverable DQ gate triggers, the pipeline returns a human_required event with clear remediation instructions.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-028-08: Implement metrics computation module
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement MAPE, RMSE, directional accuracy calculators with stable numerics and handling of zero/near-zero actuals (use standard fallback behavior to avoid division by zero).
    - [ ] - [ ] Unit test metrics against known ground-truth arrays including edge cases (zeros, negatives, NaNs).
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement aggregation helpers to compute mean and worst-window checks and serialize results into backtest_results structure.
- [ ] TASK-F-028-09: Implement promotion decision engine
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement rule engine that evaluates all PRD promotion criteria atomically and returns PASS/FAIL with per-criterion diagnostics.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add configuration to override thresholds for integration tests or workspace-level tuning.
    - [ ] - [ ] Create mapping of diagnostic codes to user-facing remediation messages used in human_required pipeline events.
- [ ] TASK-F-028-10: Implement automated repair strategies and orchestration
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement repair action: double hyperparameter trials and re-run training using previous best seed and trial history as warm-start where possible.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement repair action: increase regularization parameters (e.g., reg_lambda += factor, reduce max_depth by 1) and re-run.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement repair action: perform recursive feature elimination using feature_importance to reduce to top-30 features and re-run.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement repair action: add window-specific calendar features (binary flags) for windows failing worst-window MAPE and re-run.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement repair loop controller that attempts repairs sequentially up to 3 cycles, logs each attempt, and records repair summary in model_card.repair_cycles and audit trail.
- [ ] TASK-F-028-11: Integrate decision outputs into orchestrator flow (events + human escalation)
    - [ ] - [ ] When promotion PASS, emit agent_complete with model_card and leaderboard; when FAIL after repairs, emit human_required with remediation payload and suggested actions.
    - [ ] - [ ] Include structured payload listing failing criteria, repair attempts, and suggested user actions (e.g., expand date_range, approve features, provide annotations).
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit/integration tests that simulate failing criteria and verify repair loop behavior and human_required events.
- [ ] TASK-F-028-12: Add logging, audit, and observability for promotion & repairs
    - [ ] - [ ] Log each promotion decision with trace_id, model_id, metric snapshots, and repair actions attempted.
    - [ ] - [ ] Persist promotion decision and repair history to audit log store accessible via admin UI.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add metrics for counts of promotions vs failures and repair success rate for dashboards.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for metrics (MAPE, RMSE, directional accuracy) over varied arrays including zeros and NaNs.
- [ ] - [ ] Integration test: run a failing initial training (synthetic) that triggers a known repair (e.g., overfitting) and assert repair loop brings validation_mape below threshold or exhausts after 3 cycles.
- [ ] - [ ] Negative test: create dataset where test_size < 100 and assert promotion blocked and human_required event returned.
- [ ] - [ ] Load test: run promotion engine in parallel 10x to verify thread-safety and audit logging scales.
- [ ] - [ ] Telecom of repair results: verify that repair actions are correctly reflected in model_card.repair_cycles and audit trail accessible via API.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Promotion engine implements PRD criteria and returns deterministic PASS/FAIL with diagnostic reasons.
- [ ] - [ ] Repair loop implements the listed targeted repairs and respects max 3 cycles, with each attempt logged and persisted.
- [ ] - [ ] Orchestrator consumes promotion decisions and either continues to model persistence or emits human_required with remediation payload.
- [ ] - [ ] Unit and integration tests cover success, single-repair success, multi-repair exhaustion, and unrecoverable DQ gate cases.
- [ ] - [ ] Documentation updated describing repair strategies, diagnostic codes, and human remediation guidance.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] US-F-028-01 (training executor implementation)
- [ ] - [ ] Feature manifest & feature importance output from Stage 3 (F-025/F-026)

###### Agent Notes
- Promotion engine should be deterministic and idempotent for a given session_spec + seed. Repair actions must be narrow and reversible — do not mutate gold tables or feature manifests; only change training config/hyperparams or temporary feature selection for retrain attempts. Provide clear diagnostic codes (e.g., PROMO_FAIL_MAPE, PROMO_FAIL_WORST_WINDOW, PROMO_FAIL_OVERFIT) and include remediation text for human_required events. Record all repair attempts in model_card.repair_cycles with time, action, and resulting metrics.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-028-03: As a product engineer / QA, I want the model outputs (predictions, model_insights including SHAP for top features) and trial leaderboard to be persisted and exposed through APIs so that artifacts and UI panels can render trial history, model card, and model_insights tables.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] The system writes predictions to gold.predictions_{model_id} with the PRD-specified schema (grain_keys, target_metric, actual_value, predicted_value, lower_ci_95, upper_ci_95, residual, split, prediction_date, model_id).
- [ ] - [ ] Row count tolerance check runs before write and blocks write if written rows deviate > 2% from expected with clear error and remediation.
- [ ] - [ ] Prediction distribution check compares predicted_value distribution to training target distribution and blocks write if predicted mean deviates > 3σ, returning a calibration failure error.
- [ ] - [ ] SHAP model_insights table is written with per-row top features for promoted models (for MVP compute SHAP on sample of rows if full dataset is large) following PRD shap schema.
- [ ] - [ ] Leaderboard API endpoint /api/v1/models/{model_id}/leaderboard returns paginated JSON of trials including trial metadata and is authenticated/authorized per workspace rules.
- [ ] - [ ] All writes include lineage metadata (session_spec, governance_manifest_version, feature_manifest_version) and are audit-logged.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-028-13: Implement predictions writer with DQ checks
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement writer that accepts model_id, predictions dataframe, expected_row_count, and runs row count tolerance check (±2%).
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement distribution check comparing predicted mean/std to training target baseline and block on > 3σ deviation with error code.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement CI integrity sanity check (lower_ci_95 < predicted_value < upper_ci_95) and ensure > 99% rows pass; block write if violation > 1%.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add transactional write to warehouse/gold schema and rollback on DQ failure; log DQ outcomes to audit.
- [ ] TASK-F-028-14: Implement SHAP compute pipeline for model_insights table
    - [ ] - [ ] Integrate shap calculation for XGBoost models using TreeExplainer and compute SHAP values for top-N rows (configurable sample size) for MVP; ensure no PII columns included.
    - [ ] - [ ] Assemble model_insights rows per PRD schema: { grain_keys, feature_name, shap_value, feature_value, abs_shap_rank, model_id } and persist to gold.model_insights_{model_id}.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit test for SHAP computation on a small synthetic dataset verifying expected sign/directionality for constructed features.
- [ ] TASK-F-028-15: Implement leaderboard persistence and API endpoint
    - [ ] - [ ] Persist every trial record (trial_id, algorithm, hyperparameters, validation_mape, walk_forward_mapes, training_duration_seconds, promoted bool) to model_leaderboard table in model registry schema.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement paginated API endpoint /api/v1/models/{model_id}/leaderboard that enforces workspace auth and returns leaderboard JSON with total_count and page cursors.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add API tests for pagination, auth, and filtering by promoted flag and algorithm.
- [ ] TASK-F-028-16: Add lineage, audit logging, and access control
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure every persisted artifact (predictions, model_insights, leaderboard/model_card) includes lineage block linking to session_spec id, governance_manifest_version, and feature_manifest_version.
    - [ ] - [ ] Append a structured audit log entry for each write containing actor (system/orchestrator), trace_id, timestamp, and write stats.
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure access to leaderboard and model_card endpoints respects workspace-level ABAC rules and include tests that verify unauthorized access is rejected.
- [ ] TASK-F-028-17: Testing & validation of persistence paths
    - [ ] - [ ] End-to-end integration test: run training, persist predictions and model_insights, then fetch via gold API to assert schema and sample values.
    - [ ] - [ ] Negative test: simulate distribution check failure and assert write blocked and appropriate error logged and returned.
    - [ ] - [ ] Performance test: verify writing predictions table for up to 100K rows completes within a reasonable window and does not exceed DB transaction timeouts.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Integration E2E: run training to produce predictions and model_insights and assert persisted tables exist and schema columns match PRD.
- [ ] - [ ] DQ negative test: intentionally change predicted distribution to trigger >3σ deviation and assert write blocked and error surfaced.
- [ ] - [ ] Row count tolerance test: modify expected_row_count param to create >2% delta and assert blocking behavior and error message.
- [ ] - [ ] API auth tests: request leaderboard endpoint as workspace member and as unauthorized user and assert correct status codes.
- [ ] - [ ] SHAP sample test: verify SHAP values sign/direction on synthetic dataset and ensure PII columns not included in outputs.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Predictions and model_insights writer implemented with DQ checks and transactional safety.
- [ ] - [ ] Leaderboard persistence and paginated API endpoint implemented and covered by tests.
- [ ] - [ ] Lineage metadata and audit logs are attached to every persisted object and visible via audit API.
- [ ] - [ ] Access control enforced on retrieval APIs and validated in tests.
- [ ] - [ ] Documentation updated: predictions table schema, model_insights schema, leaderboard API contract, and error codes for DQ gate failures.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] US-F-028-01 (model training & model_card generation)
- [ ] - [ ] Warehouse write access provided by gold layer writer service (Stage 5)
- [ ] - [ ] PII flags from governance manifest (F-025) to ensure PII not included in SHAP outputs

###### Agent Notes
- Predictions writes should call deterministic validation rules before committing to the gold schema. For MVP, SHAP computation can sample up to N rows (configurable, default 1000) to keep compute low — record that sampling was used in model_card. Persist leaderboard records on every trial atomically so UIs can stream progress. Use the same trace_id across training, persistence, and audit logs. Expose explicit error codes for DQ gate failures: DQ_ROWCOUNT_TOLERANCE, DQ_DISTRIBUTION_CALIBRATION, DQ_CI_INTEGRITY to allow UI mapping to remediation steps.

#### Requirement / Feature: Walk-forward backtesting implementation (5 expanding windows)

**Feature ID:** `F-029`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, ml`

**Rationale:** Walk-forward backtesting is mandatory on temporal data; MVP must validate this behavior.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-028

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-029-01: As a training orchestrator, I want a deterministic walk-forward backtesting engine that runs 5 expanding windows, so that temporal validation metrics reflect production performance and prevent optimistic leakage.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] The engine divides the training period into 5 equally-sized expanding windows per session_spec and feature manifest.
- [ ] - [ ] For each window i, the engine trains on windows 1..i and evaluates on window i+1 for i in 1..5 (last window uses the held-out validation slice as defined).
- [ ] - [ ] The engine computes and returns MAPE, RMSE, and directional accuracy per window and the mean across windows.
- [ ] - [ ] The engine flags a walk-forward run as failed if the worst single-window metric exceeds 1.5× the mean metric.
- [ ] - [ ] The engine never uses the final test set (most recent 15%) for hyperparameter tuning and enforces test-set isolation.
- [ ] - [ ] Engine logs include trace_id, window boundaries, training row counts, evaluation metrics, and any data quality anomalies.
- [ ] - [ ] API/contract: training orchestrator receives a predictable JSON backtest_result with walk_forward_mapes (array length 5) and related metrics.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-029-01-01: Design walk-forward backtest algorithm & interface
    - [ ] - [ ] Define input schema for backtest runner (gold_table location, date_column, grain_keys, feature_manifest_version, split_config, random_seed).
    - [ ] - [ ] Define output schema for backtest_result JSON (per-window mape/rmse/directional_accuracy, mean, worst_window, training_row_counts, window_date_ranges).
    - [ ] - [ ] Write pseudocode and sequence diagram for expanding-window logic with edge-case handling (non-divisible partitions, insufficient rows).
    - [ ] - [ ] Add validation rules for minimum rows per training window (min 500 overall training rows; per-window min fallback behavior).
    - [ ] - [ ] Review design with data engineering and ML leads and iterate based on feedback.
- [ ] TASK-F-029-01-02: Implement backtest runner backend service (Python) with deterministic splitting
    - [ ] - [ ] Create new module training/backtest/walk_forward.py implementing expanding-window split generation and orchestration API.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement deterministic SQL/warehouse fetch logic that streams required windows without materializing full dataset in memory (use warehouse cursor/pagination).
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement training harness interface that accepts a train_dataframe and a predict_dataframe and returns metrics (MAPE/RMSE/directional_accuracy).
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement robust input validation and raise structured errors for insufficient rows, missing date column, or non-unique grain keys.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests covering split generation, boundary cases, and deterministic behavior.
- [ ] TASK-F-029-01-03: Add logging, observability, and tracing
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit structured logs (JSON) at start/end of each window with trace_id, job_id, window_index, row_counts, and metric results.
    - [ ] - [ ] Integrate OpenTelemetry spans for backtest overall and per-window subspans; propagate trace_id from orchestrator.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add metrics to Prometheus: backtest_duration_seconds, backtest_window_duration_seconds, backtest_failures_total, backtest_runs_total.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add error categories and map to HTTP 4xx/5xx responses for API consumers.
    - [ ] - [ ] Document log fields and telemetry in the developer README.
- [ ] TASK-F-029-01-04: Validate worst-window rule and test-set isolation
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement post-processing check that computes mean metric and worst window and enforces worst_window <= 1.5 * mean.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement explicit test-set exclusion: verify held-out test slice (most recent 15%) is not used in any training or hyperparameter selection step; raise error if violated.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add automated test that simulates a failure window and asserts rule triggers repair loop flag.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit test for a dataset that exactly matches boundary conditions (e.g., window sizes off-by-one).
