- [ ] TASK-F-029-01-05: Add CI and security validation for the module
    - [ ] - [ ] Add static type checks (mypy) and linting rules for new module.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit and integration tests to CI pipeline with coverage threshold for new files (>= 80%).
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure no secrets or DB credentials are logged; add a check to detect accidental credential logging patterns.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add dependency vulnerability scan to the pipeline for any new third-party libs used.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests: split generator, metric computations, exception cases (missing date, duplicates).
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests: end-to-end run using DuckDB with synthetic temporal dataset verifying 5 windows and metrics structure.
- [ ] - [ ] Property tests: randomized time-series lengths to validate deterministic windowing and no overlap with test set.
- [ ] - [ ] Regression tests: assert worst-window rule behavior across multiple seeds and datasets.
- [ ] - [ ] Security tests: ensure no PII/leaked data sent to logs and verify access controls on backtest endpoints.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] All unit tests for split generation, metrics, and edge-cases pass.
- [ ] - [ ] Backtest runner implemented and invoked by a local integration test against DuckDB sample dataset.
- [ ] - [ ] Structured logs and OpenTelemetry traces emitted and visible in staging observability stack.
- [ ] - [ ] Design and output schemas documented and approved in code repo README.
- [ ] - [ ] Code merged to main branch and deployable by CI pipeline.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] F-028 (Data modeling & feature engineering must provide gold table and feature manifest).

###### Agent Notes
- Implement pure-Python deterministic splitter; do not call LLMs for split logic. Use warehouse cursor/pagination to fetch window slices. Fail fast on insufficient rows and surface clear remediation messages (e.g. 'Expand date_range to include additional historical data'). Ensure outputs include full walk_forward_mapes array of length 5 even when data forces smaller windows — pad with nulls and log a warning.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-029-02: As an ML training orchestrator, I want the XGBoost training runner to execute hyperparameter trials with the walk-forward protocol and populate the model trial leaderboard, so that model selection is auditable and reproducible.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] The orchestrator trains XGBoost trials using the walk-forward backtest runner and records per-trial walk_forward_mapes (length 5), validation_mape (mean), and training_duration_seconds.
- [ ] - [ ] The orchestrator respects hyperparameter search limits (default 30 trials) and supports early stopping and pruning.
- [ ] - [ ] Every trial writes a leaderboard record with fields {trial_id, algorithm, hyperparameters, validation_mape, test_mape, training_duration_seconds, walk_forward_mapes, promoted:false} to the model_trials table.
- [ ] - [ ] If the top-2 models are within 3% on validation_mape, an ensemble trial is automatically executed and evaluated with the same walk-forward protocol.
- [ ] - [ ] Training errors (OOM, timeout) are captured, retried per policy, and surfaced to the repair loop with logging and structured error codes.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-029-02-01: Implement XGBoost training runner integrated with walk-forward backtester
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement training wrapper that accepts hyperparameters JSON, trains XGBoost with early_stopping_rounds, and returns model artifact in memory or serialized buffer.
    - [ ] - [ ] Wire training wrapper to call backtest runner for each trial: run train/predict per-window and aggregate metrics.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement consistent random seed handling for reproducibility across runs.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add retry logic for transient failures with exponential backoff (max 2 retries) and fallback to LightGBM if training time exceeds threshold and config permits.
- [ ] TASK-F-029-02-02: Implement hyperparameter search & pruning orchestration
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement Optuna-style random search orchestrator with configurable n_trials and a pruning callback tied to early_stopping results.
    - [ ] - [ ] Expose configuration for search space defaults for XGBoost (eta, max_depth, reg_lambda, subsample, colsample_bytree, n_estimators).
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement synchronous mode for <= 15 minute trainings and async queue handoff for longer runs (switch to async if estimated training_time_upper_bound > 15m).
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests for search orchestrator respecting n_trials and pruning behavior.
- [ ] TASK-F-029-02-03: Persist trial records and update leaderboard service
    - [ ] - [ ] Design model_trials table schema per PRD leaderboard record fields and create migration script for Postgres.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement DB writer to insert trial record on trial completion and mark promoted flag accordingly.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement leaderboard API endpoint /api/v1/models/{model_id}/leaderboard that returns paginated JSON and supports filter by promoted status.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add transactional guarantees: trial insert + model_card update must be atomic for a trial that becomes promoted.
- [ ] TASK-F-029-02-04: Implement promotion decision checks and ensemble trigger
    - [ ] - [ ] After all trials complete, compute top-2 validation_mape; if within 3% trigger ensemble training using average predictions across fold models and evaluate via walk-forward runner.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement promotion criteria checks (validation_mape < 15%, worst_window < 25%, test_mape <= validation_mape * 1.2, training_time < threshold, test_set_size >= 100) and set model_card.promotion_status accordingly.
    - [ ] - [ ] On promotion PASS, serialize model to model registry (pickle + onnx fallback) and update model_card with registry URI.
    - [ ] - [ ] On promotion FAIL, increment repair_cycles and enqueue targeted repair action per failure mapping.
- [ ] TASK-F-029-02-05: Add error handling, retry, and observability
    - [ ] - [ ] Add structured error types for OOM, timeout, data-quality, and convergence failures; map to user-facing remediation messages.
    - [ ] - [ ] Log hyperparameter and trial metadata to tracing system; include partial metrics on failures for debugging.
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit Prometheus metrics: trials_started_total, trials_failed_total, trials_promoted_total, ensemble_runs_total.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add alerts for repeated trial failures (>=3 failures within 30 minutes) to pager on-call.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests: training wrapper behavior, early stopping, serialization, and deterministic random_seed behavior.
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests: full hyperparameter run with synthetic data that produces known metrics, verify leaderboard records persisted and API returns them.
- [ ] - [ ] Failure mode tests: simulate OOM/timeout and assert structured error is surfaced and retries happen correctly.
- [ ] - [ ] Performance test: measure training duration and verify async handoff occurs for >15m estimated runs.
- [ ] - [ ] Security test: verify stored trial hyperparameters do not leak secrets and DB writes use parameterized queries.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] XGBoost runner executes a full hyperparameter run in staging against sample gold_table and produces populated leaderboard records.
- [ ] - [ ] Ensemble trigger executes automatically when condition met and writes ensemble trial to leaderboard.
- [ ] - [ ] Promotion criteria engine runs deterministically and updates model_card with promotion_status.
- [ ] - [ ] Leaderboard API returns consistent paginated results and documentation for fields is present.
- [ ] - [ ] All new code covered by unit and integration tests and merged via CI.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] F-028 (feature manifest + gold table must be available).
- [ ] - [ ] TASK-F-029-01-02 (backtest runner implementation).

###### Agent Notes
- For MVP keep XGBoost as primary algorithm for non-prophet temporal cases. Use deterministic seeds and fixed CV where required. Use in-memory model serialization for small datasets; for larger runs stream model artifacts to model-registry S3 bucket. Do not send raw rows to any LLM. Repair cycle mapping follows PRD table — implement targeted parameter adjustments rather than blind full re-run.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-029-03: As a product user viewing artifacts, I want backtest results and trial leaderboard entries stored and surfaced via API and artifact panels, so that model selection is transparent and I can inspect per-window performance.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Every training run stores leaderboard entries with walk_forward_mapes (array of 5), validation_mape, test_mape, training_duration_seconds, hyperparameters JSON, and promoted boolean.
- [ ] - [ ] Model_card.backtest_results contains per-window metric objects for the promoted model and is persisted to the model registry.
- [ ] - [ ] The leaderboard API (/api/v1/models/{model_id}/leaderboard) returns trial records and includes walk_forward_mapes and a trade_off_summary field.
- [ ] - [ ] Artifact generation agent can fetch and embed the top-N leaderboard records into the artifact (panel #7) without embedding raw training data.
- [ ] - [ ] Row-level access controls apply to leaderboard API; only workspace members with appropriate role can access trial hyperparameters; public tokens return sanitized view (no hyperparameter secrets).

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-029-03-01: Design and create model_trials and model_card storage schema
    - [ ] - [ ] Draft SQL migration for model_trials table with fields per PRD and JSONB for hyperparameters and walk_forward_mapes.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add model_card.backtest_results field mapping and update model_card schema version migration.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add index on model_id and created_at for leaderboard paging performance.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add RBAC rules for access control: hyperparameters visible only to workspace members with 'analyst' or higher; sanitized payload for public/embedded contexts.
- [ ] TASK-F-029-03-02: Implement leaderboard API and pagination
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement GET /api/v1/models/{model_id}/leaderboard with page & page_size params and stable ordering by validation_mape asc then training_duration_seconds asc.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement filtering/query params for promoted:true/false and algorithm type.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement response sanitization logic for token scopes (full vs public) and include trade_off_summary field.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add API contract tests and OpenAPI spec updates.
- [ ] TASK-F-029-03-03: Extend artifact generation to include leaderboard panel (backend data access)
    - [ ] - [ ] Update artifact generation agent to fetch top 10 leaderboard records and inject JSON into artifact metadata (not raw rows).
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure artifact HTML references only model_id and leaderboard API for dynamic fetch; do not embed hyperparameter secrets into HTML.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add deterministic placeholder UI in artifact for panel when no leaderboard records exist.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add artifact-level DQ gate to verify leaderboard fetch succeeds before finalizing artifact file.
- [ ] TASK-F-029-03-04: Testing and QA for leaderboard and artifact exposure
    - [ ] - [ ] Create integration test that triggers a training run and asserts leaderboard entry creation and API visibility.
    - [ ] - [ ] Create end-to-end artifact generation test that loads artifact in headless browser and asserts leaderboard panel renders via API call (mocked).
    - [ ] - [ ] Security test: verify public embed tokens cannot access hyperparameters and that RBAC rules enforced.
    - [ ] - [ ] Accessibility test: ensure leaderboard panel meets basic a11y (aria-label and keyboard navigation).

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for DB writer for model_trials and model_card updates including RBAC sanitization.
- [ ] - [ ] API contract tests for /api/v1/models/{model_id}/leaderboard with scenarios for workspace member vs public token.
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests: full pipeline run -> leaderboard record -> artifact generation -> artifact panel fetch (headless browser).
- [ ] - [ ] Load test: request leaderboard API concurrently (100 RPS) to verify paging and DB index efficiency.
- [ ] - [ ] Security review: validate no secret hyperparameters are exposed to public or embed contexts.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Leaderboard records persist for every trial and are queryable via the API with RBAC enforcement.
- [ ] - [ ] Artifact generation includes a working leaderboard panel that fetches records from the leaderboard API at load time.
- [ ] - [ ] API and artifact integration tests pass in CI and artifacts in staging render the leaderboard panel.
- [ ] - [ ] Documentation for the leaderboard API and artifact panel is published in the developer docs.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] TASK-F-029-02-03 (persist trial records).
- [ ] - [ ] TASK-F-029-01-03 (logging & tracing) for auditability.

###### Agent Notes
- Leaderboard records must be immutable after write; updates should create audit log entries. Trade_off_summary may be generated by a summary agent later, but for MVP produce a deterministic string describing validation_mape vs winner and training_duration ratio. Ensure artifact panel fetches leaderboard via authenticated gold-layer API with embed-token scope checks.

#### Requirement / Feature: Model card schema generation and persistence (ModelCard)

**Feature ID:** `F-030`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, data`

**Rationale:** Model card provides audit trail and is surfaced in artifacts per spec.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-028
- [ ] F-029

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-030-01: As an ML engineer, I want the training orchestrator to generate a deterministic, versioned ModelCard JSON for every completed model training run, so that every model has a complete, auditable record of algorithm, hyperparameters, training lineage, backtest results and promotion decision.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A JSON Schema for ModelCard is defined and stored in the repo and matches the ModelCard interface in PRD Table 29 (required fields present: model_id, workspace_id, session_id, algorithm, target_type, target_metric, hyperparameters, feature_manifest_version, feature_count, top_features, backtest_results, validation_mape, test_mape, promotion_status, repair_cycles, training_duration_seconds, trained_at, model_registry_uri, lineage).
- [ ] Training orchestrator emits a ModelCard JSON for every completed trial and for the final promoted model with schema validation passing before persistence.
- [ ] ModelCard includes walk-forward backtest results (array of 5 window metrics) consistent with the training run logs; validation_mape is computed as mean across windows and worst window metric is included.
- [ ] ModelCard generation is deterministic (same inputs -> same ModelCard JSON) aside from timestamps and training_duration_seconds; a content hash is computed and recorded.
- [ ] ModelCard generator validates presence of lineage fields (gold_table, source_tables, governance_manifest_version, semantic_layer_version) and fails the stage with a human_required event if any lineage field is missing.
- [ ] Generation logs structured events (trace_id, model_id, session_id, validation_status) to distributed tracing and pipeline audit log.
- [ ] Unit tests cover positive and negative schema validation cases and deterministic hashing behavior.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-030-01: Design and author canonical JSON Schema for ModelCard and associated sub-schemas (top_features, backtest_result, lineage)
    - [ ] Review PRD model_card interface and map each field to JSON Schema types and constraints (required, formats, enums).
    - [ ] Create separate subschemas for top_features (name, importance, shap_mean) and backtest_results (window:int, mape:float, rmse:float, directional_accuracy:float).
    - [ ] Add format checks for ISO8601 date fields and URI fields (model_registry_uri, onnx_uri).
    - [ ] Add versioning metadata in schema (schema_version field) and store schema in /schemas/model_card/v1.json in repo.
    - [ ] Document backward compatibility rules for future schema changes (semver guidance).
- [ ] TASK-F-030-02: Implement ModelCard generator module inside training orchestrator (backend/AI)
    - [ ] Define training orchestrator interface to call model_card_generator.generate(model_run_context) at end of training pipeline.
    - [ ] Implement population logic: algorithm, target_type, hyperparameters snapshot, feature_manifest_version, top_features (from SHAP mean), backtest_results (array), validation_mape, test_mape, promotion_status, repair_cycles, training_duration_seconds, trained_at, lineage.
    - [ ] Compute deterministic content_hash (SHA256 over sorted keys excluding trained_at and training_duration_seconds) and include as model_card.hash.
    - [ ] Perform JSON Schema validation (using AJV or equivalent) before returning ModelCard object; return explicit validation errors if validation fails.
    - [ ] Emit tracing/logging events with trace_id, model_id and validation outcome.
- [ ] TASK-F-030-03: Integrate ModelCard generation into existing training flow and leaderboard writer
    - [ ] Hook generator into finalization step of training orchestrator so ModelCard generation runs after walk-forward backtest and before persistence.
    - [ ] Ensure ModelCard references the trial leaderboard table name and populates field training_trial_summary linking to leaderboard.
    - [ ] If multiple trials (hyperparameter search) exist, attach top-5 trials summary in the ModelCard under a trials_summary field (trial_id, algorithm, hyperparameters, validation_mape, training_duration_seconds).
    - [ ] Ensure repair_cycles count is populated from orchestrator's repair loop counters.
    - [ ] Add unit test that simulates a training workflow and asserts ModelCard fields match expected values.
- [ ] TASK-F-030-04: Add validation, error handling and audit logging for ModelCard generation
    - [ ] On JSON Schema validation failure, raise a pipeline 'agent_error' event with remediation suggestion and mark model promotion_status='FAIL' and record validation_errors in ModelCard draft.
    - [ ] If lineage fields missing, emit a 'human_required' SSE event and persist draft ModelCard with human_review_required=true and a short remediation_message.
    - [ ] Log structured audit event to pipeline audit log (actor: training_orchestrator, action: generate_model_card, payload: {model_id, session_id, schema_validation_passed}).
    - [ ] Add metrics to monitoring (Prometheus): modelcard_generation_count, modelcard_generation_failures, modelcard_schema_validation_time_ms.
- [ ] TASK-F-030-05: Write unit and integration tests for ModelCard generator
    - [ ] Unit tests for schema validation success and failure cases (missing required fields, wrong types, invalid ISO dates).
    - [ ] Integration test that runs a mock training orchestrator with synthetic backtest results and asserts persisted ModelCard matches expected JSON Schema and content_hash deterministic behavior.
    - [ ] Add fuzz test inputs to detect injection/hallucination by AI agents (ensure only training metadata is used, not raw data).
    - [ ] Add CI pipeline step to run schema validation tests on PRs touching training or model registry code.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests validating JSON Schema constraints and deterministic hash behavior.
- [ ] Integration test: end-to-end training run (mocked training) produces valid ModelCard saved to temp store; compare fields to expected.
- [ ] Negative test: drop lineage fields and assert pipeline emits 'human_required' SSE and draft ModelCard stored with human_review_required=true.
- [ ] Schema backward-compatibility test: new schema version upgrade simulation with older saved ModelCards validation.
- [ ] Load test: generate 1000 ModelCards in loop to validate generator latency and monitoring.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] ModelCard JSON Schema stored in repo and reviewed (PR merged).
- [ ] ModelCard generator implemented and integrated into training orchestrator.
- [ ] All unit and integration tests passing in CI with coverage >= 80% for the module.
- [ ] Structured logs and metrics emitted for generation events.
- [ ] Pipeline emits human_required events when lineage is missing.
- [ ] Documentation updated: README for /schemas/model_card and generator usage.
- [ ] Code reviewed and security checklist passed (no secrets logged, no PII in ModelCard).

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-028
- [ ] F-029

###### Agent Notes
- ModelCard generation must not include any raw data samples. Use only metadata outputs from training orchestrator, SHAP aggregator, and leaderboard. Use AJV (Node) or jsonschema (Python) depending on orchestrator runtime. Ensure deterministic hashing excludes non-deterministic fields (trained_at, training_duration_seconds) but include all provenance and hyperparameter values. If AI agents propose additional free-text notes, store them in a separate 'notes' optional field and escape/sanitize content; but primary fields must be structured.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-030-02: As a backend engineer, I want ModelCard objects and associated artifacts (model binary, ONNX/pickle, SHAP bundle, leaderboard records) to be atomically persisted to the model registry and the workspace artifact record, so that artifacts and models are discoverable, linkable and retrievable with full lineage and access control.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Persisted ModelCard references a model_registry_uri that points to the model binary location and is queryable via model registry service (MLflow-compatible).
- [ ] Persistence is atomic: either ModelCard + model binaries + SHAP bundle + leaderboard entries are all persisted or none are; use transactional semantics or idempotent write with rollback on failures.
- [ ] Model binaries are stored in configured artifact storage (S3-compatible) and accessible via secure URIs; storage permissions follow workspace scoping and embed token rules.
- [ ] SHAP explainability bundles (per-row SHAP for promoted model) are persisted alongside model binary and a summary aggregate inserted in gold.model_insights_{model_id}.
- [ ] Leaderboards (trial logs) are written to model registry / model_leaderboard table and accessible via /api/v1/models/{model_id}/leaderboard endpoint (paginated).
- [ ] ModelCard persisted record version increments (semver) and is recorded in workspace artifact metadata (artifact.model_card = ModelCard).
- [ ] Security: persisted objects are encrypted at rest, registry URIs do not leak database credentials, and access control enforced via ABAC.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-030-06: Extend model registry persistence layer to accept and store ModelCard JSON and model artifact bundles
    - [ ] Add API method model_registry.store_model_bundle(model_id, bundle: {model_card, model_binary, shap_bundle, leaderboard_csv}) with idempotency_key support.
    - [ ] Implement S3-compatible storage of model_binary (pickle/onnx) and shap_bundle; use workspace-specific path prefixes and server-side encryption (SSE).
    - [ ] Generate model_registry_uri and store it in ModelCard.model_registry_uri and artifact lineage.
    - [ ] Record storage metadata (size, sha256, storage_path) and include in model registry DB table.
    - [ ] Write unit tests for storage operations including idempotency behavior.
- [ ] TASK-F-030-07: Implement transactional persistence flow with rollback and retry semantics
    - [ ] Design persistence orchestration: 1) upload binaries to object storage (temp path), 2) persist ModelCard and metadata to Postgres, 3) move binaries to final path (atomic rename) or mark as active, 4) write leaderboard records, 5) on failure rollback DB changes and delete temp objects.
    - [ ] Implement idempotent upsert path keyed by model_id + content_hash to prevent duplicate writes from retries.
    - [ ] Implement retry with exponential backoff for transient storage or DB failures; after max retries emit pipeline 'error' event and persist a failure record for manual recovery.
    - [ ] Add integration tests simulating partial failures (e.g., DB insert fails after binary upload) and assert rollback leaves no orphaned objects.
- [ ] TASK-F-030-08: Write gold layer writer connector to create gold.model_insights_{model_id} and gold.predictions_{model_id} metadata links
    - [ ] Define schema and DDL for gold.model_insights_{model_id} and gold.predictions_{model_id} per PRD Table 20/21.
    - [ ] Implement writer method to create or update artifact-specific gold tables and write model_card lineage metadata to artifact record.
    - [ ] Enforce row_count and distribution checks before finalizing writes; if checks fail block and return detailed delta errors.
    - [ ] Log DQ gate outcomes to artifact audit and include references to ModelCard.model_id in the DQ log entry.
- [ ] TASK-F-030-09: Expose leaderboard persistence and API surface for trial logs
    - [ ] Add DB table model_leaderboard with fields per PRD (trial_id, algorithm, hyperparameters JSON, validation_mape, test_mape, training_duration_seconds, walk_forward_mapes array, promoted boolean, trade_off_summary).
    - [ ] Implement background writer that records every trial to model_leaderboard during training with transactional guarantees.
    - [ ] Implement paginated API /api/v1/models/{model_id}/leaderboard with RBAC checks, rate limiting, and caching headers.
    - [ ] Add automated tests: write and read leaderboard entries; test pagination and RBAC enforced.
- [ ] TASK-F-030-10: Security and devops hardening for ModelCard persistence pipeline
    - [ ] Ensure all object storage writes use workspace-specific encryption keys (KMS) and URIs do not leak secrets; rotate keys per policy.
    - [ ] Add IAM policies restricting model registry operations to service principal(s) and workspace owners/owners' roles.
    - [ ] Instrument audit logging for all persistence actions (who wrote model_card, timestamp, actor_id) and ensure logs are append-only and exportable.
    - [ ] Add S3 lifecycle policy to manage model binary retention and provide admin tools to purge archived versions.
- [ ] TASK-F-030-11: Create migration and infra scripts (DB schema + storage buckets) and runbook
    - [ ] Create SQL migration for model_registry tables, model_leaderboard table, and artifact model_card field extension.
    - [ ] Provision object storage prefixes/buckets and set permissions via IaC (Terraform) with workspace scoping.
    - [ ] Create runbook for manual recovery of partial persistence failures (how to rehydrate DB entries and delete orphan objects).
    - [ ] Add infra unit test for Terraform plan and ensure required secrets exist in vault.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Integration test: full persistence of model_card + binary + shap + leaderboard with success path validation.
- [ ] Failure injection tests: simulate DB and storage failures to verify rollback and no orphaned objects.
- [ ] Security test: verify stored objects require proper IAM to access; verify encrypted at rest flags.
- [ ] API test: GET model_card and GET leaderboard with RBAC checks and pagination.
- [ ] Performance test: persist 50MB model binary and validate upload latency; validate system metrics and monitoring.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] ModelCard and model artifact bundle persisted to model registry and object storage with idempotent API.
- [ ] Transactional persistence flow implemented with rollback on failure and unit/integration tests for partial failure cases.
- [ ] Leaderboards and gold layer tables created and linked to model_card and artifact records.
- [ ] Security measures in place: encryption at rest, IAM restrictions, audit logging, and runbook documented.
- [ ] DB migrations and IaC merged and applied to staging; smoke test confirms retrieval of persisted model_card and artifacts.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-028
- [ ] F-029

###### Agent Notes
- Model registry should remain MLflow-compatible where possible. For binary storage use content-hashed path: s3://analytIQ-models/{workspace_id}/{model_id}/{hash}.{ext}. ModelCard.model_registry_uri should be a stable, permissioned URI returned by registry. Implement idempotency via model_id+content_hash. SHAP bundles may be large; store per-row SHAP as compressed parquet in object storage and only persist aggregates (top_features) in ModelCard. Ensure no raw training rows are written to ModelCard or logs.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-030-03: As a product user and API consumer, I want to retrieve model cards and leaderboards via secure APIs and surface them in artifact metadata so that artifacts display the model provenance and users can inspect training lineage, trial history and promotion decisions.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A secure API endpoint GET /api/v1/models/{model_id} returns the persisted ModelCard JSON with appropriate RBAC enforcement; only workspace members with workspace access can retrieve model_card.
- [ ] Endpoint GET /api/v1/models/{model_id}/leaderboard returns paginated trial logs with page_size and next_page tokens; response matches persisted leaderboard records.
- [ ] Artifact metadata API /api/v1/artifacts/{id} includes a model_card summary (model_id, algorithm, validation_mape, promotion_status) and link to full model_card URI.
- [ ] Frontend artifact detail view consumes the summary and provides a 'View model card' action that calls the model API; UI displays top_features, backtest_results and promotion_status.
- [ ] API responses include caching headers, ETag based on ModelCard.content_hash, and support conditional GET to reduce load.
- [ ] All endpoints log access in audit log with actor, timestamp, model_id/artifact_id and do not expose PII.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-030-12: Implement GET /api/v1/models/{model_id} API endpoint with schema validation and RBAC
    - [ ] Define controller method that loads ModelCard from registry DB by model_id and returns JSON Schema-validated payload.
    - [ ] Enforce ABAC: check caller workspace membership and role; return 403 if unauthorized.
    - [ ] Add ETag header using ModelCard.hash and support If-None-Match to return 304 when unchanged.
    - [ ] Add structured access audit log entry for each successful/failed request.
    - [ ] Write unit tests for RBAC and ETag behavior.
- [ ] TASK-F-030-13: Implement GET /api/v1/models/{model_id}/leaderboard endpoint
    - [ ] Query model_leaderboard table with pagination (limit, offset or cursor) and support sorting by validation_mape and training_duration_seconds.
    - [ ] Apply RBAC same as model endpoint and rate limiting for public tokens.
    - [ ] Return standard paginated response with next_page token and total_count where available.
    - [ ] Add API contract tests and documentation (OpenAPI spec).
- [ ] TASK-F-030-14: Extend /api/v1/artifacts/{id} metadata to include model_card summary and stable link
    - [ ] Update artifact record schema to include model_card_summary: {model_id, algorithm, validation_mape, promotion_status, trained_at}.
    - [ ] When artifact is created/updated, populate summary from persisted ModelCard.
    - [ ] Ensure artifact metadata API returns the link to /api/v1/models/{model_id} with proper access controls.
    - [ ] Add migration script and tests verifying artifact record contains the summary after model persistence.
- [ ] TASK-F-030-15: Frontend: Add 'View model card' UI and model card modal in artifact detail page
    - [ ] Implement a lightweight modal that fetches GET /api/v1/models/{model_id} and displays top_features (bar list), backtest_results (table), validation_mape/test_mape, promotion_status, lineage summary and link to download SHAP bundle.
    - [ ] Show promotion_status with colored badge and include 'View trial leaderboard' button that opens leaderboard panel calling /leaderboard API.
    - [ ] Handle error states (403 unauthorized, 404 not found, 500 server error) with user-friendly messages and retry button.
    - [ ] Add unit and E2E tests for modal: open artifact, click view model card, assert content renders and ETag caching used.
- [ ] TASK-F-030-16: Add monitoring, caching and rate-limiting for model card APIs
    - [ ] Add request metrics (requests_total, latency_histogram) and error rate alerts for model APIs.
    - [ ] Implement caching layer (Redis) keyed by ModelCard.hash to serve frequent reads; invalidate on new ModelCard persistence.
    - [ ] Apply API rate limits for anonymous/public embed tokens; whitelist workspace internal services.
    - [ ] Add integration tests for caching correctness and cache invalidation on model update.
- [ ] TASK-F-030-17: Write API and UI tests and documentation
    - [ ] Write integration tests covering GET model_card and leaderboard endpoints with RBAC permutations (owner, viewer, non-member).
    - [ ] Add Cypress E2E test for artifact page showing model card modal and leaderboard pagination.
    - [ ] Update API OpenAPI spec and postman collection with examples for model_card and leaderboard endpoints.
    - [ ] Document frontend usage patterns and caching expectations in product docs.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for API controllers and RBAC logic.
- [ ] Integration tests for DB-backed model_card retrieval, including ETag 304 behavior.
- [ ] Security tests: validate that unauthorized users receive 403 and that audit logs capture attempts.
- [ ] E2E tests: artifact page shows model card and leaderboard and handles caching and errors gracefully.
- [ ] Load test: concurrent GET requests for model_card endpoint with caching enabled; validate latency targets.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Model card retrieval and leaderboard endpoints implemented and documented in OpenAPI.
- [ ] Artifact metadata updated to include model_card summary and links.
- [ ] Frontend modal implemented with error handling and tests.
- [ ] RBAC enforced and ETag caching implemented; audit logs record access.
- [ ] Monitoring and rate limiting configured; alerts for API error spikes set.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-028
- [ ] F-029

###### Agent Notes
- APIs must return the canonical ModelCard JSON produced and validated earlier. Use ModelCard.hash as ETag and cache key. Enforce ABAC via existing authorization middleware. Frontend should avoid requesting full SHAP bundles by default; provide a download link (requires elevated permission) and show aggregated top_features inline. Keep payloads small; do not include per-row SHAP by default in API responses unless requested with pagination.

#### Requirement / Feature: Job queue integration for training runs (Redis + simple queue)

**Feature ID:** `F-031`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, infra`

**Rationale:** Queued training runs prevent blocking the API and allow status polling in UI.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-028

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-031-01: As an API client, I want to submit long-running model training requests asynchronously to a Redis-backed job queue, so that the API is non-blocking and I receive a job_id for polling or later retrieval of results.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The POST /api/v1/trainings endpoint accepts a validated session_spec and immediately returns 202 Accepted with a stable job_id and enqueue timestamp.
- [ ] Invalid session_spec or missing required fields return 400 with structured error details and no job is enqueued.
- [ ] A persistent training_jobs record is created in Postgres with status values: QUEUED -> RUNNING -> SUCCEEDED|FAILED|CANCELLED and includes trace_id, workspace_id, session_id, model_card reference (when promoted), start/end timestamps and last_error.
- [ ] The job payload is enqueued in Redis (Bull/BullMQ or equivalent) and visible via a deterministic queue key per workspace (e.g. queue:training:{workspace_id}).
- [ ] Job enqueue includes metadata for idempotency and replay (client_request_id or session_spec.hash). Duplicate requests with same idempotency key within 24 hours return existing job_id instead of creating a new job.
- [ ] Server-side validation rejects requests if governance manifest version referenced by session_spec is missing or marked BLOCK in Stage 0.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-031-01: Design and implement training_jobs DB table and ORM model
    - [ ] Add migration to create training_jobs table with columns: job_id (uuid), workspace_id, session_id, status, enqueue_time, start_time, end_time, last_error (text), retry_count (int), idempotency_key, payload_reference, trace_id, worker_id, model_id (nullable), created_at, updated_at
    - [ ] Define ORM model and repository methods: create_job, get_job_by_id, get_jobs_by_session, update_status, append_error, increment_retry
    - [ ] Add DB indices for job_id, idempotency_key, workspace_id, status, created_at to support queries and admin operations
    - [ ] Write unit tests for repository methods including idempotency behaviour and status transitions
    - [ ] Document schema changes in migrations README and update changelog
- [ ] TASK-F-031-02: Implement POST /api/v1/trainings endpoint to validate input and enqueue job
    - [ ] Add request schema validation using existing SessionSpec TypeScript/JSON Schema and return 400 on violations with structured error payloads
    - [ ] Check governance_manifest_version referenced in session_spec via governance service; return 409 Conflict if manifest status BLOCK or missing
    - [ ] Generate job_id, persist training_jobs record with status=QUEUED and idempotency_key if provided
    - [ ] Push job payload to Redis queue with metadata { job_id, workspace_id, session_spec_hash, enqueue_time, retry_policy, trace_id } using Bull/BullMQ client wrapper
    - [ ] Return 202 Accepted JSON: { job_id, status_url: /api/v1/trainings/{job_id}/status, enqueued_at } and log audit event
- [ ] TASK-F-031-03: Implement worker process that consumes Redis queue and runs training orchestrator
    - [ ] Create worker process service that subscribes to workspace training queue with configurable concurrency and backoff
    - [ ] On job claim, set training_jobs.status = RUNNING, set start_time and worker_id and propagate trace_id into subsequent calls
    - [ ] Call existing training orchestrator (Stage 4 orchestration code) with a bounded timeout; stream logs and progress to audit log with trace_id
    - [ ] On success, persist model_card reference and set status = SUCCEEDED, end_time, and store training_duration_seconds; on promotion, write model_registry reference
    - [ ] On failure, increment retry_count, record last_error and either requeue with exponential backoff or move to dead-letter after max_retries; set status = FAILED when exhausted
- [ ] TASK-F-031-04: Add job lifecycle logging, structured events and SSE / event bus integration
    - [ ] Emit structured audit events for job lifecycle transitions: queued, started, progress (percent/message), succeeded, failed, cancelled with trace_id and actor info
    - [ ] Wire events into pipeline audit log and distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry) with span linking to worker and training orchestrator calls
    - [ ] Expose training job events to SSE stream / message broker channel / websocket topic for real-time UI consumption
    - [ ] Implement log retention and privacy guard rails: do not log credentials or raw data; truncate stack traces > 4000 chars
    - [ ] Write unit/integration tests to assert event emission on each lifecycle transition
- [ ] TASK-F-031-05: Implement server-side validation, idempotency and error handling policies
    - [ ] Validate idempotency: if idempotency_key present and active job exists return 200 with job_id and status location header
    - [ ] Implement input payload size limits and reject overly large payloads with 413 Payload Too Large
    - [ ] Sanitize and canonicalize session_spec before hashing for idempotency to avoid false duplicates
    - [ ] Return consistent error codes and structured error objects for all failure modes (validation, governance block, internal error)
    - [ ] Add unit tests covering idempotency, validation failures, governance block responses and payload limits

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for DB repository (create, update, idempotency, indices)
- [ ] Contract tests for POST /api/v1/trainings validating schema enforcement and error payloads
- [ ] Integration test that enqueues a training request and asserts worker consumes it and updates DB to SUCCEEDED (mock training orchestrator)
- [ ] Failure path test: training orchestrator throws; assert retry_count increments and job moves to dead-letter after configured retries
- [ ] Idempotency test: repeat request with same idempotency_key and assert same job_id returned
- [ ] Governance block test: simulate governance manifest BLOCK and assert 409 response and no job created
- [ ] Load test: enqueue 200 concurrent small training jobs and assert queue length grows then drains with worker concurrency and observe latency metrics

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Database migration applied and schema reviewed
- [ ] POST /api/v1/trainings returns 202 with job_id for valid requests and 400/409 for invalid or blocked requests
- [ ] Job created in training_jobs table and enqueued in Redis with matching job_id
- [ ] Worker consumes job, updates training_jobs status transitions and persists model_card on success
- [ ] Idempotency key handling prevents duplicate job creation within configured TTL
- [ ] Automated unit and integration tests covering all happy and error paths are added and pass
- [ ] Trace_id and audit events propagate end-to-end and are visible in tracing backend

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-028
- [ ] governance service API (Stage 0) available for manifest status checks
- [ ] training orchestrator code path (Stage 4) accessible as callable service or library

###### Agent Notes
- Implement a thin job envelope that contains only references to persisted payloads (e.g., payload_reference to Postgres or S3) and small metadata in Redis to keep queue entries lightweight. Propagate existing trace_id into every worker operation and training orchestrator call. Use Bull/BullMQ for Redis queueing; configure backoff policy exponential (base 2) and max_retries = 3 for MVP. Implement dead-letter queue key naming convention queue:training:{workspace_id}:dlq and provide admin API to requeue or inspect items. Ensure all responses conform to existing API error schema and include example curl in controller comments.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-031-02: As an analyst using the web UI, I want to see the status and progress of my submitted training job in the workspace so that I can monitor training without keeping a connection open and know when the artifact is ready or if action is required.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] UI shows job list and single-job detail with statuses: QUEUED, RUNNING (with progress % and current step), SUCCEEDED (with model_card link), FAILED (with last_error) and CANCELLED
- [ ] Polling endpoint GET /api/v1/trainings/{job_id}/status returns canonical job status JSON including progress, current_step, started_at, eta_estimate (if available), and last_error; returns 404 for unknown job_id
- [ ] UI subscribes to SSE or polls every configurable interval (default 3s) and updates the job view in < 3s for status changes in normal operating conditions
- [ ] User can cancel a queued or running job via POST /api/v1/trainings/{job_id}/cancel which sets status=CANCELLED and causes worker to stop within a graceful timeout
- [ ] All UI error states are surfaced with actionable messages and retry options (e.g., requeue, open logs link if authorized)

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-031-06: Implement GET /api/v1/trainings/{job_id}/status and GET /api/v1/trainings?workspace_id=... list endpoint
    - [ ] Return structured JSON with fields: job_id, status, progress (0-100), current_step, enqueue_time, start_time, end_time, last_error, model_id (nullable), links: { model_card, logs, status_stream }
    - [ ] Authorize access: ensure only workspace members with appropriate role can query job status; return 403 for unauthorized requests
    - [ ] Implement pagination and filters for list endpoint (status, session_id, created_after) for admin UX
    - [ ] Add API contract tests to validate shape, 404 and 403 behaviours
    - [ ] Document endpoints in OpenAPI spec with examples
- [ ] TASK-F-031-07: Frontend: Add training job list component and job detail panel in workspace UI
    - [ ] Design and implement list view (thumbnail, name, submitted_by, status badge, last_updated, actions: view/cancel/requeue) using existing UI component library and workspace styles
    - [ ] Implement job detail view showing timeline of lifecycle events, progress bar, current_step messages, and links to model card or logs when available
    - [ ] Connect to status endpoint and SSE stream; fallback to 3s poll if SSE unavailable; debounce updates to avoid UI thrash
    - [ ] Add cancel button with confirmation dialog and optimistic UI update; handle cancellation failures and rollback UI state
    - [ ] Add accessibility attributes, keyboard navigation and aria-labels for progress and action buttons
- [ ] TASK-F-031-08: Implement SSE endpoint and client fallback for real-time updates
    - [ ] Expose /api/v1/trainings/{job_id}/events SSE stream emitting lifecycle events and progress messages with event types: queued, started, progress, succeeded, failed, cancelled
    - [ ] Implement server-side SSE broadcaster that subscribes to audit event bus and filters events by job_id and workspace_id
    - [ ] Implement client SSE handler with automatic reconnect/backoff; if browser blocks SSE, fallback to periodic polling
    - [ ] Add tests for server SSE emission and client-side reconnection behaviour (unit + integration)
    - [ ] Rate-limit SSE connection creation per user to avoid DoS
- [ ] TASK-F-031-09: Implement POST /api/v1/trainings/{job_id}/cancel to permit user-initiated cancellation
    - [ ] Validate caller authorization and job status before accepting cancellation request (only QUEUED or RUNNING allowed)
    - [ ] Persist status = CANCELLED with cancel_requested_by and cancel_requested_at and emit cancellation audit event
    - [ ] Worker polls cancel flag and terminates training gracefully within graceful_shutdown_timeout (e.g., 30s) and marks job accordingly
    - [ ] Return 200 with updated job status; implement idempotency for repeated cancels
    - [ ] Add tests for cancellation flow including race conditions (cancel requested while worker starting)
- [ ] TASK-F-031-10: Add UI tests and e2e flows for training job lifecycle
    - [ ] Write component unit tests for job list and job detail using front-end test harness
    - [ ] Add Cypress e2e tests: submit a training job via API, verify job appears in list, view details, cancel job and assert status changes
    - [ ] Add accessibility tests for new components using axe or equivalent
    - [ ] Add visual regression snapshots for job list and detail panel
    - [ ] Integrate tests into CI pipeline to run on PRs touching UI or API endpoints

###### Test Plan
- [ ] API contract tests for status and list endpoints including auth/403 and 404 behaviour
- [ ] SSE emission integration test: publish lifecycle event and assert SSE clients receive it
- [ ] Frontend unit tests for components, mocking status API and SSE events
- [ ] E2E test: submit job, poll until RUNNING then SUCCEEDED using mock orchestrator or small training job
- [ ] Cancellation race test: issue cancel immediately after enqueue and ensure worker ends job within graceful timeout and job status becomes CANCELLED

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Status endpoints implemented, documented and secured
- [ ] Frontend renders job list and job detail and updates in near-real-time via SSE or polling
- [ ] Cancel endpoint implemented and worker honors cancel requests within configured timeout
- [ ] End-to-end tests cover submit -> queued -> running -> succeeded/failed -> cancelled paths
- [ ] UX shows clear error and retry affordances and is accessible
- [ ] Performance target: UI status changes reflected within 3s P50 after server-side event emission under normal conditions

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] TASK-F-031-01 through TASK-F-031-05 (backend enqueue and worker)
- [ ] OpenAPI spec generator and frontend auth token plumbing

###### Agent Notes
- Ensure UI adapters reuse existing session list and artifact components where possible. Use same trace_id propagation pattern so user can click through from UI to logs/traces in observability backend. SSE channel must enforce workspace-level authorization and rate limits. Provide clear UX for situations where training is pushed to async queue due to training_time > 15 minutes (per PRD) and show 'Training in background' badge in artifact list.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-031-03: As an operations engineer, I want monitoring, alerting and admin controls for the Redis training queue and workers, so that I can detect backlogs, failures and requeue or inspect dead-letter items without manual database surgery.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Prometheus metrics emitted for queue length, job rates (enqueued, dequeued, succeeded, failed), worker concurrency, average job processing time and dead-letter count per workspace
- [ ] Alert rules defined: queue length > 100 for 5 minutes, dead-letter > 0 for 1 minute, worker crash rate > 5% in 10 minutes; alerts fire to ops channel (Slack/email) with actionable runbook link
- [ ] Admin endpoints exist (RBAC-protected) to list dead-letter items, inspect payload_reference and logs, and requeue or purge a dead-letter item; operations actions are audited
- [ ] Infrastructure as code (Terraform) definitions for Redis provisioning (MVP: single Redis cluster with AUTH and TLS) and worker autoscaling (K8s Deployment/HPA or service autoscaling) are added to repo
- [ ] Workers expose healthcheck endpoint and participate in service discovery; deployments restart with config changes without losing queued jobs

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-031-11: Instrument queue and worker with Prometheus metrics and expose health endpoints
    - [ ] Expose metrics: training_queue_length{workspace}, training_jobs_enqueued_total, training_jobs_processed_total{status}, training_job_processing_seconds_bucket, training_jobs_deadletter_total
    - [ ] Implement /health and /ready endpoints for worker with checks: Redis connectivity, DB connectivity, orchestrator reachable
    - [ ] Add metrics exporter and configure default scrape path /metrics
    - [ ] Write unit tests asserting metric increments on enqueue/complete/fail and integration test for /metrics exposure
    - [ ] Document required scrape jobs and relabel_config for Prometheus
- [ ] TASK-F-031-12: Add admin API for dead-letter inspection and requeueing
    - [ ] Implement RBAC-protected endpoints: GET /admin/queues/{workspace}/dlq, GET /admin/queues/{workspace}/dlq/{item_id}, POST /admin/queues/{workspace}/dlq/{item_id}/requeue, DELETE /admin/queues/{workspace}/dlq/{item_id}
    - [ ] Validate operations: requeue restores job record status to QUEUED and removes item from dlq, purging archives metadata and writes admin audit event
    - [ ] Ensure admin endpoints redact sensitive payload fields and enforce encryption at rest for payload_reference
    - [ ] Add integration tests for admin flows and ensure actions are logged to audit table
    - [ ] Add UI hook in workspace admin console to surface dead-letter items if feasible
- [ ] TASK-F-031-13: Provision Redis and worker deployment IaC and deployment changes
    - [ ] Add Terraform module for Redis: managed service (e.g., AWS ElastiCache or Redis Cloud) with encryption (TLS) and AUTH and network restrictions scoped to cluster VPC
    - [ ] Add K8s manifests/Helm chart changes for worker Deployment with resource limits, liveness/readiness probes and HPA settings; include configmap/secrets for Redis connection
    - [ ] Create CI/CD pipeline step for deploying worker images and performing rolling updates with zero job loss (use worker draining and graceful shutdown)
    - [ ] Add runbook documenting Redis config, scaling guidance and cost considerations for MVP
    - [ ] Add smoke test to CD pipeline that enqueues a test job and asserts worker processes it after deployment
- [ ] TASK-F-031-14: Implement alerting rules and dashboards
    - [ ] Create Grafana dashboard panels for queue length per workspace, worker throughput, average processing_time and dead-letter trends
    - [ ] Create Prometheus alert rules with thresholds from acceptance_criteria and link to runbook and playbook steps
    - [ ] Configure Slack/email alert receiver and test alert firing in staging
    - [ ] Add dashboard for recent failed job details with direct links to job_id and traces
    - [ ] Document MTTI/MTTR goals and escalation policy in runbook
- [ ] TASK-F-031-15: Security review and RBAC for admin operations and secrets
    - [ ] Ensure Redis credentials are stored in secrets manager (Vault or AWS Secrets Manager) and not checked into repo; update deployment to read secrets from manager
    - [ ] Add RBAC checks for admin endpoints tied to workspace admin role and audit access attempts
    - [ ] Pen-test admin API surface for injection, unauthorized access and mass requeue exploits; remediate issues
    - [ ] Add monitoring for abnormal admin actions (e.g., mass requeues) and alert on suspicious patterns
    - [ ] Add unit and integration tests to verify admin endpoints enforce RBAC and redact PII in responses

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Integration test asserting metrics increment on enqueue and processed events
- [ ] End-to-end alert test: simulate backlog by enqueuing > 120 jobs and assert alert fires in test Slack channel
- [ ] Admin API tests: list dlq, inspect item, requeue item and assert job processes successfully
- [ ] Deployment smoke test: deploy worker, enqueue test job, verify processing and no job loss during rolling restart
- [ ] Security tests: verify secrets are not present in logs and admin endpoints reject unauthorized callers

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Prometheus metrics exported and scraped successfully in staging
- [ ] Alerts configured and tested end-to-end to the ops channel with runbook links
- [ ] IaC for Redis and worker deployment merged and applied in staging environment
- [ ] Admin APIs for dlq inspection and requeue exist and are RBAC-protected and audited
- [ ] Worker health endpoints pass readiness probes and rolling updates do not lose queued jobs
- [ ] Security review completed and secrets management enforced

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] Redis provider account and credentials
- [ ] Observability stack (Prometheus + Grafana + alerting receiver) available in environment
- [ ] Existing RBAC user management service for authorizing admin endpoints

###### Agent Notes
- For MVP keep one Redis instance per environment but partition queues by workspace key. Use Redis ACLs and TLS. Use a small Bull/BullMQ wrapper library that emits Prometheus metrics via a shared metrics collector. Keep dead-letter items stored both in Redis DLQ and in training_jobs table with last_error for persistence. Administrative requeue must rehydrate payload_reference from persisted storage so the requeued Redis item is lightweight. Consider future migration to a more durable job system (e.g., Kubernetes Jobs or managed queue) if scale requirements grow.

#### Requirement / Feature: Model trial leaderboard logging (basic trials log table)

**Feature ID:** `F-032`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, data`

**Rationale:** Leaderboards are surfaced in artifacts; log must exist even if minimal in MVP.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-028
- [ ] F-030

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-032-01: - [ ] As a data engineer, I want a production-ready leaderboard table in the gold/model registry schema to record every model trial run (minimal MVP fields), so that model trials are auditable and accessible for artifact surface and API consumption.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] A SQL schema/migration is checked into the repo that creates a leaderboard table with fields: trial_id (uuid PK), model_id (fk), algorithm, hyperparameters (JSON), validation_mape (float), test_mape (float), training_duration_seconds (int), walk_forward_mapes (array/JSON), promoted (bool), trade_off_summary (string), created_at (timestamp), updated_at (timestamp).
- [ ] - [ ] Table created in the CI-managed dev warehouse via migration job and verified by integration test.
- [ ] - [ ] Table enforces non-null constraints where appropriate and has an index on model_id + created_at for pagination and retrieval performance.
- [ ] - [ ] Table schema includes column-level comments/docstrings and is documented in schema registry.
- [ ] - [ ] Migration has idempotent rollback and migration tests pass.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-032-01: - [ ] Define and approve the leaderboard table schema and retention policy
    - [ ] - [ ] Draft DDL for leaderboard table with types matching target warehouses (Snowflake VARIANT/ARRAY or JSON column fallback)
    - [ ] - [ ] Define NOT NULL / NULL constraints and PK/unique constraints (trial_id PK)
    - [ ] - [ ] Propose retention and archival policy (default 2 years) and soft-delete flag approach
    - [ ] - [ ] Get schema sign-off from data engineering and model registry owners (link to F-028/F-030 owners)
- [ ] TASK-F-032-02: - [ ] Implement database migration files and CI migration job
    - [ ] - [ ] Create migration SQL file(s) for target dev warehouse (Snowflake) and fallback for other dialects
    - [ ] - [ ] Add migration to migrations registry (Flyway/liquibase/house-built) with version tag F032
    - [ ] - [ ] Add rollback migration file and test rollback locally
    - [ ] - [ ] Wire CI job to run migration in ephemeral test schema and validate table exists
- [ ] TASK-F-032-03: - [ ] Add indexes, constraints, and comments for production-readiness
    - [ ] - [ ] Add composite index on (model_id, created_at DESC) and index on promoted flag
    - [ ] - [ ] Add foreign key constraint referencing model registry table (nullable if model registry not yet created) and fallback soft FK handling
    - [ ] - [ ] Add column-level comments for maintainability
    - [ ] - [ ] Add analytic view (optional) to map VARIANT/JSON fields to columns for reporting
- [ ] TASK-F-032-04: - [ ] Security & access controls for leaderboard table
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure database role reviewers specify read/write roles for pipeline service accounts and workspace members
    - [ ] - [ ] Add RBAC test verifying that only training service principal can INSERT and only analytics role can SELECT
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure PII policy scan runs against table DDL and passes (no PII fields included)
    - [ ] - [ ] Document required grants in infra-as-code (Terraform) for onboarding
- [ ] TASK-F-032-05: - [ ] Documentation and schema registry registration
    - [ ] - [ ] Add table schema entry to workspace schema registry with JSON example record
    - [ ] - [ ] Update ModelCard schema docs to reference leaderboard table location and fields
    - [ ] - [ ] Add short runbook for debugging missing or failed writes

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit test: validate generated DDL matches expected schema types for Snowflake dialect
- [ ] - [ ] Integration test: run migration against ephemeral schema and assert table + indexes + constraints exist
- [ ] - [ ] RBAC test: verify service account grants and denied principals
- [ ] - [ ] Schema compatibility test: insert a sample row and read it back, assert JSON fields parse correctly

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Migration merged and applied to dev environment via CI
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests for migration succeed in CI
- [ ] - [ ] Schema docs and runbook merged in docs repo
- [ ] - [ ] Security/RBAC approvals recorded
- [ ] - [ ] Retention policy and archival process documented

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] F-028
- [ ] - [ ] F-030

###### Agent Notes
- - [ ] Migration must use the project's agreed migration framework; prefer VARIANT for hyperparameters and walk_forward_mapes to preserve array structure. Coordinate with F-028/F-030 to confirm model_id FK semantics. Keep schema intentionally minimal (no per-row SHAP) to avoid blowup in MVP.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-032-02: - [ ] As a backend engineer for the training orchestrator, I want each training trial to reliably write a validated leaderboard row at trial completion (or failure with metadata), so that trial history is preserved for audit and artifact surface even at MVP.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Training orchestrator writes one leaderboard row per trial attempt with all required fields populated or with a defined failure record (trial_status = 'FAILED', error_message).
- [ ] - [ ] Writes are idempotent for retried trials (trial_id deterministic or deduplicated) to avoid duplicates.
- [ ] - [ ] Write operation is transactional or retriable with exponential backoff and logs structured events (trace_id, trial_id, model_id) to observability backend.
- [ ] - [ ] Validation ensures hyperparameters JSON conforms to allowed shape and mapes array length equals configured walk-forward windows (default 5); invalid records are rejected and surfaced to pipeline audit log.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-032-06: - [ ] Instrument training orchestrator to produce leaderboard payload at trial end
    - [ ] - [ ] Define in-code payload schema matching DB columns and ModelCard contract
    - [ ] - [ ] Add code path to generate payload on successful trial completion
    - [ ] - [ ] Add code path to generate failure payload capturing error_message, stacktrace summary, and duration
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure payload attaches trace_id and session_id for auditability
- [ ] TASK-F-032-07: - [ ] Implement reliable writer service to persist leaderboard rows
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement DB client integration with connection pooling and dialect-aware insert logic
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement idempotency: upsert by trial_id or use unique constraint handling
    - [ ] - [ ] Add retry logic with exponential backoff for transient DB errors and circuit-breaker for persistent failures
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit structured logs to OpenTelemetry/Honeycomb on success/failure with tags {feature: F-032, trace_id, trial_id, model_id}
- [ ] TASK-F-032-08: - [ ] Add validation and schema enforcement for payload
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement runtime validators: hyperparameters must be JSON object, walk_forward_mapes length == configured N (default 5), numeric fields within sensible ranges
    - [ ] - [ ] On validation failure, produce structured error and write a failure row with trial_status='VALIDATION_FAILED' and validation_error details
    - [ ] - [ ] Unit-test validators with edge cases (null hyperparams, negative durations, missing mapes)
    - [ ] - [ ] Log validation failure events to pipeline audit log
- [ ] TASK-F-032-09: - [ ] Add observability and metrics around leaderboard writes
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit metric: leaderboard_write_total{status=success|failure}
    - [ ] - [ ] Add histogram for write latency and retry counts
    - [ ] - [ ] Add alert rule for sustained write failures (>5 failures in 10 minutes)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add dashboards tile showing recent writes by model_id and failure rates
- [ ] TASK-F-032-10: - [ ] Testing: unit + integration for writer integration
    - [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for payload generation and idempotency behavior
    - [ ] - [ ] Integration test that runs a simulated trial and asserts row present in test DB
    - [ ] - [ ] Fault-injection test simulating DB transient errors and ensuring retries succeed
    - [ ] - [ ] End-to-end test that runs a small training job (mocked model) and asserts leaderboard row created

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for payload generator and validators (fast)
- [ ] - [ ] Integration test with ephemeral DB using migration from US-F-032-01
- [ ] - [ ] Fault-tolerance test: simulate transient DB errors and assert exponential backoff succeeds
- [ ] - [ ] Idempotency test: re-run same trial_id and assert only one row exists
- [ ] - [ ] Schema validation test: attempt to write invalid payload and assert failure row and audit log entry

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Orchestrator code merged with feature flag off-by-default
- [ ] - [ ] All unit and integration tests pass in CI
- [ ] - [ ] Observability metrics and alerting in place
- [ ] - [ ] Runbook for troubleshooting write failures added
- [ ] - [ ] Demo: run a training job in dev and show leaderboard row created

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] US-F-032-01
- [ ] - [ ] F-030

###### Agent Notes
- - [ ] Training orchestrator must call a single writer API (internal library) after each trial. Use trial_id generated before training begins so retries reuse same id. Keep payload minimal to avoid large object writes; store heavy objects (full hyperparameter search history) in model registry reference only. Coordinate trace_id propagation to link logs across agents for F-030.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-032-03: - [ ] As an API and frontend engineer, I want a paginated, authenticated API endpoint and a simple artifact leaderboard panel that reads the leaderboard table, so that analysts can inspect trial history from the model registry and artifact (MVP read-only surface).

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Implement GET /api/v1/models/{model_id}/leaderboard that returns paginated JSON of leaderboard rows ordered by created_at desc with page,size parameters and total_count.
- [ ] - [ ] Endpoint enforces workspace-level RBAC: only workspace members with view permission on the model can access; unauthorized returns 403.
- [ ] - [ ] Endpoint validates model_id exists and returns 404 if not found. Sanitizes inputs to prevent SQL injection.
- [ ] - [ ] Frontend artifact panel fetches the endpoint with embed token or user session, displays a simple table with columns: algorithm, validation_mape, test_mape, training_duration_seconds, promoted, trade_off_summary, and supports pagination and retry on error.
- [ ] - [ ] Endpoint and frontend handle empty states gracefully (no trials yet) and display an explanatory message.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-032-11: - [ ] Design API contract and add to OpenAPI spec
    - [ ] - [ ] Define request query params: page (int), size (int, capped), sort (optional)
    - [ ] - [ ] Define response shape: items[], total_count, page, size, links (next/prev)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add example responses for success, empty, 404, 403, 500
    - [ ] - [ ] Add security requirements: bearer auth + workspace scoping
- [ ] TASK-F-032-12: - [ ] Implement backend endpoint handler with pagination, RBAC and input validation
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement service layer function to query leaderboard table with parameterized queries to prevent injection
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement workspace membership + model access check (reuse model registry ACL code from F-028 if available)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement pagination and cap page size to prevent large scans
    - [ ] - [ ] Add structured logging and trace_id propagation on each request
- [ ] TASK-F-032-13: - [ ] Add automated tests for API (unit + integration)
    - [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for service layer query logic and input validation
    - [ ] - [ ] Integration tests hitting endpoint against test DB with sample leaderboard rows
    - [ ] - [ ] Security tests verifying 403/404 cases and unauthorized access attempts
    - [ ] - [ ] Performance test ensuring page fetch latency under acceptable threshold for typical page sizes
- [ ] TASK-F-032-14: - [ ] Implement frontend artifact leaderboard panel (MVP read-only)
    - [ ] - [ ] Create a minimal panel component that calls /api/v1/models/{model_id}/leaderboard and renders table rows
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement pagination controls and loading/error/empty states per artifact UI rules (retry button etc.)
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure accessibility: aria-labels, keyboard navigable table, and responsive layout
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit/storybook story and small visual test to ensure rendering
- [ ] TASK-F-032-15: - [ ] Security & DevOps wiring for API and frontend
    - [ ] - [ ] Add API route to gateway config and ensure authentication middleware applied
    - [ ] - [ ] Add CORS and embed_token handling for artifact embedded contexts
    - [ ] - [ ] Add endpoint to API monitoring with uptime check and latency SLO
    - [ ] - [ ] Add feature-flag rollout for the UI panel (default enabled in dev)
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure logs do not contain hyperparameters or secret material

###### Test Plan

- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests verifying pagination, ordering, and empty-state
- [ ] - [ ] Security tests for 403/404/401 and SQL injection attempts
- [ ] - [ ] Frontend component unit tests and end-to-end test that loads panel and validates table rows and pagination
- [ ] - [ ] Accessibility checks (axe) on the panel component

- [ ] - [ ] API integration tests pass in CI against ephemeral DB
- [ ] - [ ] Frontend panel merged with visual/tests and feature-flag toggles

- [ ] - [ ] US-F-032-02

Use existing artifact fetch/auth patterns (embed_token in query param) and reuse model registry ACL checks from F-028.
