## Sprint 8 - Promotion & Registry

**Sprint Goal:** Implement promotion checks, repair loop skeleton, and a simple model registry for MVP promotions.

### Sprint Completion Checklist
- [ ] All P0 stories complete
- [ ] All blocking defects resolved
- [ ] Tests passing
- [ ] Demo-ready increment produced
- [ ] Milestone accepted

### Epic: Training

#### Requirement / Feature: Promotion & repair-loop basic (max 3 repair cycles framework)

**Feature ID:** `F-033`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, ml`

**Rationale:** Automated repair attempts are required before surfacing failures to user.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-029
- [ ] F-030

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-033-01: As a Training Orchestrator, I want an automated repair loop that attempts up to 3 targeted repairs when promotion criteria fail, so that the system can fix common issues automatically and only escalate to a human when necessary.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] When promotion criteria all pass, the model is promoted to the model registry and promotion_status in the model_card is set to 'PASS'.
- [ ] When any promotion criterion fails, the repair loop runs a targeted repair action and retries promotion automatically, incrementing repair_cycles on the model_card each attempt.
- [ ] The repair loop stops after 3 repair cycles and emits a human_required pipeline event if promotion still fails.
- [ ] Each repair cycle is deterministic and idempotent for the same input state and logs the attempted action, reason, and outcome to the pipeline audit log.
- [ ] Repair actions are limited to the predefined targeted repairs (expand HPO, increase regularization, reduce complexity, feature selection, switch to faster algorithm) and do not regenerate the feature manifest or new gold table content in MVP.
- [ ] All repair attempts preserve privacy rules: no raw rows are sent to LLMs; only statistics and manifest metadata are used for any assistant-driven decision.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-033-01: Design repair-loop orchestrator spec and state machine
    - [ ] Draft state machine diagram for: initial_check -> repair_attempt_{1..3} -> promotion -> escalate_to_user
    - [ ] Define inputs, outputs, and side-effects for each state (including model_card mutations and audit log events)
    - [ ] Map each promotion failure reason to one or more targeted repair actions using table Failure -> Action from PRD (Validation MAPE, Overfit, Feature importance, Worst window, Training time)
    - [ ] Define API contract for invoking repair actions and for reporting status (SSE pipeline events: dq_gate, agent_start, agent_complete, human_required, error)
    - [ ] Specify retry/backoff policy and idempotency requirements for repair actions
    - [ ] Review spec with ML, Backend, and QA for signoff
- [ ] TASK-F-033-02: Implement repair-loop orchestrator backend service
    - [ ] Create new service module training/repair_loop with state machine implementation and public orchestration function run_repair_loop(model_id)
    - [ ] Implement max-cycle enforcement to limit attempts to 3 and persist repair_cycles to model_card
    - [ ] Hook orchestrator into existing training orchestrator completion path so repair loop triggers automatically on promotion failures
    - [ ] Emit SSE pipeline events at each lifecycle step with structured payloads and trace_id
    - [ ] Add input validation, error handling, and ensure sensitive data is never logged
- [ ] TASK-F-033-03: Implement concrete targeted repair actions (MVP subset)
    - [ ] Implement HPO expansion action: double n_trials and re-run pruning-aware search with same feature set
    - [ ] Implement regularization action: increase reg_lambda/add L2 and reduce max_depth by 1 for tree-based models and equivalent for LightGBM
    - [ ] Implement feature-selection action: run recursive feature elimination to top-30 features and retrain
    - [ ] Implement algorithm-switch action: switch from XGBoost to LightGBM when training time or resource issues detected
    - [ ] Implement lightweight window-specific calendar feature injection for worst-window failures (add binary indicator for dates in the problematic window)
    - [ ] Ensure each repair action returns standardized result object { success: bool, reason: string, metrics_delta: {...}, new_model_card_snapshot }
- [ ] TASK-F-033-04: Persist repair metadata and audit logging
    - [ ] Extend ModelCard schema to record repair_cycles and repair_history array with timestamped entries
    - [ ] Write audit events to pipeline audit log for: repair_attempt_started, repair_attempt_completed, repair_action_applied, promotion_attempt, promotion_result
    - [ ] Attach trace_id to every audit and LLM call for distributed tracing
    - [ ] Ensure audit events include only metadata, hyperparameters, metric summaries and not raw rows
    - [ ] Implement DB migration for model_card changes and migration tests
- [ ] TASK-F-033-05: Add observability, metrics and alerts for repair loop
    - [ ] Emit Prometheus metrics: repair_loop_attempts_total, repair_loop_success_total, repair_loop_failures_total, repair_loop_duration_seconds
    - [ ] Log structured events for each repair attempt with severity levels
    - [ ] Create Grafana dashboard panel for repair loop health and latent failures
    - [ ] Create alert rule for >5 failed promotions in 1 hour for a workspace to notify ML owners
    - [ ] Document metrics and alerts in runbook
- [ ] TASK-F-033-06: Add unit & integration tests for repair loop logic
    - [ ] Write unit tests for state machine transitions including edge cases (immediate pass, pass after n attempts, fail after max attempts)
    - [ ] Mock targeted repair actions to verify orchestrator calls correct actions based on failure reason
    - [ ] Add integration test that runs a full training + repair loop using small synthetic dataset to simulate each failure mode
    - [ ] Add tests verifying audit log entries and model_card.repair_cycles increments
    - [ ] Add tests ensuring no raw data is sent to any LLM or external log

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Run unit tests for state machine and repair action modules (CI)
- [ ] Execute integration test: simulate Validation MAPE > threshold, verify HPO expansion retrain and promotion retry succeed when possible
- [ ] Execute integration test: simulate Overfit (test >> validation) and verify regularization action is applied and retrain occurs
- [ ] Execute failure-path test: ensure after 3 unsuccessful repair cycles SSE event human_required is emitted and promotion_status remains 'FAIL'
- [ ] Security test: verify no sensitive column values are emitted in logs or agent prompts
- [ ] Load test: simulate 50 concurrent repair loops to validate queuing and job isolation
- [ ] Manual QA: review audit logs, repair_history content, trace_id propagation and review runbook steps

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Repair-loop spec signed off by ML and Backend leads
- [ ] Orchestrator service implemented, tested, and deployed to staging behind feature flag
- [ ] Three targeted repair actions implemented and callable from orchestrator
- [ ] Repair loop persists repair_cycles and repair_history to model_card and writes audit events
- [ ] Unit and integration tests pass in CI with coverage >= 80% for changed modules
- [ ] Monitoring dashboards and alert rules created and runbook documented
- [ ] End-to-end demo showing a failed promotion repaired automatically at least once in staging

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-029
- [ ] F-030

###### Agent Notes
- Repair-loop orchestrator should treat LLM/agent usage as a tool: only provide statistical summaries, model_card metadata, and feature manifest versions to any assistant. Repair actions must be deterministic and idempotent; avoid regenerating gold tables in MVP. Use the PRD's Failure -> Targeted Repair mapping as canonical mapping. Emit structured SSE events for UI to display progress. Persist repair_cycles to model_card and include repair details in model registry artifact.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-033-02: As a Backend Engineer, I want reliable promotion checks and a thin model registry integration that records promotions and stores model metadata, so that promoted models are discoverable and auditable for MVP promotions.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Promotion checks run deterministically using the promotion criteria defined in the PRD (validation_mape < 15%, worst_window < 25%, test_mape <= validation_mape * 1.2, feature importance concentration, training_time < 15 minutes, minimum test set size >= 100).
- [ ] A promotion API endpoint exists that will promote a model to the registry only when all criteria pass and will return structured reasons when it fails.
- [ ] On promotion, a ModelCard is written to the model registry store (MLflow-compatible artifact store stub) with model binary URI, onnx_uri (if produced), feature_manifest_version, backtest_results, promotion_status='PASS', repair_cycles=0.
- [ ] Promotion decisions and the full model_card are stored in the pipeline audit log with actor and timestamp.
- [ ] Registry write is atomic: model binary + model_card metadata either both persist or none persist; rollback and error handling implemented.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-033-07: Implement promotion-check service and API endpoint
    - [ ] Create POST /api/v1/models/{model_id}/promote endpoint with authentication and RBAC check (workspace role required)
    - [ ] Implement server-side validation of promotion criteria using model_card fields and backtest results
    - [ ] Return structured JSON on success { promoted: true, model_id, registry_uri } and on failure { promoted: false, failures: [ { criterion, observed, threshold, message } ] }
    - [ ] Add input validation, rate limiting, and detailed error messages for invalid model_id or missing artifacts
    - [ ] Add logging for each promotion attempt including trace_id and actor_id
- [ ] TASK-F-033-08: Integrate thin model registry write path (MVP)
    - [ ] Implement registry adapter that writes model metadata to the MLflow-compatible store: model_card JSON + artifact pointers (pickle/ONNX URI) and leaderboards CSV
    - [ ] Implement atomic write pattern: write artifacts to object storage, then insert model_card into Postgres within a transaction; roll back on failure
    - [ ] Add function to set promotion_status and persist repair_cycles and trained_at
    - [ ] Add basic registry index endpoint GET /api/v1/models/{model_id} that returns model_card and registry_uri
    - [ ] Add unit tests for registry adapter and transaction rollback behavior
- [ ] TASK-F-033-09: Add security and access control to promotion flow
    - [ ] Enforce ABAC: only workspace members with appropriate role can promote models; deny cross-workspace promotions
    - [ ] Ensure embed/registry URIs include workspace-scoped access tokens and are not world-readable by default
    - [ ] Ensure credentials and secrets used for object storage and registry are retrieved from secrets manager and never logged
    - [ ] Add tests to confirm unauthorized users receive 403 on promotion endpoint
    - [ ] Document RBAC rules and update API spec
- [ ] TASK-F-033-10: Add deterministic validation & unit tests for promotion criteria
    - [ ] Implement unit validators for each criterion with clear numeric comparisons and boundary handling (e.g., floating point tolerances)
    - [ ] Add unit tests for edge cases: exactly on threshold, missing metrics, empty backtest windows
    - [ ] Add tests to ensure worst-window criterion uses max() across windows and compares to 1.5× mean where required
    - [ ] Add tests that verify feature importance concentration calculation (top-10 >= 70%)
    - [ ] Add CI checks to run these tests on every PR
- [ ] TASK-F-033-11: Add integration tests and staging deployment
    - [ ] Write integration test scenario where model meets all criteria and promotion API returns success and registry contains model_card
    - [ ] Write integration test scenario where promotion fails and response lists failures and does not write to registry
    - [ ] Deploy promotion-check service to staging behind feature flag and run end-to-end promotion flows
    - [ ] Verify atomicity by simulating storage failure during artifact write and confirming no partial registry state
    - [ ] Document staging runbook for manual promotion testing

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit test validators for each promotion criterion including boundary cases
- [ ] Integration test: train a small model in staging that meets criteria and verify promotion and registry entries
- [ ] Integration test: produce a model with validation MAPE > 15% and verify failure reasons returned and no registry write
- [ ] Chaos test: simulate object storage network failure mid-write and verify rollback and consistent state
- [ ] Security test: attempt promotion with unauthorized user and verify 403
- [ ] Manual QA: run through UI promotion flow to confirm SSE events and UI messages match backend responses

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Promotion endpoint implemented with RBAC and input validation
- [ ] All promotion criteria implemented with deterministic validators and unit test coverage
- [ ] Thin model registry writes model_card and artifacts atomically and is reachable via GET endpoint
- [ ] Integration tests demonstrating both success and failure promotion paths pass in CI
- [ ] Promotion flow emits audit events with actor_id, trace_id and stored in pipeline audit log
- [ ] Feature flag gating the registry integration enabled for staged rollout

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-029
- [ ] F-030

###### Agent Notes
- Promotion-check service must use existing deterministic validators from PRD. Do not call any LLMs in this flow. Use model_card and backtest arrays as canonical inputs. Keep model registry integration thin for MVP (store model_card JSON and artifact URIs); full MLflow server can be integrated later. Ensure all writes are transactional and revert on partial failures.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-033-03: As a QA Lead / Analyst, I want transparent logging and pipeline events for repair cycles and promotion decisions surfaced via SSE and stored in the audit log, so that operators and users can understand why a promotion failed and what repairs were attempted.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every promotion attempt and repair cycle emits SSE events with structured payloads consumable by the UI: agent_start, agent_complete, dq_gate, human_required, error, and agent_log entries.
- [ ] Audit log contains a complete, queryable trail for each model: promotion attempts, repair actions applied, metrics before and after each attempt, actor (automated or user), and trace_id.
- [ ] UI can show a chronological list of repair attempts for a given model using the audit entries and model_card.repair_history.
- [ ] Sensitive information (raw rows, PII) is never present in SSE payloads or audit logs; only metrics, hyperparameters, and feature manifest metadata are allowed.
- [ ] Tests verify that when a promotion fails, the UI-relevant SSE stream includes at least: failure_reasons array, attempted_repair_action name, repair_result metrics_delta, and final escalation flag when applicable.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-033-12: Define SSE event schema and log formats for repair/promotion lifecycle
    - [ ] Define JSON schema for pipeline SSE events used by UI panels for: agent_start, agent_complete, dq_gate, repair_attempt, promotion_attempt, human_required, error
    - [ ] Define audit log entry schema with fields: event_type, model_id, workspace_id, actor_id, timestamp, trace_id, payload_summary
    - [ ] Publish schema to API spec repository and add JSON Schema validators to backend
    - [ ] Add example payloads for UI integration testing
    - [ ] Review schemas with frontend and security teams to ensure no sensitive fields included
- [ ] TASK-F-033-13: Implement SSE emission and audit-log writes in orchestrator and promotion flows
    - [ ] Add SSE emission hooks in repair-loop orchestrator at start and end of each repair attempt and on promotion attempt results
    - [ ] Write audit-log entries for every emitted SSE event and persist to pipeline audit store with append-only semantics
    - [ ] Ensure trace_id is generated at pipeline start and propagated to all subsequent events and logs
    - [ ] Implement structured logging in backend (JSON) with log level mapping and error handling
    - [ ] Add tests to verify SSE payload schemas and audit log entries for sample flows
- [ ] TASK-F-033-14: Frontend: surface repair-cycle timeline for model card and pipeline UI (MVP read-only)
    - [ ] Add a read-only 'Repair History' accordion to model details UI that consumes GET /api/v1/models/{model_id}/repair_history or audit API
    - [ ] Render chronological list of repair attempts with timestamp, action name, summary metrics_delta and outcome icon
    - [ ] Implement link to pipeline trace view using trace_id to open full SSE event log
    - [ ] Handle error states and rate-limit responses gracefully with retry and user messaging
    - [ ] Add unit tests for UI components and end-to-end test to verify timeline shows after a simulated repair run
- [ ] TASK-F-033-15: Add QA tests and data privacy checks for logs and events
    - [ ] Create automated QA test that scans emitted SSE and audit entries for any field matching PII patterns and fails if found
    - [ ] Add tests to confirm large payloads are truncated to summary fields per schema (no raw arrays of rows)
    - [ ] Add integration test that verifies SSE events are emitted in expected order for a failing promotion with 2 repair attempts then escalation
    - [ ] Perform manual security review of sample logs and SSE payloads before staging rollout
    - [ ] Add CI job to run log privacy checks on test outputs
- [ ] TASK-F-033-16: Document operator runbook for interpreting repair loop events and manually intervening
    - [ ] Write step-by-step runbook: how to read repair_history entries, how to view trace in tracing tool, and how to escalate to Model Owner
    - [ ] Include troubleshooting checklist for common failures and recommended manual actions
    - [ ] Publish runbook to internal runbook repo and link from staging monitoring dashboard
    - [ ] Train one on-call engineer and record a short demo walkthrough
    - [ ] Add runbook link to monitoring alert messages

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for SSE schema validator and audit log writer
- [ ] Integration tests to simulate promotion failure then 1-3 repair attempts and verify SSE event ordering and audit persistence
- [ ] Privacy tests to scan emitted events for PII regex matches
- [ ] End-to-end UI test: simulate repair loop in staging and assert frontend repair history shows accurate entries
- [ ] Operational test: fire alert and follow runbook to retrieve trace_id and inspect logs
- [ ] Performance test: ensure SSE stream remains responsive under 50 concurrent pipelines

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] SSE event schema published and validated in backend
- [ ] Orchestrator emits SSE events at each repair and promotion lifecycle step and audit log entries persisted
- [ ] Frontend shows repair history for model details page and links to trace view
- [ ] Automated tests validate privacy of logs and correct SSE emission order
- [ ] Runbook published and on-call trained
- [ ] Manual sign-off from QA that UI and logs provide sufficient traceability for promotion decisions

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-029
- [ ] F-030

###### Agent Notes
- Emit minimal, structured SSE payloads that the UI can render; avoid any free-form text from LLMs in these events. Use trace_id consistently. When summarizing model metrics in events, include only numeric summaries (validation_mape, test_mape, training_time_seconds, top_features list limited to 10 names). Ensure audit log entries are append-only and redact any inadvertent PII before persist.

#### Requirement / Feature: Training result API (status, metrics, links to model_card)

**Feature ID:** `F-034`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, api`

**Rationale:** UI needs programmatic access to training results and status.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-032
- [ ] F-030

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-034-01: As a frontend developer, I want a concise training-job status API per model_id, so that the UI can show real-time training/progression state, progress %, current stage, and error/failure details to users and support live streaming UI updates.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GET /api/v1/models/{model_id}/status returns 200 with JSON: { model_id, status: [queued|running|succeeded|failed|cancelled], progress_percent (0-100), current_stage (string), started_at (ISO8601), last_updated_at (ISO8601), trace_id, error_code (nullable), error_message (nullable) } for existing model jobs.
- [ ] Status endpoint returns 404 for unknown model_id and 401/403 for unauthorized requests according to ABAC workspace scoping.
- [ ] Status values strictly conform to the enum and progress_percent is an integer 0-100.
- [ ] Backend persists and updates status events atomically and emits a pipeline audit log event with trace_id each status change.
- [ ] API includes ETag and Last-Modified headers for caching and supports conditional GET (304).
- [ ] Status updates from the training orchestrator (F-032) are consumed and reflected within 5 seconds for synchronous runs; async training writes are eventually consistent (P95 < 30s).
- [ ] All requests and responses are validated against JSON Schema; any schema violation returns 400 with a clear error payload.
- [ ] All errors logged with structured tracing (trace_id) and rate-limited alerts emitted for repeated failures (>5/min).

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-034-01: Design and document status API contract and JSON Schema
    - [ ] Draft OpenAPI spec for GET /api/v1/models/{model_id}/status including request/response examples and error responses
    - [ ] Create JSON Schema for response payload and common error payloads
    - [ ] Review contract with frontend, training orchestrator (F-032) owners, and security for ABAC requirements
    - [ ] Add API contract to internal API docs and update change log
- [ ] TASK-F-034-02: Implement backend endpoint and service layer for status retrieval
    - [ ] Add route and controller for GET /api/v1/models/{model_id}/status in backend service
    - [ ] Implement service that reads status from training_jobs table and returns normalized view
    - [ ] Add RBAC/ABAC check to ensure workspace-scoped access
    - [ ] Add response caching headers (ETag, Last-Modified) and conditional GET support
- [ ] TASK-F-034-03: Persist training status updates from the training orchestrator into DB
    - [ ] Define training_jobs table schema / migration: fields { model_id PK, session_id, workspace_id, status, progress_percent, current_stage, started_at, last_updated_at, trace_id, error_code, error_message, raw_payload JSON }
    - [ ] Implement idempotent upsert API for orchestrator to call when stage changes
    - [ ] Wire training orchestrator (F-032) to call upsert endpoint or write into queue/topic on each stage transition
    - [ ] Add DB constraints and indexes: workspace_id index, last_updated_at index
- [ ] TASK-F-034-04: Add logging, tracing, and monitoring for status updates
    - [ ] Emit structured audit log event on every status change including trace_id and input_hash/output_hash
    - [ ] Integrate with distributed tracing (propagate trace_id from orchestrator to API responses)
    - [ ] Create Prometheus metrics: training_status_changes_total, training_status_errors_total, training_status_update_latency_seconds
    - [ ] Add alerting rule for repeated status write failures (>5/min) and endpoint 5xx rate
- [ ] TASK-F-034-05: Write automated tests and CI checks for status endpoint
    - [ ] Unit tests for service layer: mapping DB rows to API payloads and schema validation
    - [ ] Integration tests with in-memory DB for upsert + retrieval flows including RBAC checks
    - [ ] Contract test validating API output against agreed OpenAPI/JSON Schema
    - [ ] Add CI step to run these tests on PRs
- [ ] TASK-F-034-06: Frontend integration and UI component work
    - [ ] Implement a small status component that polls GET /status with exponential backoff and updates UI
    - [ ] Hook into SSE/streaming pipeline if available to support real-time events (optional enhancement)
    - [ ] Display progress bar, human-readable current_stage, and error card with retry link when status == failed
    - [ ] Add e2e test for UI showing status transitions
- [ ] TASK-F-034-07: DevOps & rollout
    - [ ] Deploy DB migration to staging and run data migration script if needed
    - [ ] Deploy service to staging behind feature-flag and run smoke tests
    - [ ] Coordinate rollout with consumer teams and schedule a production deploy with monitoring

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit test: service returns correct payload given training_jobs DB row with all fields populated
- [ ] Unit test: service returns 400 on malformed model_id and 404 on missing model_id
- [ ] Integration test: orchestrator upsert -> DB write -> API read returns the same status with trace_id propagation
- [ ] Security test: user from different workspace receives 403 when calling status endpoint for a model outside their workspace
- [ ] Contract test: validate API response against JSON Schema for all status enums
- [ ] Load test: simulate 500 concurrent pollers to ensure API latency < P95 target (500ms) and DB query plan OK
- [ ] Failure test: simulate DB write errors and assert alerts fire and API returns 500 with structured error payload

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] OpenAPI + JSON Schema merged to repo and published to internal docs
- [ ] GET /api/v1/models/{model_id}/status implemented and passes all unit + integration tests
- [ ] Training orchestrator writes status updates and status endpoint reflects them in staging
- [ ] RBAC enforced and verified via automated tests
- [ ] Structured logging and tracing integrated and dashboards/alerts configured
- [ ] Frontend status component implemented and e2e verified
- [ ] Feature flagged and deployed to production with monitoring in place

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-032
- [ ] F-030

###### Agent Notes
- Status updates will be produced by the training orchestrator (F-032). Use a single canonical training_jobs table as the source of truth; orchestrator must emit idempotent upserts containing trace_id and a normalized current_stage string. Prefer events on a durable queue (Redis/BullMQ or Pub/Sub) for high-throughput async training jobs; consuming service writes to DB with optimistic concurrency using last_updated_at to avoid out-of-order regressions. Ensure training orchestrator includes minimal structured payload only (no PII); store raw_payload only if masked. Plan for eventual SSE push to UI by reusing the trace_id as SSE stream id.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-034-02: As a product analyst or user of the artifact, I want an API to retrieve training metrics and the model trial leaderboard for a model_id, so that the UI can render the leaderboard panel, show per-trial metrics (validation/test MAPE, walk_forward_mapes), hyperparameters, training_duration_seconds, and trade-off summaries.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GET /api/v1/models/{model_id}/leaderboard returns 200 with paginated JSON array of leaderboard records matching the spec: { trial_id, algorithm, hyperparameters (JSON), validation_mape, test_mape, training_duration_seconds, walk_forward_mapes (array of 5), promoted: bool, trade_off_summary }.
- [ ] Leaderboard API supports query params: page, page_size, sort_by (validation_mape|training_duration_seconds), filter promoted:true/false, algorithm.
- [ ] Leaderboard API returns total_count and page metadata and adheres to pagination limits (max page_size 100).
- [ ] Aggregated summary endpoint GET /api/v1/models/{model_id}/metrics returns model-level metrics consistent with ModelCard schema: validation_mape, test_mape, backtest_results array, top_features, feature_count.
- [ ] Leaderboard entries are written during training runs by the training orchestrator and are immutable after the model is promoted; modifications create an audit event.
- [ ] Unauthorized access returns 401/403; non-existent model returns 404.
- [ ] All inputs validated and outputs validated against JSON Schema; invalid query params return 400 with error details.
- [ ] Leaderboard and metrics APIs sanitize any fields that may contain sensitive info in hyperparameters (no secrets), and are logged with trace_id.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-034-08: Design leaderboard and metrics API contracts and JSON Schemas
    - [ ] Define JSON Schema for leaderboard record and paginated response
    - [ ] Define JSON Schema for aggregated model metrics matching ModelCard fields used by UI
    - [ ] Get sign-off from analytics, product, and frontend teams
    - [ ] Document rate limits and pagination semantics in API docs
- [ ] TASK-F-034-09: Persist trial records from training orchestrator to leaderboard table
    - [ ] Design and migrate leaderboard table: { trial_id PK, model_id FK, algorithm, hyperparameters JSON (no secrets), validation_mape, test_mape, walk_forward_mapes JSON, training_duration_seconds, promoted bool, created_at, trace_id }
    - [ ] Implement orchestrator-side write pattern: record trial at completion with idempotent upsert semantics
    - [ ] Add DB triggers or application-level hooks to append audit log event on insert
    - [ ] Add constraints to prevent secrets in hyperparameters (simple key whitelist + size limit)
- [ ] TASK-F-034-10: Implement leaderboard API endpoints and query layer
    - [ ] Implement GET /api/v1/models/{model_id}/leaderboard with pagination, sorting, filtering
    - [ ] Implement GET /api/v1/models/{model_id}/metrics to return model-level aggregate metrics and backtest array
    - [ ] Implement parameter validation and safe SQL generation to avoid injection
    - [ ] Ensure results are cached for short TTL (5 minutes) to reduce DB load for artifact panel
- [ ] TASK-F-034-11: Frontend: leaderboard panel integration and UX
    - [ ] Render paginated leaderboard table in artifact panel with columns per spec
    - [ ] Implement 'Switch to this model' button that calls promotion endpoint (Stage 5) and shows confirmation modal
    - [ ] Show trade_off_summary as tooltip and provide expand/collapse per row
    - [ ] Add client-side sorting and filters that call API backend
- [ ] TASK-F-034-12: Testing and QA for leaderboard and metrics API
    - [ ] Unit tests for DB query layer and pagination edge cases (page_size limits, empty pages)
    - [ ] Integration tests covering orchestrator writes -> leaderboard read consistency
    - [ ] Property tests to ensure walk_forward_mapes length == 5 and numeric types preserved
    - [ ] Security tests to ensure hyperparameters contain no secrets and RBAC enforced
- [ ] TASK-F-034-13: Monitoring, observability and data integrity checks
    - [ ] Add Prometheus metrics: leaderboard_reads_total, leaderboard_write_errors_total, leaderboard_cache_hits
    - [ ] Add a daily data-integrity job that verifies count(trials per model) equals persisted trial events logged in training audit for that model
    - [ ] Add an alert for out-of-order or missing walk_forward_mapes length anomalies

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit test: leaderboard API returns correct page metadata and respects page_size limits
- [ ] Integration test: orchestrator writes three trials -> API returns them ordered by validation_mape when requested
- [ ] Contract test: leaderboard records validate against JSON Schema and walk_forward_mapes length enforced
- [ ] Security test: attempt to include 'secret' key in hyperparameters writes should be rejected with 400
- [ ] Performance test: fetch leaderboard for a model with 10k trials within acceptable latency (P95 < 1s) using DB paging
- [ ] UI e2e: artifact leaderboard panel shows trials, trade_off_summary, and successful call to 'Switch to this model' invokes expected backend endpoint

- [ ] GET endpoints implemented, pass unit/integration/contract tests, and respond within P95 latency target

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-032
- [ ] F-030

###### Agent Notes
- Leaderboard entries are generated by Stage 4 training orchestrator (F-032) after each trial completes. Orchestrator should emit a structured 'trial_complete' event with trial_id and minimal hyperparameters (strip secrets). Use a durable event queue to guarantee persistence even if training worker fails. The trade_off_summary can be computed by a lightweight summary agent after all trials complete; store that string in the trial record to avoid recomputation during reads. Ensure trial records are immutable post-promotion; any manual overrides must create an audit entry and not mutate historical records.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-034-03: As a data scientist or workspace admin, I want a single model metadata endpoint that returns the full model_card (per ModelCard schema) plus links to the model_registry_uri and artifact(s) (artifact_url(s)), so that the UI and other services can link to the model binary, model_card JSON, and the artifact HTML for lineage and promotion flows.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GET /api/v1/models/{model_id} returns 200 with ModelCard object consistent with ModelCard schema in PRD plus fields: model_registry_uri, artifact_links: [{ artifact_id, artifact_url, artifact_version }].
- [ ] ModelCard fields validation: algorithm, feature_manifest_version, validation_mape, test_mape, backtest_results array, promotion_status, trained_at, model_registry_uri must be present for promoted models.
- [ ] For non-promoted or in-progress models, model_card may be partial but response must include status reference and model_id; clients must be able to fetch model_card when promotion_status == PASS.
- [ ] Response must include model_card versioning metadata and immutable model_id; GET on archived model returns archived:true flag.
- [ ] Unauthorized workspace or cross-workspace access blocked per ABAC; public embed token links are returned only if the requesting principal has access.
- [ ] All returned URIs must be signed or point to internal registry endpoints; model_registry_uri must be an authenticated API URL (not raw storage path) or an onnx/pickle URI behind signed access.
- [ ] 404 for unknown model_id; 400 for malformed requests; 500 on server errors with trace_id.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-034-14: Design model metadata API contract and ModelCard enrichment spec
    - [ ] Write OpenAPI for GET /api/v1/models/{model_id} including ModelCard fields and artifact_links
    - [ ] Define how model_registry_uri is formatted (https endpoint to registry service) and include expected auth method
    - [ ] Define artifact_links schema and include version/semver fields
    - [ ] Align contract with ModelCard schema in PRD and get sign-off from ML and product teams
- [ ] TASK-F-034-15: Implement model_card persistence and retrieval
    - [ ] Ensure training orchestrator writes final model_card JSON to model_cards table and model_registry service with model_registry_uri on promotion
    - [ ] Implement GET endpoint to fetch model_card by model_id reading from model_cards table and resolving model_registry_uri
    - [ ] Implement logic to include artifact links by querying artifact record table for artifacts referencing the model_id
    - [ ] Add caching with short TTL (5 minutes) and ETag support
- [ ] TASK-F-034-16: Security: validate and sanitize URIs and enforce access controls
    - [ ] Validate model_registry_uri format and ensure it is not an S3 presigned URL exposed directly unless signed via registry service
    - [ ] Enforce workspace scoping: only principals with access to workspace or allowed cross-workspace scope can retrieve model_card and artifact_links
    - [ ] Add audit log entry on model_card reads including principal_id and reason
    - [ ] Add unit tests to ensure secrets are not returned in model_card (hyperparameters sanitized)
- [ ] TASK-F-034-17: Frontend: link model_card and artifact from artifact UI
    - [ ] Add 'View model card' link in artifact Data Quality/Model Info footer that calls GET /api/v1/models/{model_id}
    - [ ] Render key ModelCard fields (algorithm, validation_mape, test_mape, trained_at) and link to full registry page
    - [ ] Handle partial model_card states (in-progress) with a disabled 'Download model' button and tooltip explaining status
    - [ ] Add e2e test verifying model_card link opens registry URL in a new trusted tab
- [ ] TASK-F-034-18: Testing and QA for model_card endpoint
    - [ ] Unit tests for model_card serialization and backward compatibility of schema fields
    - [ ] Integration test to verify artifact_links returned match artifact records referencing model_id
    - [ ] Contract test for ModelCard schema compliance
    - [ ] Security penetration test to ensure no path traversal or leak of storage credentials via model_registry_uri
- [ ] TASK-F-034-19: DevOps: registry integration and access logging
    - [ ] Coordinate with model registry service to ensure model binaries are registered and model_registry_uri resolves via authenticated proxy
    - [ ] Ensure registry service logs access and stores lineage mapping from model_id to storage path
    - [ ] Add monitoring for failed registry URI resolutions and create alert for >1% failure rate

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit test: model_card GET returns expected ModelCard fields for promoted model
- [ ] Integration test: model_card GET includes artifact_links that exist in artifact table referencing the model_id
- [ ] Security test: ensure an unauthorized user from another workspace receives 403 and no partial leak
- [ ] Contract test: model_card JSON validates against the ModelCard schema from PRD
- [ ] Failure test: simulate missing model_registry_uri and assert API returns model_card with registry_uri:null and a warning in logs

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] ModelCard OpenAPI + JSON Schema merged and published
- [ ] Training orchestrator writes model_card upon completion/promotion and model_registry_uri is populated
- [ ] GET /api/v1/models/{model_id} implemented, passes unit/integration tests, and returns artifact links

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-032
- [ ] F-030

###### Agent Notes
- ModelCard should be the canonical immutable record for a trained model. Training orchestrator writes the ModelCard as a single JSON blob (ModelCard schema) and publishes a pointer (model_registry_uri) to the model binary in the model registry service. For promoted models, registry writes must succeed before promotion_status is set to PASS; if registry upload fails, orchestrator triggers repair loop (per PRD). Ensure the model_card includes trace_id to correlate with training status events and pipeline audit logs. Consider storing a compact index table for quick model_id -> artifact lookup to avoid expensive joins at read time.

### Epic: Model Registry

#### Requirement / Feature: Model registry basic storage (store model_card + artifact pointers)

**Feature ID:** `F-035`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, data`

**Rationale:** Models and their cards must be registered and retrievable for artifact generation.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-030
- [ ] F-034

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-035-01: As a workspace member (analyst or data scientist), I want promoted models to be persisted as versioned model_card records with pointers to artifacts and binary artifact URIs, so that artifacts, gold layer tables, and downstream consumers can resolve model metadata and retrieve the model binary for inference, explainability, or audit.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] A model_card record schema exists matching the PRD ModelCard interface and persists model metadata for promoted models.
- [ ] - [ ] model_card records include mandatory fields: model_id, workspace_id, session_id, algorithm, target_type, target_metric, hyperparameters, feature_manifest_version, feature_count, top_features, backtest_results, validation_mape, test_mape, promotion_status, training_duration_seconds, trained_at, model_registry_uri and lineage sub-object.
- [ ] - [ ] model_card stores artifact pointers: model_registry_uri (S3/obj store URL), onnx_uri (optional), and a list of artifact_ids that reference artifacts produced by the session.
- [ ] - [ ] Model persistence runs in the Stage 4->5 promotion flow and is transactional with the gold-layer write so either both model_card and gold tables are written or neither is committed.
- [ ] - [ ] Model_card creation validates schema and rejects malformed payloads with 4xx responses logged and observable.
- [ ] - [ ] Unit and integration tests cover success and failure paths for model_card persistence.
- [ ] - [ ] Audit log entry (traceable by trace_id) is emitted for every model_card create/update with actor/session info.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-035-01: Design model_registry DB schema and JSON schema validation
    - [ ] - [ ] Draft SQL table schema for model_registry.table_model_cards in Postgres: columns matching ModelCard interface and jsonb fields for hyperparameters, top_features, backtest_results, lineage
    - [ ] - [ ] Produce JSON Schema (Draft-07 or later) for API payload validation matching the PRD ModelCard shape
    - [ ] - [ ] Define constraints and indexes: primary key on model_id, workspace_id + trained_at index, foreign key constraint to sessions table (session_id) if exists
    - [ ] - [ ] Document retention and archival policy (archive older versions to object store) and soft-delete strategy
    - [ ] - [ ] Review schema with data engineering and security for PII, ensure no PII fields are persisted in model_card
- [ ] TASK-F-035-02: Implement DB migration and persistence layer (backend)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement SQL migration (Flyway/DBT/Alma) to create model_registry.table_model_cards with defined columns and indexes
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement backend repository/service methods: create_model_card(modelCard), update_model_card(model_id, patch), get_model_card(model_id)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement transactional persistence that links model_card write to gold-layer table write using distributed transaction or two-phase commit pattern (or deterministic ordering with compensating action)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add DB-level constraints and NOT NULL where applicable and JSONB column for hyperparameters/backtest_results
    - [ ] - [ ] Add database access tests (migrations up/down, schema asserts) in CI
- [ ] TASK-F-035-03: Implement API endpoints for model_card create and retrieval
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement POST /api/v1/models to accept model_card payload and persist using repository layer; return 201 with location header
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement GET /api/v1/models/{model_id} to return model_card JSON with appropriate authorization checks
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement GET /api/v1/models?workspace_id={id}&session_id={id} paginated list endpoint for workspace consumers
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate requests against JSON Schema; return 400 with structured error body on validation failure
    - [ ] - [ ] Add OpenAPI docs for new endpoints and example payloads
- [ ] TASK-F-035-04: Integrate model registry write into training/promotion flow (agent backend)
    - [ ] - [ ] Update Training Orchestrator to call POST /api/v1/models as the final promotion step after promotion criteria pass
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure orchestrator includes artifact pointers: artifact_id(s) and model_registry_uri(s) returned from object store upload step
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure service propagates trace_id and session_id for auditability
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement retry/backoff and idempotency key on model_card creation to tolerate transient failures
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement compensating delete/mark-failed if model_card created but subsequent gold table write fails (or vice versa) to maintain consistency
- [ ] TASK-F-035-05: Object store integration for model binary and URIs (devops/data)
    - [ ] - [ ] Define object store path convention: s3://{bucket}/models/{workspace_id}/{model_id}/{model_id}.onnx and record URI in model_registry_uri
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement uploading of model binary and optional ONNX artifact during training job and return signed URI or internal storage URI
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure object store write sets ACLs and encryption (SSE) and logs upload events
    - [ ] - [ ] Add lifecycle rule: move archived model binaries to colder storage after 90 days per retention policy
- [ ] TASK-F-035-06: Observability, logging and security for registry operations
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit structured logs on model_card create/update/get with trace_id, workspace_id, session_id, actor, latency
    - [ ] - [ ] Add OpenTelemetry span instrumentation for repository and API handlers
    - [ ] - [ ] Add role-based access control checks: only workspace members with appropriate roles can create model_cards; read access limited to workspace members or via embed tokens
    - [ ] - [ ] Add rate limiting for model registry endpoints and validate tokens
- [ ] TASK-F-035-07: Testing: unit, integration, and contract tests
    - [ ] - [ ] Write unit tests for validation logic (JSON Schema) and repository methods
    - [ ] - [ ] Write integration tests that exercise POST/GET endpoints against a test DB and a mocked object store
    - [ ] - [ ] Add contract tests to ensure API responses conform to ModelCard schema
    - [ ] - [ ] Add CI job to run these tests and fail on schema drift

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for schema validation: valid model_card, missing required fields, invalid types
- [ ] - [ ] Integration test: simulate training flow; upload binary to object store mock; POST model_card; GET model_card; assert fields and URIs
- [ ] - [ ] Transactional consistency test: force failure in gold-layer write and assert model_card is rolled back or marked failed
- [ ] - [ ] Security test: verify unauthorized users receive 403 on create; verify read scoped to workspace
- [ ] - [ ] Load test: POST model_card at expected peak rate and assert latency < P95 target
- [ ] - [ ] Audit / observability test: verify trace_id propagates and appears in logs and tracing backend

- [ ] - [ ] SQL migration applied and present in source control
- [ ] - [ ] POST/GET model registry endpoints implemented and documented in OpenAPI
- [ ] - [ ] Training orchestrator persists model_card on promotion and includes artifact pointers
- [ ] - [ ] End-to-end integration test demonstrates model_card + binary URI persisted and retrievable
- [ ] - [ ] RBAC, logging, and tracing implemented for registry operations
- [ ] - [ ] CI green on unit/integration/contract tests

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] F-030
- [ ] - [ ] F-034
- [ ] - [ ] Object store (S3) credentials/config available in dev and prod

###### Agent Notes
- Model_card schema must strictly match PRD ModelCard. Use JSON Schema validation at API boundary. Persist only non-PII fields—if any field might contain PII, block and require governance review. Ensure atomic link between model_card and gold-layer write: prefer local DB transaction ordering with compensating actions instead of distributed two-phase commit for MVP. Return model_registry_uri immediately after model binary upload; models stored in object store should be immutable (write-once path). Provide idempotency key header to POST to prevent duplicate model entries from retried training jobs.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-035-02: As an artifact author and artifact consumer, I want artifacts to contain and expose pointers to the promoted model_card (model_id and model_registry_uri) so that artifacts can show model provenance, create deep links to model details, and the artifact metadata API can return the model reference for downstream tooling.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Artifact metadata schema includes model_card pointer fields: model_id and model_registry_uri when an artifact was produced by a model session.
- [ ] - [ ] POST artifact creation flow accepts and persists model pointers atomically with artifact metadata.
- [ ] - [ ] GET /api/v1/artifacts/{id} returns model pointer fields and a link to GET /api/v1/models/{model_id}.
- [ ] - [ ] UI artifact metadata panel displays a link to 'View model details' which resolves via GET /api/v1/models/{model_id}.
- [ ] - [ ] If model pointer is missing or model is archived, API returns a clear warning field in artifact metadata and UI shows 'Model archived' state.
- [ ] - [ ] Tests cover persistence, retrieval, and UI display of model pointers.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-035-08: Extend artifact metadata schema and DB migration
    - [ ] - [ ] Update artifact table schema to add nullable fields: model_id (uuid), model_registry_uri (varchar), model_snapshot_version (semver)
    - [ ] - [ ] Write migration and ensure backward compatibility for older artifacts (null values allowed)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add index on model_id for fast joins with model_registry table
    - [ ] - [ ] Document schema change in data catalog
- [ ] TASK-F-035-09: Persist model pointers during artifact generation (backend/agent integration)
    - [ ] - [ ] Update Artifact Generation Agent to include model pointer fields in artifact metadata payload
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure artifact write service persists model pointers transactionally with artifact HTML/object storage writes
    - [ ] - [ ] If artifact generation runs asynchronously, ensure message includes model identifiers and background job writes pointers on completion
    - [ ] - [ ] Add error handling: if model pointer cannot be resolved, persist artifact with pointer as null and emit warning event
- [ ] TASK-F-035-10: API and frontend: expose and display model info on artifact page
    - [ ] - [ ] Update GET /api/v1/artifacts/{id} to return model_id and model_registry_uri fields in metadata payload
    - [ ] - [ ] Update frontend artifact metadata panel to show Model ID, algorithm, validation_mape and a 'View model details' link which calls GET /api/v1/models/{model_id}
    - [ ] - [ ] Add UI warning state for archived/missing models
    - [ ] - [ ] Add automated UI test (Puppeteer/Cypress) to assert metadata panel renders model link
- [ ] TASK-F-035-11: Testing: integration between artifact and model registry
    - [ ] - [ ] Integration test: run end-to-end session that trains + promotes a model; generate artifact; assert artifact metadata contains model_id and model_registry_uri
    - [ ] - [ ] Negative test: simulate model archival and ensure artifact API returns archived flag and UI shows archived state
    - [ ] - [ ] Contract test: artifact metadata JSON schema includes model pointer fields
- [ ] TASK-F-035-12: Security & access controls for model pointers in artifacts
    - [ ] - [ ] Enforce that GET artifact metadata returns model pointers only to users with access to both artifact and model workspace
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure embed tokens provided for public artifacts do not leak internal model_registry_uri (use ephemeral signed access or masked pointer)
    - [ ] - [ ] Log access to model pointers for audit

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] End-to-end test: train a model, promote, generate artifact; GET artifact metadata and assert model_id and URIs present
- [ ] - [ ] UI E2E: open artifact page and click 'View model details', assert model modal loaded
- [ ] - [ ] Security test: request artifact metadata with a user lacking workspace access and assert model pointers are not returned

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] US-F-035-01 (model registry persistence)
- [ ] - [ ] Artifact generation pipeline (Stage 6) integration points

###### Agent Notes
- Artifact generator must include model pointers in the metadata payload. For public/embed artifacts, do not include direct internal S3 URIs; instead include a model_id and an object-store access flow that can return signed URLs on authorized GET /api/v1/models/{model_id}/download with short expiry. Ensure trace_id is propagated from training to artifact generation and stored on artifact.metadata for lineage.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-035-03: As an operations and QA lead, I want the model registry to be auditable, secure, and covered by tests and monitoring so that model persistence is production-rigorous, observable, and recoverable.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] All model_card create/update/delete actions emit structured audit events with actor_id, timestamp, session_id and trace_id.
- [ ] - [ ] RBAC rules are enforced and documented for model registry endpoints; unauthorized attempts are denied and logged.
- [ ] - [ ] Backup and restore procedures for model_card table exist and are documented; model binaries are stored with lifecycle rules.
- [ ] - [ ] Health checks and metrics exist: registry API latency, error rate, model_card count per workspace, object store upload success rate.
- [ ] - [ ] Automated tests (unit, integration, contract) and a QA runbook exist and pass in CI.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-035-13: Implement audit logging and structured events
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit an audit event (to audit log stream) on every model_card create/update/archive with fields: actor_id, workspace_id, model_id, session_id, action, payload_hash, trace_id
    - [ ] - [ ] Persist a copy of the event in append-only audit table for customer-accessible audit logs
    - [ ] - [ ] Add retention and export capability for audit logs for compliance
- [ ] TASK-F-035-14: Implement RBAC & security reviews
    - [ ] - [ ] Add middleware enforcing ABAC: verify actor's workspace role before allowing POST/PUT/DELETE on model registry endpoints
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests to assert 403 for insufficient roles and 200 for permitted roles
    - [ ] - [ ] Conduct a security review and ensure no sensitive data (PII) is written in model_card fields
- [ ] TASK-F-035-15: Monitoring, health checks and SLOs
    - [ ] - [ ] Add Prometheus metrics for model registry: request_count, request_latency_ms, error_count, success_upload_count
    - [ ] - [ ] Expose a /healthz endpoint that verifies DB connectivity, object store connectivity, and schema integrity
    - [ ] - [ ] Add alert rules: error_rate > 1% for 5m, object_store_upload_fail_rate > 0.5% for 5m
- [ ] TASK-F-035-16: Backup, restore and disaster recovery
    - [ ] - [ ] Add nightly logical export of model_card table to object store (encrypted) and test restore in staging
    - [ ] - [ ] Document manual restore steps and runbook for incidents
    - [ ] - [ ] Verify model binary redundancy (multi-AZ or multi-region) per storage policy
- [ ] TASK-F-035-17: QA test matrix and CI integration
    - [ ] - [ ] Define QA test matrix covering create, update, get, list, archive scenarios across roles
    - [ ] - [ ] Add these tests to CI pipeline with test DB and mocked object store; fail PR on regressions
    - [ ] - [ ] Run performance smoke test to validate registry can handle expected promotion burst

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Manual QA checklist: create model_card, update, archive and verify audit log entries and DB rows
- [ ] - [ ] Security pentest focused on model_card endpoints and stored URIs to ensure no unauthenticated access to binaries
- [ ] - [ ] Disaster recovery test: restore model_card from backup and validate integrity against object store binaries
- [ ] - [ ] Monitoring test: simulate errors and ensure alerts fire to on-call channel

- [ ] - [ ] RBAC enforced and tested with automated tests
- [ ] - [ ] Monitoring dashboards created and alerts configured
- [ ] - [ ] Backup and restore procedures validated in staging
- [ ] - [ ] QA matrix and CI integration completed and passing

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] US-F-035-01
- [ ] - [ ] Infrastructure (Prometheus, object store, tracing backend)

###### Agent Notes
- For auditability, include an immutable model_card versioning field (semver) and record repair_cycles and promotion decisions. Use append-only audit table with minimal retention policy defined per workspace. For MVP, encryption at rest and in transit is mandatory. Instrument key paths with OpenTelemetry and ensure trace_id is propagated from orchestration runtime into model registry writes. Provide example cURL and SDK snippets for model retrieval with signed temporary download endpoint for binaries.

## Sprint 9 - Artifact Generator Core

### Epic: Artifact Engine

#### Requirement / Feature: Self-contained HTML artifact generator scaffold (single-file output)

**Feature ID:** `F-036`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, artifact`

**Rationale:** Artifact engine is the primary deliverable from an AnalytIQ session; MVP needs basic output.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-035
- [ ] F-025

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-036-01: As an Analyst, I want the system to generate a single self-contained HTML artifact for a session (KPI + primary time series panels), so that I can download, host, or embed a live, interactive dashboard without any build step.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Produce one single-file HTML per artifact with all JS and CSS inlined and no external build step
- [ ] Allow external dependencies only from cdnjs.cloudflare.com (D3 v7, lodash 4.x, Recharts via React CDN allowed) and reference them via crossorigin CDN script tags
- [ ] Include <meta name='analytiq-artifact-id'> and <meta name='workspace-id'> tags with correct values
- [ ] Define window.__analytiq_state global object and expose updateFilters(patch) function that updates state and re-renders all panels
- [ ] Fetch all panel data at runtime via fetch() calls to gold layer REST endpoints; never embed raw data in HTML
- [ ] Render KPI row (3 metrics) and primary time series (actual vs predicted + 95% CI ribbon) using inline JS and D3 or allowed CDN libs
- [ ] Implement skeleton loading states and error states with retry buttons for both panels
- [ ] Ensure keyboard focusability and aria-labels on SVGs and controls for both panels
- [ ] File size before gzip/minification < 250KB; generator must apply minification and re-check size
- [ ] Persist generated HTML to artifact storage (S3-compatible) and create/update artifact record with artifact_url and html_size_bytes

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-036-01: Implement backend artifact generation API and orchestration
    - [ ] Design POST /api/v1/artifacts/generate endpoint that accepts session_spec and authenticated user context
    - [ ] Validate session_spec schema and permission (workspace membership + session owner/editor) and return 400 on invalid
    - [ ] Fetch session metadata, model_card, and required lineage metadata from Postgres; log input_hash and trace_id
    - [ ] Call artifact generator module to render HTML string from template and injected metadata
    - [ ] Run deterministic validator (see US-F-036-02) on generated HTML and perform repair loop up to 2 cycles before failing
    - [ ] Persist final HTML to S3-compatible object storage with content-type text/html and set CDN invalidation headers
    - [ ] Create artifact record in Postgres (artifact_id, version semver, artifact_url, html_size_bytes) and return artifact metadata
- [ ] TASK-F-036-02: Create HTML template and client-side scaffold (inline JS/CSS)
    - [ ] Author base HTML template with inline CSS custom properties for theme and prefers-color-scheme support
    - [ ] Implement header bar skeleton: title, date range, last refreshed, share button, export menu markup and placeholders
    - [ ] Implement KPI row markup with three metric cards and skeleton loaders
    - [ ] Implement primary time series markup with SVG placeholder, tooltip container, and residuals subplot placeholder
    - [ ] Add window.__analytiq_state initialization block and updateFilters(patch) implementation that triggers renderAllPanels()
    - [ ] Integrate D3 v7 and lodash from cdnjs.cloudflare.com via script tags; guard for CDN load failure and fallback error UI
- [ ] TASK-F-036-03: Implement client data fetching, rendering, and interaction for KPI and time series panels
    - [ ] Implement fetch wrappers that include embed_token query param validation and include exponential backoff retry logic
    - [ ] Implement KPI data fetch from /api/v1/gold/{workspace_id}/predictions endpoint with pagination and map responses to card UI
    - [ ] Implement time series data fetch with date range and filters and map to D3-friendly arrays
    - [ ] Render KPI cards and time series chart using D3, including 95% CI ribbon and basic zoom/brush support
    - [ ] Add click-to-filter behavior: clicking a time series point updates window.__analytiq_state.filters and calls updateFilters
    - [ ] Serialize drill/filter state into URL hash and restore state on load
    - [ ] Add CSV and PNG export buttons for each panel using Blob and canvas APIs and ensure accessible labels
- [ ] TASK-F-036-04: Add backend support for embed tokens, artifact metadata, and storage integration
    - [ ] Implement embed token generation using HS256 JWT signed with workspace secret including artifact_id and scope
    - [ ] Enforce allowed_origins on server-side gold layer API requests when embed_token is presented
    - [ ] Implement artifact object storage writer that uploads HTML to configured S3 bucket and returns CDN URL
    - [ ] Update artifact metadata service to record version, created_at, html_size_bytes, dq_monitor initial status
    - [ ] Log artifact generation events (started, validator_pass, validator_repair_attempt, written) to audit log with trace_id
- [ ] TASK-F-036-05: Implement CI + devops deployment and runtime configuration
    - [ ] Add unit and integration test steps to CI for artifact generator module and deterministic validator
    - [ ] Provision S3 bucket and set CDN distribution configuration for artifact hosting and caching
    - [ ] Add environment configuration for artifact generator (S3 bucket, CDN base URL, workspace signing keys) in secrets manager
    - [ ] Create monitoring alert for artifact generation failures (5xx or validator failures) via existing observability stack
- [ ] TASK-F-036-06: Add security, validation, and logging concerns
    - [ ] Ensure no database credentials or PII are ever embedded in HTML or logged; redact sensitive fields in logs
    - [ ] Validate that generated HTML contains required meta tags and window.__analytiq_state before persisting
    - [ ] Log LLM agent calls only metadata-level (prompt_tokens, latency) and avoid storing session data in plain text
    - [ ] Add rate limiting to artifact generation endpoint to protect workspace budgets and LLM quotas

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Write unit tests for generator template functions to assert presence of meta tags, state object, and required functions
- [ ] Write integration test that posts a valid session_spec and asserts artifact record creation and S3 upload
- [ ] Stub gold layer API and run E2E test that loads generated HTML in headless browser and asserts KPI and chart rendered
- [ ] Run automated size test that asserts generated HTML < 250KB and re-runs minification if required
- [ ] Simulate CDN load failure and assert client shows a graceful error with retry
- [ ] Verify embed_token enforcement by calling gold endpoints with both valid and invalid tokens and asserting 401/403

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Backend endpoint returns 201 with artifact metadata and artifact_url after successful generation
- [ ] Artifact HTML file is stored in S3 and served via CDN URL included in artifact_url
- [ ] KPI and primary time series panels render live data from gold layer API at artifact load
- [ ] All client-side behavior operates through window.__analytiq_state and updateFilters(patch)
- [ ] Artifact passes deterministic validator and recorded html_size_bytes < 250000 bytes pre-compression
- [ ] Unit, integration, and end-to-end tests covering generator and client rendering are added and passing in CI
- [ ] Audit logs for generation events and validator outcomes are recorded with trace_id

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-035
- [ ] F-025

###### Agent Notes
- Generator agent must produce deterministic HTML given same session_spec + model_card + semantic versions. Keep generated JS minimal, avoid bundlers, and prefer D3 for charting for the MVP. Include verbose structured logs for each step (input_hash, template_version, validator_result) to aid troubleshooting.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-036-02: As an Engineering QA Lead, I want a deterministic post-generation validator and repair loop for artifacts, so that every artifact conforms to accessibility, security, and file-size quality gates before being published.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Run deterministic validator on every generated HTML and produce PASS/WARN/FAIL per rule
- [ ] Implement validation rules: CSS custom properties (no hardcoded hex), presence of aria attributes for interactive elements, no position: fixed, no localStorage/sessionStorage, all fetch() calls wrapped with try/catch and error UI, no inline global event handlers, presence of required meta tags, definition of window.__analytiq_state and updateFilters, and html_size_bytes < 250KB
- [ ] Attempt automatic repair loop up to 2 cycles for WARN/FAIL where repairable (minification, small DOM adjustments, inject missing meta tags) and log each repair attempt
- [ ] If unrepairable after max cycles, mark artifact generation failed and return clear remediation message in API response and audit log
- [ ] Expose validator output JSON into artifact metadata for UI consumption (dq_monitor with last_check and status)

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-036-07: Design deterministic validator service
    - [ ] Define validator rules schema and JSON output format with rule_id, description, status, details
    - [ ] Implement rule checks as independent, testable functions (css_check, aria_check, fetch_wrapped_check, storage_usage_check, meta_check, state_check, size_check)
    - [ ] Ensure validator returns structured repairable flag per rule and aggregated dq_gate_status PASS|WARN|FAIL
    - [ ] Add trace_id propagation to validator logs and include timing metrics
    - [ ] Document validator contract for artifact generator integration
- [ ] TASK-F-036-08: Implement automatic repair handlers for repairable failures
    - [ ] Implement HTML minification handler that removes comments, collapses whitespace, and inlines critical styles where safe
    - [ ] Implement meta tag injection handler to add missing analytiq-artifact-id and workspace-id
    - [ ] Implement aria attribute injection for common controls where role/label can be inferred (with conservative defaults)
    - [ ] Implement CSS custom property replacement detection and fail-safe rewrite where hardcoded hexs can be synthesized to CSS vars
    - [ ] Add a safe sandboxed attempt to re-run repair handlers and re-validate, with an incrementing repair_cycle counter
- [ ] TASK-F-036-09: Integrate validator with artifact generation orchestration and audit logging
    - [ ] Call validator synchronously after generating initial HTML and before persisting
    - [ ] Run repair loop up to 2 cycles calling repair handlers when rules are repairable
    - [ ] If validator still fails, persist validator output in artifact record with dq_monitor.status=FAIL and return 422 with remediation instructions
    - [ ] Record validator result and repair steps in pipeline audit log for user and admin inspection
- [ ] TASK-F-036-10: Add tests for validator and repair handlers
    - [ ] Create unit tests for each rule function with positive and negative fixtures
    - [ ] Create integration tests covering repairable failures and ensuring successful repair cycles
    - [ ] Create negative test fixtures that exercise unrepairable failures and assert generator returns expected error payload
    - [ ] Add regression test that verifies a previously known invalid HTML file is repaired or fails deterministically
- [ ] TASK-F-036-11: Expose validator results to UI and monitoring
    - [ ] Extend artifact metadata API to include dq_monitor { last_check, status, alerts }
    - [ ] Emit metrics to observability backend for validator pass/fail counts and average repair cycles
    - [ ] Add dashboard alert for recurring validator failures across a workspace

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit-test each rule with multiple HTML fixtures including edge cases (missing updateFilters, inline storage usage, hardcoded colours)
- [ ] Integration test that runs generator+validator+repair loop against a deliberately malformed HTML fixture and asserts final outcome
- [ ] Performance test to ensure validator runtime meets artifact generation latency target (P50 < 10s, P95 < 25s)
- [ ] Security scan of repair handlers to ensure no arbitrary code execution or unsafe DOM manipulations
- [ ] Failure-mode test that asserts API returns 422 with validator output after unrepaired failures

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Validator runs automatically for every artifact generation request and returns structured JSON
- [ ] Automatic repairs run for repairable rules and are logged; max 2 cycles enforced
- [ ] Artifacts that cannot be repaired are blocked from publication and return clear remediation messages
- [ ] Validator unit and integration tests pass in CI and validator metrics emitted to observability stack
- [ ] dq_monitor is populated on artifact metadata and visible to workspace users via artifact API

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] TASK-F-036-01
- [ ] TASK-F-036-02

###### Agent Notes
- Validator must be deterministic and idempotent. Repair handlers may only perform syntactic, non-semantic fixes. Never attempt to change business logic or data fetching URLs during repair. Always capture and persist original generated HTML and each repair iteration for audit and rollback.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-036-03: As a Consumer of the artifact (embedded viewer), I want the artifact to fetch live gold-layer data and support basic click-to-drill cross-filtering between KPI and time series panels, so that interactions in one panel update the other and drill state is shareable via URL.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Artifact uses fetch() to call gold layer endpoints for predictions and model_insights and handles network errors with retry and error UI
- [ ] Clicking a time series point applies a filter to window.__analytiq_state.filters and KPI cards update to reflect filtered data within 500ms (typical gold API latencies cached at < 1.5s)
- [ ] All fetch() calls are wrapped in try/catch and show a bordered error card with a retry button on failure
- [ ] Drill/filter state is serializable to URL hash and rehydrated on page load restoring filters and panel states
- [ ] Artifact enforces embed_token validation in requests and gold API returns 401/403 for invalid tokens
- [ ] Implement keyboard and aria interactions for applying and clearing filters to satisfy basic accessibility

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-036-12: Implement client-side state management and URL serialization
    - [ ] Define canonical window.__analytiq_state shape including filters, date_range, panel_config and version
    - [ ] Implement updateFilters(patch) to immutably apply patch, push URL hash update, and call renderAllPanels()
    - [ ] Implement readHashState() on load to rehydrate filters and panel state and invoke initial render
    - [ ] Add debounce logic on filter updates to batch rapid clicks and reduce redundant API calls
    - [ ] Add unit tests for state reducer and URL hash serialization/deserialization
- [ ] TASK-F-036-13: Implement cross-filtering behavior between KPI and time series panels
    - [ ] Wire time series point click handler to call updateFilters({ dimension: value }) and set single active drill chip
    - [ ] Subscribe KPI rendering to state changes and fetch filtered KPI metrics from gold.predictions endpoint
    - [ ] Add clear-drill control that resets the single-dimension filter and re-renders both panels
    - [ ] Add visual filter chip in header that is keyboard-accessible and has aria-label describing active filter
- [ ] TASK-F-036-14: Implement robust fetch wrapper and network error handling
    - [ ] Implement fetchWithRetries(url, opts) with configurable retries, backoff, and circuit-breaker behavior
    - [ ] Wrap all API calls in try/catch and surface panel-specific error cards with retry buttons that re-invoke fetchWithRetries
    - [ ] Log network errors to client-side telemetry and emit structured events to server when embed_token is rejected
    - [ ] Test behavior by simulating network timeouts, 401 responses, and slow responses in integration tests
- [ ] TASK-F-036-15: Add accessibility and keyboard interactions for filtering and exports
    - [ ] Ensure SVG charts have role='img' and aria-label describing chart type and key controls
    - [ ] Ensure filter chips, retry buttons, and export buttons are reachable via Tab and have clear aria-labels
    - [ ] Add keyboard shortcut (Enter on focused data point) to trigger the same filter action as click
    - [ ] Add automated axe-core accessibility check in CI for artifact render in headless browser
- [ ] TASK-F-036-16: Test and validate performance and UX for interactive flows
    - [ ] Create E2E scenario that loads artifact, clicks a time series point, and asserts KPI update within SLA
    - [ ] Run load test against gold layer endpoints with N parallel artifact viewers to validate caching and API latency targets
    - [ ] Measure initial artifact page load time and ensure P50 < 3s, P95 < 6s under test conditions
    - [ ] Add monitoring alerts for client-side errors rate and slow-first-render events

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Create E2E tests in Playwright that verify click-to-filter flow: load artifact, click on point, assert KPI values changed and URL hash updated
- [ ] Simulate invalid embed_token and assert gold API returns 401 and artifact shows error card
- [ ] Run accessibility tests (axe) against the rendered artifact and fix violations
- [ ] Run load tests for concurrent viewers to validate gold API caching and artifact client performance under realistic latency

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Clicking time series points updates KPI panel via updateFilters and state persisted to URL hash
- [ ] All API calls use fetchWithRetries and surface friendly error UI with retry controls
- [ ] Embed_token is validated server-side and invalid tokens lead to appropriate 401/403 behavior
- [ ] Accessibility checks added and pass automated axe-core tests in CI
- [ ] Interactive flow E2E tests pass in CI and performance metrics meet artifact P50 targets

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] TASK-F-036-02
- [ ] TASK-F-036-04

###### Agent Notes
- Artifact client should be conservative: prefer retry and clear error states rather than silent failures. Avoid complex client-side logic; keep state minimal and ensure all filter-driven queries include workspace_id and artifact_id for server-side authorization and caching.

#### Requirement / Feature: Deterministic artifact validator (meta tags, aria checks, file size check)

**Feature ID:** `F-039`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, artifact`

**Rationale:** Validation is required before writing artifacts to workspace store per PRD.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-036

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-039-01: As an engineering team member, I want a deterministic, server-side artifact validator service that inspects generated artifact HTML and returns machine-readable validation results, so that artifacts written to the workspace store comply with the PRD checklist before persistence.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Validator exposes a well-documented HTTP API endpoint (POST /api/v1/artifacts/validate) that accepts artifact HTML and returns a deterministic JSON result with pass/warn/fail per rule.
- [ ] - [ ] Validator enforces all checklist rules: meta tags analytiq-artifact-id and workspace-id, aria attributes/roles present on interactive elements, no hardcoded hex color usage (must use CSS custom properties), no position: fixed, no localStorage/sessionStorage, fetch() calls wrapped with try/catch detection, no inline global event handlers, window.__analytiq_state declared, updateFilters() used for filter updates, and file size < 250KB after deterministic minification.
- [ ] - [ ] Each rule returns a unique error code, severity (WARN|FAIL), human-readable message, repair_hint, and location(s) within the HTML (line/offset or DOM selector if detectable).
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests cover positive & negative cases for each rule with >= 90% validator service coverage.
- [ ] - [ ] Validator logs structured events with trace_id, artifact_id (if present in meta), workspace_id, request size, validation result summary, and rule-level counts; logs are emitted to the platform's observability pipeline.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-039-01: Design validator JSON schema, API contract, and error codes
    - [ ] - [ ] Draft JSON Schema for validator request (artifact_html: string, artifact_id?: string, workspace_id?: string, trace_id?: string, options?: object).
    - [ ] - [ ] Draft JSON Schema for validator response (overall_status, timestamp, rules: [{ code, severity, message, repair_hint, locations }], size_bytes_before, size_bytes_after_minify).
    - [ ] - [ ] Define canonical error codes and severities for each check (e.g., VLD_META_MISSING, VLD_ARIA_MISSING, VLD_COLOR_HARDCODED, etc.).
    - [ ] - [ ] Create API OpenAPI spec entry for POST /api/v1/artifacts/validate and update internal API docs.
    - [ ] - [ ] Review schema with security and accessibility leads and record sign-off as PR comment.
- [ ] TASK-F-039-02: Implement core validator engine (backend service)
    - [ ] - [ ] Scaffold a new service module in the artifact-engine backend repo with API handler and DI integration into the existing artifact pipeline.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement HTML parsing pipeline using a deterministic parser (e.g., jsdom) and produce an AST/DOM for rule checks.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement rule checks for meta tags, window.__analytiq_state, updateFilters usage (pattern search), and localStorage/sessionStorage usage.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement rule checks for inline event handlers and global object assignments (regex/static AST scan for window.XXX =).
    - [ ] - [ ] Integrate axe-core programmatic checks for basic ARIA presence and report missing aria-label/role on interactive elements.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests for each rule with fixtures (valid and invalid HTML samples).
- [ ] TASK-F-039-03: Implement deterministic minification and file size checking
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement a deterministic minifier (HTML/CSS/JS) with configuration to avoid lossy transforms that could change runtime behaviour (strip comments, collapse whitespace, reorder attributes deterministically).
    - [ ] - [ ] Add a minification step that runs inside validator and returns size_bytes_after_minify.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add a check that fails/warns when size_bytes_after_minify >= 250000 bytes and returns repair_hint to reduce external libs or simplify templates.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests with sample artifacts around size thresholds to ensure correct minification and detection.
- [ ] TASK-F-039-04: Implement forbidden-pattern checks (position:fixed, hardcoded hex colors, fetch error handling detection)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement CSS scan to detect hardcoded hex values (#rrggbb or #rgb) and flag if present (allow CSS custom property usage).
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement detection for 'position: fixed' occurrences in inline and internal stylesheets and report location(s).
