    - [ ] - [ ] Implement JS static scan to detect fetch() calls that are not wrapped in try/catch or calls that lack .catch handlers (conservative static heuristics).
    - [ ] - [ ] Unit test each detection with positive and negative fixtures and include edge cases (minified inline scripts).
- [ ] TASK-F-039-05: Add observability, structured logging, metrics and tracing
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit structured logs for each validation request: { trace_id, artifact_id, workspace_id, request_size, result_summary, rule_counts } at INFO level.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add metrics: validation_requests_total, validation_failures_total (by code), validation_latency_ms histogram.
    - [ ] - [ ] Attach OpenTelemetry trace/span around validation execution and propagate incoming trace_id if present.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add alerts/runbook notes for sudden spike in FAIL statuses (devops task to onboard alerts).
- [ ] TASK-F-039-06: Testing, security review, and policy gating
    - [ ] - [ ] Create unit tests and integration tests; ensure CI runs them and enforces >= 90% coverage for validator module.
    - [ ] - [ ] Run static analysis and dependency vulnerability scan; remediate findings or add approved exceptions.
    - [ ] - [ ] Conduct a security review for script-parsing logic (no execution of artifact JS), ensure no eval or dynamic execution of artifact content in validator.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add validator to pre-deploy policy checks behind a feature flag for canary rollout.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests: run rule-level tests for each validation rule (positive & negative samples).
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests: POST real artifact HTML through endpoint and assert response shape and codes.
- [ ] - [ ] Accessibility tests: run axe-core assertions and fail when ARIA rules missing for interactive elements.
- [ ] - [ ] Minification tests: assert size reduction deterministic and threshold enforcement.
- [ ] - [ ] Security tests: assert validator never executes artifact JS and rejects extremely large payloads gracefully.
- [ ] - [ ] Fuzz tests: random malformed HTML inputs ensure graceful failure (400) and no resource exhaustion.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] API spec merged and deployed to staging
- [ ] - [ ] Validator service implements all checklist rules with structured JSON output
- [ ] - [ ] Unit & integration tests passing in CI with coverage threshold met
- [ ] - [ ] Structured logs & metrics emitted and visible in staging observability dashboards
- [ ] - [ ] Security/arch review completed and signed off
- [ ] - [ ] Feature flag exists to toggle validator in the artifact write path

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] F-036 (Artifact Generator core) and its artifact HTML template contract
- [ ] - [ ] Observability platform (OpenTelemetry collector & metrics backend)
- [ ] - [ ] Access to artifact generator test fixtures (samples)

###### Agent Notes
- - [ ] Validator response MUST be strictly deterministic and idempotent for the same artifact byte input.
- [ ] Provide example repair_hint strings concise enough for agent repair loop to act (e.g., "Replace hex color #ff6600 with CSS var(--primary-color)").
- [ ] Return selectors where possible to enable targeted repairs (CSS selector or approximate line/offset). If selector detection fails, include small code snippet context.
- [ ] Expose configuration toggles: max_size_bytes, aria_required_selector_list, allow_position_fixed_flag for testing.
- [ ] Ensure validator never executes untrusted JS; use static parsing-only libraries (no vm/eval).

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-039-02: As an artifact engine delivery lead, I want the validator integrated into the artifact generation pipeline with a deterministic repair loop (max 2 cycles) so that artifact generation either produces a passing artifact or surfaces actionable remediation to the user/agent.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Artifact generation flow calls validator synchronously before persisting to workspace store and receives structured result.
- [ ] - [ ] On FAIL-level errors, artifact write is blocked and a repair instruction is returned to the artifact generation agent; the repair loop runs up to 2 repair cycles automatically.
- [ ] - [ ] Repair loop sends targeted repair instructions (repair_hint + location) back to generation agent and accepts repaired HTML back for re-validation; results are deterministic.
- [ ] - [ ] On persistent FAIL after 2 cycles, pipeline emits a human_required SSE event with validator summary and stores a blocked artifact draft with reasons for user review.
- [ ] - [ ] All relevant DQ gate SSE events are emitted (type: dq_gate) with traceable trace_id and audit entry recorded.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-039-07: Integrate validator into artifact write path (backend)
    - [ ] - [ ] Update artifact generator persistence flow to call POST /api/v1/artifacts/validate with artifact HTML prior to S3 upload and DB record creation.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add transactional logic: only persist artifact if validator returns overall_status == PASS or only WARNs (per config).
    - [ ] - [ ] Add feature flag to gate integration for gradual rollout.
    - [ ] - [ ] Update API behavior to return a validation_result object in /api/v1/artifacts response when artifact is rejected (403 with details).
- [ ] TASK-F-039-08: Implement deterministic repair loop orchestration (max 2 cycles)
    - [ ] - [ ] Design repair loop orchestration state machine: INIT -> VALIDATE -> REPAIR_REQUEST -> REPAIR_APPLY -> VALIDATE (repeat up to 2) -> FINAL_DECISION.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement server-side orchestration that transforms validator.rule.repair_hint into a structured repair prompt for the artifact generation agent (per PRD: targeted repair prompt).
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure repair attempts are idempotent and include a repair_attempts counter in the artifact draft metadata.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement backoff and safe-abort rules (e.g., if repair introduces new FAIL codes unrelated to prior ones, abort early).
- [ ] TASK-F-039-09: Emit deterministic SSE events and audit logs for validator outcomes
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit SSE events of type 'dq_gate' with payload { gate: 'artifact_validator', status: PASS|WARN|FAIL, summary, trace_id } during pipeline streaming.
    - [ ] - [ ] Append a structured audit log entry to the pipeline audit log including full validator response (redact nothing except sensitive secrets).
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement backend handlers to surface validator failures to UI via the session run status and the artifact generation modal.
- [ ] TASK-F-039-10: Implement integration tests & E2E flows for repair loop
    - [ ] - [ ] Create E2E test that simulates an artifact with a single failing rule, verifies 1 automatic repair cycle, and final PASS after repair (happy path).
    - [ ] - [ ] Create E2E test that simulates an artifact with persistent failure and verifies 2 repair cycles then human_required SSE and blocked draft state.
    - [ ] - [ ] Test integration with agent prompt exchange: verify repair_hint maps to generation-agent prompt and the returned repaired HTML is re-validated.
    - [ ] - [ ] Run E2E tests in CI under the feature-flag gate.
- [ ] TASK-F-039-11: Add monitoring, alerting, and dashboard for validator results in pipeline runs
    - [ ] - [ ] Add a dashboard tile showing validator pass rate, top failing codes, median repair cycles per artifact, and validation latency.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add alerts for regression: >5% FAIL rate in a 24h window or median validation latency > 2s.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add runbook for operations to triage validator false positives (how to re-run, how to bypass under emergency with Owner approval).
- [ ] TASK-F-039-12: Security & access control updates for blocked drafts and human review
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure blocked artifact drafts are stored encrypted and access-controlled; only artifact owners and workspace admins can view draft HTML.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add UI endpoint for owner to download draft and see validator report; adding 'force publish' requires Owner role and audit log entry.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement retention policy for blocked drafts (e.g., auto-delete after 30 days unless resolved).

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Integration test: generate artifact with missing meta tags -> expect validator FAIL, repair loop invoked, SSE human_required after 2 cycles.
- [ ] - [ ] Integration test: generate artifact with minor WARN-level issues -> expect write allowed with warnings recorded.
- [ ] - [ ] Security test: verify blocked draft access control and audit trail generation.
- [ ] - [ ] Performance test: validate end-to-end artifact generation + validation latency within artifact generation P50 target (<= 10s for artifact generation overall).

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Validator is called in the artifact write path and blocks writes on FAIL-level errors by default.
- [ ] - [ ] Repair loop runs automatically up to 2 cycles with metrics tracked and deterministic behavior validated.
- [ ] - [ ] SSE dq_gate events emitted and audit entries created for every validation outcome.
- [ ] - [ ] E2E tests for successful repair and blocked draft paths passing in CI.
- [ ] - [ ] UI surfaces validator failure details for human remediation and respects access controls.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] US-F-039-01 (validator API & schema) must be implemented and deployed to staging.
- [ ] - [ ] F-036 (Artifact Generator core) for repair prompt consumer integration.
- [ ] - [ ] Session orchestration SSE endpoint (streaming) in place.

###### Agent Notes
- - [ ] Repair prompts MUST be highly prescriptive and actionable; include code-snippet examples and selectors where available.
- [ ] Orchestration must attach the original validator response to the repair request to the generation agent to avoid any LLM hallucination.
- [ ] Maintain idempotency: the same repaired HTML input must always produce the same validator response.
- [ ] Limit repair loop to automated repairs only; if agent cannot produce a PASS after 2 cycles, pipeline escalates to human_required with full validator payload.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-039-03: As a frontend engineer, I want the artifact generator templates and client-side patterns updated to produce validator-compliant HTML (meta tags, aria attributes, no localStorage, window.__analytiq_state usage, wrapped fetch) so that generated artifacts pass validation without excessive repairs.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Artifact generator templates include <meta name='analytiq-artifact-id'> and <meta name='workspace-id'> populated at generation time.
- [ ] - [ ] Templates avoid hardcoded hex colors and instead use CSS custom properties; at least one theme variable (--primary-color) exists and is used for visuals.
- [ ] - [ ] Templates declare window.__analytiq_state and provide updateFilters(patch) hook; all filter state mutations call updateFilters rather than direct state mutation.
- [ ] - [ ] Templates do not use localStorage/sessionStorage; any attempt to use them is removed or replaced with in-memory state and optional server-side persistence via workspace API.
- [ ] - [ ] Templates wrap fetch calls in try/catch and provide consistent error-state rendering per panel.
- [ ] - [ ] Generated artifacts produced by the artifact generator pass validator checks in 95% of sampled cases (goal for minimal repair cycles).

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-039-13: Update artifact HTML templates (frontend templates agent / generator)
    - [ ] - [ ] Modify HTML template generator to inject required meta tags analytiq-artifact-id and workspace-id at generation time.
    - [ ] - [ ] Replace any hardcoded hex colors in templates with CSS custom property references and provide a default :root block with variables.
    - [ ] - [ ] Insert a deterministic header that sets window.__analytiq_state = { filters: {}, meta: { artifact_id, workspace_id }, version: 'v1' } and implement updateFilters(patch) stub used by panels.
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure all panels import/updateFilters() rather than writing to global state directly; add linter rules to enforce this pattern.
- [ ] TASK-F-039-14: Implement client-side static analysis and linting as part of artifact generation
    - [ ] - [ ] Add a static lint step that scans the generated HTML for forbidden patterns (localStorage, sessionStorage, window.onclick assignments, inline onXYZ attributes) and fails the generator build if found (behind feature flag initially).
    - [ ] - [ ] Integrate accessibility checks (axe-core) into generation-time tests to catch missing aria labels on known interactive components.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add a CSS scan to ensure no raw hex color tokens are present in generated CSS.
- [ ] TASK-F-039-15: Create client-side test harness for artifact validation pre-flight
    - [ ] - [ ] Build a headless validation harness that runs in CI: generate artifact HTML, call validator API, and assert PASS or only allowed WARN codes.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add test fixtures for common panel permutations (KPI only, timeseries+KPI, full artifact) and assert validator pass rate.
    - [ ] - [ ] Wire harness into artifact generation CI job so pull requests fail early when templates introduce validator regressions.
- [ ] TASK-F-039-16: Devops and CDN publish flow updates (size enforcement and upload)
    - [ ] - [ ] After validator PASS, run deterministic minifier and re-check size; if size exceeds 250KB, fail the publish step and surface top-size contributors in logs.
    - [ ] - [ ] Automate artifact upload to S3/CDN only after validator PASS and size check PASS; set html_size_bytes in artifact metadata.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add CI job metric to track artifact sizes and alert if median artifact size grows > 10% week-over-week.
- [ ] TASK-F-039-17: Documentation, linters, and onboarding
    - [ ] - [ ] Update developer documentation describing required patterns for artifact templates (meta tags, window.__analytiq_state, updateFilters, fetch wrapping).
    - [ ] - [ ] Add ESLint/HTMLHint rules (or custom plugin) to the artifact templates repo to block forbidden patterns and enforce aria attributes on components.
    - [ ] - [ ] Communicate changes to frontend teams and run a short training/demo session showing how to author validator-compliant templates.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] CI pre-flight: generate artifacts from templates -> call validator -> assert PASS or defined WARN codes.
- [ ] - [ ] Accessibility smoke tests: run axe-core against generated artifact in headless browser and fail on critical violations.
- [ ] - [ ] Size regression test: baseline artifact sizes and run CI assertion to ensure new artifacts do not exceed threshold.
- [ ] - [ ] Manual exploratory QA: load generated artifact in iframe, run keyboard navigation checks, verify updateFilters() triggers and aria-label presence.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] All artifact template changes merged and feature-flagged in staging.
- [ ] - [ ] CI pre-flight harness validates generated artifacts and fails on regressions.
- [ ] - [ ] Generated artifacts in staging pass validator in >= 95% of sampled cases.
- [ ] - [ ] Documentation and lint rules published and applied.
- [ ] - [ ] CDN upload flow gated behind validator PASS and size check.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] US-F-039-01 (validator API and error codes) for integration tests and harness.
- [ ] - [ ] F-036 (Artifact Generator core) for template contract and generator hooks.
- [ ] - [ ] CDN/S3 credentials and artifact metadata schema updates.

###### Agent Notes
- - [ ] Templates must emit deterministic HTML; avoid inclusion of any random or timestamped content that would break idempotency checks.
- [ ] Provide a 'validator-exemption' opt-out header for trusted internal artifacts (only for emergency Owner override) with strict audit logging.
- [ ] Lint rules should be surfaced as pre-commit/pre-push hooks in the templates repo to catch issues early.
- [ ] When writing repair_hint text, favor short, concrete diffs or replace-examples rather than abstract guidance so repair agent can apply fixes reliably.

#### Requirement / Feature: Artifact metadata storage & object-store upload (HTML + metadata)

**Feature ID:** `F-040`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, storage`

**Rationale:** Artifacts must be stored and retrievable via CDN links.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-036
- [ ] F-035

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-040-01: As the artifact engine, I want to persist artifact metadata (artifact record, versioning, lineage pointers, status) so that artifacts are discoverable, auditable, and reproducible.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Artifact metadata record persists in Postgres with required schema fields: artifact_id, name, description, owner_id, workspace_id, version (semver), created_at, updated_at, session_spec, model_card_ref, lineage (source_tables, gold_table, feature_manifest_version, governance_manifest_version, model_id, model_registry_uri), access, share_links, embed_tokens (meta), refresh_schedule, artifact_url, html_size_bytes, dq_monitor.
- [ ] - [ ] Create API POST /api/v1/artifacts that accepts session_spec + artifact metadata and returns artifact_id and initial status ('uploading').
- [ ] - [ ] Artifact metadata write is atomic and uses status transitions: uploading -> ready | failed; on failure metadata contains error reason and last_attempt_at.
- [ ] - [ ] Metadata write enforces workspace-level authorization and ABAC checks; only workspace members with appropriate role can create artifacts.
- [ ] - [ ] Metadata version increments according to semver rules: new generation increments minor; explicit override allowed only by Owner.
- [ ] - [ ] All metadata writes are logged to audit log with trace_id, actor_id, and input_hash.
- [ ] - [ ] Schema migrations and TypeScript interfaces are provided and validated by CI.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-040-01: Design and implement Postgres artifact metadata schema and migration
    - [ ] - [ ] Define SQL migration for artifacts table and supporting tables (share_links, embed_tokens, access) matching PRD schema.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add necessary indices (artifact_id PK, workspace_id, owner_id, created_at) and foreign key constraints.
    - [ ] - [ ] Write TypeScript/ORM models and JSON schema validators for artifact payloads.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add DB-level constraints for non-null required fields and size limits (e.g. name length, description length).
    - [ ] - [ ] Run migration in a local/dev environment and validate schema with sample data.
- [ ] TASK-F-040-02: Implement backend service layer to create/update artifact metadata
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement service function createArtifact(session_spec, metadata) that inserts record with status='uploading' and returns artifact_id.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement updateArtifactStatus(artifact_id, status, details) to support uploading, ready, failed states and record last_attempt_at and error messages.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add input validation (JSON Schema) and semantic checks: session_spec match workspace, required lineage fields present.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement idempotency key handling to make POST /api/v1/artifacts idempotent on retries.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add structured logging and attach trace_id to DB calls.
- [ ] TASK-F-040-03: Implement API endpoint POST /api/v1/artifacts and GET /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/metadata
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement route handlers with RBAC checks using workspace ACLs.
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate request payloads against JSON schemas and return 400 on validation error with detailed error message.
    - [ ] - [ ] Return 201 with artifact_id and status after create; include Location header to metadata endpoint.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement GET endpoint to return artifact metadata, hide sensitive fields (e.g. DB internal IDs) for unauthorized callers.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add OpenAPI contract changes and update API docs.
- [ ] TASK-F-040-04: Frontend: Add 'Save artifact' flow to call metadata API and show status
    - [ ] - [ ] Add UI call from artifact preview/save button to POST /api/v1/artifacts with session_spec and metadata.
    - [ ] - [ ] Show status states in UI: Uploading (spinner), Ready (link), Failed (error + retry).
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate user permissions client-side and show meaningful error when not authorized.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add telemetry events for save attempts, success, failure with error categories.
- [ ] TASK-F-040-05: Add server-side audit logging and monitoring for metadata operations
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit structured audit events to audit log with fields: event_type, actor_id, artifact_id, workspace_id, input_hash, timestamp.
    - [ ] - [ ] Instrument metrics for artifact creations, status transitions, create failures and surface in Grafana.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add alerts for repeated failures (rate > threshold) to on-call channel.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add Sentry/error capture for unexpected exceptions with trace_id.
- [ ] TASK-F-040-06: Write unit and integration tests for metadata API and service
    - [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for service functions: createArtifact, updateArtifactStatus, idempotency behavior.
    - [ ] - [ ] Integration tests against test Postgres instance validating DB constraints and migrations.
    - [ ] - [ ] API contract tests for POST/GET endpoints including auth and error cases.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add CI pipeline step to run these tests and enforce coverage threshold for changed files.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for schema validation and service logic including idempotency
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests creating artifacts end-to-end in a test DB, asserting status transitions
- [ ] - [ ] API negative tests: malformed payload, unauthorized user, missing lineage fields
- [ ] - [ ] Load test creating N artifacts concurrently to ensure DB locks and idempotency behave
- [ ] - [ ] Manual QA: create artifact via UI, verify metadata record & audit log entries

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Postgres migration merged and deployed to dev environment
- [ ] - [ ] API endpoints implemented, documented, and passing CI
- [ ] - [ ] Unit and integration tests passing in CI
- [ ] - [ ] Audit logging and metrics emitting correctly
- [ ] - [ ] Frontend save flow integrated and shows status transitions
- [ ] - [ ] RBAC checks enforce workspace permissions
- [ ] - [ ] Dependency features F-036 and F-035 noted and integration points validated

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] F-036
- [ ] - [ ] F-035

###### Agent Notes
- Implement metadata writes as two-phase: write initial record status='uploading' before attempting object-store upload; on upload success update to 'ready' with artifact_url and html_size_bytes; on error set status='failed' with error_code. Use idempotency keys to allow safe retries. Ensure schema strictly matches PRD artifact schema and include trace_id propagation. Keep the metadata model minimal for MVP but extensible for later fields.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-040-02: As the artifact engine, I want to upload the generated single-file HTML artifact to object storage and expose a CDN-served URL so that artifacts are hostable and retrievable via CDN links.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Artifact HTML files are uploaded to S3-compatible object store (production: S3/CloudFront) under workspace-scoped prefix and stored encrypted at rest.
- [ ] - [ ] Upload sets object metadata: Content-Type=text/html, Content-Encoding if minified, Cache-Control=public,max-age=3600 (configurable), and custom metadata: analytiq-artifact-id, workspace-id.
- [ ] - [ ] After successful upload the artifact metadata record is updated with artifact_url (CDN URL), html_size_bytes, and status='ready'.
- [ ] - [ ] On artifact refresh/upload, CDN cache is invalidated for the artifact URL (or versioned URL used) so the latest artifact is served immediately.
- [ ] - [ ] Upload implements retries with exponential backoff and marks metadata status='failed' with error on repeated failures.
- [ ] - [ ] Object-store access is private; CDN serves via signed origin or signed URL. Public share links require separate snapshot flow (out-of-scope except metadata toggles).
- [ ] - [ ] Deterministic artifact validator runs pre-upload (file size < 250KB, meta tags present, no forbidden patterns) and fails upload with clear error messages if validation fails.
- [ ] - [ ] All upload operations are logged with trace_id and upload latency metrics are emitted.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-040-07: Implement HTML artifact validation & minification pipeline
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement deterministic validator that checks: meta tags analytiq-artifact-id and workspace-id, no hardcoded color hex values, aria attributes presence, no position:fixed, no localStorage/sessionStorage usage, fetch() calls wrapped in try/catch, window.__analytiq_state defined, file size < 250KB after minification constraint.
    - [ ] - [ ] Integrate a safe minifier step (HTML/JS minify) that preserves required accessibility attributes and meta tags.
    - [ ] - [ ] Return structured validation errors and map to user-friendly messages for UI.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests covering each validation rule with positive and negative samples.
- [ ] TASK-F-040-08: Implement object-store upload service (S3-compatible) and CDN integration
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement uploadArtifactToObjectStore(artifact_id, html_bytes, metadata) that uploads to configured bucket under prefix /artifacts/{workspace_id}/{artifact_id}/{version}.html.
    - [ ] - [ ] Configure upload object metadata headers (Content-Type, Cache-Control) and set server-side encryption flag.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement versioned URLs (include semver or generation timestamp) to simplify cache invalidation and satisfy immediate consistency via URL rotation.
    - [ ] - [ ] Integrate with CDN (CloudFront) to produce canonical cdn_url and implement invalidation step for non-versioned flows.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement exponential backoff retry on transient errors and fail-fast on client errors, logging attempts and final status.
- [ ] TASK-F-040-09: Update metadata record post-upload and handle status transitions
    - [ ] - [ ] After successful upload update Postgres artifact record with artifact_url (CDN URL), html_size_bytes, version, status='ready', updated_at.
    - [ ] - [ ] On upload failure update record status='failed' and write error_code, error_message, retry_count.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement background job to retry failed uploads up to configurable max_retries and send alerts on persistent failures.
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure updates are idempotent and race-condition safe (use optimistic update or transactional locks).
- [ ] TASK-F-040-10: DevOps: Provision object store bucket, IAM roles, and CDN distribution
    - [ ] - [ ] Provision S3 bucket with lifecycle rules and encryption; restrict public access and enable bucket versioning for artifact safety.
    - [ ] - [ ] Create IAM role/policy for service to put/get objects and restrict to artifact prefixes.
    - [ ] - [ ] Create CDN distribution (CloudFront) or alternative with origin access to bucket and default TTL; document invalidation approach.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add monitoring dashboards for upload latency, error rates, and storage usage; add CI/CD config to deploy infra changes.
- [ ] TASK-F-040-11: Security: Ensure object access & encryption and auditability
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure server-side encryption (SSE-S3 or SSE-KMS) is enabled and encryption keys are managed by infra team.
    - [ ] - [ ] Restrict bucket policies to allow only service role and CDN origin to access objects.
    - [ ] - [ ] Audit access logs (S3 access logs / CloudFront logs) piped to observability storage for compliance auditing.
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure artifact_url does not leak workspace secrets and that public share requires snapshot flow controlled elsewhere.
- [ ] TASK-F-040-12: Testing: Write tests for upload and CDN flows
    - [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for upload service mocking S3/SDK behaviors including transient errors and retries.
    - [ ] - [ ] Integration tests that perform an upload to a staging bucket and verify artifact_url is reachable via CDN or signed URL.
    - [ ] - [ ] End-to-end test: generate sample artifact HTML, validate, upload, update metadata, and verify metadata reflects size and URL.
    - [ ] - [ ] Failure tests: validator rejects broken HTML; network intermittency causes retry path; persistent failure sets status='failed'.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for validator rules and minifier correctness
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests uploading to staging S3 and verifying headers/metadata
- [ ] - [ ] E2E tests: artifact generation agent -> validator -> upload -> metadata update
- [ ] - [ ] CDN cache-behavior test: upload new version and verify immediate serving (either via invalidation or versioned URL)
- [ ] - [ ] Security tests: attempt unauthorized object access and assert denial; validate encryption at rest
- [ ] - [ ] Load test: simulate high-frequency artifact uploads and measure upload latency and retry behavior

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Validator and minifier implemented and run in CI for artifact generation pipeline
- [ ] - [ ] Object store and CDN provisioned and accessible from service account
- [ ] - [ ] Upload flow implemented with retries, status updates, and logging
- [ ] - [ ] CDN URL returned and stored in artifact metadata with correct headers and size
- [ ] - [ ] Security controls (encryption, IAM) applied and access logs enabled
- [ ] - [ ] End-to-end tests passing in staging and documented manual QA checklist

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] F-036
- [ ] - [ ] F-035

###### Agent Notes
- Use versioned object key pattern to avoid complex CDN invalidations: /artifacts/{workspace}/{artifact_id}/{version}.html. Validator must run synchronously before upload and return structured error codes consumable by the UI. Keep upload idempotent by using artifact_id+version as deterministic key. Record upload metrics and make failure reasons actionable (validation_error, network_error, permission_error). For MVP, serve artifacts via CDN with private origin and public read for CDN; public snapshot flow implemented separately.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-040-03: As a workspace member or external embed consumer, I want to retrieve artifact metadata and receive signed embed tokens/CDN links so that I can view or embed artifacts with appropriate access controls.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Implement GET /api/v1/artifacts and GET /api/v1/artifacts/{id} to list and fetch artifact metadata respecting workspace ACLs.
- [ ] - [ ] Implement POST /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/share to create share links and embed tokens; response includes token, expires_at, role, allowed_origins for embed tokens.
- [ ] - [ ] Embed tokens are HS256 JWTs signed with workspace-specific secret and include claims: artifact_id, workspace_id, scope (read_only|interactive|admin), expires_at, allowed_origins; tokens must be verifiable server-side.
- [ ] - [ ] Token issuance enforces RBAC: only artifact owner or workspace admin can create embed tokens with scope > read_only; allowed_origins enforced on creation.
- [ ] - [ ] GET endpoints redact secrets and enforce rate limits for public tokens; public share token uses snapshot or separate flow (configurable).
- [ ] - [ ] Token validation endpoint exists for gold layer API to validate embed_token and allowed_origins server-side.
- [ ] - [ ] All share and token creation events recorded in audit log with actor and timestamp.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-040-13: Implement artifact metadata retrieval APIs with RBAC and pagination
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement GET /api/v1/artifacts (list) with filters: workspace_id, owner_id, tag, updated_since; add pagination.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement GET /api/v1/artifacts/{id} returning metadata and artifact_url only to authorized callers; redact sensitive fields for public callers.
    - [ ] - [ ] Enforce ABAC checks and return 403 for unauthorized access.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add API instrumentation and include trace_id in responses for troubleshooting.
- [ ] TASK-F-040-14: Implement share link and embed token issuance API
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement POST /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/share that supports generating share_link (snapshot/public) and embed_token (JWT) with requested scope and expiry.
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate that caller has permission to create requested scope; enforce allowed_origins array format and reasonable length.
    - [ ] - [ ] Store share metadata in artifacts.share_links and embed_tokens tables including created_by and usage counters.
    - [ ] - [ ] Return structured response with token, expiry, and usage guidance for embedding.
- [ ] TASK-F-040-15: Implement token signing, rotation, and server-side validation logic
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement JWT signing util using HS256 with workspace-specific secret stored in secrets manager; include key id header for rotation support.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement token validation middleware for gold layer API to validate artifact token claims and allowed_origins and reject if expired or origin mismatch.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement secret rotation strategy: support multiple active keys and key-id lookup for validation.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add tests for expired token, wrong origin, tampered token, and scope enforcement.
- [ ] TASK-F-040-16: Frontend: Share dialog and embed copy flow
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement Share dialog that calls POST /share and displays returned token/link with copy button and expiry info.
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate allowed_origins input and show warnings if wildcard origins present.
    - [ ] - [ ] Show token usage guidance and which features are allowed under each scope.
    - [ ] - [ ] Track telemetry when share link created and when embed code copied.
- [ ] TASK-F-040-17: Testing: Token issuance & validation tests and security checks
    - [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for JWT generation and validation including claim correctness and signature verification.
    - [ ] - [ ] Integration tests where a client uses embed_token to fetch gold layer endpoints and is authorized; negative tests for origin mismatch.
    - [ ] - [ ] Penetration test to verify token cannot be re-used across workspaces and cannot escalate privileges.
    - [ ] - [ ] Load test signing endpoint to ensure key generation doesn't become a bottleneck.
- [ ] TASK-F-040-18: Monitoring & auditing for share/token events
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit audit events for share creation, share revocation, and token validation failures.
    - [ ] - [ ] Create dashboard showing share token issuance rate, expired-token access attempts, and unusual origin patterns.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement daily job to revoke tokens past allowed rotation window and notify artifact owners of long-lived tokens.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for API handlers and RBAC enforcement
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests for token issuance and server-side validation against gold-layer endpoints
- [ ] - [ ] Security tests covering token tampering, origin violations, and cross-workspace token use
- [ ] - [ ] Manual QA: create embed token in UI, embed artifact in iframe on allowed origin, verify access; attempt embed from disallowed origin and verify denial
- [ ] - [ ] Regression tests ensuring existing artifact retrieval flows are unchanged

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] List and get metadata APIs implemented with RBAC and pagination
- [ ] - [ ] Share/token issuance API implemented with HS256 signing and rotation support
- [ ] - [ ] Gold-layer token validation middleware integrated and tested end-to-end
- [ ] - [ ] Frontend share dialog implemented and documented
- [ ] - [ ] Audit logging for all share/token events is enabled
- [ ] - [ ] Security and penetration tests passed in staging

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] F-036
- [ ] - [ ] F-035

###### Agent Notes
- Follow the principle of least privilege for embed token scopes. Use workspace-specific signing keys stored in secrets manager with key IDs to support rotation. Enforce allowed_origins both at issuance and at validation time on the gold-layer API. Implement share creation as a metadata-only operation that references artifact_url rather than embedding data. For reproducibility, include session_spec and model_card references in token claims only if necessary; prefer minimal claims. Log every token creation and validation event with trace_id for troubleshooting.

### Epic: Artifact Panels

#### Requirement / Feature: KPI row panel implementation (actual vs predicted, MAPE badge)

**Feature ID:** `F-037`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `frontend, artifact`

**Rationale:** KPI row is part of MVP artifact panels per PRD.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-036
- [ ] F-025

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-037-01: As a Viewer of an AnalytIQ artifact, I want to see a KPI row showing actual vs predicted values with a MAPE badge, trend arrow and period selector, so that I can quickly understand model accuracy and recent performance at a glance.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The KPI row displays up to 4 metric cards (default 3) with: metric name, actual_value, predicted_value, absolute_error, MAPE badge, trend arrow indicating direction vs previous period, and a period comparison (WoW, MoM, YoY) selector.
- [ ] MAPE is computed and shown as a percentage with one decimal place and color-coded badge states: green (≤ validation_mape), amber (> validation_mape and ≤ validation_mape * 1.5), red (> validation_mape * 1.5).
- [ ] KPI values update when global filters in window.__analytiq_state change and when the period selector is adjusted.
- [ ] Loading state: each KPI card shows a skeleton loader until its data resolves. If the API call fails, the card shows an error message and a retry button.
- [ ] Accessibility: each KPI card has aria-label (describing metric and values), role='article', keyboard focusable, and passes automated axe checks for the KPI row.
- [ ] Responsive: KPI row stacks into a single column under 768px and matches artifact responsive rules.
- [ ] No hardcoded color hex values; all colors use CSS custom properties defined in the artifact header.
- [ ] All fetch() calls used by KPI row are wrapped in try/catch, log errors via the artifact logging endpoint, and surface user-friendly error text.
- [ ] File-size and performance: KPI row implementation does not increase artifact HTML beyond budget (artifact target < 250KB) and individual panel render time meets artifact panel budget (primary render within P50 target).

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-037-01: Implement frontend KPIRow component and KPICard subcomponents in the artifact HTML template
    - [ ] Create KPIRow container that reads metrics list from window.__analytiq_state and renders KPICard components
    - [ ] Implement KPICard to display metric name, actual_value, predicted_value, absolute_error, MAPE badge and trend arrow
    - [ ] Implement period selector control (WoW, MoM, YoY) inside KPIRow with accessible keyboard behavior
    - [ ] Ensure CSS uses custom properties (no hardcoded hex) and responsive layout rules for breakpoints <768px
    - [ ] Add skeleton loader UI and error UI with retry button wired to re-fetch for that card
    - [ ] Add unit visual tests (snapshot) for KPIRow and KPICard rendering states
- [ ] TASK-F-037-02: Integrate KPIRow with global state management and panel re-render flow
    - [ ] Read filter and date_range from window.__analytiq_state and subscribe to updateFilters() changes
    - [ ] Implement memoized data query key to avoid redundant fetches when unrelated filters change
    - [ ] Trigger panel re-render when period selector changes and append period param to gold API calls
    - [ ] Emit analytics event (artifact_panel_viewed) on KPIRow mount and KPICard retry clicks
    - [ ] Add defensive checks for missing state values and show fallback text 'No data available' when appropriate
- [ ] TASK-F-037-03: Add frontend validation, error handling and logging for KPI panel
    - [ ] Wrap all fetch() calls in try/catch and show error card with retry action on failure
    - [ ] Add console-safe logging and call artifact logging endpoint /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/panel_logs on errors
    - [ ] Validate and sanitize all numeric values from API before display (isFinite checks, fallback null handling)
    - [ ] Implement rate-limit backoff UI when gold API returns 429 with Retry-After
    - [ ] Add automated UI tests to assert error flows and retry behavior
- [ ] TASK-F-037-04: Implement accessibility and localization for KPI row
    - [ ] Add aria-labels and roles to KPICard elements; ensure trend arrow has accessible description
    - [ ] Ensure keyboard focus order and focus-visible outlines for retry and period selector
    - [ ] Localize numeric formatting using Intl.NumberFormat with workspace locale from metadata
    - [ ] Run automated accessibility checks (axe) in CI and fix violations
    - [ ] Add i18n keys for all user-facing strings and validate translations for supported locales
- [ ] TASK-F-037-05: Create frontend tests and QA test cases for KPI row
    - [ ] Write unit tests for KPICard calculation logic (MAPE, absolute_error, trend determination)
    - [ ] Write end-to-end Cypress tests for KPI row: loading, success, error retry, filter-driven updates
    - [ ] Add visual regression checks for desktop and mobile renderings
    - [ ] Add performance test to ensure KPI panel renders within target time on CI test harness
    - [ ] Document manual test checklist for QA engineers

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests: verify MAPE calculation, absolute_error, trend direction for edge cases (zeros, nulls, division by zero protection)
- [ ] Integration tests: mock gold API responses for actual/predicted/validation_mape and assert display logic for badge colors
- [ ] E2E tests: Cypress scenario for: successful load, API failure -> retry, filter change updates values, period selector behavior
- [ ] Accessibility tests: run axe-core in CI and manual keyboard navigation check
- [ ] Performance tests: measure KPI panel render time on CI artifact harness and ensure within target

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] KPIRow component merged to main branch with green CI including unit, integration, E2E and accessibility tests
- [ ] MAPE badge logic implemented and covered by unit tests
- [ ] All fetch calls are error-handled, logged, and retryable in the UI
- [ ] Accessibility automated checks pass and manual accessibility review completed
- [ ] UI passes visual regression for designated viewports and artifact HTML size budget verified
- [ ] Peer review completed and UX sign-off received from product designer

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-036
- [ ] F-025

###### Agent Notes
- KPIRow must read values from gold.predictions aggregated endpoints. Avoid embedding data in HTML. Use window.__analytiq_state and updateFilters() to drive re-renders. Maintain minimal DOM and CSS to stay within artifact size budget. Use CSS custom properties for all colors; trend arrow should be an inline SVG with aria-hidden=false and accessible label.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-037-02: As an Artifact Backend Engineer, I want an API surface and server-side aggregation that returns KPI summary (actual, predicted, absolute_error, MAPE, previous_period comparison) for requested filters and period windows, so that the KPI row can fetch fast, accurate, and audited metrics.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A new gold-layer read-only endpoint /api/v1/gold/{workspace_id}/kpi_summary supports GET with params: artifact_id, metric, date_start, date_end, period_compare (wow|mom|yoy), filters (JSON), and returns JSON with fields: actual_value, predicted_value, absolute_error, mape, prev_actual, prev_predicted, validation_mape (from model_card), sample_count.
- [ ] MAPE computed server-side as mean(|(actual - predicted)/actual|) × 100 for rows where actual != 0; rows with actual 0 are excluded and exclusion_count returned; if exclusion_count > 50% of rows, API returns mape: null and warning in payload.
- [ ] Endpoint enforces auth via embed_token or user session and verifies artifact_id and workspace_id match token scope; returns 401 on invalid token and 403 for cross-workspace attempts.
- [ ] Response capped to a lightweight aggregate (single row) to keep latency low; endpoint leverages Redis query cache with 5-minute TTL to satisfy artifact performance targets and responds under P95 latency target.
- [ ] All queries include audit logging with trace_id, query_plan hash, row_count and returned sample_count and are recorded in pipeline audit log.
- [ ] Endpoint returns deterministic schema and error codes and logs errors to observability backend (OpenTelemetry traces and Sentry)

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-037-06: Design API contract and add endpoint handler for KPI summary
    - [ ] Draft OpenAPI spec for GET /api/v1/gold/{workspace_id}/kpi_summary with query params and response schema
    - [ ] Add server-side handler in gold API service that validates params and token scopes
    - [ ] Implement request input validation and return 400 for invalid param combinations
    - [ ] Add unit tests for parameter validation, auth checks, and error codes
    - [ ] Document endpoint in internal API docs
- [ ] TASK-F-037-07: Implement server-side aggregation SQL and MAPE computation
    - [ ] Create parameterized SQL template that aggregates actual_value and predicted_value for the requested date range and grouping
    - [ ] Implement MAPE calculation excluding rows with actual_value = 0 and return exclusion_count and sample_count
    - [ ] Add handling: if sample_count < 1 or exclusion_count/sample_count > 0.5 then set mape to null and include warning reason
    - [ ] Write unit tests for SQL logic and mape calculation with synthetic datasets covering edge cases (all zeros, nulls, small sample sizes)
    - [ ] Add explain-plan logging to help troubleshoot slow queries
- [ ] TASK-F-037-08: Add caching, performance and observability
    - [ ] Add Redis caching for the aggregated KPI response keyed by workspace_id + artifact_id + filters + date_range with 5 minute TTL
    - [ ] Instrument endpoint with OpenTelemetry trace and metrics for latency, cache_hit, cache_miss
    - [ ] Add Sentry error capture with contextual tags: workspace_id, artifact_id, metric
    - [ ] Add rate limiting behavior for public or embed tokens per artifact (100 req/hr) and return 429 with Retry-After when exceeded
    - [ ] Load-test endpoint with representative query patterns and tune indexes or materialized views if required
- [ ] TASK-F-037-09: Security and authorization checks
    - [ ] Verify embed_token or session auth and enforce workspace scope and artifact_id binding
    - [ ] Ensure query builder escapes filters to prevent SQL injection and use parameterized queries
    - [ ] Block requests that reference PII fields in filters unless workspace ACL permits; return 403 when blocked
    - [ ] Log auth checks and failed attempts to security audit logs
    - [ ] Conduct threat modeling review for the endpoint
- [ ] TASK-F-037-10: Backend tests and CI integration
    - [ ] Write unit tests for endpoint handler and SQL aggregation functions
    - [ ] Write integration tests that spin up a test DB with gold.predictions sample data and assert accurate KPI aggregates including MAPE logic
    - [ ] Add contract tests ensuring API matches OpenAPI spec
    - [ ] Add CI job to run backend integration tests and load test smoke checks
    - [ ] Coordinate with frontend team to run end-to-end tests using mocked and real endpoint responses

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests: validate parameter parsing, auth enforcement and error responses
- [ ] Integration tests: populate test gold.predictions table and validate aggregates and MAPE for normal, zero-actual, nulls and small-sample scenarios
- [ ] Security tests: run SQL injection fuzzing, verify token scope enforcement and PII filter block behavior
- [ ] Performance tests: simulate concurrent requests and confirm average latency and cache behavior; assert 95th percentile meets SLA
- [ ] Observability validation: ensure trace logs include trace_id propagation and audit log entries are created

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] KPI summary endpoint implemented, tested, documented and merged to main branch with green CI
- [ ] Endpoint enforces auth, parameter validation, caching and rate limiting
- [ ] MAPE computation verified by unit and integration tests for edge cases and documented in API docs
- [ ] Observability and audit logging for each request are present and validated in staging
- [ ] Security review completed and no SQL injection/PII leaks present
- [ ] Performance tests meet P95 latency targets and Redis cache reduces backend load

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-036
- [ ] F-025

###### Agent Notes
- Keep server-side aggregation lightweight — return single-row aggregates only. Rely on existing gold.predictions table schema. Ensure mape calculation excludes zeros and returns clear warning payload when excluded rows are significant. Tie response to model_card.validation_mape for badge color logic. Use workspace-scoped Redis cache and honor embed token rate limits. Emit trace_id to frontend so artifact logs can be correlated end-to-end.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-037-03: As an Artifact Generation Agent and DevOps lead, I want the artifact generation pipeline and deterministic validator to include and verify the KPI row snippet and MAPE badge rules, so that generated artifact HTML meets production constraints and the KPI row behaves correctly in generated artifacts.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Artifact generation agent produces a KPI row HTML/JS snippet that matches the frontend KPIRow API contract and includes meta tags analytiq-artifact-id and workspace-id.
- [ ] Deterministic artifact validator verifies: no hardcoded color hex values in the KPI snippet, aria attributes present, fetch() calls wrapped in try/catch, window.__analytiq_state remains defined and updateFilters used, and file size after minification remains < 250KB.
- [ ] On validator failures, the artifact generation agent runs a repair cycle with targeted fixes and succeeds within maximum 2 repair cycles or surfaces actionable error messages to the user.
- [ ] Generated artifact loads and KPI row fetches the kpi_summary endpoint with embed_token in query string; failed network requests show retry UI and logged errors.
- [ ] Regression tests confirm that KPI row integration does not break click-to-drill or global filter serialization via URL hash.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-037-11: Update artifact generation agent templates and prompts to include KPI row
    - [ ] Modify artifact HTML template generator to include KPIRow and necessary inline JS that binds to window.__analytiq_state
    - [ ] Update agent prompt to require inclusion of KPI metadata and CSS custom properties only (no hex values)
    - [ ] Add example snippet of KPIRow usage in agent prompt to guide generation
    - [ ] Add versioned template test fixtures to ensure reproducible generation
- [ ] TASK-F-037-12: Extend deterministic artifact validator to check KPI row rules
    - [ ] Add validations: detect hardcoded hex colors, missing aria-label attributes in KPI cards, missing meta tags analytiq-artifact-id and workspace-id
    - [ ] Verify all fetch() occurrences inside KPI row snippet are wrapped in try/catch and have retry handlers
    - [ ] Validate that window.__analytiq_state exists and updateFilters usage is present
    - [ ] Add validator unit tests with positive and negative HTML samples
- [ ] TASK-F-037-13: Implement artifact-level integration tests and CI checks
    - [ ] Create end-to-end test that generates an artifact in a staging environment, uploads to object storage, fetches the HTML and runs the deterministic validator against it
    - [ ] Run a headless browser test that loads the generated artifact and asserts KPIRow renders and calls kpi_summary endpoint with embed_token
    - [ ] Add artifact size check after minification and fail CI if > 250KB
    - [ ] Add smoke test to ensure KPI row does not break existing panels (time series) and click-to-drill
- [ ] TASK-F-037-14: Deploy and monitor KPI row artifacts in staging with observability
    - [ ] Deploy updated artifact generator to staging workflow and create sample artifacts for QA
    - [ ] Instrument artifact logs to capture KPI panel fetch latency, error rate, and retry events and forward to observability dashboard
    - [ ] Configure alert if KPI panel error rate > 5% on staging
    - [ ] Run manual QA sessions to exercise period selector, mobile stacking and accessibility
- [ ] TASK-F-037-15: Security and release gating
    - [ ] Run artifact HTML security scan to detect inline event handler usage or localStorage usage and fail if present
    - [ ] Ensure embed_token query param usage follows allowed_origins enforcement on server side
    - [ ] Add release notes and rollback plan for the artifact generator change
    - [ ] Coordinate rollout with workspace ops to re-generate impacted artifacts

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Generator unit tests: ensure agent prompt variations produce expected KPI snippet placeholders
- [ ] Validator tests: run validator against intentionally failing and passing HTML and assert correct repair loop behavior
- [ ] End-to-end test: generate artifact, upload to staging, load in headless browser, stub kpi_summary responses and assert KPI rendering, retry behavior and accessibility attributes
- [ ] Regression tests: load full artifact in staging and verify click-to-drill and time series panel remain functional
- [ ] Release smoke: regenerate a small set of production artifacts in a canary workspace and monitor logs for 24 hours

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Artifact generation agent updated and produces KPI row snippet consistently in staging artifacts
- [ ] Deterministic artifact validator checks for KPI row-specific rules and passes artifacts in staging
- [ ] CI artifact generation + validation + headless render tests are green
- [ ] Staging observability shows KPI panel latency and error rates within acceptable bounds
- [ ] Security scan passes and release notes + rollback plan documented

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-036
- [ ] F-025

###### Agent Notes
- Update the artifact-generation agent prompt to explicitly require the KPIRow component and link the kpi_summary endpoint schema. The agent must not inline numeric data; all data must be fetched at load time. Add deterministic validator checks for KPI row specifics and include targeted repair prompts describing exact fixes (e.g., 'replace hardcoded color #FF0000 with var(--color-danger)'). Ensure the validator returns structured repair suggestions to the agent. Keep generated JS minimal and dependency-free; reuse existing inlined helper utilities for fetch+retry and Intl formatting.

#### Requirement / Feature: Primary time series panel with CI ribbon (actual vs predicted)

**Feature ID:** `F-038`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `frontend, artifact`

**Rationale:** Primary time series is required for MVP artifact preview and core insight.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-036
- [ ] F-025

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-038-01: As an analyst viewing an artifact, I want a primary time series panel that shows actual vs predicted values with a 95% CI ribbon and a residuals subplot, so that I can quickly evaluate forecast accuracy, uncertainty, and recent model performance.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] The panel renders a time series with two lines: actual_value and predicted_value for the selected date range.
- [ ] - [ ] The panel renders a 95% CI ribbon using lower_ci_95 and upper_ci_95 around predicted_value for forecast and historical forecast rows where CI is present.
- [ ] - [ ] A residuals subplot is displayed beneath the main chart showing residual = actual_value - predicted_value for rows with actual_value present, and blank/placeholder for future forecast rows.
- [ ] - [ ] The panel reads live data via fetch() from gold.predictions endpoint; no data is hardcoded into the HTML.
- [ ] - [ ] Date-range brush and zoom are available and update window.__analytiq_state via updateFilters() and re-query the gold API.
- [ ] - [ ] Click-to-drill on any data point cross-filters other panels and populates the drill chip; second click opens drill-through drawer (drawer fetching grain-level rows from gold.predictions).
- [ ] - [ ] Loading skeleton state is shown while API data resolves; API errors display an error card with retry button.
- [ ] - [ ] Chart elements include accessible attributes: svg has aria-label, role='img', focusable elements for keyboard navigation, and all controls have aria-labels.
- [ ] - [ ] The panel's download buttons produce PNG (canvas) and CSV (Blob) exports of the currently visible series.
- [ ] - [ ] Artifact-level validator rules are satisfied for this panel (no hardcoded colors, uses CSS custom properties, file size target respected after minification).

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-038-01: Frontend: Implement primary time series chart component
    - [ ] - [ ] Create a lightweight chart module using Recharts/D3 per artifact engine rules that draws actual and predicted lines and a CI ribbon using provided series.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add residuals subplot component aligned on x-axis and visually linked to main chart.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement date-range brush/zoom that calls updateFilters({ date_range: ... }) and triggers re-render and data re-fetch.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement click-to-drill single-click cross-filter and double-click drill-through drawer integration hooks.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add PNG (canvas) and CSV (Blob) export buttons scoped to visible range and filters.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement skeleton loading UI and an error-card UI with retry action that re-invokes the fetch.
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure all interactive elements have aria-labels, keyboard focus order, and role attributes; add unit tests for accessibility attributes.
- [ ] TASK-F-038-02: Frontend: Integrate with artifact state & artifact agent contract
    - [ ] - [ ] Read artifact metadata (artifact_id, workspace_id) from <meta> tags and pass embed_token from query param to gold API calls.
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure component reads and writes to window.__analytiq_state and routes all filter updates through updateFilters(patch).
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement URL hash serialization for drill state so sharing preserves drill filters.
    - [ ] - [ ] Wrap all fetch() calls in try/catch and bubble deterministic error objects to the panel error UI.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add instrumentation hooks for user interactions (hover, click-to-drill, export) to emit analytics events to the artifact telemetry endpoint.
- [ ] TASK-F-038-03: Backend: Define and implement panel-specific gold API endpoint
    - [ ] - [ ] Design JSON response schema for /api/v1/artifacts/{artifact_id}/panels/primary-timeseries supporting: times[], actual_values[], predicted_values[], lower_ci_95[], upper_ci_95[], residuals[], split[] and metadata { model_id, feature_manifest_version }.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement endpoint that accepts date_range, grain, filters, and returns aggregated timeseries at the artifact's grain using gold.predictions table.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add server-side validation and JSON Schema enforcement of response shape; return 400 with structured error for invalid inputs.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement caching layer (Redis) keyed by artifact_id + filters + date_range with short TTL (5 minutes) and cache invalidation on artifact refresh.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add structured logging (trace_id, artifact_id, workspace_id, query_duration_ms, row_count) and metrics to monitor endpoint latency and error rates.
- [ ] TASK-F-038-04: Data: Validate gold.predictions availability and CI integrity for panel use
    - [ ] - [ ] Confirm gold.predictions_{model_id} contains required columns: grain_keys, target_metric, actual_value, predicted_value, lower_ci_95, upper_ci_95, residual, split, prediction_date, model_id.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement a pre-query validator that enforces CI integrity check: for non-null CI rows, lower_ci_95 <= predicted_value <= upper_ci_95; log violations and mark rows as suspect in response metadata.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement fallback behavior: if CI columns missing or invalid, API returns predicted_value without ribbon and includes 'ci_available': false in metadata.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add SQL example templates for the endpoint queries and include parameterized safe SQL generation to avoid injection.
- [ ] TASK-F-038-05: AI / Model integration: Ensure model outputs include CI and residuals
    - [ ] - [ ] Confirm Stage 5 writer writes lower_ci_95 and upper_ci_95 for every predicted row (or records nulls when unavailable) and residual when actual_value present.
    - [ ] - [ ] Coordinate with training orchestrator to ensure CI calculation method and serialization matches artifact expectations (95% CI columns exist and are numeric).
    - [ ] - [ ] Add validation tests in model pipeline that flag CI integrity violations (CI integrity gate) and prevent write if violations exceed thresholds.
    - [ ] - [ ] Update model_card metadata mapping so API can return model_id and feature_manifest_version in panel metadata.
- [ ] TASK-F-038-06: Security & Access: Enforce embed token and origin restrictions
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate embed_token (HS256) on every gold API request and ensure token scope includes read access for artifact_id and workspace_id.
    - [ ] - [ ] Enforce allowed_origins check server-side for requests originating from embedded artifacts.
    - [ ] - [ ] Return 401/403 structured error responses for invalid or expired tokens with remediation message.
    - [ ] - [ ] Log token validation events and suspicious origin attempts for security audit.
- [ ] TASK-F-038-07: DevOps / Build: Ensure artifact bundle size and CDN rules
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure panel code uses inline JS/CSS, relies only on cdnjs.cloudflare.com allowed libs, and is included in artifact HTML as per artifact agent contract.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add build-time minification and dead-code elimination to guarantee artifact HTML impact stays within budget; verify compressed and uncompressed size thresholds in CI.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add CDN caching headers and verify artifact HTML served via S3/CDN with invalidation when artifact version updates.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add deployment scripts to run artifact validator as a post-build step and fail build on deterministic validator failures (except auto-repairable ones).
- [ ] TASK-F-038-08: Testing: Implement unit, integration, accessibility, and visual tests for the panel
    - [ ] - [ ] Write unit tests for frontend chart rendering logic and state management (updateFilters integration), mocking API responses.
    - [ ] - [ ] Write integration tests that spin up a test artifact HTML and mock gold API to verify data flows, loading, error handling, and drill interactions.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add accessibility (axe-core) tests validating aria attributes, keyboard focus, and color contrast for the panel.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add visual regression tests (Chromatic or Percy) capturing baseline snapshots and failing on unexpected diffs.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for chart component logic with mocked series (pass/fail criteria: > 90% coverage on component logic).
- [ ] - [ ] Integration test hitting /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/panels/primary-timeseries with realistic mock rows including CI and residuals; validate DOM entries for lines, ribbon, and residuals.
- [ ] - [ ] Accessibility test run (axe) ensuring no critical violations; keyboard navigation and aria attributes verified.
- [ ] - [ ] Visual regression snapshots for light/dark modes and multiple viewport widths; baseline created and approved.
- [ ] - [ ] API contract tests validating JSON Schema compliance and CI integrity metadata behavior for missing/invalid CI.
- [ ] - [ ] Manual exploratory tests verifying click-to-drill cross-filter interactions and drill-through drawer content up to 1,000 rows.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Panel component merged to main branch with code review approved.
- [ ] - [ ] Backend endpoint implemented, tested, and documented in API docs with JSON schema.
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests and accessibility tests passing in CI.
- [ ] - [ ] Artifact validator passes for the generated artifact HTML including panel (no hardcoded color hex, aria attributes present, updateFilters present).
- [ ] - [ ] Performance target: panel renders within artifact page load budget (artifact page load P50 < 3s in CI synthetic test).
- [ ] - [ ] End-to-end manual QA checklist completed and signed off by QA lead.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-036
- [ ] F-025
- [ ] gold.predictions table (Stage 5 write)
- [ ] /api/v1/artifacts/{id} metadata endpoint

###### Agent Notes
- Produce a self-contained client-side component using Recharts/D3 from cdnjs.cloudflare.com. Ensure all fetch() calls are wrapped in try/catch and updateFilters() is used for all filter state changes. Avoid hardcoded color hex values — use CSS custom properties. Keep artifact impact minimal: avoid large helper libraries; prefer small util functions and offload heavy aggregation to gold API. Include meta tags analytiq-artifact-id and workspace-id in generated HTML and ensure panel registers analytics events with the artifact telemetry endpoint.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-038-02: As a backend/data engineer powering artifacts, I want a deterministic, cached, and schema-validated gold API that serves time-series data (actual, predicted, CI, residual) for the primary panel, so that the frontend can reliably render the panel and the artifact validator can verify CI integrity.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] REST endpoint /api/v1/artifacts/{artifact_id}/panels/primary-timeseries exists and returns JSON that conforms to documented schema.
- [ ] - [ ] Endpoint validates request parameters (date_range, grain, filters) and returns 400 for invalid input with structured error payload.
- [ ] - [ ] Endpoint enforces embed_token scope and returns 401/403 for invalid/expired tokens.
- [ ] - [ ] Endpoint computes or aggregates rows from gold.predictions table at requested grain and includes metadata: model_id, feature_manifest_version, ci_available boolean.
- [ ] - [ ] Endpoint runs CI integrity check before returning: report ci_integrity_violations_count and if >0 include a warning in metadata but still return results unless violation threshold exceeds configured block threshold.
- [ ] - [ ] Endpoint responses are cached (Redis) and cache keys respect filters and embed token workspace scoping; TTL default 5 minutes.
- [ ] - [ ] Endpoint logs structured metrics and traces for observability and exposes latency/error-rate metrics to telemetry dashboards.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-038-09: API design and JSON schema
    - [ ] - [ ] Draft JSON response schema including arrays: times[], actual_values[], predicted_values[], lower_ci_95[], upper_ci_95[], residuals[], split[], and metadata { model_id, feature_manifest_version, ci_available, ci_integrity_violations_count }.
    - [ ] - [ ] Publish schema to API docs and add an OpenAPI entry for the endpoint with examples for normal, missing-CI, and error responses.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement server-side JSON Schema validation for both incoming query params and outgoing responses in a middleware layer.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add contract tests that validate sample responses against the schema.
- [ ] TASK-F-038-10: SQL & data access: implement safe SQL generation and aggregation
    - [ ] - [ ] Create parametrized SQL template to aggregate gold.predictions by requested grain and date_range, using warehouse-safe parameter binding to avoid injection.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement row_count tolerance check against expected rows and surface discrepancy in metadata if tolerance > 2%.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement fallback when CI columns are null: mark ci_available=false in metadata and still compute predicted_value and residual where possible.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests for SQL builder covering date ranges, filters, and grain permutations.
- [ ] TASK-F-038-11: Caching, rate limits, and performance
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement Redis caching with keys keyed on artifact_id + filter_hash + date_range + semantic_layer_version; set TTL to 300s default and allow per-artifact override.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add rate-limiting middleware for public tokens to enforce per-artifact request caps (e.g. 100 req/hr for public tokens).
    - [ ] - [ ] Add query timeout and circuit-breaker fallback: if warehouse query times out, return a 503 with user-friendly remediation and capture telemetry.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add CI to measure p50/p95 latency and ensure API target (< 500ms p50) is met in synthetic tests.
- [ ] TASK-F-038-12: Security & token validation
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement embed_token HS256 validation, scope check for artifact_id/workspace_id and token expiry check in middleware.
    - [ ] - [ ] Verify allowed_origins for embedded requests server-side and log mismatches as security events.
    - [ ] - [ ] Return clear HTTP 401/403 JSON errors for invalid tokens and include remediation text (e.g. 'embed token expired; request new token').
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests and penetration test checklist items for token handling and header injection.
- [ ] TASK-F-038-13: Observability & logging
    - [ ] - [ ] Instrument endpoint to emit structured logs (trace_id, artifact_id, workspace_id, user_agent, origin, duration_ms, rows_returned).
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit metrics to Prometheus for request rate, latency histogram, error rate, cache hit/miss ratio.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add distributed tracing propagation for the endpoint and annotate calls to the warehouse and cache.
    - [ ] - [ ] Create a dashboard in Grafana showing p50/p95 latency, error rate, and CI integrity violation counts.
- [ ] TASK-F-038-14: Tests: unit, integration, and contract tests
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement unit tests for parameter parsing, SQL builder, and token middleware (edge cases: invalid date_range, missing grain).
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement integration tests against a test Snowflake/DuckDB fixture with seeded gold.predictions sample data (including CI edge cases).
    - [ ] - [ ] Add contract tests to CI that validate response JSON against the published schema for multiple sample input combinations.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add chaos test to simulate missing CI columns and verify API returns metadata.ci_available=false and uses fallback behavior.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Contract tests verifying response schema for normal, missing-CI, and invalid-input cases.
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests against a seeded test warehouse dataset to validate CI integrity detection and residual calculation.
- [ ] - [ ] Load test to validate cache effectiveness and API p95 latency under expected artifact browsing loads.
- [ ] - [ ] Security test cases for token expiry, origin mismatch, and rate-limit enforcement.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Endpoint merged and deployed to staging with OpenAPI documentation available.
- [ ] - [ ] Contract tests passing in CI against mock responses and against seeded staging data.
- [ ] - [ ] Caching and rate-limiting functioning with observable metrics and alerts for error spikes.
- [ ] - [ ] Token validation middleware unit tests and penetration checks completed.
- [ ] - [ ] Backend integration with frontend panel validated via end-to-end integration test.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] gold.predictions_{model_id} writer (Stage 5)
- [ ] Authentication service for embed tokens
- [ ] Redis cache and Prometheus/Grafana observability stack

###### Agent Notes
- Ensure the endpoint is deterministic and returns reproducible outputs given the same artifact session_spec and filters. Any schema violations should surface as deterministic errors back to the artifact generator agent via the pipeline trace_id so repair loops can act. Keep response payload minimal (arrays of primitives) to reduce artifact bandwidth, and include ci_available and ci_integrity_violations_count in metadata for frontend display logic.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-038-03: As QA and accessibility lead, I want to validate that the primary time series panel meets accessibility, performance, and artifact validation standards so that the panel is production-ready and compliant with product-level artifact rules.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] The panel passes automated accessibility checks with no critical violations (axe-core standards) and manual keyboard navigation test passes.
- [ ] - [ ] Visual regression tests exist with approved baselines for light and dark modes and responsive breakpoints.
- [ ] - [ ] Artifact-level deterministic validator checks pass for the generated HTML including checks: no hardcoded hex colors, meta tags present, window.__analytiq_state present, fetch() wrapped in try/catch, file size < 250KB after minification.
- [ ] - [ ] Performance targets are met in CI synthetic tests: artifact page load P50 < 3s, panel render P50 < 1s when using cached API response.
- [ ] - [ ] Error states and retry behaviors are verified manually and via automated integration tests.
- [ ] - [ ] Security checks confirm that embed token validation and allowed_origins enforcement are present and logging captures invalid attempts.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-038-15: Accessibility testing and remediation
    - [ ] - [ ] Run axe-core automated scan on the panel in artifact HTML and log all violations.
    - [ ] - [ ] Manually test keyboard-only navigation for all interactive controls (brush, zoom, drill, export, retry) and fix tab order and focus ring issues.
    - [ ] - [ ] Verify color contrast for lines, ribbon, and residual markers; switch to CSS custom properties and test both light/dark themes.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add ARIA labels and roles for chart SVG, tooltips, control buttons, and drill drawer; add hidden text for screen readers where needed.
- [ ] TASK-F-038-16: Performance & bundle size verification
    - [ ] - [ ] Run Lighthouse and synthetic page load tests in CI to validate artifact page load P50/P95 targets; iterate on optimizations if targets missed.
    - [ ] - [ ] Measure artifact HTML uncompressed and compressed sizes after minification; ensure < 250KB uncompressed target is met or add tree-shaking to reduce size.
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate API caching effectiveness via synthetic tests and verify panel render time with cached payload.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add a build-time enforcement step that fails the build if artifact asset sizes exceed configured thresholds.
- [ ] TASK-F-038-17: Visual regression and cross-browser tests
    - [ ] - [ ] Capture baseline screenshots for desktop and mobile breakpoints in both light and dark themes and add to visual regression suite (Percy/Chromatic).
    - [ ] - [ ] Add automated cross-browser runs for Chrome, Firefox, and Safari in CI for the panel interactions.
    - [ ] - [ ] Review and approve baselines; triage any failing visual diffs and fix layout/responsive issues.
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate print/PDF export behavior for the panel as part of full artifact PDF export.
- [ ] TASK-F-038-18: End-to-end testing and QA signoff
    - [ ] - [ ] Create end-to-end tests that run a full session: generate artifact, visit artifact HTML, mock gold API, assert panel renders actual/predicted/CI, perform click-to-drill and verify cross-filtering.
    - [ ] - [ ] Execute manual exploratory tests covering edge cases: missing CI, incomplete residuals, large date ranges, slow network, expired embed token.
    - [ ] - [ ] Produce a QA checklist artifact documenting test cases, expected behavior, and screenshots for product review.
    - [ ] - [ ] Obtain QA signoff from QA lead and product owner before marking story done.
- [ ] TASK-F-038-19: Security and privacy validation
    - [ ] - [ ] Run token validation penetration tests to ensure embed_token cannot be forged and allowed_origins enforced server-side.
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate that no PII columns are surfaced in the panel; confirm gold.predictions masking/enforcement policy applied per governance manifest.
    - [ ] - [ ] Verify logs do not contain sensitive token material or raw data; ensure only safe telemetry is recorded.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add security test cases to CI that simulate expired tokens and cross-origin requests to validate correct error behavior.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Automated accessibility (axe) and manual keyboard navigation tests.
- [ ] - [ ] Lighthouse and synthetic load testing for P50/P95 artifact page load metrics.
- [ ] - [ ] Visual regression tests for multiple viewports and themes.
- [ ] - [ ] E2E test using CI pipeline that generates an artifact in staging and validates panel behavior with mocked gold API.
- [ ] - [ ] Security test suite covering token expiry, origin checks, and PII exposure checks.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Accessibility automated and manual checks completed with no critical blockers.
- [ ] - [ ] Visual regression baselines verified and included in CI.
- [ ] - [ ] Performance and bundle size checks passing in CI with defined thresholds.
- [ ] - [ ] End-to-end tests and integration tests passing in CI, and QA signoff recorded.
- [ ] - [ ] Security validation and privacy checks passed and documented.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] Frontend implementation branch for panel
- [ ] Backend primary-timeseries endpoint deployed to staging
- [ ] Artifact validator tooling

###### Agent Notes
- When the artifact generation agent composes the final HTML, ensure the primary time series panel's CSS custom properties are used for colors and that skeleton loaders and error cards are present. The agent should include meta tags analytiq-artifact-id and workspace-id and define window.__analytiq_state plus updateFilters() before any panel script executes. The agent should run deterministic validator and include the primary time series in the validation checklist; any validator warnings should attempt auto-repair (e.g., replace hardcoded color hex with CSS variable) before surfacing to user.

## Sprint 10 - Workspace UI & Save/Share Flow

**Sprint Goal:** Provide preview in chat, artifact list, save-to-workspace and basic manual share dialog.

### Sprint Completion Checklist
- [ ] All P0 stories complete
- [ ] All blocking defects resolved
- [ ] Tests passing
- [ ] Demo-ready increment produced
- [ ] Milestone accepted

### Epic: UI - Artifact

#### Requirement / Feature: Artifact preview in chat (embedded preview modal from session)

**Feature ID:** `F-041`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `frontend`

**Rationale:** UX shows artifact preview immediately after pipeline completes per MVP flow.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-036
- [ ] F-040

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-041-01: As a conversation user, I want to see an embedded artifact preview modal appear in the chat immediately after the pipeline completes, so that I can inspect the generated dashboard artifact without leaving the session.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] When the session pipeline emits an artifact_ready event via the streaming session channel, the chat UI opens a modal containing an embedded preview of the artifact.
- [ ] The preview loads the artifact HTML using an iframe or secure inline renderer and renders the artifact's header + KPI + primary time series panel at minimum.
- [ ] The preview uses a server-generated embed token scoped to the artifact and session and the token is attached to all gold-layer API calls from the artifact.
- [ ] The modal shows a loading skeleton while the artifact HTML and its initial API requests load and shows a clear error card and retry action if the artifact fails to load.
