- [ ] The modal includes a visible 'Save to workspace' CTA that calls the save endpoint (F-040) and is disabled if the user lacks permission.
- [ ] All UI elements in the modal follow accessibility rules: aria-labels present, focus is trapped inside modal while open, modal closable via ESC and close button.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-041-01: Implement session listener for artifact_ready events and modal trigger
    - [ ] Subscribe to the session SSE/SSE-like stream and listen for type: artifact_ready
    - [ ] Parse event payload, validate artifact_id and artifact_url fields, and log receipt with trace_id
    - [ ] Debounce duplicate artifact_ready events to prevent multiple modal opens for the same session run
    - [ ] Add feature flag toggle to enable preview modal in MVP to support progressive rollout
    - [ ] Write unit tests for the event handler to simulate artifact_ready payload variations
- [ ] TASK-F-041-02: Build modal container and UX skeleton in frontend
    - [ ] Create Modal component with focus trap, ESC handler, and close button
    - [ ] Design and implement skeleton loaders matching artifact panels (header, KPI, timeseries)
    - [ ] Add accessibility attributes (role='dialog', aria-modal='true', aria-labelledby)
    - [ ] Implement telemetry events: modal_opened, modal_closed, preview_loaded, preview_error
    - [ ] Create storybook stories for modal states: loading, loaded, error, permission-denied
- [ ] TASK-F-041-03: Render artifact preview inside modal via secure iframe and sandboxing
    - [ ] Implement iframe wrapper with sandbox attributes (allow-scripts, allow-same-origin only as required) and enforced referrerPolicy
    - [ ] Fetch server-side preview embed token endpoint and append token as query param to artifact_url (embed_token)
    - [ ] Implement retry/backoff logic for iframe load errors and show error card with retry button
    - [ ] Ensure iframe width/height responsive behavior and mobile-friendly stacking under 768px
    - [ ] Log iframe load timing and error details to observability backend (trace_id correlation)
- [ ] TASK-F-041-04: Integrate Save-to-Workspace CTA and permission control
    - [ ] Call /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/save or F-040 save endpoint when user clicks 'Save to workspace'
    - [ ] Disable CTA if current user lacks 'Analyst' or higher role; show tooltip explaining permission requirement
    - [ ] Show success toast and update workspace artifact list cache on successful save
    - [ ] Implement optimistic UI state and revert on server error with detailed error message
    - [ ] Add tests covering permission-denied and save-success flows
- [ ] TASK-F-041-05: Add frontend validation, error handling and logging
    - [ ] Validate artifact_url and embed_token presence before attempting iframe load; surface descriptive error if missing
    - [ ] Catch and display CORS/403/401 responses from artifact fetch as a specific error card with remediation steps
    - [ ] Instrument logs with user_id, workspace_id, artifact_id, session_id and trace_id for debugging
    - [ ] Add client-side enforcement of file size warning if iframe reports artifact size header > 250KB (warn user)
    - [ ] Add automated lint rules to ensure no inline event handlers or prohibited globals are introduced in modal code
- [ ] TASK-F-041-06: Testing: unit, integration and accessibility tests for frontend
    - [ ] Write unit tests for modal open/close, focus trap, and error states
    - [ ] Create integration test that mocks SSE artifact_ready, mock token endpoint, and asserts iframe load
    - [ ] Add E2E Cypress test that simulates pipeline completion and verifies Save-to-Workspace flow
    - [ ] Run axe-core accessibility checks on modal and fix any violations
    - [ ] Add regression test to CI that verifies modal not opened for artifact_ready events from other workspaces

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests: event handler parsing, modal component behavior, iframe wrapper logic
- [ ] Integration tests: mocked SSE + token endpoint to assert modal lifecycle and token attachment
- [ ] E2E: full flow starting session → run pipeline (mock) → artifact_ready emitted → modal opens → Save-to-workspace works
- [ ] Security tests: ensure token is not leaked in browser console logs and token is not stored in localStorage/sessionStorage
- [ ] Accessibility tests: axe automated checks on modal; keyboard navigation tested manually
- [ ] Performance: measure time from artifact_ready to first paint of iframe and assert < 3s under test CDN conditions

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Modal opens on artifact_ready events and contains embedded artifact preview
- [ ] Iframe preview loads successfully with valid embed token and handles errors with retry
- [ ] Save-to-workspace CTA calls F-040 and updates artifact list on success
- [ ] All unit/integration/E2E tests pass in CI
- [ ] Accessibility (WCAG) checks pass with no critical violations
- [ ] Telemetry events for open/close/load/error are emitted and visible in observability dashboard
- [ ] Code reviewed and merged, feature flag added for gradual rollout

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-036
- [ ] F-040
- [ ] Backend: embed token generation endpoint (new or existing /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/preview-token)
- [ ] Session SSE streaming infra (type: artifact_ready) is available

###### Agent Notes
- Agent should listen to the session streaming channel and open the preview modal only for artifact_ready events that match the current session_id and workspace_id. Use an ephemeral preview token endpoint that returns a signed HS256 token scoped to artifact_id + workspace_id + session_id. Keep frontend implementation framework-agnostic but follow existing UI patterns for modals and telemetry. Ensure sandboxed iframe usage and minimal iframe privileges.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-041-02: As an analyst interacting with the preview, I want basic artifact interactions (filters applied, drill-through trigger and thumbnail snapshot) so that I can validate results quickly and save or share the artifact without needing to open the full workspace page.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The embedded artifact responds to filter updates originating from the chat modal UI and re-renders all panels via the artifact's updateFilters() entrypoint.
- [ ] A 'Drill' action on the modal opens the artifact's drill drawer inside the iframe (or in a proxied drawer) showing top-level grain rows up to configured limit and a CSV export action.
- [ ] The preview modal includes a 'Save snapshot' action that creates a workspace artifact record (metadata + snapshot thumbnail) by calling the workspace API and returns the artifact_id.
- [ ] Interactions performed in the preview do not persist permanent state to the workspace unless the user clicks 'Save to workspace' or 'Save snapshot'.
- [ ] Filter and drill interactions are debounced to avoid excessive gold-layer API calls and obey rate limits.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-041-07: Implement modal-level filter controls and bridge to artifact updateFilters()
    - [ ] Add a compact filter strip inside the modal: date range, top dimension picker, and a clear-all button
    - [ ] Implement client-side bridge that calls iframe.contentWindow.updateFilters(patch) with structured patch payload
    - [ ] Add input validation on filters to prevent invalid date ranges or unsupported grains
    - [ ] Debounce filter changes (300–500ms) and show a loading indicator while panels re-render
    - [ ] Handle failure cases where updateFilters() is missing: show a fallback message and a 'Open artifact' button
- [ ] TASK-F-041-08: Support drill-through interactions from preview
    - [ ] Expose a 'Drill' button in modal that proxies a double-click drill command into the iframe using postMessage when updateFilters() isn't adequate
    - [ ] If artifact supports drill drawer, ensure drawer opens inside iframe; otherwise, proxy request to backend gold API to fetch top 1,000 rows and render in a modal drawer outside the iframe
    - [ ] Provide CSV export for the drill-through result via Blob API and stream download
    - [ ] Log drill interactions with context (panel_id, clicked_value, user_id) for audit
    - [ ] Add tests for both iframe-internal drill and proxied drill fallback
- [ ] TASK-F-041-09: Implement 'Save snapshot' thumbnail and metadata save
    - [ ] Implement a lightweight server API call to request a thumbnail capture of the artifact URL (server-side headless render) or generate client-side snapshot via canvas fallback if permitted
    - [ ] Post metadata to /api/v1/artifacts (or F-040) with session_spec and thumbnail to create a new artifact record
    - [ ] Handle server errors and show appropriate UI messages with retry option
    - [ ] Ensure snapshot creation respects workspace branding and size rules and returns artifact_id for immediate use
    - [ ] Add backend logging for snapshot creation with storage URI and size
- [ ] TASK-F-041-10: Add throttling and rate-limit protections
    - [ ] Implement client-side throttling for filter changes and drill actions with queueing and exponential backoff on 429 responses
    - [ ] Surface a 'Rate limit reached' soft-warning to users and suggest slower interaction pace
    - [ ] Instrument rate-limited responses in telemetry and add alerting thresholds for high-frequency preview usage
    - [ ] Coordinate with backend to ensure preview tokens have request quotas distinct from persistent embed tokens
    - [ ] Add integration tests to simulate rapid user interactions and verify throttling behavior
- [ ] TASK-F-041-11: Testing: interaction, permissions and integration tests
    - [ ] Write integration tests that simulate filter updates and assert iframe content receives updateFilters() calls
    - [ ] E2E test to validate drill-through drawer rendering via iframe and proxied fallback
    - [ ] Security test to ensure proxied drill payloads respect row-level security and do not leak unauthorized rows
    - [ ] Add tests verifying snapshot creation workflow when thumbnail service returns success/failure
    - [ ] Run manual exploratory testing to validate cross-origin messaging and mobile interaction

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for filter control components and postMessage bridge
- [ ] Integration tests mocking iframe updateFilters() success and missing-hook error
- [ ] E2E Cypress flows for drill-through and Save snapshot
- [ ] Security tests to assert RLS and permission enforcement on proxied drill and snapshot endpoints
- [ ] Load test to validate server thumbnail service under concurrent snapshot requests

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Modal filter controls call artifact updateFilters() reliably and re-render artifact panels
- [ ] Drill-through opens either inside iframe or via proxied drawer and supports CSV export
- [ ] Save snapshot creates a workspace artifact record and returns artifact_id
- [ ] All interactions are rate-limited/debounced and telemetry is emitted for interaction events
- [ ] End-to-end tests covering interactions pass in CI

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-036
- [ ] F-040
- [ ] Artifact engine (stage 6) supports updateFilters() hook in artifacts
- [ ] Thumbnail capture/service or safe client-side snapshot fallback

###### Agent Notes
- When implementing filter bridge, prefer calling the declared window.__analytiq_state.updateFilters API if exposed by artifact. If not exposed, use postMessage with strict origin checking. Thumbnail capture should be optional and resilient — prefer server-side headless capture for accurate thumbnails, but include a graceful failure path that still allows metadata-only saves.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-041-03: As a workspace admin and platform operator, I want the preview flow to enforce access control, token security, logging, and CDN/observability so that previews are secure, auditable, and performant in production.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Embed tokens issued for previews are signed HS256 JWTs, scoped to artifact_id + workspace_id + session_id, and have a short expiry (configurable, default 1 hour).
- [ ] Preview tokens enforce allowed_origins and are validated by the gold-layer API on every request originating from embedded artifact preview.
- [ ] Unauthorized preview attempts (expired token, workspace mismatch, revoked user) return a 401/403 and the modal shows a clear remediation message.
- [ ] All preview events emit structured logs with trace_id, user_id, workspace_id, artifact_id, and are available in observability with linkable traces.
- [ ] CDN and caching headers for artifact HTML are set to allow preview but do not cache preview tokens; server responds with Cache-Control: no-store when token query present.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-041-12: Implement backend preview token endpoint and validation middleware
    - [ ] Add endpoint POST /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/preview-token that validates user permissions and session context
    - [ ] Generate HS256 JWT with claims: artifact_id, workspace_id, session_id, user_id, exp, scope: preview
    - [ ] Store short-lived preview token audit record (token_id, user_id, artifact_id, issued_at, expires_at) in Postgres for revocation logging
    - [ ] Implement middleware in gold-layer API to validate preview token signature, scope, expiry, and allowed_origins header
    - [ ] Write unit tests for token generation, signature verification and negative cases (expired, wrong workspace, revoked)
- [ ] TASK-F-041-13: Enforce allowed_origins, CORS, and cache policies on artifact HTML serving
    - [ ] Ensure artifact CDN origin checks and that server strips token from Referer logs and never emits tokens in Set-Cookie
    - [ ] Set Cache-Control: no-store and appropriate Vary headers when embed_token query param is present
    - [ ] Add server-side validation to reject requests where origin not in token.allowed_origins
    - [ ] Add integration test to ensure public artifact endpoints still work for non-preview scenarios
    - [ ] Document caching and origin behavior for SRE and security review
- [ ] TASK-F-041-14: Add observability, structured logging and audit trail for preview flow
    - [ ] Emit structured audit events for token issuance, preview_opened, preview_failed_load, preview_saved_snapshot
    - [ ] Propagate trace_id from session pipeline to token issuance logs and iframe load logs
    - [ ] Create dashboard tile showing preview usage: preview_count, failures_by_error_type, avg_load_time
    - [ ] Add alert rule for sudden spike in preview errors or token issuance failures
    - [ ] Ensure logs do not contain raw embed_token values (mask tokens in logs)
- [ ] TASK-F-041-15: Security review, hardening and pen-test steps for preview functionality
    - [ ] Perform threat model review for iframe embedding and postMessage communication
    - [ ] Run automated secrets-scan and ensure no tokens are written to localStorage/sessionStorage or console
    - [ ] Conduct focused penetration test on preview token issuance and gold-layer API validation
    - [ ] Remediate findings and document risk mitigations in security notes
    - [ ] Obtain sign-off from Security lead before enabling preview feature flag in production
- [ ] TASK-F-041-16: Testing: backend and infra tests for token lifecycle and error handling
    - [ ] Write unit tests for preview token endpoint covering happy path and failure modes
    - [ ] Integration tests that simulate expired token and workspace mismatch returning proper 401/403 payload
    - [ ] Load test token endpoint under concurrent preview requests and ensure DB write latency is within SLA
    - [ ] Add automated CI step verifying that artifact HTML served with token query param includes Cache-Control: no-store
    - [ ] Document roll-back procedure if preview token service causes auth regressions

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for token generation and middleware validation
- [ ] Integration tests for end-to-end preview request with valid/invalid tokens
- [ ] Penetration test focused on iframe token leakage and postMessage origin spoofing
- [ ] Load test for preview-token endpoint to ensure issuance under concurrent demand
- [ ] Observability tests: validate trace_id propagation from session -> token -> iframe load

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Preview token endpoint implemented and tested; tokens are HS256 signed and short-lived
- [ ] Gold-layer API validates tokens and allowed_origins and rejects unauthorized requests
- [ ] CDN and server caching policies are set to avoid token caching
- [ ] Structured logging and observability dashboards for preview flow are in place
- [ ] Security review completed and remediation applied; feature flag gated until sign-off
- [ ] All backend unit/integration/load tests pass in CI

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-036
- [ ] Auth/ABAC service for user role checks
- [ ] Gold layer API middleware for token validation and allowed_origins enforcement
- [ ] SRE for CDN/cache header configuration

###### Agent Notes
- Token lifecycle should be short to reduce blast radius; include token_id and revocation record to support immediate revocation if needed. Validate origin on server side rather than relying purely on iframe sandbox. Mask tokens in logs and never persist them. Ensure preview token endpoint enforces the same ABAC rules as artifact retrieval to prevent cross-workspace data leaks.

### Epic: Workspace

#### Requirement / Feature: Workspace artifact list screen (thumbnails, owner, last updated)

**Feature ID:** `F-042`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `frontend`

**Rationale:** Workspace needs a central view of artifacts; mockups provide list layout for MVP.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-040

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-042-01: As a workspace member, I want a paginated artifact list that shows each artifact's thumbnail, owner, last updated timestamp, and basic DQ/share status, so that I can quickly discover and open relevant artifacts.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] List endpoint returns artifacts ordered by last_updated desc by default and supports page & page_size parameters.
- [ ] Each list item displays: thumbnail image (or placeholder), artifact name, owner display name, last_updated (humanized and ISO), and DQ status badge (PASS|WARN|FAIL).
- [ ] Thumbnails load progressively with skeleton placeholders and have accessible alt text 'Artifact thumbnail for {artifact_name}'.
- [ ] If thumbnail fetch fails, UI shows a cached placeholder and logs the error; user can retry via a per-item refresh action.
- [ ] Non-members or users without artifacts:list permission receive 403 from API; frontend surfaces a clear 'Access denied' state.
- [ ] API and UI surface link to artifact detail page / artifact HTML URL; clicking item opens artifact in new tab with appropriate embed token flow (out of scope for embedding but link must be present).

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-042-01: Backend: Implement artifacts list API with thumbnails, owner, last_updated, DQ status, pagination and permission checks
    - [ ] Add SQL query to artifacts metadata table selecting artifact_id, name, owner_id, owner_display_name, updated_at, dq_monitor.status, thumbnail_path, html_size_bytes; support ORDER BY updated_at DESC and pagination (limit/offset)
    - [ ] Implement API handler GET /api/v1/artifacts with optional query params: page, page_size, q (search), order_by, sort_direction; validate query params and apply defaults
    - [ ] Enforce authorization: verify caller has workspace membership and artifacts:read permission; return 403 when not authorized and log attempt
    - [ ] Attach pre-signed thumbnail URL from object storage (S3) with short expiry; on missing thumbnail set thumbnail_url to null
    - [ ] Add structured logging: request_id, workspace_id, user_id, query_params, result_count; log errors with stack/trace
    - [ ] Write unit tests for pagination, permission denial, and thumbnail_url generation
    - [ ] Document API contract in OpenAPI spec with response schema matching workspace artifact record subset
- [ ] TASK-F-042-02: Frontend: Build ArtifactList React component showing items with thumbnail, owner, last updated and DQ badge
    - [ ] Create ArtifactList container that calls GET /api/v1/artifacts with pagination and maps API response to UI models
    - [ ] Implement ArtifactCard component rendering image (with img src), artifact title, owner avatar/name, last updated (relative + hover ISO), DQ badge and share status icon
    - [ ] Add skeleton loaders for initial fetch and shimmer for thumbnail area
    - [ ] Implement error states: global fetch error (retry button) and per-item thumbnail error (fallback placeholder + retry thumbnail button)
    - [ ] Add keyboard accessibility: list items focusable, Enter opens artifact, aria-labels for thumbnails and buttons, and role=list/role=listitem
    - [ ] Add unit & component tests (Jest + React Testing Library) for render states, accessibility attributes, and click behavior
- [ ] TASK-F-042-03: Data/Backend: Generate and store artifact thumbnails during artifact creation/refresh pipeline
    - [ ] Update artifact generation job (Stage 6 agent output handler) to create a 320x180 thumbnail PNG from the artifact HTML render pipeline using headless browser (Puppeteer) or snapshot tool
    - [ ] Apply image optimization: convert to WebP (fallback PNG), max file size target 20KB for thumbnail, and store in S3 under artifacts/{artifact_id}/thumbnail.webp
    - [ ] On thumbnail generation failure, retry up to 2 times with exponential backoff and write failure_reason to artifact metadata
    - [ ] Expose thumbnail_path in artifact metadata table and set thumbnail_generated_at timestamp
    - [ ] Write integration tests for thumbnail generation job and S3 upload (mocked) and add monitoring metric for thumbnail_generation_failures
- [ ] TASK-F-042-04: DevOps/CDN: Serve thumbnails via CDN with caching and invalidation on artifact refresh
    - [ ] Add S3 origin path and CloudFront (or equivalent CDN) behavior for /artifacts/{artifact_id}/thumbnail.* with Cache-Control max-age 3600
    - [ ] Implement cache invalidation hook in artifact refresh pipeline to invalidate CDN object when thumbnail is regenerated
    - [ ] Provision IAM role with least privilege for thumbnail upload and CDN invalidation; rotate keys per secrets policy
    - [ ] Add monitoring alert for CDN 5xx rate > 1% and S3 upload errors > 0.5% in 15min window
    - [ ] Document operational runbook for thumbnail cache invalidation and troubleshooting
- [ ] TASK-F-042-05: Security: Ensure thumbnails and artifact metadata respect workspace access controls
    - [ ] Make sure pre-signed thumbnail URLs include workspace_id claim and short TTL (5 minutes) to prevent long-lived access
    - [ ] Enforce server-side checks: any direct gold/API endpoints referenced in artifact metadata must not leak credentials; thumbnails are static assets with no sensitive data
    - [ ] Add audit log entry for thumbnail URL generation and artifact list API access including actor_id and timestamp
    - [ ] Perform threat model review focusing on public thumbnail enumeration and ensure public artifacts only expose allowed fields
    - [ ] Add automated test to assert that non-members cannot obtain thumbnail_url in API response
- [ ] TASK-F-042-06: Testing: End-to-end and QA tests for artifact list feature
    - [ ] Create E2E Cypress tests covering: successful list load, pagination navigation, thumbnail fallback, permission denied state, and opening artifact detail link
    - [ ] Add accessibility (axe) checks in E2E to assert aria attributes, keyboard navigation, and color contrast for badges
    - [ ] Add API integration tests to validate pagination, order_by updated_at, and correct DQ badge mapping
    - [ ] Add performance test to assert first-page API response < 300ms for 50 artifacts stored (mocked), and thumbnail load time < 200ms from CDN (simulated)
    - [ ] Run test matrix in CI for Chrome and Firefox headless

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests: backend handlers (pagination, permission), frontend components (render states), thumbnail generation unit mocks
- [ ] Integration tests: API -> DB metadata -> S3 pre-signed URL flow with mocked storage
- [ ] E2E tests: Cypress flows for list load, pagination, thumbnail fallback, open artifact link, and access denied
- [ ] Accessibility tests: axe rules for aria labels, keyboard navigation and color contrast
- [ ] Performance tests: measure API latency and thumbnail load times in CI synthetic environment
- [ ] Security tests: verify thumbnail URL TTL enforcement and unauthorized access produces 403

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] GET /api/v1/artifacts returns paginated artifact list with thumbnail_url or null and passes backend unit/integration tests
- [ ] ArtifactList UI component renders correctly for empty, loading, success, and error states and passes frontend unit tests and accessibility checks
- [ ] Thumbnail generation job runs as part of artifact generation and stores optimized thumbnail in S3; CDN serves thumbnail with cache invalidation on refresh
- [ ] Authorization enforced end-to-end; 403 states handled in UI with explanatory copy
- [ ] Observability: logs, metrics and alerts configured for API errors, thumbnail generation failures and CDN errors
- [ ] Documentation updated: API OpenAPI spec, frontend component usage, operational runbook for cache invalidation

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-040
- [ ] Existing artifact generation pipeline (Stage 6) for thumbnail hook
- [ ] Object storage (S3) and CDN provisioned in infra

###### Agent Notes
- Backend must expose thumbnail_url but not embed thumbnails in artifact HTML; keep thumbnail generation lightweight (headless browser snapshot) and optimized to WebP. Use pre-signed short lived URLs and CDN caching with invalidation hook. Coordinate with Stage 6 artifact generator to call thumbnail job after artifact HTML is produced. Prefer offset/limit pagination for MVP; add cursor pagination if scale requires. Instrument logs with request_id and trace_id for correlation with pipeline traces.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-042-02: As an artifact owner, I want the artifact list to show ownership and last-updated so I can know who to contact and whether artifacts are stale, and I want quick actions (open, refresh thumbnail, view history) to be available from the list.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each artifact card shows owner avatar (or initials), owner name, and 'last updated' formatted relative (e.g., '3 days ago') and full ISO on hover
- [ ] Hover/focus state reveals quick actions: Open artifact (opens new tab), Refresh thumbnail (triggers regenerate thumbnail job, returns 202 accepted and UI shows transient 'regenerating' state), View version history link
- [ ] Refresh thumbnail action enforces owner or workspace-admin permission; unauthorized users receive inline tooltip 'Permission required' and 403 logged
- [ ] Owner name links to user profile (workspace context) page; link present only if user has permission to view profiles
- [ ] Action buttons are reachable by keyboard and have aria-labels; action failures surface user-friendly error message and a retry option

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-042-07: Frontend: Add owner display and quick action controls to ArtifactCard
    - [ ] Extend ArtifactCard to include owner avatar (with fallback initials) and owner display name with profile link when permitted
    - [ ] Implement quick actions toolbar revealed on hover/focus with buttons: Open, Refresh thumbnail, Version history
    - [ ] Hook Refresh thumbnail button to POST /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/thumbnail/regenerate and show optimistic UI state 'Regenerating…' with polling for thumbnail_generated_at update
    - [ ] Implement keyboard navigation and aria-labels for each quick action; add tooltip text for disabled states
    - [ ] Add component tests for quick actions visibility and permission disabled state
- [ ] TASK-F-042-08: Backend: Add thumbnail regenerate endpoint with permission checks and queued job dispatch
    - [ ] Create POST /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/thumbnail/regenerate endpoint that verifies caller is artifact owner or workspace admin; return 202 Accepted with job_id
    - [ ] Enqueue thumbnail generation job to the existing job queue (Redis/BullMQ) and set artifact.thumbnail_regeneration_requested_at
    - [ ] Validate artifact exists and is in workspace; return 404 for missing artifact; log all invalid attempts
    - [ ] Record audit log entry {actor, artifact_id, action: 'thumbnail_regenerate_request', job_id, timestamp}
    - [ ] Add unit tests for permission denial, queue enqueue, and response shape
- [ ] TASK-F-042-09: Backend/Jobs: Implement regenerate worker behavior and idempotency
    - [ ] Worker picks up regenerate job, fetches latest artifact HTML from artifact storage, generates thumbnail using same pipeline, uploads to S3 and updates artifact metadata (thumbnail_path, thumbnail_generated_at)
    - [ ] Ensure idempotency by checking job metadata and preventing duplicate concurrent regeneration for same artifact_id (use distributed lock)
    - [ ] On failure mark job as failed and write failure_reason; retry policy: up to 2 retries with backoff
    - [ ] Emit metrics: thumbnail_regenerate_started, succeeded, failed and job_duration_seconds
    - [ ] Add integration test that simulates regenerate API call and validates metadata update (mock storage)
- [ ] TASK-F-042-10: UX/Copy: Design error and permission messaging for quick actions
    - [ ] Define copy for permission denied: 'You must be the artifact owner or workspace admin to regenerate thumbnails.'
    - [ ] Define copy for regeneration success: 'Thumbnail regeneration queued; refresh will appear within a minute.'
    - [ ] Design inline toasts for success/error and a non-modal banner for regeneration in progress
    - [ ] Add small 'i' tooltip next to last-updated explaining refresh schedule and DQ refresh behavior
    - [ ] Add tests verifying copy appears in correct states
- [ ] TASK-F-042-11: Testing: Verify quick actions workflows and permission boundaries
    - [ ] E2E test where artifact owner triggers thumbnail regeneration and UI shows 'Regenerating' and subsequent thumbnail update
    - [ ] E2E test where non-owner triggers regeneration and receives 403 with tooltip; verify no job enqueued
    - [ ] Unit tests for regenerate endpoint authorization logic and audit log generation
    - [ ] Accessibility tests ensuring quick action toolbar is reachable and announced by screen readers

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for frontend permission UI and backend regenerate endpoint
- [ ] Integration tests for queue enqueue -> worker -> S3 update -> metadata change
- [ ] E2E tests for owner and non-owner flows covering success and failure scenarios
- [ ] Accessibility tests for quick actions and owner links
- [ ] Log/metric validation: audit log entries and job metrics present after actions

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Owner display and quick actions implemented and tested in UI with keyboard accessibility
- [ ] POST /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/thumbnail/regenerate endpoint implemented, authorized, enqueues job and returns 202
- [ ] Regenerate worker updates artifact metadata and CDN invalidation runs; UI polling detects new thumbnail and clears regenerating state
- [ ] Audit logs created for regenerate requests and permission-denied attempts
- [ ] Documentation updated for owner quick actions and permission model

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-040
- [ ] TASK-F-042-03 (thumbnail generation job)
- [ ] Artifact metadata table and audit log infrastructure

###### Agent Notes
- Regeneration must be queued, not synchronous. Use distributed locks to avoid duplicate work. Keep regenerate endpoint idempotent and return job_id for tracing. Frontend should poll artifact metadata every 5s for up to 60s after regenerate action to surface the updated thumbnail, but back off if job takes longer and show link to job status.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-042-03: As an operator/QA, I want the artifact list API and UI to handle large workspaces (pagination, search, filtering by owner/DQ status) reliably and log errors and metrics, so that the list scales and incidents are traceable.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] List API supports page, page_size (capped at 200), q (name/description full-text), owner_id filter, and dq_status filter, with server-side validation
- [ ] API returns total_count and page metadata to enable UI pagination controls; responses include trace_id for correlating logs
- [ ] UI supports search by artifact name, filter by owner, and filter by DQ badge; applying filters updates query params in URL for linkability
- [ ] Server-side rate limiting applies: 60 requests/min per user to /api/v1/artifacts; 429 returned with Retry-After when exceeded
- [ ] All API errors and thumbnail generation failures emit structured logs and metrics (error counts, latencies); alerts configured for error rate and latency breaches

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-042-12: Backend: Add search, filtering, total_count, trace_id and rate limiting to artifacts list API
    - [ ] Implement full-text search on artifacts.name and artifacts.description using DB text index or search service (Postgres trigram / tsvector) and parameterized q param
    - [ ] Add filters owner_id and dq_status to SQL builder with safe parameter binding to prevent injection
    - [ ] Return response envelope: { items: [...], page: N, page_size: M, total_count: T, trace_id: UUID }
    - [ ] Integrate request_id/trace_id generation and propagate to logs and downstream services
    - [ ] Integrate rate limiter middleware (per-user) with 429 on exceed and set Retry-After header; write unit tests for limiter behavior
    - [ ] Add DB explain plan review for the search query and add indexes as needed to meet performance targets
- [ ] TASK-F-042-13: Frontend: Add search input, owner & DQ filters, URL param sync and debounced queries
    - [ ] Add search input with 300ms debounce that updates URL query param q and triggers API call
    - [ ] Add owner filter dropdown (populated from workspace members API) and DQ status filter chips that update URL and API params
    - [ ] Implement client-side caching of paginated pages to reduce duplicate API calls during navigation (cache TTL 60s)
    - [ ] Show total_count and page controls; handle edge cases when page > total_pages by navigating to last page
    - [ ] Add unit/E2E tests for search + filter + URL sync and for rate limit 429 UI handling
- [ ] TASK-F-042-14: Observability: Add metrics, structured logs, dashboards, and alerts
    - [ ] Emit metrics: artifacts_list_requests_total, artifacts_list_errors_total, artifacts_list_latency_seconds_bucket by workspace and endpoint
    - [ ] Log structured events with trace_id, user_id, workspace_id, query_params and DB latency; capture thumbnail generation failure logs with artifact_id and job_id
    - [ ] Create Grafana dashboard panels for request rate, error rate, P95 latency, and thumbnail_generation_failures
    - [ ] Add alert: increase in artifacts_list_errors_total > 1% over 5m OR P95 latency > 800ms for 5m
    - [ ] Add runbook entry linking alerts to remediation steps and who to contact (frontend/backend owners)
- [ ] TASK-F-042-15: Security/Testing: Penetration and privacy checks for search/filter and thumbnails
    - [ ] Add security tests to ensure q parameter sanitized to prevent SQL injection and XSS in UI
    - [ ] Validate that search results do not expose private artifact fields (session_spec, model_card) in list responses
    - [ ] Run a small pen-test scenario to attempt enumeration of artifacts across workspaces and assert failures for cross-workspace requests
    - [ ] Add unit tests for rate-limiter bypass attempts and for header-based auth edge cases
    - [ ] Document privacy constraints and verify thumbnails do not contain PII before being published
- [ ] TASK-F-042-16: Testing: Load and performance testing for artifact list
    - [ ] Create synthetic dataset of 50k artifacts and run backend load tests to validate API p50/p95/p99 targets under expected concurrent users
    - [ ] Test DB query performance with realistic filters and add indexes or switch to search service if needed
    - [ ] Run frontend stress test for pagination navigation and client-side caching behavior
    - [ ] Validate CDN serves thumbnails at scale and monitor cache hit ratio; tune Cache-Control headers accordingly
    - [ ] Record results and accept only if P95 response < 500ms for first page under baseline load

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for API param validation, filter logic and rate limiting middleware
- [ ] Integration tests for search SQL correctness and index effectiveness
- [ ] Load tests with 50k artifacts dataset to validate latency and scale
- [ ] E2E tests for frontend search/filter behaviors and 429 handling
- [ ] Monitoring and alert smoke tests to ensure alerts fire on injected error conditions

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Artifacts list API supports search, filters, pagination with total_count and returns trace_id; passes unit and integration tests
- [ ] Frontend search and filters implemented with URL sync and debounced queries; passes E2E and accessibility tests
- [ ] Rate limiting present and returns 429 with Retry-After; test coverage for limiter included
- [ ] Observability: metrics and dashboards deployed; alerts configured and verified via simulated error injection
- [ ] Performance testing completed against synthetic dataset and DB tuned/indexed to meet latency targets

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-040
- [ ] Workspace members API for owner list
- [ ] Metrics/logging infra and RBAC service

###### Agent Notes
- Use DB-native full-text search (tsvector) for MVP to avoid introducing a new search service. Cap page_size at 200 to protect DB; default 25. Include trace_id in responses for easy correlation in logs and tracing systems. If DB performance is insufficient under load tests, consider moving q to a lightweight search index (Elastic/Meilisearch) in next iteration.

#### Requirement / Feature: Save artifact flow (session → artifact record → store)

**Feature ID:** `F-043`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `frontend, backend`

**Rationale:** Users must be able to persist generated artifacts to workspace.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-041
- [ ] F-040

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-043-01: As an Analyst, I want to save a generated artifact from the chat/session preview to my workspace, so that the artifact is persisted, appears in the workspace artifact list, and is reproducible (session_spec + model card + HTML + metadata).

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Show a 'Save to workspace' CTA in the session/chat preview that is enabled only when artifact generation completed with status 'artifact_ready'.
- [ ] Create an artifact record in the workspace Postgres metadata table matching the Workspace Artifact Record Schema (artifact_id, name, description, owner_id, workspace_id, version, created_at, updated_at, session_spec, model_card, lineage, access, share_links, embed_tokens, refresh_schedule, artifact_url, embed_url, html_size_bytes, dq_monitor).
- [ ] Persist the artifact HTML file to object storage (S3-compatible) under the configured workspace bucket and return a CDN-served artifact_url and embed_url with correct CORS/allowed_origins.
- [ ] Store artifact metadata and returned storage URIs transactionally: either both metadata and HTML persist or neither (atomic write or compensating rollback).
- [ ] Generate and persist a thumbnail (PNG) derived from artifact HTML for the artifact list and store thumbnail URL in artifact record.
- [ ] Set artifact owner to current user and default access to Owner role with explicit ACL row created.
- [ ] Return 200 with artifact metadata from GET /api/v1/artifacts/{id} and 404 for non-existent or unauthorized access.
- [ ] Log audit event 'artifact_created' with actor, trace_id, session_id, artifact_id and storage URIs.
- [ ] Surface user-friendly error states in the UI when save fails, with retry and 'report issue' actions.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-043-01: Frontend: Add 'Save to workspace' flow in chat preview and artifact preview UI
    - [ ] Render 'Save to workspace' button in chat preview header and disable until pipeline emits 'artifact_ready' event
    - [ ] Implement modal to capture artifact name, description, and optional tags when user clicks Save
    - [ ] Show saving progress state and disable duplicate clicks; display success, validation, and error UI states
    - [ ] Call backend POST /api/v1/artifacts to persist artifact and poll for result using SSE or polling fallback
    - [ ] Fetch and render artifact list refresh event on success to add new artifact thumbnail and metadata to list view
- [ ] TASK-F-043-02: Backend: Implement POST /api/v1/artifacts endpoint to create artifact record and store HTML
    - [ ] Validate caller authentication and workspace membership; enforce ABAC: only workspace members can create artifacts
    - [ ] Validate payload contains session_spec_id and model_card_id and artifact_html reference (or request generation if not provided)
    - [ ] Persist artifact metadata row in Postgres with status 'creating' and generated artifact_id (uuid)
    - [ ] Upload artifact HTML to S3-compatible bucket with key workspace/{workspace_id}/artifacts/{artifact_id}/artifact.html and set object ACL to private
    - [ ] Generate embed_url (CDN + artifact path) and persist artifact_url and embed_url to metadata row, update status to 'ready'
    - [ ] Return artifact metadata JSON and trigger audit log entry and artifact list cache invalidation
- [ ] TASK-F-043-03: Data/Storage: Implement HTML storage, thumbnail generation, and CDN invalidation
    - [ ] Write HTML file to object storage with server-side encryption and content-type text/html
    - [ ] Invoke headless renderer (Chromium) to capture 1200x675 thumbnail PNG of rendered artifact HTML in a sandboxed container
    - [ ] Upload thumbnail to object storage and persist thumbnail URL in artifact metadata
    - [ ] Invalidate CDN cache for artifact_url and thumbnail path or publish updated object to CDN origin
    - [ ] Add retry/backoff for storage and renderer failures and emit structured logs for failures
- [ ] TASK-F-043-04: Security: Enforce ACLs and create default embed token generation path
    - [ ] Persist default access entry for owner_id with role=owner when creating artifact record
    - [ ] Validate that embed tokens are signed with workspace secret and contain artifact_id and workspace_id claims
    - [ ] Implement server-side check to enforce allowed_origins when validating embed token calls to gold layer APIs
    - [ ] Do not include PII in artifact metadata; mask any sensitive fields before storing views or descriptions
    - [ ] Log token creation events to audit log and set expiry default of 24 hours; allow admin override via request
- [ ] TASK-F-043-05: DevOps/Infra: Provision object storage, configure CDN and secrets, and ensure monitoring
    - [ ] Provision S3-compatible bucket(s) per environment and configure lifecycle and server-side encryption
    - [ ] Create CDN distribution pointing to object storage origin and configure cache invalidation API access
    - [ ] Store workspace signing secrets in secrets manager and rotate per policy
    - [ ] Add CloudWatch / Prometheus metrics for artifact creation rate, storage errors, and thumbnail generation failures
    - [ ] Define alerting for repeated storage/render failures (>5 failures / 1h) to SRE team
- [ ] TASK-F-043-06: Testing: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests for save flow
    - [ ] Write unit tests for backend validation logic, ACL enforcement, and transactional behavior
    - [ ] Write integration tests that simulate full save flow: POST artifact with session_spec reference, store HTML to fake S3, verify DB record and CDN link
    - [ ] Add E2E Cypress test that saves an artifact from the chat preview UI and verifies artifact appears in artifact list with thumbnail
    - [ ] Add negative tests for insufficient permissions, storage failure, and invalid session_spec to verify proper error messages
    - [ ] Add load test to simulate concurrent saves and ensure system maintains atomicity and does not create duplicate records

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Execute unit tests for backend validation and ACL enforcement
- [ ] Run integration tests with local S3 emulation to verify transactional write behavior and rollback on failure
- [ ] Run E2E Cypress test: generate artifact preview, click Save, fill modal, verify artifact list displays new artifact and thumbnail
- [ ] Simulate storage failure and assert UI shows retry option and no metadata row left in 'creating' state after rollback
- [ ] Verify audit log contains artifact_created event with correct actor, session_id, artifact_id, and storage URIs

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Implement UI button + modal with validations and progress states
- [ ] Implement backend endpoint with transactional metadata + HTML storage and thumbnail generation
- [ ] Persist artifact metadata conforming to Artifact Record Schema and return valid artifact_url and embed_url
- [ ] Enforce workspace ACLs and create owner access record
- [ ] Add audit log entries for artifact creation and embed token issuance
- [ ] Add automated unit, integration, and E2E tests passing in CI
- [ ] Provisioned storage/CDN and monitoring configured with alerts

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-041
- [ ] F-040
- [ ] Endpoint: GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/spec to retrieve session_spec
- [ ] Endpoint: GET /api/v1/models/{model_id} to retrieve model_card

###### Agent Notes
- Assume session_spec and model_card are available from prior pipeline stages (dependencies F-041, F-040). Use trace_id from session SSE stream for cross-system tracing. Thumbnail generation must run in sandboxed headless renderer; ensure no network requests leak credentials. Keep artifact HTML upload atomic relative to DB row to avoid orphaned storage objects.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-043-02: As a Product QA / Owner, I want every saved artifact to pass deterministic artifact validation and, when possible, automatically execute the artifact repair loop (max 2 cycles) before finalizing the save, so that saved artifacts meet accessibility, security, and artifact engine quality gates.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Run deterministic artifact validator on artifact HTML prior to finalizing artifact record; validator must check required meta tags, file size, aria attributes, no localStorage/sessionStorage, fetch() error handling, no inline global event handlers, and CSS custom property color usage
- [ ] Attempt targeted repair up to 2 cycles by invoking the artifact generation agent with repair hints; update artifact HTML and re-run validator after each repair
- [ ] If validator passes, mark artifact status 'ready' and proceed with normal save flow; if validator fails after 2 repair cycles, mark artifact status 'validation_failed', persist validator report, and surface remediation to user with actionable guidance
- [ ] Record deterministic validator results and repair cycle actions in audit log with trace_id and artifact_id
- [ ] Surface validator status and repair progress in UI modal and artifact metadata (dq_monitor) with PASS|WARN|FAIL

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-043-07: Backend/AI Orchestration: Implement artifact validation + repair orchestration service
    - [ ] Implement deterministic validator service that runs the checklist and returns structured validation_report JSON with pass/warn/fail per rule
    - [ ] Implement repair orchestration that calls the Artifact Generation Agent (agent interface) with targeted repair hints describing failed rules and receives repaired HTML candidate
    - [ ] Implement retry loop logic limited to 2 repair cycles and track repair_cycle_count on artifact record
    - [ ] Persist validation reports and repair actions in artifacts.validation_history and audit log
    - [ ] Expose validation API for frontend to poll validation status and to download full validator report
- [ ] TASK-F-043-08: Frontend: Show validation status, repair progress, and final results in Save modal and artifact detail
    - [ ] Display validation progress spinner and stepper while validator runs and during repair cycles
    - [ ] Render per-rule validation results with helpful remediation text and link to 'view raw HTML' for owner/editor roles
    - [ ] Allow user to cancel automatic repair and surface 'Save as draft (validation_failed)' option with warning
    - [ ] Display dq_monitor summary (PASS|WARN|FAIL) on artifact detail and in artifact list DQ badge
    - [ ] Implement UI to download validator report JSON for debugging
- [ ] TASK-F-043-09: AI Agent: Provide repair prompt templates and sandboxed execution for artifact regeneration
    - [ ] Design repair prompt templates that take validator_report and produce minimal HTML fixes (e.g. replace inline color hex with CSS variable, add aria-label, wrap fetch in try/catch)
    - [ ] Run Artifact Generation Agent in isolated environment with allowed toolset and enforce JSON-only outputs for replacement patches
    - [ ] Validate agent outputs via JSON Schema and sanitize HTML before storing or rendering
    - [ ] Log agent request/response with latency and token usage (respecting LLM logging policy) for observability and cost tracking
    - [ ] Fail fast and return deterministic repair failure message when agent output is invalid or missing required meta tags
- [ ] TASK-F-043-10: Security & Compliance: Ensure validator and repair loop do not expose secrets and maintain safe HTML sanitization
    - [ ] Run HTML sanitization to strip any <script> tags that attempt to access cookies or local secrets before running validator/thumbnail renderer
    - [ ] Block requests in artifact HTML to blacklisted domains and log any external network calls discovered during headless render
    - [ ] Ensure repair agent never receives or outputs secrets or workspace database credentials
    - [ ] Add an allowlist for cdnjs.cloudflare.com domains and verify external dependency URLs in repaired HTML
    - [ ] Record PII exposure checks in validator report and block artifact save if PII is embedded in HTML
- [ ] TASK-F-043-11: Testing: Validate validator accuracy and repair loop effectiveness
    - [ ] Create unit tests for each deterministic validation rule with positive and negative test HTML samples
    - [ ] Create integration tests that simulate a failing artifact, run 1-2 automated repairs, and assert final state transitions for pass and fail cases
    - [ ] Add regression tests to ensure repair prompts produce HTML that addresses specific validator failures (e.g. missing meta tags, hardcoded color usage)
    - [ ] Add fuzz tests for repaired HTML to ensure sanitizer removes disallowed constructs
    - [ ] Add monitoring to ensure repair loop success rate and track cases that end in 'validation_failed' for product review

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Run unit tests for each validator rule against curated HTML test corpus
- [ ] Execute integration tests that simulate both auto-repair success and failure, verifying artifact record states 'ready' and 'validation_failed'
- [ ] Run E2E test: create artifact with known failing rules, verify UI shows repair cycles, and confirm final status and audit entries
- [ ] Measure repair loop latency and ensure 95th percentile repair + validation < artifact save timeout (e.g. 60s) or escalate to background job with correct user notification
- [ ] Verify sanitizer strips disallowed constructs and records them in validation_report

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Deterministic validator implemented and returns structured report for each HTML artifact
- [ ] Repair orchestration calls artifact generation agent with repair hints and accepts repaired HTML up to 2 cycles
- [ ] Frontend surfaces per-rule validation feedback and allows owner to save as draft or retry repair
- [ ] Persist validation reports and repair history in artifact record and audit log
- [ ] Add automated tests for validator and repair loop and pass them in CI
- [ ] Ensure sanitizer and security checks prevent secret leaks and block PII in artifacts

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] TASK-F-043-02 (artifact persistence) for storage / status transitions
- [ ] Agent interface for Artifact Generation Agent (Stage 6)
- [ ] Headless renderer infrastructure for thumbnailing and validation

###### Agent Notes
- Validator must be deterministic and not rely on LLMs for rule checks. Repair orchestration should provide minimal diffs/patches to the artifact generator agent and reject any agent output that does not include the required meta tags or violates size or allowed domain rules. Keep repair cycles count and LLM token usage in artifact model_card.repair_cycles.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-043-03: As an Analyst and Workspace Admin, I want saved artifacts to be versioned, scheduled for refresh, and support manual refresh, so that artifacts can be kept up-to-date, have retrievable history, and enable scheduled drift monitoring.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Persist artifact.version using semver and increment on each regeneration; keep previous versions accessible via artifact version history API
- [ ] Allow user to configure refresh_schedule (cron + timezone) during save or from artifact settings; persist schedule in artifact.refresh_schedule
- [ ] Register scheduled refresh with job queue (Redis + BullMQ) and ensure jobs trigger /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/refresh to re-run Stage 5 write and artifact regeneration without altering session_spec
- [ ] Expose POST /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/refresh for manual refresh that runs synchronously for short runs (< 45s) or enqueues async job for long runs with in-app notification on completion
- [ ] Update dq_monitor and artifact.updated_at after each refresh; emit drift_alert audit event when drift thresholds exceeded
- [ ] Create new artifact version on successful refresh and update artifact list thumbnail and artifact_url; maintain previous versions in version history

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-043-12: Backend: Implement artifact versioning and refresh endpoints
    - [ ] Extend artifact metadata schema to include version_history and semver versioning fields
    - [ ] Implement POST /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/refresh that validates caller permissions and enqueues refresh job or performs synchronous refresh based on workload estimation
    - [ ] Implement job handler that runs Stage 5 gold layer persistence and Stage 6 artifact generation and then updates artifact metadata to new version
    - [ ] Implement GET /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/versions to list versions and GET /api/v1/artifacts/{id}?version=x to fetch specific version metadata and URL
    - [ ] Emit audit log entries for refresh start/completion/failure with trace_id and model_card changes
- [ ] TASK-F-043-13: Scheduler/DevOps: Integrate with job queue and ensure reliable cron scheduling
    - [ ] Add job queue consumer for artifact refresh jobs backed by Redis + BullMQ with concurrency limits
    - [ ] Implement scheduled job registration logic on artifact creation/update to register/deregister cron jobs in scheduler service
    - [ ] Implement retry/backoff policy for refresh jobs (max 3 retries) and alerting for persistent failures
    - [ ] Add monitoring dashboards for refresh job success rate, latency, and drift alerts
    - [ ] Document operational runbook for refresh job failures and schedule misfires
- [ ] TASK-F-043-14: Frontend: Provide refresh controls and version history UI
    - [ ] Add 'Refresh now' button on artifact detail page with confirmation modal and progress indicator
    - [ ] Render refresh schedule settings (cron + timezone picker) and allow Owner/Editor to edit and save schedule
    - [ ] Display version history list with timestamp, user, changelog (session_spec snapshot), and link to open a specific version
    - [ ] Show dq_monitor status and drift badges on artifact header and in artifact list
    - [ ] Notify user in-app and via email (if enabled) when refresh completes or fails
- [ ] TASK-F-043-15: Model/Data: Ensure refresh executes Stage 5 persistence and partial Stage 6 generation correctly
    - [ ] On refresh job, re-run Stage 5 gold layer output persistence with same model_card and session_spec (re-score with existing model) and validate row counts and CI integrity gates
    - [ ] If Stage 5 DQ gate blocks, mark artifact refresh as failed, persist dq_monitor alerts, and do not promote new artifact version
    - [ ] If Stage 5 passes, re-run Stage 6 artifact generation agent to produce new HTML and store new artifact version
    - [ ] Update artifact lineage fields with new gold_table and model_registry_uri if changed, and increment version
- [ ] TASK-F-043-16: Testing: Validate refresh scheduling, manual refresh, and versioning behavior
    - [ ] Unit test refresh endpoint permission checks and job enqueueing logic
    - [ ] Integration test scheduled refresh registration flow: create artifact with schedule, verify job created and executed by queue consumer (use time mocking)
    - [ ] E2E test manual refresh triggers Stage 5 and Stage 6, produces new artifact version, and updates artifact list thumbnail and metadata
    - [ ] Simulate Stage 5 DQ gate failure during refresh and assert artifact remains at previous version and dq_monitor records failure
    - [ ] Test concurrent refresh requests and ensure concurrency limits and deduplication prevent overlapping refreshes

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Run unit tests for versioning logic and endpoint permission enforcement
- [ ] Run integration tests to validate refresh job flow and DB updates using test queue
- [ ] Run E2E scenario: save artifact, trigger manual refresh, verify new artifact version and updated thumbnail
- [ ] Simulate DQ gate failures during refresh and assert correct failure handling and no version bump
- [ ] Validate scheduled refresh executes according to cron (time-mocked) and produces expected artifacts

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Artifact semver versioning implemented and previous versions retrievable
- [ ] Refresh schedule persisted and registered with job queue on create/update
- [ ] Manual refresh endpoint implemented with synchronous/async handling and correct permission checks
- [ ] Refresh job runs Stage 5 and Stage 6 and creates new artifact version on success
- [ ] dq_monitor updated and drift alerts emitted when thresholds breached
- [ ] Automated tests for scheduling, refresh, and versioning passing in CI
- [ ] Operational monitoring and alerting for refresh job queue configured

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] TASK-F-043-02 (artifact persistence) for version creation and storage
- [ ] Job queue infrastructure (Redis + BullMQ) and scheduler
- [ ] Stage 5 gold layer outputs and Stage 6 artifact generation agent

###### Agent Notes
- Refresh job should reuse the original session_spec and model_card; do not re-train models during scheduled refresh (Stage 4 not invoked). If Stage 5 persistence fails DQ gates, include detailed dq_monitor report in the artifact record and email owner with remediation steps. Trace_id must flow through scheduled jobs for observability.

### Epic: Sharing

#### Requirement / Feature: Manual share modal (within-workspace share by email/username)

**Feature ID:** `F-044`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `frontend, backend`

**Rationale:** Basic share is required in MVP; full embed/token support is deferred.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-043
- [ ] F-040

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-044-01: As an artifact Owner or Editor, I want to open a manual Share modal, add workspace members by email or username, select a role (Viewer / Editor / Owner), optionally add an optional message, and confirm sharing so that selected workspace members receive the appropriate access to the artifact within the same workspace.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] The Share button on an artifact opens a modal consistent with workspace branding.
- [ ] - [ ] The modal supports searching workspace members by username or email with autosuggest and debounced network calls.
- [ ] - [ ] The modal allows selecting a role per recipient: Viewer, Editor, Owner; role selection enforces permission constraints (only Owners can assign Owner role).
- [ ] - [ ] The modal validates recipient exists in workspace (error if not), and shows inline error for unknown emails/usernames with suggested remediation.
- [ ] - [ ] On confirm, backend share API updates artifact access atomically and returns updated access list; frontend reflects updated share list immediately (optimistic UI with rollback on error).
- [ ] - [ ] Share action is audited (actor, artifact_id, recipients, assigned roles, timestamp) and available in artifact activity feed.
- [ ] - [ ] All user-visible error states (validation, permission denied, network failure, rate limit) are shown with actionable messages and retry options.
- [ ] - [ ] Role constraints enforced server-side (user cannot elevate others beyond their own permitted max role).

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-044-01: Frontend: Design and implement Share modal UI and interactions
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement modal layout per mockup: recipient search input, result list, role dropdown per recipient, optional message text area, confirm/cancel buttons.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement autosuggest search with 300ms debounce, show username/email + avatar + current role badge in results.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add client-side validation: empty recipient, invalid email format, duplicate recipient, role selection rules (disable Owner option if current user cannot assign).
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement optimistic UI update: add recipients to UI pending API response; implement rollback path on failure.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement accessibility: focus trap, keyboard navigation, aria-labels on inputs and role controls, and screen-reader announcements for success/error states.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests for UI components and integration tests (Jest + React Testing Library) covering validation and optimistic update flow.
- [ ] TASK-F-044-02: Backend: Implement /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/share POST endpoint and controller logic
    - [ ] - [ ] Define request payload schema: { recipients: [{ principal_id|username|email, role }], message?: string, notify?: boolean } and register in OpenAPI.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement server-side validation: artifact exists, user authenticated, current user permission check to share, recipients are workspace members, role assignment allowed.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement atomic DB transaction to upsert artifact access records and create audit log entries; ensure idempotency by request idempotency-key if provided.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement server-side rate limiting / throttling per user to prevent mass share spam; return 429 with Retry-After header.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement detailed error responses with codes: 400 (validation), 403 (permission), 404 (artifact/member not found), 409 (role conflict), 429 (rate limit), 500 (server error).
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests for controller logic and integration tests hitting DB (use test DB fixtures).
- [ ] TASK-F-044-03: Data: Schema and persistence for artifact access and audit
    - [ ] - [ ] Create/extend DB table artifact_access: { id, artifact_id (FK), principal_id, principal_type, role, created_by, created_at, updated_at, created_via: 'manual_share' } with proper indexes.
    - [ ] - [ ] Create audit table artifact_share_audit: { audit_id, artifact_id, actor_id, recipients_json, roles_json, message, action, timestamp }.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add DB migration scripts (up/down) and include in CI migration checks.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add DB constraints and foreign keys to enforce workspace scoping (artifact.workspace_id == member.workspace_id) where feasible.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add Postgres row-level triggers or functions to emit events to notification queue (e.g., NOTIFY or enqueue to Redis) after share changes.
- [ ] TASK-F-044-04: Notifications: Email delivery and in-app notification enqueue
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement server-side notification enqueuer that creates notification records for recipients when notify=true; store notification_pref and delivery_channel in payload.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement email template for share invite that contains artifact name, sharer name, assigned role, optional message, deep link to artifact (front-end route), and guidance about access rights.
    - [ ] - [ ] Integrate with existing email service (SES/SendGrid) with retries, exponential backoff, and logging; store send status in notifications table.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement in-app notification creation and surface in workspace activity feed and bell icon.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add tests for enqueuer, template rendering, and simulate send successes/failures.
- [ ] TASK-F-044-05: Security: Authorization, input validation, and logging
    - [ ] - [ ] Enforce ABAC rules server-side: validate current user's maximum assignable role and workspace membership.
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate and sanitize all input fields to prevent injection (no raw HTML in message, escape/strip dangerous chars).
    - [ ] - [ ] Log structured events for every share attempt (actor_id, artifact_id, request_id, recipients, roles, outcome, error if any) to observability pipeline.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add monitoring alerts for anomalous share patterns (e.g., same user sharing > 100 artifacts in 1 hour).
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests for authorization paths and negative tests for role escalation attempts.
- [ ] TASK-F-044-06: Testing & QA: E2E, accessibility, security, and usability testing
    - [ ] - [ ] Write E2E Cypress tests covering full happy path: open modal, search, add recipients, assign roles, confirm, verify access updated and email queued.
    - [ ] - [ ] Write negative E2E tests: invalid recipient, permission denied, network failure, rate limit handling and rollback behavior.
    - [ ] - [ ] Run accessibility (axe) checks on modal and fix violations.
    - [ ] - [ ] Conduct manual exploratory tests for concurrency: two users sharing same artifact concurrently; verify last-writer wins deterministic behavior.
    - [ ] - [ ] Perform basic security testing: input fuzzing for message field, attempt role escalation via crafted requests.
- [ ] TASK-F-044-07: DevOps & Release: Feature flagging, rollout, and monitoring
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement backend feature flag gating for manual share (configurable per workspace) and ensure modal UI respects it.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add new DB migrations to deployment pipeline and ensure zero-downtime migration steps.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add metrics (share_attempts, share_successes, share_failures, email_send_failures) to telemetry and dashboards.
    - [ ] - [ ] Create runbook for email deliverability failures and rollback procedure for migration problems.
    - [ ] - [ ] Coordinate canary deploy to 5% of workspaces, monitor errors and alerting, then full rollout.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for frontend components (validation, autosuggest, role UI).
- [ ] - [ ] Unit and integration tests for backend share controller (positive and negative flows).
- [ ] - [ ] DB migration tests in CI (rollback/up/down).
- [ ] - [ ] E2E tests (Cypress) for complete modal flow including optimistic UI and rollback.
- [ ] - [ ] Accessibility tests (axe) integrated in CI.
- [ ] - [ ] Security tests: permission escalation checks, input sanitization tests.
- [ ] - [ ] Load test: simulate concurrent share requests and rate limiting behavior.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Share modal implemented in frontend matching design and accessibility requirements.
- [ ] - [ ] Backend share API implemented, validated, and tested with CI coverage.
- [ ] - [ ] DB migrations applied and artifact_access/audit tables created with indexes.
- [ ] - [ ] Notifications enqueued and email template integrated with retries.
- [ ] - [ ] Authorization enforced server-side and audited in logs.
- [ ] - [ ] E2E, unit, integration, and security tests added and passing in CI.
- [ ] - [ ] Monitoring dashboards and alerting for share metrics are live.
- [ ] - [ ] Feature flag present and rollout plan executed with rollback path.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] F-043
- [ ] - [ ] F-040
- [ ] - [ ] Existing user/workspace membership service and artifact metadata API

###### Agent Notes
-  - [ ] Use existing /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/share POST contract as canonical if present; otherwise implement new endpoint consistent with OpenAPI standards.
 - [ ] Reuse existing user lookup endpoint for autosuggest; implement server-side fallback to resolve email→user_id if username provided.
 - [ ] Enforce workspace scoping on backend: recipients must be members of the same workspace; return precise 400/403 errors when violated.
 - [ ] Implement optimistic UI but ensure final state reconciles with server response; include operation idempotency-key support for retries from frontend.
 - [ ] Hook audit events to existing observability (trace_id propagation) and include actor/request ids for traceability.
 - [ ] Respect feature flag gating; include telemetry to measure share adoption before removing flag.
 - [ ] Keep modal and API lightweight; do not implement cross-workspace or public link logic here (out of scope).

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-044-02: As a recipient workspace member, I want to receive a clear email and in-app notification when an artifact is shared with me (including the assigned role and link) so that I can access the artifact immediately with the appropriate permissions.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] When notify=true, recipients receive an email within expected SLA (P95 < 2 minutes) with artifact name, sharer, role, optional message, and a deep link.
- [ ] - [ ] Email contains no PII beyond recipient name/email and is safe from HTML/script injection; message is sanitized.
- [ ] - [ ] In-app notification appears in recipient's notification center and links to artifact; clicking deep link opens artifact if user has session; otherwise prompts sign-in then navigates.
- [ ] - [ ] If email bounce or send failure occurs, backend retries with exponential backoff and logs failure; the sharer sees a non-blocking warning in UI about delivery problems.
- [ ] - [ ] Email templates support localization (en-US default) and include unsubscribe/notification preference link.
- [ ] - [ ] Tests cover email send success/failure, HTML sanitization, and deep-link correctness for signed-in and signed-out users.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-044-08: Email: Build and integrate share invitation email template
    - [ ] - [ ] Create responsive, accessible email HTML template with placeholders for artifact name, sharer, role, message, and CTA link.
    - [ ] - [ ] Sanitize message and user-provided content server-side before rendering template to prevent XSS.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add localization keys and default English copy; design for extension to other locales.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests to validate template rendering with edge-case inputs (long messages, special characters).
    - [ ] - [ ] Store email template version in audit record for traceability.
- [ ] TASK-F-044-09: Notifications: Enqueue and deliver in-app notifications
    - [ ] - [ ] Create notification record with type 'artifact_share', payload { artifact_id, sharer_id, role, message } and push to recipient's notification queue.
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure notification appears in UI feed and links to artifact; implement deep linking behavior including sign-in flow.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add tests verifying notification creation, UI rendering, and deep-link navigation both signed-in and signed-out.
    - [ ] - [ ] Instrument notification delivery metrics and error rates.
- [ ] TASK-F-044-10: Email reliability: Integrate retries, error handling, and observability
    - [ ] - [ ] Use existing transactional email provider with documented retry semantics; implement retry policy with exponential backoff up to 3 attempts and then mark failed.
    - [ ] - [ ] Log SMTP/SES provider responses and surface failures to monitoring; send alert on abnormal failure spikes.
    - [ ] - [ ] On persistent failure, update audit record and surface a non-blocking warning to the sharer in UI (e.g., 'Email delivery failed for 1 recipient').
    - [ ] - [ ] Add integration tests that simulate provider transient errors and final failure.
- [ ] TASK-F-044-11: Deep linking & auth: Ensure artifact link resolves correctly
    - [ ] - [ ] Create deep link URL pattern that includes artifact_id and optional short token for UX (no access elevation) and validate server-side on follow-up.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement frontend route that, if user not signed in, redirects to sign-in and then to artifact page after auth; preserve original intent in redirect state.
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate that server-side access check enforces permissions after sign-in; do not bypass server-side ACL checks.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add E2E tests for flow: email click when signed-in, when signed-out, and when recipient has insufficient permissions.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for template rendering and sanitization.
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests for enqueueing notification records and email send orchestration.
- [ ] - [ ] E2E tests that simulate clicking deep link both signed-in and signed-out.
- [ ] - [ ] Failure mode tests for email provider transient and permanent failures.
- [ ] - [ ] Accessibility tests for email HTML (alt text, readable link text).

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Email template implemented, sanitized, and localized keys present.
- [ ] - [ ] In-app notifications created and surfaced with correct payload and deep link behavior.
- [ ] - [ ] Email retry/backoff and failure handling implemented with monitoring.
- [ ] - [ ] E2E tests for email/deep-link and sign-in flows passing in CI.
- [ ] - [ ] Audit records reflect notification send attempts and outcomes.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] TASK-F-044-02 (backend share API)
- [ ] - [ ] Email provider credentials and sending infrastructure (SES/SendGrid)
- [ ] - [ ] Notification center UI

###### Agent Notes
-  - [ ] Keep emails simple and plaintext-fallback friendly; include both HTML and text versions.
 - [ ] Do not include artifact data or actual dataset rows in email (privacy rule).
 - [ ] Deep link should not grant access; server validates session and artifact ACLs on page load.
 - [ ] Reuse existing notification queue/topic and backoff/retry middleware to remain consistent with other transactional messages.
 - [ ] Record email template version and delivery metadata in audit record for troubleshooting.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-044-03: As a workspace admin and engineer, I want server-side safeguards (authorization, validation, audit logs, observability, rate limits) for manual shares so that sharing is secure, auditable, and operationally manageable in production.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] All share modifications are recorded in artifact_share_audit with actor, recipients, roles, message, and timestamp.
- [ ] - [ ] Server enforces ABAC: a user cannot grant a role higher than their max_assignable_role; Owner assignment restricted to Owners/Admins only.
- [ ] - [ ] All suspicious share patterns (mass share, repeated changes) trigger alerts to SRE/Workspace admin if thresholds exceeded.
- [ ] - [ ] Share API is rate-limited per user and per workspace; appropriate HTTP 429 returned with Retry-After when exceeded.
- [ ] - [ ] Observability includes tracing (trace_id propagated), metrics (success/fail rates), and structured logs for forensic analysis.
- [ ] - [ ] Automated tests validate permission boundaries and audit record creation.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-044-12: Backend: Implement authorization and ABAC enforcement
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement server-side check to compute current user's max_assignable_role based on workspace role and ACL policy.
    - [ ] - [ ] Deny any share request that assigns a role higher than allowed; return 403 with clear error code.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests covering various role combinations (Admin sharing Owner, Editor trying to assign Owner, Viewer attempting to share).
    - [ ] - [ ] Document authorization rules in developer docs and OpenAPI spec for the endpoint.
- [ ] TASK-F-044-13: Observability & Logging: Structured logs and tracing
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit structured log events for every share attempt and outcome including trace_id, request_id, actor_id, artifact_id, recipients, roles, and error detail (if any).
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure trace_id is propagated from frontend through backend and into DB audit entry and email send job.
    - [ ] - [ ] Instrument metrics share_attempts_total, share_success_total, share_failures_total, share_rate_limit_hits and publish to monitoring backend.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add Kibana/Logs dashboards for quick triage of share errors and recent share activity.
- [ ] TASK-F-044-14: Rate limiting & abuse protection
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement per-user and per-workspace rate limits using central rate limiting service (Redis token bucket or API gateway feature).
    - [ ] - [ ] Return standardized 429 responses with Retry-After header and human-friendly message in UI.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add alerting rule for unusual patterns (e.g., > 200 share attempts by a user in 1h) to SRE channel.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add tests that simulate burst activity and verify server enforces limits consistently.
- [ ] TASK-F-044-15: Audit & retention: Persist and surface share audit records
    - [ ] - [ ] Persist share action to artifact_share_audit table within same transaction as access changes to avoid inconsistency.
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement API to retrieve artifact activity feed (paginated) for artifact owners and admins; include share events.
    - [ ] - [ ] Define retention policy for audit rows (e.g., keep 2 years) and implement archival job to move old records to cold storage.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add tests for audit record content, retrieval API, and retention job.
- [ ] TASK-F-044-16: Security testing: Penetration and negative tests
    - [ ] - [ ] Perform targeted security tests attempting role escalation via crafted API requests and ensure server blocks them.
    - [ ] - [ ] Run SAST/DAST scans on new endpoint code and remediate findings.
    - [ ] - [ ] Add automated regression tests for any discovered vulnerabilities.

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for ABAC logic with all role combinations.
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests ensuring audit row written in same transaction as access upsert.
- [ ] - [ ] Load tests to validate rate limiting behavior.
- [ ] - [ ] Security regression tests (role escalation attempts blocked).
- [ ] - [ ] Smoke tests for monitoring alerts and dashboard visibility.

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Authorization rules implemented and tested across edge cases.
- [ ] - [ ] Structured logs and tracing integrated and visible in observability systems.
- [ ] - [ ] Rate limiting active and behaving as expected with alerts configured.
- [ ] - [ ] Audit records persisted atomically and activity feed API available.
- [ ] - [ ] Security and penetration tests executed and issues remediated.

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] Identity & access management / user service
- [ ] - [ ] Observability/telemetry infra (tracing, logging, metrics)
- [ ] - [ ] Rate limiting infra (Redis or API gateway)

###### Agent Notes
-  - [ ] Use existing ABAC policy engine where available; otherwise implement deterministic max_assignable_role logic derived from workspace role mapping.
