 - [ ] Ensure audit writes occur inside same DB transaction as permission changes to avoid partial states.
 - [ ] Propagate trace_id to email job workers to correlate email delivery with original request.
 - [ ] Prefer centralized rate limiter (Redis) to keep logic consistent across APIs; avoid client-side-only limitations.
 - [ ] Retention and archival decisions should be configurable per workspace; default retention 2 years to comply with audit needs.

## Milestone F - Workspace Services, Observability & UI QA

- [ ] Artifact record schema and RBAC implemented
- [ ] Basic audit log and API endpoints for manifest/semantic/gold queries exist

## Sprint 11 - Workspace Services & RBAC

**Sprint Goal:** Implement workspace backend services, artifact record API, and basic RBAC.

### Sprint Completion Checklist

- [ ] All blocking defects resolved
- [ ] Tests passing

### Epic: Workspace

#### Requirement / Feature: Artifact record schema & artifact APIs (create/list/get)

**Feature ID:** `F-045`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, api`

**Rationale:** Artifact metadata must be queryable by UI and used for share/refresh actions.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-040

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-045-01: As a workspace service engineer, I want a production-grade artifact record schema and DB migrations, so that artifact metadata (for UI, share, and refresh) is stored consistently, queryable, auditable, and performant.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Schema implements the artifact record fields defined in the PRD (Table 26): artifact_id (uuid), name, description, owner_id, workspace_id, version (semver), created_at, updated_at, session_spec (JSON), model_card (JSON, nullable), lineage (JSON), access (JSON array), share_links (JSON array), embed_tokens (JSON array), refresh_schedule (JSON), artifact_url, embed_url, html_size_bytes, dq_monitor (JSON).
- [ ] - [ ] Database constraints enforced: artifact_id PK, workspace_id + artifact_id unique index, owner_id foreign key (or null-safe), created_at/updated_at not null, jsonb columns validated for shape at application layer.
- [ ] - [ ] Migrations include forward and rollback scripts and run cleanly in CI on a staging DB snapshot.
- [ ] - [ ] Sensitive fields (e.g., embed token secrets) are not stored in plaintext; embed token metadata is stored but token secret storage uses the secure secrets manager or one-way signing; migration includes no plaintext secrets.
- [ ] - [ ] Indexes created for common queries: (workspace_id, updated_at) for listing, (owner_id) for owner queries, and GIN index for session_spec and model_card search where applicable.
- [ ] - [ ] Audit triggers or application-level audit log recorded for create/update/delete operations with trace_id propagation.
- [ ] - [ ] Schema documented in repo (ERD + JSON Schema) and added to API contract reference.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-045-01: - [ ] Design artifact record schema and JSON schema validators
    - [ ] - [ ] Draft SQL DDL for artifact table consistent with PRD Table 26
    - [ ] - [ ] Produce JSON Schema for session_spec, model_card (minimal), lineage and dq_monitor fields
    - [ ] - [ ] Review schema with product, data, and security (include PII gating note)
    - [ ] - [ ] Produce ERD diagram and write README explaining fields and constraints
- [ ] TASK-F-045-02: - [ ] Implement DB migration scripts (forward + rollback)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement migration to create artifact table with specified columns and types (uuid, jsonb, timestamps, etc.)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add indexes: (workspace_id, updated_at), (owner_id), GIN on session_spec/model_card if appropriate
    - [ ] - [ ] Add constraints and FK references where available (owner_id -> users table) with ON DELETE SET NULL behavior
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement rollback migration and validate by running forward+rollback in CI matrix
- [ ] TASK-F-045-03: - [ ] Implement ORM/data layer models and repository methods
    - [ ] - [ ] Add Artifact model to backend codebase (fields typed, JSON fields mapped to typed DTOs)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement repository methods: createArtifact(), getArtifactById(), listArtifactsByWorkspace() with pagination and filters
    - [ ] - [ ] Add DB-level query optimizations (select only necessary columns by API)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests for repository covering happy path and typical DB errors
- [ ] TASK-F-045-04: - [ ] Security & privacy review and PII handling
    - [ ] - [ ] Verify no PII is stored in artifact.session_spec or model_card per LLM data minimization policy; add sanitizer that strips sample data
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure embed token secrets are not written to DB; store only metadata and token_id; use secrets manager for actual keys
    - [ ] - [ ] Add encryption-at-rest configuration verification for DB columns if required by compliance
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests that attempt to inject PII into session_spec and assert sanitizer removes sensitive fields
- [ ] TASK-F-045-05: - [ ] CI pipeline & migration automation
    - [ ] - [ ] Add migration step to CI that runs migrations against a fresh Postgres (or configured DB) instance
    - [ ] - [ ] Add schema linting (sqlfluff or equivalent) and JSON Schema validation step
    - [ ] - [ ] Include migration smoke test that inserts a sample artifact record and asserts expected indexes exist
    - [ ] - [ ] Document deployment checklist for running migration in production (backups, downtime considerations)
- [ ] TASK-F-045-06: - [ ] Observability & logging for DB layer
    - [ ] - [ ] Instrument repository methods to emit structured logs with trace_id and operation (create/get/list)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add DB latency metrics and alert thresholds to monitoring dashboard
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure failed DB migrations emit an alert to the release channel with logs attached
    - [ ] - [ ] Add a query plan check for the listArtifacts query to avoid full-table scans

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for JSON schema validators and repository methods
- [ ] - [ ] Migration integration test: run forward + rollback against a clean DB image
- [ ] - [ ] Schema contract test: validate sample artifact JSON against JSON Schema
- [ ] - [ ] Security test: verify sensitive fields are stripped and embed tokens are not persisted
- [ ] - [ ] Performance test: listArtifacts query returns first page within P95 latency target on synthetic dataset (10k artifacts)
- [ ] - [ ] Failover test: simulate DB connection loss and verify graceful error surface and retry/backoff behavior

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] All schema DDL and JSON Schemas merged into repo
- [ ] - [ ] Migrations pass in CI and rollback validated
- [ ] - [ ] Repository methods implemented and covered by unit tests (≥ 90% function coverage for new code)
- [ ] - [ ] Security review signed off (PII sanitizer verified; embed tokens handled via secrets manager)
- [ ] - [ ] ERD and README documentation checked into docs folder
- [ ] - [ ] Monitoring/dashboard updated and basic alerts created

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] F-040
- [ ] - [ ] Workspace user table (users) migration present in DB
- [ ] - [ ] Artifact storage (S3) configured (for artifact_url expectations)

###### Agent Notes
- - [ ] Schema must match PRD Table 26 exactly for all fields required by frontend and share/refresh flows
- [ ] Keep JSON fields intentionally permissive but validate critical keys (artifact_id, session_spec.session_id, model_card.model_id) via JSON Schema in application layer
- [ ] Avoid storing raw model binaries or secrets in artifact record; store model_card metadata only and link to model registry URI
- [ ] Design GIN indexes conservatively; measure index size impact on production DB before adding more
- [ ] Use trace_id propagation in all DB operations for observability and auditability

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-045-02: As a frontend and API consumer, I want reliable artifact APIs (create, list, get) with validation, RBAC, pagination, and deterministic responses, so that UI and automation can query artifacts for listing, detail view, share and refresh actions.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Implement POST /api/v1/artifacts to create artifact records with server-side validation and schema enforcement; returns 201 + artifact metadata (excluding sensitive token secrets).
- [ ] - [ ] Implement GET /api/v1/artifacts (list) supporting workspace filter, pagination (cursor or page+size), sorting by updated_at, and optional owner_id filter; responses include total_count and page cursors where applicable.
- [ ] - [ ] Implement GET /api/v1/artifacts/{id} returning artifact metadata and artifact_url/embed_url if caller authorized; 404 for not found, 403 for unauthorized workspace access.
- [ ] - [ ] All endpoints enforce ABAC per PRD (workspace membership + resource-level ACLs) and return proper HTTP codes (400,401,403,404,429,500).
- [ ] - [ ] Input validation with descriptive error messages and JSON Schema validation for session_spec and model_card minimal structure.
- [ ] - [ ] Structured logging and tracing present for each API call including trace_id, user_id, workspace_id, latency, and outcome.
- [ ] - [ ] Unit, integration, and contract tests cover happy path and error cases; API docs (OpenAPI/Swagger) added.
- [ ] - [ ] Rate limiting and basic DoS protection applied at API gateway; 429 returned when exceeded.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-045-07: - [ ] Implement API routes and controllers for create/list/get
    - [ ] - [ ] Define request/response DTOs and OpenAPI path specs for POST /api/v1/artifacts, GET /api/v1/artifacts, GET /api/v1/artifacts/{id}
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement controller logic with input validation and error mapping
    - [ ] - [ ] Wire controllers to repository methods implemented in US-F-045-01
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement response sanitization to exclude secrets and enforce access rules
- [ ] TASK-F-045-08: - [ ] Implement authorization (ABAC) and RBAC enforcement
    - [ ] - [ ] Integrate with existing auth service to resolve user -> workspace membership and roles
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement resource ACL checks per artifact.access array (viewer|editor|owner) and workspace defaults
    - [ ] - [ ] Add tests for role matrix: workspace admin, owner, editor, viewer, external user
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure GET artifact hides sensitive fields for roles without access (embed_url returned only when allowed)
- [ ] TASK-F-045-09: - [ ] Validation, error handling, and logging
    - [ ] - [ ] Wire JSON Schema validators for payloads and return consistent 400 error payloads on validation failure
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement centralized error handler mapping exceptions to proper HTTP codes
    - [ ] - [ ] Add structured logs (trace_id, user_id, workspace_id, route, input_size, latency, error_code)
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure 5xx errors include sanitized stack traces in logs but generic messages to clients
- [ ] TASK-F-045-0A: - [ ] Pagination, filtering, and sorting on list endpoint
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement cursor-based pagination (preferred) with next_cursor in response; fallback to page+size if runtime constraints
    - [ ] - [ ] Support filters: workspace_id (required), owner_id (optional), name search (case-insensitive, indexed), tag filtering if present
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit and integration tests to validate pagination edge cases (empty sets, last page, invalid cursors)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add limit enforcement (max page size) and return 400 when exceeded
- [ ] TASK-F-045-0B: - [ ] Tests, API contract and docs
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests for controllers and auth rules (mocked auth)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add integration tests running against test DB instance covering create/list/get happy and error cases
    - [ ] - [ ] Add contract tests ensuring API responses conform to OpenAPI spec
    - [ ] - [ ] Publish OpenAPI spec and add examples for frontend consumption (Postman/Insomnia collection)
- [ ] TASK-F-045-0C: - [ ] Operational concerns: rate limiting, metrics, and alerts
    - [ ] - [ ] Add metrics emission for request count, success/failure counts, and latency histogram for each route
    - [ ] - [ ] Configure API gateway rate limiting for artifact endpoints and test 429 behavior
    - [ ] - [ ] Add alerting rule for 5xx error spike on artifact endpoints

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for controller validation, auth rules, and repository integration mocks
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests: end-to-end create -> get -> list using test DB and test user accounts
- [ ] - [ ] Contract tests against OpenAPI spec; fail CI on spec drift
- [ ] - [ ] Security tests: role matrix tests, attempt unauthorized access and assert 403
- [ ] - [ ] Load test: burst create requests to measure DB write capacity and API gateway throttling
- [ ] - [ ] Mutation tests: feed malformed session_spec / model_card JSON and assert 400 responses

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] API endpoints implemented and passing unit and integration tests
- [ ] - [ ] OpenAPI/Swagger documentation added and validated by contract tests
- [ ] - [ ] RBAC/ABAC enforcement validated by tests and security review
- [ ] - [ ] Structured logging and trace_id propagation implemented
- [ ] - [ ] Performance gate: list endpoint returns first page within 500ms median on staging dataset
- [ ] - [ ] Rate limiting and monitoring configured

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] US-F-045-01 (artifact schema/migrations)
- [ ] - [ ] Auth service (OAuth2 / user->workspace membership) available
- [ ] - [ ] CI integration for running integration tests with test DB

###### Agent Notes
- - [ ] API must return stable artifact metadata shape; clients will depend on fields like artifact_url, embed_url, version, and dq_monitor
- [ ] Keep create endpoint idempotent by accepting client-provided artifact_id (uuid) and returning existing record if present to support retry logic
- [ ] Use cursor-based pagination for scalability; include total_count only as an optional expensive operation
- [ ] Implement optimistic concurrency on artifact updates via version (semver) or updated_at checks to avoid lost updates
- [ ] Ensure trace_id is accepted via header and propagated; log it with every event for downstream tracing

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-045-03: As a product QA lead and frontend engineer, I want the artifact APIs to be consumable by the UI and include the metadata required for share and refresh workflows, so that the workspace UI can list artifacts, open artifact detail, and drive share/refresh actions without additional backend changes.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Artifact list API provides fields required by UI thumbnail and list (artifact_id, name, owner_id, workspace_id, updated_at, artifact_url, html_size_bytes, dq_monitor.status, model_card.model_id optional).
- [ ] - [ ] Artifact get API returns session_spec and lineage fields sufficient to populate the 'View lineage' and 'Open in session' flows (session_spec.session_id, session_spec.semantic_layer_version, lineage.gold_table, lineage.model_id).
- [ ] - [ ] Create API persists refresh_schedule and dq_monitor initial object so scheduled refresh service can pick records without schema changes.
- [ ] - [ ] Frontend stub pages implemented to consume list/get APIs: artifact list with pagination and artifact detail page showing metadata and 'Open artifact' button that loads artifact_url in iframe using embed_token workflow (embed token generation is out-of-scope but metadata must be present to support it).
- [ ] - [ ] E2E tests validate UI can call list and get endpoints and render expected fields; mock embed flow verifies iframe src built correctly from artifact.embed_url.
- [ ] - [ ] Error states displayed on UI for 401/403/404 and retry behavior implemented.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-045-0D: - [ ] Frontend: implement artifact list & detail consumers (stubs)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement ArtifactList component that calls GET /api/v1/artifacts with workspace_id and renders thumbnail, name, owner, updated_at, dq status
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement ArtifactDetail component that calls GET /api/v1/artifacts/{id} and displays metadata, lineage, and session_spec summary
    - [ ] - [ ] Add loading, empty state, and error UI states with retry buttons
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit and snapshot tests for new components
- [ ] TASK-F-045-0E: - [ ] Backend: expose minimal metadata optimized payloads for UI
    - [ ] - [ ] Add a lightweight projection endpoint or query param (fields=thumbnail,name,updated_at,dq_monitor) to reduce payload size for list view
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement server-side thumbnail preview URL generation (if stored) or placeholder logic
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure list endpoint supports sorting by updated_at desc for UI default
- [ ] TASK-F-045-0F: - [ ] QA: end-to-end and contract tests for UI workflows
    - [ ] - [ ] Create E2E test that authenticates a test user, lists artifacts, opens detail, and verifies iframe src built from embed_url
    - [ ] - [ ] Add API contract assertions in E2E tests to ensure UI-facing fields do not change unexpectedly
    - [ ] - [ ] Add accessibility checks for the artifact list/detail pages (aria attributes, keyboard nav)
- [ ] TASK-F-045-0G: - [ ] DevOps: deploy UI and backend stubs to staging and run smoke tests
    - [ ] - [ ] Deploy backend feature branch to staging environment with DB migration applied
    - [ ] - [ ] Deploy frontend feature branch that points to staging API endpoints
    - [ ] - [ ] Run automated smoke tests including list -> detail -> load iframe (mocked embed token) and report failures
- [ ] TASK-F-045-0H: - [ ] Testing: security and performance checks for UI flows
    - [ ] - [ ] Perform manual exploratory testing for authorization edge cases (cross-workspace access attempts)
    - [ ] - [ ] Performance test: artifact list page load end-to-end under simulated 100 concurrent users
    - [ ] - [ ] Run accessibility linting and address critical failures

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] E2E test: authentication -> list -> detail -> open iframe (mocked embed) across staging
- [ ] - [ ] Contract test: Validate list response contains UI-required fields for at least first 20 artifacts
- [ ] - [ ] Security test: ensure embed_url is not returned to unauthorized roles
- [ ] - [ ] Performance test: render artifact list with 10k artifacts simulated on backend and measure page response times

###### Definition of Done

- [ ] - [ ] E2E tests pass in CI for the UI flows that consume create/list/get APIs

###### Agent Notes
- - [ ] API payloads must be stable and minimal for list views to keep frontend performant
- [ ] Frontend should request only required fields via fields= query param to reduce bandwidth and allow server projection
- [ ] Create endpoint should support optimistic UI flows: return created metadata immediately; the artifact HTML generation pipeline may complete asynchronously (artifact_url may be null initially) — UI must handle that state
- [ ] Use feature flags to gate UI changes until backend contracts are stable

#### Requirement / Feature: Access control basics (workspace roles: Admin/Analyst/Viewer enforcement)

**Feature ID:** `F-046`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, security`

**Rationale:** RBAC is required for safe sharing and access to governance/semantic features.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-045

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-046-01: As a workspace owner/admin, I want the backend to enforce workspace roles (Admin / Analyst / Viewer) for core API operations, so that workspace resources (integrations, sessions, artifacts) are protected and users only perform permitted actions.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Enforce workspace-level roles: Admin has full workspace privileges, Analyst can create sessions/artifacts and share within workspace, Viewer has read-only access to artifacts and sessions
- [ ] Denied requests return 403 with structured JSON error { code, message, required_role, action } and are logged with trace_id and user_id
- [ ] Authorization logic is applied as middleware/guard on these endpoints at minimum: POST /api/v1/integrations (Admin only), POST /api/v1/sessions (Analyst+Admin), GET /api/v1/artifacts and GET /api/v1/artifacts/{id} (Viewer+), POST /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/share (Analyst+Admin/Owner) and POST /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/refresh (Owner/Analyst if owner) per existing ownership rules
- [ ] Role evaluation uses workspace_members table (workspace_id, user_id, role) and is authoritative; missing membership => 401
- [ ] Unit and integration test coverage for role matrix (allowed + denied) > 95% for modified code paths
- [ ] Logging emits structured auth events to existing audit stream with fields: trace_id, user_id, workspace_id, endpoint, action, allowed:bool, reason
- [ ] Migration that adds/validates workspace_members and role enum applied in staging and production with rollback script

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-046-01: Design & implement database schema changes for workspace membership and roles
    - [ ] Add migration to create workspace_members table (workspace_id UUID, user_id UUID, role ENUM('admin','analyst','viewer'), created_at, updated_at) with unique(workspace_id,user_id)
    - [ ] Add migration to seed roles for existing workspace owners from current workspace table (backfill script)
    - [ ] Add rollback migration to drop table and revert seed in case of failure
    - [ ] Add DB constraints and indexes: index on workspace_id, user_id and foreign keys to workspaces and users tables
    - [ ] Write DB integration smoke script to validate membership lookup performance (< 5ms P95)
- [ ] TASK-F-046-02: Implement authorization middleware/guard in backend API gateway/service
    - [ ] Create middleware that extracts user_id from authenticated request (OAuth token / API key) and workspace_id from path or resource
    - [ ] Implement lookup to workspace_members table and map to role; cache short-lived in in-memory cache (TTL 60s) to reduce DB calls
    - [ ] Expose helper functions: requireRole(workspace_id, user_id, allowedRoles, action) returning (allowed, reason)
    - [ ] Return standardized 401 for unauthenticated, 403 for unauthorized with structured JSON error and proper HTTP headers
    - [ ] Add unit tests for middleware logic and cache behavior
- [ ] TASK-F-046-03: Apply RBAC checks to critical API endpoints
    - [ ] Enforce Admin-only on POST /api/v1/integrations and workspace settings endpoints
    - [ ] Enforce Analyst+Admin on POST /api/v1/sessions and POST /api/v1/artifacts (create)
    - [ ] Enforce Viewer+ on GET /api/v1/artifacts, GET /api/v1/artifacts/{id}, GET /api/v1/models/{model_id}
    - [ ] Enforce sharing/owner semantics for POST /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/share based on artifact.owner_id and workspace role mapping (Analyst or Admin can create share links if owner or Admin)
    - [ ] Ensure ownership overrides where artifact.owner_id grants Editor/Owner rights distinct from workspace role; document precedence
- [ ] TASK-F-046-04: Implement error handling, logging, and observability for authorization flow
    - [ ] Log every auth decision to audit log stream with fields: timestamp, trace_id, user_id, workspace_id, endpoint, action, role, allowed(boolean), reason
    - [ ] Emit metrics: auth_denied_count, auth_allowed_count, auth_lookup_latency_ms as Prometheus metrics
    - [ ] Integrate with distributed tracing: attach span when doing membership DB lookup and middleware evaluations
    - [ ] Implement rate-limited alert for spikes in auth_denied_count (> 50/hour for a workspace)
- [ ] TASK-F-046-05: Write backend unit & integration tests for RBAC enforcement
    - [ ] Unit tests for role mapping helper functions and caching logic
    - [ ] Integration tests (API-level) for allowed and denied permutations for each endpoint and role matrix
    - [ ] Add tests for error payloads and headers and for missing membership (401)
    - [ ] Add test for concurrent requests hitting cache invalidation path
- [ ] TASK-F-046-06: Release plan and DB migration rollout
    - [ ] Prepare migration runbook with pre-checks (ensure all workspaces have at least one admin)
    - [ ] Coordinate DB migration during off-peak window and run in staging then prod
    - [ ] Feature-flag new middleware to allow staged rollout
    - [ ] Run smoke tests post-migration and validate audit logs

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Run unit tests for middleware and helper functions locally and in CI
- [ ] Run integration API tests for each endpoint and role matrix (Admin/Analyst/Viewer/None) asserting 200/201 for allowed and 401/403 for disallowed
- [ ] Execute DB migration in staging and validate membership queries and seed backfill
- [ ] Simulate cache expiry and concurrent membership updates to verify cache coherence
- [ ] Load test auth lookup path with synthetic traffic and assert P95 latency < 50ms
- [ ] Security test: attempt privilege escalation (use token for user in another workspace) and assert 403
- [ ] Regression test existing endpoints for unchanged behavior when feature flag off

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] All code merged to main and peer-reviewed
- [ ] Database migrations applied successfully in staging and then production with rollback plan
- [ ] Middleware active behind feature flag and exercised in integration tests
- [ ] Unit and integration tests pass in CI with coverage targets met
- [ ] Structured auth error responses implemented and documented in API spec
- [ ] Audit logs and Prometheus metrics for auth flows are emitted and visible in observability dashboards
- [ ] Security review completed and no open security findings

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-045

###### Agent Notes
- Implement authorization as a composable middleware in the existing API service. Use the workspace_members table as the source of truth; cache reads for 60s with safe invalidation on membership change events. Ensure middleware integrates with existing OAuth token decoding to obtain user_id. Return structured 401/403 JSON and log decisions to the audit topic. Keep RBAC checks thin and Fast-Fail to minimize added latency; if a role is missing, treat as 401. Coordinate DB migration with Product to ensure every workspace has an admin before enforcement.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-046-02: As an Analyst or Viewer using the web UI, I want the frontend to reflect workspace role permissions (show/hide/disable actions and surface clear error states), so that users only see actions they can perform and get helpful feedback if blocked.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Workspace header shows current user's role for the active workspace (Admin / Analyst / Viewer)
- [ ] UI hides or disables actions based on role: 'Connect integration' and 'Workspace settings' hidden for non-Admins; 'New Analysis' and 'Save to workspace' disabled for Viewers; 'Share' UI disabled when user lacks permission
- [ ] Attempting to invoke a disallowed action triggers a deterministic client-side guard that shows a 403-style modal with guidance and a link to request access
- [ ] Frontend gracefully handles 401/403 responses from backend: shows consistent toasts/modals, logs the event to client-side telemetry, and does not show sensitive UI state
- [ ] E2E tests validate role-based UI permutations for Admin/Analyst/Viewer and cover accessibility (aria) for the role indicator

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-046-07: Expose workspace role to frontend via API & client context
    - [ ] Add /api/v1/workspaces/{id}/memberships/current endpoint (or extend workspace metadata) to return current user's role and membership expiry
    - [ ] Cache role in client-side workspace context provider with TTL matching backend cache (60s) and invalidate on workspace switch
    - [ ] Add client-side TypeScript types for roles and update SDK interfaces
    - [ ] Unit test the context provider for initial fetch, cache, and invalidation
- [ ] TASK-F-046-08: Update UI components to be role-aware
    - [ ] Update workspace header to display role badge and tooltip describing capabilities
    - [ ] Hide 'Connect integration' and workspace settings controls for non-Admins
    - [ ] Disable New Analysis / Save actions for Viewer role with tooltip 'Contact your workspace Admin to request upgrade'
    - [ ] Update artifact list item controls (Share, Edit, Delete) to reflect both workspace role and artifact-level ownership/role mapping
    - [ ] Add visual affordance for read-only sessions (e.g. 'Read-only view' banner) when Viewer
- [ ] TASK-F-046-09: Implement client-side guards and error handling for auth failures
    - [ ] Implement a guard component that checks requiredRole prop and prevents action execution if role insufficient
    - [ ] On API 401/403 responses, map to a user-facing modal with structured message and action (request access link) and log to client telemetry
    - [ ] Ensure UI does not expose disabled endpoints' inputs or PII when a request fails
    - [ ] Add unit tests for guard component and error modal mapping
- [ ] TASK-F-046-10: End-to-end and accessibility testing
    - [ ] Add E2E tests (Cypress / Playwright) that run role permutations: Admin, Analyst, Viewer — validate visible/hidden/disabled actions and API-level enforcement
    - [ ] Add accessibility checks ensuring role badge and error modal have aria attributes and keyboard focus traps
    - [ ] Record visual snapshots for role-specific UI states in CI
- [ ] TASK-F-046-11: Documentation & in-app help
    - [ ] Update Settings -> Workspace Roles help page describing capabilities of Admin/Analyst/Viewer
    - [ ] Add an inline 'Request access' flow link in the 403 modal that opens an email or in-app request card pre-populated with workspace and action
    - [ ] Update API docs with frontend contract for membership endpoint

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit test context provider and role parsing logic
- [ ] Integration test UI components with mocked membership API responses for each role
- [ ] E2E test flows: Admin can create integration; Analyst can start session and save artifact; Viewer can view but cannot save or create
- [ ] Simulate backend 403 responses and assert that error modal appears and telemetry is emitted
- [ ] Accessibility tests verifying aria-labels, focus, and keyboard navigation for role badge and modals

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Frontend changes merged and deployed to staging
- [ ] Client obtains role via API and caches it in workspace context
- [ ] All role-sensitive UI elements hide/disable correctly for each role
- [ ] E2E tests for role permutations pass in CI
- [ ] Accessibility checks pass for new UI elements
- [ ] User-facing docs updated and in-app 403 flow implemented
- [ ] Telemetry emits role-change and denied-action events

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-045
- [ ] US-F-046-01

###### Agent Notes
- Frontend should rely on the backend-provided membership endpoint rather than computing role from JWT scopes. Implement a lightweight WorkspaceRoleContext with a single source of truth. Use feature flag to gate UI behavior until backend middleware is live. Prefer disabling actions with explanatory tooltips for Viewer role rather than removing elements entirely where discoverability matters. Ensure error handling maps backend structured error payload to the modal content.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-046-03: As the platform SRE / security lead, I want audit logging, metrics, rollout controls, and tests around RBAC changes, so that role enforcement is observable, reversible, and safe to release to production.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All authorization decisions are recorded to an append-only audit table/topic with required fields (timestamp, trace_id, user_id, workspace_id, resource, action, required_role, resolved_role, allowed, reason)
- [ ] Prometheus metrics exist for auth_denied_count, auth_allowed_count, auth_latency_histogram and are scraped by monitoring stack
- [ ] A rollout plan exists with a feature flag for middleware enforcement and a rollback path to disable enforcement without code rollback
- [ ] CI gates include unit, integration, and E2E RBAC tests; merge blocked if RBAC tests fail
- [ ] Security review completed and no high-severity issues open

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-046-12: Implement audit log sink and append-only audit table
    - [ ] Create audit logs table schema (audit_id UUID, timestamp, trace_id, user_id, workspace_id, resource, action, required_role, resolved_role, allowed boolean, reason, metadata JSON)
    - [ ] Implement backend writer to append authorization events in a non-blocking fashion (fire-and-forget via job queue) and fallback to durable queue on DB errors
    - [ ] Ensure audit table has append-only policy; restrict access via DB roles
    - [ ] Add nightly export job to ship audit logs to long-term storage for compliance
- [ ] TASK-F-046-13: Emit and wire up observability metrics & alerts
    - [ ] Instrument middleware to emit Prometheus metrics: auth_denied_count, auth_allowed_count, auth_lookup_latency_ms histogram
    - [ ] Create Grafana dashboard panels for RBAC metrics and a per-workspace view for denied_count
    - [ ] Create alert: auth_denied_count spike (e.g. >50 in 1 hour) to ops channel
    - [ ] Document runbook for investigating denied spikes
- [ ] TASK-F-046-14: CI, tests and merge gating
    - [ ] Add RBAC integration test suite that runs against a test DB and API container in CI
    - [ ] Add E2E smoke test in CI that toggles feature flag off/on and asserts behavior
    - [ ] Fail CI on RBAC test failures and add test reporting to PR checks
    - [ ] Add load test scenario to ensure auth lookup scales and caches correctly
- [ ] TASK-F-046-15: Feature flag rollout and deployment automation
    - [ ] Add feature flag (config service / env) to gate middleware enforcement path
    - [ ] Update deployment scripts to support gradual rollout (percent-based) and immediate rollback capability
    - [ ] Create script that backfills workspace_members and validates every workspace has an Admin before enabling enforcement
    - [ ] Coordinate rollout with Product and Customer Success for chosen pilot workspaces
- [ ] TASK-F-046-16: Security review and compliance checks
    - [ ] Run internal security review focusing on auth token handling, membership lookup security, and audit log protections
    - [ ] Verify no sensitive credentials are logged and membership lookups do not leak PII
    - [ ] Perform threat model update and record mitigations
    - [ ] Obtain sign-off from InfoSec before enabling feature-flag in production

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Verify audit records appear for every auth decision in staging and validate schema and retention policy
- [ ] Run CI test suite including RBAC integration tests and E2E smoke tests
- [ ] Trigger synthetic denied requests to verify metrics and alerting pipeline
- [ ] Run the backfill validation script to ensure no workspace lacks an Admin, then enable feature flag in a small pilot (1–3 workspaces) and validate behavior
- [ ] Perform security pen-test focused on RBAC endpoints and membership enumeration attempts
- [ ] Validate rollback flow: disable feature flag and verify system returns to permissive behavior immediately

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Audit log table and writer deployed and validated in staging
- [ ] Prometheus metrics scraped and Grafana dashboard created
- [ ] Feature flag implemented and tested with rollback capability
- [ ] CI contains RBAC integration and E2E tests and gates merges
- [ ] Load tests show acceptable auth lookup latency and cache behavior
- [ ] Security review completed with sign-off and all high/critical issues resolved
- [ ] Runbook for investigating RBAC denials created and accessible to SRE/Support

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-045
- [ ] US-F-046-01

###### Agent Notes
- Treat audit logging as high-priority compliance data: writes should be durable and append-only; consider using a write-ahead queue (Redis stream or Kafka) to decouple from request path. Ensure audit logs are scrubbed of PII and adhere to retention policy. Implement Prometheus metrics and a Grafana dashboard; add alerts for anomalous denied spikes. Use a feature flag with percentage rollout to pilot with a small set of workspaces before global enablement.

#### Requirement / Feature: Manual refresh endpoint (trigger re-score and artifact regeneration)

**Feature ID:** `F-047`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, api`

**Rationale:** Users must be able to trigger manual refreshes in MVP; scheduled refresh deferred.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-045
- [ ] F-035

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-047-01: As an Artifact Owner, I want to trigger a manual refresh for a saved artifact via an API endpoint, so that the system re-scores the model outputs against current data and regenerates the artifact HTML with updated predictions and DQ checks.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The POST /api/v1/artifacts/{artifact_id}/refresh endpoint returns 202 Accepted with a job_id when a refresh is successfully enqueued.
- [ ] Only the artifact Owner, workspace Admin, or a principal with explicit 'refresh' permission can enqueue a manual refresh; other callers receive 403 Forbidden.
- [ ] The endpoint validates that the artifact exists and that a promoted model_id is present; if no model exists, the endpoint returns 409 Conflict with an actionable message.
- [ ] The refresh job executes a re-score flow that uses the currently promoted model (no retrain) and produces updated gold layer prediction tables and model_insights tables; any critical DQ gate failure aborts write and marks job status as FAILED with the DQ failure details.
- [ ] Artifact generation agent is invoked after successful re-score; regenerated HTML is written to object storage and artifact metadata (version, updated_at, html_size_bytes, dq_monitor) is updated atomically.
- [ ] A pipeline trace_id is generated and propagated to all subservices and returned in the enqueue response and final job status for observability.
- [ ] The endpoint is idempotent for repeated requests with the same idempotency key: repeated calls return the existing enqueued job_id and do not schedule duplicate jobs within a 5-minute deduplication window.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-047-01: Design and document API contract for manual refresh
    - [ ] Define request URL, method, and path parameter: POST /api/v1/artifacts/{artifact_id}/refresh
    - [ ] Specify request body shape: { idempotency_key?: string, force?: boolean } and allowed headers (Authorization, X-Trace-Id)
    - [ ] Specify response codes and payloads: 202 { job_id, trace_id, status_url }, 403, 404, 409, 429, 5xx
    - [ ] Document RBAC rules and required scopes for Owner/Admin/refresh-permission
    - [ ] Add OpenAPI spec entry and auto-generate client stubs for backend/frontend teams
- [ ] TASK-F-047-02: Implement backend handler and validation
    - [ ] Implement controller to authenticate caller and enforce RBAC using ABAC checks against artifact.access list
    - [ ] Validate artifact existence and read artifact record including model_id, session_spec, and lineage
    - [ ] Return 409 with clear message if artifact has no promoted model or model_id is archived
    - [ ] Enforce idempotency: check idempotency_key against Redis dedupe store and return existing job_id if within 5 minutes
    - [ ] Emit structured logs and metrics for request validation outcomes
- [ ] TASK-F-047-03: Enqueue and orchestrate re-score job in job queue
    - [ ] Create job payload with job_id, artifact_id, model_id, session_spec, trace_id, requested_by, and idempotency_key
    - [ ] Push job onto Redis/BullMQ queue with priority normal and TTL for dedupe window
    - [ ] Implement job handler skeleton that coordinates Stage 5 (re-score/write) and Stage 6 (artifact generation)
    - [ ] Implement job status table updates in Postgres: job_id, status (ENQUEUED/IN_PROGRESS/SUCCEEDED/FAILED), started_at, finished_at, error_details
    - [ ] Add unit tests for enqueue logic and deduplication behavior
- [ ] TASK-F-047-04: Implement re-score execution (Stage 5 runner invocation)
    - [ ] Invoke the gold-layer inference path to compute predicted_value for forecast rows using the promoted model binary (model registry URI) and current feature manifest
    - [ ] Execute DQ checks as defined in Stage 5 (row count tolerance, prediction distribution, CI integrity, forecast horizon integrity) and abort with error if any critical gate fails
    - [ ] Write gold.predictions_{model_id} and gold.model_insights_{model_id} tables into warehouse using existing persistence service
    - [ ] Ensure writes are transactional where supported and write audit record with governance_manifest_version and feature_manifest_version
    - [ ] Record SHAP computations and ensure shap table is written or skipped with logged reason
- [ ] TASK-F-047-05: Invoke artifact generation agent (Stage 6) and persist HTML
    - [ ] Trigger artifact generation agent with updated model_card, gold table references, and session_spec via agent queue or service call
    - [ ] Validate artifact generator output against deterministic validator rules (meta tags present, aria attributes, file size <= 250KB, no hardcoded colors, fetch try/catch) and run repair loop up to 2 cycles
    - [ ] Upload final HTML to S3-compatible object storage and set artifact_url and embed_url metadata
    - [ ] Update artifact record version (semver patch bump), html_size_bytes, updated_at and dq_monitor status
    - [ ] Invalidate CDN cache for artifact_url and publish artifact updated event to topic (pub/sub)
- [ ] TASK-F-047-06: Add observability, logging and audit logging
    - [ ] Attach trace_id to all spawned jobs and add OpenTelemetry spans for Stage 5 and Stage 6 calls
    - [ ] Log DQ gate outcomes, model_id used, number of rows written, and artifact version change as structured JSON to audit log
    - [ ] Emit Prometheus metrics: manual_refresh_requests_total, manual_refresh_success_total, manual_refresh_failures_total, refresh_latency_seconds
    - [ ] Create a dashboard for refresh job health and recent failures
    - [ ] Implement alerts for repeated refresh failures (>3 in 1 hour) to SRE on-call channel
- [ ] TASK-F-047-07: Security and operational controls
    - [ ] Validate embed token and workspace_id consistency when scheduling refresh for artifacts with cross-workspace or public share tokens
    - [ ] Ensure no database credentials or PII are logged; sanitize logs
    - [ ] Rate-limit manual refresh endpoint per workspace to configurable threshold (default 10/hour) and return 429 when exceeded
    - [ ] Require workspace admin config flag to allow force=true refresh that bypasses paused scheduled refresh state
    - [ ] Add role-based audit entry for every manual refresh action

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for controller validation, RBAC enforcement, idempotency key handling, and response codes
- [ ] Integration test that enqueues a refresh and runs a mocked Stage 5 re-score that writes to a test warehouse (or duckDB) and ensures predicted table and shap table are written
- [ ] End-to-end test that invokes the real artifact generation agent in a staging environment and asserts final artifact HTML uploaded, validator passes, and artifact record updated
- [ ] Negative test: call endpoint when artifact has no promoted model_id and assert 409 with actionable message
- [ ] Negative test: simulate Stage 5 DQ gate failure and assert job status is FAILED and artifact record not updated
- [ ] Security test: ensure a non-owner caller gets 403 and that logs do not contain PII or database credentials
- [ ] Load test: enqueue 50 concurrent refresh requests for different artifacts and verify job queue behavior and rate-limiting

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Backend API implemented and covered by unit tests for validation, RBAC, and idempotency logic
- [ ] Refresh jobs can be enqueued and transition through ENQUEUED -> IN_PROGRESS -> SUCCEEDED/FAILED in job table
- [ ] Re-scoring path writes gold.predictions and gold.model_insights tables and respects Stage 5 DQ gates
- [ ] Artifact generator is invoked and regenerated HTML is uploaded to object storage and accessible via artifact_url
- [ ] Deterministic artifact validator passes or repair loop completes with logged outcome
- [ ] Trace_id propagated across services and audit log contains refresh action with actor and timestamp
- [ ] End-to-end integration test exists and is automated in CI (mocked model and warehouse)

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-045
- [ ] F-035

###### Agent Notes
- The manual refresh job must orchestrate two deterministic services: Stage 5 re-score/persistence and Stage 6 artifact generation. Use existing model registry URI and feature_manifest_version from artifact.session_spec. Do not retrain the model in the MVP manual refresh flow. Propagate trace_id and idempotency_key through job payload. Ensure DQ gate evaluation is deterministic and any BLOCK-level failure causes early abort and returns DQ details in job failure. Artifact generator must run its deterministic validator and perform up to 2 repair cycles automatically; only surface to user if repair fails. Use Redis/BullMQ for dedupe and job queueing; persist job status in Postgres for UI polling.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-047-02: As a Workspace user interacting with the UI, I want a 'Refresh' button and real-time progress/status updates so that I can trigger a manual refresh and monitor the pipeline progress until the artifact is updated.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The UI shows a 'Refresh' button on artifact detail and in artifact list owner actions when the caller has permission; button is disabled for unauthorized users.
- [ ] When the user confirms refresh, the UI calls POST /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/refresh with an idempotency_key and displays immediate feedback (enqueued) with returned job_id and trace_id.
- [ ] The UI subscribes to Server-Sent Events (SSE) /jobs/{job_id}/events or polls job status_url and displays stage-by-stage progress messages: re-score start, DQ gate pass/fail, artifact generation start, validator pass/repair, completed/failed.
- [ ] On successful completion the artifact thumbnail, last_updated timestamp, version, and DQ health badge refresh in-place without a full page reload.
- [ ] On failure the UI surfaces clear, actionable error messages with a link to the job log and the trace_id for support.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-047-08: Add 'Refresh' action in frontend artifact detail and list
    - [ ] Add Refresh button to artifact detail header and artifact list owner dropdown when user has Owner or refresh permission
    - [ ] Implement confirmation modal explaining 'This will re-score using the currently promoted model and regenerate the artifact. This may update predictions and DQ status.'
    - [ ] Disable button and show spinner after user confirms until job is enqueued or error returned
    - [ ] Wire idempotency_key generation per UI action (uuidv4) and persist in local state to enable re-use if user retries
    - [ ] Add unit/component tests for button visibility and modal behavior
- [ ] TASK-F-047-09: Implement job subscription UI for progress and final status
    - [ ] On successful enqueue, open a progress drawer that listens to SSE /jobs/{job_id}/events and renders the event stream (agent_start, dq_gate, agent_complete, artifact_ready, error)
    - [ ] Fallback to polling job status_url every 3s if SSE unsupported or connection fails
    - [ ] Map server events to human-readable steps and include trace_id and link to job details
    - [ ] Update artifact list and detail caches when job completes successfully
    - [ ] Add UI tests for SSE events rendering and polling fallback
- [ ] TASK-F-047-10: Implement optimistic UI updates and cache invalidation
    - [ ] On job completion success event, fetch updated artifact metadata and replace artifact list and detail stores atomically
    - [ ] Invalidate CDN cached thumbnail or artifact preview and show 'Refreshing...' until new preview ready
    - [ ] Ensure that concurrent UI actions (multiple refreshes) show the correct job_id and status for each request
    - [ ] Add integration test that triggers refresh and validates UI updates in staging with mocked SSE events
    - [ ] Document UX copy and error messages in design spec
- [ ] TASK-F-047-11: Accessibility and internationalization
    - [ ] Ensure Refresh button and modal are keyboard accessible and have aria-labels
    - [ ] Localize confirmation modal, progress messages, success and failure messages
    - [ ] Add screen-reader announcement when refresh starts and when it completes
    - [ ] Add accessibility tests for modal and progress drawer

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Component tests for Refresh button visibility per role and for confirmation modal behavior
- [ ] Integration test of enqueue flow: frontend calls POST refresh, receives 202 and job_id, opens progress drawer
- [ ] E2E test using staging job runner: simulate SSE events from job worker and assert UI renders each step and final updated artifact metadata
- [ ] Accessibility tests for keyboard and screen-reader announcements
- [ ] Negative test: user without permission attempts refresh and receives 403 UI message
- [ ] Network failure test: SSE disconnect fallback to polling observed and continues to show progress

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Refresh button visible only to authorized principals and covered by frontend unit tests
- [ ] Enqueue call made with idempotency_key and response job_id & trace_id handled by UI
- [ ] Progress drawer displays server events in order and falls back to polling when SSE unavailable
- [ ] Artifact metadata and thumbnail update automatically after completion
- [ ] Failure states display actionable messages and trace_id linked to job log

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-045
- [ ] F-035

###### Agent Notes
- Frontend should not attempt to interpret agent outputs beyond mapping event types to localized user messages. Use the job status_url returned on enqueue for polling if SSE is not viable. Preserve idempotency_key across retries so duplicate enqueues are avoided. Show trace_id prominently in failure messages for SRE support.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-047-03: As an SRE/QA Lead, I want the manual refresh flow to be observable, testable, and resilient so that refresh jobs are reliable, auditable, and fail safely under load or DQ problems.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Refresh job lifecycle is persisted to job table with traceable fields: job_id, artifact_id, model_id, requested_by, trace_id, status, started_at, finished_at, error_details.
- [ ] Retry and backoff logic applies to transient failures (warehouse timeouts, temporary model registry errors) with configurable retry limits; permanent errors surface immediately without infinite retries.
- [ ] Job deduplication works: duplicate refresh requests for same artifact with same idempotency_key or within dedupe window do not create duplicate work.
- [ ] Monitoring is available: Prometheus metrics, log traces, and a dashboard with success/failure rates and average latency; alerts fire on high failure rate or queue backlog.
- [ ] Automated CI checks include unit tests, integration tests with a test warehouse (DuckDB) and a smoke end-to-end test pipeline.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-047-12: Implement reliable job worker with retries and backoff
    - [ ] Implement transient error detection heuristics (5xx vs 4xx) and classify retryable errors
    - [ ] Apply exponential backoff with jitter and max_attempts (default 3) for retryable failures
    - [ ] Mark job as FAILED immediately for non-retryable errors (DQ BLOCK, invalid model_id)
    - [ ] Emit retry and final-failure events to job events stream for UI and audit
    - [ ] Add unit tests for retry/backoff logic including simulated transient warehouse timeouts
- [ ] TASK-F-047-13: Implement job deduplication and idempotency enforcement in worker
    - [ ] Use Redis set or Bloom filter keyed by artifact_id + idempotency_key with 5-minute TTL to avoid duplicates
    - [ ] Enforce single in-progress refresh per artifact (lock using Redis distributed lock) unless force=true
    - [ ] Release locks deterministically on success or failure and add watchdog to release stale locks after TTL
    - [ ] Add tests that simulate concurrent enqueue attempts and assert only one job executes
    - [ ] Document dedupe semantics in operational runbook
- [ ] TASK-F-047-14: Add monitoring, metrics, and alerting
    - [ ] Export Prometheus metrics: queued_jobs, running_jobs, succeeded_jobs, failed_jobs, avg_refresh_latency_seconds, dq_gate_failure_count
    - [ ] Create Grafana dashboard visualizing job throughput, success rate, average latency, queue backlog, and recent failure traces
    - [ ] Configure alerting rules: job_failure_rate > 10% over 15m, queue_backlog > 50 for > 5m, repeated DQ BLOCKs for same artifact
    - [ ] Add automated uptime test that enqueues a small synthetic refresh once per 15 minutes and asserts completion within SLO
    - [ ] Document alert runbook with triage steps and owners
- [ ] TASK-F-047-15: Add CI and QA automated tests
    - [ ] Add unit tests for all new backend modules with coverage thresholds
    - [ ] Add integration tests that spin up a DuckDB instance and mock model registry to execute full re-score and artifact generation (agent calls stubbed) in CI
    - [ ] Add end-to-end smoke test in staging that runs a real refresh on a non-production workspace and asserts artifact updated
    - [ ] Add regression test to ensure DQ gate failures do not write predictions to warehouse
    - [ ] Create test data fixtures and CI secrets handling for staging test runs
- [ ] TASK-F-047-16: Security review and access logging
    - [ ] Run security code review for new endpoint and worker to verify token validation and ABAC enforcement
    - [ ] Add structured audit log entry per refresh action with actor_id, workspace_id, artifact_id, action='manual_refresh', and result
    - [ ] Ensure logs are scrubbed of PII and database credentials and that retention policy applies
    - [ ] Add a privacy/PII check in the worker that blocks any accidental model payload logs
    - [ ] Coordinate with security team for approval before release

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Simulate transient warehouse timeout and assert job retries with exponential backoff and eventual success or failure within configured attempts
- [ ] Simulate DQ gate BLOCK and assert job fails without writing prediction tables and records failure details in job.error_details
- [ ] Concurrency test: fire 10 simultaneous refresh requests for same artifact and assert single worker runs using Redis lock
- [ ] Monitoring test: trigger several successful and failed refreshes and verify dashboard metrics update and alerts fire for configured thresholds
- [ ] Security test: verify audit log contains actor and that unauthorized user attempts are logged with 403
- [ ] Chaos test in staging: randomly kill worker process during refresh and assert job re-queued or failed cleanly and locks cleared

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Worker retry/backoff and deduplication behavior tested and deployed in staging
- [ ] Prometheus metrics emitted and Grafana dashboard created and accessible to SRE
- [ ] CI pipeline includes unit, integration (DuckDB), and staging smoke tests that exercise manual refresh flow
- [ ] Audit log entries created for every manual refresh with required fields and retained per policy
- [ ] Operational runbook exists describing how to triage failing refresh jobs and how to unblock stuck locks

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-045
- [ ] F-035

###### Agent Notes
- Worker must treat re-score and artifact generation as deterministic, idempotent operations where possible. Use trace_id to correlate LLM agent calls, warehouse query traces, and object storage uploads. When Stage 5 or Stage 6 callouts to AI agents are needed, use existing agent orchestration channels and ensure no raw data rows are sent to LLMs — only schema, aggregated statistics, and model metadata per PRD. The job worker should fail fast on DQ BLOCK conditions and return precise failure payloads for UI and audit.

### Epic: Observability

#### Requirement / Feature: Audit log skeleton (append-only pipeline & promotion events)

**Feature ID:** `F-048`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, infra`

**Rationale:** Auditability is required for governance and MVP compliance.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-045

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-048-01: As a platform operator, I want an append-only audit log for pipeline events, so that every pipeline run is auditable, tamper-resistant, and traceable for governance and incident investigation.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Persist structured audit events for every pipeline stage transition with fields: event_id, trace_id, workspace_id, session_id (if applicable), agent_name, stage_name, event_type, timestamp, actor_id, payload (JSON), and schema_version
- [ ] Ensure audit store is append-only: no UPDATE or DELETE allowed at the database level; attempts to update or delete return a clear error and are rejected
- [ ] Guarantee event write API returns 201 on first successful record and 409 for duplicate event_id to enable idempotent client behavior
- [ ] Record events with monotonic timestamps and preserve original payload bytes (no normalization/destructive transformation) for replay
- [ ] Write path is resilient: transient failures retry with exponential backoff and provide observability metrics for write success/failure
- [ ] Audit records are encrypted at rest and access-controlled by workspace ACLs
- [ ] Audit entries linkable by trace_id to OpenTelemetry traces and pipeline logs

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-048-01: Design audit event schema and storage model
    - [ ] Document JSON Schema for audit_event including required fields and example payloads
    - [ ] Decide physical store: create DB table DDL for Postgres (or alternative append-only store) including column types and indexes
    - [ ] Define partitioning strategy (by workspace_id and event_date) and retention metadata
    - [ ] Define primary key and uniqueness constraint on event_id to support idempotency
    - [ ] Define access control model and encryption-at-rest requirements for the store
- [ ] TASK-F-048-02: Implement database migration and append-only enforcement
    - [ ] Create migration script to add audit_events table with columns and indexes
    - [ ] Add DB trigger or policy to prevent UPDATE and DELETE on audit_events table and to raise clear errors
    - [ ] Add index on trace_id, workspace_id, event_type, and timestamp for query performance
    - [ ] Deploy migration to staging DB and validate constraints
    - [ ] Add monitoring alert for any attempted UPDATE/DELETE on audit_events
- [ ] TASK-F-048-03: Implement backend write API for audit events
    - [ ] Create POST /api/v1/audit/events endpoint that accepts validated audit_event JSON
    - [ ] Validate JSON Schema server-side and return 400 on schema violations with error details
    - [ ] Implement idempotency by using event_id uniqueness and return 409 on duplicate
    - [ ] Log API calls to platform logs and emit metrics (write_latency_ms, write_success, write_failure)
    - [ ] Add authenticated access with ABAC enforcement (only platform services with proper scope can write)
- [ ] TASK-F-048-04: Instrument pipeline components & agents to emit audit events
    - [ ] Update orchestrator to emit stage_start and stage_complete events with trace_id and agent metadata
    - [ ] Update data modeler, training orchestrator, and gold writer code paths to emit lifecycle and DQ gate events
    - [ ] Add structured promotion-related event emissions (see US-F-048-02) at promotion decision points
    - [ ] Implement graceful retry logic on event emission failures and fall back to local retry queue if endpoint unavailable
    - [ ] Add unit tests that verify correct event payloads are produced for synthetic pipeline runs
- [ ] TASK-F-048-05: Implement infra & security controls for audit store
    - [ ] Enable encryption-at-rest for DB or storage (KMS configuration) and document key rotation procedure
    - [ ] Apply DB role privileges: only audit-writer service role has INSERT; read roles scoped by ABAC for read endpoints
    - [ ] Add network policies to restrict access to audit DB from approved service accounts
    - [ ] Add audit storage backups and define backup encryption and retention
    - [ ] Add monitoring dashboards for audit write rates, error rates, and storage utilization
- [ ] TASK-F-048-06: Create retention & archival job for audit events
    - [ ] Implement scheduled job to export events older than retention_window to encrypted S3 as Parquet for long-term archive
    - [ ] Verify the job only moves and does not delete original rows until a legal retention window elapses and deletion is validated by compliance
    - [ ] Add configuration flags and migration scripts to toggle retention windows per workspace
    - [ ] Add test harness to validate exported files contain exact original payload bytes
    - [ ] Add monitoring and alerting for archival job failures
- [ ] TASK-F-048-07: Add tests and CI integration for audit write path
    - [ ] Write unit tests for schema validation and API handler edge cases
    - [ ] Write integration tests that spin up a test DB and POST events to the endpoint asserting stored rows and index behavior
    - [ ] Add property tests for idempotency behavior (POST same event twice)
    - [ ] Add a negative test that attempts UPDATE/DELETE and asserts rejection
    - [ ] Add the tests to CI pipeline and enforce coverage gate for the audit module

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Run unit tests for schema validation and API handler
- [ ] Execute integration test: run a synthetic pipeline that produces stage events and assert audit rows appear with correct trace_id and payload
- [ ] Execute negative tests that attempt UPDATE and DELETE on audit_events and assert failure
- [ ] Run idempotency test: POST same event_id twice and assert second returns 409 and no duplicate row
- [ ] Run security test: attempt write API without proper service token and assert 401/403
- [ ] Run end-to-end test that links audit events to OpenTelemetry trace_id and confirm trace lookup resolves to same pipeline run

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Audit_event JSON Schema documented and reviewed
- [ ] DB migration deployed to staging and production with append-only enforcement
- [ ] POST /api/v1/audit/events implemented with schema validation and idempotency
- [ ] Pipeline stages emit audit events for start/complete/fail with trace_id propagation
- [ ] Encryption-at-rest and access controls configured and validated
- [ ] Automated tests (unit + integration) pass in CI
- [ ] Monitoring dashboards and alerts for write errors and archival job present

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-045

###### Agent Notes
- Use a compact, versioned JSON Schema for audit_event and require event_id UUID v4 from clients to support idempotency. Enforce append-only at DB level via trigger/policy to prevent accidental mutation. Propagate existing trace_id from orchestration runtime into every event. Prefer Postgres append-only table with partitioning by event_date and workspace_id for simple queries; consider background archival to encrypted S3/Parquet for long retention. Emit metrics for write latency and failures. Ensure write API authenticates using machine service tokens scoped to only allow INSERTs. Record original payload bytes for fidelity and replayability.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-048-02: As a compliance officer, I want all model promotion and repair-loop events recorded in the audit log, so that every promotion decision and repair action is reproducible and reviewable.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Log promotion-related events with event_type values: promotion_attempt, promotion_pass, promotion_fail, promotion_repair_start, promotion_repair_end, promotion_archive
- [ ] Include promotion payload fields: model_id, champion_model_id (if replaced), session_id, workspace_id, validation_metrics (validation_mape, walk_forward_mapes), test_metrics (test_mape), promotion_criteria, repair_cycles, repair_actions_taken, actor_id, timestamp
- [ ] Ensure promotion events are logged before any model registry state change and contain an external immutable reference to the model artifact (model_registry_uri)
- [ ] Ensure repair-loop actions are logged with actionable details sufficient to replicate the repair step (e.g., hyperparameter change, feature set change, algorithm switch)
- [ ] Ensure promotion audit events are linkable to the pipeline audit trace_id and to the model_card stored in model registry

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-048-08: Extend audit_event schema for promotion and repair events
    - [ ] Add allowed event_type enum values for promotion and repair lifecycle events
    - [ ] Add structured promotion_payload schema with typed fields (model_id, champion_model_id, metrics, criteria, repair_cycles, actions)
    - [ ] Version the audit_event schema and document change notes for downstream consumers
    - [ ] Add example promotion event JSON to docs for operators and reviewers
- [ ] TASK-F-048-09: Instrument the training orchestrator and model registry integration
    - [ ] Add emission of promotion_attempt event immediately prior to evaluating promotion criteria
    - [ ] Emit promotion_pass or promotion_fail event immediately after promotion decision with full metrics snapshot and model_registry_uri
    - [ ] Emit promotion_repair_start and promotion_repair_end events for each repair cycle including repair action details
    - [ ] Emit promotion_archive event when an older champion is archived with archive reason and timestamp
    - [ ] Ensure all promotion events include trace_id and session_id for linkage
- [ ] TASK-F-048-10: Enforce write-order guarantees for promotion events
    - [ ] Implement transactional flow: write promotion event, then write model registry state change; if registry change fails, write a compensating audit event
    - [ ] Add a pre-promotion audit checkpoint saved atomically with required promotion inputs (feature_manifest_version, governance_manifest_version)
    - [ ] Add test harness that simulates failed model registry write and verifies compensating event is present
    - [ ] Add retries with exponential backoff and a dead-letter queue for promotion events failing to persist
- [ ] TASK-F-048-11: Add validation and DQ checks for promotion payloads
    - [ ] Validate required metric fields are present and within expected types and ranges before accepting promotion events
    - [ ] Reject promotion events missing model_registry_uri or model_id with 400 and log detailed error
    - [ ] Add unit tests for payload validation rules
    - [ ] Log validation failures separately for monitoring and escalation
- [ ] TASK-F-048-12: Add audit-driven model promotion review tooling (backend hooks)
    - [ ] Expose promotion event stream to audit consumers via paginated endpoint and optional SSE for live review
    - [ ] Add a flag in event metadata to mark events as reviewed (write-once: review actions are additional audit events, not updates to existing event)
    - [ ] Document how external compliance tools can subscribe to promotion events via webhook connector
    - [ ] Add integration test exercising webhook subscription and delivery guarantees
- [ ] TASK-F-048-13: Add tests and QA for promotion event recording
    - [ ] Simulate successful promotion and assert promotion_pass event exists and model_registry_uri matches model card
    - [ ] Simulate failed promotion with repair cycles and assert repair_start/end events logged with correct actions
    - [ ] Assert that a promotion event is present before any change in model_registry promotion_status
    - [ ] Add load test that runs multiple concurrent promotions to validate ordering and idempotency

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Run unit tests for promotion payload validation
- [ ] Run integration test: trigger a model training that reaches promotion decision and assert promotion_attempt and promotion_pass/fail are present with expected metrics
- [ ] Run failure scenario: mock model registry write failure and assert compensating audit event logged
- [ ] Run concurrency test: start 10 concurrent training sessions and assert ordered promotion events and no lost events
- [ ] Run security test: verify write API for promotion events respects ABAC and only training orchestrator role can write promotion events

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Promotion and repair event schema extensions implemented and documented
- [ ] Training orchestrator emits promotion lifecycle events at correct points and with required fields
- [ ] Promotion events are persisted atomically with model registry changes or compensated when failures occur
- [ ] All promotion event code paths covered by unit and integration tests
- [ ] Monitoring configured for promotion event emission failures and ordering anomalies
- [ ] Compliance documentation updated with examples and consumer guidance

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-045
- [ ] US-F-048-01

###### Agent Notes
- Promotion events must be recorded before mutating model registry state. Use a two-phase flow: (1) persist promotion_attempt audit event with full snapshot of candidate metrics and inputs, (2) attempt model registry state change, (3) persist promotion_pass or promotion_fail event and model_registry_uri. If (2) fails, persist promotion_compensate event with failure_reason. Use event_id ties (UUID) to correlate all promotion lifecycle events. Repair-loop actions should be explicit and human-readable (e.g., 'increase_reg_lambda', 'reduce_max_depth', 'switch_to_lightgbm') and include pre/post hyperparameter values. Avoid sending any raw data rows to audit; include only metadata and metric summaries. Maintain schema_version on events to support future enrichment.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-048-03: As a developer and QA lead, I want a secure query API and a minimal admin audit viewer (read-only) so that I can search, filter, export, and review audit events for debugging and compliance checks.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Provide GET /api/v1/audit/events endpoint with query parameters: workspace_id, trace_id, session_id, event_type, start_time, end_time, actor_id, limit, offset
- [ ] Ensure GET endpoint enforces ABAC: workspace members can only read events for their workspace unless they have admin role
- [ ] Provide pagination and CSV export for filtered result sets (CSV export initiates background job when result size > threshold)
- [ ] Return events in chronological order by timestamp by default and allow sort by timestamp desc
- [ ] Ensure read endpoints are efficient: typical paginated query (< 100 rows) returns in < 500ms for P50
- [ ] Provide a lightweight internal React-based admin page that lists events with filters and export button; the page requires admin flag to access

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-048-14: Implement read/query API for audit events
    - [ ] Create GET /api/v1/audit/events with support for filters and pagination
    - [ ] Validate and sanitize query parameters and apply rate limits to prevent expensive full-table scans
    - [ ] Implement ABAC enforcement to restrict results to workspace scope
    - [ ] Add server-side response caching for repeated queries with short TTL
    - [ ] Add API metrics for query latency and result size
- [ ] TASK-F-048-15: Add CSV export functionality for query results
    - [ ] Implement synchronous CSV generation for small result sets and return file stream
    - [ ] Implement asynchronous export job for large result sets that writes to secure S3 and returns presigned URL
    - [ ] Add throttling and quota per workspace for exports
    - [ ] Add tests verifying CSV contents match JSON payload values exactly
- [ ] TASK-F-048-16: Implement minimal admin UI for audit viewing
    - [ ] Create React page under /admin/audit that hits GET /api/v1/audit/events and renders paginated table
    - [ ] Add filter controls for workspace_id, event_type, trace_id, and date range
    - [ ] Add 'Export CSV' button that calls export endpoint and shows progress
    - [ ] Add links from each row to related artifact/model pages via model_registry_uri or session_id if present
    - [ ] Ensure UI uses workspace auth guard and only exposes UI to admin role
- [ ] TASK-F-048-17: Optimize query performance and add DB indexes
    - [ ] Analyze slow queries from staging and add or refine indexes on (workspace_id, timestamp), (trace_id), and (event_type, timestamp)
    - [ ] Benchmark common filter combinations and tune partition pruning
    - [ ] Add explain-plan-based tests to CI to detect regressions
    - [ ] Document query performance SLAs and instrumentation
- [ ] TASK-F-048-18: Add tests and security validation for read paths
    - [ ] Add unit tests for query parameter parsing and ABAC enforcement
    - [ ] Add integration tests that assert workspace-scoped users cannot read other workspace events
    - [ ] Add UI tests (Cypress) validating filters, pagination, and export flow
    - [ ] Add performance test validating P50 query latency < 500ms for common queries
- [ ] TASK-F-048-19: Add observability for read endpoints
    - [ ] Instrument read API with tracing and attach trace_id to response headers
    - [ ] Emit metrics for query counts, latencies, and export job durations
    - [ ] Create Grafana dashboard to monitor read throughput and latency
    - [ ] Add alert when average query latency exceeds SLA or export job failures increase

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit test query parsing, parameter validation, and ABAC enforcement
- [ ] Integration test that creates 200 synthetic events across workspaces and validates filter results and pagination
- [ ] E2E UI test that uses admin persona to filter by event_type and export CSV and then validate file contents
- [ ] Performance test simulating 50 concurrent read requests and assert P95 latency < 1s
- [ ] Security penetration test: attempt to access audit events from unauthorized workspace user and assert 403

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] GET /api/v1/audit/events implemented with filters, pagination, ABAC enforcement and tests
- [ ] CSV export implemented with synchronous and async modes and export job monitoring
- [ ] Admin UI implemented, behind admin guard, and integrated with API
- [ ] Indexes and query optimizations applied and benchmarked for common queries
- [ ] Security tests confirm workspace-scoped reads and rate limiting
- [ ] Monitoring dashboards and alerts for read path created

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] US-F-048-01

###### Agent Notes
- Keep admin UI minimal and read-only; do not attempt to edit or delete events from UI. For exports, ensure the CSV file contains exact original payload JSON as a string column in addition to parsed fields. Use server-side pagination using indexed columns to avoid full-table scans. For very large audit volumes, prioritize partition pruning and archived exports to S3 to avoid high DB load. Ensure all read access logs themselves are recorded to an operations audit channel (separate from customer audit store) for security review.

---

## Sprint 12 - Frontend Onboarding & UX Resilience

**Sprint Goal:** Ship onboarding CTA, integration setup screens, session run status UI, and robust loaders/errors per artifact and session flows.

### Sprint Completion Checklist
- [ ] All P0 stories complete
- [ ] All blocking defects resolved
- [ ] Tests passing
- [ ] Demo-ready increment produced
- [ ] Milestone accepted

### Epic: Onboarding

#### Requirement / Feature: Workspace home onboarding CTA and 'Start your first analysis' flow (frontend)

**Feature ID:** `F-049`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `frontend`

**Rationale:** MVP UX requires a clear first-run flow for new users as specified in PRD.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-001
- [ ] F-004

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-049-01: As a new workspace user, I want a prominent, accessible 'Start your first analysis' CTA on the empty workspace home so that I can discover and begin the first-run onboarding flow with one click.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CTA is visible on workspace home when the workspace has zero artifacts
- [ ] CTA label reads exactly "Start your first analysis" and is localizable
- [ ] CTA meets WCAG AA color contrast and keyboard focus rules and has an accessible name (aria-label)
- [ ] Clicking CTA opens the first-run onboarding modal / full-screen flow without page reload
- [ ] CTA click emits analytics event workspace.onboarding_cta.click with workspace_id and user_id
- [ ] CTA is hidden for users who do not have at least 'Analyst' workspace role and replaced with contextual message
- [ ] Automated visual snapshot exists for desktop and mobile breakpoints

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-049-01: Implement workspace home CTA component and placement
    - [ ] Create React component OnboardingCTA with props: workspaceId, userRole, artifactCount
    - [ ] Implement responsive styles matching mockups for desktop and mobile (stack below 768px)
    - [ ] Add aria-label and keyboard focus styles; ensure role=button for non-button containers
    - [ ] Wire component visibility: show only when artifactCount === 0 and userRole >= Analyst
    - [ ] Localize CTA text via i18n key analytiq.workspace.onboarding_cta.label
    - [ ] Add unit tests for render, role gating, and keyboard activation
    - [ ] Add visual snapshot tests for 320px, 768px, 1366px
- [ ] TASK-F-049-02: Design and approve small CTA animation / microinteraction
    - [ ] Create spec for microinteraction: subtle pulse on first render, no motion for reduced-motion users
    - [ ] Add CSS prefers-reduced-motion check to disable animation
    - [ ] Design review with Product/Design and accessibility specialist
    - [ ] Export assets and document animation timing for dev
- [ ] TASK-F-049-03: Instrument analytics and client-side logging for CTA interactions
    - [ ] Emit analytics event workspace.onboarding_cta.view on component mount if visible
    - [ ] Emit workspace.onboarding_cta.click with metadata (workspace_id, user_id, timestamp) on click
    - [ ] Log non-sensitive debug message to client console on CTA open for dev (strip in prod builds)
    - [ ] Add telemetry guard to respect user telemetry opt-out settings
- [ ] TASK-F-049-04: Security & privacy review for CTA flow entry point
    - [ ] Verify no credentials or PII are collected by CTA itself
    - [ ] Confirm CTA does not render integrations or external URLs until explicit user action
    - [ ] Add unit test to ensure component does not leak workspace secrets to DOM or logs
    - [ ] Record security review outcome in ticket

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit test: render CTA when artifactCount=0 and not render otherwise
- [ ] Integration test: clicking CTA opens onboarding modal and navigates to first step
- [ ] E2E test: new user flow from workspace home through CTA open to first onboarding step using Cypress
- [ ] Accessibility check: axe-core automated run on the page with CTA visible
- [ ] Manual QA: keyboard-only navigation and screen reader announcement validation
- [ ] Telemetry smoke: verify analytics events appear in dev telemetry pipeline

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] CTA component implemented and merged to main branch
- [ ] Unit tests and visual snapshots passing in CI
- [ ] Accessibility audit (keyboard, ARIA, contrast) passed
- [ ] Analytics events emitted and validated in dev telemetry
- [ ] Feature flag gating exists to rollout to pilot workspaces
- [ ] Documentation updated: workspace home component and onboarding entry point

###### Story Dependencies
