- [ ] F-001
- [ ] F-004

###### Agent Notes
- Implement a small, stateless frontend component that only determines visibility and emits an event to open the onboarding flow. Do not implement integrations or heavy logic here—opening the flow should call an onboarding route/modal handler. Feature-flag this work for phased rollout and QA.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-049-02: As a first-time user I want a guided 'Start your first analysis' onboarding flow that helps me connect a data source, runs governance profiling, and surfaces progress so that I can complete initial setup confidently and see health badges or required review items.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Onboarding flow includes a connector selection step that lists connectors supported by backend (calls GET /api/v1/integrations) and shows minimal descriptions and icons
- [ ] Connector credential form submits securely to backend POST /api/v1/integrations with client-side validation and shows success/failure states without exposing secrets
- [ ] After connector submission, governance progress UI displays streaming stage updates from the orchestration SSE endpoint and maps SSE event types to step UI (planning, agent_start, dq_gate, agent_complete, human_required, error, artifact_ready)
- [ ] Progress UI shows an estimated time and per-step status icons (pending, running, success, warn, fail) and displaying human_required pauses with clear CTA for required action
- [ ] Governance result screen shows a summarized governance manifest: table health scores, PII flags, and low-confidence definitions with a visible 'Review' badge and ability to 'Skip for now' with warning
- [ ] All API errors surface user-friendly messages, provide retry buttons, and log structured error details to client-side logging infrastructure
- [ ] The flow respects auth and ABAC: only workspace members can add integrations and secrets are never persisted in client logs or localStorage

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-049-05: Build connector selection and secure credential entry UI
    - [ ] Implement ConnectorsList component that fetches available connectors from GET /api/v1/integrations (or a lightweight connectors catalog endpoint)
    - [ ] Implement ConnectorCard item with icon, short description, and 'Connect' button
    - [ ] Implement secure ConnectorCredentialsForm: fields per connector, inline client-side validation, and show obscured input for sensitive fields
    - [ ] On submit, call POST /api/v1/integrations; use fetch with credentials omitted from logs and show spinner
    - [ ] Handle server-side validation errors: map field-level errors to form inputs and show top-level errors with suggested fixes
    - [ ] Add unit and integration tests for form validation and error mapping
- [ ] TASK-F-049-06: Implement SSE client and governance progress UI mapped to pipeline events
    - [ ] Implement SSE subscription helper that reconnects with exponential backoff on transient network issues and exposes parsed events
    - [ ] Map SSE event.type values from PRD to UI step states: planning → 'Cataloging', agent_start → 'Profiling', dq_gate → show gate result and details, agent_complete → mark completed step, human_required → pause and show explicit action, error → show failure panel
    - [ ] Show estimated time and running spinner; update progress percent heuristically as steps complete
    - [ ] Implement 'Cancel' and 'Retry' actions that call POST /api/v1/sessions/{id}/cancel or restart endpoints (create API stubs if backend not ready)
    - [ ] Add unit tests to validate SSE event parsing and UI state transitions including reconnect behavior
    - [ ] Add telemetry events for pipeline_step.started/completed/failed and connect errors
- [ ] TASK-F-049-07: Render condensed governance manifest summary and low-confidence review entry points
    - [ ] Design condensed manifest UI: list tables with health_score, PII flags, and definition_confidence badges
    - [ ] Implement ManifestSummary component to display top issues and provide 'Open full manifest' link
    - [ ] Implement 'Review definitions' modal anchor that opens semantic layer review UI (or placeholder with link to semantic editor) and a 'Skip for now' option with inline warning
    - [ ] Ensure PII flagged columns are highlighted and require explicit admin acknowledgement before enabling ML stages
    - [ ] Add tests to confirm skip flow records a warning in telemetry and toggles state appropriately
- [ ] TASK-F-049-08: Backend/devops coordination and lightweight API contract stubs
    - [ ] Document minimal API contract used by frontend: endpoints, expected SSE event types and payload shapes, error codes
    - [ ] Add integration tests (mocked) that validate frontend handles the documented SSE event stream
    - [ ] Work with backend engineers to implement or stub temporary endpoints with proper CORS and auth for the dev environment
    - [ ] Add CI job to run integration tests against API mocks
- [ ] TASK-F-049-09: Security & logging: ensure secrets handling and audit logging
    - [ ] Ensure credential input never written to localStorage, sessionStorage, or query params
    - [ ] Confirm fetch() calls sending credentials use TLS and do not include credentials in client-side logs
    - [ ] Add structured client-side log entries (level, component, workspace_id, error_code) to telemetry pipeline
    - [ ] Add automated test to assert that credential fields are not present in window.__analytiq_state or global variables

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for ConnectorCredentialsForm validation and secure submission
- [ ] Integration tests that mock POST /api/v1/integrations and return success/failure responses
- [ ] SSE integration test simulating a full governance stream and asserting UI step transitions (use a headless SSE server)
- [ ] Manual QA: validation of PII flag visibility, review badge, and skip flow
- [ ] Security test: automated scan to ensure no secrets in storage and no accidental console.log of credentials
- [ ] Load test: SSE client reconnect load under network flakiness simulated by throttling

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Connector list and credential form implemented and merged
- [ ] SSE-driven governance progress UI implemented and tested with mocked stream
- [ ] Governance manifest summary renders and low-confidence definitions show a 'Review' badge
- [ ] All form errors and pipeline errors surface friendly messages and provide retry
- [ ] Security checks (no secrets in storage/logs) validated in CI
- [ ] Integration contract documented and backend agrees on SSE payload schema

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-001
- [ ] F-004

###### Agent Notes
- SSE stream mapping must be robust to extra/unrecognized event types; unknown events should be logged and shown in a collapsible 'raw event' inspector for debug. For MVP, stream should be mocked if backend not ready; ensure the UI can function with deterministic mock sequences. Respect ABAC: only show the 'connect' flows to users with required role.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-049-03: As a new user I want the 'Start your first analysis' flow to create a session, let me confirm the session_spec (semantic context), run the pipeline with live status updates, and surface an embedded artifact preview so that I can save the artifact to the workspace when the pipeline completes.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User can open 'New Analysis' from onboarding flow and the UI fetches semantic layer via GET /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema before starting the session
- [ ] Session planner UI displays suggested explores, metrics and presents at most one clarifying multiple-choice question when intent_confidence < 0.85
- [ ] User can confirm the plain-English session_spec before execution and confirm/modify date_range and target_metric
- [ ] Starting the session posts to POST /api/v1/sessions and subscribes to SSE /api/v1/sessions/{id}/stream to display pipeline status with step-level messages mapped to PRD event types
- [ ] When artifact_ready event is received, UI shows live embedded artifact preview (iframe) using artifact_url from event and includes Save to workspace button
- [ ] All API failures (schema fetch, session start, SSE disconnect, artifact fetch) present clear error UI with retry and do not expose raw stack traces
- [ ] Saving artifact to workspace calls POST /api/v1/artifacts and on success shows artifact in workspace list and emits analytics event workspace.artifact.created

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-049-10: Implement session planner UI for initial analysis confirmation
    - [ ] Fetch semantic layer: call GET /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema and handle loading/error states
    - [ ] Render explores and metric chips as selectable UI elements using semantic names only
    - [ ] Implement one-question clarifier UI that appears when suggested intent_confidence < 0.85 and enforces multiple-choice only
    - [ ] Render plain-English session_spec preview and allow inline edits for date_range and prediction_horizon
    - [ ] Add unit tests for semantic fetch success and failure flows
- [ ] TASK-F-049-11: Integrate session start API and SSE pipeline status handling
    - [ ] Implement startSession() to POST /api/v1/sessions with confirmed session_spec and receive session_id
    - [ ] Subscribe to SSE at /api/v1/sessions/{session_id}/message with robust reconnect/backoff and heartbeat handling
    - [ ] Map SSE event types to status timeline UI (Building feature table → Training models → Backtesting → Generating dashboard)
    - [ ] Implement step-level logs expandable to show agent messages, dq_gate details, and remediation suggestions from human_required events
    - [ ] Add tests to simulate SSE event sequences and assert UI updates and final artifact_ready handling
- [ ] TASK-F-049-12: Render artifact preview and Save to workspace flow
    - [ ] On artifact_ready, parse event payload for artifact_url, artifact_id, and display embedded artifact in sandboxed iframe with allow-same-origin disabled as appropriate
    - [ ] Implement Save to workspace button that posts POST /api/v1/artifacts with minimal metadata and session_spec
    - [ ] Handle artifact save success by refreshing workspace artifacts list and navigating user to artifact details view
    - [ ] Implement fallback when artifact preview fails to load: show download link, retry load, and error card with actionable suggestions
    - [ ] Add tests for iframe rendering, save flow, and failure fallback
- [ ] TASK-F-049-13: Error handling, retries, and observability
    - [ ] Standardize error display component to translate backend error codes into user-friendly messages and suggested actions
    - [ ] Implement client-side retry strategies for session start and artifact fetch with exponential backoff and capped retries
    - [ ] Add structured client logs for session lifecycle events (session_start, session_error, session_complete) with trace_id if supplied
    - [ ] Expose a debug panel (dev-only) showing raw SSE events for faster debugging during QA
- [ ] TASK-F-049-14: Testing and QA: E2E and accessibility
    - [ ] Write Cypress E2E test covering: open onboarding CTA → connect minimal mock integration → start new analysis → confirm spec → run through mocked pipeline SSE → artifact preview → save artifact
    - [ ] Run automated accessibility tests (axe) on session planner and pipeline timeline screens
    - [ ] Coordinate manual exploratory testing session with QA to cover network interruptions, SSE reconnections, and error states
    - [ ] Add regression test to CI to ensure onboarding flow does not regress on future UI changes

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for session planner UI and API call stubbing
- [ ] SSE simulation test: assert timeline transitions and proper handling of human_required events
- [ ] Cypress E2E test that covers full happy path and key failure paths (schema fetch error, session start 500, SSE disconnect)
- [ ] Accessibility test: keyboard-only flow and screen reader announcements for session_spec confirmation
- [ ] Security test: ensure iframe uses sandbox attributes and embed_token query param is not leaked to parent document
- [ ] Performance test: validate artifact preview loads within PRD target times in test harness

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Semantic schema load, session confirm, session start, SSE-driven pipeline UI, artifact preview, and save flows implemented and merged
- [ ] Automated unit, integration, and E2E tests added and passing in CI
- [ ] Accessibility rules (aria, keyboard, contrast) validated for planner, timeline, and artifact preview
- [ ] Retry and error handling implemented and verified under simulated failure modes
- [ ] Telemetry and structured logs present for all key lifecycle events
- [ ] Documentation updated: onboarding first analysis flow, known limitations, and debug instructions

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-001
- [ ] F-004

###### Agent Notes
- UI must treat SSE as authoritative for pipeline state; do not attempt to infer state from partial messages. Correlate client logs with trace_id included in SSE events when present. For MVP, backend may return mock artifact_url; ensure iframe sandboxing and CSP are enforced. Keep the session_spec confirmation step minimal — do not surface raw SQL or column names.

### Epic: UI - Integrations

#### Requirement / Feature: Integration setup wizard screen (frontend credential form + validation)

**Feature ID:** `F-050`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `frontend`

**Rationale:** Onboarding includes connecting a data source via secure form per MVP mockups.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-004
- [ ] F-003

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-050-01: As a workspace admin, I want a guided integration setup wizard with a secure credential form so that I can connect a new data source during onboarding with clear validation and no secret leakage.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Wizard UI presents connector selection and a credential form matching the selected connector (Snowflake, BigQuery, Postgres, MySQL, DuckDB) with the exact fields required by backend API
- [ ] Client-side validation for required fields, syntactic checks (host, port, project id), and immediate field-level error messages
- [ ] Sensitive fields (password, private_key) are masked by default with an explicit reveal toggle that is logged as a non-secret event
- [ ] Credentials are submitted only over HTTPS to the backend endpoint POST /api/v1/integrations and are not stored in frontend logs or localStorage/sessionStorage
- [ ] On submit, UI shows atomic transaction state: validating -> storing -> queued_for_governance with an operation id returned from backend
- [ ] If backend returns success, the wizard shows a link/button to view governance manifest and next steps; if backend returns error, the wizard surfaces mapped, actionable error messages
- [ ] Form and flows meet WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility for keyboard navigation, labels, aria attributes, and screen-reader compatibility

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-050-01: Implement wizard UI shell and connector selection screen
    - [ ] Create React route/page for /workspace/:id/integrations/new with guarded access by workspace role
    - [ ] Implement connector picker component with icons, short descriptions, and analytic event on selection
    - [ ] Wire selection to credential form renderer that switches fields per connector type
    - [ ] Implement breadcrumb and back/cancel behaviors consistent with MVP mockups
    - [ ] Add unit tests for connector picker and route authorization
- [ ] TASK-F-050-02: Build credential form components with client-side validation
    - [ ] Define form schema for each connector (field names, types, placeholders, validators)
    - [ ] Implement reusable controlled input components with label, help text, and error display
    - [ ] Integrate validation library (Yup or Zod) and implement synchronous checks (required, host format, port numeric, JSON key valid)
    - [ ] Implement masked inputs for secrets with accessible reveal toggle and no persistence in localStorage
    - [ ] Add visual inline helper for OAuth vs username/password flows
- [ ] TASK-F-050-03: Integrate submit flow to backend with streaming validation feedback
    - [ ] Implement frontend call to POST /api/v1/integrations with encrypted payload via HTTPS
    - [ ] Attach a trace_id header returned by backend and show it in non-sensitive UI for support
    - [ ] Open SSE/WebSocket to /api/v1/integrations/{op_id}/events to stream validation progress (cataloging, profiling, health score)
    - [ ] Render stepper UI with skeleton states, success/warn/fail badges per streamed event
    - [ ] Retry transient network failures with exponential backoff and surface retry attempts to user
- [ ] TASK-F-050-04: Implement client-side telemetry and secure logging rules
    - [ ] Emit anonymized telemetry events for form usage, connector type, and validation outcomes without including secrets
    - [ ] Add guard rails in logging utilities to block accidental serialization of fields named password, private_key, token, secret
    - [ ] Add unit tests asserting logging sanitizer strips secret fields
    - [ ] Update frontend error boundary to capture and redact any integration form state before sending to Sentry
- [ ] TASK-F-050-05: UX copy, accessibility attributes, and error mapping
    - [ ] Write concise microcopy for each field, error state, and next-step guidance
    - [ ] Add aria-labels, aria-describedby for helper text, and role attributes for progress steps
    - [ ] Map backend error codes to friendly messages (e.g., auth_failed -> 'Invalid credentials', network_unreachable -> 'Network unreachable')
    - [ ] Add keyboard-only and screen-reader acceptance tests

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for form components and validation rules with coverage > 80%
- [ ] Integration test that mocks backend API to verify submission flow and SSE event handling
- [ ] E2E Cypress test: render wizard, fill Snowflake credentials (mock), submit, verify stepper progresses to queued_for_governance state
- [ ] Accessibility automated tests (axe) and manual keyboard/screen-reader verification
- [ ] Security linting to assert no localStorage/sessionStorage usage for secret fields

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] All frontend code merged to feature branch with peer review
- [ ] Credential form implements connector-specific fields and client-side validation
- [ ] Form submits to POST /api/v1/integrations and opens SSE stream to receive progress events
- [ ] Secrets are masked in UI, never stored in localStorage/sessionStorage, and not present in any logs
- [ ] Automated unit tests for form components and client-side validation pass in CI
- [ ] Accessibility checks (axe-core) run in CI with no blocking critical failures
- [ ] Feature behind feature-flag and documented in onboarding docs

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-004
- [ ] F-003
- [ ] Backend POST /api/v1/integrations endpoint
- [ ] Secrets manager (Vault or AWS Secrets Manager) integration available in dev/staging

###### Agent Notes
- Implement UI as lightweight React components. Use controlled inputs, Zod/Yup for validation, and a small state machine for wizard steps. Do not attempt to validate credentials client-side beyond syntax; always defer live connection validation to backend. Ensure all requests include trace_id and CSRF token. Use SSE for progress streaming as product-level streaming protocol is SSE in PRD.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-050-02: As an analyst or admin, I want immediate, actionable connection validation and clear error states so that I can correct credentials or network issues and know when governance profiling begins.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Submitting credentials triggers backend connection validation that streams structured events: cataloging, profiling, define, health_score, dq_gate_status
- [ ] UI displays PASS/WARN/BLOCK states per gate and an overall status with recommended next action
- [ ] Errors are mapped to user-friendly messages and include remediation hints (e.g., open firewall port 5432, enable OAuth and paste service account JSON)
- [ ] On WARN the UI allows explicit user acknowledgement to proceed to limited descriptive analytics (if allowed by policies); on BLOCK the UI prevents proceeding and links to admin review workflow
- [ ] All validation attempts are logged in the audit trail with an operation id, actor id, timestamp, and non-secret diagnostics

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-050-06: Design and implement backend connection validation service
    - [ ] Create an async validation job handler that attempts a non-destructive connection and sample queries against the source
    - [ ] Implement connectors for Snowflake, Postgres, MySQL, BigQuery that run safe catalog and profile queries limited to 10k sample rows
    - [ ] Emit structured SSE events matching pipeline step types: cataloging, profiling, define, health_score, dq_gate
    - [ ] Ensure validation job masks or excludes any PII values from diagnostics
    - [ ] Write unit tests for connector adapters using recorded fixtures
- [ ] TASK-F-050-07: Implement mapping of native DB errors to UX-friendly error codes
    - [ ] Define canonical error codes: auth_failed, network_unreachable, dns_error, permission_denied, ssl_required, schema_missing
    - [ ] Implement adapter layer that converts driver exceptions to canonical codes with sanitized diagnostic text
    - [ ] Create table of remediation hints per code for UI consumption
    - [ ] Add integration tests that simulate common failure modes and assert correct mapping
- [ ] TASK-F-050-08: Hook validation results into governance pipeline initiation
    - [ ] On successful validation, enqueue governance agent job and return operation id and governance_job_id to frontend
    - [ ] If validation returns WARN/BLOCK update integration record with dq_gate_status and human_review_required flag
    - [ ] Ensure governance initiation does not receive raw credentials; pass connector reference and secrets token stored in secrets manager
    - [ ] Add telemetry and audit log entries for governance enqueue with actor_id and trace_id
- [ ] TASK-F-050-09: Frontend: render streamed validation events and gate outcomes
    - [ ] Implement stepper UI that receives events and updates step states with PASS/WARN/BLOCK icons
    - [ ] Show detailed panel on step click with sanitized diagnostics and remediation hints
    - [ ] Add 'Acknowledge Warning' CTA when WARN is present with explicit checkbox confirm
    - [ ] On BLOCK, disable Next and show 'Request review' button that opens an Admin support modal
- [ ] TASK-F-050-10: Logging, auditing, and observability for validation jobs
    - [ ] Log structured events to audit store: { op_id, workspace_id, integration_id, actor_id, status, non-secret_diagnostics }
    - [ ] Emit OpenTelemetry spans for connector validation operations with trace_id propagation
    - [ ] Add Prometheus metrics: integration_validation_requests_total, integration_validation_failures_total, avg_validation_latency_seconds
    - [ ] Add alerting rule for repeated integration validation failures (>5 failures in 10m) to oncall channel

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for error mapping and connector adapters with simulated driver exceptions
- [ ] Integration tests that spin up ephemeral DB instances (Postgres/MySQL) to verify catalog/profile sampling
- [ ] E2E test that submits credentials, consumes SSE events, and verifies UI stepper transitions
- [ ] Security test to confirm diagnostics do not include secret substrings and that audit logs redact secrets
- [ ] Load test: run 100 concurrent validation jobs in staging and monitor latency and failure rates

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Backend validation jobs run asynchronously and emit SSE events consumed by frontend
- [ ] All common DB error cases are mapped to canonical codes and remediation hints
- [ ] Governance job enqueuing occurs only after validation success or explicit WARN acknowledgement
- [ ] Audit log records each validation attempt without storing secrets
- [ ] Integration validation metrics and traces are visible in staging observability dashboards
- [ ] Automated integration tests and E2E tests for streaming validation pass

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] Secrets manager write/read API
- [ ] SSE/WebSocket streaming infra already present (or implemented per F-003)
- [ ] Connector driver libs available in backend runtime

###### Agent Notes
- Validation logic must be conservative: use read-only sample queries and never attempt destructive operations. Keep the diagnostic payload minimal and structured. Use the existing SSE event types per PRD (type: planning, agent_start, dq_gate, agent_complete, error) and ensure clients can reconcile op_id. Implement connector adapters with well-defined timeouts and circuit-breaker behavior.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-050-03: As a QA/security lead, I want the integration wizard to enforce storage, transport, and audit security controls and pass automated accessibility tests so that the onboarding flow is production-safe and compliant with platform security policies.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All credentials submitted by the UI are delivered to backend over TLS and persisted only to the configured secrets manager (Vault or AWS Secrets Manager) encrypted with AES-256
- [ ] No secret values are written to logs, audit entries only include non-secret diagnostics and a hashed secret footprint for correlation
- [ ] Secrets are not stored in browser storage or in any artifact returned to the frontend
- [ ] Frontend and backend pass OWASP dependency checks and static analysis with no critical vulnerabilities
- [ ] Accessibility gate: axe-core automated checks produce zero critical violations in the integration wizard flows

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-050-11: Backend: implement secure secrets storage and access patterns
    - [ ] Implement server-side handler that receives credential payload and stores it in secrets manager with workspace-scoped key
    - [ ] Return a secrets_token/reference to integration record instead of raw credentials
    - [ ] Implement secret rotation hooks and metadata for created_by and created_at
    - [ ] Add server-side unit tests to verify storage path and readback of non-secret metadata
- [ ] TASK-F-050-12: Security hardening: logging, sanitization, and static analysis
    - [ ] Add middleware to redact secret-like fields from any outgoing logs and error payloads
    - [ ] Run dependency vulnerability scans (Snyk/OSS Index) and fix critical/high issues
    - [ ] Add automated CI job that fails PRs on new critical vulnerabilities or failing static analysis
    - [ ] Add unit tests to assert redaction utilities remove secrets from sample payloads
- [ ] TASK-F-050-13: DevOps: infra and access control for secrets manager
    - [ ] Provision secrets manager paths for dev/staging/prod with least-privilege IAM policies
    - [ ] Create CI/CD step to deploy backend changes with environment-specific secrets config
    - [ ] Implement monitoring and alerting for secrets manager access anomalies
    - [ ] Document runbooks for emergency secret rotation
- [ ] TASK-F-050-14: Testing: accessibility, security, and penetration checks
    - [ ] Add axe-core automated accessibility tests in CI for the integration wizard route
    - [ ] Run automated penetration tests targeting the integration API endpoints and verify no secret exfiltration
    - [ ] Perform manual keyboard and screen-reader QA for the wizard by QA team
    - [ ] Create regression tests asserting CSRF tokens and anti-forgery headers are required for POST requests
- [ ] TASK-F-050-15: Documentation and runbook updates
    - [ ] Document integration setup security model and data flow diagram in the engineering wiki
    - [ ] Publish instructions for workspace admins about least-privilege DB users and firewall rules
    - [ ] Include test accounts and steps in QA checklist for onboarding verification
    - [ ] Add troubleshooting guide for common validation errors with example fixes

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Automated CI security scans and dependency checks triggered on PR
- [ ] axe-core accessibility checks as part of PR gating
- [ ] Manual pen-test verification of integration endpoints and SSE streaming
- [ ] Audit log verification test: submit integration and assert audit record contains op_id, actor, timestamps, and no secrets
- [ ] Disaster recovery test: rotate secret and verify integration validation fails gracefully with guidance

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Secrets stored only in secrets manager with AES-256 at rest and IAM-restricted access
- [ ] No secret values appear in logs, traces, artifacts, or UI payloads
- [ ] CI passes dependency and static security scans; critical issues resolved
- [ ] Accessibility automation and manual checks complete with documented sign-off
- [ ] Runbooks and docs published and referenced in the onboarding playbook

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] Secrets manager (Vault or AWS Secrets Manager) provisioned
- [ ] CI/CD pipelines that run security scans
- [ ] Oncall and monitoring channels configured for alerting

###### Agent Notes
- Treat secrets as first-class restricted resources. Backend must never put secrets in any asynchronous event payloads consumed by agents; instead pass a secrets token that only the authorized service can dereference. Ensure CI/CD exposes a smoke test environment for secrets flows so QA can validate without using production secrets.

### Epic: UI - Sessions

#### Requirement / Feature: Session run status UI (pipeline progress cards: Building features → Training → Generating artifact)

**Feature ID:** `F-051`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `frontend`

**Rationale:** Users need visibility into pipeline progress; MVP mockups show step cards.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-018
- [ ] F-031

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-051-01: - [ ] As a workspace analyst, I want a real-time session run status UI composed of pipeline progress cards showing stages (Building features → Training → Generating artifact), so that I can monitor pipeline progress, surface stage-level outcomes, and act on blocking issues without leaving the workspace.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Progress UI is visible on the session details page and when a session run is active.
- [ ] - [ ] Pipeline stages render as ordered cards: Building features, Training, Generating artifact (cards may include sub-steps where applicable).
- [ ] - [ ] Card states map to streaming events: queued/running/success/warn/fail/cancelled and update in real time via SSE per PRD streaming protocol (types: agent_start, dq_gate, agent_complete, human_required, error, artifact_ready).
- [ ] - [ ] Each card shows at minimum: human-readable status, last updated timestamp, estimated duration or spinner, brief status message, and a link/button to expand full stage details/logs.
- [ ] - [ ] On dq_gate with WARN or FAIL the relevant card shows a clear badge and actionable remediation text; FAIL blocks subsequent stages and shows 'paused' / 'awaiting human input' state when applicable.
- [ ] - [ ] UI includes a compact summary header with global run status, progress percent (approx), and trace_id that links to logs/tracing view.
- [ ] - [ ] All visual elements meet accessibility requirements (aria labels, keyboard navigation) and responsive behavior (stacks below 768px).
- [ ] - [ ] Unauthorized users cannot subscribe to the SSE stream; SSE reconnect/backoff and error states are handled with visible retry controls.
- [ ] - [ ] UX matches MVP thin styling and re-uses existing workspace component tokens; file size and performance targets preserved.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-051-01: - [ ] Frontend: Create pipeline progress card components and layout on the session details page
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement Card container component with stage title, status badge, time/eta area, and expand/collapse control
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement compact summary header showing global status, progress percent, and trace_id link
    - [ ] - [ ] Add icons/spinners and micro-animations for state transitions (queued -> running -> success/fail)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement responsive CSS to stack cards below 768px and ensure layout matches mockups
    - [ ] - [ ] Add aria-labels, roles and keyboard focus handling for all interactive elements
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests for render states and accessibility attributes
- [ ] TASK-F-051-02: - [ ] Frontend: Implement SSE client and session-run state machine to drive UI updates
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement SSE connection to /api/v1/sessions/{id}/message using event types defined in PRD
    - [ ] - [ ] Parse events (agent_start, dq_gate, agent_complete, human_required, error, artifact_ready) into normalized stage state objects
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement reconnection strategy with exponential backoff and visible UI retry when disconnected
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate and handle malformed/unknown events with logging and safe no-op updates
    - [ ] - [ ] Add tests that mock SSE events to verify UI state transitions and reconnection behavior
    - [ ] - [ ] Instrument client to emit telemetry events for SSE lifecycle (connected, disconnected, errors)
- [ ] TASK-F-051-03: - [ ] Backend: Provide session streaming endpoint and normalized stage events
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement SSE endpoint /api/v1/sessions/{id}/message that authenticates and authorizes the caller
    - [ ] - [ ] Map internal agent orchestration messages to public SSE event types per PRD schema with consistent payload fields (stage_id, stage_name, status, message, timestamp, trace_id, details_url)
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure dq_gate events include gate_type, status (PASS|WARN|FAIL), and remediation payload when present
    - [ ] - [ ] Add server-side validation for outgoing event JSON and log any outbound event schema violations
    - [ ] - [ ] Add rate limiting and connection limits; return meaningful HTTP error codes for rejected connections
    - [ ] - [ ] Add integration tests that simulate pipeline runs and assert SSE payloads
- [ ] TASK-F-051-04: - [ ] Backend/AI: Instrument orchestration layer (agent runtime) to publish stage lifecycle events and include trace_id
    - [ ] - [ ] Add event publishing hooks at agent start/complete, dq_gate result, human_required, and artifact_ready points
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure each event includes trace_id, session_id, stage_name, stage_id, status, human_message, and optional error_code
    - [ ] - [ ] Redact secrets and any PII from event payloads before publishing
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests for event generation and redaction logic
    - [ ] - [ ] Add logs at INFO level for normal transitions and ERROR level for failures with stack traces
- [ ] TASK-F-051-05: - [ ] Data/Storage: Persist session run status and stage history to DB for audit and UI replay
    - [ ] - [ ] Define session_run_state table schema with session_id, run_id, stage_id, status, message, details_url, trace_id, created_at
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement write path from orchestrator into the session_run_state table for each stage transition
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement API GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/runs/{run_id}/history to retrieve stage timeline for UI expand/collapse
    - [ ] - [ ] Add migration scripts and backward-compatibility checks
    - [ ] - [ ] Add DB-level indexes on session_id and run_id for efficient retrieval
    - [ ] - [ ] Add tests to validate persistence and replay correctness
- [ ] TASK-F-051-06: - [ ] DevOps/Observability: Add metrics, traces and alerting for session streaming and UI scale
    - [ ] - [ ] Add Prometheus metrics: sse_connections, sse_errors, events_emitted_total, events_dropped_total
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure trace_id is propagated to OpenTelemetry and visible in Jaeger traces
    - [ ] - [ ] Create Grafana dashboard for SSE health and stage failure rates
    - [ ] - [ ] Add CI job to run lightweight load test simulating concurrent SSE connections
    - [ ] - [ ] Document ops runbook for investigating stuck runs and failing SSE
- [ ] TASK-F-051-07: - [ ] Security: Ensure secure access, authz and redaction for SSE and stage details
    - [ ] - [ ] Enforce API auth (OAuth 2.0) and ABAC checks on SSE connections and stage history endpoints
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement server-side redaction of any sensitive fields in event payloads
    - [ ] - [ ] Add role-based access control: only session owner or workspace members can subscribe
    - [ ] - [ ] Add penetration test checklist entries for SSE endpoint (CSRF, SSE hijack, header checks)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add automated security tests to CI verifying unauthorized access is rejected

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for card render states and SSE client parser
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests mocking orchestration to verify SSE-based UI updates
- [ ] - [ ] Contract tests validating SSE event schema emitted by backend
- [ ] - [ ] E2E test: simulate full pipeline run with successful path and assert UI shows each stage and final artifact_ready state
- [ ] - [ ] E2E test: simulate dq_gate.FAIL and human_required flows and assert UI blocks and surfaces remediation
- [ ] - [ ] Accessibility test via axe to assert aria attributes and keyboard navigability
- [ ] - [ ] Load test: verify SSE endpoint supports expected concurrent connections without degradation
- [ ] - [ ] Security test: attempt unauthorized SSE subscription and assert rejection

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] All frontend components implemented and reviewed with design
- [ ] - [ ] SSE stream connected to orchestration events and state updates reliably in staging
- [ ] - [ ] Backend publishes normalized events and persists stage history
- [ ] - [ ] Accessibility checks passed (axe CI) and responsive behavior verified
- [ ] - [ ] Unit, integration and end-to-end tests green in CI
- [ ] - [ ] Prometheus metrics and tracing integrated and dashboards created
- [ ] - [ ] Feature flagged and rollout plan documented
- [ ] - [ ] Product/QA sign-off on UX and error states

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] F-018
- [ ] - [ ] F-031
- [ ] - [ ] /api/v1/sessions streaming protocol per PRD (ensure matching event schema)
- [ ] - [ ] OpenTelemetry tracing infra (Jaeger/Honeycomb) available

###### Agent Notes
- - [ ] Map frontend state machine exactly to the PRD streaming event types; avoid adding new ad-hoc event types.
- [ ] Keep payloads minimal and structured; prefer a details_url to fetch verbose logs rather than embedding large logs in SSE messages.
- [ ] Implement optimistic UI behavior for short-running stages (show spinner + estimated time) but always trust authoritative event updates from SSE.
- [ ] Use existing workspace UI tokens/components where possible to maintain consistency and reduce bundle size.
- [ ] Feature-flag the new UI and gate server-side SSE behavior behind the same flag for safe rollout.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-051-02: - [ ] As an analyst, I want to drill into stage-level details and DQ gate results (with remediation suggestions) from each pipeline progress card, so that I can understand why a stage is paused/failed and either accept suggested fixes or escalate appropriately.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Stage expand opens a detail view/modal showing chronological stage events and structured DQ gate results when present.
- [ ] - [ ] DQ gate results show gate_name, status (PASS|WARN|FAIL), numeric metrics if applicable, failing columns/expressions, remediation suggestion text, and a button to view full report/logs.
- [ ] - [ ] On human_required events the UI presents the required action (e.g., 'confirm feature inclusion') and an explicit CTA to resume the pipeline; user actions are sent back to the orchestration via POST /api/v1/sessions/{id}/action with appropriate payload.
- [ ] - [ ] Detail view provides a link to persisted run history and to the model trial leaderboard when in the Training stage (if available).
- [ ] - [ ] Error and remediation messages are actionable, non-hallucinated, and produced deterministically by the orchestration layer or DQ validators.
- [ ] - [ ] All DQ payloads and full logs are sanitized (no credentials) before display.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-051-08: - [ ] Frontend: Implement stage detail modal with DQ gate table and action CTAs
    - [ ] - [ ] Design and implement the detail modal layout with timeline on left and DQ gate table on right
    - [ ] - [ ] Render DQ gate rows with status badge, short message, failing fields list, and remediation text
    - [ ] - [ ] Add 'View full report' button to open persisted DQ report in a new tab or drawer
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement human_required action form (confirm/modify/abort) and hook to POST action API
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit and integration tests for modal behavior and form submissions
- [ ] TASK-F-051-09: - [ ] Backend: Add DQ gate details API and action handling for human_required responses
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/runs/{run_id}/stages/{stage_id}/dq to return structured dq_gate results and remediation payloads
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement POST /api/v1/sessions/{id}/action to accept user decisions (e.g., accept_feature, expand_date_range) and validate payload against allowed actions
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure POST action is audited with actor, timestamp, and stored in session_run_state and audit log
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit and integration tests for DQ API and action handler including validation failures
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate that action triggers are idempotent or handled safely if retried
- [ ] TASK-F-051-10: - [ ] AI/Orchestration: Ensure DQ gate engine emits remediation payloads and human_required events
    - [ ] - [ ] Extend dq_gate results schema to include remediation_text, suggested_action_enum, and confidence_score
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit human_required SSE event with required_action and allowed_options when a gate requires explicit user confirmation
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure the remediation content is deterministic and derived from the DQ validator rules, not heuristics
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests validating remediation payloads for common failure modes (nulls, leakage, grain uniqueness)
- [ ] TASK-F-051-11: - [ ] Data: Persist full DQ reports (structured JSON) and surface a stable details_url
    - [ ] - [ ] Define dq_report storage schema and retention policy (e.g., 90 days by default)
    - [ ] - [ ] Store full profile statistics used to generate the gate (null rates, sample rows, offending joins) as JSON blob
    - [ ] - [ ] Generate details_url included in SSE/DQ APIs that the UI can fetch for full report rendering
    - [ ] - [ ] Add background job to purge old DQ reports after retention window
- [ ] TASK-F-051-12: - [ ] Testing: Create QA flows and automated tests for DQ failure and human_required flows
    - [ ] - [ ] Create automated scenario that triggers leakage_risk > 0.3 and assert UI blocks and shows remediation
    - [ ] - [ ] Create automated scenario that triggers PK uniqueness violation and assert blocking behavior
    - [ ] - [ ] Manual QA checklist for verifying data shown in DQ report matches underlying samples (no PII leakage)
    - [ ] - [ ] Accessibility and keyboard tests for modal interactions

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for DQ API JSON schema and validation logic
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests for action POST flow and orchestration resume behavior
- [ ] - [ ] E2E test to simulate a dq_gate.FAIL and assert UI blocks subsequent stages and resumes on user action
- [ ] - [ ] Security test ensuring DQ report does not expose PII or credentials
- [ ] - [ ] Manual QA to verify remediation text is actionable and non-hallucinated

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Detail modal displays structured DQ payloads and remediation reliably across scenarios
- [ ] - [ ] Actions posted from UI are validated, audited, and cause expected orchestration transitions
- [ ] - [ ] Full DQ reports persisted and accessible via details_url
- [ ] - [ ] Tests cover key failure and human_required flows
- [ ] - [ ] QA and product sign-off on remediation messaging and action UX

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] US-F-051-01 (session run status and SSE plumbing)
- [ ] - [ ] DQ gate implementation from Stage 0/3 per PRD (existing orchestration hooks)
- [ ] - [ ] Persistence (dq_report storage) available

###### Agent Notes
- - [ ] DQ remediation text must be generated deterministically from validator rules; do not rely on LLM free-form messages for remediation.
- [ ] Actions accepted from UI should map to a small allowed enum of server-side operations to avoid arbitrary commands being executed by orchestration.
- [ ] Persist full DQ JSON reports to avoid sending large payloads over SSE; use details_url in SSE instead.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-051-03: - [ ] As an engineering lead / operator, I want observability surfaced in the session UI (trace_id, link to raw logs, and summarized error messages) with role-based access, so that operations and engineers can debug failing runs quickly and audit run history.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Every session run card and detail view displays a trace_id that opens the distributed trace view (Jaeger/Honeycomb) in a new tab when clicked.
- [ ] - [ ] A logs panel is available in the stage detail modal that streams sanitized orchestrator/agent logs (no credentials/PII).
- [ ] - [ ] Only users with appropriate workspace roles can see raw logs (Owner/Editor/Operator); Viewer sees summarized messages only.
- [ ] - [ ] Logs are paginated/streamed and include timestamps, level, event_type and trace_id; redacted fields show placeholder text and are logged server-side with redaction markers.
- [ ] - [ ] Observability metrics (stage duration histogram, failure rate) are emitted and visible in Grafana dashboard created by DevOps task.
- [ ] - [ ] Operations can download a zipped run bundle (logs + DQ report + session_spec + model_card) for offline forensic analysis.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-051-13: - [ ] Backend/Observability: Ensure trace_id propagation and log collection for session runs
    - [ ] - [ ] Propagate trace_id through all agent calls, DB writes, and event publications
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure logs emitted by agents and orchestration include structured fields: trace_id, session_id, run_id, stage_id, level, message, timestamp
    - [ ] - [ ] Configure OpenTelemetry exporter to send traces to Jaeger/Honeycomb and ensure retention policy
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement server-side redaction rules for logs (PII/credentials regex + tokens) and add tests verifying redaction
    - [ ] - [ ] Expose secure link format for trace_id to open tracing UI in new tab (read-only)
- [ ] TASK-F-051-14: - [ ] Frontend: Add logs panel and role-based display logic to stage detail modal
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement logs panel that can stream via WebSocket or paginated API GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/runs/{run_id}/logs
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement role-based rendering: full logs for Owner/Editor/Operator, summarized only for Viewer
    - [ ] - [ ] Add log filters (level, time range) and a 'Download run bundle' CTA
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit/integration tests for RBAC enforcement and log streaming UI behavior
- [ ] TASK-F-051-15: - [ ] Backend: Implement logs API and run bundle exporter
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/runs/{run_id}/logs with authz checks and pagination
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement run bundle exporter that zips logs (redacted), dq_report JSON, session_spec, and model_card and exposes a signed download URL
    - [ ] - [ ] Add rate limiting and audit logging for bundle downloads
    - [ ] - [ ] Add tests for correct bundle contents and RBAC enforcement
- [ ] TASK-F-051-16: - [ ] DevOps: Create Grafana dashboards and alerts for session run observability
    - [ ] - [ ] Add dashboard panels: active runs, average stage duration, failure rate by stage, SSE connection errors
    - [ ] - [ ] Create alerts for high failure rate (> X% over Y minutes) and high SSE error rate
    - [ ] - [ ] Document escalation path and playbook for investigating alerts
- [ ] TASK-F-051-17: - [ ] Security/Compliance: Audit logging and access controls for logs and run bundles
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure all access to logs and run bundle downloads are recorded in audit log with actor and timestamp
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure run bundle download tokens expire and are single-use
    - [ ] - [ ] Add compliance checklist entries for retention and deletion of run artifacts

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for trace_id propagation and structured logging format
- [ ] - [ ] Integration test to ensure links open tracing UI and include correct trace_id
- [ ] - [ ] RBAC tests verifying Viewer cannot access raw logs or download bundles
- [ ] - [ ] E2E test that simulates a failing stage and verifies logs panel surfaces sanitized error and trace_id
- [ ] - [ ] Manual ops validation of Grafana dashboards and alert firing

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] trace_id visible and links to tracing tool for all session runs
- [ ] - [ ] Logs panel streams/supplies sanitized logs with RBAC enforced
- [ ] - [ ] Run bundle export works and is audited
- [ ] - [ ] Grafana dashboards and alerts provisioned and documented
- [ ] - [ ] Unit/integration/e2e tests for observability paths pass
- [ ] - [ ] Security review of log redaction and bundle downloads completed

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] OpenTelemetry & tracing backend (Jaeger/Honeycomb) provisioned
- [ ] - [ ] Logging infrastructure and retention policies available
- [ ] - [ ] Session run persistence (TASK-F-051-05) implemented

###### Agent Notes
- - [ ] Ensure redaction logic is deterministic and well-tested (avoid partial redaction patterns that leave secrets visible).
- [ ] Use existing audit_log append-only store for recording bundle downloads and user actions.
- [ ] Keep logs retention and bundle lifetime consistent with workspace compliance settings.

### Epic: UI - Resilience

#### Requirement / Feature: Error states & skeleton loaders for chat and artifact panels

**Feature ID:** `F-052`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `frontend`

**Rationale:** Stable UX requires explicit loading and error handling patterns per artifact validation rules.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-036
- [ ] F-018

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-052-01: As an end user, I want chat UI to show skeleton loaders while messages and session state are loading and a clear panel-level error state with retry when the chat stream or agent response fails so that I always see explicit progress, can recover from transient failures, and understand remediation actions.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Show a message-level skeleton placeholder for each incoming message while awaiting agent response within chat panel
- [ ] Show a global chat initialization skeleton while session_spec and semantic context are loading
- [ ] On API or SSE stream error, replace the affected message skeleton with a bordered error card containing: error summary, error_code, retry button, and a link to view trace_id
- [ ] Retry action re-invokes the same session/message endpoint with exponential backoff and shows updated skeleton and outcome
- [ ] All chat skeletons and error cards include aria attributes for screen readers and keyboard-focusable retry controls
- [ ] Client logs the error event with trace_id, http status, agent name, and user_id to the observability pipeline
- [ ] Frontend shows an unobtrusive toast for fatal session-level failures with CTAs: Retry, Save Draft, Contact Support

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-052-01: Design skeleton and error UX for chat
    - [ ] Create visual specs for message-level skeleton (height, shimmer, placeholder avatars) and global chat skeleton
    - [ ] Create visual specs for error card states including content layout, copy, retry button, and trace_id display
    - [ ] Produce accessibility spec: aria-labels, roles, keyboard focus order, contrast checks
    - [ ] Obtain PM/design signoff and attach to ticket
- [ ] TASK-F-052-02: Implement frontend skeleton components and error card for chat
    - [ ] Add MessageSkeleton React/Vue/TS component with configurable width/rows and CSS custom properties for dark mode
    - [ ] Add ChatInitSkeleton component displayed during session_spec and semantic context load
    - [ ] Add ChatErrorCard component with props: error_code, message, trace_id, retryCallback and ensure retry is keyboard accessible
    - [ ] Integrate skeletons into chat render path to show while awaiting SSE / fetch responses
- [ ] TASK-F-052-03: Backend: standardize chat error payloads and SSE error events
    - [ ] Define error JSON schema for chat endpoints: { code, message, remediation, trace_id, retryable: bool }
    - [ ] Update session/message SSE producer to emit type: error events with the above schema when agents fail
    - [ ] Ensure server attaches trace_id header (X-Trace-Id) on all chat responses and SSE connection handshake
    - [ ] Add tests validating SSE error event shape and trace_id propagation
- [ ] TASK-F-052-04: Implement retry logic and telemetry for chat retries
    - [ ] Implement client-side exponential backoff retry strategy with max attempts and jitter for retryable errors
    - [ ] Record telemetry event for each retry attempt including attempt_count and error_code
    - [ ] Implement server-side idempotency for message replays (idempotency_key) to avoid duplicated agent runs
    - [ ] Add configuration toggle/feature-flag to disable automatic retries in dev/test environments
- [ ] TASK-F-052-05: Testing and QA for chat skeletons and error states
    - [ ] Write unit tests for skeleton components and ChatErrorCard accessibility attributes
    - [ ] Write integration tests mocking SSE stream errors to verify UI transitions from skeleton to error and back on retry
    - [ ] Add end-to-end Cypress tests covering: normal response flow, transient error + retry success, permanent error + user actions
    - [ ] Add performance test to ensure skeleton render does not exceed 50ms on first paint for chat panel
- [ ] TASK-F-052-06: DevOps and observability
    - [ ] Add log lines and structured events for chat errors to the observability pipeline with trace_id and session_id
    - [ ] Instrument distributed tracing propagation from frontend request through SSE producer and agent calls
    - [ ] Add an alert rule for repeated chat SSE errors per workspace (>5 errors/min) to the SRE rota
    - [ ] Deploy frontend feature-flag gating via LaunchDarkly (or similar) for progressive rollout

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for skeleton and error components verifying DOM structure and props
- [ ] Integration tests that mock SSE streams to assert UI transitions: loading -> success, loading -> error -> retry -> success
- [ ] E2E tests (Cypress) simulating network failures and validating retry flows and toasts
- [ ] Accessibility tests: automated axe checks and manual screen-reader walkthroughs for retry flow
- [ ] Performance test: measure first paint and skeleton render time on representative devices
- [ ] Backend contract tests that validate error JSON schema and X-Trace-Id header propagation

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] All chat skeleton components implemented and imported where messages are rendered
- [ ] ChatErrorCard implemented and displayed for all error cases defined in acceptance criteria
- [ ] Backend emits SSE error events and standard error payloads with trace_id
- [ ] Retry logic works end-to-end with idempotency and telemetry recorded for each attempt
- [ ] Automated unit, integration, and e2e tests pass in CI
- [ ] Accessibility checks pass (axe-core automated checks + keyboard navigation manual pass)
- [ ] Observability events and tracing surface the full error context and alerts are configured

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-036
- [ ] F-018

###### Agent Notes
- Ensure message-level skeletons are small and lightweight to keep artifact HTML small; reuse existing skeleton CSS tokens from artifact panels. Back-end SSE error events must follow the streaming event schema in the PRD (type: error) and include remediation text. Use idempotency keys for retryed messages to avoid duplicate agent invocations. Coordinate with F-036 and F-018 owners for shared session and semantic_context loading hooks.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-052-02: As an artifact viewer, I want each dashboard panel to show a skeleton loader while panel data is fetching and a deterministic error card with retry when the panel API fails so that the artifact always communicates panel-level progress and recovers from partial failures without breaking the entire artifact.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each artifact panel (KPI, time series, feature importance, dimension breakdown, forecast, leaderboard, DQ footer) shows a skeleton loader while its gold layer API call is in-flight
- [ ] If a panel fetch() call throws or returns non-2xx, the panel displays a bordered error card with concise error message, error_code, retry button, and trace_id; other panels continue rendering
- [ ] All fetch() calls within artifact HTML are wrapped in try/catch and surface structured errors to the panel error card as required by the deterministic validator
- [ ] Retry button calls the same endpoint with the same query params and updates the panel state on success
- [ ] Skeletons and error cards obey responsive and dark mode CSS custom properties and include aria-labels and role attributes
- [ ] Artifact generator agent includes skeleton markup in generated HTML and passes artifact validator checks (no hardcoded colors, aria attributes present, fetch wrapped in try/catch)

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-052-07: Design skeletons and error cards for artifact panels
    - [ ] Create lightweight skeleton patterns for each standard panel type matching grid/layout constraints
    - [ ] Define error card copy templates for panel-level failures including user-friendly remediation and trace_id display
    - [ ] Produce dark-mode CSS variables and responsive behavior for skeletons and error cards
    - [ ] Get design and accessibility signoff for panel patterns
- [ ] TASK-F-052-08: Update artifact generation agent to emit skeleton and error handling code
    - [ ] Modify artifact HTML templates to include skeleton markup for each panel and ensure window.__analytiq_state initialization
    - [ ] Wrap all fetch() calls in try/catch and render panel-level ErrorCard with error payload including trace_id
    - [ ] Add standardized retry handler that re-fetches panel data and re-renders only the affected panel
    - [ ] Update artifact validator logic to check for presence of skeleton markup and try/catch wrapped fetch calls
- [ ] TASK-F-052-09: Frontend runtime: implement panel skeletons, error rendering, and retry wiring
    - [ ] Implement PanelSkeleton, PanelErrorCard, and PanelLoader utilities for reuse across artifact templates
    - [ ] Implement fetchWithDiagnostics wrapper that attaches X-Trace-Id, records timing, and returns structured error objects
    - [ ] Wire retry button to call fetchWithDiagnostics with same URL and params and ensure CSV/PNG download buttons respect panel recovery state
    - [ ] Ensure window.__analytiq_state.updateFilters and re-render pipeline tolerates partial panel failures
- [ ] TASK-F-052-10: Backend: ensure gold layer API returns structured error payloads and traceable headers
    - [ ] Define the gold layer API error contract: { code, message, retriable, trace_id, details } and document in API spec
    - [ ] Instrument gold layer endpoints to return X-Trace-Id header and structured JSON error bodies on failures
    - [ ] Ensure rate-limit and quota responses include retry-after headers and retriable=false where applicable
    - [ ] Add unit tests for error cases and integration tests validating header and body shape
- [ ] TASK-F-052-11: Testing and QA for artifact skeletons and panel errors
    - [ ] Add unit tests for PanelSkeleton and PanelErrorCard verifying DOM, aria attributes, and dark-mode variables
    - [ ] Add artifact generation agent unit/integration tests verifying try/catch wrapping and skeleton presence in generated HTML
    - [ ] Create e2e artifact render tests that simulate per-panel API failures to validate independent panel recovery and retry behavior
    - [ ] Run artifact validator locally on sample artifacts to ensure no hardcoded colors and no global event handlers
- [ ] TASK-F-052-12: Security and performance checks for artifacts
    - [ ] Verify that trace_id and error details emitted to client do not include sensitive payloads or raw PII
    - [ ] Ensure skeleton and error markup does not increase artifact HTML size above 250KB; minify and bench file size
    - [ ] Add monitoring for frequent per-panel failures and create alerting thresholds for artifact refresh error rates
    - [ ] Ensure embed_token usage remains enforced when retry triggers requests from embedded artifacts

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for PanelSkeleton and PanelErrorCard including aria-label presence and keyboard focus tests
- [ ] Integration tests verifying fetchWithDiagnostics returns structured errors and that retry re-invokes fetch
- [ ] E2E tests that simulate one or more panel API failures and validate that other panels render and retries succeed
- [ ] Static analysis to ensure artifact generator includes try/catch around all fetch() calls
- [ ] Accessibility manual test pass for screen-reader and keyboard interaction with retry buttons
- [ ] Performance test to measure artifact HTML size and panel render times under cold and warm CDN cache

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] All artifact panels include skeleton loader markup and display while data is fetching
- [ ] All fetch() calls in generated artifacts are wrapped in try/catch and return structured errors to panel-level error cards
- [ ] Retry flows re-fetch and re-render only affected panels without reloading the full artifact
- [ ] Artifact validator passes on generated artifacts (skeletons present, try/catch used, aria attributes present, no hardcoded colors)
- [ ] Unit, integration, and e2e tests covering per-panel failure and recovery are passing in CI
- [ ] No increase in artifact HTML size beyond target thresholds after minification
- [ ] Observability and security checks for panel error info are in place

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-036
- [ ] F-018

###### Agent Notes
- Artifact generator changes must remain within the single-file, zero-framework constraint. Prefer minimal vanilla JS for skeleton and error rendering to avoid file size growth. fetchWithDiagnostics should attach workspace_id and artifact_id as headers and include X-Trace-Id returned by backend. Coordinate with gold layer API team to ensure error contract and headers are implemented; update /api/v1/gold/* API docs. Ensure generated HTML still meets validator constraints in PRD §4.7.2 and §4.7.7.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-052-03: As an SRE/engineer, I want standardized error schemas, tracing, and observability for panel and chat errors surfaced to the UI so that errors are actionable, traceable across services, and trigger alerts when systemic failures occur.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Define and publish a standard error schema used by chat SSE events and gold layer API: { code, message, retriable, remediation, trace_id, details }
- [ ] All UI-facing error surfaces display trace_id and a non-sensitive remediation string returned by backend
- [ ] Backend logs errors with trace_id, service, endpoint, http_status, and agent_name to the structured logging pipeline
- [ ] Distributed tracing propagates trace_id across agent calls, warehouse queries, and artifact generation, visible in tracing UI
- [ ] Create monitoring alerts for high rate of retriable errors in a workspace and for repeated non-retriable errors across workspaces
- [ ] Document runbook for common error codes and remediation steps accessible from support console

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-052-13: Specify and document the standard error schema and HTTP header conventions
    - [ ] Draft error JSON schema compatible with existing SSE and REST conventions and include fields: code, message, retriable, remediation, trace_id, details
    - [ ] Define HTTP header X-Trace-Id generation and propagation rules for frontend and backend
    - [ ] Publish schema to API docs and notify all dependent teams; include example payloads and recommended client handling
    - [ ] Add schema to contract tests used by CI for backend services
- [ ] TASK-F-052-14: Implement tracing and logging for errors across services
    - [ ] Ensure every service attaches and forwards X-Trace-Id and emits structured logs containing trace_id, service_name, endpoint, agent_name
    - [ ] Instrument agent runtime to include trace_id in LLM calls and include model+prompt tokens metadata (obfuscated as per privacy rules)
    - [ ] Add log enrichment for workspace_id and artifact_id to correlate UI errors to workspace activity
    - [ ] Add CI contract tests verifying trace_id appears in logs for simulated failures
- [ ] TASK-F-052-15: Monitoring and alerting for error patterns
    - [ ] Create Kibana/Grafana dashboards showing error rates by code, workspace, and endpoint
    - [ ] Add alert: workspace-level retriable error rate > 5/min for 5m triggers pager for SRE
    - [ ] Add alert: system-wide non-retriable error spike > threshold triggers ops-channel notification
    - [ ] Create automated report for top N recurring error codes weekly to product and engineering leads
- [ ] TASK-F-052-16: Security review and privacy gating for error payloads
    - [ ] Run threat model to ensure error.details does not leak PII or sensitive SQL; redact any sensitive fields
    - [ ] Add automated test to reject error payloads containing patterns matching PII detectors
    - [ ] Ensure agent logging obfuscates any raw user data before persisting
    - [ ] Obtain security signoff for exposing trace_id to users in UI
- [ ] TASK-F-052-17: Support runbook and developer docs
    - [ ] Write a runbook mapping common error codes to remediation steps for support engineers
    - [ ] Add developer guide showing how to instrument services to emit the standard error schema and how UI should render and retry
    - [ ] Provide sample curl/SSE examples and postman collection entries for error cases
    - [ ] Host a 30-min walkthrough session with support and on-call engineers

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Contract tests ensuring services return the defined error JSON schema for simulated failures
- [ ] Integration tests validating trace_id propagation from frontend request to backend log and back to UI
- [ ] Load tests to simulate high error rates and verify alerts trigger correctly
- [ ] Security tests to scan error payloads for PII and sensitive patterns
- [ ] Manual verification of runbook remediation steps for top 5 error codes

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Standard error schema published and enforced by backend contract tests
- [ ] Trace_id propagated end-to-end and visible in UI error cards and server logs
- [ ] Structured logs and tracing available in observability tools with dashboards and alerts configured
- [ ] Security review passed ensuring no PII in error payloads exposed to clients
- [ ] Support runbook published and developer docs updated
- [ ] Automated tests for contract, tracing, and log enrichment passing in CI

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-036
- [ ] F-018

###### Agent Notes
- Keep the error schema extensible but stable; prefer small integer codes and canonical strings for programmatic handling. Expose trace_id to users but avoid exposing stack traces or raw SQL. Use existing distributed tracing solution (OpenTelemetry) and ensure sampling strategy captures error traces at high fidelity. Coordinate with observability and security teams to wire alerts to the correct on-call rotations and to validate that error.details are sanitized before being passed to the UI.
