## Sprint 13 - Deterministic Gate APIs & Query Endpoints

**Sprint Goal:** Expose deterministic gate and query endpoints required by MVP flows and enable basic observability.

### Sprint Completion Checklist
- [ ] All P0 stories complete
- [ ] All blocking defects resolved
- [ ] Tests passing
- [ ] Demo-ready increment produced
- [ ] Milestone accepted

### Epic: DQ Engine

#### Requirement / Feature: Data Quality Gate engine (evaluate gates and produce pass/warn/block outcomes)

**Feature ID:** `F-053`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, data`

**Rationale:** DQ gates are deterministic checkpoints across stages; engine required end-to-end.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-006
- [ ] F-009

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-053-01: As the pipeline orchestrator, I want a deterministic Data Quality (DQ) Gate engine that evaluates standardized DQ rules against a governance_manifest and gold-layer artifacts so that each pipeline stage can advance, warn, or block reliably based on machine-checkable outcomes.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Given a governance_manifest JSON input, the engine evaluates configured DQ rules and returns a structured dq_gate_result JSON with outcome values PASS|WARN|BLOCK
- [ ] dq_gate_result contains rule_id, rule_name, severity, outcome, human_readable_message, details (keyed metrics), trace_id, evaluated_at timestamp, and suggested_remediation when outcome is WARN or BLOCK
- [ ] Engine evaluates the MVP gate set: schema_fingerprint, pk_uniqueness, null_rate_for_key_columns, row_count_minimum, freshness_sla, pii_detection and distribution_shift (3σ) as defined in PRD tables
- [ ] Engine deterministically produces the same outcome for the same input governance_manifest and random_seed-independent logic; non-deterministic operations (e.g. sampling) are bounded and logged with sample_seed
- [ ] On BLOCK outcome, the engine returns HTTP 409 (or service-level error signal) when invoked via API and emits an SSE dq_gate event with type 'dq_gate' and outcome BLOCK for consuming orchestrator
- [ ] On WARN outcome, the engine returns HTTP 200 with dq_gate_result and emits SSE event with outcome WARN; pipeline may continue but audit log records warning
- [ ] On PASS outcome, the engine returns HTTP 200 with dq_gate_result and emits SSE event with outcome PASS
- [ ] All evaluations are logged to the pipeline audit log with actor, trace_id, governance_manifest_version, and dq_gate_result hash and persisted to the dq_gate_results table
- [ ] The engine exposes a validation layer: it fails fast for invalid input (missing manifest_version, workspace_id, generated_at) with HTTP 400 and a structured error payload
- [ ] Performance: synchronous evaluation for governance_manifest with ≤ 500 tables completes within 60s P95 for MVP connector (Snowflake/duckDB), otherwise returns an async job id and responds with 202 Accepted
- [ ] Comprehensive unit, integration, and E2E tests exist and pass in CI; feature covered by automated QA scenarios described in the test_plan

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-053-01: Design dq_gate_result JSON schema and JSON Schema validator
    - [ ] Draft dq_gate_result JSON specification mapping to PRD fields (rule_id, rule_name, severity, outcome, message, details, trace_id, evaluated_at, suggested_remediation)
    - [ ] Author JSON Schema (Draft-07) for dq_gate_result and error responses
    - [ ] Write TypeScript/POJO model classes for dq_gate_result and error payloads
    - [ ] Add unit tests validating schema acceptance/rejection with example payloads
    - [ ] Document schema in API docs and agent contract docs
- [ ] TASK-F-053-02: Implement core DQ rule evaluator service (backend) with pluggable rule registry
    - [ ] Create backend service module that accepts parsed governance_manifest and executes rule pipeline
    - [ ] Implement pluggable rule registry interface and register MVP rule implementations (schema_fingerprint, pk_uniqueness, null_rate_key_cols, row_count_minimum, freshness_sla, pii_detection, distribution_shift)
    - [ ] Implement deterministic sampling utilities with seed handling for any sample-based checks
    - [ ] Map rule results to PASS|WARN|BLOCK based on PRD thresholds and decision matrix
    - [ ] Add detailed logging for each rule execution (timing, inputs, outputs, sample_seed)
    - [ ] Unit test each rule with positive/negative/edge-case fixtures
- [ ] TASK-F-053-03: Add API endpoints and job endpoints to evaluate DQ gates synchronously and asynchronously
    - [ ] Define REST API: POST /api/v1/dq/evaluate (synchronous) and POST /api/v1/dq/evaluate/async (returns job id)
    - [ ] Implement request validation middleware checking governance_manifest presence and minimal fields (manifest_version, workspace_id, generated_at)
    - [ ] Implement synchronous path calling rule evaluator and returning dq_gate_result with proper HTTP codes (200, 202, 400, 409 depending on outcome)
    - [ ] Implement async job enqueue path using existing job queue (BullMQ/Redis) with job status endpoints GET /api/v1/dq/jobs/{job_id}
    - [ ] Write integration tests that mock governance_manifest inputs and assert API responses and status codes
- [ ] TASK-F-053-04: Emit SSE events and integrate with pipeline orchestrator event stream
    - [ ] Implement SSE publisher that emits type: 'dq_gate' events with the dq_gate_result payload and trace_id
    - [ ] Ensure SSE conforms with system streaming protocol event types defined in PRD (type: dq_gate)
    - [ ] Add retry/backoff on SSE delivery failures and log delivery status
    - [ ] Add unit/integration tests asserting SSE payload correctness and event header fields
    - [ ] Update orchestrator consumer integration to subscribe to dq_gate events and act on PASS/WARN/BLOCK
- [ ] TASK-F-053-05: Persist DQ gate evaluations to audit storage and expose query endpoints
    - [ ] Design dq_gate_results table schema in Postgres (id, workspace_id, session_id, manifest_version, trace_id, rule_results JSONB, overall_outcome, evaluated_at, created_by)
    - [ ] Implement DB persistence layer and DAO methods for write/read with pagination
    - [ ] Add endpoint GET /api/v1/dq/results?workspace_id=&manifest_version=&outcome=&limit=&cursor=
    - [ ] Add retention policy and archival plan (default 365 days) configuration
    - [ ] Add integration tests to assert persisted records and query responses
- [ ] TASK-F-053-06: Integrate observability: metrics, tracing, and structured logging
    - [ ] Add OpenTelemetry trace propagation support: ensure trace_id is accepted and propagated to rule executions and DB writes
    - [ ] Emit Prometheus metrics: dq_gate_evaluations_total{outcome}, dq_gate_rule_duration_seconds_bucket{rule_id}
    - [ ] Add structured logs for each evaluation including trace_id, rule outcomes, durations, and sample_seed
    - [ ] Instrument health check endpoint and add readiness/liveness probes
    - [ ] Add dashboards / Grafana panels for rule failure rates and latency
- [ ] TASK-F-053-07: Add security and validation: input sanitization, authz checks and secrets handling
    - [ ] Require authenticated calls; verify caller has workspace-member or workspace-admin role via ABAC
    - [ ] Validate manifest ownership: ensure governance_manifest.workspace_id matches caller's workspace_id
    - [ ] Sanitize and reject any governance_manifest that includes database credentials or sensitive fields
    - [ ] Ensure PII detections are not logged with full column samples; mask sensitive values in logs
    - [ ] Add security unit tests for authz failure, malformed manifest, and attempt to evaluate foreign workspace manifest

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for each DQ rule with fixture governance_manifests (pass/warn/block/edge)
- [ ] API contract tests for POST /api/v1/dq/evaluate validating status codes and payload shapes
- [ ] Integration test: pipeline simulator submits governance_manifest and asserts SSE dq_gate event consumed by orchestrator and pipeline halted on BLOCK
- [ ] DB persistence test ensuring dq_gate_results contains written records with correct hashing and trace_id
- [ ] Security tests: unauthorized user denied, workspace mismatch rejected, PII masked in logs
- [ ] Performance test: P95 evaluation latency under 60s for governance_manifest up to 500 tables; add async fallback test for large manifests
- [ ] CI gating: all tests run in CI, lint and schema validations enforced

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] dq_gate_result JSON Schema approved and committed to repo
- [ ] Core DQ rules implemented with unit tests and coverage >= 80% for module
- [ ] Synchronous and async evaluation API endpoints implemented with request validation and status codes
- [ ] SSE dq_gate events emitted and consumed by orchestrator in a demo pipeline
- [ ] Evaluation results persisted to dq_gate_results table and queryable via API
- [ ] OpenTelemetry tracing and Prometheus metrics integrated; dashboards created
- [ ] AuthN/AuthZ enforced for evaluation endpoints; security review completed
- [ ] All acceptance criteria met and automated tests passing in CI

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] F-006
- [ ] F-009

###### Agent Notes
- DQ engine must accept the governance_manifest schema produced by Stage-0 (F-006) and integrate with downstream orchestrator (F-009). All rule logic should be deterministic and unit-testable; sampling must be seeded and logged. Map PRD thresholds exactly; make the rule registry pluggable for future gates. Persist results for audit and enable query by workspace_id and manifest_version. SSE emission must follow the platform streaming protocol type: 'dq_gate' and include trace_id and compact details only (no full data samples). Provide both sync and async APIs to accommodate large manifests and slow warehouse profiling. Ensure ABAC checks to prevent cross-workspace evaluation. Use existing job queue and tracing infra where possible.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-053-02: As an SRE/Observability owner, I want the DQ Gate engine to emit structured metrics, traces, logs and audit events so that we can monitor DQ gate health, latency, and failure modes and alert on systemic issues.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every DQ evaluation emits an OpenTelemetry trace with trace_id included in dq_gate_result and persisted audit record
- [ ] Prometheus metrics exposed: dq_gate_evaluations_total{workspace_id,outcome}, dq_gate_rule_duration_seconds_bucket{rule_id}, dq_gate_evaluation_errors_total
- [ ] Structured logs written with JSON fields: timestamp, workspace_id, trace_id, manifest_version, rule_id, rule_outcome, duration_ms, severity
- [ ] Grafana dashboards created showing evaluation rate, failure rate by rule, 95th latency by rule, and top blocking rules
- [ ] Alerts configured: high rate of BLOCK outcomes (>3% over 1h) and evaluation error rate (>1% over 15m) trigger pager/Slack
- [ ] Audit events persisted for every evaluation with immutability guarantee and retention policy configured

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-053-08: Instrument OpenTelemetry tracing for rule evaluation and API handlers
    - [ ] Add trace spans for API handler, rule registry dispatcher, individual rule execution, persistence write, and SSE emission
    - [ ] Ensure incoming trace_id propagation from orchestrator and include span links to parent traces
    - [ ] Write unit tests asserting trace spans are created and annotated with expected attributes
    - [ ] Document trace attributes contract for downstream observability consumers
- [ ] TASK-F-053-09: Export Prometheus metrics and integrate with existing metrics namespace
    - [ ] Implement metric collectors for dq_gate_evaluations_total, dq_gate_rule_duration_seconds histograms, dq_gate_eval_errors_total
    - [ ] Register metrics with central metrics registry and add necessary labels (workspace_id, rule_id, outcome)
    - [ ] Add unit tests validating metrics increment on evaluation flows
    - [ ] Create short runbook on metric semantics and cardinality considerations
- [ ] TASK-F-053-10: Add structured logging and ensure sensitive data masking
    - [ ] Standardize log schema and integrate with existing structured logger (timestamp, level, trace_id, workspace_id, manifest_version, rule_outcome, duration_ms, message)
    - [ ] Mask or omit raw column values or any PII from log payloads; only log flags and statistics
    - [ ] Add log-level configuration and ensure WARN/BLOCK produce INFO+ logs and errors produce ERROR logs
    - [ ] Add unit tests that verify masking and log format
- [ ] TASK-F-053-11: Persist immutable audit events and expose read API for compliance
    - [ ] Implement write-once append to audit store for each dq_gate evaluation (append-only Postgres table with integrity constraints)
    - [ ] Add API GET /api/v1/dq/audit?workspace_id=&since=&until=&outcome=
    - [ ] Implement retention/archival job to export older records to object storage and mark archived
    - [ ] Add tests validating immutability and query correctness
- [ ] TASK-F-053-12: Create dashboards and alerts
    - [ ] Build Grafana dashboards with panels: evaluations per minute, top blocking rules, P95 latency per rule, error rate
    - [ ] Define alert rules: BLOCK rate > 3% over 1h, eval error rate >1% over 15m, evaluation latency P95 > 120s
    - [ ] Add alert playbooks describing remediation steps and responsible teams
    - [ ] Validate alerts in staging by triggering synthetic BLOCK/WARN/PASS events

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Integration test verifying that a synchronous evaluation creates trace spans and a persisted audit record with matching trace_id
- [ ] Metrics test asserting that metrics counters/histograms update after simulated evaluations
- [ ] Logging test verifying logs conform to schema and that PII is masked (fuzzed inputs)
- [ ] Alert simulation tests: generate synthetic failing rates and verify alerts fire in the staging alerting pipeline
- [ ] Load test to validate metrics and tracing scale under 100 concurrent evaluation requests

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] OpenTelemetry traces emitted for all evaluation flows and visible in tracing backend
- [ ] Prometheus metrics instrumented and scraped by metrics pipeline
- [ ] Structured logging enabled with PII masking and retention policy
- [ ] Audit store written and API for querying audit events implemented
- [ ] Grafana dashboards created and alerts configured with playbooks
- [ ] Observability unit/integration tests added and passing in CI

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] TASK-F-053-02
- [ ] TASK-F-053-05

###### Agent Notes
- Observability must reuse platform-wide OpenTelemetry and Prometheus conventions. Keep metric cardinality controlled (avoid high-cardinality labels like full manifest_version in frequent metrics; use hashing or sampling for long IDs). Audit records must be append-only with integrity checks and accessible for compliance. Ensure dashboards and alert playbooks are delivered with the release to enable on-call teams to act quickly.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-053-03: As a QA lead and security engineer, I want the DQ Gate engine to have comprehensive validation, error handling, security checks, and automated test coverage so that the engine is robust in production and prevents accidental data leaks or cross-workspace access.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Invalid or malformed governance_manifest inputs return HTTP 400 with schema validation errors and are not executed
- [ ] Requests from principals without workspace membership or insufficient role are rejected with HTTP 403 and logged
- [ ] Engine masks PII in logs and never persists raw row samples; PII flags remain in the manifest and are stored as metadata only
- [ ] Engine handles partial failures: a failing rule execution sets rule outcome to ERROR with details and overall outcome to WARN if other rules pass; orchestration receives a clear error payload
- [ ] Retry/backoff implemented for transient errors (warehouse connection or queue) with configurable retry policy and circuit breaker
- [ ] Unit tests, integration tests and end-to-end tests cover success, warning, blocking, malformed manifest, auth failure, and retry scenarios

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-053-13: Add input validation, error mapping and standardized error responses
    - [ ] Integrate JSON Schema validation for incoming governance_manifest and return structured 400 errors listing missing/invalid fields
    - [ ] Implement centralized error mapper translating internal exceptions to standard API error codes and messages
    - [ ] Add error codes catalog for DQ engine errors (DQ-4001 malformed_manifest, DQ-4010 auth_failed, DQ-5001 rule_exec_error, etc.)
    - [ ] Add unit tests for validation and error mapping
- [ ] TASK-F-053-14: Implement authZ checks and workspace boundary enforcement
    - [ ] Verify caller's identity and workspace membership using ABAC; reject if caller not member of manifest.workspace_id
    - [ ] Ensure role checks: only workspace_member or workspace_admin can initiate evaluation; admin-only ops documented
    - [ ] Add tests for cross-workspace attempt rejection
    - [ ] Add logging for auth failures without exposing sensitive details
- [ ] TASK-F-053-15: Implement retry/backoff and circuit breaker for transient failures
    - [ ] Implement retry wrapper for rule executions that can fail due to transient infra (DB or warehouse), with exponential backoff and max attempts
    - [ ] Add circuit breaker for repeated persistent failures per-rule to avoid cascading slowness
    - [ ] Add metrics for retries and circuit breaker events
    - [ ] Test retry logic via integration tests simulating transient failures
- [ ] TASK-F-053-16: Add QA automation: unit, integration, E2E, and security tests
    - [ ] Write unit tests for validation, each rule, mapper, and persistence layer
    - [ ] Write integration tests that run the DQ engine end-to-end using sample governance_manifests and a test DB (duckDB) to validate distribution_shift and pk uniqueness checks
    - [ ] Create E2E pipeline test that demonstrates pipeline halt on BLOCK and continued run on PASS/WARN
    - [ ] Add security tests checking PII masking, authZ enforcement, and log redaction
- [ ] TASK-F-053-17: Prepare runbooks, playbooks and documentation for operations and incident response
    - [ ] Document common failure modes and remediation steps for each DQ rule (e.g., PK violation resolution, freshness SLA remediation)
    - [ ] Create incident playbook for persistent BLOCK spikes including rollback/disable gate guidance
    - [ ] Add developer docs describing how to add new rules to the registry and unit test patterns
    - [ ] Train on-call SRE/Dev team with a brief run-through and sample incidents

###### Test Plan
- [ ] Schema validation tests with manifest permutations (missing fields, malformed types, extra fields) asserting HTTP 400 responses
- [ ] Auth tests: valid workspace member allowed, non-member denied (403), role-limited operations blocked
- [ ] Retry tests simulating transient DB/warehouse failures and verifying retries and success/failure propagation
- [ ] End-to-end pipeline test verifying BLOCK halts pipeline and WARN does not halt, with orchestrator logs confirming decisions
- [ ] Security tests checking that logs do not contain raw sample values or sensitive strings; run static analysis for secrets
- [ ] Regression tests to ensure no change to thresholds without explicit config changes

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] Input validation returns structured 400 errors and blocks execution for malformed manifests
- [ ] AuthZ enforced and cross-workspace misuse rejected with tests
- [ ] Retry/backoff and circuit breaker implemented and tested
- [ ] Comprehensive test coverage: unit, integration, E2E, and security tests passing in CI
- [ ] Runbooks and playbooks published in wiki and training completed with sign-off
- [ ] No PII in logs or persisted samples; security review completed

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] TASK-F-053-01
- [ ] TASK-F-053-02
- [ ] TASK-F-053-05

###### Agent Notes
- Security and QA must be baked in — do not allow ad-hoc logging of raw samples. Use duckDB for deterministic integration tests and Snowflake mocks for connector-specific behaviors. Ensure error codes are stable for clients (orchestrator) to rely on. Retry and circuit-breaker defaults should be configurable via feature flags; keep conservative defaults for MVP. Coordinate with F-006 team for governance_manifest schema changes and F-009 orchestrator team for event contract verification.

### Epic: Governance

#### Requirement / Feature: Governance manifest API (GET manifest per integration)

**Feature ID:** `F-054`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, api`

**Rationale:** Frontends and agents need programmatic access to the manifest for stages and auditing.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-007
- [ ] F-006

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-054-01: As a backend API consumer (frontend or automation), I want a secure, versioned REST endpoint to fetch the governance manifest for a given integration_id, so that frontends and agents can programmatically read catalog, profiling, PII flags, lineage, and DQ gate status for stages and auditing.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] GET /api/v1/integrations/{integration_id}/manifest exists and is documented in OpenAPI.
- [ ] - [ ] Response JSON conforms to the governance manifest schema defined in the PRD (manifest_version, workspace_id, integration_id, generated_at, tables, lineage_edges, dq_gate_status, human_review_required).
- [ ] - [ ] Successful response returns 200 and the most recent manifest version for the integration_id when the caller is authorized and integration exists.
- [ ] - [ ] Unauthorized calls return 401; authenticated but unauthorized workspace members return 403; missing integration_id returns 404.
- [ ] - [ ] Response excludes secrets/credentials and any sensitive database connection details (never leak DB credentials).
- [ ] - [ ] Endpoint enforces workspace-level authorization: caller must be a member of the workspace containing the integration or have a valid server-to-server API key with appropriate scope.
- [ ] - [ ] Endpoint logs structured access events (actor_id, workspace_id, integration_id, manifest_version, trace_id) and emits OpenTelemetry traces.
- [ ] - [ ] Manifest responses are cacheable with ETag/If-None-Match and return 304 when unchanged; cache invalidation occurs on new manifest generation.
- [ ] - [ ] Performance targets: P50 < 30s, P95 < 90s for manifest generation retrieval (if generation needed); typical cached retrieval P50 < 500ms.
- [ ] - [ ] Full unit, integration, contract, and security tests exist and pass in CI.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-054-01: Design API contract and update OpenAPI spec for GET manifest endpoint
    - [ ] - [ ] Draft OpenAPI path /api/v1/integrations/{integration_id}/manifest with path parameters and responses
    - [ ] - [ ] Define request auth requirements (OAuth bearer, API key) and required scopes
    - [ ] - [ ] Paste the PRD governance_manifest JSON schema as the response schema with field descriptions and examples
    - [ ] - [ ] Add 200, 304, 401, 403, 404, 429, 500 responses with example payloads
    - [ ] - [ ] Review and sign off with product, security, and frontend stakeholders
- [ ] TASK-F-054-02: Implement backend controller and route handler for manifest retrieval
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement route handler that accepts integration_id and authenticates caller
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate integration exists and load integration metadata (workspace_id, stored manifest pointer)
    - [ ] - [ ] Authorize caller by checking workspace membership or API key scope
    - [ ] - [ ] Fetch manifest from storage (Postgres blob store or S3) and return JSON with proper headers
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement ETag generation (hash of manifest payload) and 304 handling for If-None-Match
    - [ ] - [ ] Add structured logging of access events including trace_id
- [ ] TASK-F-054-03: Implement storage layer and retrieval logic for governance manifests
    - [ ] - [ ] Add DB table or object store mapping for integration_id -> latest_manifest_version -> storage_uri
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement service to read manifest JSON from storage (S3 or Postgres JSONB) and validate against JSON Schema
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement atomic update/invalidation hook used by manifest generation to update pointer to latest version
    - [ ] - [ ] Add index on integration_id + manifest_version for fast lookups
- [ ] TASK-F-054-04: Add caching and rate limiting for manifest reads
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement Redis cache with key manifest:integration:{integration_id}:latest and TTL 300s
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure cache invalidation occurs when a new manifest version is written (pub/sub invalidation or DB trigger)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add API-level rate limiting (per-workspace or per-api-key) with 429 responses and Retry-After header
    - [ ] - [ ] Add cache hit/miss metrics and Redis error handling fallback to storage reads
- [ ] TASK-F-054-05: Add validation, error handling and monitoring
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate returned manifest against JSON Schema and return 500 with error_id if validation fails
    - [ ] - [ ] Map and return clear error payloads for 400/401/403/404/429/500 with remediation hints
    - [ ] - [ ] Instrument OpenTelemetry traces and metrics (request_count, latency, errors, cache_hit_rate)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add alert (PagerDuty) for sustained 5xx rate > 1% over 5 minutes
- [ ] TASK-F-054-06: Write automated tests (unit, integration, contract) and add CI steps
    - [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for controller auth, error mapping, ETag logic and header behavior
    - [ ] - [ ] Integration tests using test DB and S3/localstack to assert manifest retrieval end-to-end
    - [ ] - [ ] Contract tests that response JSON validates against PRD JSON Schema
    - [ ] - [ ] Add test fixture for a manifest with PII flags and verify no credentials returned
    - [ ] - [ ] Add test to assert 304 behavior when If-None-Match provided
- [ ] TASK-F-054-07: Documentation, SDK examples and frontend integration story
    - [ ] - [ ] Document endpoint in API docs with example curl and bearer token usage
    - [ ] - [ ] Add example fetch code snippet for frontend and for agent automation (server-to-server)
    - [ ] - [ ] Create a short migration note for frontend teams about caching/ETag handling
    - [ ] - [ ] Coordinate with frontend to run a smoke test against the staging endpoint
- [ ] TASK-F-054-08: Security review and SRE/DevOps rollout
    - [ ] - [ ] Conduct threat review to ensure no secrets exposure in manifest and logs
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure PII detection flags are present and manifest consumers are reminded to honor PII
    - [ ] - [ ] Add deployment manifest for Kubernetes and required RBAC changes
    - [ ] - [ ] Run a canary rollout to 10% of traffic and monitor metrics before full rollout

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit test: controller auth, ETag, header handling, error mapping
- [ ] - [ ] Integration test: full read path from storage to API response (localstack + test DB)
- [ ] - [ ] Contract test: validate response payloads against JSON Schema (positive/negative cases)
- [ ] - [ ] Security test: static analysis of logs to ensure no DB credentials in logs or payload
- [ ] - [ ] Authz tests: workspace member allowed, non-member denied, API key scopes enforced
- [ ] - [ ] Performance test: cache hit path P50 < 500ms; cold generation path P50 < 30s
- [ ] - [ ] Load test: 100 RPS per workspace-level limit with expected error rates < 0.5%
- [ ] - [ ] Regression test: manifest write invalidation triggers cache purge and subsequent GET returns new version

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Endpoint implemented and merged to main branch
- [ ] - [ ] OpenAPI spec updated and published
- [ ] - [ ] All unit/integration/contract tests pass in CI
- [ ] - [ ] Automated security checks and a manual security review completed
- [ ] - [ ] E2E smoke test by frontend and agent teams passed in staging
- [ ] - [ ] Observability (traces, metrics, logs) and alerts configured
- [ ] - [ ] Documentation and SDK snippets published
- [ ] - [ ] Feature gated or canaryed via deployment pipeline and rolled to prod

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] F-007 (Integrations registration & metadata store)
- [ ] - [ ] F-006 (Authentication & Authorization infrastructure)
- [ ] - ] Database / object storage access (Postgres JSONB or S3) available

###### Agent Notes
- Implement exactly the documented PRD governance_manifest schema. Use the canonical endpoint path /api/v1/integrations/{integration_id}/manifest. Always enforce workspace authorization: derive workspace_id from integration metadata and verify caller membership or API key scope. Never include DB credentials or secrets in the response or logs. Use JSON Schema to validate stored manifests on read and fail-safe (return 500 + unique error_id) if schema validation fails. Implement ETag using SHA256(manifest_json) for efficient client caching and support If-None-Match -> 304. Cache manifests in Redis for 5 minutes; ensure cache invalidation on new manifest writes via pub/sub topic 'manifest.updated.{integration_id}'. Emit structured logs and OpenTelemetry spans: include trace_id, actor_id, integration_id, manifest_version. Instrument metrics: manifest_read_count, manifest_read_latency_ms, manifest_cache_hit_rate, manifest_5xx_rate. Follow existing service conventions for auth middleware, error envelopes, and feature flags. Coordinate with F-007 writer to ensure writer updates the latest pointer atomically.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-054-02: As a frontend developer, I want to request and render the governance manifest for an integration_id (with caching and ETag), so that the Governance UI and session planner can display table health, PII flags, and human_review_required badges reliably and efficiently.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Frontend can call GET /api/v1/integrations/{integration_id}/manifest with valid bearer token and receive 200 or 304 correctly.
- [ ] - [ ] Frontend uses ETag/If-None-Match to avoid redundant payloads and handles 304 gracefully.
- [ ] - [ ] UI displays table list, health_score, definition_confidence badges, PII flags, and human_review_required prominently per manifest content.
- [ ] - [ ] UI shows friendly error states for 401/403/404 and retry controls for 5xx.
- [ ] - [ ] Frontend does not render or attempt to log any sensitive fields; any PII flagged columns are visually flagged and access-controlled in the UI.
- [ ] - [ ] Integration verifies latency and UX: first render with cached manifest < 1s, cold fetch under 5s in staging.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-054-09: Add frontend API client method and ETag support
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement client method getGovernanceManifest(integrationId) that sends Authorization header and preserves ETag between calls
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement If-None-Match header handling and 304 path to return cached data
    - [ ] - [ ] Add retry/backoff for transient 5xx with maximum 3 retries
    - [ ] - [ ] Write unit tests for client method and mock 200/304/401/403/404/429/500 responses
- [ ] TASK-F-054-10: Render governance manifest in Governance UI and session planner
    - [ ] - [ ] Build table list UI components to display table name, health_score, row_count, freshness_lag_hrs
    - [ ] - [ ] Add PII badge UI that highlights columns with pii_flags and shows pattern_type tooltip
    - [ ] - [ ] Add definition_confidence badge with review action if 'low' or human_review_required is true
    - [ ] - [ ] Add skeleton and error states with retry button for panel-level failures
- [ ] TASK-F-054-11: Accessibility, telemetry, and UX polish
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure components have proper aria-labels and keyboard navigation
    - [ ] - [ ] Send telemetry event on manifest load success/failure including manifest_version and latency
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit and E2E tests (Cypress) that assert UI rendering and error behavior
    - [ ] - [ ] Coordinate with product on copy for PII and review badges
- [ ] TASK-F-054-12: Frontend security and data handling
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure the frontend strips any unexpected sensitive fields before logging
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement role-based UI element rendering: only show review controls to workspace admins
    - [ ] - [ ] Add a guard that prevents the UI from attempting to load manifest if workspace_id mismatch is detected

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for client ETag and 304 handling
- [ ] - [ ] Cypress E2E tests for manifest rendering, PII badges, and review button availability by role
- [ ] - [ ] Performance test: first render under staging latency target
- [ ] - [ ] Security test: verify no credentials printed to console or analytics
- [ ] - [ ] Accessibility audit on the governance panels

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Frontend client method merged and published in shared client library
- [ ] - [ ] Governance UI renders live manifests in staging and production smoke tests
- [ ] - [ ] E2E tests pass in CI for render and error states
- [ ] - [ ] Accessibility and telemetry checks passed
- [ ] - [ ] Frontend integration smoke test with backend endpoint passes

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] Backend GET endpoint implemented (US-F-054-01 completion)
- [ ] - [ ] Auth tokens & workspace membership checks available (F-006)

###### Agent Notes
- Frontend should use the shared API client and preserve ETag per integration_id in local in-memory cache or session storage for a single-page session (do not persist long-term). Respect 304 responses and update UI only when manifest_version changes. Ensure the UI displays PII flags but never shows raw sensitive values. Add debug toggle to show raw manifest only in local dev with a visible red warning. Frontend telemetry should not include any PII fields; only include metadata (manifest_version, integration_id, table_count, health_score summary). Coordinate with backend on header names for ETag (use standard ETag) and trace-id propagation so traces correlate across frontend/backend.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-054-03: As an automation/agent (orchestrator), I want a reliable programmatic endpoint to fetch the governance manifest for pipeline decisions (e.g., semantic layer generation, DQ gating), so that agents can validate source table health, PII flags, and lineage before advancing pipeline stages.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] Agents can call the endpoint using service API keys and receive JSON manifest that passes JSON Schema validation.
- [ ] - [ ] The endpoint authenticates and authorizes service API keys and enforces scope restrictions (read:manifest for that integration/workspace).
- [ ] - [ ] Manifest includes human_review_required boolean and dq_gate_status to allow deterministic gating by agents.
- [ ] - [ ] Endpoint supports conditional requests (ETag/If-None-Match) to reduce bandwidth in high-frequency agent polling scenarios.
- [ ] - [ ] Agent-facing failures are deterministic with clear remediation (e.g., 403 -> escalate to owner; dq_gate_status=BLOCK -> halt pipeline).
- [ ] - [ ] Audit log entries include agent identity and access timestamp for pipeline auditability.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-054-13: Add API key/service account auth pathway and scope checks for agent consumers
    - [ ] - [ ] Extend auth middleware to accept API keys with scopes and map service accounts to workspace principals
    - [ ] - [ ] Create 'read:manifest' scope and enforce that only keys with this scope can call the manifest endpoint for non-interactive agents
    - [ ] - [ ] Add tests to validate scope enforcement and key rotation behaviors
    - [ ] - [ ] Document the API-key usage pattern for agent implementers
- [ ] TASK-F-054-14: Enhance error payloads for deterministic agent handling
    - [ ] - [ ] Define structured error envelope with fields { code, message, remediation, detail, error_id }
    - [ ] - [ ] Map DQ gate statuses in response or separate endpoint to allow agents to programmatically decide next steps
    - [ ] - [ ] Add explicit 409 or 424 status options for manifest states that block progress with remediation text
    - [ ] - [ ] Add tests verifying agent behavior upon each error case
- [ ] TASK-F-054-15: Add audit logging for agent accesses and manifest-driven gating events
    - [ ] - [ ] Add audit table writes for every manifest read by an agent: { actor_id, integration_id, manifest_version, action, timestamp }
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure logs are append-only and exported to the audit log storage per PRD
    - [ ] - [ ] Add queries/dashboards for recent manifest reads and gate-triggered pipeline halts
- [ ] TASK-F-054-16: Agent integration tests and pipeline gating simulation
    - [ ] - [ ] Create test harness agent that exercises manifest fetch, handles dq_gate_status=BLOCK/WARN/PASS, and logs remediation paths
    - [ ] - [ ] Run pipeline simulation in staging where manifest indicates PII -> ensure pipeline halts before ML stages
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate that agents honor ETag and backoff to avoid thundering herd

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for API key auth and scope restrictions
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests where an agent uses API key to fetch manifest and responds to dq_gate_status values
- [ ] - [ ] End-to-end staging test: run Stage 0 governance manifest generation -> agent fetch -> pipeline halts on DQ BLOCK
- [ ] - [ ] Audit log verification ensuring entries are recorded and immutable

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Service-account/API key auth implemented and documented
- [ ] - [ ] Agents can retrieve manifests and receive deterministic error payloads
- [ ] - [ ] Audit logs record agent manifest reads for compliance
- [ ] - [ ] Agent integration tests pass and pipeline simulations verified in staging

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] Auth backend for API keys/service accounts (F-006 extension)
- [ ] - [ ] Manifest writer that updates latest pointer (F-007)

###### Agent Notes
- Agents must call with a service API key that includes 'read:manifest' scope. Implement idempotent read logic: if 304 returned, reuse cached manifest; if dq_gate_status==BLOCK, agent should halt and surface the human_review_required flag to the orchestration UI. Error envelopes should include 'remediation' string with suggested next action (e.g., 'Run governance review UI or re-run profiling'). Ensure agent calls set x-trace-id header and the backend returns it in response headers for trace correlation. Avoid blind polling: recommend using a pub/sub notification from manifest writer to trigger agent re-fetch on new versions rather than frequent polling.

### Epic: Semantic Layer

#### Requirement / Feature: Semantic schema API (GET cube_schema per workspace)

**Feature ID:** `F-055`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, api`

**Rationale:** Session planner and UI require a stable semantic schema endpoint.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-011
- [ ] F-012

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-055-01: As a frontend developer / session-planner agent, I want a stable GET /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema endpoint that returns the workspace's cube_schema JSON, so that the UI and session planner can render explores, metrics, and dimensions deterministically.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] The endpoint GET /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema returns HTTP 200 with a Cube.dev-compatible JSON payload (v0.35+ shape) for an existing workspace.
- [ ] - [ ] The response includes semantic_layer_version and governance_manifest_version fields and uses semver for semantic_layer_version.
- [ ] - [ ] The endpoint enforces workspace-scoped authorization: only workspace members (per ABAC) or valid embed tokens may access; unauthorized requests return 401/403 appropriately.
- [ ] - [ ] The endpoint validates workspace_id path param and returns 400 for malformed IDs and 404 for non-existent workspaces.
- [ ] - [ ] Response includes caching headers (ETag + Cache-Control) and supports conditional GET (If-None-Match -> 304).
- [ ] - [ ] Response size and schema conform to contract; if schema generation is in-progress return 202 with status payload including trace_id and estimated_ready_at.
- [ ] - [ ] The endpoint logs a trace_id propagated to downstream services and records structured audit events: request_id, workspace_id, actor_id, semantic_layer_version, response_status.
- [ ] - [ ] API respects rate limits and returns 429 with Retry-After when exceeded.
- [ ] - [ ] Endpoint responds within P50 target < 500ms under normal cache-hit conditions (measured in CI and monitoring).
- [ ] - [ ] Error responses include machine-friendly error codes and human-friendly messages; no sensitive data or credentials are leaked.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-055-01: Design API contract and JSON Schema for GET /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema
    - [ ] - [ ] Draft OpenAPI spec update including path, query params, response 200/202/400/401/403/404/429/500 shapes
    - [ ] - [ ] Author JSON Schema for cube_schema response (validate Cube.dev v0.35+ compatibility fields) and publish to repo /specs/semantic-schema.json
    - [ ] - [ ] Define error-code map and machine-readable error payload format { code, message, details }
    - [ ] - [ ] Get API contract sign-off from product, frontend, and AI agent owners
- [ ] TASK-F-055-02: Implement backend handler for GET semantic schema (service layer + controller)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement controller endpoint with path param workspace_id and request authentication middleware
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement service method fetchSemanticSchema(workspace_id, options) that reads from semantic store and returns schema + metadata
    - [ ] - [ ] Add validation of workspace existence and authorization checks via ABAC service
    - [ ] - [ ] Wire ETag generation from semantic_layer_version and support If-None-Match to return 304
- [ ] TASK-F-055-03: Implement semantic schema storage & retrieval integration
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement retrieval from canonical source: workspace-scoped Git repository at /semantic/{workspace_id}/schema/ OR semantic DB table fallback
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement fallback behavior: if git schema missing but governance_manifest present, return 202 with status and kick off async generator
    - [ ] - [ ] Add read caching in Redis with configurable TTL and version-keyed cache entries (key: semantic_schema:{workspace_id}:{version})
    - [ ] - [ ] Add validation step that loads the JSON Schema and performs schema validation before returning payload
- [ ] TASK-F-055-04: Add security, authorization, and PII gating
    - [ ] - [ ] Enforce ABAC: query workspace membership and roles; reject unauthorized access with 403
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure that any PII flags from governance_manifest are surfaced as metadata fields but do not include raw PII values in responses
    - [ ] - [ ] Log access audit event to audit log store including actor_id and fetched semantic_layer_version
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate that embed tokens used are single-purpose and allowed_origins (if applicable) before returning schema
- [ ] TASK-F-055-05: Add observability, tracing, metrics and logging
    - [ ] - [ ] Instrument endpoint with OpenTelemetry tracing and include trace_id in responses and logs
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit metrics: request_count, success_count, error_count, latency_histogram, cache_hit_ratio, generation_in_progress_count
    - [ ] - [ ] Add structured logging for request lifecycle including sanitized parameters and response code
    - [ ] - [ ] Create Grafana dashboard panels for endpoint latency, errors, and cache hit rate
- [ ] TASK-F-055-06: Write automated tests and CI checks
    - [ ] - [ ] Unit tests for controller/service: success, malformed workspace_id, unauthorized, not found, cache behavior
    - [ ] - [ ] Integration tests that spin up a test Git repo and verify retrieval of schema and ETag behavior
    - [ ] - [ ] Contract test that validates response against published JSON Schema (fail CI on mismatch)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add API smoke test to CI pipeline to hit endpoint with test workspace and assert 200

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit test: valid workspace returns 200 and payload conforms to JSON Schema
- [ ] - [ ] Unit test: malformed workspace_id returns 400
- [ ] - [ ] Unit test: non-member user returns 403
- [ ] - [ ] Integration test: missing schema but governance manifest present returns 202 and triggers generation job
- [ ] - [ ] Integration test: ETag/If-None-Match returns 304 when version unchanged
- [ ] - [ ] Contract test: response validated against /specs/semantic-schema.json in CI
- [ ] - [ ] Performance test: simulate 100 RPS for 1m with cache warm and assert P95 latency < 1s
- [ ] - [ ] Security test: ensure no raw credentials or PII are included in payload or logs
- [ ] - [ ] Rate-limit test: exceed allowed requests and assert 429 with Retry-After header

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] OpenAPI spec updated and merged
- [ ] - [ ] Endpoint implemented, deployed to staging, and returns valid cube_schema JSON
- [ ] - [ ] JSON Schema contract tests pass in CI every PR
- [ ] - [ ] Authorization and PII gating enforced and audit logs recorded
- [ ] - [ ] Caching headers and conditional GET support working (ETag/304)
- [ ] - [ ] Monitoring dashboards created and basic alerts configured
- [ ] - [ ] Performance validated (P50 < 500ms cache-hit) in staging load test

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] F-011 (Governance manifest generation available)
- [ ] - [ ] F-012 (Semantic layer construction pipeline producing cube_schema files)
- [ ] - [ ] Auth/ABAC service (existing workspace membership APIs)

###### Agent Notes
- - [ ] The semantic schema is the canonical vocabulary for UI and agents; implement strict JSON Schema validation server-side. - [ ] Primary source is workspace Git repo at /semantic/{workspace_id}/schema/; if absent, fall back to semantic DB table and include generation status. - [ ] Propagate trace_id through the callstack and include it in the 202/200 responses for troubleshooting. - [ ] Avoid returning raw sample data; include only definitions, stats, and confidence scores from the governance manifest. - [ ] Coordinate with F-011/F-012 teams to ensure semantic_layer_version is incremented semantically (semver) and stored with the schema artifact.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-055-02: As an AI agent (session planner / semantic layer agent), I want the endpoint to serve validated cube_schema JSON and to expose confidence metadata, so that agents can deterministically build session_specs and avoid using low-confidence definitions in ML flows.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] The returned schema includes per-definition confidence: high|medium|low for metrics and dimensions.
- [ ] - [ ] The endpoint returns valid cube_schema JSON that passes server-side JSON Schema validation on every request before serving (defensive validation).
- [ ] - [ ] Low-confidence items are included but flagged; if agents attempt to use low-confidence items for ML feature sets the API returns a field human_review_required: true in the session response context (or session planner is required to call the review flow).
- [ ] - [ ] If the semantic_layer has conflicts (e.g., metric name collisions), the endpoint returns a status block listing conflicts and a generated conflict_id; agents receive 409 with conflict details for programmatic handling.
- [ ] - [ ] Agents receive no raw sample rows or PII; only statistical metadata from governance_manifest is present.
- [ ] - [ ] All responses include semantic_layer_version and governance_manifest_version for reproducibility and audit.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-055-07: Enforce JSON Schema validation in retrieval path and fail-safe behavior
    - [ ] - [ ] Integrate JSON Schema validator into service layer to validate cube_schema prior to returning
    - [ ] - [ ] If validation fails, return 500 with validation errors and create an internal ticket/audit entry; in staging also surface to product team
    - [ ] - [ ] Provide an admin-only endpoint /api/v1/semantic/{workspace_id}/schema/validate to re-run validation and show error details
    - [ ] - [ ] Add CI job to run full semantic schema validation against all workspaces' schemas in test environment nightly
- [ ] TASK-F-055-08: Expose confidence metadata and conflict reporting in API response
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure response has fields for definition_confidence per metric/dimension as produced by semantic pipeline
    - [ ] - [ ] Add top-level status.conflicts array listing metric/dimension name, description, diff link, and conflict_id
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement logic to return 409 when a client requests to use a schema that has unresolved conflicts (include conflict_id and remediation link)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests that simulate conflict scenarios and assert 409 payload structure
- [ ] TASK-F-055-09: Add agent-consumption optimizations and metadata
    - [ ] - [ ] Include a compact index in response: explores_index with metric_count and top_dimensions for fast agent lookups
    - [ ] - [ ] Include semantic_layer_version and governance_manifest_version in response headers and body for agents to log
    - [ ] - [ ] Provide optional query param ?compact=true to return minimal payload (index only) for agent warm-up calls
    - [ ] - [ ] Document the compact mode in API docs for agent implementers
- [ ] TASK-F-055-10: Add defensive headers and response shaping for downstream AI agents
    - [ ] - [ ] Add header X-Semantic-Version and X-Governance-Version to all responses
    - [ ] - [ ] Add header X-Trace-Id echoing request trace for debugging
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure response JSON never exceeds configured max payload size; return 413 if too large and recommend compact mode
    - [ ] - [ ] Add server-side rate limiting per-agent identity and workspaces to avoid large-scale agent storms
- [ ] TASK-F-055-11: Write agent-focused integration tests and sample client
    - [ ] - [ ] Build a lightweight agent test client that requests compact schema and validates fields required by the session planner
    - [ ] - [ ] Create integration test that the session planner uses schema compact mode and produces a valid session_spec when intent confidence > 0.85
    - [ ] - [ ] Add regression test that low-confidence items are present and cause session planner to surface human_review_required when used
    - [ ] - [ ] Add CI contract validation to prevent accidental removal of confidence fields

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Contract test: validate presence of definition_confidence on metrics and dimensions
- [ ] - [ ] Integration test: compact mode returns index-only payload and is <= 20KB
- [ ] - [ ] Flow test: agent uses returned schema to construct session_spec; when low-confidence item is included, session planner must require human_review_required flag
- [ ] - [ ] Negative test: corrupt schema stored in git should result in 500 and an admin alert
- [ ] - [ ] Security test: assert no fields in response contain raw sample rows or PII markers beyond flagged metadata

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] JSON Schema validation runs for every served schema and CI blocks PRs that break the schema contract
- [ ] - [ ] Agents can fetch compact schema and receive required metadata to build session_specs
- [ ] - [ ] Conflict reporting and confidence metadata present and documented
- [ ] - [ ] Agent integration tests pass in CI
- [ ] - [ ] No raw rows or PII are present in responses and tests validate this

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] F-011 (governance manifest must provide confidence and PII flags)
- [ ] - [ ] Session planner agent consumers (coordination with Stage 2 owners)

###### Agent Notes
- - [ ] Agents must call the compact mode for warm-ups; full schema should be retrieved only when required. - [ ] The endpoint must never return raw sample rows. Only include statistical summaries and confidence. - [ ] Return semantic_layer_version and governance_manifest_version for reproducibility; agents should store these with any session_spec they produce. - [ ] If conflicts exist, return 409 with conflict_id to allow automated workflows to surface human review tasks.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-055-03: As QA / DevOps lead, I want monitoring, testing, and CI/CD checks for the semantic schema API, so that we maintain reliability, detect regressions early, and ensure production readiness.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] CI pipeline runs unit, integration, and contract tests for schema endpoint on every PR; PRs fail on contract/schema mismatches.
- [ ] - [ ] Production monitoring includes metrics and alerts for high error_rate (>1% sustained), p95 latency breaches, cache-miss spikes, and schema-generation failures.
- [ ] - [ ] A scheduled end-to-end smoke test runs every 15 minutes that fetches the schema for a staging workspace and validates JSON Schema and presence of version headers.
- [ ] - [ ] Playbook documented for common failures (403 auth failure, 500 validation failure, 202 generation pending), including remediation steps and owners.
- [ ] - [ ] Load test baseline established and automated nightly load tests run to detect regression in P95 latency and throughput.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-055-12: Add CI checks and contract tests
    - [ ] - [ ] Add JSON Schema contract validation step to CI pipeline that validates example payloads and fails on mismatch
    - [ ] - [ ] Add integration test stage that clones a sample semantic git repo and runs retrieval in isolated test environment
    - [ ] - [ ] Add PR lint rule requiring OpenAPI spec updates if endpoint signatures change
    - [ ] - [ ] Document test scenarios and add to test matrix
- [ ] TASK-F-055-13: Implement production monitoring, alerts, and dashboards
    - [ ] - [ ] Instrument metrics emission for request_count, error_count_by_code, latency_histogram, cache_hit_ratio
    - [ ] - [ ] Create Grafana dashboard for semantic schema API with panels for latency (P50/P95/P99), error rates, and cache hit ratio
    - [ ] - [ ] Create alert rules: error_rate_alert (>1% sustained 5m), p95_latency_alert (>1s sustained 5m), cache_miss_spike (>30% increase 5m)
    - [ ] - [ ] Configure PagerDuty/Slack alert channels and assign on-call rotation
- [ ] TASK-F-055-14: Build synthetic/uptime tests and deploy to monitoring
    - [ ] - [ ] Create synthetic check that runs every 15 minutes: GET schema for test workspace -> validate JSON Schema -> assert X-Semantic-Version present
    - [ ] - [ ] Add synthetic check to uptime service and set alerts on failure after 2 consecutive runs
    - [ ] - [ ] Add runbook links to synthetic check failures
- [ ] TASK-F-055-15: Define load & performance baseline and automated load tests
    - [ ] - [ ] Create k6 (or equivalent) load test script covering cache-hit and cache-miss scenarios
    - [ ] - [ ] Run baseline load tests in staging and store P50/P95/P99 metrics
    - [ ] - [ ] Schedule nightly load tests and fail if P95 increases beyond 2x baseline
    - [ ] - [ ] Document scaling guidance (Redis pool sizes, backend instance counts) based on load results
- [ ] TASK-F-055-16: Create operational runbook and postmortem templates
    - [ ] - [ ] Draft runbook covering 401/403 auth failures, 500 validation errors, 202 generation pending, 429 rate limit hits
    - [ ] - [ ] Create postmortem template and link to on-call Slack/PagerDuty escalation
    - [ ] - [ ] Add contact list of owners: semantic layer team, governance team, infra team

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] CI unit tests: controller, service, cache logic
- [ ] - [ ] CI integration tests: fetch from sample git repo, validate ETag behavior
- [ ] - [ ] Nightly contract validation across all sample schemas
- [ ] - [ ] Synthetic monitoring check every 15 minutes validating JSON Schema and headers
- [ ] - [ ] Load test: 100 RPS sustained for 5 minutes with cache warm and cold scenarios; assert P95 thresholds
- [ ] - [ ] Chaos test: temporarily block access to git storage and assert API returns 202 and alerts on generation failure

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] CI pipeline enforces contract validation and integration tests for endpoint
- [ ] - [ ] Monitoring dashboards and alerts are live and tested via synthetic failures
- [ ] - [ ] Synthetic checks run every 15 minutes and alert on failures
- [ ] - [ ] Load test baseline established and nightly load tests scheduled
- [ ] - [ ] Runbook published and accessible to on-call engineers

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] Observability platform (OpenTelemetry, Grafana, Alerting integration)
- [ ] - [ ] CI runner with access to test Git repos for integration tests

###### Agent Notes
- - [ ] Add contract tests to prevent accidental schema drift. - [ ] Synthetic checks must validate both compact and full payload shapes. - [ ] Use existing observability infra and ensure telemetry includes workspace_id and semantic_layer_version for correlation. - [ ] Coordinate runbook with governance (F-011) and semantic pipeline (F-012) teams to ensure owner mappings for generation failures.

### Epic: Gold Layer

#### Requirement / Feature: Gold layer query API (paginated gold/{workspace}/{table} endpoint)

**Feature ID:** `F-056`  
**Priority:** `P0`  
**Labels:** `backend, api`

**Rationale:** Artifacts must fetch live data at render time; MVP needs a working gold query endpoint.

##### Feature Dependencies
- [ ] F-025
- [ ] F-040

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-056-01: As an artifact runtime (frontend) I want a paginated, authenticated GET /api/v1/gold/{workspace_id}/{table} endpoint so that dashboard artifacts fetch live gold-layer rows at render time without embedding data in HTML.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] The endpoint exists at GET /api/v1/gold/{workspace_id}/{table} and returns HTTP 200 on success with a JSON body matching the agreed response schema.
- [ ] - [ ] The endpoint supports pagination via page_size (int) and page_token (opaque cursor) query params, with defaults page_size=100 and max page_size=1000.
- [ ] - [ ] The response includes next_page_token when more rows are available and total_estimated_rows when available; page tokens are opaque and base64-encoded.
- [ ] - [ ] The endpoint accepts either a workspace-scoped embed_token (query param) OR standard Authorization bearer token and validates token scope, workspace_id, and table access.
- [ ] - [ ] Allowed origins for embed tokens are enforced server-side and requests from disallowed origins return HTTP 403.
- [ ] - [ ] The endpoint enforces that only gold layer tables (naming prefix gold_) within the specified workspace are queryable and returns HTTP 404 for non-existent or non-gold tables.
- [ ] - [ ] The endpoint returns stable, deterministic sorting by the gold table's primary grain keys (provided by metadata); calls with identical cursor and params return consistent rows.
- [ ] - [ ] The endpoint returns appropriate error codes and JSON error objects for invalid params (400), unauthorized (401/403), rate-limited (429), and server errors (500).

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-056-01: Define API contract and OpenAPI spec for gold query endpoint
    - [ ] - [ ] Draft OpenAPI YAML/JSON for GET /api/v1/gold/{workspace_id}/{table} including path params, query params, headers, response schema, and error schema
    - [ ] - [ ] Define pagination model (page_size, page_token) and include example requests/responses for first page and paginated follow-up
    - [ ] - [ ] Add authentication options: embed_token (query) and Authorization: Bearer header; document token validation semantics and allowed_origins enforcement
    - [ ] - [ ] Add rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, Retry-After) and include in spec examples
    - [ ] - [ ] Create API contract PR and get approval from API owner and security
- [ ] TASK-F-056-02: Implement authentication & authorization middleware for gold endpoint
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement embed token verifier: HS256 signature verification, expiry check, scope (workspace_id) validation, artifact/artifact-scoped restrictions
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement bearer token verifier: validate session/service token, fetch user workspace memberships and permissions, enforce read access
    - [ ] - [ ] Enforce allowed_origins for embed tokens by checking Origin header and token.allowed_origins
    - [ ] - [ ] Return consistent error payloads for authentication/authorization failures (401 vs 403) and log attempts for audit
- [ ] TASK-F-056-03: Implement controller & service layer to validate params, build safe queries, and fetch rows from warehouse interface
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate workspace_id and table naming convention (must start with analytics_{workspace} or gold_ prefix depending on implementation) and return 404 for invalid tables
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate and sanitize query params: page_size (int, 1..1000), page_token (opaque), filters (if allowed), sort keys (if allowed)
    - [ ] - [ ] Use warehouse interface (abstracted connector) to generate parameterized SQL SELECT limited by page_size + 1 for next page detection; never interpolate user strings into SQL
    - [ ] - [ ] Map warehouse result rows to canonical JSON response (grain_keys variant, typed columns per gold table schema), include model_id and split fields when present
    - [ ] - [ ] Add structured logging for request start, query text (redact credentials), row count, duration, trace_id
- [ ] TASK-F-056-04: Implement cursor pagination and stable ordering
    - [ ] - [ ] Choose and implement cursor encoding: base64-encoded JSON containing last_row_sort_values + server-side validation TTL
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure SQL uses deterministic ORDER BY on the gold table grain keys to support cursor progression
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement decode/validate logic for page_token and SQL predicate generation (e.g. WHERE (key1,key2,...) > (:cursor_values))
    - [ ] - [ ] Add safety checks to prevent skipping or duplicating rows when concurrent writes happen (document eventual consistency window)
- [ ] TASK-F-056-05: Add caching and short-term result caching to reduce warehouse load
    - [ ] - [ ] Add Redis-based response caching keyed by workspace.table + canonicalized params (filters, page_size, page_token where appropriate) with TTL=300s
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement cache-control behavior: cache only reads without dynamic per-user personalization; bypass cache for requests with user-scoped auth that requires RLS
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit cache hit/miss metrics and include X-Cache header in responses
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement cache invalidation hooks to purge keys when gold table is rewritten (subscribe to gold-table-write events)
- [ ] TASK-F-056-06: Frontend artifact integration and UX handling
    - [ ] - [ ] Update artifact runtime fetch code to call GET /api/v1/gold/{workspace}/{table}?page_size=100 and support page_token continuation
    - [ ] - [ ] Pass embed_token as query param when artifact is embedded; fallback to Authorization header when available
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement artifact-side retry with exponential backoff for transient 5xx and 429 responses and show retry button in UI error card
    - [ ] - [ ] Handle pagination for drill-through drawer with a hard limit of 1,000 rows and CSV export that streams additional pages (server supports subsequent pages)
- [ ] TASK-F-056-07: Implement automated tests and CI checks for the endpoint
    - [ ] - [ ] Write unit tests for controller: param validation, token rejection, error mapping, and cursor encoding/decoding
    - [ ] - [ ] Write integration tests using a warehouse mock harness (DuckDB/Minimock) to validate SQL generation and pagination semantics
    - [ ] - [ ] Add contract tests that assert OpenAPI responses match implementation and that pagination tokens round-trip
    - [ ] - [ ] Add CI job to run tests and a linting check for SQL generation to prevent non-parameterized queries

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit tests: controller validation, token verification failure cases, cursor encode/decode
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests: run endpoint against a local DuckDB instance seeded with sample gold table rows to validate ordering and pagination
- [ ] - [ ] Contract tests: assert OpenAPI examples match runtime responses including headers and error shapes
- [ ] - [ ] End-to-end artifact test: load an artifact in a test environment using an embed_token and assert panels fetch rows and render retry/error UI paths
- [ ] - [ ] Security tests: verify embed_token cannot access other workspace_id, Origin header enforcement, and token expiry handling
- [ ] - [ ] Load test: synthetic traffic to verify P50 < 500ms and P95 < 1.5s for typical page_size=100 queries; ensure rate limiting behavior under burst
- [ ] - [ ] Cache behavior tests: confirm cache hits reduce warehouse calls and X-Cache header present

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] API OpenAPI spec merged and published
- [ ] - [ ] Endpoint implemented behind existing router and reachable in staging
- [ ] - [ ] Auth middleware validates embed_token and bearer tokens and enforces allowed_origins
- [ ] - [ ] Pagination implemented with stable cursors and documented behavior
- [ ] - [ ] Redis caching enabled with cache metrics emitted
- [ ] - [ ] Frontend artifact fetch updated and handles errors/retries
- [ ] - [ ] Unit, integration, and contract tests pass in CI
- [ ] - [ ] Observability: logs, trace_id propagation, and metrics (latency, error rate, cache hit) present in staging
- [ ] - [ ] Security review completed and no high-severity findings

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] F-025
- [ ] - [ ] F-040

###### Agent Notes
-  - [ ] Response JSON schema: { data: [ { grain_keys: object, columns: { col_name: value } } ], next_page_token?: string, total_estimated_rows?: int, meta: { returned: int, req_page_size: int } }. - [ ] Keep SQL generated via warehouse interface using parameterized queries only. - [ ] Cursor must include sort key values and a server-side integrity signature (HMAC with service key) to prevent tampering. - [ ] When implementing, reuse the existing warehouse connector abstractions from F-025 and auth utilities from F-040. - [ ] Prefer deterministic ORDER BY on grain keys; document behavior when concurrent writes occur (eventual consistency).

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-056-02: As a platform/operator I want the gold query API to enforce row-level security, rate limiting, observability, and safe defaults so that the API is production-safe and audit-ready.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] The endpoint enforces row-level security (RLS) for workspace-scoped tokens and returns only rows visible to the requesting principal.
- [ ] - [ ] Rate limiting is applied per-token and per-embed-token scope with clear headers and documented limits; clients receive 429 when exceeded.
- [ ] - [ ] All requests propagate a trace_id and generate structured logs including workspace_id, table, authenticated_principal, query_duration_ms, row_count, and trace links to OpenTelemetry.
- [ ] - [ ] Metrics emitted to monitoring: request_count, request_latency_ms (p50/p95), error_count by status code, cache_hit_rate, and rate_limit_count.
- [ ] - [ ] Audit events are emitted for embed_token use, token validation failures, and gold-table writes invalidating cache.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-056-08: Implement row-level security enforcement and RLS test harness
    - [ ] - [ ] Define RLS policy evaluation interface that the gold query service calls with token/principal context to get per-request WHERE clause or pushdown predicate
    - [ ] - [ ] Integrate RLS interface with warehouse connector so predicates are applied in generated SQL as parameterized filters
    - [ ] - [ ] Add unit tests covering RLS policy application for owner, viewer, and public embed token scenarios
    - [ ] - [ ] Document RLS semantics for embed tokens (embed tokens are workspace-scoped and may have coarse RLS depending on token scope)
- [ ] TASK-F-056-09: Add rate limiting & quota enforcement
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement per-token and per-embed-key rate limiter backed by Redis with configurable windows and limits
    - [ ] - [ ] Add rate limit middleware to set X-RateLimit-* response headers and return 429 with Retry-After when exceeded
    - [ ] - [ ] Add tests to simulate bursts and verify limits and headers are correct
    - [ ] - [ ] Add monitoring alert rule for rate limit saturation
- [ ] TASK-F-056-10: Add observability: tracing, structured logs, and metrics
    - [ ] - [ ] Propagate incoming request trace_id or generate one and include in logs and response headers (X-Trace-Id)
    - [ ] - [ ] Instrument code paths with OpenTelemetry spans around token validation, SQL generation, warehouse call, and cache lookup
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit metrics to the platform metrics service (request_count, latency_histogram, error_count, cache_hit_rate, rate_limit_count)
    - [ ] - [ ] Add dashboards and alerting in staging for high error rate, increased latency, or cache-miss storm
- [ ] TASK-F-056-11: Add audit logging and cache-invalidation hooks
    - [ ] - [ ] Emit audit records (append-only) for embed token uses, token validation failures, and when a gold_table_write event triggers cache purge
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement and subscribe to a gold-table-write event topic so cache keys are invalidated on writes
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure audit log entries contain trace_id, actor_id or token_id, workspace_id, table, and timestamp
- [ ] TASK-F-056-12: Operational docs and runbook
    - [ ] - [ ] Write runbook pages: how to troubleshoot 429s, token validation failures, and slow queries; include key logs and dashboards
    - [ ] - [ ] Publish API operational SLA and rate limit defaults to internal docs
    - [ ] - [ ] Add a rollback plan for disabling caching or rate limiting in emergency

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] RLS functional tests that exercise different principals and assert returned rows match policy
- [ ] - [ ] Load & rate-limit tests: generate concurrent requests to validate rate limiter and 429 behavior; assert headers and Retry-After correctness
- [ ] - [ ] Observability tests: verify trace_id surfaces in logs and traces can be followed across services; verify metrics emitted and dashboards updated
- [ ] - [ ] Audit log tests: validate audit entries created for embed token usage and cache invalidation events
- [ ] - [ ] Chaos test: simulate Redis failure and validate graceful degradation (deny caching, still serve requests) and alerting

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] RLS interface implemented and tested for owner/viewer/embed_token flows
- [ ] - [ ] Rate limiter in place with configurable limits and headers
- [ ] - [ ] OpenTelemetry tracing and structured logs emitted for all gold API requests
- [ ] - [ ] Monitoring dashboards and alerts created for error rate and latency
- [ ] - [ ] Audit events emitted for key security-relevant operations
- [ ] - [ ] Runbook published and on-call notified

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] F-025
- [ ] - [ ] F-040

###### Agent Notes
-  - [ ] RLS must be a deterministic predicate that the warehouse can evaluate; avoid transferring row sets to the app for filtering. - [ ] If underlying warehouse supports native RLS/policy pushdown (e.g. Snowflake row access policies), prefer pushdown; otherwise apply parameterized WHERE predicates. - [ ] Rate limits must be configurable per workspace; default embed token public limits should be more restrictive. - [ ] Emit both metrics and logs with workspace_id and table to allow per-workspace alerting.

##### User Story
- [ ] US-F-056-03: As QA lead and security engineer I want deterministic validation, robust error handling, and thorough automated tests so that the gold query endpoint is production-rigorous and safe for MVP artifact usage.

###### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] - [ ] All validation rules run deterministically and return structured error payloads with error_code, message, and remediation_hint fields.
- [ ] - [ ] The service returns 400 for invalid params, 401/403 for auth issues, 404 for missing gold tables, 429 for rate limit, and 500 for unexpected errors; error payloads are documented in OpenAPI.
- [ ] - [ ] Test coverage: controller and service unit tests >= 90% on critical paths; integration tests run in CI for main branch.
- [ ] - [ ] Security scanning (SAST) and dependency vulnerability scan pass with no critical findings before merge.
- [ ] - [ ] Performance tests meet baseline P50 < 500ms and P95 < 1.5s for typical page_size=100 queries in staging.

###### Tasks
- [ ] TASK-F-056-13: Implement deterministic request validation and unified error handling
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement a validation layer that returns typed validation errors for params (page_size range, token format, workspace/table pattern)
    - [ ] - [ ] Implement global error handler that maps internal errors to API error objects with fields { error_code, message, remediation_hint }
    - [ ] - [ ] Document all error codes in OpenAPI and include example payloads for each HTTP status
- [ ] TASK-F-056-14: Add CI integration tests and coverage thresholds
    - [ ] - [ ] Add integration test job that runs against in-memory or Dockerized DuckDB/warehouse mock and seeds a gold table fixture
    - [ ] - [ ] Ensure nightly test pipeline runs contract & performance smoke tests against staging
    - [ ] - [ ] Configure coverage gating for critical modules (fail CI if coverage < 90% for controller/service modules)
- [ ] TASK-F-056-15: Run security scans and remediate findings
    - [ ] - [ ] Run SAST (static analysis) and dependency vulnerability scans on the branch
    - [ ] - [ ] Fix any high/critical findings prior to merge and document acceptance of any low/medium issues with mitigation
    - [ ] - [ ] Add CI step to fail on new critical/severe vulnerabilities
- [ ] TASK-F-056-16: Performance & load testing
    - [ ] - [ ] Create JMeter/locust scenario for typical artifact fetch patterns (page_size=100, 1 request per panel) and run in staging
    - [ ] - [ ] Validate latency targets (P50 < 500ms, P95 < 1.5s) and identify slow query plans with EXPLAIN from warehouse connector
    - [ ] - [ ] If queries exceed targets, add indexing/materialized pre-agg recommendations or adjust cache TTLs and re-run
- [ ] TASK-F-056-17: QA exploratory and regression testing
    - [ ] - [ ] QA to perform exploratory tests covering invalid tokens, expired tokens, origin mismatch, malformed page_tokens, and concurrent pagination
    - [ ] - [ ] Verify artifact UI shows proper error cards, retry flows, and CSV export behavior for paginated drill-through
    - [ ] - [ ] Run regression checklist to ensure no previous gold-layer behavior is broken

###### Test Plan
- [ ] - [ ] Unit test matrix for positive and negative parameter combinations
- [ ] - [ ] Integration tests hitting warehouse mock verifying SQL parameterization and pagination correctness
- [ ] - [ ] Contract tests ensuring OpenAPI responses and error payloads match implementation
- [ ] - [ ] Security tests for token tampering attempts (altered base64 cursor, forged embed_token signature) and origin enforcement
- [ ] - [ ] Load tests to verify latency and throughput targets; replay production-ish artifact traffic patterns
- [ ] - [ ] Regression tests to ensure cache invalidation on gold table writes works

###### Definition of Done
- [ ] - [ ] Deterministic validation and unified error responses implemented and documented
- [ ] - [ ] CI integration tests passing on main branch and coverage gates met
- [ ] - [ ] Security and dependency scans passed with no critical issues
- [ ] - [ ] Performance tests meet baseline targets in staging or have documented mitigation plan
- [ ] - [ ] QA exploratory test sign-off recorded in ticket and test case results uploaded

###### Story Dependencies
- [ ] - [ ] F-025
- [ ] - [ ] F-040

###### Agent Notes
-  - [ ] Standardize error_code taxonomy (e.g. GOLD_400_INVALID_PARAM, GOLD_401_TOKEN_INVALID, GOLD_403_ORIGIN_BLOCKED, GOLD_404_TABLE_NOT_FOUND, GOLD_429_RATE_LIMIT, GOLD_500_INTERNAL). - [ ] Contract tests must be run as part of PR gating. - [ ] For performance profiling, capture warehouse query text and EXPLAIN plans; avoid logging PII. - [ ] Ensure cursors are resilient to replay/tamper by signing with server HMAC and short TTL. - [ ] Prefer using DuckDB in CI for deterministic integration tests; isolate Snowflake usage to manual performance runs in staging.

# Deferred Backlog

- [ ] Full feature engineering subagent with encoding strategies (one-hot, target encoding CV, hash/frequency), robust imputation, collinearity dropping and top-200 MI selection
    - Reason: Phase 2 scope: MVP includes only minimal feature manifest scaffold; full FE requires additional infra and validation.
    - Suggested Release: Release 2

- [ ] LightGBM, Ridge baselines, Prophet integration and automated ensemble promotion
    - Reason: MVP ships XGBoost primary to reduce complexity; multi-algo support and ensemble logic deferred to Phase 2.
    - Suggested Release: Release 2

- [ ] SHAP explainability computations and feature importance panel in artifacts
    - Reason: Artifact MVP includes KPI and primary time series; SHAP storage and UI panels require extra storage and compute planning.
    - Suggested Release: Release 2

- [ ] Dimension breakdown panel, forecast panel (horizon sliders) and full click-to-drill drawer with row-level SHAP
    - Reason: Complex artifact panels and drill UX deferred until basic artifact engine is stable and performance tuned.
    - Suggested Release: Release 2

- [ ] Embed token system (HS256, allowed_origins), public links with password/expiry and cross-workspace scoped tokens
    - Reason: Secure embed/token workflows require production-grade signing infra and origin enforcement; postpone until Release 2.
    - Suggested Release: Release 2

- [ ] Scheduled refresh, drift monitoring, and one-click retrain automation
    - Reason: MVP supports manual refresh; scheduled drift monitoring requires additional monitoring and alert infra.
    - Suggested Release: Release 2

- [ ] dbt project import as semantic layer explores and dbt test integration
    - Reason: dbt integration requires mapping dbt artifacts and tests; outside MVP connector scope.
    - Suggested Release: Release 2

- [ ] Champion/challenger framework and async training for datasets >1M rows (distributed training)
    - Reason: Scaling training and champion management are enterprise features planned for Phase 3.
    - Suggested Release: Release 2
